eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Farasii la Sadusiiduma la wa'am na ti ba makɛ la Yezu bihɛ. Nyaa yele en yeti, iŋɛ daalehum en ze'ele saazuo na.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yinɛ han ma'ɛ, ya ni yeti, Zina dela dabehesunɛ, se'ere en la sɔi la, sagbama la mu'ɛ mɛ.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Ti bulika me, ya wan yeti, Sabeeo wan lobe zina se'ere en sɔi la, sagbama la mu'ɛ mɛ gee bɔta saa. Ya mi' sagbama yɛla pa'ala, gee ya kan ta'am pa'alɛ saŋa woo daalehum en de se'em.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Be'em la tuunfi'iha buuri saŋa en eere daalehum. Gee daalehum ka boi en wan bo' ba han dagena Yona daalehum la ma'a. Ti a bahɛ ba gee kiŋɛ.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Yezu zahereba la en yakɛ ateko la yakenɛ deyima ha la, ba tamɛ mɛ ka tara dia.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Tara ya mɛhi suŋa, gee la ya mina Farasii la Sadusiiduma dabelɛ la yele.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Tumam en ka tari dia na la en sɔi ti a tɔgera bala.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Yezu en mi' ba puti'irɛ la, ti a yele ba yeti, yamam asakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya duma wa, Ŋwani ti ya tɔgera ya ka tari dia na yele?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Yamam kelum ka bɔkera? Yamam ka tɛri boroboro banuu la n daa diihe nɛreba tuha anuu la, la pitɔ se'em ti ya daa doohe va pirɛ la?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Bii boroboro bayopɔi ti n daa dikɛ diihe tuha anaahi la, la pitɔ se'em ti ya daa doohe va pirɛ la?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ŋwani ti ya koŋe ya bɔkɛ ti n ka tɔgeri dia yele? Mini ya Farasii la Sadusiiduma la dabelɛ la yele.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Bala ti ba nyaa bɔkɛ ti a ka yeti ba ba tara mɛhi suŋa, dabelɛ la yele, gee Farasii la Sadusiiduma la pa'alegɔ la.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Yezu en paaɛ Kasariya Filipi tiŋa puan ba'ahɛ la, ti a nyaa soke a zahereba la yeti, Nɛreba yeti mam Asaala Dayua wa dela ani.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Ti ba yeti, Sɛba yeti fum dela Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra la, ti sɛba me yeti fum dela Eliya, ti basɛba mɛ kelum yeti fum dela Yɛremiya bii nɔtɔgeheba la ayima.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Gee yamam yeti mam dela ani.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita lerege yeti, Fum n de Kiresta la, Yinɛ vua la Dayua.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Sum boi mɛ la hɔ Simɔn, Yona dayua wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi ingɔŋɔ la ziim n lilege dina wa bo' fum, gee mam Sɔ se'em en boi saazuo ha.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: N yeti fum, fum dela Pita, tankana wa zuo ti mam wan mɛ en pu'uhegɔ yire la, ti kum paŋa kan malum nyaŋɛ dɛ.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: N wan bo' fum saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la kparehi, sɛla woo ti fum bobe tiŋa wa zuo, saazuo ha me wan bobe. Sɛla wuu ti fum lorege bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo, saazuo ha me wan lorege bahɛ.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Ti a kagɛ a zahereba la paaɛ paaɛ ti ba da yele nɛra nɛra ti eŋa n da Kiresta la.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Sankaŋa puan yɛ'a ha, ti Yezu pɔhɛ yɛta a zahereba la yeti, la dela kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm ta namehɛ namehekãtɛ kiinduma la, malema kiinduma duma la gɔnmi'ineba la nu'uhen, gee ti ba ku' en. Gee dabeha atã daarɛ a wan vo'e a kum la puan.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Ti Pita kɔkɛ Yezu lebe kinkɛleŋa ta ɔbera a tuba ti a da tɔgera bala. Yinɛ gu'. Daana! Dina kan malum paaɛ ya.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Gee Yezu ŋmɛregɛ yele Pita yeti, Fɔregɛ mam zi'an Sagena wa. Fum dela pirega pita mam, se'ere en sɔi la, fum puti'irɛ ka boi saazuo yɛla puan, gee asaala yɛla puan.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Ti Yezu yele a zahereba la yeti, Se'em woo en boti a dɔla mam, a daana zagehɛ a mɛŋa gee dikɛ a pumpuuredɔɔ zi dɔla mam.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em en boti a gu' a vom, wan bɔregɛ bɔ bahɛ. Ti se'em me en bɔregɛ a vom mam zuo, wan nyɛ bɔ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Beni nyuurɔ ti a wan nyɛ, a han nyɛ tiŋa wa lɔgerɔ za'a gee koŋe a nyɔvore? Bii a wan dikɛ la beni ta'am tee ba nyɔvore?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala dayua la yeti a wa'am me na la a suŋereba, a Sɔ suŋa la puan, bilam ti a wan yɔ nɛrawoo doohe en tum se'em.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti basɛba boi yan ze' wa puan kan lem kum, gee wan nyɛ Asaala Dayua la kinɛ a sɔ'ɔlum la puan na.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yezu n nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la, a zom la zoore zuo, en ta zi'ire ba'ahɛ la, ti a zahereba la paaɛ a zi'an.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Ti a ya'ɛ nuurɛ, pɔhɛ pa'ala ba yeti:.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en mi' ti ba boti sia puan suŋɛ, bamam en sɔi saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en kele, ba wan nyɛ subugehum.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en sigehe ba mɛhi, bamam n wan nyɛ tiŋa wa nyuurɔ.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba ti tuuntɔɔ vom kom, la ko'nyuuro tara ba, Ba wan tigehɛ ba.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en zoti nɛreba nimbu'ɔ. Ba wan to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba suya en ka tari dɛgerɔ. Ba wan nyɛ Yinɛ.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en sugeni. Ba wan yi'ira ba Yinɛ kɔma.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba ti ba mɛleha ba tuuntɔɔ vom zuo. Ba dinɛ dela saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Sum boi mɛ la yamam, ti nɛreba en tuuri ya, gee namehera ya, yi'ira ya yu'yooro yima yima en dagi yelemɛŋerɛ, mam zuo.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Sireni ya gee nyɛɛna, gee ya yɔɔrɛ karegɛ mɛ saazuo ha. Bala mɛa ti ba yuum mɛleha Yinɛ nɔtɔgeheba sɛba en diŋɛ yamam nɛŋa na la.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Yamam en de tiŋa wa zuo yaarum; gee yaarum han bɔregɛ bu za'afum, bu wan iŋɛ la ŋwani le ta'am lebe bu puan na? Yaarum bumɔ ka le ana suŋa bo' sɛla sɛla, han dagena ba ka'ɛ bu bahɛ ti ba niira la nama.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Yamam dela tiŋa wa zuo niifum. Tiŋa en mɛ yagelɛ zoore zuo, kan ta'am lile.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Nɛra ka laŋɛ butolela dikɛ pi'ɔ vuge ka; gee a dikɛ ka sugela la butolela sugelego zi'an, ti ka niiɛ bo' nɛra woo yire la puan.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Bala mɛa, bahɛ ya ti ya niifum la niira nɛreba nɛŋan, ti ba nyɛ ya tuunsuma la, la ba dikɛ pɛka bo' ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha la.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Da ti'ihɛ ya ti mam wa'am na ti n saalum la nɔze'elego la bii nɔtɔgeheba yɛla la. Mam ka wa'am na ti en saalum ba bahɛ mɛ, gee mam wa'am na ti bu wa'am la asira na.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Gulehegɔ wa dela tuuhum en de Yezu Kiresta en Deviti dayua, Aberaham dayua la.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Mam yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti han dagena ti saazuo la timpaberɛ n tole, gulehɛgɔ pika yɛla la puan kan makɛ saalum, han dagena a za'a wa'am na tum mɛ.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Aberaham dela Aziki sɔ, ti Aziki dɛna Yakɔbe sɔ, ti Yakɔbe dɛna Yuda la a suurɔ sɔ.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Bala zuo, fum se'em woo n galum nɔze'elego la puan yelepika gee pa'ala nɛreba ti ba ita bala, eŋa daana en wan ta dɛna nɛre pika saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan; gee se'em woo en dole a za'a, gee me pa'ala nɛreba bala, wan ta dɛna nɛrekãtɛ Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: Ti Yuda dɛna Pɛrezi la Zera sɔ, ti ba ma dɛna Tama. Ti Pɛrezi dɛna Hɛzirɔn sɔ, ti Hɛzirɔn dɛna Aram sɔ.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Mam yeti ya mɛ wa, ti han dagena ya tuuntɔɔ vom la gaŋɛ la Gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasiiduma la, yamam me pun kan makɛ kɛ̃ Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: Ti Aram dɛna Aminadabi sɔ, ti Aminadabi dɛna Nason sɔ, ti Nason dɛna Salemon sɔ.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Ya wum ti ba yuum yele bo' diimen diimen ha la yeti, Da ku nɛra; gee fum se'em en ku nɛra wan nyɛ gakerɛ.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: Ti Salemon dɛna Boazi sɔ, ti a ma dɛna Arahab. Ti Boazi dɛna Ɔbɛd sɔ, ti a ma dɛna Arutu. Ti Ɔbɛd dɛna Yesi sɔ.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, fum se'em en iŋɛ suure la a suuɔ wan nyɛ gakerɛ. Fum se'em en tu fum suuɔ wan paaɛ kiinduma zi'an. Fum se'em en yele nɛra ti, Gerego wa, ye' la bugum tiŋa.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: ti Yesi dɛna Naba Deviti sɔ. Ti Deviti dɛna Solomɔn sɔ, ti a ma dɛna Yuriya pɔga la.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Bala, ya han bɔ'ɔra ya bo'olum kaabegɔ bimbeni la zuo, gee tiɛ ti fum suuɔ tari se'ere la hɔ.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: Ti Solomɔn dɛna Yerobuam sɔ, ti Yerobuam dɛna Abiya sɔ, ti Abiya dɛna Asaf sɔ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: bahɛ fum bo'olum la kaabegɔ bimbeni la zi'an gee kiŋɛ ta sugelum fum suuɔ la, gee nyaa wa'am na bo' fum bo'olum la.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: Ti Asaf dɛna Yehosafate sɔ, ti Yehosafate dɛna Yoram sɔ, ti Yoram dɛna Yuziya sɔ.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Ti Yezu zom uureŋɔ, yakɛ kiŋɛ a mɛŋa tiŋa.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Naaɛ la se'em en zuuhe fum la ka'lam ti ya nam boi sore zuo yɛ'a naba yire la. Han dagena bala, a wan dikɛ fum iŋɛ naba nu'uhen, ti eŋa me dikɛ fum bo' kpɛ'ɛnduma, ti bamam me dikɛ fum lobe bahɛ paka deo puan.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: Ti Yuziya sɔ dɛna Yotan, ti Yotan sɔ dɛna Ahazi, ti Ahazi sɔ dɛna Hɛzekaya.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: La ka yuue ti nɛreba basɛba en bala zɛɛra nɛra iŋa en ki ti a gã'a suŋɔ zuo kina na. Yezu en nyɛ ba sakerɛ la, a yele buraa la iŋa en ki la yeti, Bia wa, fum be'em la bahɛ ti fum suure gã'arɛ. Fum be'em la bahɛ bo' fum.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ, ti fum kan makɛ yehe na han dagena fum ta yɔ la hanɛ la za'a kpiniŋam.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: Ti Hɛzekaya sɔ dɛna Manaasɛ, ti Manaasɛ sɔ dɛna Amɔsi, ti Amɔsi dɛna Yoseya.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba basɛba gɔgena la ba mɛhi yeti, Burakana wa tuuri mɛ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Ya wum ti ba yuum yeti, Da tum kabohi.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: Ti Yoseya sɔ dɛna Yekonaya la a suurɔ. Sankaŋa en de apɛregɛ loŋe Babilɔn la.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Gee Yezu en mi' ba puti'irɛ la, a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ti'iha be'em ya puti'ira puan?.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, se'em woo en bihɛ pɔka la kunkoma'afum pun tum la kabohi la pɔka la mɛ a suuren.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Ti apɛregɛ-loŋe Babilɔn saŋa la pooren, ti Yekonia dɔgɛ a dayua Salatiɛle, ti Salatiɛle dɛna Zerubabɛle sɔ.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: De deni en de naana? N yeti fum be'em la bahɛ bo' fum la, bii n yeti, Ihege kina la?.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Fum zuɔ niho han bahɛ ti fum tuna be'em, kpikɛ bɔ lobe bahɛ; la ani suŋa ti fum bɔregɛ fum iŋa la zi'an kayima bahɛ, nyana iŋa la za'a en ta dikɛ bahɛ bugum tiŋa la puan.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: ti Zerubabɛle dɛna Abiyud sɔ, ti Abiyud dɛna Eliyakim sɔ, ti Eliyakim dɛna Azɔr sɔ.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Gee bala ti ya baŋɛ ti asaala dayua la tari paŋa tiŋa wa zuo ti a bahɛ nɛreba be'em bahɛ bo' ba. Gee nyaa yele buraa la iŋa n ki la yeti, Ihege vaaɛ fum suŋɔ la fum kule fum yire.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Ti fum zuɔ nu'o han baha ti fum tuna be'em, ŋmaaɛ kɔ, lobe bahɛ; la ani suŋa ti fum bɔregɛ fum iŋa la zi'an kayima bahɛ nyana iŋa la za'a en ta dikɛ bahɛ bugum tiŋa la puan.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: ti Azɔr dɛna Zadɔki sɔ, ti Zadɔki dɛna Akim sɔ, ti Akim dɛna Eliyud sɔ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Ti buraa la ihege ze'ele, kina kula a yire.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Ba me yuum tɔgɛ yeti, Se'em woo en bahɛ a pɔga, a daana gulehɛ lebehego gɔŋɔ bo' ka, gee bahɛ ka.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: ti Eliyud dɛna Ɛliyaza sɔ, ti Ɛliyaza dɛna Matan sɔ, ti Matan dɛna Yakɔbe sɔ.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la en nyɛ ŋwana la, dabeem en sãɛ ba, ti ba pɛgɛra Yinɛ, se'em en dikɛ paŋa kana taaba bo' saaleba.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, se'em woo en bahɛ a pɔga han dagena ka tum la kabohi; a bahɛ ti pɔge kaŋa galum mɛ; ti se'em woo en di pɔka ti ba bahɛ ka tum la kabohi la pɔge kana.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: ti Yakɔbe dɛna Yosefe sɔ, Mɛɛri sira la sɔ, Mɛɛri se'em en dɔgɛ Yezu, se'em ti ba yi'ira Kiresta la.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Yezu en kini la, a nyɛ la buraa ti ba yi'ira Matu, ti a zi'a kuba to'ohego deo puan, ti a yele en yeti, Dɔla mam ti a ihege doohe en.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, ya wum ti ba yuum yele diimen diimen duma yeti, Nɛra da pɔ pumpɔreŋɔ pua, gee tum fum pu'ulɛ nuurɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti fum iŋɛ se'ere bo' Daana la.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Bala, buuri tuuhum la za'a, ze'ele Aberaham ta paaɛ Deviti dela buuri tuuhum pia la anaahi. Ze'ele Deviti ta paaɛ apɛregɛ-loŋe Babilɔn saŋa la dela, buuri tuuhum pia la bunaahi. Ze'ele a apɛregɛ loŋe Babilɔn ta paaɛ Kiresta kinɛ saŋa la, me dela buuri tuuhum pia la anaahi.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Yezu la a zahereba la ta zi'a la yire puan dɛla taaba dia saŋa. Ti kubetɔ'ɔhereba la be'em duma zo'e zo'e me n bala wa'am na zi'a dɛla la Yezu la a zahereba la.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Gee mam yeti, da pun makɛ pɔ la fii me. Da makɛ pɔ saazuo wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ na'am kuka zi'an en bala.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Nananawa, Yezu en de Kiresta la dɔgum en doohe la sadɔtɔ kuna. Lan wa eŋa ti a ma Mɛɛri yeti a ele Yosefe la. Gee se'em ma'a ti ba ta yeti ba nyaa zi'ire la taaba la, a nyɛ ti pun tara la puurɛ, Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasiiduma la en nyɛ ŋwana la, ba soke Yezu zahereba la yeti, Ŋwani ti ya pa'ala la naaɛ la kubetɔ'ɔhereba la be'em duma dita.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: bii fum pɔ timpaberɛ wa, se'ere sɔi la, Yinɛ nama dafeere n bala, bii fum pɔ Yerusalɛm, se'ere en sɔi la, Yerusalɛm dela na'kãtɛ la tiŋa.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: Gee Yosefe a sira la, ka bɔta a diihe en vi nɛreba nɛŋan la, maahum mɛ ti a bahɛ en bããlam.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Dabeha aŋa ha puan la, ti Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra la wa bɔna la Yudiya muuen ha muula baha na yeti.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Gee Yezu n wum dɛna wa, a yeti, Sɛba en de ba mɛhi ka eeri tiba sɛba en bɛ̃'ɛri ma'a.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Da pɔ fum zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, fum kan ta'am bahɛ ti zuo zonko lebege pɛɛlega bii sabega.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Gee en ti'ihɛ ku ti a iŋɛ ŋwana wa la, ti Yinɛ suŋera niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' en zaaheŋɔ puan yi' en yeti, Yosefe, Deviti dayua, da tara dabeem ti dabeem ti fum to'e Mɛɛri ti a dɛna fum pɔga. Se'ere sɔi la, bisɛka puurɛ ti a tara wa, dela Fa'a Sia la paŋa n bahɛ ti la iŋɛ bala.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Gee kiŋɛ ya ta zamehɛ yele dina wa vuurɛ. Mam nini tigɛ mɛ la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ dagi kaabegɔ. Mam ka wa'am na ti n yi' la tuuntɔɔ duma, gee be'em duma.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Tee ya, gee Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la pɔri na.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Bahɛ ti fum ɛ̃ɛ dɛna ɛ̃ɛ, bii fum Aayi dɛna aayi; sɛla en dagi ŋwana ze'ele la be'em puan na.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: A wan dɔgɛ la budibela ti fum sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Yezu. Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa n yeti a faaɛ a nɛreba ba be'em puan bahɛ.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Ti Yɔn zahereba wa'am Yezu zi'an na soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti tumam la Farasiiduma la luti la nuurɛ tabela, gee ti fum zahereba la ka zageha ya mɛhi.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Nɛre-ina n bala ti nɔtɔgeha Azaaya tɔgɛ a yele yeti, Kua kayima n kaahi muuen ha baha na yeti, Maafum ya Daana la sore, maalɛ ya a sore la tuuntɔɔ. .

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Ya wum ti ba yuum yele diimen diimen ha la ti, Niho teere la niho, ti Nyɛnɛ tɛɛra nyɛnɛ.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Yele ana za'a iŋɛ ti la bahɛ ti Daana la en tɔgɛ se'em doohe a nɔtɔgeha puan la wa'am sira na. La yeti.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Pɔgedire de'eŋo saama la suya kan ta'am sagum, Pɔgedita la en boi la ba la. Bii ba suya wan ta'am sagum? Gee dabeha kini na ti ba wan dikɛ Pɔgedita la gee bahɛ ba, bilam ti ba nyaa wan luta nuurɛ.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yɔn yɛ la futo ti ba dikɛ yugenɛ kɔberɔ wugɛ, ti a sɛganɛ dɛna duŋa ganɛ baalega ti a dikɛ lu' a sia, ti a dia dɛna sunwa'ara la muuen seero.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, da lerege se'em en tuue fum. Gee se'em han lobe fum zuɔ sapɛka, ŋmɛregɛ gɔbega sapɛka la pa'ahɛ en.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Bihɛ, pɔgesakãherɛ wan nyɔkɛ puurɛ dɔgɛ budibela, ti ba sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Emanuwɛl. De vuurɛ dela, Yinɛ boi la tɔ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Nɛra ka dikeri tanɛ paalɛ ligera fukɛka, tanɛ paalɛ la wan ahegɛ fukɛka la, ti ahegerɛ dinɛ wan ba' gaŋɛ.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Pooren ti Farasiiduma la gɔnmi'ineba ze'ele Yerusalɛm wa'am a zi'an na soke en yeti.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Ti Yerusalɛm tiŋa nɛreba, la sɛba woo n boi Yudiya nɛreba za'a kina a zi'an na, la tinse'ehi woo en kaaɛ Yɔɔdan kulaa la nɛreba za'a.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Se'em me han yi' fum naba yire ti a to'e fum banaa, yɛɛ damsika la pa'ahɛ en.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Yosefe en ihege a guum la puan la, a tum doohe la Daana suŋera la en bo' en nuurɛ se'em la, to'e Mɛɛri ti a dɛna a pɔga.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: Bii ba dikɛ daam en kele su'ura lankɛka puan. La han iŋɛ bala, laŋa la wan tahɛ ti daam la ka'ɛ, ti laŋa la me sagum. Gee daam en kele su'uri la lampaalega puan, ti ba za'a kan sagum.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Ŋwani ti fum zahereba la galum kiinduma malema la? Ba ka peere nu'uhi gee dita.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: ti ba puta ba be'em ti a sɔɔra ba ko'om Yɔɔdan kulaa la puan.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Se'em han pɛregɛ fum ti fum kiŋɛ miilɛ deyina la en, kiŋɛ miilɛ pa'ahɛ en.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Saŋa kaŋa la, ti Yezu la a zahereba la dɔla va'am puan tɔla vo'ohego dabeherɛ, ti a zahereba la kom duna, ti ba kɔ'ɔra va'am la puan ki ɔbera.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Yezu en ta pa'alɛ a zahereba pia la ba yi' la ba'ahɛ la, a ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ ta pa'ala mɛ gee muula a yelefum la ba tinkãra puan.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Gee Yosefe ka makɛ naaɛ la Mɛɛri ta paaɛ se'em ma'a ti Mɛɛri dɔgɛ budibela la, ti Yosefe sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Yezu.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: En boi bini tɔgera ŋwana bɔ'ɔra ba la, ti zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana ayima en bala paaɛ na dɔbelɛ bɛlena Yezu yeti, N poyua n nan kɔ'ɔm lu ma'aherɛ ma'aherɛ. Bala wa'am na dikɛ fum nu'o pagelɛ a zuo ti a vo'e.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Yezu en ze'ele zoore la zuo sige na la, nɛrekuuŋɔ en dole en.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Gee ŋwani ti yamam galena Yinɛ pa'alegɔ ya buuri malema zuo?.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Gee Yɔn en ta nyɛ ti Farasii la Sadusiiduma la kini a zi'an na ti a sɔ ba ko'om la, a yele ba yeti, Yamam kampɛbila wa! Ani en yele ya la ninmu'urɛ ti ya zɔ li'ihɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim wa n kini na wa?.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Bo' se'em woo en suhɛ hɔ sɛla, da tuta nu'o la se'em woo en boti a peŋe sɛla hɔ zi'an.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Farasiiduma la en nyɛ ŋwana la, ba yele en yeti, Bihɛ, fum zahereba la iti la sɛla en dagi ba iŋɛ vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Nananawa, Yɔn en boi pakadeen la gee wum sɛla ti Kiresta la, ita la, a bo' la a zahereba la nuurɛ.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Ti Yezu ihege doohe en, ti a zahereba la doohe la.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: ti kunkone deyima paaɛ a nɛŋan kpa' duma yele en yeti, Daana, fum han bɔta tibɛ mam.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yeti, Na'aha fum sɔ la fum ma, fum se'em en tɔgɛ be'em bo' fum sɔ bii fum ma dela fum ki.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Wani ya wala sɛ'a n pa'alɛ ti ya teere la.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Ya wum ti ba yuum yeti, Nɔŋɛ fum mabia, gee yaaɛ fum dima.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yamam ka kãalɛ Deviti la a nɛreba en yuum iŋɛ se'em, ba kom en yuum duni la?.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: yeti, Fum n bala wan wa'am na la bii tu gura ayima.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Bilam bilam ti pɔka ziim en ka'ari yuuma pia la ayi', wa'am na ze'ele Yezu pooren kalum sãberɛ en yule a fuo la tiŋa.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Ti Yezu tiɛ a nu'o kalum en gee yeti, Mam boti, to'e fum tibegɔ! Bilam bilam ti buraa wa iŋɛ sum.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: De daarɛ mɛa ti Yezu yehe yire ta zi'a ateko nuuren.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Gee yamam yeti, nɛra han yele a sɔ bii a ma yeti, suŋɛ se'ere woo ti fum wan nyɛ ni mam zi'an la, mam dikɛ de bo' la Yinɛ. Bala zuo, nɛre-ina daana ka na'aha a sɔ le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Da kɔ'ɔm kɔ'ɛ ya puti'ira yɛta ya mɛhi yeti, Aberaham en de tumam sɔyaaba la zuo. N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti Yinɛ wan ta'am bahɛ ti kuga wa asɛ'a lebege Aberaham kɔma.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Gee mam ya yeti, nɔŋɛ ya dinduma gee pu'uha bɔ'ɔra sɛba en daani ya.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Deviti yuum kɛ̃ la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ta obe tuulum boroboro la n de kihere bo' eŋa bii a nɛreba la ti ba obe, han dagena malema daana la ma'a.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta yele Yɔn ya wum gee nyɛ sɛla.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka wa yele ka mɛŋa yeti, N han yeen kalum a fuo wa ma'a n wan nyɛ n tibegɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ti Yezu yele buraa wa yeti, Bihɛ suŋa la fum da yele nɛra nɛra se'ere, gee tari fum mɛŋa ta pa'alɛ malema daana la, gee bo' ba'a bahega bo'olum sebo ti Moose bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya bɔ'ɔra la, ti la dɛna sɛla iŋɛ gã'a bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ en ka tã ita lagehɛ a zi'an na, ti a zom uureŋɔ zuo zi'a bini gee ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la zɛ'a ko'om la nuuren.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Bala, ya buuri malema wa zuo, ti ya ka bihɛ nyɛ Yinɛ yelefum wa.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nananawa, liya la maahum mɛ gã'a tia woo tilen, ti tisɛka woo en ka wani walesuma, liya la wan ŋmaaɛ tikaŋa bahɛ, ti ba dikɛ ka bahɛ bugum la puan.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Ŋwana ti ya wan dɛna ya Sɔ se'em en boi saazuo ha wa kɔma. Se'ere en sɔi la, a baheri ti wuntɛɛŋa nyɛkera bɔ'ɔra nɛrebe'ero la nɛresuma za'a mɛ, gee tuna saa bɔ'ɔra nɛretuntɔɔ la nɛre ŋmɛlema za'a.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Bii ya ka kãalɛ nɔze'elego la puan, ti vo'ohego dabehere la, malema duma la en boi pu'uhegɔ yire la galum vo'ohego dabeherɛ la mɛ gee ti la ka galum?.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: fɔɔhi nini pigehɛ mɛ, ti kɔɔrenɔ kina, ti kunkɔma nyɛ tibegɔ, ti wahi wuna, ti kum ihege, ti naheduma wum faarɛ yelefum la.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Yezu en ŋmɛregɛ nyɛ pɔka wa la, a yeti; N poyua, bahɛ ti fum suure gã'arɛ, fum sakerɛ la n bahɛ ti fum dɛna fum mɛŋa. Yehe bala ma'a, bilam bilam ti pɔka dɛna ka mɛŋa.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Yezu en kɛ̃ Kapɛnum la, ti tansugere nɛŋa en tari pɛntiireba kɔbega wa'am a zi'an na.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Ti a yele ba yɛla zo'e zo'e mageha puan yeti, Kelehe ya! Buta en yehe ti a burɛ.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Birekɔgehi wa, Azaaya en purɛ ya yele se'em la dela yelemɛŋerɛ. A yeti.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Mam sɔɔri ya mɛ la ko'om wa, ti la pa'alɛ ti ya tee ya puti'ira ya be'em puan. Gee se'em en bi'ihɛ gani mam boi mam pooren kina na. Mam pun ka seke ti n zãla a tagera. Eŋa wan sɔ ya mɛ la Fa'a Sia la, la bugum.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Yamam han nɔŋɛ sɛba en nɔŋɛ ya ma'a, beni yɔɔrɛ ti ya wan to'e? Sɛba en to'ohe kuba pun ka ita la bala?.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti sɛla en gani pu'uhegɔ yire la boi ka'lam.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Sum boi la se'em en ka piti luta mam zuo.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Yezu en paaɛ kpɛ'ɛma la yire la, nyɛ ti taliten-pɛɛbereba peeberi, ti nɛreba ana gagelam.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: bɛlena en yeti, Daana, en yameŋa en kɔ'ɔm kaarum gã'a ka ta'na ihegera ti la ba' wuna en.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Ti en buti la, ti bumbuura la basɛba zɔgɛ sore zuo, ti niihe wa'am na di ba.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Nɛrebana wa na'ahɛ mam mɛ la ba nɔya, gee ba suya zaaɛ mɛ la mam.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: A zãli a yeelego pi'ɔ mɛ a nu'uhen, a wan yeele disɛka woo ti a tɔ a toore la puan la, vaaɛ a kisuma la su' bããren. Gee bunvagela la, a wan nyɔ' ba la bugum n ka diti kiŋera puan.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Yamam han pu'uha ya suurɔ ma'a, yamam iti la beni gana nɛra woo en iti se'em la? Sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ me pun ka ita la bala mɛa?.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Gee ya han mi' ni yetɔgebuna wa vuurɛ. Mam eere la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ gee dagena kaabegɔ, ya kan gakɛ ni sɛba en ka galum la.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Ban ye' lɛbera la, ti Yezu pɔhɛ tɔgera Yɔn yele bɔ'ɔra nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Beni ti yamam yuum kɛ̃ muuen la ha ti ya ta bihɛ? Muɔ ti sabeeo tara vɛgelena bii?.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yiregɛ ya, pugela la ka ki, a gihi mɛ. Ti nɛreba la taaɛ la'ara en.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, N wan wa'am na tibɛ en.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ti basɛba lu tahi zi'an, zi'an ti tintɔnɔ ka zo'e, Ti ba yehe kalam, tintɔnɔ en ka zo'e la zuo.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Ba pu'uhi mam la yoo, Ba pa'ali la asaala yɛla gee yeti Yinɛ pa'alegɔ. .

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Ti Yezu ze'ele Galilii wa'am Yɔn zi'an Yɔɔdan kulaa la puan na, ti Yɔn sɔ en ko'om.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Deni ya sɛba en bi'ɛ kahegɛ woo ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha bi'ɛ kahegɛ se'em la.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala dayua la n de vo'ohego dabeherɛ la Daana.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Gee ya yuum kɛ̃ muuen la ha ti ya ta bihɛ la beni? Nɛra en bagɛ ka mɛŋa la banaahi? Bihɛ ya, sɛba en bagerɛ banaahi zi' la na'am yiya.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: Ban yiregɛ nɛreba la ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu kɛ̃ gurɛ bia la nu'o ti ka ihege.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Ti Pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma wa yeti, Aayi, naa, Daana, mam ka nari fum wa'am mam zɔŋɔ wa puan na, yeen tɔgɛ yelefum la, gee n yameŋa wa wan iŋɛ sum.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Gee wuntɛɛŋa en ŋmɛ na la, ba zɛkɛ mɛ, ba nyɛga en ka sige la zuo, ba ki mɛ.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Ti Yezu yi' nɛrekuuŋɔ la paaɛ a nɛŋan na yele ba yeti, Kelehe ya, la ya bɔkɛ.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Ti Yɔn ka sakɛ gee yeti: Fum tugum nari ni ti fum sɔ mam, ti fum tugum yɛ'a mam zi'an na.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Ti Yezu bahɛ bilam gee kɛ̃ ba zamehegɔ yire la puan.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Ti yele wa to'e tiŋa la zi'an woo.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Mam boi la kpɛ'ɛnduma nu'uhen, ti pɛntiireba me bɔna mam me nu'uhen. Mam han yele ayima yeti, Kiŋɛ, a ni kiŋɛ mɛ. N han le yele ayima me yeti, Wa'ana, a ni wa'am me na. N ta'am yele n da'aberɛ yeti, Iŋɛ ŋwana, ti a iŋɛ.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Gee ya yuum tugum yehe ti ya bihɛ la beni? Nɔtɔgeha? Ɛ̃ɛ, en yeti ya me wa, a gani nɔtɔgeha.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Dagi sɛla en ki'ire nɛra nuuren en galeni nɛra, gee sɛla en ze'eti a nuuren na.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Ti Bumbuura la basɛba zɔgɛ gu'uhi puan, ti gu'uhi la zo'e na kpãrɛ ba.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Gee Yezu lerege en yeti, Bahɛ ti la dɛna bala nananawa. La dela ma'a bo' tu, ti tu doohe ŋwana tum sɛla wuu tuuntɔɔ. Ti Yɔn sakɛ.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Bihɛ, ti buraa bɔna bini ti ka nu'o ki. Ti ba soke Yezu yeti, La de ma'a, ti ba tibera vo'ohego dabeherɛ yo'? Ba soke ŋwana la ba ta'am zɛregɛ en.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Ti Yezu ze'ele bilam yɛ'a, ti fɔɔhi siyi dɔla en kaaha vɔɔla yeti, Zɔ tu nimbu'ɔ Deviti dayua wa.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yezu en kelehe en ba'ahɛ la, la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ ti a yele sɛba en dole en la yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, Izirale tiŋa wa puan mam ka nyɛ nɛra ti a nam ŋwana.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Nɛre kana en bala ti ba gulehɛ a yele yeti, Bihɛ ya, en tuni la n bituneŋa ti ka diŋɛ fum nɛŋa, Se'em en wan maalɛ fum sore biŋe fum.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Pooren ti a zahereba la paaɛ en, yele en yeti, Fum mi ti la daam Farasiiduma la mɛ, ban wum fum yele se'em la.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ti Bumbuura la basɛba me zɔgɛ pu'uhegɔ zi'an yagum zuto, basɛba yagum zuto kɔbega kɔbega, ti basɛba yagum zuto piyoobe piyoobe, ti basɛba me yagum zuto pihitã pihitã.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Ti en sɔ Yezu ko'om ba'ahɛ la, ti a ze'ele ko'om la puan kɔ'ɔm dɔɔra na la, a yeti a bihɛ ti saazuo en yu'ɛ bo' en, ti a nyɛ ti Yinɛ Sia sigera a zuo na ana wuu dayene la, wa'am na yagelɛ a zuo.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Han deni yamam ayima en tari ni pehego kuyina ti ku lu bulega puan vo'ohego dabeherɛ la, ya kan yaaɛ ni kɔ?.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: En ta kɛ̃ yire la puan la, fɔɔhi siyi wa doohe paaɛ a zi'an mɛ, ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Yamam tari sakerɛ ti mam wan ta'am tibɛ ya? Ti ba lerege yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ Daana.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: N yele ya ti, nɛreba kekeko wan ze'ele dapɔya la zanɔya bɔba wa'am na di la Aberaham, Aziki, la Yakɔbe, saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: En yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti asaala woo ti pɔka dɔgɛ, se'em ka boi ihege na tabelɛ tara zuo gana Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra wa. Gee la bala za'a, se'em en pɔ'ɛ saazuo sɔ'ɔlum puan la gani en.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Tisɛka ti mam Sɔ en boi saazuo la ka sɛ wan vuuɛ.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Bahɛ ya ti se'em woo en tari tuba ti a wuna, a kɛleha.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Ti kua ze'ele saazuo tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Mam mɛŋa Dayua n ŋwana, se'em ti n nɔŋɛ, se'em ti n nini tigɛ la en.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Asaala ka gani pehego? Bala zuo, la de ma'a ti nɛra tum sum vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Ti Yezu kalum ba nini la gee yeti, Ya sakerɛ en de se'em la, la tum bala mɛa bo' ya.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: gee ti sɛba en wan sooe ni sɔ'ɔlum la, ba lobe ba bahɛ yiŋa lika la puan ti kaaha gee ɔbera gi'ira nyɛna.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Kɔ'ɔm ze'ele Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra saŋa la wa'am na paaɛ zina, saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la kɔ'ɔm bɔna la gbiregeri puan. Ti sɛba en pɛregɛ la en nyaŋɛ bɔ.

eng: Leave the Pharisees alone. They are blind people who are guides to blind people. But if a blind person leads another blind person, they will both fall into a ditch.
local: Bahɛ ya ba ba ma'a, ba dela fɔɔhi en pa'ala fɔɔhi. Fua han data ka tadaana, ba za'a wan lu boko puan.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Pooren ti a zahereba la wa'am a zi'an na soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum tara mageha tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛreba.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Gee nyaa yele buraa la yeti, Tiɛ fum nu'o la. Ti buraa la tiɛ ka nu'o la, ti ku iŋɛ sum, kɔ'ɔm ana wuu nu'o kuna la.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Ti ba nini la pikɛ, ti Yezu kagɛ ba yeti, Da bahɛ ya ti nɛra baŋɛ yele wa.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la yeti, Kula, la wan dɛna bala bɔ'ɔra hɔ, wuu fum n sakɛ se'em la. Ti yameŋa la tuntuna nyɛ a tibegɔ hankaŋa mɛa puan.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Gee Pita yele en yeti, Garehɛ makerɛ wa bo' tu.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Nɔtɔgeheba la nɔze'elego la za'a purɛ la bala ti Yɔn ta wa'am na.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Han dɛna yamam, ba bo' yamam mɛ ti ya bɔkera saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la yele sɛ'a en sugɛ, gee ba ka bo' bamam bala.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Gee Farasiiduma la yehe mɛ ta ku nuurɛ la taaba ti ba bihɛ ban wan iŋɛ se'em ku en.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Gee ban yehe la, ba kɔ'ɔm dikɛ Yezu yele mɛ sãɛ tiŋa la za'a.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Yezu en kɛ̃ Pita yire la, a nyɛ ti Pita dɛɛmpɔka n gã' suŋɔ zuo gbi'ira la ɔɔrɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Ti a yeti, Yamam me kelum ka tara bɔkerɛ?.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Ya han wan sakɛ, eŋa en bala en de Eliya ti ba yuum purɛ ti a wan lebe na la.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em en tari la, ba wan bo' en pa'ahɛ ti en malum tara gaŋɛ. Gee se'em me en ka tari sɛla la, La fii wa ti ba tara la, ba wan to'e la a zi'an.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Yezu en ta baŋɛ dina wa la, a bahɛ bilam mɛ, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ doohe a poore, ti a tibɛ ba za'a.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: La ka yuue, ban yehe ba'ahɛ la, ti ba tari buraa ti kulekpa'bɛ'a dɔla ka, ti ka ka tana tɔgera wa'am Yezu zi'an na.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: ti a kalum a nu'o, ti ɔɔrɔ la bahɛ en, ti a nyɛ paŋa ihege bo' en ko'om.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Yamam ka nyɛti ti sɛla woo en ni doohe nuurɛ puan kɛ̃ ni sige la puuren ta tole yehe?.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha!.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Se'ere zuo ti mam tara mageha tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba la dela, Ba biheri, gee ba ka nyɛti, ba kɛlehi, gee ba wuni, bii ba bɔkera.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: gee ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ ti, ba da bahɛ ti nɛreba baŋɛ en.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Ti Yezu sa'ɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a la, ti gɔɔŋɔ la nyaa pɔhɛ tɔgera, ti la iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Ti ba yeti, Tu ka tabelɛ nyɛ yele dina wa taaba Izirale tiŋa wa.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Yu'ukuŋɔ mɛa ti nɛreba tari nɛreba kekeko ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla ba wa'am na, ti a sa'ahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la, la yetɔgeyimɔ ma'a, gee tibɛ sɛba woo en bɛ̃'ɛri.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Gee sɛla en yeheri nuuren na, ze'eti la suure puan na. Dina en galeni nɛra.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Gee beni ti en wan dikɛ yamam zina beere nɛreba wa makɛ? Ya ani woo kɔma en zi' da'a yi'ira taaba yeti.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Bana puan ti Azaaya yelefum la wa'am asira na. Ya sirum wan kɛleha gee ya kan malum bɔkɛ, ya me sirum wan biha gee ya kan malum nyɛ.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: Dina wa dela, nɔtɔgeha Azaaya en yuum purɛ se'em la wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Gee Farasiiduma la yeti, Yezu tari la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi naba paŋa sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: Dina wa tum ti nɔtɔgeha Azaaya en purɛ se'em en wa'am sira na. A yeti, Eŋa mɛŋa en to'e tumam tɔregɔ la zi tumam bã'ahi.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, suure la puan ti be'em ti'ihegɔ, nɛrekua, kabohi, yaluntuuma, nageyigum, aze' nyɛ daana pumparema, nɛreba tua zɛ'ɛta na.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: Tumam peebe la yia bahɛ ya, gee ya ka wa; ti tu kaahɛ, gee ya ka kele kuunɔ.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛrebana wa puti'ira ba' ki mɛ, ti ba tuba kpa' ka wuna, ti ba kɔ'ɔm mum ba nini, ti ba da bihɛ la ba nini la, gee kɛleha la ba tuba la, gee bɔkera la ba suya, la ba ŋmɛregɛ lebena, ti en tibɛ ba.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Bihɛ, mam yameŋa en ŋwana, se'em ti mam looe, Se'em ti mam suure nɔŋɛ, se'em en tigeri mam nini. N wan dikɛ n Sia iŋɛ a puan; a wan muulɛ buurɔ bo' sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la za'a.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Ti Yezu dɔla tinkãra la timbibehi za'a pa'ala ba zamehegɔ yiya la puan, muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum faarɛ yelefum la, gee tibera bã'ahi la teem yima yima.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Yezu en nyɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la bibigere na la, ti a bo' a zahereba nuurɛ yeti, bahɛ ya ti tu yakɛ yakenɛ deyima la ha.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Bana en galeni nɛra, gee a ka pee nu'uhi gee dita ka galeni nɛra.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yɔn yuum wa'am na ka dita ka nyuura ti ba yeti Kulekpa'bɛ'a en dole en.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Gee sum boi yamam nini puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, i nyɛti, ti sum bɔna ya tuba puan, se'ere en sɔi la, a wuni.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: A kan zabera bii a tahera, bii nɛra wum a yele sadɔtɔ zuo.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: En nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la, ba nimbu'ɔ en nyɔkɛ en. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ba' bɔna daaŋɔ puan, yiregɛ wuu piihi en ka tari daana la.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Bilam ti gɔnmina ayima paaɛ a zi'an na yele en yeti, Pa'ala, mam wan doohe fum kiŋɛ zi'an woo ti fum yɛ'a.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Ti Yezu bahɛ bilam, gee kiŋɛ Taai la Sidɔn sɔ'ɔlum puan.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Ti Asaala Dayua wa'am na dita gee nyuura, ti ba yeti, Bihɛ ya, a dela puya'an daana la danyuura. A dela kubetɔ'ɔhereba la be'emduma zɔ. Gee la bala za'a, Yɛm daana tuuma en bo'ori en buurɔ.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta ya wa, nɔtɔgeheba, la nɛretuntɔɔ duma zo'e zo'e yuum boti ba nyɛ yamam en nyɛ sɛla wa mɛ, gee ka nyɛ la, gee bɔta ti ba wum yamam en wum sɛla wa, gee ka wum.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: Muɔ seko en kɔ'ɛ nyure, a kan kikɛ kɔ bahɛ. Bii a pebele bugum n diti bahɛ. Gura ti yelemɛŋerɛ ta sooe.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, Hmm! Buŋa la zo'e mɛ, gee tuntuneba la n pɔ'ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Yezu lebehe en yeti, Piini tari yogero mɛ kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra, ti niihe tara tugerɔ me kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra, gee Asaala Dayua wa ka tari zi'an ti a wan dikɛ a zuo kugelɛ.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Bilam bilam ti Keenan pɔka en ze'ele tinkaŋa mɛa puan yehe na vɔɔla baha na yeti, Zɔ en nimbu'ɔ, Daana, Deviti dayua wa, en poyua ti kulekpa'bɛ'a tara ka mɛleha.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ zɛregera tinse'ehi ti a paŋa tuuma tum si puan zo'e, beni zuo ti nɛreba la zagehɛ teere la.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Wum ya buta la makerɛ vuurɛ.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: A yu'urɛ puan ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma me wan tara beere puti'irɛ.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Bala, suhɛ ya va'am Daana la, ti a tum tuntuneba a buŋa la puan na.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ti zahereba a yima yele en yeti, Daana, yia bahɛ ti en lebe laaɛ n sɔ ba'ahɛ gee wa'am na doohe fum.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Gee Yezu ka lerege en se'ere. Ti a zahereba la ɛbegɛ en yeti, Sa'ɛ en ti a kiŋɛ gee a ba' vɔɔla mɛ dɔla tɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: Yele boi yamam Korazinduma zuo! Yele boi yamam Bɛtesaadaduma zuo! Se'ere en sɔi la, paŋa tuuma ana taaba wa han tum ni la Taai la Sidɔn tihi puan, ŋwana ma'a ba tee ni tole tole, gee yɛ susageŋɔ futo, vaaɛ tampɛgɛlum yeele ba mɛhi, pa'ali ti ba tee ya.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Se'em woo en ni wum sɔ'ɔlum la yɛla gee ka bɔkɛ a, Sagena la ni wa'am mɛ na zɛ sɛla ti ba burɛ a suuren la. Bana en de bumbuura sɛ'a en zɔgɛ sore zuo la.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Ti ba tari buraa ti kulekpa'bɛ'a dɔla ka, ti ka dɛna fua la gɔɔŋɔ wa'am Yezu zi'an na ti a tibɛ ka, ti buraa wa en de gɔŋɔ wa tana tɔgera gee nyɛta.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, Dɔla mam gee bahɛ ti kum duma pi' ba kum.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Ba tum mam la Izirale piihi se'ehi en bɔregɛ la ma'a zi'an na.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Gee en yeti ya mɛ wa ti, gakerɛ daarɛ, la wan ana ma'ma' bo' Taai la Sidɔn tihi gana yamam.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a pakerɛ zo'e zo'e, ti ba yɛta yeti, Nɛra wa wan ta'am dɛna Deviti dayua.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Han dɛna sɛba en zɔgɛ tahi zi'an la, dela se'em en ni wum yelefum la, to'e bu bilam bilam la nyɛɛma.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Yezu en zom uureŋɔ la, a zahereba la doohe en mɛ.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Gee pɔka wa wa'am me na kpa' duma a nɛŋan, yɛta yeti, Daana suŋɛ mam.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Yamam Kapɛnumduma wa, Yamam ti'ihɛ ti ya wan kɔ'ɔm zɛkɛ ya mɛhi mɛ zom saazuo? Aayi, ba wan tari ya na bahɛ la ki'intiŋa. Se'ere en sɔi la, paŋa tuunsɛ'a en tum bo' ya la han tum ni la Sodom tiŋa puan, ka wan boni na paaɛ zina.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Gee Farasiiduma la en wum dina wa la, ba yeti, Beelezebule en de kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi naba la en dole buraa wa ti ka tana sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: gee nɛre-ina daana wa nyɛga ka ba, gee a yeen ŋmibe la fii wa, gee daaŋɔ bii mɛlehegɔ han paaɛ en yelefum wa zuo, a duma ni lu mɛ bilam bilam.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Ti kusebekãtɛ pɔhɛ lɔbera ko'om la puan. Kusebego la paŋa ba' zo'e mɛ ti uureŋɔ la yeti ku muhi, ti Yezu bɔna uureŋɔ la gulengukɔ puan giha.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, La dagi ma'a ti n dikɛ kɔma dia lobe bo' baahi.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Gee en yeti ya mɛ wa, ti gakerɛ daarɛ, la wan ta ana ma'ma' bo' Sodom tiŋa gana yamam.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yezu n baŋɛ ban ti'ihɛ se'em la, ti a yele ba yeti, Sɔ'ɔlum sebo en welege zabera la bu mɛŋa wan lebege daboo. Tiŋa bii Yire me ka boi welege zabera la de mɛŋa gee wan ta'am ze'ele.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Han dɛna sɛba en zɔgɛ gu'uhi puan la, eŋa dela se'em en wum yelefum la, gee ti tiŋa wa zuo yɛla nyɛlum la tarega pa'ahɛ la ni gu' yelefum la ti bu ka ta'am wam sɛla.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Ti ba paaɛ niiɛ en yeti, Daana, faaɛ tɔ, tu kiiri mɛ.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Ti Pɔka la yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ n Daana, gee baahi la diti di zɔra la en zɔgeri si duma dia zi'an la mɛ.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Sankana puan, ti Yezu tɔgɛ yeti, Mam bo' n Sɔ saazuo la tiŋa za'a Daana, fara fara se'ere zuo, fum dikɛ yele ana sugɛ yɛm la mi'ilum duma mɛ, gee tugum lilege a bo' kɔmbilɛɛhi.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Bala, Sagena la han tulege sa'aha Sagena, a welege mɛ zabera la a mɛŋa. A sɔ'ɔlum la yeti bu iŋɛ la ŋwani ze'ele?.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Gee bumbuura sɛ'a ti ba burɛ pu'uhegɔ zi'an la, bamam en de sɛba en wum yelefum la gee bɔkɛ bɔ, bana en de sɛba en wam biili zo'e zo'e, ti sɛba yagum kɔbega kɔbega, ti sɛba me yagum piyoobe piyoobe, ti basɛba me yagum pihitã pihitã.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya tara dabeem bala? Ya sakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya. Gee nyaa ihege tahɛ kusebego la, la ateko la ti komi'ihi ma'ɛ suram.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Bilam ti Yezu lerege pɔka wa yeti, Pɔka wa, fum sakerɛ zo'e ya, la iŋɛ bo' fum wuu fum boti se'em la. Ti pɔka wa poyua la nyɛ ka tibegɔ bilam bilam.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Ɛ̃ɛ n Sɔ, fum zu'uhegɔ suboolum en pun daa dɛna se'em n bala.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Yezu le dikɛ la makerɛ deyima biŋe ba yeti, Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la wan dikɛ makɛ mɛ la nɛra en burɛ a bumbuura suma a va'am puan.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛreba la za'a pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba yɛta yeti, Beni buraa en ŋwana ti kusebego la ateko pun ta wuna a tugum.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Yezu en ta bahɛ bilam la, a kiŋɛ doohe la Galilii ateko la, ta zom zoore zuo zi'ire bilam.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Mam Sɔ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la mam nu'uhen. Nɛra ka mi' dayua la, han dagena Sɔ la. Nɛra me ka mi' Sɔ la, han dagena dayua la, la se'em ti dayua la bɔta ti a lilege Sɔ la pa'alɛ en.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Gee la han dɛna la Yinɛ Sia la ti mam tara sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yi paaɛ ya mɛ me na n bala.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Gee yu'uŋɔ ti nɛrawoo ta giha la, ti dindaana kiŋɛ ta lobege mɔyileŋo pa'ahɛ ki la puan gee kiŋɛ.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Yezu en paaɛ Gadara en boi ateko la yakenɛ deyima ha la, ti buraahi siyi ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla si, ze'ele yɔɔrɔ la zi'an yɛhera na, tu'uhɛ en. Buraahi wa ba' daana mɛ ti nɛra ka dɔla bilam bɔba tɔla.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ kina a zi'an na, tara wɔbehi, kɔɔrenɔ, fɔɔhi, gɔɔnɔ, la basɛba zo'e zo'e, tari ba na gã'arɛ Yezu nɛŋan, ti a me tibɛ ba.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Wa'am ya en zi'an na, yamam sɛba woo n taregɛ gee zɛ zeero, n wan bo' ya vo'ohego.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Bii nɛra wan iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am kɔ' kɛ̃ paŋa daana yire fã a lɔgerɔ, gee ka lu' paŋa daana la biŋe yia? Bala ti a nyaa wan ta'am fã yire la suŋa suŋa.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Bumbuura la en ta zo'e ita nini la, ti mɔyileŋo la me ihege na, lagum zɛ'a la ba.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba pɔhɛ nyabera yeti, Beni ti fum tari la tɔ Yinɛ dayua wa? Fum yeti fum pɔgelum tu mɛ gee ti saŋa la mɛŋa mɛŋa la nan ka paaɛ.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, la ban nyɛ gɔɔnɔ en tɔgeri, ti kɔɔrenɔ kina, ti wɔbehi kina, ti fɔɔhi nyɛta. Ti ba pɛgɛra Izirale Yinɛ la.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Dikɛ ya mam yɔka la yɔgɛ ya mɛhi, gee zamehɛ mam zi'an, Se'ere en sɔi la, mam dela nɛre ma'ma', gee sigehe n mɛŋa n suure puan. Ya wan nyɛ vo'ohego bo' ya sɛɛhi.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Se'em en ka suni la mam zaberɛ mɛ la mam, Se'em me en ka ka'aheri bɔ'ɔra mam, yiregerɛ bahera mɛ.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Ti yidaana la yamehi wa'am na yele en yeti, Daana, fum ka burɛ la bumbuura suma va'am la puan? Gee mɔyileŋo la ze'ele la bɛ na.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Kurikuri zo'e zo'e en ka zaaɛ zɛ'a bilam ɔbera.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Pooren ti Yezu yi' a zahereba la paaɛ a nɛŋan yeti, Nɛreba wa tari mam la nimbaalega, se'ere en sɔi la, ba boi la mam la dabeha atã en ŋwana gee ka tara sɛla wan di. N ka boti n bahɛ ba ti ba kule la kom. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ta'am ta lige sore.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Mam yɔka la dela naana, ti n zeero la fakɛ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Bala zuo, mam yeti, nɛreba wan nyɛ sugeri dia be'em la zɛɛhi woo puan, gee se'em woo en zeem Fa'a Sia la kan nyɛ sugeri dia.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Dima en tum de bala. Ti yamehi la yele en yeti, Fum boti tu kiŋɛ ta vɔ mɔyileŋo la bahɛ.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: Ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la belum en yeti, Fum han sa'ara tɔ, bahɛ ti tu kɛ̃ kurikuri wa puan.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Ti a zahereba yele en yeti, Bɛha ti tu wan nyɛ dia zo'e muɔ wa puan diihe nɛrekuuŋɔ kuna taaba wa.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Se'em woo en tɔgɛ se'ere pɔ'ɛ Asaala Dayua wa, wan nyɛ sugeri dia, gee se'em woo en tɔgɛ se'ere pɔ'ɛ Fa'a Sia la kan malum nyɛ sugeri dia, zina bii beere.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Aayi, ya han ta vuura mɔyileŋo la, ki la wan lagum doohe.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Boroboro ba lɛ ti ya tara? Ti ba yeti, Ba yopɔi la zim fii wa.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ti Yezu bo' ba nuurɛ yeti Kini ya! Ti ba yehe na kɛ̃ kurikuri la puan, bilam bilam ti kurikuri la za'a zɔ gbiri gbiri zulege zoore la ta lu ko'om la puan ki.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Bihɛ tia suŋa gee ka wala me wan ani suŋa. Bii da bihɛ tia suŋa gee ka wala me ka ana suŋa. Se'ere en sɔi la, tia wala en pa'alɛ kan de tisɛka.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ba za'a lagum zɔ'ɔra gura buŋa saŋa. Buŋa saŋa la, en wan ta yele buneba la yeti, Kɛ̃ ya mɔyileŋo la yia bobe ba bobene bobene ba nyɔ', gee nyaa bum dia la iŋɛ en bããren. .

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Ti Yezu bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ la nuurɛ ti ba zi'ire tiŋa.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Ti dunkiima la zɔ kalam paaɛ tiŋa la puan yele sɛla woo en iŋɛ buraahi la ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla si la.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Yamam kampɛbibehi wa, yamam yeti ya iŋɛ la ŋwani tɔgera yelesuma, gee dɛna be'em duma wa? Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla en boi suuren zo'e zo'e la ti nuurɛ tɔgera.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Ti bilam bilam tiŋa la nɛreba za'a wa'am na ti ba bihɛ Yezu. Ban nyɛ en la, ba belum en mɛ ti a bahɛ bamam tiŋa ko'om la nuuren la.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Ti a to'e boroboro bayopɔi la la zim la. En bo' fara ba'ahɛ la, a fige boroboro la mɛ bo' a zahereba, ti bamam me dikɛ tɔrɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Ti a le dikɛ makerɛ deyima biŋe ba yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la ani wuu nangembiileho ti nɛra burɛ a va'am puan.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Nɛresuma yeheri la yelesuma sɛ'a en boi a lɔgesunɔ la puan, ti nɛrebɛ'a me, yeheri yeletoseto en boi a lɔge tooro la puan la.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ti ba za'a di ta tigɛ, ti a zahereba la doohe lagehɛ boroboro zɔra la ti ba tigɛ gee bahɛ la, pirɛ pitɔ tuyopɔi.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Biiliho buna wa pɔ'ɛ mɛ biila wuu puan, gee bu han yehe na, bu ni dɛna la tuntuo kãtɛ, nyaa ta lebege tia, ti niihe nyaa ni wa'am na tɛ' ba tugerɔ ka zuo.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: Mam yeti ya ti, gakerɛ daarɛ, nɛrawoo wan yele yetɔgeyooro seto ti ba tɔgɛ la ba nɔya.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Sɛba en di la dela buraahi tuha anaahi, ti kɔma la pɔgehi kãlɛ ka pa'ahɛ.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Ti Yezu le yele ba makerɛ deyima yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la ani me wuu dabelɛ fii ti pɔka dikɛ gɛregɛ wama atã zom, ti bu bahɛ ti zom la za'a ihege se'em la.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Fum tugum en wan ta bo' fum buurɔ, fum tugum en wan ta sagum hɔ.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: En keehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la ba'ahɛ la, a zom la uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Magadan tiŋa.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Yezu yele nɛrekuuŋɔ la yele ana za'a la mageha puan, a ka tɔgɛ se'ere bo' ba ka dikɛ doohe mageha puan.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma yele en yeti, Pa'ala, tu boti tu nyɛ la daalehum fum zi'an.
