eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: Dina iŋɛ ti nɔtɔgeha la purega la wa'am asira na. A yuum purɛ yeti, Mam wan ya'ɛ en nuurɛ tɔgɛ la mageha, N wan muulɛ sɛla en sugɛ tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo na.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Gee Yezu lebehe ba yeti, Be'em la baga kaabegɔ buuri saŋa en eere daalehum, gee daalehum ka boi wan bo' ba, han dagena nɔtɔgeha Yona daalehum la.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Ti Yezu bahɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la gee kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ yise'ere ti a kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la. Ti a zahereba la paaɛ soke en yeti, Garehɛ bu tɔ va'am puan mɔyileŋo makerɛ la vuurɛ.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Wuu Yona en yuum boi ko'om bunsɛla la puuren dabeha atã wuntɛɛŋa la yu'uŋɔ se'em la, bala mɛa ti Asaala dayua wa wan ta bɔna tiŋa wa puan ha dabeha atã, wuntɛɛŋa la yu'uŋɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Se'em en burɛ bumbuulesuma la dela Asaala Dayua la.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Ninevɛ tiŋa nɛreba gakerɛ daarɛ la, wan ta ihege la zina beere nɛreba wa, tɔgɛ lubɛ ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'ere en sɔi la, ban yuum wum Yona muulegɔ la, ba kɔ'ɔm tee ba vom mɛ. Nyaa bihɛ ya, se'em en gani Yona n ŋwana.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Va'am la dela timpaberɛ wa, ti bumbuulesuma la dɛna sɔ'ɔlum la kɔma, ti mɔyileŋo la dɛna Sagena la kɔma.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Tiŋanuurɛ pɔgenaba la, gakerɛ daarɛ wan ta ihege la zina beere nɛreba wa tɔgɛ lubɛ ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum ze'ele la zi'an en zaaɛ wa'am na ti a kelehe Solomɔn yɛm la. Nyaa Bihɛ ya, se'em en gani Solomɔn n ŋwana.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: dima la en burɛ mɔyileŋo la dela Sagena la, ti buŋa la me dɛna timpaberɛ wa ba'ahegɔ saŋa, ti buneba la dɛna Yinɛ suŋereba.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Sɛfi'iha han ni yehe nɛra puan, ka ni ɛɛra tuuna la kpaahenɛ puan, ɛɛra vo'ohego zi'an, gee ka nyɛ.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Wuu mɔyileŋo la ti ba lagehɛ nyɔ' se'em la bugum la, la wan ta ana la bala tiŋa wa ba'ahegɔ saŋa.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: A ni yeti, N wan lebe la en yikɛka la ti n ze'ele de puan na la. A han paaɛ na, a nyɛ ti yire la ze' la voore, piihɛ, ti sɛla woo ana suŋa.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Asaala dayua la wan ta tum a suŋereba na, ti ba doohe a sɔ'ɔlum la puan lagehɛ sɛla woo en baheri ti nɛreba tuna be'em, la tuumbe'ero duma la za'a yehe a sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: A nyaa ni lebe mɛ ta tari Sɛfi'iha siyopɔi se'ehi tuuma n fi'ɛ gana eŋa, ti ba wa'am na kɛ̃. Ti nɛra kaŋa ba'ahegɔ nyaa ni ba' sagum mɛ gana a yia la. Bala mɛa ti la wan ana bo' zina beere be'em nɛreba wa.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: nyaa tari ba ta lobe bahɛ bugum peelum la puan. Bilam ti ba wan kaaha gee ɔbera nyɛna.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yezu en kelum bɔna bini tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛrekuuŋɔ la, bihɛ, a ma la a suurɔ wa'am na zɛ'a yiŋa ti ba tɔgɛ la en.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Gee tuntɔɔduma la wan ta nyakera mɛ wuu wuntɛɛŋa ba Sɔ sɔ'ɔlum la puan. Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba ti a wuna, kɛleha.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Ti Nɛra ayima yele en yeti, Bihɛ, fum ma la fum suurɔ ze' yiŋa bɔta ti ba tɔgɛ bo' fum.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la ani mɛ wuu tuulum lɔkɔ en sugɛ va'am puan, ti nɛra ayima ta nyɛ kɔ malum sugɛ kɔ, gee nyaa nyɛɛna lɛbera, ta paaɛ vaaɛ sɛla woo ti a tara ta koohe ta da' zi'an la.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Gee se'em en yele en ŋwana wa la, Yezu lebe en yeti, Mam ma dela ani, ti en suurɔ dɛna bani.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la ani wuu lɛɛba en eeri tuulum nunɔm lɔgesunɔ.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Gee tiɛ nu'o pa'ala a zahereba la, gee yeti, Bihɛ, Mam ma la mam suurɔ n ŋwana.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: En ta nyɛ tuulum lɔkɔ kuyima en ba' ana suŋa la, a kiŋɛ mɛ ta koohe sɛla woo ti a tara, wa'am na da' kɔ.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en tuni mam sɔ n boi saazuo ha sumboolum, nɛre eŋa en de mam suuɔ, mam taya, la mam ma.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la ani mɛ wuu yuka ti ba dikɛ lobe mɔgerɛ puan nyɔgɛ zim buuri buuri la.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Yuka la en ta pirɛ la, ba ve'e ka mɛ dooe doone na, nyaa zi'ire gahɛ zim suma la iŋɛ ba pitɔ puan, gee lobe bumbe'ero la bahɛ.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Ti Fa'a Sia la nyaa tari Yezu kiŋɛ muuen ha, ti Sagena la makɛ en.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: Lan wan ta ana se'em ba'ahegɔ saŋa la en bala. Yinɛ suŋereba la wan ta wa'am na, yelehe be'emduma la tuntɔɔduma la puan.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Ti Yezu zagehɛ a mɛŋa dabeha pihinaahi wuntɛɛŋa la yu'uŋɔ. Ti kom nyaa tuura en.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: lobe ba bahɛ bugum peelum la puan, zi'an ti ba wan ta kaaha gee ɔbera nyɛna.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Ti pumpã'afum daana la wa'am a zi'an na yele en yeti, Fum han dɛna la Yinɛ bia, bo' kuga wa nuurɛ ti ba lebege boroboro.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Yamam bɔkɛ yele ana wa za'a? Ti ba lerege yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Gee Yezu lebehe en yeti, La gulehɛ yeti, Asaala ka vɔi la boroboro ma'a, gee yelefum sebo woo en ze'eti Yinɛ nuuren na. .

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Bala zuo, gɔnmina se'em woo en zamehɛ mina saazuo sɔ'ɔlum wa yɛla ani wuu Yidaana en gbɛ'ɛri lɔgepaala la lɔgekɛgehi a lɔgerɔ puan yɛhera na la.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Naba Hɛrɔd saŋa puan, ban wa dɔgɛ Yezu Bɛtelehɛm en boi Yudiya puan ba'ahɛ la, bihɛ ya, baŋereba duma ze'ele dapɔya bɔba ha wa'am Yerusalɛm na.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Ti Sagena la tari Yezu kiŋɛ tuulum tiŋa la puan puan, ta tari en zom pu'uhegɔ yire la zuin zuin ha ta yagelɛ mɔpile nyu'a zuo.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Yezu en ba'ahɛ mageha ana wa la, a bahɛ bilam mɛ.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Sankaŋa puan, ti Hɛrɔd en de timbihera la wum Yezu yele mɛ.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: sɔkera yeti, Bɛha ti ba dɔgɛ se'em en de Yuuduma naba la? Beni zuo, tu nyɛ la ka ŋmarebila dapɔya ha, wa'am na ti tu na'ahɛ en.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: gee yele en yeti, Fum han dɛna Yinɛ bia, ze'ele ka'lam lobe fum mɛŋa bahɛ tiŋa ha. Gee la gulehɛ yeti, Yinɛ wan bo' a suŋereba nuurɛ fum zuo, ti ba kɔkɛ hɔ ba nu'uhen, ti fum na'arɛ kan ŋmɛ' kugere. .

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Ti Yezu wa'am a mɛŋa tiŋa na, pɔhɛ pa'ala nɛreba zamehegɔ yiya la puan ti la iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ zo'e zo'e. Ti ba yɛta yeti, Burakana nyɛ yɛm buna wa la paŋa tuun-ana wa la bɛ?.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: nyaa a yele a tuntuneba yeti, Ena wa dela Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra la. A ihege mɛ a kum la puan. Ŋwana zuo ti pansina wa tuna a puan la.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Da gakɛ, ti ba mɛ da gakɛ hɔ.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Mini ya ti ya wan ita ya itesuŋɔ nɛreba nɛŋan ti ba nyɛta ya. La han ana bala, ya ka le tara yɔɔrɛ ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha la zi'an.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Naba Hɛrɔd en wum ŋwana la, dabeem n kɛ en paaɛ paaɛ, la Yerusalɛm nɛreba za'a;.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, Le yɛ'ɛha, la gulehɛ yeti, Da makɛ Daana la en de fum Yinɛ la bihɛ. .

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Dagi ena en de kpa'ara la dayua la? Dagi a ma yu'urɛ n yi'iri Mɛɛri la? Dagi a suurɔ en de Yemesi, Yosefe, Simɔn la Yudasi?.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Hɛrɔd pugum nyɔkɛ Yɔn mɛ, ba' en, dikɛ en iŋɛ paka deo puan, doohe Hɛrɔdiya en de Hɛrɔd suuɔ Filipi pɔga la zuo.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum n gakeri se'em la, ba wan gakɛ fum la bala. Fum n makere se'em tiha la, bala mɛa tu fum wan lebehe to'e.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Bala zuo, ya han ni bɔ'ɔra bo'olum, da ŋmɛ' ya luŋa wuu birekɔgenduma la en iti se'em ba zamehegɔ yiya la sadɔtɔ zuto ti nɛreba pɛgɛ ba la. Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, bamam pun to'e ba yɔɔrɛ mɛ.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: ti a yi' malema kiinduma la za'a, la nɛreba la gɔnmi'ineba lagehɛ na viihɛ ba zi'an ti ba wan dɔgɛ Kiresta la.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha ti Sagena la tari Yezu ta zom zoore en wugɛ paaɛ paaɛ zuo, pa'alɛ en sɔ'ɔlum duma woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, la ba suhɛ;.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Dagi a suurɔ za'a en naaɛ la tɔ kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra wa? Bɛha, ti buraa kana wa nyɛ yele ana za'a.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yɔn yeti Hɛrɔd mɛ saŋa woo ti, La kihi mɛ ti a fã a suuɔ pɔga di.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ŋwani ti fum nyɛta sɔkɔ la n boi fum mabia niho puan, gee pun ka mina ti dɔɔ boi fum mɛŋa niho puan?.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Gee ya han ni bɔ'ɔra bo'olum, da bahɛ ti ya gɔbega nu'o mina zuɔ nu'o la en iti sɛla.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Bɛtelehɛm n boi Yudiya puan, Se'ere n sɔi la, nɔtɔgeha la n gulehɛ se'em biŋe n bala:.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: gee yele en yeti, Bana za'a n wan dikɛ bo' fum, fum han wan lu tiŋa na'ahɛ mam.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Ti ba zeem en, ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Nɔtɔgeha tari na'ahegɔ mɛ zi'an woo, han dagena a mɛŋa tiŋa bii a mɛŋa yiya puan ma'a.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: La Hɛrɔd en daa pun bɔta ti a ku Yɔn la, a daa zoti la nɛrekuuŋɔ la dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, bamam nam ti Yɔn dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: Bii ŋwani ti fum wan ta'am yele fum mabia yeti, Bahɛ ti pebele sɔkɔ wa fum niho wa puan bahɛ, gee ti dɔɔ bɔna fum mɛŋa niho la puan?.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Bala ti ya bo'olum la wan sugɛ, ti ya Sɔ la en nyɛti sɛla en sugɛ la wan yɔ ya.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Fum Bɛtelehɛm, n boi Yuda puan wa, kan dɛna timbila Yuda naduma nɛŋan; se'ere n sɔi la, fum puan ti naba wan yehe na; dɛna dunkiina bo' mam nɛreba Iziraleduma. .

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, Fɔregɛ mam zi'an ha, Sagena wa! Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Na'aha Daana la en de fum Yinɛ la, ma'a, gee tuna bɔ'ɔra eŋa ma'a. .

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: Ŋwana zuo, Yezu ka tum daalehum tuuma zo'e bilam, ban ka nam bo' en la zuo.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Gee Hɛrɔd dɔgum daarɛ en wa paaɛ, ti Hɛrɔdiya poyua wa' nɛrekuuŋɔ la nɛŋan, ti Hɛrɔd puurɛ pee paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Yamam birekɔgehi wa, fooe dɔɔ la fum mɛŋa niho la puan yia, la fum ta'am nyɛta suŋa dikɛ sɔkɔ la fum mabia la niho la puan.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Ya ni han yeti ya pu'uhɛ, da ni ani ya wuu birekɔgenduma la. Bama nɔŋɛ la ba ni dooe zɛ'a saazuo zamehegɔ yiya la puan, la sadɔtɔ kãra zi'ihi woo puan, pu'uha ti nɛreba nyɛ ba. N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti ba pun to'e ba yɔɔrɛ mɛ.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Ti Hɛrɔd yi' baŋereba la bããlam ti a baŋɛ ba zi'an, hansɛka mɔpi ti ba nyɛ ŋmarebila la.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Bilam ti Sagena la bahɛ en, ti Yinɛ suŋereba en bala wa'am na kpemehe en.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: ti la paaɛ en, ti a biŋe nuurɛ la pɔtɛ ti a wan bo' en sɛla woo ti a soke en.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Da dikɛ sɛla en de tuulum lɔkɔ bo' baahi. Da me dikɛ fum tuulum nunɔm lɔgerɔ bo' kurikuri, ba wan nɛ ba tabɛ tiŋa, gee ŋmɛregɛ aafum hɔ zɔra zɔra.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Gee ya han ni yeti ya pu'uhɛ, kɛ̃ ya bɔ'ɔ, la ya yu' kuleŋa, gee pu'uhɛ ya Sɔ la en nyɛti sɛla en sugɛ la, ti ya sɔ la en nyɛti sɛla n sugɛ la, wan yɔ ya.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Yezu en ta wum ti ba nyɔkɛ Yɔn pagɛ mɛ la, a yehe mɛ kiŋɛ Galilii.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Gee nyaa tum ba Bɛtelehɛm yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta ɛ bia la ninmu'urɛ, ya ha nyɛ ka, bahɛ ya ti n tuberɛ wum, ti mam me kiŋɛ ta na'ahɛ ka.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Ti pugela la ma ɛbegɛ en ti a yeti, Dikɛ Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra la zuo iŋɛ lapɛrega puan bo' ze'ele ka'lam bo' mam.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Soke, gee ba wan bo' fum. Ɛ gee fum wan nyɛ. Ŋmɛ gee kuleŋa la wan yu'ɛ bo' fum.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Yamam han ni pu'uha, da kɔ'ɔm yeen kɔ'ɔm bebele ya sɛla en ka tari vuurɛ wuu sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ en iti se'em la. Ba ti'ihɛ ti yetɔgum woko la ti Yinɛ wan wum.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: A bahɛ Nazareti mɛ, gee kiŋɛ ta kɛ'ɛra Kapɛnum en lɛm ateko la, bɔna Zebulun la Nafetali sɔ'ɔlum puan la.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Ban wum Naba la sɔhega la ba'ahɛ la, ba yehe mɛ yɛ'a, bihɛ nyɛ ti ŋmarebila n bala, bɔna ba nɛŋa, ŋmarebila sɛka ti ba nyɛ dapɔya yia la, ti ka kina tara tara ta teke zi'an ti bia la bɔna la.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Ti Naba wa suure sagum, gee en diŋɛ biŋe a nuurɛ la, la a saama la en zi' dɛla taaba la zuo, ti a bo' nuurɛ ti ba ta tari ku na.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Se'em woo en soke, wan to'e, ti se'em woo en ɛ, wan nyɛ, ti se'em woo en ŋmɛ', kuleŋa la wan yu'ɛ bo' en.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Da ani ya wuu bamam la, se'ere en sɔi la, ya Sɔ la pun mina yan eere sɛla mɛ, gee ti ya baŋɛ sɔkera en la.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: ti la bahɛ ti Yinɛ nɔtɔgeha Azaaya en purɛ se'em la wa'am asira na.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Ban nyɛ ti ŋmarebila la, ba nyɛɛm la nyɛɛŋɔ en ka tari makerɛ.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Gee nyaa tum ti ba kiŋɛ ta fike Yɔn zuo paka deo la puan na.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Se'em boi ya puan, ti a bia han suhɛ en boroboro, a dikɛ kugere bo' en?.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Bala zuo, ni pu'uheri ya ŋwana: Tu Sɔ en boi saazuo ha, Fum yu'urɛ dɛna tuulum gana sɛla woo.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Zebulun la Nafetali tihi wa en boi ateko sore zuo wa, bɔna Yɔɔdan yakenɛ, Galilii en de sɛba en dagi Yuuduma wa.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Ti ban kɛ̃ yire la puan la, ba nyɛ bia la mɛ la ka ma Mɛɛri. Ti ba kpa' duma na'ahɛ ka, gee nyaa yu'uhɛ ba tuulum lɔgerɔ tapɔtɔ yehe salema, tuulum digelum la sɛla en tari nyuuŋo suŋɔ ti ba yi'ira Mɛɛ bo' ka.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Ti ba tari zuo la, la lapɛrega puan na bo' pugela la, ti ka tari ta bo' ka ma.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Bii bia la suhɛ ziho ti fum bo' en bunsɛla?.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Fum sɔ'ɔlum wa'am na. Fum sumboolum tum, tiŋa wa zuo wuu lan tuni saazuo ha se'em la.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: nɛresɛba n daa zi' lika puan la nyaa nyɛ la niifum kãtɛ, Yamam sɛba nama en daa boi yɔɔen la, niifum nyaa niiɛ bo' ya.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Yinɛ n ka'am ba zaaheŋɔ puan la ninmu'urɛ yeti, ba da lebe Naba Hɛrɔd zi'an la zuo, ban lebere ba mɛŋa tiŋa la, ba tee la sore deyima.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Ti Yɔn zahereba la wa'am na dikɛ a iŋa la ta laaɛ gee kiŋɛ ta yele Yezu.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Ti yamam en de be'em duma, han mina sɛla en ani suŋa bɔ'ɔra ya kɔma, ŋwani ti ya Sɔ se'em en boi saazuo ha wa, kan bo' sɛba woo en soke en la lɔgesunɔ gana bala.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Bo' tu tu zina dabeherɛ dia.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Pɔhɛ saŋa kaŋa, ti Yezu pɔhɛ muula yeti, Tee ya, gee saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la lɛm na.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Baŋereba la n kule la, ti Daana suŋera niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' Yosefe zaaheŋɔ puan yeti, Ihege tari bia la, la ka ma zɔ loŋe Igipiti ta bɔna bini gura se'em ma'a ti n wan ta yele fum ti fum lebe na. Se'ere n sɔi la, Hɛrɔd maahum mɛ ti a ɛ bia la ku ka.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yezu en wum dina la, a bahɛ bilam mɛ gee kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ uureŋɔ kiŋɛ zi'an ti nɛreba ka bɔna bilam a ma'a. Gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la en wum bala la, ba yehe la ba tihi puan kiŋɛ la nama doohe a poore.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Sɛla woo puan, ita nɛreba wuu fum boti nɛreba ita fum se'em la. Nɔze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba la pa'alegɔ en bala.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Gee bahɛ tu sama bo' tu, wuu tumam n baheri tu taaba sama se'em la.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Yezu en eere kaara Galilii kulaa la, a nyɛ la nɛra la a suuɔ. Simɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Pita, la Anduru en de a yibega la, ti ba bahera ba yuka ko'om la puan, se'ere n sɔi la, ba dela zim nyɔgereba.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Ti Yosefe ihege, tari bia la, la ka ma zɔ loŋe Igipiti yu'uŋɔ.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Yezu en ze'ele uureŋɔ la puan sige na la, a nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la mɛ, zɔ ba nimbu'ɔ, gee tibɛ sɛba en wa'am na la nɛreba sɛba n bɛ̃'ɛri la.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Doohe nayaŋa mika la puan kɛ̃. Se'ere en sɔi la, nayaŋa sɛka en dole yɛ'a sageŋɔ puan la yalegɛ mɛ ti ka sore la zuo kinɛ dɛna naana, ti nɛreba kekeko kina de zuo.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Da tari tu kɛ̃ magehegɔ puan gee faaɛ tɔ be'em daana la nu'uhen bahɛ. Sɔ'ɔlum la dela fum dinɛ, paŋa suŋa, wuuha wuuha, Amɛn. Yia yia gulehɛgɔ la tuseto ka tari ka'lam.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Doohe ya mam, ti en iŋɛ ya ti ya tara nɛreba kina n zi'an na.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: ta bɔna bilam tara tara ti Hɛrɔd ta ki. Yele dina iŋɛ ti la doohe la se'ere ti Daana pun tɔgɛ doohe a nɔtɔgeha puan yeti, Igipiti puan na, ti mam yi' n dayua la.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Yinɛ en ta luta la, ti a zahereba la paaɛ a zi'an yele en yeti, Ka'lam dela muɔ puan, gee ti yinɛ me luta, bahɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ wa ti ba yiregɛ kiŋɛ timbibehi puan la ba ta'am da' dia bo' ba mɛhi.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Gee nayaŋa sɛka en dole yɛ'a vom tiŋa la puan la mike mɛ, ti ka sore la zuo kini kpe'em, ti nɛreba fii wa en dole ku zuo.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Ya han bahɛ ya taaba tuure bo' ba, ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha me wan bahɛ ya tuure bo' ya.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba bahɛ ba yugehi la gee doohe en.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, La dagi ba kiŋɛ, yamam bo' ba sɛla ti ba di.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Mini ya nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma la en kini ya zi'an na wa yele. Ba dikɛ la piihi gana furɛ gee ba puan puan ha, ba mɛhi, gee ba dela sahi en obere ti la kiha.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Gee yamam han ka bahɛ ya taaba tuure bo' ba, ya Sɔ la me kan bahɛ ya tuure bo' ya.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: Ti en ze'ele bilam yɛ'a la, a nyɛ nɛra la a suuɔ, Yemesi en de Zebedii dayua la, la a suuɔ Yɔn, ti ba boi uureŋɔ puan la ba sɔ Zebedii maala ba yugehi, ti a yi' ba.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Dina wa tum doohe la se'ere ti Yɛremiya nɔtɔgeha la yuum purɛ yeti.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Tu ka tari sɛla ka'lam, han dagena boroboro banuu la zim i yi' ma'a.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Ya wan baŋɛ ba doohe ba wala puan. Ba pi'ihi ta'ama la zaahi tiŋa, bii ba pi'iha mu'uro tuntuuro tiŋa?.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Ya han luta nuurɛ, da ni zuŋe ya nɛhi wuu birekɔgenduma la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba ni zuŋe ba nɛhi la bala ti ba pa'alɛ la nɛreba ti bamam luti la nuurɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ba pun to'e ba yɔɔrɛ mɛ.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba bahɛ uureŋɔ la, la ba sɔ la, gee doohe en.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Fabelegɔ en ze'eti Arama tiŋa na, kaahi dɔɔrɔ la fabelegɔ n voole baha na. Arakɛle en kele a kɔma beere buuri zuo, a zagehi pa'ahegɔ mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka boi.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Ti Yezu yeti, Tari ya ba na bo' mam ka'lam.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Bala mɛa, tisuŋa woo wani la walesuma, gee ti tibe'o woo me wuna wala pu'uha.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Gee yamam han luta nuurɛ, ni sɔ̃ ya zuto la kaam, gee pee ya nɛhi.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Ti Yezu dɔla Galilii za'a pa'ala nɛreba zamehegɔ yiya puan, gee muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum faarɛ yɛla, gee tibera teem la bã'ahi se'ehi woo en tari nɛreba la.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Hɛrɔd en ta ki la, ti Yosefe yeti a bihɛ, ti Daana suŋera en bala niiɛ a mɛŋa zaaheŋɔ puan bo' en yeti.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Gee bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ la nuurɛ ti ba zi'ire muɔ la zuo, gee nyaa to'e boroboro banuu la zim iyi' la, gorege bihɛ saazuo gee purɛ sum, gee nyaa figehe boroboro la bo' a zahereba la, ti a zahereba la me to'e tɔta nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Tisuŋa kan ta'am wam wala pu'uha, bii tibe'o ta'am wam walesuma.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: Ŋwana ti nɛreba kan baŋɛ ya nɔlurɛ la, gee ya Sɔ la en nyɛti sɛla en sugɛ la ma'a. Ya Sɔ la en nyɛti sɛla n sugɛ la wan yɔ ya lan nari se'em.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Ŋwana ti a yu'urɛ to'e Siriya tiŋa za'a, ti ba tara bã'duma woo, sɛba n tari teem yima yima, induma, kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi en tari sɛba, sɛba en luti kilenki'ire, la kɔɔrenɔ wa'am a zi'an na, ti a tibɛ ba za'a.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: Ihege, tari bia la, la ka ma la lebe Izirale tiŋa puan, se'ere n sɔi la, sɛba n dole bia la nama la ki ya.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Ti ba za'a di ta tigɛ, gee ti ba doohe lagehɛ sɛla ti ba di gee gee la, ti ba pirɛ pitɔ pia la tuyi'.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Tisɛka woo en ka wani walesuma, ba wan ŋmaaɛ ka bahɛ, dikɛ ka bahɛ bugumen.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Da kã'ahera ya lɔgerɔ biŋera ya mɛhi timpaberɛ wa zuo, zi'an ti guŋa wan mɔgɛ, ti tiŋan-ziim di, bii nageyigehi kɔ' kɛ̃ zu.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ dɔla a poore ze'ele Galilii, Dekapolise en de , Yerusalɛm, Yudiya, la Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Ti Yosefe ihege tari bia la, la ka ma la lebe Izirale tiŋa.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Sɛba en di la wan paaɛ wuu buraahi tuha anuu, ti pɔgehi la kɔma ka pa'ahɛ.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Ya wan baŋɛ ba doohe la ba wula puan.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Gee tugum boti ya ya lɔgerɔ biŋera saazuo ha, zi'an ti guŋa kan ta'am mɔgɛ, ti tiŋanziim kan ta'am di, bii nageyigehi kɔ' kɛ̃ zu.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Gee Yosefe en ta baŋɛ ti Akelaus en sooe a sɔ Hɛrɔd na'arɛ dɛna Naba Yudiya puan la, dabeem en kɛ̃ en ti a ka bɔta ti a lebe. Gee Yinɛ en ka'am en ninmu'urɛ zaaheŋɔ puan la, a ihege mɛ kiŋɛ Galilii.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum lɔgerɔ n boi zi'an la, fum suure boi la bilam.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Bilam bilam ti a bahɛ ti a zahereba la zom uureŋɔ tole nɛŋa yakera ko'om la yakenɛ deyima la ha, gee ti a leen keehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Dagi se'em woo en yeen yi'ira mam N Daana, N Daana, n wan kɛ̃ saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan, gee se'em n tuni mam Sɔ n boi saazuo ha la sumboolum.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: A ta kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la tiŋa ti ba yi'ira Nazareti, ti la bahɛ ti nɔtɔgeheba n tɔgɛ se'ere biŋe la wa'am sira na, Tu wan yi'ira en ti Nazaareŋa.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Niho n de niifum bo' inganɛ wa za'a. Bala zuo, fum niho han ana suŋa, fum iŋa za'a tari niifum mɛ.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: N keehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la ba'ahɛ la, a zom la zoore zuo a ma'a ta pu'uha. Ti yinɛ ta ma'ɛ ti a kelum bɔna bini a ma'a.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: De daarɛ, nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan yele mam yeti, N Daana, N Daana, dagi fum yu'urɛ ti tumam daa tara muula yelefum la, gee sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee tuna tuunkãra yima yima la la fum yu'urɛ puan.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Gee fum niho han ka barɛ, fum iŋa za'a boi la lika puan. Niifum la en boi fum puan la han dɛna lika, lika kaŋa wan ba' sobege!.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: gee bala ma'a ti ko'mi'ihi en tu'uhɛ uureŋɔ la, ti ku nan zaaɛ la doone, se'ere en sɔi la, sabeeo la lobere tu'uha ba mɛ.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Bilam, N wan yele ba yeti, Mam ka tabelɛ mi' ya, fɔregɛ ya mam zi'an ha, yelebe'iteba wa.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Ze'ele gogo kaareŋa anaahi bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ ti Yezu kina ko'om la tu'uha a zahereba la .

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Se'em woo en wum mam yelefum buna wa, gee dikɛ bu tum, ani wuu yɛm daana en mɛ a yire yagelɛ kuga ɛ̃berɛ zuo la.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Bala, mam yeti, ya da fabela vom wa yɛla, sɛla ti ya wan di bii sɛla ti ya wan nyu, bii ya iŋa wa yele, sɛla ti ya wan yɛ. Nyɔvore wa ka gani dia, ti inganɛ wa gana futo?.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Gee a zahereba la en nyɛ en ti a kini ko'om la zuo la, dabeem en kɛ̃' ba paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba yeti, A dela kukɔ! Gee kaahɛ la dabeem.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Ti saahi ni, ti ko'om di, ti kusebego lobe, ŋmɛ' yire la, gee yire la ka lu, se'ere en sɔi la, de yage la kuga ɛberɛ zuo.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Bihɛ ya niihe wa en ɛgeri saazuo wa, ba ka buti bii kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra, bii lageha sũna bã puan, gee ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha wa en diihe ba. Yamam ka tari tibehum gana bana wa?.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Gee bilam bilam ti Yezu tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Da bahɛ ya ti ya paleŋa ɛgera, la dela mam, da tari ya dabeem.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Gee Se'em woo en wum mam yelefum buna wa, gee ka dikɛ bu tum, ani wuu gɔɔŋɔ en mɛ a yire tintombi'ihego zuo la.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Gee ya se'em fabelegɔ wan ta'am dikɛ gogo kaareŋa ayina pa'ahɛ a vom yuure puan?.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Ti Pita lerege en yeti, Daana, la han sirum dɛna la fum, bo' nuurɛ ti en kiŋɛ ko'om la zuo wa'am fum zi'an na.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Gee ŋwani ti ya fabela futo yɛla? Bihɛ ya mɔpuuro wa n boi bɔ'en wa, ban zo'ore se'em, ba ka tuni, ba ka wugeri futo.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Ti saahi ni, ti ko'om di, ti kusebego lobe, ŋmɛ' yire la, ti yire la lu, lu damɛ wuregam.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: gee n yele ya yeti, la naba Solomɔn la a na'am suŋa la za'a ka ye' mɔpuuo la kuyina suŋa puan na.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Wa'ana. Bala ti Pita ze'ele uureŋɔ la puan pɔhɛ kina ko'om la zuo tɔɔra Yezu.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Yezu en ta tɔgɛ yele ana wa ba'ahɛ la, la iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la la pakerɛ zo'e zo'e, la a pa'alegɔ la.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Ti Yinɛ han yɛɛla samana zuo muurɔ en boi zina ma'a gee ti beere ti ba yuuhɛ ba nyɔ, ŋwani ti a kan yɛɛlɛ yamam gaŋɛ bala, sakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya duma wa?.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Gee en nyɛ sabeeo kãtɛ la, ti dabeem sãɛ en, ti a pɔhɛ muhera, nyaa kaahɛ bahɛ na yeti, Daana faaɛ mam.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a pa'alegɔ la tari la paŋa, a ka pa'ali ba wuu gɔnmi'ineba la.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Bala zuo, da fabele ya yeti Tu wan di la beni? Bii tu wan nyu la beni? Bii tu wan yɛ la beni.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Bilam bilam ti Yezu tiɛ a nu'o gurɛ en, gee yele en yeti, Fum sakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya, ŋwani ti fum bu'ura.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Sɛba en ka dole Yinɛ la en mu'ɛ nini la yele ana. Gee yelemɛŋerɛ, yamam Sɔ la en boi saazuo ha la pun mina ti ya nari yele ana za'a.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Ban zom uureŋɔ la zuo la, sabeeo la go'e mɛ.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Gee tugum tiregɛ ya ɛɛra Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan kɛ̃'a la bu tuuntɔɔ vom la yɛla yia, gee lɔge tuna za'a wan dikɛ pa'ahɛ ya.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Ti sɛba en boi uureŋɔ la puan la, na'ahɛ en gee yɛta yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ fum sirum dela Yinɛ Dayua.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Bala, da fabela ya beere yele, gee beere mɛŋa wan baŋɛ de mɛŋa yele. Gee zina daaŋɔ la pun seke zina mɛ.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Ban yakɛ yakenɛ la, ba kiŋɛ la Genesarete.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Tiŋa la nɛreba en ta baŋɛ en la, ba muulɛ mɛ kaaɛ tiŋa la, tari bã'duma za'a wa'am a zi'an na.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: gee belum en ti ba kalum a fuo la nɔgbanɛ. Sɛba woo en kalum ku la, nyɛ ba tibega mɛ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Dabeha ayoobe n tole, Ti Yezu doohe la Pita, Yemesi la a suuɔ Yɔn zom zoore zuin zuin ha ba ma'a.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Ti Yezu iŋa ta tee ba nɛŋan, ti a nɛŋa nyakera wuu wuntɛɛŋa la, ti a lɔgerɔ pelege kirikiri.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Moose la Eliya n bala niiɛ na zɛ'a la ba, tɔgera la Yezu.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ti Pita yele Yezu yeti, N daana, ka'lam bɔŋa ani suŋa. Fum han bɔta, mam wan ti' suga atã ka'lam, fum deyima, Moose deyima, Eliya me deyima.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: En boi bini tɔgera la, ti sagbanɛ en nyagere n bala wa'am na pi' ba. Ti kua ze'ele sagbanɛ la puan tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Mam dayua n ŋwana, se'em ti mam nɔŋɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Mam nini n tigɛ la se'em n bala, kɛleha ya en.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: A zahereba la en wum ŋwana la, ba lu la tiŋa ti dabeem sãɛ ba.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Ti Yezu wa'am na kalum ba gee yeti, Ihege ya, de tara ya dabeem.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Ban gorege bihɛ la, ba ka nyɛ nɛra nɛra gee Yezu a ma'a.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Ban ze'ele zoore la zuo sigera na la, ti Yezu kagɛ ba yeti, Da yele ya nɛra nɛra lilegere wa, ta paaɛ hansɛka ti Asaala Dayua la ta ihege a kum la puan.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Ti a zahereba la soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti gɔnmi'ineba la yeti, Eliya n wan wa'am yia na.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Eliya sirum kini na, a wan bahɛ ti sɛla wuu lebe ze'ele suŋa.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, Eliya pugum wa'am me na, gee ba ka baŋɛ en, gee iŋɛ en ban boti se'em. Bala mɛa ti Asaala Dayua la me yeti a nyɛ namehegɔ ba nu'uhen.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Ŋwana ti a zahereba la bɔkɛ ti a tɔgeri la Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra la yele.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Ban paaɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la zi'an la, buraa en paaɛ a zi'an na, kpa' duma a nɛŋan.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: gee yeti, N Daana, zɔ n dayua wa nimbu'ɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, a luti la kilenki'ire. A nameheri mɛ paaɛ paaɛ. A ni ta'am ta lu bugum puan mɛ, bii a lu ko'om puan.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Ti mam tari en wa'am fum zahereba la zi'an na, gee ba ka nyaŋɛ tibɛ en.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Yamam akatare sakerɛ, la ŋmɛlema buuri wa, mam yeti n bɔna la ya yuue la ŋwani? Mam yeti n ŋmibe la ya ta paaɛ la ŋwani? Tari ya en wa'am ka'lam na bo' mam.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Ti Yezu tahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a la, ti ka yehe a puan, ti bia la nyɛ ka tibegɔ bilam bilam.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Pooren ti a zahereba la wa'am a zi'an na ba ma'a, soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti tumam ka ta'am sa'ɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a la ti ka yehe.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ya sakerɛ la en pɔ'ɛ la en sɔi. Yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta ya wa, ti ya han tara sakerɛ ti de zuna la nangembiileho, ya wan yele zoore yeti, Vuuɛ ka'lam, ti de sirum vuuɛ, ti sɛla ka bɔna wan kpe'em bo' ya.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Yezu la a zahereba la en ta lagehɛ Galilii la, ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ba wan koohe Asaala Dayua wa bo' iŋɛ saaleba nu'uhen.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: ti ba ku' en, gee dabeha atã daarɛ a wan vo'e. Ti a zahereba la suya sagum paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Paaɛ Kapɛnum la, ti sɛba en to'ohe pu'uhegɔ yire kuba la wa'am Pita zi'an na yeti, Yamam pa'ala la ka lagum yɔɔra pu'uhegɔ yire kuba la bii.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Ti Pita yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, a yɔɔri. Ti ban ta kule yire na la, Yezu tɔgɛ la yele deni yia, soke yeti, Fum ti'ihɛ la dinɛ Simɔn? Tiŋa wa zuo na'duma to'ohe kuba la bani zi'an? Ba mɛhi kɔma bii saama.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, Saama zi'an ti Yezu yeti, La han dɛna bala, ba kɔma la yi boi la fai.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Gee tumam en ka boti tu zeem ba la zuo, kiŋɛ mɔgeren ta lobe zim baŋa, zim sebo ti fum nyɔkɛ yia la, fum han ya'ɛ bu nuurɛ, fum wan nyɛ lagekɔberɛ. Dikɛ bumɔ ta bo' ba, ti la dɛna fum la mam en bala.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Ti Yezu yi' a zahereba pia la bayi' la, bo' ba kpe'eŋo ti ba ta'na sɛfi'iha gee sa'aha ba nɛreba puan, gee tibera bã'ahi la teem woo.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: A tuntuneba pia la bayi' la yu'ura en ŋwana; Yia dela Simɔn, se'em ti ba Yi'ira Pita la, la a suuɔ Anduru, Yemesi en de Zebedii dayua, la a suuɔ Yɔn.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: Filipi la Batolomi, Tɔmasi la Matu kuba tɔ'ɔha la, Yemesi en de Alifeyusi dayua la, la Tadeyusi.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: Simɔn en de Keenan buuri la, la Yudasi Isikariyɔte se'em en wan koohe Yezu la.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Yezu zahereba pia la bayi' wa, ti a tum gee kagɛ ba yeti, Da kiŋɛ ya sɛba en dagi Yuuduma buuri la zi'an zi'an, da kɛ̃ ya Samariyaduma tinsɛka puan.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Gee tugum kiŋɛ ya Izirale piihi wa en bɔregɛ wa zi'an.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Yan kini la, muuli ya faarɛ yelefum wa yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la lɛm ya mɛ na.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Tibɛ ya bã'duma, ihege ya kum, tibɛ ya kunkɔma, sa'ahɛ ya kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi. Ya to'e la zaŋa, bala, bo' ya zaŋa.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Da dikɛ ya salema bii salempɛɛlega bii salema a sɛ'a ti ba yi'ira kɔpa ya sɛgana puan.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: da dikɛ ya tapɔtɔ, futo tuyi' bii tagera pirefum buyi', bii doore, se'ere en sɔi la, ba wan bo' tuntuneba ba nuurɛ dia.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Tinkãtɛ bii timbila sɛka woo puan ti ya kɛ̃, soke ya baŋɛ se'em en boi ka puan dɛna nɛresuŋɔ, la ya bɔna bilam ta paaɛ ya yɛhega.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Ya han kɛ̃ yire puan, ni pu'uhɛ ya.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Yire la duma han to'e ya, bahɛ ti ya suma'ahum la sige de puan, gee ba han ka to'e ya, luke ya suma'ahum ka'a la.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Se'em se'em han ka to'e ya bii kelehe ya yelefum la, yan yeheri la, kpa'ahɛ ya nama puuhego bahɛ tinkaŋa bii yidinɛ puan.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ ti, gakerɛ daarɛ la, la wan ta ana ma'ma' bo' Sodom la Gomora tihi gana tinkaŋa taaba.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Bihɛ ya, mam tuni ya mɛ wuu piihi la sahi tiŋasuka. Wikɛ wuu kampiihi la, gee dɛna ya naana wuu dayɛma la.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Mina ya ba suŋa suŋa, se'ere en sɔi la, bamam en wan nyɔgɛ ya kiŋɛ tugum zi'ihi, gee ŋmɛ' ya ba zamehegɔ yiya la puan.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Ba wan tari ya kiŋɛ timbihereba la naduma zi'ihen mam zuo, ti la dɛna sɛla n gã' bo' bamam la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Ba han ta dikɛ tihɛ, da fabele ya, yan wan tɔgɛ se'em bii yan wan lebehe ba se'em. Yan wan yele se'ere la, wan dikɛ bo' ya hankaŋa puan.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dagi yamam mɛhi en tɔgeri, gee ya Sɔ Sia la en tɔgeri dɔla ya puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Suuɔ wan ta kɔɔha suuɔ ti ba ku, ti sɔ koohe a bia, ti kɔma me ihege la ba dɔgereba ku ba.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɛra woo wan ta yaaɛ ya mam zuo, gee se'em n ŋmibe ze'ele ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ n wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Ba han mɛleha ya tiŋa kayima puan, zɔ ya loŋe tiŋa kayima. N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti ya kan ta'am lomehe Izirale tihi wa ba'ahɛ gee ti Asaala Dayua wa lebena.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Zahera ka gani a pa'ala, bii yameŋa gana ka kpɛ'ɛma.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: La ani suŋa ti zahera ani wuu a pa'ala, ti yameŋa ana wuu ka kpɛ'ɛma. Ba han yi' yidaana la Beelezebule, yu'yooro tuni taaba ti ba nyaa wan yi'ira a yire nɛreba la?.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Bala zuo, da tara ya dabeem la ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla ka boi pi' kan pilege, sɛla me ka boi sugɛ kan niiɛ na.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Mam yele ya sɛla la lika puan, muuli ya de peelumen. Ban waahum sɛla bo' fum la, zom gɔhegɔ muulɛ de.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Da zoti ya sɛba n wan ta'am ku inganɛ la ma'a gee kan ta'am ku sia la. Gee tugum zoti ya se'em en wan ta'am sagum inganɛ la sia la bugum tiŋa la puan.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Ba ka koohe amɔkiiha duma ba yi' la kɔbevuŋa ma'a? Gee amɔkiiha la ayima me ka luti tiŋa ti ya Sɔ wa ka mina.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: La ya zuo zonto za'a a kãalɛ ba mɛ.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Bala zuo, da tara ya dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, yamam tari tuulum gana amɔkiiha duma en zo'e.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Se'em woo en ze'ele nɛreba tiŋasuka purɛ mam yu'urɛ suŋa suŋa, mam me wan ta ze'ele en Sɔ en boi saazuo ha la nɛŋan me purɛ a yu'urɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Gee Se'em woo en ze'ele nɛreba tiŋasuka zagehɛ mam, mam me wan ta ze'ele n Sɔ en boi saazuo ha nɛŋan zagehɛ en.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Da ti'ihɛ ya ti mam wa'am na ti n tari la suma'ahum wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na, mam ka wa'am na ti n tari suma'ahum na, gee su'a.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam wa'am na ti n dikɛ la nɛra sa'ahɛ la sɔ, dikɛ poyua sa'ahɛ la ka ma, dikɛ poyua sa'ahɛ la ka danyaama.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: Nɛra dima paaɛ paaɛ ze'eti la a mɛŋa yire puan na.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Se'em woo en nɔŋɛ a sɔ bii a ma gana mam ka nari ma. Se'em woo me n nɔŋɛ a dayua bii poyua gana mam ka nari mam.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Se'em wuu n ka sakɛ dikɛ a pumpuuredɔɔ zi dɔla mam ka nari ma.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Se'em woo n zigeri ti a gu' a vom wan bɔregɛ bu bahɛ. Gee se'em woo me n ka gagɛ a vom yele mam zuo wan nyɛ vom.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Se'em woo en to'e ya, to'e la mam, ti se'em woo en to'e mam me to'e la se'em en tum mam na la.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Se'em en to'e nɔtɔgeha, en de nɔtɔgeha la zuo, wan to'e nɔtɔgeha yɔɔrɛ. Ti se'em me en to'e nɛretuntɔɔ en de nɛretuntɔɔ la zuo wan to'e nɛretuntɔɔ yɔɔrɛ.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Se'em woo en pun dikɛ ko'ma'ahega la wala bo' bumbibehi sina wa ayima ti ba de mam zahereba zuo, n yeti ya la Yelemɛŋerɛ ti, ba se'em kan koŋe ba yɔɔrɛ.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Sankaŋa ti Yezu zahereba la wa'am a zi'an na soke en yeti, Ani en gani za'a saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Ti a yi' bia na ze'ele ba nɛŋan.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: ti a yeti, N yeti ya la Yelemɛŋerɛ, ti han dagena ya tee mɛ ana wuu kɔma, ya kan malum kɛ̃' saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Se'em woo en sigehe a mɛa wuu bia wa, n de se'em n gani za'a saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Se'em woo n to'e bia n ani woo ŋwana mam yu'urɛ zuo, to'e la mam.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Ya se'em han dikɛ pirega lɔkɔ beŋe ti la pirɛ bumbibehi wa ayima en sakɛ bo' mam wa, la wan ana suŋa bo' a daana ti ba dikɛ nɛɛrɛ kugekãtɛ lu' yule a yugela tari en ta za'ɛ bahɛ ko'omen ti a muhi sige tiŋa tiŋa ha.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Yele boi tiŋa wa zuo, la sɛla en piti nɛreba lubera n boi ka zuo la. Yele sɛ'a en piti nɛreba lubera wan ba'afum wa'am me na, gee yele boi fum se'em daana zuo ti pirega lɔkɔ wa wan ze'ele na la.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Fum nu'o bii na'arɛ han bahɛ ti fum pita luta, ŋmaaɛ bahɛ, la wan ana suŋa bo' fum ti fum kɛ̃ vom puan la nu'yiŋɔ la na'yinɛ nyana fum tara nu'uhi siyi bii nama ayi' gee ti ba dikɛ fum bahɛ bugum sebo n ka diti kiŋera la puan.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Fum niho han bahɛ ti fum pita luta, kpikɛ bu lobe bahɛ, la wan ana suŋa bo' fum ti fum ta kɛ̃' vom puan la ninyimɔ nyana fum tara nini iyi' gee ti ba ta dikɛ fum lobe bahɛ bugum tiŋa.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Bihɛ ya suŋa la ya da bihɛ bumbibehi wa ayima yoo. Mam yeti ya mɛ wa, ti saazuo ha, ba suŋereba saŋa woo kɔ'ɔm nyɛta mam Sɔ en boi saazuo nɛŋa mɛ saŋa woo.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Yamam ti'ihɛ la deni? Nɛra han tara piihi kɔbega, ti kuyima bɔregɛ, a kan bahɛ pihiwai' la siwai' la zoore zuo gee ɛ peseko en bɔregɛ la me yoi?.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: La han iŋɛ nyɛ kɔ, yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti a puurɛ wan pee mɛ, la peyiŋɔ la, gana piihi pihiwai' la siwai' la en ka bɔregɛ la.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: Bala mɛa ti, la dagena ya Sɔ n boi saazuo ha la puti'irɛ ti bumbibehi wa ayima bɔregɛ.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Se'em han bɔna lageŋɔ puan han tuue fum, pa'alɛ en a tuure la, fum la eŋa ma'a tiŋasuka. A han kelehe fum, fum nyaŋɛ fum suuɔ la lebe me na en bala.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Gee a han ka kelehe fum, doohe la nɛra ayima bii bayi' pa'ahɛ fum puan, ti yetɔgesebo woo ti ya wan tɔgɛ la, nɛreba bayi' batã wan dɛna aze' nyɛ duma yeti bumɔ n bala.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Gee a han zagehɛ ka kelehe bamam, yele lageŋɔ la, ti se'em n tuue wa han kelum zagehɛ ti a pun kan kelehe lageŋɔ la, iŋɛ ya nɛre-ina daana wuu se'em en ka zoti Yezu, bii kubetɔ'ɔha la.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti sɛla woo ti ya lu' tiŋa wa zuo, saazuo ha me wan lu'. Sɛla woo ti ya lorege bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo, saazuo ha wan me wan lorege bahɛ.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta ya wa, ya bayi' han ku nuurɛ tiŋa wa zuo doohe la sɛla woo ti ya soke bɔta, mam Sɔ n boi saazuo la wan tum dɛ bo' ya.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, zi'an ti nɛreba ba yi' ba tã lagum taaba mam yu'urɛ puan, mam boi bini mɛ la ba.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Ti Pita paaɛ Yezu nɛŋan soke en yeti, Daana, se'em n suuɔ han tuue mam, sugum nuuren bulɛ ti n wan di sugeri bo' en? La tara tara ta paaɛ woo sugum nuuren buyopɔi.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Dagi sugum nuuren buyopɔi ma'a, gee mam yeti fum la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, sugum nuuren pihiyopɔi sugum nuurɛ buyopɔi.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Dina wa zuo, saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la dikɛ makɛ mɛ la naba en yeti a baŋɛ a yamehi tuuma en kini se'em.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: En pɔhɛ viihegɔ la, ayima tari la a hanɛ la ligeri en de talenta tuha pia, ti ba tari en wa'am na.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Gee yameŋa wa n kan nyaŋɛ yɔ hanɛ la zuo, ti a daana wa bo' nuurɛ ti ba koohe en la a pɔga, la a kɔma, la lɔgerɔ yɔ hanɛ la.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ti yameŋa wa pɔrɛ nama a daana wa nɛŋan ŋmɛ'ɛra nupu'ɔ gee na'aha a daana la yɛta yeti, Iŋɛ sugeri bo' mam, gee n wan yɔ sɛla woo.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ yameŋa wa daana la, ti a bahɛ en, gee di sugeri bo' a hanɛ la bo' en.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: Gee yameŋa kana mɛa wa en yehe na la, a wa'am na nyɛ la a yameŋa tadaana kayima n me tari a hanɛ dinaari kɔbega, ti a kɔ'ɔm gurɛ a kunkore kɛgera gee yeti Yɔ mam n hanɛ la.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Ti a yameŋa tadaana wa pɔrɛ nama a nɛŋan ŋmɛ'ɛra nu'pu'ɔ yɛta en yeti, Iŋɛ sugeri bo' mam gee en wan yɔ hɔ.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Gee a kɔ'ɔm mum la nini zagehɛ, gee tari en ta pagɛ ti a yɔ hanɛ la za'a.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: Naba la yamehi sise'ehi n nyɛ sɛla n iŋɛ la, ba suya n sagum paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba kiŋɛ ta yele ba daana la sɛla n iŋɛ.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Bala, ti a daana la yi' en na yele en yeti, Putɔya wa, mam bahɛ fum hanɛ la za'a bo' fum mɛ fum pɔri nama ŋmɛ nupu'ɔ la zuo.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Fum me ka suni ni ti fum zɔ fum yameŋa tadaana wa nimbu'ɔ, wuu mam zɔ fum nimbu'ɔ se'em la.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Ti a daana la dikɛ susutuulɛ, dikɛ en bo' ti ba namehɛ en ta paaɛ hansɛka ti a wan yɔ hanɛ la za'a za'a.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Ŋwana ti mam Sɔ en boi saazuo ha me wan dikɛ iŋɛ ya, ya se'em han ka di sugeri bo' a suuɔ a suure puan.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la ani wuu va'am daana en tari a tiihi wala va'am nyaa yehe bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ ti a ta ɛ tuntuneba ti ba tum a va'am puan.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: En tɔgɛ la tuntuneba la ba dabeherɛ yɔɔrɛ la en de dinaari ayima ku la, a tari ba kiŋɛ la a va'am la puan.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: En le ta yehe gogo kaareŋa buwai' bɔba la, a nyɛ ti tuntuneba basɛba n zɛ'a yoo da'a la puan.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam me kiŋɛ n tiihi wala va'am la puan ta tum, n wan yɔ ya la nari se'em. Ti ba kiŋɛ.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Ti a le ta yehe wuu wuntɛɛŋa en boi zuo tiŋasuka, la gogo kaareŋa atã puan iŋɛ la bala mɛa.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Ti la le ta dɛna wuu gogo kaareŋa bunuu zaanuurɛ bɔba, ti a le yehe ta nyɛ ti basɛba zɛ'a, ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya zɛ'a ka'lam yoo beere n yilegɛ daarɛ.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Ti ba yeti, Nɛra ka dikɛ tɔ. Ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam me kiŋɛ n tiihi wala va'am la puan.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Yezu en tɔgɛ yele ana ba'ahɛ la, a bahɛ Galilii mɛ gee kiŋɛ Yudiya n boi Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha la.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Yinɛ n ta ma'ɛ la, ti va'am daana la yele se'em n biheri a tuuma la yeti, Yi' tuntuneba la, la fum bo' ba, ba yɔɔrɛ. Pɔhɛ sɛba n wa'am pooren na la ta paaɛ yia duma la.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Ti Nɛrekuuŋɔ doohe a poore, ti a tibɛ ba bilam.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Sɛba n wa'am gogo kaareŋa bunuu zaanuurɛ bɔba la n paaɛ la, a bo' ba za'a la dabeherɛ yɔɔrɛ dabeherɛ yɔɔrɛ en dinaari ayima.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Ti Farasii basɛba wa'am a zi'an na, ban boti ba makɛ en bihɛ la, ti ba soke en yeti, La de ma'a, ti buraa lebehe a pɔga yele se'ere puan.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Lan paaɛ sɛba n wa'am yia la, ba ti'ihɛ ti bamam wan to'e ni gaŋɛ poorenduma la, gee ba za'a to'e la dabeherɛ yɔɔrɛ mɛa en de dinaari ayima ayima.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe ba yeti, Yamam ka kãalɛ lan gulehɛ yeti Se'em en naam ba pi'ileŋo puan na la naam ba la buraa la pɔka.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Ban to'e la, ba gɔgeni mɛ la va'am daana la.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: Gee le yeti, Dina wa zuo la, buraa wan bahɛ ka sɔ la ka ma gee naaɛ la a pɔga, ti ba bayi' la dɛna inganɛ deyina?.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: yɛta yeti, Poorenduma wa tum la gogo kaareŋa buyina ma'a gee ti fum bahɛ ti ba zuna la tumam, tumam n tugum tum ni bala la bulikan ha na, ti wuntɛɛŋa wuŋɛ tu.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Bala, ba ka le dɛna bayi', ba nyaa dela inganɛ deyina. Bala, sɛla ti Yinɛ dikɛ naaɛ taaba, nɛra de welege.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Gee va'am daana la lebehe ba ayima yeti, N zɔ, mam ka tuue ya, ya ka ku ni nuurɛ la mam la dabeherɛ yɔɔrɛ en de dinaari ayima?.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Gee ŋwani ti Moose bo' tu nuurɛ ti tu ta'am bo' ka sa'arɛ gɔŋɔ gee nyaa lebehe ka.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Tari ya sɛla n de ya dinɛ la kina. Mam mɛŋa n boti n yɔ poorenduma wa ti la zuna wuu mam bu ya se'em la.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya sukãberɛ la zuo ti Moose bo' ya sore ti ya lɛbeha ya pɔgeba, gee pi'ileŋo puan na la, la dagi bala.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: N ka tari sore ti n iŋɛ mam boti se'em la n mɛŋa bunɔ? Bii ya yuunɛ mɛ la n bu'a la.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: Gee n yeti ya mɛ wa, ti se'em woo en lebehe a pɔga han gee tee di pɔga han dagi ka galum mɛ zuo, tum la kabohi.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Bala, sɛba n tole nɛŋa wan ta yee pooren, ti sɛba n yee pooren wan ta wa'am na tole nɛŋa.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la yele en yeti, La han dɛna bala sira la pɔga tiŋasuka, la ye ani suŋa ti nɛra da di pɔga bii ele sira.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Yezu en wa yɛ'a zɔna Yerusalɛm la, a doohe la a zahereba pia la bayi' la ma'a, ban ye' la, ti a yɛta ba sore la zuo yeti.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, Dagi nɛra woo en wan ta'am to'e pa'ali kuna wa, gee sɛba ma'a ti Yinɛ dikɛ pa'ali kuna bo' ba la.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Bihɛ ya, tu ye' zɔna la Yerusalɛm wa. Ba wan ta dikɛ Asaala Dayua wa iŋɛ malema kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba nu'uhen, ti ba gakɛ en ti a dela a ki.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba kɔ'ɔm dɛna la bula ba maduma puuren na, ti sɛba me dɛna bula doohe la nɛreba en iŋɛ ba, ti sɛba me iŋɛ ba mɛhi dɛna bula saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la zuo. Se'em n wan ta'am bɔkɛ yele dina wa la, a bɔkɛ.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Ba nyaa wan dikɛ en iŋɛ sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la nu'uhen, ti ba ɛregɛ en, gee ŋmɛ' en, gee tari en ta kpa'. Gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Pooren ti ba tari kɔmbibehi wa'am a zi'an na, ti a dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuo pu'uhɛ bo' ba. Ti Yezu zahereba la tahera sɛba en tari kɔma la wa'am na la.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Ti Zebedii dayɔɔhi ma wa wa'am Yezu zi'an na la a dayɔɔhi la, paaɛ na kpa' duma Yezu nɛŋan, suhɛ en ti a iŋɛ se'ere bo' en.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti kɔma la wa'am n zi'an na. Da gu'uri ya ba, se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en ani wuu kɔma wa la, n sɔi saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Fum boti la beni? Ti pɔka wa yeti, Bu sore ti n dayɔɔhi siyi wa, ka yima ta zi'ire fum zuɔ nu'o ti kayima me ta zi'ire fum gɔbega nu'o fum sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Gee nyaa dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ kɔma la zuto gee kiŋɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Ya ka mi' sɛla ti ya sɔkera wa. Yamam wan nyaŋɛ nyu wula wa ti mam bɔna bini ti n nyu ka puan wa? Ti ba yeti, Tu wan nyaŋɛ.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Bilam bilam ti nɛra ayima wa'am a zi'an na yeti, Pa'ala, itesuŋɔ kuni ti en wan tum tara vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba ti, Ya sirum wan nyaŋɛ nyu mam wula wa puan. Gee ya zi'ire n zuɔ nu'o la n gɔbega nu'o bɔba dagi mam n wan bo' bala. Bilam dela sɛba ti mam Sɔ maahum ka ti ka dɛna ba dinɛ la zi'an.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum sɔkera mam ti sum yele? Nɛra ayima ma'a n de suŋa. Fum han bɔta ti fum kɛ̃' vom puan, gurɛ nɔze'elego la suŋa suŋa.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Zahereba pia la n wum dina wa la, ba suure n nyiie mɛ la ba suurɔ tu yi' la.
