eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Ti a soke en yeti, Aŋa bɛɛ? Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum da ku ka'ɛ ziim Fum da tum kabohi, fum da zu, Fum da dɛna aze' nyɛ pumparema.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Gee Yezu yi ba mɛ paaɛ a zi'an na yele ba yeti, Ya mi ti, sɛba n dagi Yuuduma nɛheduma tari la yɛla ɔ̃bela ba nɛreba, ti ba nɛrekãreba la me pɛregera ba bala mɛa me.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Na'aha fum sɔ la fum ma; gee me nɔŋɛ hɔ mabia wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛŋa se'em la.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Gee la kan dɛna bala yamam puan, gee se'em en boti a dɛna nɛrekãtɛ ya puan, dela a dɛna ya yameŋa.

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Gee budibele paalega wa yele Yezu yeti, Mam pun ita yele ana za'a mɛ n biilemen na; beni ti en kelum gee.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Ti se'em me n boti a bɔna ya nɛŋa, a dɛna ya da'aberɛ.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum nyaa han bɔta ti fum bi'ɛ kahegɛ, nyaa kiŋɛ ta koohe fum lɔgerɔ la, dikɛ ligeri la bo' naheduma, ti fum tarega la nyaa bɔna saazuo ha, gee ti fum nyaa wa'am na dɔla mam.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Wuu Asaala Dayua la n ka wa'am na ti ba tuna bɔ'ɔra en, gee a tugum tuna bɔ'ɔra, pun ta dikɛ a vom yɔ to'e nɛreba zo'e zo'e se'em la.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Bumbile-paalega wa n wum ŋwana la, a kiŋɛ mɛ la susageŋɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, a ba' pãrɛ mɛ.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Ban baheri Yeriko la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ zo'e zo'e n doohe a poore.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele a zahereba la yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti la wan kpe'em mɛ bo' buntata ti a kɛ̃' saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Bilam bilam Ti Fɔɔhi siyi zi'a sore nuuren. Ban wum ti Yezu n dole bilam tɔla la, ba voole mɛ yeti, Daana, zɔ tu nimbu'ɔ, Deviti dayua wa.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: N le yele pa'ahɛ, la de naana bo' yugenɛ ti de doohe fupiho yoko puan tole, gana buntata kɛ̃' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Ti Nɛrekuuŋɔ la tahɛ ba la ninmu'urɛ yeti, ŋmɛkɛ ya nɔya la, gee ba tugum malum voole gaŋɛ la bala yeti, Zɔ tu nimbu'ɔ, Daana, Deviti dayua wa.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: A zahereba la n wum dina wa la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba yeti, Ani n yi wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ti Yezu teke ze'ele su'u, gee yi ba yeti, Ya boti n iŋɛ la beni bo' ya.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ ba, gee yeti, Saaleba zi'an la kan ta'am tum. Gee Yinɛ zi'an sɛla woo wan ta'am tum.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Ti ba yeti, Daana, bahɛ ti tu nini wa nyɛta.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita lerege Yezu yeti, Bihɛ, tumam bahɛ sɛla woo mɛ gee dɔla la fum. Beni nyuurɔ ti tu wan nyɛ de puan.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Ti la ve'e Yezu paaɛ paaɛ, ti a paaɛ kalum ba nini la. Bilam bilam ti ba nini la nyɛta ti ba doohe en.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la Yelemɛŋerɛ wa ti, sɛla woo paalega saŋa la han ta paaɛ, ti Asaala Dayua la zi'ire a suŋa na'am kuka la zuo ba'ahɛ, yamam sɛba en doohe mam la me wan zi'a na'am kugehi pia la tuyi' me bihera Izirale buuri pia la ba yi' la.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Se'em woo n bahɛ yiya, suurɔ, bii tapa, sɔ bii ma, kɔma, bii vatɔ mam yu'urɛ wa zuo, wan lebehe nyɛ ba kɔbehi, gee me wan to'e vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Gee sɛba zo'e zo'e en diŋɛ nɛŋa, wan ta yee pooren ti sɛba en yee pooren ta diŋɛ nɛŋa.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, ti Yezu dikɛ makerɛ tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti;.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la ani woo, naba en iŋɛ pɔgedire de'eŋo bo' a dayua.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Nyaa tum a da'aba ti ba doohe yi' sɛba ti a yele ba a pɔgedire de'eŋo la yele la na, gee ba ka wa'am na.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Ti a le tum da'aba asɛ'a yeti, Ta yele ya sɛba ti n yele ba n pɔgedire de'eŋo ti, bihɛ, ba'ahɛ yu'uŋɔ dia la. Mam ku la lɔlehi la nagepɔla, ti ba maahum sɛla woo ba'ahɛ. Wa'am ya pɔgedire de'eŋo la na.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Gee ba ka dikɛ de yi' sɛla, gee kiŋɛ ba bunɔ, ti ayima kiŋɛ a va'amen, ti ayima mɛ kiŋɛ a koohego.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: gee ti basɛba la me gi' da'aba wa tum ba sɛla en ka ani suŋa, gee ku ba.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Ti Naba la sunsuya nyiie paaɛ paaɛ, ti a tum a pɛntiireba ti ba kiŋɛ ta ku nɛre kuureba bana wa, gee nyɔ' ba tihi bahɛ.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Ti a yele a da'aberɛ la yeti, Pɔgedire de'eŋo la maahum ba'ahɛ, gee sɛba ti en yi la ka nari ba wa'am na.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Kiŋɛ ya kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, ta doohe sadɔkãra la woo zuto la ya yi' yan nyɛ se'em woo tari wa'am pɔgedire malema la puan na.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Ti a da'aba wa la kiŋɛ sadɔtɔ la zuto, ta lagehɛ ban nyɛ nɛre se'em woo, nɛresuma la nɛrebe'ero na, ti pɔgedire de'eŋo zi'an la pirɛ kinkiŋi la saama.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Gee naba la en wa'am na ti a bihɛ saama la, a nyɛ ti buraa boi nɛreba la puan ka yɛ pɔgedire fuo.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Ti a yele en yeti, N zɔ, fum iŋɛ la ŋwani kɛ̃ ka'lam na gee ka yɛ pɔgedire fuo la? Ti a zɛ'a ka tara nuurɛ ya'ara.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ti naba la yele a yamehi la yeti, Bobe ya en, nu'uhi la nama ta bahɛ lika puan, zi'an ti a wan kaaha gee gi'ira nu'uhi gee ɔbera nyɛna.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Ba yi' la kuuŋɔ, gee fii ti ba wan looe.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Pooren ti Farasiiduma la kiŋɛ ta ku nuurɛ ti ba bɛ̃rɛ en doohe en tɔgɛ se'em la.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Bala, ti ba tum ba zahereba, la Hɛrɔdiduma, ti ba paaɛ na yeti, Pa'ala, tu mi' ti fum dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, gee pa'ala Yinɛ sore la ti la dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ, gee ka tara ninkɔrɛ la se'em me, se'ere en sɔi la, fum ka gaheri nɛreba.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Bala zuo, yele tu fum puti'irɛ, la de sore ti tu yɔɔra kuba bɔ'ɔra Siiza bii la dagi de sore.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Gee Yezu en mi' ba putɛ'be'o la, a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya makera mam, birekɔgehi wa?.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Bo' ya mam lagekɔberɛ n tari yɔɔra kuba la na. Ti ba dikɛ lagekɔberɛ en de dinaari ayima bu en.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: Ti a soke ba yeti, Ani zuo n ŋwana? Ti ani gulehɛgɔ en ŋwana.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ti ba lerege yeti Siiza, ti a yele ba yeti, Dikɛ ya sɛla n de Siiza dinɛ bo' Siiza, gee dikɛ sɛla n de Yinɛ dinɛ bo' Yinɛ.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Ban wum dina la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba bahɛ en gee kiŋɛ.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Didaarɛ mɛa ti Sadusii duma wa'am a zi'an na tɔgera ti, vo'ore ka boi. Gee nyaa soke en yeti.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Pooren ti Yezu yele nɛrekuuŋɔ la, la a zahereba la yeti.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Pa'ala, Moose pa'alegɔ la puan yeti, Nɛra han ki gee ka tara bia la a pɔga, a suuɔ di pokuurɛ la, la a dɔgɛ kɔma purɛ a suuɔ la yu'urɛ.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasiiduma zi' la Moose kuka la zuo.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Nananawa suurɔ tuyopɔi n boi la tɔ, ti kiima di pɔga, ka dɔgɛ la ka gee ki, gee gee pɔka la bahɛ a suuɔ.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
local: Bala, iti ya sɛla woo ti ba pa'ala la gee dɔla kɔ suŋa suŋa. Gee da iti ya ba itegɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba itegɔ la ka dole ba pa'alegɔ la.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Ti se'em n tagelɛ me iŋɛ bala mɛa, ti butã daana la me iŋɛ bala mɛa, ta paaɛ buyopɔi daana la.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Ba ni bobe la zeero en tibegɛ ka tana ziira dikɛ pagelɛ nɛreba bɔgerɔ zuo, gee bamam mɛhi ka maahum ti ba pun tiɛ ba nu'dibila lagum te'ele la fii me.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a ti pɔka la me ta ki.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Ba tuni ba tuuma za'a ti nɛreba n nyɛ ba Se'ere en sɔi la ba pɛ la pa'alegɔ la tiihegɔ funini kãra, gee pɛ sãba wogerɔ yule ba futo la tiŋa.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Vo'ore la, pɔka wa yeti ka dɛna la ba bayopɔi la puan ani pɔga, ba za'a n di ka kaaɛ wa.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Ba nɔŋɛ la ba zi'a na'ahegɔ zi'ihi, de'enɔ puan, gee me nɔŋɛ kugekãra zi'ihi la, zamehegɔ yiya la puan.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Ya tuue mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ka mi' gɔŋɔ la, bii Yinɛ paŋa la.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Ba me nɔŋɛ la nɛreba dɔbeta tiŋa da'ahi puan pu'uha ba, gee me nɔŋɛ nɛreba yi'ira ba Rabi.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, vo'ore la puan, pɔgedire bii Sire-ele kan ta bɔna, gee nɛreba wan ta ani wuu suŋereba n boi saazuo ha la.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Gee ba kan yi'ira yamam Rabi se'ere en sɔi la ya tari la pa'ala ayima ti yamam za'a dɛna suurɔ.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: La han dɛna vo'ore kum puan la, ya ka kãalɛ Yinɛ en yele se'em la, A yeti.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Da yi' ya se'em ti ya sɔ tiŋa wa zuo, ya tari la sɔ kayima ma'a, se'em n boi saazuo ha.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Mam dela Aberaham Yinɛ, Aziki Yinɛ, la Yakɔbe Yinɛ. A dela vɔpa Yinɛ gee dagi ki'induma Yinɛ.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Bii ba yi'ira yamam nɛheduma se'ere en sɔi la, ya tari la nɛŋadaana ayima ma'a se'em n de Kiresta la.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la en wum ŋwana la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ la a pa'alegɔ la.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Se'em n de nɛrekãtɛ ya puan, eŋa n wan dɛna ya yameŋa.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Farasiiduma la n wum ti Yezu lerege Sadusiiduma la ba ka tara nuurɛ ya'ara la, ti bamam me lagehɛ taaba.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Se'em woo n zɛkeri ba mɛhi wan sige tiŋa, ti se'em woo n sigehe a mɛŋa, ba wan zɛkɛ a.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Ti ba puan a yima dɛna yetɔgetɔgera, soke Yezu sokere ti a makɛ en bihɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Gee yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma, birekɔgehi wa. Ya gu' la nɛreba bahɛ saazuo ɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yiŋa, se'ere en sɔi la, yamam mɛhi kan kɛ̃, ti sɛba me han yeti ba kɛ̃ ti ya gu'ura ba.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Pa'ala, nɔze'elego la puan, kuni n gani za'a.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma, birekɔgenduma wa. Ya ni yagehɛ la ateko la tiŋa tihi ti ya ta tee nɛreyinɛ en dagi Yuda buuri, ti a lebege ana wuu yamam la, ba'ahɛ,ya ni iŋɛ en mɛ ti a malum dɛna bugum tiŋa bia sugum nuuren buyi' gana yamam mɛhi.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Ti a yele n yeti Fum nɔŋɛ fum Daana Yinɛ la fum suure za'a la fum sia za'a la fum puti'irɛ za'a.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, fɔnɛheduma wa, yamam yeti se'em han pɔ pu'uhegɔ yire la, la ka tari yele, gee se'em en pɔ salema la n boi pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, dela a tum pɔrɛ la en de se'em la'.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Dina n gani za'a gee dɛna yia nɔze'elego.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Fɔgɔɔnɔ wa wa, ba ini n tari tuulum, salema la bii pu'uhegɔ yire la en bahɛ n bahɛ ti salema la tara tuulum la?.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Se'ere en tagelɛ la me kɔ'ɔm ana mɛ wuu yia dinɛ la. Fum nɔŋɛ hɔ mabia wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛa se'em la.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ya me le yeti, Se'em wuu n pɔ kaabegɔ bimbeni la, la ka tari yele, gee se'em n pɔ bo'olum la n dɔgɛ kaabegɔ bimbeni la zuo la dela a tum a dɛ.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Nɔze'elego la za'a puan, ze'elego tuna tuyi' wa zuo ti nɔze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba yelefum la za'a yaga.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Bihɛ yan fɔ se'em, ba ini n tari tuulum, bo'olum la bii kaabegɔ bimbeni la n bahɛ ti bo'olum la tara tuulum la?.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Farasiiduma wa ta lagehɛ la taaba, ti Yezu soke ba sokere dina wa yeti.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Bala, se'em woo n pɔ kaabegɔ bimbeni la, pɔ dɛ mɛ la sɛla woo n boi de zuo.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Yamam ti'ihɛ ti Kiresta dela ani? Ani dayua en de en? Ti ba yele en ti, Deviti dayua.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Se'em woo n pɔ pu'uhegɔ yire la, pɔ la pu'uhegɔ yire la, pɔ de mɛ la se'em daana en boi de puan la.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Ti Yezu yeti, La yi iŋɛ la ŋwani ti Sia bahɛ ti Deviti yi'ira en a Daana, yɛta ti.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Se'em woo me n pɔ saazuo, pɔ la Yinɛ na'am kuka la, la se'em n zi' ku zuo la.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Daana la yele mam Daana, la yeti, Zi'ire en zuɔ nu'o na, gura ti n ta bahɛ ti fum dinduma ta lebege fum nama dafeere. .

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma wa, birekɔgenduma wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya bo'ori ya pia pia bo'olum pun ta ta' la ziirɔ ti ya yɔ' ti ya dugɛ, la ya zɛsaala za'a, la yele-pigehi pigehi gee tum bahɛ yelekãra sɛ'a en boi nɔze'elego puan dɛna buurɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la sakerɛ. Yele ana en de ni ya ita gee da bahɛ yele pigehi la me.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Deviti han yi'ira en a Daana, la wan iŋɛ la ŋwani ti a dɛna a dayua.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Fɔnɛheduma wa, ya tugum tubihɛ la zu'a bahɛ, gee vole yugenɛ.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Se'em daa ka boi ba puan nyaŋɛ lerege sokere la, bii ze'ele daan dinɛ daarɛ, se'em bɔna lakɛ nuurɛ ti a soke en sokere.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma wa, birekɔgenduma wa. Ya peere la wanɛ la laa poore, gee ba puan pirɛ mɛ la nyika la faaɛ lɔgerɔ ma'a.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Farasii fɔɔhi wa. Pee ya wanɛ la puan yia bala ti yiŋa la me wan ana filam.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma wa, birekɔgenduma wa. Ya ani mɛ wuu yɔɔrɔ, ti ba ŋmɛ bɔmbɔrehe peelego iŋɛ tu, ti tu yiŋa ana suŋa, gee ti tu puan pirɛ la ki'in kuba, la dɛgɛ seto woo la.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Bala mɛa ti ya yiŋa vom ani tuuntɔɔ nɛreba nɛŋan, gee ti ya puan la dɛna birekɔgenɛ la ŋmɛleŋɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma wa, birekɔgenduma wa. Ya dole mɛta la nɔtɔgeheba la yɔɔrɔ gee dɔla gula yɛla zɛ'ɛta ti ya tɛra tuuntɔɔ duma la.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: gee yɛta ya mɛhi yeti, Tumam yuum han boni, tu yaabe duma saŋa ha la, tumam yuum kan lagum ka'ɛ ni nɔtɔgeheba la ziim.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ban kiŋɛ na pɔra Yerusalɛm na la, paaɛ Bɛtefege en lɛm, Olive Zoore la, ti a tum a zahereba bayi.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Ya tɔgeri yele sɛ'a en yuum iŋɛ wa bahera la ya mɛhi zuto, se'ere en la ya dela sɛba en yuum ka'ahɛ nɔtɔgeheba la ziim la yaahi.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya timbila n boi ya nɛŋan ha wa, yan kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri ka puan la, bilam bilam ya wan nyɛ boŋa n lu' zɛ'a, la ka bia, lorege ya si na bo' mam.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Yi kɔ'ɔm tum ya ya yaabeduma tuuma la ti la pirɛ.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Se'em han soke ya se'ere, yeen yele en yeti Daana la en boti si. Gee a wan bahɛ ti si lebe kalam na.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Bunsɛlenduma wa kampiihi buuri wa Yamam wan iŋɛ la ŋwani piiɛ ti ba kan gakɛ kɛ̃ ya ta bahɛ bugum tiŋa la puan? Dikɛ ya bahɛ bugum la puan?.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Dina wa iŋɛ ti la doohe la yelese'ere n tɔgɛ doohe nɔtɔgeha la puan se'em yeti.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Se'ere zuo, bihɛ mam tum nɔtɔgeheba, yɛm duma, la gɔnmi'ineba. Ti ya ku basɛba, kpa' basɛba, gee basɛba me, ya ŋmɛ'suŋa suŋa ba mɛ ya zamehegɔ yiya la puan, gee dɔla tihi tihi mɛleha ba.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Yezu en yehe pu'uhegɔ yire yɛ'a la, ti a zahereba la paaɛ en, nyaa pa'ala en pu'uhegɔ yire la mɛɛhi la en mɛ suŋa se'em.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Yele ya Zaayɔn poyua wa yeti, Bihɛ ya, ya naba la en bala yɛ'a ya zi'an na la, a sigehe la a mɛa, ba' boŋa, a ba' la bombilepaalega.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Bala zuo ti tuntɔɔduma la woo ziim la ti ba ka'ahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo la taala wan dɔla ya . Ze'ele tuuntɔɔ daana Ebɛl, ta paaɛ Barakaya dayua Zekariya se'em ti ya ku biŋe tuulum saraa la la kaabegɔ bimbeni la tiŋasuka la.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya nyɛ bana wa za'a? N yeti la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti kugere la deyina kan ta yee paga de tadaana zuo, ba wan gbirege ba za'a bahɛ.

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Ti a zahereba la kiŋɛ ta iŋɛ wuu Yezu n pa'ali ba se'em la.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti nɛrebana wa ziim taala wan dɔla hankana buuri wa.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti Yezu ta zi'a Olive Zoore zuo, ti a zahereba la wa'am a zi'an ba ma'a na, yele en yeti, Yele tu, saŋa kani ti dina wa wan iŋɛ, la daalehum buni n wan pa'alɛ fum lɛbega na, la ba'ahegɔ saŋa la.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: tari boŋa la, la ka bia la paaɛ na, dikɛ ba futo pagelɛ si zuto, ti Yezu nyaa ba'.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Yerusalɛm, Yerusalɛm, tinsɛka en kuuri ka nɔtɔgeheba gee lɔbera sɛba ti ba tum a zi'an na la kuga. Saŋa zo'e zo'e ti mam zigera ti n lagehɛ ni fum kɔma wa bɔbeyinɛ wuu nua n kã'ahɛ ka kɔma iŋɛ ka kelumpagehi puan se'em la, gee fum ka boti.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Bihɛ ya mɛŋa suŋa ti nɛra da bahɛ ti ya bɔregɛ.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ me digehɛ ba futo yarum sore la zuo, ti sɛba aafum tiihi vɔtɔ me yarum sore la zuo.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Bihɛ, fum yire la n bala nyaa lebege daboo zɛ'a wa.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na dikɛ mam yu'urɛ yɛta ti, Mam n de Kiresta la. Ba wan bahɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ bɔregɛ.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ sɛba n boi nɛŋa la, la sɛba n boi pooren la vɔɔla gee yɛta yeti, Hozaana Deviti dayua wa. Sum boi mɛ la se'em n wa'am Daana la yu'urɛ puan na. Hozaana n boi zuin zuin ha.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam yeti fum mɛ wa, ti fum kan le malum nyɛ mam, gura ti fum ta ya'ɛ fum nuurɛ yeti, Sum sirum boi mɛ la se'em daana n wa'am Daana la yu'urɛ puan na la. .

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya wan wuna zaba yɛla la zaba yelebibehi. Gee da bahɛ ya ti ya paleŋa ɛgera, se'ere en sɔi la, yele ana woo wan ita, gee dagi ba'ahegɔ saŋa la n bala.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yezu en ta kɛ̃ Yerusalɛm la, tiŋa la za'a gbirege mɛ, sɔkera yeti, Ani n de ena wa.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tihi wan ihege pɛntɛrɛ la taaba, ti sɔ'ɔlum tɛ' pɛɛma la taaba. Kom pɛɛlega wan sigera zi'ihi zo'e zuto, ti tinmiiŋo sigera zi'ihi zo'e zo'e zuto.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en dole en la yeti, Ena n de Yezu, dɛna nɔtɔgeha yehe Nazareti n boi Galilii puan la.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Yele ana wa za'a wan ita gee a dela too la viregere n nan bala.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ti Yezu ta kɛ̃' pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, sa'ahɛ sɛba en koohe gee da'ara lɔgerɔ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la, gee tulehe sɛba n teehe ligeri la teebula kpabɛ, la sɛba n koohe dayɛma la me kugehi kpabɛ.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Ba wan nyɔgɛ ya tihɛ ti ba namehɛ ya, gee ku ya. Tihi wa za'a wan yaaɛ ya mam yu'urɛ wa zuo.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Gee yeti, La gulehɛ yeti, Ba wan yi'ira mam yire wa la pu'uhegɔ yire. Gee yamam dikɛ de lebege la nageyigehi suka zi'an.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Nɛreba zo'e wan pita luta, ŋma'ara taaba gee yaaɛ taaba.

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Ti Fɔɔhi la wɔbehi wa'am a zi'an pu'uhegɔ yire la puan na, ti a tibɛ ba.

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Gee malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la n nyɛ daalehum la ti Yezu tum la, gee me wum ti kɔma la vɔɔla pu'uhegɔ yire la yɛta ti, Hozaana Deviti Dayua wa la, ti ba suya nyiie paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Ŋmɛlema n wan pa'aha la zuo, nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɔŋerɛ wan ma'ɛ.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Ti ba soke Yezu yeti, Fum wuni ban yeti se'em wa? Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ n wuni. Yamam ka kãalɛ lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Kɔma, la kɔmbilɛɛhi nɔya ti fum maahum ti ba pɛgɛ fum la.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Gee se'em en ŋmibe ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ n wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Ti Yezu bahɛ ba, gee yehe tiŋa la puan kiŋɛ Bɛtani tinkãtɛ la puan ta gã'arɛ.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum faarɛ yelefum wa wan muulɛ paaɛ timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo, ti la dɛna aze' nyɛ daana bɔ'ɔra tihi za'a, gee ti ba'ahegɔ la baŋɛ wa'am na.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: Nananawa, bulika ti a lebe tinkãtɛ la puan la, ti a kom duna.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Bala, ya han ta nyɛ ti sageŋɔ daalehum lɔkɔ la ze' fa'a zi'an la, wuu nɔtɔgeha Daniɛl en yuum pɔrɛ se'em la .

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Ti a nyɛ ti kinkaŋa zɛ'a sore la nuuren, ti a paaɛ ka tiŋa, gee a ka nyɛ sɛsɛla ka zuo, gee vuurɔ ma'a. Ti a yele tia la ti, Fum kan le malum wam dia! Ti kinkaŋa la kɔ'ɔm ku'ɛ bilam bilam.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Dina wa han iŋɛ, sɛba en boi Yudiya, zɔ ya zom zɔya zuto.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: A zahereba la n nyɛ ŋwana la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ, ti ba yɛta yeti, La iŋɛ la ŋwani ti kinkaŋa la ki bala kalam.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Se'em me en yage gɔhegɔ, da le sige ti a dikɛ a lɔkɔ a yire.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta ya wa, ti ya han tara sakerɛ gee ka bu'ura, dagi la ya wan iŋɛ la sɛla n iŋɛ kinkaŋa wa ma'a, gee ya han pun yele zoore wa yeti, Vuuɛ ka'lam lobe fum mɛŋa bahɛ ateko wa puan, la wan iŋɛ la bala mɛa.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Se'em me en boi va'amen da lebe yire ti a dikɛ fuo.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Sɛla woo ti ya soke pu'uhegɔ puan nam, ya wan to'e.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Yele boi pɔgepuuhi, la sɛba en tari kɔma ti ba mɔgera hankaŋa puan la.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Yezu en kɛ̃' pu'uhegɔ yire la, ti malema kiinduma, la yizuto kiinduma wa'am Yezu zi'an na ti a boi bini pa'ala mɛ. Ti ba soke en yeti, Beni paŋa ti fum tara tuna yele ana wa. Ani n bo' fum kpe'eŋo kuna.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya ti ya zua wa da ta wa'am sapɔɔlegɔ saŋa bii vo'ohego dabeherɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam me wan soke ya sokere deyina, ti ya han lerege mam, mam me wan yele ya pansɛka ti n tara tuna n tuuma wa la.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, hankaŋa namehegɔ la wan ba' gaŋɛ mɛ, ka tabelɛ bɔna tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan ta paaɛ zina na, aayi, gee me pun kan le makɛ wa'am na.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Yɔn ko'om sua la, bu ze'ele la saazuo na bii asaala zi'an? Ti ba bu'ura la taaba yeti, Tu han yeti, bu ze'ele la saazuo na, a wan soke tu yeti, Ŋwani ti ya yi ka nam bo' en.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Yinɛ han ka ŋmaaɛ dabeha aŋa la girego bahɛ, nɛra ka bu n wan nyɛ faarɛ. Gee sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la zuo, a wan ŋmaaɛ dabeha aŋa girega girega.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Gee tu me han yeti, La ze'ele la asaala zi'an na, tu ye mina nɛrekuuŋɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, bamam za'a bihɛ ya Yɔn dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Bala la, nɛra han ta yele fum yeti Bihɛ, Kiresta n ŋwana, bii a boi la ke. De nam de.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Bala la, ba lerege Yezu yeti, Tu ka mi. Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam me kan yele ya kpe'eŋo seko ti n tara tuna yele ana wa.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta pumparenduma la nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma wan wa'am na tuna daalehum kãra, nyalema ti ba pã'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba bɔregɛ. La han wan ta'am iŋɛ ni, ba wan lagum pã'ahɛ sɛba ti Yinɛ mɛŋa looe la.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Yamam puti'irɛ dela deni yele dina wa puan? Buraa en tari a dayɔɔhi siyi, ta yele kiima la daarɛ deyima yeti, N dayua, kiŋɛ tiihi dia va'am la puan va'amen ta tum zina.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Bihɛ, mam pugum yele ya gee ti daarɛ la nan ka paaɛ.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Ti a lerege en yeti, Mam kan nyaŋɛ. Gee pooren ti a tee a puti'irɛ kiŋɛ.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Bala la, ba han yele ya yeti, Bihɛ, a boi la muuen ha, da yehe ya kiŋɛ bini. Ba han yeti, Bihɛ ya, a boi la bɔ'nɛŋan ha, da nam ya.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Ti sɔ la kiŋɛ yibega la zi'an ta yele en bala mɛa, ti a lerege en yeti, N daana, n wan kiŋɛ. Gee pooren tee a puti'irɛ ka kiŋɛ.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, wuu saa n ni nyakɛ dapɔya bɔba ti la niiɛ paaɛ zanɔya bɔba se'em la, bala mɛa ti Asaala Dayua lɛbega na la wan ta ana.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Kɔma bayi' wa puan, ba ani n tum ba sɔ la puti'irɛ? Ti ba yeti, Yia daana la. Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta ya wa, ti kubetɔ'ɔhereba la yalunduma la pun diŋɛ ya nɛŋa mɛ kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ze'a ti nɛnɔ bɔna la, ziiro lagehɛ la bilam.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yɔn n wa'am na pa'ala ya tuuntɔɔ sore la, ya ka nam bo' en, gee kubetɔ'ɔhereba la, la yalunduma la nam bo' en mɛ. Ti yamam n pun nyɛ ŋwana wa la, ya ka tee ya suya nam bo' en.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Namehegɔ dabeha la han tole, bilam bilam ti, wuntɛɛŋa wan sobege, ti ŋmarega kan niiɛ ka niifum la. Ti ŋmarebibehi ze'ele saazuo luta na, Saazuo pahi wan miim.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Le Kelehe ya makerɛ deyima. Yidaana en burɛ tiihi wala a va'am, mɛ nangoone kaaɛ bu, gee tu' daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko ti ba ŋmɛkera tiihi la wula daam la bini, gee mɛ limehego dewoko bini, nyaa dikɛ va'am la bo' bihereba, gee kiŋɛ satiŋa n zaaɛ.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Bala ma'a ti Asaala Dayua daalehum wan niiɛ saazuo wa ha, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo buuri za'a, wan kɛla, gee ti ba nini nyɛ Ti Asaala Dayua wa bɔna saazuo sagbama puan kina na la pankãtɛ la suŋa.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Go'ohego saŋa n ta paaɛ la, ti a tum a yamehi bihereba la zi'an ti ba kiŋɛ ta to'e a tɔtɛ na bo' en.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Tintanɛ wan ta peebe paŋa paŋa, ti a yi' a suŋereba tum ba ti ba doohe tiŋa wa luga anaahi la za'a lagehɛ sɛba ti a pun looe, ze'ele lugerɛ deyima ta paaɛ lugerɛ deyima.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Gee sɛba en biheri va'am la gi' a da'aba la mɛ, ŋmɛ da'aba, ku ayima, gee lobe ayima la kuga.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Nananawa, Zamehɛ ya yɛm kinkaŋa puan. Kinkaŋa han ta tara yilipaala, pɔhɛ puhera vɔpaala, ya ni mina ti siiɔ pɔri.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Ti a le tum da'aba sɛba n gani yia duma la, ti ba le iŋɛ bamam me bala mɛa.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Bala mɛa, ya han ta nyɛta yele ana wa za'a, ya mina ti a pɔri na, a kɔ'ɔm bɔna la ya nayaŋa nuuren.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, a tum la a mɛa dayua yeti, Ba wan na'ahɛ en.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti buuri kana saŋa wa kan ki ba'ahɛ gee ti ana za'a wan wa'am na tum.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Gee va'am bihereba la n nyɛ a dayua wa la, ba ɛbegɛ la taaba yeti, Asooevaaɛ daana la n ŋwana. Wa'am ya na, ti tu ku en gee sɔi vaaɛ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuo la tiŋa wa wan tole, gee mam yelefum wa kan malum tole.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Bala ti ba gi' en, vuue en yehe tiihi wala va'am la yiŋa ha ta ku en.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Gee han dɛna dabeherɛ la, la saŋa la, nɛra nɛra ka mi', Suŋereba en boi saazuo ka mi', bii dayua la diimen diimen gulehɛgɔ tuseto ka tari ka'lam, gee Sɔ la ma'a en mi'.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Nananawa, Va'am daana la han wa'am na, a wan iŋɛ va'am bihereba wa la beni.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Wuu Nowa dabeha la puan n yuum ani se'em la, bala mɛa ti Asaala Dayua la kinɛ na wan ana.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, A wan dikɛ bunyaalehi la putɔpa bana wa, sagum ba, gee ti a dikɛ va'am la tee bo' sɛba n wan bu en a tɔtɛ va'am la dia go'ohego saŋa.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Dabeha sɛ'a puan ti ko'om la yuum nan ka di tiŋa la, nɛreba la yuum diti mɛ, gee nyuura, gee dita pɔgeba gee ɛleha ba poyɔɔhi ta paaɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti Nowa kɛ̃ uureŋɔ la puan la.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yamam ka tabelɛ kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la puan n yeti, Tɔŋɔ seko ti mɛteba la zagehɛ la, tɔŋɔ kuŋɔ n ni ta dɛna ɛ̃berɛ de mɛŋa tɔŋɔ la. Daana la n tuun-dina wa bala, ti tu biha ti la dɛna pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Ba yuum ka mi' sɛla n yeti la iŋɛ, ta paaɛ se'em ma'a ti ko'om la wa'am na di piiɛ ba za'a kiŋɛ. Bala mɛa ti Asaala Dayua lɛbega na ta ana.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Bala zuo, n yeti ya mɛ wa, ti ba wan ta to'e Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum ya nu'uhen tee dikɛ bo' nɛresɛba n wan wuna sɔ'ɔlum la wala.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Nɛreba bayi' wan ta bɔna va'amen, ti ba dikɛ ayima gee bahɛ ayima.

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Se'em woo n lu kugere wa zuo wan kɔ zɔra zɔra. Gee kugere wa me n lu se'em woo zuo, de wan mɛregɛ a daana mɛrehɛ mɛrehɛ.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Pɔgehi siyi wan ta bɔna nɛɛren, ti ba dikɛ ayima gee bahɛ ayima.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Malema kiinduma, la Farasiiduma la n wum makerɛ wa la, ba baŋɛ ti a yeti la bamam.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Bala zuo da gihere ya, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ka mi' daan se'ere ti ya Daana la lɛbera na.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Ti ba bɔta ba nyɔkɛ en gee ba zoti la nɛrekuuŋɔ la dabeem, Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛrekuuŋɔ la bihɛ ti Yezu dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Gee bɔkɛ ya dina wa, ti yidaana han mina yu'uŋɔ puan, hansɛka ti nageyiga wan wa'am na, a kan giha. A kan bahɛ ti ba kɔ a yire la.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Bala zuo, yamam me dela ya maahum, se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala Dayua la ye' la hansɛka ti ya ka ti'iha ti a ye' na.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Ani en n bala dɛna da'aberɛ en yelemɛŋerɛ daana tara yɛm. Da'aberɛ'e sɛka ti a daana looe en ti a bihera a yire yɛla, bɔ'ɔra da'aberɛ sina la ba dia hansɛka.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Sum wan bɔna la da'aberɛ sɛka ti a daana la ta paaɛ na nyɛ en ti a boi tuuma puan.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti kpɛ'ɛma la wan bahɛ ti da'aberɛ'ɛ la bihera sɛlawoo ti a tara la.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Gee da'aberɛ la en de putɔya la han ti'ihɛ bo' a mɛŋa yeti, Mam kpɛ'ɛma wa ba' yuura mɛ.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: gee pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra a da'aba taaba, gee dita gee nyuura la bugereba.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: Da'aberɛ daana la wan wa'am la daan se'ere ti a ka ti'iha ti a wan wa'am na, la hansɛka ti a kan mina.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Daana wa wan wa'am na ŋma en zɔra zɔra, gee dikɛ en pa'ahɛ birekɔgenduma la puan ha. Zi'an ti kaahega la nyɛna ɔbega bɔna la.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Vo'ohego daarɛ la n tole la, pɔhega dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ bulika, ti Mɛɛri Magedalin la Mɛɛri ayima la, kiŋɛ ti ba bihɛ yɔɔ la.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti tiŋa la miim mɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana suŋera en ze'ele saazuo sige na bilege yɔɔ kugere la lebe pooren, zi'a de zuo.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Suŋera wa nyagere mɛ wuu saa bugum la, ti a lɔgerɔ pelege kerikeri wuu sakuga la.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Dabeem en sãɛ ba la zuo, ti gureba la gbi' pabegɛ ana wuu ba ki mɛ la.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Gee suŋera la yele pɔgehi la yeti, Da zɔ ya dabeem. Mam mi' ti ya eere la Yezu ti ba kpa' la.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: A ka boi ka'lam, se'ere en sɔi la, a vo'e mɛ, wuu en daa pun yele se'em la. Wa'am ya na bihɛ zi'an ti ba daa biŋe en la.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Kiŋɛ ya kalam ta yele a zahereba la yeti, Yezu vo'e a kum la puan. Yelemɛŋerɛ, a boi ya nɛŋa yɛ'a Galilii ha. Bilam ti ya wan ta nyɛ en. Mam sɔɔleŋa en ŋwana bo' ya.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Bala ti ba bahɛ yɔɔ la zi'an kalam la dabeem, la pupeelum kãtɛ, zɔ ta yele a zahereba la.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Yezu en bala tu'uhɛ ba yeti, Suma'afum bɔna la ya ti ba kiŋɛ paaɛ en, gurɛ a nama, Na'ahɛ en.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Da tari ya dabeem, kiŋɛ ya ta yele n suurɔ la ti ba kiŋɛ Galilii. Bilam ti ba wan nyɛ mam.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Pɔgehi la n ye'ehe la, ti gureba la basɛba kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan ta yele malema kiinduma la sɛla woo en iŋɛ la.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Beere n ta nyiie la, ti malema kiinduma la za'a, la yizuto kiinduma pun sɔhɛ mɛ la taaba ba wan iŋɛ se'em ku Yezu.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Ti Malema kiinduma duma la n sɔhɛ la yizuto kiinduma la ba'ahɛ la, ba za'a ku la nuurɛ ti ba bo' pɛntiireba gu'ureba la ligeri zo'e zo'e.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Ti ba ba' en, tari en kiŋɛ ta bo' timbihega nɛŋadaana Palete.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: gee yele ba yeti, Ta yele ya ba yeti, A zahereba la n wa'am yu'uŋɔ na zu en kiŋɛ se'em ma'a ti guum zɛɛ tumam la.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Yudasi, se'em n koohe Yezu la n ta wum ti ba gakɛ Yezu me la, la daam en mɛ, ti a tari salempɛɛla kila pihitã la lebe ta bo' malema kiinduma la yizuto kiinduma la.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Ti ŋwana wa han lu Timbihega daana la tuban, tumam wan nyɛ en, gee gu' ti daaŋɔ da paaɛ ya.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: A yele ba yeti, Mam tum la be'em la mam en koohe nɛra en ka mi' se'ere. Gee ba lebehe yeti, Dina pakɛ tumam la bɛ? Fum mɛŋa la fum yɛla.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Ŋwana ti ba to'e ba ligeri, gee iŋɛ ban pa'ali ba se'em la. Yuuduma kelum tɔgera yele dina wa mɛ la zina.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Yudasi en dikɛ salempɛɛlega kila la lobe bahɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la, a yehe mɛ, kiŋɛ ta yule la mi'ihi.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Ti a zahereba pia la ayima la kiŋɛ Galilii, zoore se'ere ti Yezu pa'alɛ ba la.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Gee malema kiinduma la n pi'ihe salempɛɛlega kila la, ba yeti, La dagi de sore ti tu dikɛ ba pa'ahɛ Yinɛ ligeri la puan, i dela ziim ligeri.

eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Ba nyɛ en la, ba na'ahɛ en mɛ, gee basɛba bu'uri mɛ.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Ban zi'ire sɔhɛ la taaba ba'ahɛ la, ba dikɛ ligeri la ta da' la yagumbaha daana zi'an ti ka dɛna saama pi'a zi'an.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Ti Yezu paaɛ ba na, yele ba yeti, Paŋa za'a en boi saazuo ha, la timpaberɛ wa zuo dikɛ bo' la mam.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Dina wa zuo, samanɛ dinɛ yu'urɛ yi'ira la ziim samanɛ, wa'am na paaɛ zina.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Bala, kiŋɛ ya timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, la ya maahum zahereba bo' mam. Sɔ ya ba ko'om doohe, Sɔ la, la Dayua la, la Fa'a Sia yu'urɛ puan.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: La wa'am na tum doohe la nɔtɔgeha Yɛremiya n yuum purɛ se'em yeti, Ba to'e la salempɛɛlega kila pihitã en de se'em ti ba pun di a da'a ba'ahɛ la yua ligeri la. Se'em ti Izirale nɛreba basɛba pun di a da'a ku la.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: gee pa'alɛ ya ba, ti ba nana sɛla woo ti mam bo' nuurɛ ti ya ita la. Gee tɛri ya ti, mam boi la ya mɛ saŋa woo, ta paara tiŋa wa ba'ahegɔ saŋa.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: Ba dikɛ ligeri wa ta da' la yagumbaha samanɛ wuu Daana la en tɔgɛ se'em bo' mam la.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ti Yezu zɛ'a timbihega nɛŋadaana la nɛŋan, ti a sɔkera en yeti, Fum en de Yuuduma naba la? Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum yele la bala wa.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Gee malema kiinduma la, la yizuto kiinduma la en zuuheri en la, a ka lerege.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Ti Palete soke en yeti, Fum ka wuni ban tari yele sɛ'a zo'e zo'e zuuhera fum wa.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Gee Yezu ka lerege en se'ere, la yeleyinɛ me. Ti la iŋɛ naba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Azɔɛtole de'eŋo saŋa puan la, la dela ba malema ti timbihega nɛŋa daana la ni yu'ɛ nɛra paka deen puan bahɛ na, nɛrese'em ti nɛreba la bɔta ti ba yu'ɛ en bahɛ na.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Sankana wa la, ba tari la tuunfi'iha daana ayima se'em yu'urɛ en yehe, ti ba yi'ira en Barabase, Diimen Diimen gulehɛgɔ la Yi'iri en Yezu Barabase.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Ban lagehɛ na la, ti Palete soke ba yeti, Ani ti ya bɔta ti en yu'ɛ bahɛ bo' ya, Barabase bii Yezu se'em en ti ba yi'ira Kiresta la.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a mi' ti nyuure zuo ti ba tari Yezu na bo' en la.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Ti la iŋɛ, ti Yezu en tɔgɛ yele ana woo ba'ahɛ la, a yele a zahereba la yeti.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Eŋa n zi' kuka la zuo ti a gakɛ la, ti a pɔga bo' en sɔɔleŋa yeti, Da bahɛ ti fum nu'o lagum bɔna buraa wa en de tuuntɔɔ wa kua puan, se'ere n sɔi la, zina mam namehɛ en zaaheŋɔ puan mɛ a zuo.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Ya mi' ti dabeha ayi' han tole, azɔɛtole de'eŋo la en ye' na. Ba wan koohe Asaala Dayua la tihɛ ti ba kpa' en.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Malema kiinduma, la yizuto kiinduma la, lagehɛ la malema nɛŋadaana la ti ba yi'ira Kaifase la yire .

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Ti malema kiinduma la, la yizuto kiinduma la belum nɛreba la ti ba yeti, ba yu'ɛ Barabase bahɛ gee ku Yezu.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: nyaa ku nuurɛ, ti ba wan doohe yɛmbɛ'ɛlega sore nyɔkɛ Yezu la ba ku en.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Ti timbihega nɛŋadaana wa le soke ba yeti bana bayi' wa, Ini ti ya bɔta ti n yu'ɛ bo' ya? Ti ba lebehe yeti, Barabase.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Gee ba yele ba mɛhi yeti, Tu kan tum dɛ azɔɛtole saŋa la, la ta'am tari gbirege wa'am nɛreba la puan na.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Ti Palete yele ba yeti, Gee n wan iŋɛ Yezu la ti ba yi'ira Kiresta la la bem? Ti nɛreba la za'a yeti, Bahɛ ti ba kpa' en.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yezu en ta bɔna Bɛtani, Simɔn insageŋɔ daana la yire la.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ti a soke ba yeti, Ŋwani, Beni be'em ti a tum? Gee nɛreba la malum voole gaŋɛ la bala yeti, Bahɛ ti ba kpa' en.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: ti pɔka wa'am a zi'an na, tara lila ti ba dikɛ kugesuma yagum ka, ti kaam da'a n kpe'em bɔna ka puan. Ti pɔka la ka'ɛ kaam la yeele Yezu zuo, se'em ma'a ti a zi'a dɛla, dia puan la.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Bala, Palete n ta baŋɛ ti a kan ta'am iŋɛ sɛla, gbiregeri n tugum yɛ'a na la, ti a dikɛ ko'om pee a nu'uhi nɛrekuuŋɔ la nɛŋan yeti, Mam ka mi' se'ere buraa wa ziim ka'arɛ puan. Ya mɛhi la ya yɛla.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Gee Yezu zahereba la n nyɛ ŋwana la, ba suya nyiie mɛ paaɛ paaɛ ti ba yeti, sageŋɔ kuna wa vuurɛ? Kaam wa ŋwana?.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Ti nɛreba la za'a yeti, A ziim taala dɔla tumam la tu kɔma.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Kabuna wa wan ta'am koohe lagekãtɛ, dikɛ bo' naheduma.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Yezu Kiresta en de Yinɛ dayua la Faarɛ yelehum la pɔhega.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Ti a yu'ɛ Barabase bo' ba, gee ti ban ŋmɛ' Yezu ba'ahɛ la, ti a nyaa bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba tari en ta kpa'.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Gee Yezu en baŋɛ ŋwana wa la, a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya daana pɔka wa? A tum la tunsunɛ bo' mam.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: La pɔhɛ mɛ wuu nɔtɔgeha Azaaya en gulehɛ se'em yeti: Bihɛ, n tuni la bituneŋa ti a diŋɛ fum nɛŋa, se'em en wan maalɛ fum sore la.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: La wa iŋɛ la dabeha fii wa ti Yezu lebe Kapɛnum na, ti nɛreba wum ti a kule na.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Ti timbihega nɛŋa daana wa pɛntiireba tari Yezu ta kɛ̃ timbihega daana yikãtɛ la puan, ti ba kã'ahɛ tiŋa la pɛntiireba woo na mum en kaaɛ.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Naheduma la boi la ya mɛ saŋa woo, gee mam kan bɔna la ya saŋa woo.

eng: a voice shouting in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight .
local: A wan dɛna la kua sɛka en wan bɔna kpaahenɛ boi ha kaaha baheri na yeti, Maalɛ ya Daana la sore la, bahɛ ya ti a sadɔtɔ la woo tiɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: Nɛreba la ba' lagehɛ zo'e gaŋɛ mɛ mikera ti fole ka bɔna la fii me, ta paaɛ bɔ'ɔ la nuuren. Ti Yezu tɔgera yelehum la bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Ti ba yɛɛhɛ a lɔgerɔ, gee dikɛ fumɔlegɔ yɛ en.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la Pɔka wa n ka'ɛ kaam wa yeele mam iŋa wa, a maahum mam mɛ n bala gura n laarɛ.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la wa niiɛ a mɛŋa la kpaahenɛ puan ha, muula suteere la yelebe'ero sugeri dia ko'om sua.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: Bala ma'a ti nɛreba banaahi zɛ kɔɔreŋɔ wa'am Yezu zi'an na.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Ban pa'am gu'uhi muŋa la ba'ahɛ la, ba dikɛ vuge la a zuo, dikɛ dibega zãlɛ a zuɔ nu'o gee nyaa dɔbeta a nɛŋan ɛregera en yeti, Yuuduma naba wa, bɔna wuuha.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti zɛ'sɛka woo timpaberɛ wa zuo ti ba muulɛ faarɛ yelefum wa, ba wan tɔgɛ pɔka wa n iŋɛ se'em wa tiiha ka yele.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Ti nɛreba kekeko ze'ele Yudiya kinkɛlehi, la Yerusalɛm timpaberɛ, kina a zi'an, nyaa puta ba be'em gee ti a bɔ'ɔra ba ko'om sua Yɔɔdan kulaa la puan.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Gee nɛreba la n mikere ti fole ka bɔna ti ba wan ta'am tari en paaɛ Yezu zi'an la zuo, ti ba zom ta lakɛ bɔ'ɔ la tɔ zi'an ti Yezu zi'an la, nyaa dikɛ kɔɔreŋɔ la en ga' ku suŋɔ zuo la, doohe bilam sigehe en Yezu nɛŋan.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Ti ba tubihɛ nɔtuurum ma' en, gee to'e doore la ŋmɛ a zuo.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Yezu zahereba pia la bayi' la, ayima, se'em ti ba yi'ira Yudasi Isikariyɔte la, kiŋɛ la malema kiinduma la zi'an.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yɔn yɛ la yugenɛ kɔberɔ fuo, dikɛ sɛganɛ lu' a sia, ti a dia dɛna sunwa'ara la seero.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yezu n nyɛ ba sakerɛ la, a yele kɔɔreŋɔ la yeti, N dayua, n saalum fum be'em bahɛ.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Ban ɛregɛ en ba'ahɛ la, ba yɛɛhɛ a lɔgerɔ la mɛ, gee tee dikɛ a mɛŋa lɔgerɔ la yɛ en, gee tari en kiŋɛ ti ba ta kpa.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: ta yeti, Ya wan bo' mam la beni n han koohe en bo' ya? Ti ba yɔ en salempɛɛlega kila pihitã.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: A muulɛ yeti, Se'em n kpe'em gana mam boi mam pooren kina na. Mam pɔ'ɛ gaŋɛ n zãla a tagera.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Ŋwana ti gɔnmi'ineba basɛba en zi' bini nyaa sɔkera ba mɛhi ba suya puan yeti.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban kiŋɛ yehe ha la, ba paaɛ la buraa n yehe Saarin tiŋa ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Simɔn, ti ba pɛregɛ buraa wa ti ka zɛ pumpuuredɔɔ la.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Yehe hankaŋa, a pɔhɛ ɛɛra la fole se'ere ti a wan koohe Yezu.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mam sɔɔri ya mɛ la ko'om wa, gee eŋa wan ta sɔɔra ya mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Ŋwani ti nɛra ena wa tɔgera ŋwana? A tuuri la Yinɛ! Ani wan ta'am saalum nɛra be'em bahɛ han dagena Yinɛ ma'a.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ti ban kiŋɛ na paaɛ zi'an ti ba yi'ira Gɔlegɔta la, .

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo pɔhega daarɛ la, ti Yezu zahereba la wa'am a zi'an na yele en yeti, Bɛha ti fum bɔta ti tu maahum azɔɛtole de'eŋo dia la bo' fum.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Dabeha aŋa puan, ti Yezu yehe Nazareti n boi Galilii puan wa'am na ti Yɔn sɔ en Yɔɔdan kulaa puan.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Bilam bilam ti Yezu pun baŋɛ sia puan ban ti'iheri se'em ba suya puan. Ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya sɔgeha ya mɛhi sɔgehi ana ya suya puan?.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: ti ba bo' en daam ti a nyu, ti ya'am gɛregɛ bini, gee n lem la, a zagehɛ mɛ.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya kɛ̃ tinkãtɛ wa puan, la ya kiŋɛ buraa kayima zi'an, ta yele ka yeti, Pa'ala la yeti, Mam saŋa paaɛ ya. Mam la n zahereba wan di azɔɛtole de'eŋo la fum yire. .

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: En ze'ele ko'om la puan kɔ'ɔm dɔɔra na la, a nyɛ ti saazuo n yo'e, ti Sia la ana wuu dayene, sigera a zuo na.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: De dinɛ n tɔi, n yele kɔɔreŋɔ wa yeti, n saalum fum be'em bahɛ bii ihege vaaɛ fum suŋɔ kina?.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Ban kpa' en ba'ahɛ la, ba ŋmɛ' la gbaŋa tɔrɛ a futo la. Ŋwana iŋɛ ti sɛla ti nɔtɔgeha la tɔgɛ la wa'am sira na. Ba tɔrɛ la mam futo la ba mɛhi, gee ŋmɛ gbaŋa futo zuo.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Ti a zahereba la tum wuu Yezu en yele ba se'em la, maahum azɔɛtole la.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Ti kua ze'ele saazuo tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Fum en de mam Dayua, se'em ti n nɔŋɛ, ti n nini tigɛ la en.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: Gee lan wan iŋɛ se'em ti ya mina ti mam Asaala Dayua wa tari kpe'eŋo timpaberɛ wa zuo ti n saalum nɛra be'em bahɛ n ŋwana. Gee nyaa yele kɔɔreŋɔ la yeti.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Ti ba nyaa zi'a bilam mɛa bihera en.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Zaanuurɛ en ta paaɛ la, ti Yezu zi'ire a zi'irego zi'an la zahereba pia la bayi' la.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Ti bilam bilam ti Fa'a Sia la tari Yezu la paŋa kɛ̃' kpaahenɛ puan.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: N yeti fum, ihege ze'ele,vaaɛ fum suŋɔ kule.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: A zuin, ba gulehɛ la gulehɛgɔ yagelɛ ti gulehɛgɔ la yeti, Yezu en ŋwana, Yuuduma naba la.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: ti ban boi bini dita la, ti a yele ba, yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, yamam ayima wan koohe mam.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: A boi kpaahenɛ la puan dabeha pinaahi, ti Sagena la makɛ en bihɛ. Ti a bɔna la muuen duhi, ti Yinɛ suŋereba gura en.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: Ti kɔɔreŋɔ wa vaaɛ ihege ze'ele bilam bilam. Vaaɛ a suŋɔ kiŋɛ ba woo nininta yehe. Ti la iŋɛ ba za'a waabe pakerɛ, ti ba na'ahera Yinɛ yeti, Tu ka tabelɛ nyɛ yele dina wa taaba.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Nageyigehi siyi ti ba lagum kpa' la Yezu, ayima a zuɔ nu'o, ayima a gɔbega nu'o.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Ti ba suure sagum paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba pɔhɛ sɔkera en ayima ayima yeti; Mam mi' ti la dagi mam n Daana.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Ban nyɔkɛ Yɔn pagɛ la, Yezu wa'am la Galilii na muula Yinɛ Faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Se'em n kɔ'ɔm tum laa wa puan la mam la, en wan koohe mam.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Sɛba woo n dole bilam bɔba tɔla, kɔ'ɔm tuura mɛ gee lɔbera ba zuto.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Yezu lebe kiŋɛ la Ateko nuuren ti nɛrekuuŋɔ lagehɛ a zi'an na ti a pa'ala ba.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: yeti, Saŋa la paaɛ ya, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la ka zaaɛ. Tee ya la ya sakɛ bo' Faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: Yezu n kinɛ yɛ'ɛha la, nyɛ Alifeyusi dayua Levi, ti a zi'an kuba to'ohego debila puan, ti a yele en yeti, Dɔla mam. Ti a ihege dɔla en.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: gee yɛta yeti, Fum se'em n wan gbirege pu'uhegɔ yire la bahɛ gee mɛ dɛ dabeha atã puan la n bala, faaɛ fum mɛŋa. Fum han sirum dɛna Yinɛ dayua la, ze'ele pumpuuredɔɔ wa zuo sige na.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Asaala Dayua la wan ba'afum kiŋɛ wuu ban pun gulehɛ se'em la, gee yele boi se'em daana en koohe en la zuo! La wan ani ni suŋa bo' en, ba han pun ka dɔgɛ buraa kana wa.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Yezu n dole Galilii Mɔgerɛ kaara la, a nyɛ la Simɔn la a suuɔ Anduru ti ba lɔbera yugehi mɔgerɛ la puan, se'ere en sɔi la ba dela zim nyɔgereba.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Yezu ta bɔna la Levi yire ti yu'uŋɔ dia saŋa paaɛ. Ti sɛba woo n to'ohe kuba, la be'emduma zo'e zo'e bɔna bini, lagum zi'a la Yezu la a zahereba la. Nɛrebana taaba wa zo'e mɛ lagum dɔla Yezu.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Bala mɛa ti malema kiinduma, gɔnmi'ineba, la yizuto kiinduma la ɛregera yeti.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Ŋwana ti Yudasi, se'em en wan koohe en la yeti, Mam mi' ti la dagi mam n Daana? Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum yele la bala.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, dole ya mam ti n iŋɛ ya ti ya tara nɛreba kina na bɔ'ɔra mam.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Ti Farasiiduma, la gɔnmi'ineba la n nyɛ ti Yezu diti la kubetɔ'ɔhereba la be'emduma la, ti ba yele a zahereba la yeti, Ŋwani ti a naaɛ la kubetɔ'ɔhereba la be'emduma dita.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: A faaɛ sɛba mɛ, gee kan ta'am faaɛ amɛŋa. Eŋa en de Izirale naba. A ze'ele pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo sige nananawa na, gee tu wan sakɛ bo' en.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Ban boi bini dita la, ti Yezu dikɛ boroboro, bo' purɛ sum. En purɛ sum bahɛ de zuo ba'ahɛ la, a figehe de mɛ, dikɛ bo' zahereba la, yeti, To'e ya, la ya obe. Mam iŋa n ŋwana.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba bahɛ ba yugehi la gee doohe a poore.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Yezu n wum ŋwana la, ti a yele ba yeti, Sɛba ka n bɛ̃'ɛri ka eere tiba, han dagena sɛba n bɛ̃'ɛri. Mam ka wa'am na ti n yi' la tuuntɔɔ duma, gee be'em duma.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: A dele la Yinɛ. Yinɛ la yi faaɛ en bahɛ nananawa na, wuu a han bɔta, se'ere en sɔi la, a yeti eŋa dela Yinɛ Dayua.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Ti a dikɛ wula, bo' fara ba'ahɛ, dikɛ bo' ba yeti Nyu ya de puan, ya za'a.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Ti Yezu le kɛ̃' zamehegɔ yire la puan, ti buraa bɔna bini ti ka nu'o ki.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: En kiŋɛ a nɛŋa fii la, a nyɛ ti Zebedii dayua Yemesi la a suuɔ Yɔn me boi ba uureŋɔ puan maala ba yugehi.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Saŋa kayima ti Yɔn zahereba, la Farasiiduma la bɔna nɔlure puan. Ti nɛreba wa'am na soke Yezu yeti, Ŋwani ti Yɔn zahereba la Farasii zahereba la luta nuurɛ gee ti fum zahereba la ka luta.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Ti Nageyigehi la ti ba kpa' la Yezu la me lagum woole Yezu bala mɛa.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Mam nɔpu'ulegɔ ziim n bala, wan ka'ɛ bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e yelebe'ero sugeri dia.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la ana bããlam gee bihera nyaa a wan tibɛ en vo'ohego dabeherɛ wa puan, ti ba nyɛ se'ere tɔgɛ.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Bilam bilam ti a yi' ba ti ba bahɛ ba sɔ Zebedii uureŋɔ la puan, la a tuntuneba la gee doohe en.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Yezu lerege ba yeti, Pɔgedire de'eŋo saama kan ta'am lu' nuurɛ pɔgedire puan, bii ba wan nyaŋɛ? Pɔgedita la n nan boi pɔgedire de'eŋo la puan la ba la, ba kan ta'am lu' nuurɛ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Ze'ele Wuntɛɛŋa yɛ'a ha, ti lika wa'am na pi' tiŋa la za'a tari ta paaɛ gogo kaareŋa butã bɔba.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti mam kan malum nyu tia wula daam wa, gura dabeherɛ la, ti n wan ta nyu bu paalega la ya n Sɔ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Ti Yezu yele buraa la nu'o n ki la yeti, Ihege wa'am n nɛŋan na.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Ba kiŋɛ la Kapɛnum, ti vo'ohego daarɛ paaɛ, ti a kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃' zamehegɔ yire puan ta pɔhɛ pa'alɛ.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Gee la kinɛ na ti pɔgedita la kan ta bɔna la ba, bilam ti ba nyaa wan lu' nuurɛ.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Ti gogo kaareŋa butã bɔba la, ti Yezu kaahɛ voole bahɛ na yeti, Eli Eli lama sabakitani? De vuurɛ dela, N Yinɛ, N Yinɛ, ŋwani ti fum zagehɛ mam.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ban yuum na'ahegɔ yuunɛ ba'ahɛ la, ba yehe mɛ kiŋɛ Olive Zoore la zuo.
