eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Gee nyaa soke ba yeti, La de sore ti nɛra tum sum vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan bii a tum be'em vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan? A faaɛ bahɛ bii a ku? Ti ba za'a yee suram.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Ti iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ la a pa'alegɔ se'ere en sɔi la, a pa'alɛ ba mɛ wuu nɛra en tari kpe'eŋo la, gee dagi gɔnmi'ineba n pa'alɛ se'em la!.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Ya mi' ti nɛra ka dikeri tanpaalɛ ligera fukɛka. A han iŋɛ bala, tanɛ kɛka la wan ahegɛ gee bahɛ tanpaalɛ la, ti anhegere dina nyaa wan gana za'a.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Nɛreba la n ze' bilam la basɛba n wum la, ba yeti, Buraa wa yi'iri la Eliya.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya za'a wan ta pirɛ lu ha, se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, N wan damɛ dunkiina la, ti piihi la za'a yiregɛ.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ ba woo kaaɛ, ti a suure nyiie. La daam Yezu mɛ la ban tari sukãberɛ se'ere taaba la. Ti a yele buraa la yeti, Tiɛ fum nu'o la. Ti buraa wa tiɛ ka nu'o la, ti nu'o la iŋɛ sum.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Bala ma'a la ti zamehegɔ yire la puan, buraa en boi bini tara Kulekpa'bɛ'a.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: Nɛra me ka dikeri daam n kele su'ura talankɛka puan. A han iŋɛ bala, daam la wan tahɛ talaŋa la, ka'ɛ, ti talaŋa la me aahum bahɛ yoo. Gee ba dikeri daam n kele su'ura la talaŋa paalega puan.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba ayima zɔ ta dikɛ soosoo tum daam mi'ihego puan, dikɛ vile dibega dikɛ ta bɔ'ɔra en ti a nyu.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Gee n han vo'e, n wan bɔna ya nɛŋa yɛ'a Galilii.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Bilam Bilam ti Farasiiduma la vaaɛ ihege yehe kalam ta naaɛ la Hɛrɔd nɛreba ti ba bihɛ ba wan iŋɛ se'em ku en.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: kaahɛ bahena yeti, Fum tari la bem la tu, Yezu en yehe Nazareti na wa? Fum wa'am na ti fum sagum tu mɛ? Mam mi' fum n de se'em mɛ. Fum dela Fa'a Daana, ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ deyima, ti Yezu la a zahereba dɔla ki va'am puan tɔla. Ti ban kinɛ la, ti a zahereba la pɔhɛ kɔ'ɔra ki la ɔbera.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Gee basɛba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu gu'uhi ya bihɛ Elia wan wa'am na faaɛ en bahɛ.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, La ba za'a n pun wan ta pirɛ lu fum zuo, mam kan malum pirɛ lu.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Ti Yezu bahɛ bilam la a zahereba la gee kiŋɛ Ateko nuuren, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ ze'ele Galilii dɔla a poore.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Ti Yezu tahɛ en yeti, ŋmɛkɛ fum nuurɛ la, gee tole yehe!.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la yele Yezu yeti, Bihɛ, ŋwani ti ba ita sɛla n dagi ba iŋɛ vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Ti Yezu lebe kaahɛ supi, gee vo'ohe a vo'ohum za'a.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Gee Ti Yezu yele en ti, N yeti fum la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yu'uŋɔ kuna wa puan, gee ti nɔdɔɔ baŋɛ kaahɛ la, fum wan ki'ihɛ mam sugum nuuren butã.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: Ban wum yelekãra sɛ'a woo ti Yezu ita la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ ze'ele Yudiya, Yerusalɛm, Idumia, Yɔɔdan pooren ha la tinse'ehi woo n boi Taai, la Sidɔn tihi puan la, wa'am a zi'an na.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a la bahɛ en ti a lu lige, gee kaahɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, gee nyaa yehe a puan na.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Yamam ka tabelɛ kãalɛ Deviti n yuum iŋɛ se'em, kom yuum nyɔkɛ en la a nɛreba la?.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Sankaŋa ti pu'uhegɔ yire puan tanɛ la ze'ele saazuo ahegɛ buyi' na paaɛ tiŋa. Ti tiŋa miim, ti tahi wɔ̃rɛ.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, La han pun dɛna mam ki la hɔ, mam kan ki'ihɛ hɔ. Ti zahereba la za'a yele bala mɛa.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Ti a yele a zahereba la ti ba maahum uureŋɔ la bo' en nɛrekuuŋɔ la zuo, ti ba da ta bibige en na.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, Beni n ŋwana? Pa'alesaaŋa la kpe'eŋo! A bo' Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la mɛ a nuurɛ, ti ba nam en.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Deviti yuum kɛ̃' la Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, se'em ma'a ti Abiata dɛna malema nɛŋa daana la, ta obe boroboro se'ere n de tuulum boroboro, ti nɛra yoo ka suna ti a obe, han dagena malema daana la ma'a. Gee Deviti obe mɛ, gee bo' a nɛreba la n dole en la.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Ti Yɔɔrɔ be'ehe, ti nɛresunduma sɛba en pun ki la ihege ba kum la puan.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Ti Yezu kiŋɛ la a zahereba la ta paaɛ zi'an ti ba yi'ira Gɛtesemane la. Ti a yele ba yeti, Zi' ya ka'lam gee ti n tole nɛŋa ha ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: He had healed so many people that everyone who was sick pushed forward so that they could touch him.
local: Se'ere sɔi la, Yezu tibɛ la bã'ahi zo'e zo'e, bala ti sɛba woo en bɛ̃'ɛri bibigera en na ti a kalum ba la a nu'o.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Ti Yezu yu'urɛ to'e tinse'ehi en kaaɛ Galilii la za'a.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yinɛ iŋɛ vo'ohego dabeherɛ la bo' la asaala, gee dagi asaala bo' vo'ohego dabeherɛ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Yezu en ta vo'e a kum la puan la, ti nɛresunduma wa nyaa yehe ba yɔɔrɔ la puan na kɛ̃ tuulum tiŋa la puan, dikɛ ba mɛhi pa'alɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Ti a doohe la Pita, la Zebedii dayɔɔhi siyi la yɛ'a, ti a suure pɔhɛ Sagena, ti a giila kɔ'ɔha.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi ni han tu'uha en, ba ni lu la a nɛŋan gee yɛta yeti, Fum en de Yinɛ Dayua la.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Yezu la a zahereba wa n kɔ'ɔm ze'ele zamehegɔ yire la yehe na la, ba kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃' la Simɔn la Anduru duma yire. Yezu, la Yemesi, la Yɔn.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Bala, Asaala Dayua wa n sɔi vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Pɛntiira la la sɛba n boi la en ti ba zɛ'a gura Yezu la en nyɛ sɛla woo en iŋɛ la, dabeem kãtɛ en kɛ̃ ba, ti ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, buraa wa dela Yinɛ Dayua.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam suure n ba' sagum ti la ana wuu n yeti n ki mɛ la kum. Boi ni ya ka'lam, da biha ya.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Gee Yezu me ni tahɛ ba yeti, ba da vɔɔla ti se'em baŋɛ eŋa.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Sankana wa puan ti Simɔn dɛɛm pɔka ga' la bɔ'en la ɔɔrɔ, ti ba waaɛ Yezu kalam.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Pɔgehi zo'e zo'e me boi bini, zɛ'a kinkɛleŋa ha gee biha na. Si doohe Yezu la bala Galilii na tuna bɔ'ɔra en.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: En kiŋɛ fii la, a lu la tiŋa a liko pu'uhɛ yeti, N Sɔ, la han wan ta'am iŋɛ, bahɛ ti wandina wa tole gee bahɛ mam, gee dagi mam boti se'em, gee fum n boti se'em.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Ti Yezu wa'am na gurɛ pɔka la nu'o dooe ka zi'ire, bilam bilam ti ɔɔrɔ la bahɛ pɔka la, ti ka kiŋɛ ta tari ko'om wa'am na bo' ba.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Yezu zom la zoore zuo, gee yi' en boti sɛba la ti ba wa'am a zi'an na .

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Pɔgehi wa puan, Mɛɛri Magedalin, boi bini mɛ, la Mɛɛri se'em en de Yemesi la Yosefe ma la, la pɔge sɛka en me de Zebedii dayɔɔhi siyi la ma la.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: Gee lebena paaɛ a zahereba la ti ba gihi mɛ, ti a soke Pita yeti; Bala yamam kan ta'am gu'uhi mam gogo kaareŋa buyina ma'a?.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Zaanuurɛ dinɛ mɛa, yintule n ta ana viie la, ti nɛreba tari bã'duma woo, la sɛba n tari Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la wa'am Yezu zi'an na.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Ti a looe ba puan nɛrepia la bayi', sigɛ ba yu'urɛ tuntuneba sɛba en boi la en, ti a tuna ba ti ba yɛha muula yelehum la.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Zaanuurɛ n ta paaɛ la, ti buntata ayima en ze'ele Arimatiya, ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Yosefe, me dɛna Yezu zaha.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Da gihi ya, gee pu'uhi ya ti ya da kɛ̃ makerɛ puan. Sia la kɔ'ɔm bɔta mɛ gee inganɛ la la n tari tɔregɔ.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Ti timpaberɛ la nɛreba za'a lagehɛ na zi'an yire la nayaŋa nuuren.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: gee to'e kpe'eŋo ta'na sa'aha Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Eŋa n kiŋɛ Palete zi'an, ta suhɛ en ti a dikɛ Yezu iŋa la bo' en. Ti Palete bo' nuurɛ ti ba dikɛ iŋa la bo' en.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Ti a le lebe buyi' puan ta pu'uhɛ yeti, N Sɔ, wanɛ wa han kan ta'am tole gee bahɛ mam gee dɛna la n nyu de puan, fum sumboolum tum.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Ti Yezu tibɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e, sɛba n bɛ̃'ɛri bã'ahi yima yima, gee sa'ahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi zo'e zo'e nɛreba puan bahɛ. Gee ka bo' kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la sore ti si tɔgera, se'ere en sɔi la, si mi' en.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: En looe nɛrepia la bayi' la se'em n bala. Simɔn.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Ti Yosefe to'e iŋa la, dikɛ fupɛɛlega n ani suŋa pɔbɛ iŋa la.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Ti a le lebe na, nyɛ ti ba gihi mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, guum n vilegeri ba.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Bulika, se'em ma'a ti la nam ana biin la, ti Yezu ihege yehe kiŋɛ kɛ̃' muuen ha ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: Zebedii dayua Yemesi la a suuɔ Yɔn .

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: nyaa tari ta laaɛ a mɛŋa yɔpɔɔlegɔ puan, a nyɔ yɔɔ wa la taŋa puan. Ti a nyaa bilege tankãtɛ lige yɔɔ la nuurɛ gee kule.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Ti a le bahɛ ba, gee kiŋɛ ta pu'uhɛ pa'ahɛ sugum nuuren butã, pu'uhɛ pu'uhi kuŋɔ mɛa.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Ti Simɔn la a taaba la me mu'ɛ nini ɛɛra en.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Nyaa dikɛ Anduru, Filipi, Batolomi, Matu, Tɔmasi, Alifeyusi dayua Yemesi, Tadeyusi, Simɔn se'em en lagum pa'ahɛ sɛba nini en mu'ɛ ti Izirale bɔna fai la.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Mɛɛri Magedalin la Mɛɛri ayima n daa boi bini, da yee zi'a tɔ yɔɔ la.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Gee nyaa lebe a zahereba la zi'an na yele ba yeti, Yamam kelum giha mɛ gee sũna? Bihɛ ya, saŋa la paaɛ ya, gee ba pun koohe Asaala Dayua la iŋɛ be'emduma nu'uhen.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Ban nyɛ en la, ba yele en yeti bihɛ, Nɛra woo eere fum mɛ.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: la Yudasi Isikariyɔte, se'em n wan koohe Yezu la. Ti Yezu kule.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Ti yimbeere la en de maaheŋɔ dabeherɛ la me yimbeere la, Ti malema kiinduma la, la Farasiiduma lagehɛ Palete zi'an.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ihege ya ti tu kiŋɛ. Bihɛ ya, se'em n koohe mam la ŋwana.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ ya tihi n boi ti kɔrɔmkɔrɔm wa ti n ta muulɛ yelehum la bilam me. Se'ere zuo ti mam yehe na en bala.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la le lagehɛ na, ti ba pun kan ta'am di.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: nyaa yele en yeti, Naa, tu tɛri ti pumparuma la n daa nan vɔi la, a daa yeti, Dabeha atã daarɛ n wan ihege.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: En nan boi bini tɔgera la, ti Yudasi, n de a zahereba pia la bayi' la puan la paaɛ na, la nɛrekuuŋɔ, en zãli sɔ'ɔhi la dɔya, ti malema kiinduma duma, la yizuto kiinduma la tum ba na.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Ti a doohe Galilii la za'a muula yelehum la ba zamehegɔ yiya la puan, gee sa'ahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi nɛreba puan.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Yezu buuri la n wum la, ba yehe mɛ ti ba gu' en, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba basɛba tɔgeri mɛ ti, A zuo en lobere.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Bala zuo, bo' nuurɛ ti ba gura yɔɔ la ta paaɛ dabeha atã daarɛ la. Han dagena bala a zahereba la wan kiŋɛ ta zu en kiŋɛ gee yele nɛreba ti, A vo'e a kum la puan. Ti pumpɔreŋɔ kuna wan gana yia pumpɔreŋɔ la.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Se'em n koohe en la, bo' ba la daalehum yeti, Se'em ti n mɔgɛ a nuurɛ la, eŋa n bala nyɔkɛ ya en.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Ti kunkone wa'am a zi'an na, kpa' duma gee bɛlena en yeti, Fum han bɔta, fum tibɛ mam.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba en ze'ele Yerusalɛm na yeti, Beelezebule n boi a puan, gee me tara Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi nɛŋa daana paŋa sa'aha Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Ti Palete yele ba yeti, Ya tari pɛntiireba sɛba en de gureba kiŋɛ ya ya ta gura yɔɔ la suŋa suŋa wuu yan wan nyaŋɛ se'em.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Bilam bilam ti a paaɛ Yezu nɛŋan na, yele en yeti, Rabi, fum fara fara, gee mɔgɛ a nuurɛ.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Ti la ve'e Yezu paaɛ paaɛ ti a tiɛ a nu'o kalum en, gee yele en yeti, Mam boti, to'e fum tibegɔ.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Ti Yezu yi' nɛreba la wa'am a zi'an na, nyaa dikɛ yetɔgum biŋere tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Ŋwani ti Sagena wan ta'am sa'aha Sagena nɛreba puan?.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Ŋwana ti ba kiŋɛ la gureba la, gee malum lɛ, yɔɔ la suŋa suŋa suŋa.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ti Yezu yele n yeti, N zɔ, tum fum wa'am na ti fum tum se'ere la. Ti ba nyaa paaɛ na gi' Yezu, nyɔkɛ en.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Bilam bilam ti a kunkom la bahɛ en ti a iŋa saalum.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Sɔ'ɔlum han yelege zabera la bu mɛŋa, Sɔ'ɔlum bumɔ kan ta'am ze'ele.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Yezu zahereba la a yima gurɛ a sɔ'a fooe kɛ̃' malema nɛŋa daana da'aberɛ tuberɛ fike.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Ti Yezu kagɛ en gee bahɛ ti a kiŋɛ.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yire han yelege zabera la de mɛŋa, yire dinɛ kan ta'am ze'ele.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Dikɛ fum su'a la lebe su' ka su'an zi'an, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo n dikɛ su'a kiiri la su'a puan.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Yezu yele en yeti, Bihɛ suŋa, da yele nɛra nɛra. Gee tari fum mɛŋa ta pa'alɛ Malema daana la, gee bo' ban'an bahega bo'olum sebo ti Moose bo' nuurɛ ti ya bɔ'ɔra la, ti la dɛna yelese'ere n iŋɛ gã'a bo' ba.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Bala, Sagena la han ihege zabera la a mɛŋa, yelehe, a kan ta'am ze'ele, gee a ba'ahegɔ n paaɛ.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: Fum ti'ihɛ ti mam kan ta'am yele n Sɔ mɛ, ti a me kan gu'um gee tum suŋereba n en de pɛntiireba tansuga pia la ayi' me yoi?.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Nɛra ka boi n wan ta'am kɛ̃' paŋadaana yire ta fa' a lɔgerɔ han dagena a lu' paŋadaana la mɛ gee nyaa nyaŋɛ fa' en.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Gee buraa wa yehe mɛ kɔ'ɔm muula fai zi'an woo ti la bahɛ ti Yezu ka le ta'na ɛɛra timpaberɛ puan yoo yoo, gee kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra timpaberɛ la kinkɛlehi ti nɛreba la ze'ele zi'ihi yima yima kina a ze'a na.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Gee gulehɛgɔ la yeti ku iŋɛ la ŋwani wa'am asira na, lan gulehɛ yeti, la yeti la tum doohe la ŋwana la.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: N yeti ya la Yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti sugeri wan bɔna bo' nɛreba ba be'em woo la yetɔgeyooro seto woo ti ba tɔgɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Sankaŋa puan, ti Yezu yele nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Yamam yehe me na la sɔ'ɔhi la dɔya ti ya nyɔkɛ mam woo n dela nageyiga? Beere n yilegeri woo mam ni zi'a la pu'uhegɔ yire la pa'ala, ti yamam ka nyɔkɛ mam.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: gee se'em woo n tɔgɛ yetɔgeyooro bo' Fa'a Sia la kan malum nyɛ sugeri, gee a tuue la tuure se'ere en kan malum bahɛ en.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Gee yele ana za'a tum ti, nɔtɔgeheba gulehɛgɔ la n wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na. Bilam ti a zahereba la za'a bahɛ en gee zɔ.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Yezu en yele ŋwana wa dela ban yeti, Kulekpa'bɛ'a en dole en la.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Sɛba n nyɔkɛ Yezu la, tari en kiŋɛ la malema kiima daana Kaifase yire, zi'an ti gɔnmi'ineba la yizuto kiinduma lagehɛ la.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Ti Yezu ma la a suurɔ wa'am na zi'a yire la yiŋa, gee tum ti ba yi' Yezu na.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Gee Pita boi la zaaɛ zaarɛ dɔla ba poore fiifiihe wa'am na paaɛ malema kiima la yire zinzakan la, wa'am na zi'a la sɛba n guri yire la, gee biha lan yeti la ba'ahɛ se'em.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n zi' kaaɛ en la, nyaa yele en yeti, bihɛ, Fum ma la fum suurɔ n boi yiŋa ɛɛra fum.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Malema kiinduma la, la kiinduma la nyaa eere la pumpɔrum seko ti ba wan parum bo' Yezu ti ba ta'am doohe bilam ku en.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Bani en de mam ma la mam suurɔ.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Gee ba ka nyɛ pumpɔreŋɔ kuŋɔ taaba, la pumparenduma zo'e zo'e n pun yehe na parum la. Pooren ti ba bayi' yehe na.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Gee nyaa bihɛ sɛba en zi' kaaɛ en la, gee yeti, Mam ma la mam suurɔ en ŋwana!.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: yeti, Nɛrekana wa yeti, eŋa wan ta'am gbi' Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire wa bahɛ gee lebe mɛ dɛ dabeha atã puan.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Se'em woo n tuni Yinɛ sumboolum la, eŋa daana en de mam suuɔ, la mam ma.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Ti Malema kiima la vaaɛ ihege saazuo yeti, Fum ka tari sɛla wan lebehe? Beni yɛla n bala ti ba tɔgera baha fum zuo wa.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Gee Yezu ka lerege ba se'ere. Ti Malema kiima la yele en yeti, Mam pɔ la Yinɛ vua wa ti fum han sirum dɛna la Kiresta la, Yinɛ Dayua la, yele tu.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum yele la bala wa. Gee n yeti fum mɛ wa, Ti hankana yɛ'a ha, fum wan ta nyɛ Asaala Dayua la, ti a zi'a paŋa zuɔ nu'o ze'ele saazuo sagbama wa puan kina na.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Ti malema kiima la aafum a futo gee yeti, A tu Yinɛ! Ŋwani ti ya le kelum ɛɛra aze' nyɛ daana? Bihɛ ya nyaa wum a tuuri la.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Yamam ku la deni? Ti ba yeti, A kɔ'ɔm dɛna la a ki.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Ti ba tubeha nɔtuurum ma'ara a nɛŋa, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra en, ti sɛba me lɔbera a pɛgɛhi.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: gee ni yeti, Purɛ pa'alɛ tɔ Kiresta wa, se'em n lobe fum pɛka wa la.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Sankana puan la, ti Pita zi' la yiŋa zinzakan la puan, ti pugebila n de da'aberɛ wa'am a zi'an na yele en yeti, Fum me ni dɔla mɛ la Yezu Galiliiŋa la.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Gee Pita ki'ihɛ mɛ ba za'a nɛŋan yeti, Mam ka mi' fum tɔgeri sɛla wa yele.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Pita n ze'ele bɔ'paka la puan yehe na la, pugela n me n de da'aberɛ me nyɛ en, gee yele nɛreba la n ze' la yeti, Buraa kana wa ni lagum bɔna mɛ la Yezu n de nazaareŋa la.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Ti Pita le ki'ihɛ la pɔtɛ yeti, Mam ka mi buraa wa.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Ti Pita pɔhɛ pɔta baha a mɛŋa zuo yeti, Mam ka mi' buraa wa. Sankaŋa puan la, Bilam bilam ti nɔdɔɔ kaahɛ.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Bilam ti Pita tiɛ Yezu n yele ni yeti. Gee ti nɔdɔɔ wan kaahɛ la, fum wan ta ki'ihɛ mam butã la. Bilam ti a yehe ta kele nintam ma'aha.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Ti Yezu le pɔhɛ pa'alegɔ Ateko nuuren, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ en ka tari makerɛ lagehɛ kaaɛ en mum, ti fole ka bɔna. Bala ti a zom yaga uureŋɔ zuo, ko'om la zuo, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la mike zi'a doone.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ pa'alɛ ba yɛla zo'e zo'e dɔla mageha puan. A yele ba yeti.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Kelehe ya suŋa. Kaara n yehe ti a ta lobege bumbuura.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: En ta lɔbegera la, ti bumbuura la basɛba lu sadɔɔ zuo, ti niihe wa'am na pi'ihe bamam.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Ti Basɛba lu taŋa zuo, zi'an ti tintɔnɔ ka zo'e. Ti bumbuura wa yehe kalam kalam se'ere en sɔi la, tintɔnɔ ka zo'e ba tilen.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: Ti wuntɛɛntuulega ŋmɛ'. Ti ba nyɛga la en ka ba' la zuo, ba ki mɛ.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Ti bumbuura la basɛba me lu gu'uhi zi'an, ti gu'uhi la zo'e na kpãrɛ ba ti ba ka iŋɛ.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Ti bumbuura la basɛba lu pu'uhegɔ zi'an, iŋɛ zukãra, gee zɔ'ɔra, gee wɔgera pa'aha, yagena zuto pita' pita', piyoobe piyoobe ti sɛba pun yagena kɔbehi kɔbehi.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Yezu en yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a yeti, Se'em woo en tari tuba ti a wuna, a kɛleha.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Yezu en ta bɔna a ma'a la, ti nɛresɛba en boi bilam la, la a zahereba pia la bayi' la, wa'am na soke en mageha la vuurɛ en de se'em.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam ma'a en mi' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yelesɛ'a en sugɛ, gee sɛba en boi yiŋa la, sɛla woo dole la mageha puan bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: Ti la bahɛ ti, ba bihera gee ka nyɛta, ti ba kɛleha, gee ka bɔkera. Ŋwana ti ba kan tee ba suya ti Yinɛ di sugeri bo' ba. .

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Gee nyaa yele ba yeti, Yamam ka bɔkɛ makerɛ dina wa vuurɛ? Gee ya nyaa wan iŋɛ la ŋwani bɔkɛ mageha la za'a vuurɛ?.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Kaara la en lobege sɛla la dela yelehum la.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Bumbuura sɛba en lu sore zuo la dela, sɛba ni wam yelehum la, ti Sagena la wa'am na dikɛ yelesuma la ti ba burɛ ba sunsuya puan la kalam gee bahɛ ba.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Bumbuura sɛ'a en lu taŋa zuo dela Nɛresɛba ni wam Yelehum la to'e bu bilam bilam la nyɛɛma.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: Gee ban ka ba' nyɛga la, ba ka yuure. Daaŋɔ bii namehegɔ han paaɛ ba yelehum la zuo, ba ni zagehɛ yelehum la mɛ bilam bilam.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Bumbuura sɛ'a en lu gu'uhi zi'an la dela, nɛresɛba en ni wam yelehum la.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: gee ti timpaberɛ wa vom yɛla pakɛ ba, la tarega yɛla pa'ahegɔ yɛla, la ba suya en boti lɔgeseto yɛla wa'am na pɛregɛ yelehum la, ti bu kan ta'am wam.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Bumbuura sɛ'a en lu zɛ'pu'uhegɔ zi'an la dela, nɛresɛba en ni wam yelehum la, to'e bu, nyaa ŋwana, pita' pita', piyoobe piyoobe, la kɔbehi kɔbehi.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Yezu yele ba yeti, Ba ni laŋɛ butolela yire puan dikɛ la pi'ɔ vugele ka, bii ba dikɛ ka ze'ele gɔregɔ leŋen, gee ka dikera sugeta ka sugelego zi'an?.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Sɛla ka boi pi' kan pilege, bii sɛla bɔna sugɛ kan niiɛ peelum puan na.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Se'em woo en tari tuba ti a wuna, a kɛleha.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Ti a le yele ba yeti, Be'e ya tuba kɛleha sɛla ti ya wuna wa suŋa suŋa. Fum n makɛ se'em bo' la, bala mɛa ti fum wan lebehe to'e, gee kelum to'e pa'ahɛ.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Sɛba en tari zo'e la, ba wan bo' ba zo'e pa'ahɛ, sɛba me en pun ka tari sɛla la, sɛla fii ti ba tara la, ba wan to'e la ba zi'an.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: A me le yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la kɛ̃'a ani mɛ wuu nɛra en lobe a bumbuura la.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: gee lebe kule ta gihera gee ihegera beere en yilegeri woo ti bumbuura la ta tahɛ yehe na zɔ'ɔra ti a ka mina la iŋɛ se'em.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Tiŋa la en ni bahɛ ti tile yehe yia na, tagela zuo ti nini nyaa wa'am zuo la puan na.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Gee dia la han bi'ɛ ba'ahɛ, a dikɛ la a kɛɛrega yɛ'ɛha, se'ere en sɔi la, kɛ'a saŋa paaɛ ya.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Yezu le yele pa'ahɛ yeti, tu wan dikɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa makɛ mɛ la bem, bii tu wan dikɛ la makerɛ deni makɛ bo' bu?.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: La ani wuu Nanginɛ biileho wa en pɔ'ɛ biila woo puan se'em wa ti ba dikɛ burɛ;.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: gee ba han burɛ en, a ni yehe mɛ dɛna tikãtɛ gana tiihi la woo, tɛ' yilekãra ti niihe zo'e zo'e tɛ'ɛra ba tugerɔ a ma'ahum la puan.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: A dikɛ la mageha kãra ana tɔgɛ yelehum la bo' nɛreba la. Ban wuni a se'em, ba wum.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: A ka tɔgeri se'ere bɔ'ɔra ba han dagena mageha puan. Gee a ni pa'alɛ la a zahereba la ma'a makerɛ woo vuurɛ en de se'em.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: De daarɛ mɛa Zaanuurɛ, ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu yakɛ ko'om wa yakenɛ deyima wa ha.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Ti ba bahɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la, gee ti a zahereba la, la eŋa bɔna uureŋɔ la puan wuu eŋa en pun boni ku puan se'em la. Ti uurenɔ tuseto me lagum doohe en.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti sabeeo kãtɛ en bala vukera ko'om la puan ti uureŋɔ la yɛ'ɛha muhega.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Gee ti Yezu gã'a uureŋɔ la gulengukɔ puan giha. Ti ba kiŋɛ ta niiɛ n yeti, Pa'ala, la ka daani hɔ la tumam en yeti tu bahɛ yoo wa.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Ti a ihege tahɛ sabeeo la, gee yele ko'mi'ihi la yeti, Suram! Ana bããlam! Ti sabeeo la go'e, ti sɛla woo ma'ɛ.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Ti a soke ba yeti, Ŋwani ti dabeem tara ya? Yamam kelum ka tara sakerɛ.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Ti dabeem sãɛ ba paaɛ paaɛ ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, Buraa kana wa ani la ŋwani en bala, ti sabeeo la Ateko ko'om nana en.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Ti ba yakɛ Ateko la yakenɛ deyima la ha, Gerasene.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Yezu en kɔ'ɔm ze'ele uureŋɔ la puan sige na la, bilam bilam, ti buraa ti Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla ka ze'ele yɔɔrɔ zuto tu'uha en na.

eng: This man lived among the tombs, and no one was ever strong enough to restrain him, even with a chain.
local: A kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la yɔɔrɔ wa zi'an. Ti nɛra ka bɔna wan ta'am gu' en. Kuto zɔtɔ pun kan nyaŋɛ ba' n.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ni ba' n mɛ la bãhi la zɔtɔ, gee a ni kɔ' zɔtɔ la mɛ gee kɔ' bãhi la mɛ zɔra zɔra, ti nɛra ka bɔna tara paŋa wan gi' en.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Daarɛ woo yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, a boi la yɔɔrɔ zi'an wa, gee zɔna zɔya zuto, nyabera gee tara kugetampɛtihi ŋmata a mɛŋa.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: En nyɛ Yezu ti a boi la zaaɛ zaarɛ la, ti a zɔ ta lu a nɛŋan.

eng: shouting, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you wont torture me.
local: Gee kaahɛ supi yeti, Yezu, gaŋɛ za'a daana Dayua wa, fum tara la deni la mam? N dikɛ la Yinɛ bɛlena fum, ti fum da sagum mam.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded him, Unclean spirit, come out of the man.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa n yeti ŋwana wa dela,Yezu pun bo' en la nuurɛ yeti, Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa tole yehe a puan na.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Fum yu'urɛ? Ti a lerege yeti, Mam yu'urɛ dela tuha tuha. Se'ere en sɔi la, tu zo'e mɛ.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a la nyaa belum Yezu ninmu'urɛ ti a da dige ba yehe tiŋa la puan.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Bala ma'a la ti kurikuri zo'e zo'e en boi kunkune zuo ha ɔbera.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a la bɛlena en yeti, Bahɛ ti tu kɛ̃' kurikuri wa puan. Bo' tu sore ti tu kɛ̃' ba puan.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ti Yezu bo' ba sore ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la yehi kɛ̃' kurikuri la nii la en de wuu tuha ayi' la puan, ti bamam me zɔ zom ateko la kunkune zuo, nyaa zulege sige ko'om la puan ha ta muhi ba za'a ki.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Sɛba en dole kurikuri la en nyɛ ŋwana la, ba zɔ mɛ ta muulɛ bo' sɛba en boi tinkãtɛ puan, la sɛba en boi timbibehi puan. Ti nɛreba la wa'am na ti ba bihɛ lan de sɛla iŋɛ.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yezu zi'an la, ba nyɛ ti buraa la ti Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla en la, zi' mɛ, yɛ lɔgerɔ dɛna a mɛŋa. Buraa sɛka ti Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi tuha tuha dole ni ka, ti ba zoti ni ka la en bala.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Sɛba en nyɛ sɛla en iŋɛ buraa la, la kurikuri la, n yele nɛreba sɛla iŋɛ.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Ti nɛreba la pɔhɛ bɛlena Yezu ti a bahɛ ba tiŋa.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Ti Yezu bɔna bini ti a zom uureŋɔ, ti buraa la ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dole ni en la bɛlena Yezu ti a bahɛ ti a doohe en, ta kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la en.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Gee Yezu zagehɛ mɛ gee yele en yeti, Kule yire fum mɛŋa nɛreba zi'an, la fum yele ba Daana la en tum yele kãtɛ se'ere bo' fum, la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ se'ere ti a dikɛ bo' fum.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Ti Buraa wa bahɛ bilam gee kiŋɛ Dekapolise timpaberɛ ta muula bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo Yezu en tum se'ere bo' en. Ti la iŋɛ nɛra woo pakerɛ.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Yezu le zom la uureŋɔ, le yakɛ ateko yakenɛ deyima la ha, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ lagehɛ kaaɛ en, ti a zi'a ko'om la nɔgbene.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Ti zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana ayima, yu'urɛ en yi'iri Yaaruse wa'am na ti a nyɛ Yezu. En nyɛ Yezu la, a lu la a nɛŋan.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: bɛlena en tabela yeti, N bipugela bila en boi ha kɔ'ɔm yɛ'ɛha kum. Bala, wa'am na dikɛ fum nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo ti a iŋɛ sum vɔna.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Bala ti Yezu doohe la en kiŋɛ a yire. Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ mikera dɔla en.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Ti pɔka bɔna nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan luta sɛkãra yuuma pia la ayi'.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Pɔka wa doohe tibeduma mɛ zo'e zo'e ta taregɛ, sagum a lɔgerɔ za'a, gee ti teere ka bɔna, la tugum malum nyalegɛ la nuurɛ.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Pɔka wa pun wum Yezu yele mɛ, ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la en boi Yezu pooren la, nyaa tole na kalum Yezu fuo.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yele a mɛŋa yeti, Mam han pun kalum a fuo wa ma'a, n ban'an wa wan bahɛ.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Bilam bilam ti a sɛkãra lua la ŋmaaɛ, ti sɛla iŋɛ a iŋa puan ti a baŋɛ ti ban'an la sirum bahɛ mɛ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Bilam bilam ti Yezu baŋɛ ti paŋa ze'ele a zi'an yehe. Ti a ŋmɛreha a mɛŋa nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan kaara gee sɔkera yeti, Ani en kalum n fuo wa.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, Fum mɛŋa la nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ wa en mikere za'ara luta fum zuo na se'em, ŋwani ti fum le sɔkera yeti, ani en kalum hɔ.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Ti Yezu ze'ele bããlam biha nɛrekuuŋɔ la kaara ti a baŋɛ se'em iŋɛ bala la.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Pɔka la, en mi' sɛla iŋɛ a iŋa puan la, yehe na la dabeem, ti a iŋa gbɛ'ɛra, ti a lu Yezu nɛŋan, yele en yelemɛŋerɛ la za'a.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele pɔka la yeti, N poyua, fum sakerɛ en tibɛ fum. Kina la suma'ahum, fum me nyɛ tibegɔ.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yezu n boi bini tɔgera la, ti nɛreba basɛba ze'ele zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana la yire wa'am na yele en yeti, Fum poyua la pun ki mɛ. Ŋwani ti fum yeti fum daam pa'ala la.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Gee Yezu en wum ban en yele se'em la, ti a yele zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana wa yeti, Da zɔ dabeem, yeen sakɛ.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Yezu en ye' yire la puan la, a ka bahɛ ti nɛra doohe en han dagena Pita, Yemesi, la Yɔn en de Yemesi suuɔ la ma'a .

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Ban paaɛ zamehegɔ yire nɛŋa daana wa yire la, a nyɛ la kɔ'ɔm ana saii zi'an woo, ti nɛreba kaaha gee fabela vɔɔla.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Yezu en kɛ̃' yire la puan la, a soke ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya bahɛ ti zi'an woo ana bala saii la kaahega? Bia wa ka ki, ka gihi mɛ.

eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Ti ba la'ara en. Ti a bahɛ ti nɛra woo yehe yiŋa, gee ti a tari bia la sɔ la ka ma la sɛba en doohe la en wa'am na la, ti ba kɛ̃' bia la en ga' zi'an la.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Ti Yezu gurɛ pugebila la nu'o gee yele en yeti, Talita kum de vuurɛ dela, Pugebila wa, ihege.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Bilam bilam ti pugela wa ihege, pɔhɛ kina kaara . Nɛreba la en nyɛ ŋwana la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ zo'e zo'e.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Ti Yezu ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da bahɛ ti nɛra baŋɛ yele wa. Gee nyaa yele ba ti ba bo' pugela wa dia ti ka di.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Yezu bahɛ bilam gee wa'am a mɛŋa timpaberɛ na, ti a zahereba la doohe en wa'am na.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ, ti Yezu pɔhɛ pa'alegɔ zamehegɔ yire la puan. Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wum pa'alegɔ la ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ. Ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, Buraa wa nyɛ yele ana woo la bɛha? A Yɛm kirega wa ze'ele la bɛha na? Bihɛ pansɛka en boi a nu'uhi ti a tara tuna kirehi se'ehi!.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Dagi kpa'ara la en bala, dagi Mɛɛri bia en bala, a yibehi dagi Yemesi, Yosi, Yudasi, la Simɔn. Dagi a tapa en bala boi la tu ka'lam wa? Ti ba suure nyiie la en.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Nɔtɔgeheba ka tari zuo ba mɛhi tihi, ba mɛhi suurɔ, la ba mɛhi yiya puan.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Ŋwana wa zuo, Yezu ka ta'am tum tuunkãra bilam han dagena nɛreba fii wa ti a dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto tibɛ ba.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Ti la iŋɛ Yezu pakerɛ la ban ka sakɛ bo' en la. Ti Yezu dɔla timbibehi puan pa'ala nɛreba yelehum la.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: A ta yi' la a zahereba pia la bayi' la, wa'am a zi'an na, sigɛ ba bayi' bayi' ti ba yehe ta tum, gee bo' ba kpe'eŋo ti ba ta'na sa'aha Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Gee yele ba yeti, ba da dikɛ sɛsɛla ba kinɛ wa puan, han dagena doore ma'a. Ba da tara dia, tapɔɔ bii ligeri ba funini puan.

eng: He told them to wear sandals but not to put on two shirts.
local: Ba pire tagera gee da yɛ futo tuyi' tuyi'.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: A yele ba yeti, Ya han ta paaɛ yire se'ere puan ti ba to'e ya, boni ya bilam gura daanse'ere daarɛ ti ya wan bahɛ tinkaŋa.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Tinsɛka duma han ka to'e ya, gee zagehɛ ti ba kan kelehe ya, yan baheri tinkaŋa puan la, kpa'ahɛ ya ya nama pu'uhegɔ bahɛ ka puan ti la dɛna yele iŋɛ gã'a.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Bala ti ba nyaa yehe ta muula yɛta ti, nɛra woo tee.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Gee nyaa sa'ahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi zo'e zo'e nɛreba puan, gee sɔ' bã'duma zo'e zo'e la kaam, tibɛ ba.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Ti Naba Hɛrɔd wum Yezu yele, se'ere en sɔi la, Yezu yu'urɛ yehe mɛ zi'an woo. Sɛba yeti , A dela Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la en vo'e kum puan lebe na. Bala zuo ti pahi sina bɔna a puan tuna.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Gee sɛba yeti, A dela Elia, ti sɛba me yeti, A dela nɔtɔgeha wuu diimen diimen nɔtɔgeheba la ayima.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Gee Naba Hɛrɔd en ta wum Yezu yele la, a yeti, Bihɛ ya, Yɔn se'em ti mam yuum fike a zuo la, en vo'e lebe na.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Naba Hɛrɔd mɛŋa en yuum tum pɛntiireba ti ba kiŋɛ ta nyɔkɛ Yɔn, bobe en, tari en na pagɛ paka deen doohe la a suuɔ Filipi pɔga Hɛrɔdiya ti Naba Hɛrɔd fã di la zuo.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Yɔn yuum yeti, La dagi de sore ti a fa' a suuɔ pɔga. La kihi mɛ ti fum di fum suuɔ pɔga.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Dina zuo ti Hɛrɔdiya tara diŋo la Yɔn, zigera ti a ku en. Gee ka nyaŋɛ.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: Hɛrɔd zoti Yɔn mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, a mi' ti Yɔn dela tuuntɔɔ la fa'a nɛra bala ti a yuum gu'ura en. Hɛrɔd han ni wum Yɔn tugum a giila en ni kɔ'ɔhɛ, gee la bala za'a, a ni kelum bɔta a kɛleha Yɔn mɛ.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Gee fole en wa gã'arɛ bo' Hɛrɔd, doohe la Hɛrɔd iŋɛ la a dɔgum daarɛ de'eŋo bo' a yire tuntuneba, a kpɛ'ɛnduma la Galilii Na'duma.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Ti Hɛrɔd pɔga Hɛrɔdiya poyua yehe na wa' de'eŋo la puan, ti la pee Naba Hɛrɔd la a saama la puurɛ, ti Naba wa yele pugela la yeti, Yele mam sɛla woo ti fum bɔta, gee n wan bo' fum.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Ti Naba wa pɔ yele pugela wa yeti, Yele mam sɛla woo ti fum bɔta, la han pun dɛna la mam sɔ'ɔlum wa puregerɛ.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Ti pugela wa yehe ta soke a ma yeti, N yeti n boti la bem? Ti a ma lebehe bilam bilam, Ta yeti fum boti la Yɔn zuo.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Ti pugela wa zɔ lebe Naba la zi'an kalam ta yele Naba la yeti, Mam boti la fum dikɛ Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la zuo iŋɛ lapɛrega puan tari na bo' mam nananawa.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Ti Naba la suure sagum. Gee en diŋɛ pɔ la, la saama la en zi' gee wum la zuo, a kan ta'am zagehɛ pugela en yele se'em la.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Bilam bilam ti Naba la tum a pɛntiira yeti, kiŋɛ ta tari Yɔn zuo na. Ti a kiŋɛ paka deen la puan ta fike Yɔn zuo.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: dikɛ iŋɛ lapɛrega puan tari na bo' pugela la. Ti pugela la me tari zuo la na bo' ka ma la me.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Ti Yɔn zahereba ta wam, wa'am na dikɛ iŋa la ta laaɛ.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la lagehɛ a zi'an na, nyaa yele en sɛla woo ti bamam tum gee pa'alɛ nɛreba la.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele ba yeti, Wa'am ya na ti tu kiŋɛ ya muuen ha tu ma'a la tu ta vo'ohe fii. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba zo'e zo'e en kinɛ ba zi'an na gee lɛbera, ti saŋa la fii wa ka bɔna ti ba pun wan di.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Bala ti ba zom uureŋɔ yakɛ kiŋɛ muuen ha ba ma'a.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Ban ye' la ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nyɛ ba baŋɛ, nyaa yehe tinse'ehi n boi bilam la, kabela la nama diŋɛ ta paaɛ ban n ye' zi'an la ba yia.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Yezu n ta paaɛ ko'om la nuuren la, a nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la mɛ, ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ en. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ani wuu piihi n ka tari dunkiina la. Ti a pɔhɛ pa'ala ba yɛla zo'e zo'e.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Yinɛ n ta nyuulena la, ti a zahereba la paaɛ a zi'an yele en yeti, ka'lam dela muuen, ti yinɛ me nyuulum.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Bala, bahɛ nɛreba wa ti ba yiregɛ, La ba ta'am kiŋɛ tinse'ehi n lɛm wa la timbibehi wa puan ta da' dia di.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yamam mɛhi bo' ba dia ti ba di. Ti ba yele en yeti, Tumam wan ta'am nyɛ dinaari kɔbehi siyi da' dia bo' nɛreba wa ti ba di.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ya tari la boroboro ba lɛ? Kiŋɛ ya ta bihɛ. Ti ba kiŋɛ ta bihɛ lebe na yele en yeti, Tu tari la boroboro banuu, la zim i yi'.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Ti a bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la tigehe taaba zi'ire muɔ la zuo.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Bala ti nɛreba wa tigehe taaba kɔbega kɔbega, la pihinuu pihinuu zi'ire.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Ti Yezu dikɛ boroboro banuu la zim i yi' la, gorege bihɛ saazuo, purɛ sum bahɛ dia la zuo gee fige boroboro la, dikɛ bo' a zahereba la yeti, ba to'e tɔrɛ nɛreba la, gee figehe zim i yi' la me bo' ba za'a.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Ti ba to'e tɔrɛ nɛreba la, ti ba za'a di tigɛ.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: gee ti ba doohe va boroboro la, la zim la ti ba obe tigɛ gee bahɛ la, pirɛ pitɔ pia la tu yi'.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Sɛba n di boroboro la paaɛ buraahi tuhekɔbehi sinuu.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Bala n ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu bahɛ ti a zahereba la zom uureŋɔ diŋɛ nɛŋa yakera ko'om la yagenɛ deyima ha, Bɛtesaada bɔba, gee ti a yee keehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la ba'ahɛ.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: En bahɛ ba ba'ahɛ la, a zom la zoore la zuo ti a ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Yinɛ n ta ma'ɛ la, ti a zahereba la pun bɔna uureŋɔ la puan mɛ yɛ'ɛha gee ti a ma'a yee bɔna doone.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Yezu n ta nyɛ ti uureŋɔ la mɔŋa kpe'em bo' a zahereba la, doohe la kusebego n lobere tu'uha ba la, ti a sige ko'om la zuo kina tu'uha ba na. Sankaŋa ti la kɔ'ɔm ita la funa funa wuu gogo muma anaahi bɔba la, ti a kina tɔɔra ba na wuu a yeti a zom la ba uureŋɔ la zuo la.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Gee ban nyɛ en ti a kina ko'om la zuo la, ti ba ti'ihɛ ti a dela kukɔ, ti ba kaahɛ.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba za'a nyɛ en mɛ ti dabeem nyɔkɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Gee bilam bilam ti Yezu tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Nyɔkɛ ya ya mɛhi gee la dela mam. Da zoti ya dabeem.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Gee nyaa zom uureŋɔ la zuo la ba, ti kusebego la ma'aɛ, ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba pun ka bɔkɛ boroboro kirega la vuurɛ, gee me kãbɛ ba suya sina.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Ban yakɛ ko'om la, ba paaɛ la Genesarete timpaberɛ, nyaa lu' ba uureŋɔ la.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: ze'ele ku puan sige na la, ti nɛreba pun baŋɛ en kalam.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: zɔta ɛɛra timpaberɛ la puan zi'an woo tara bã'duma n ga' ba sunɔ zuto, kina zi'an woo ti ba wum ti a boi bini.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Ti zi'an woo ti Yezu kiŋɛ, tinkãra bii timbibehi puan, bii va'am puan, nɛreba ni tari la bã'duma yehe na beŋe da'ahi puan, gee belum en ti a bahɛ ti ba yeen kalum a fuo la timpaberɛ, ti se'em woo n ni kalum ni sirum nyɛ la tibegɔ.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la Gɔnmi'ineba basɛba ze'ele Yerusalɛm na, lagehɛ taaba Yezu zi'an.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Ba nyɛ mɛ ti Yezu zahereba la basɛba diti mɛ la galeŋɔ nu'uhi, ba ka pee ba nu'uhi la gee dita.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.

Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Ba me ka diti sɛla en ze'ele da'a na gee ka pɛɛra la. Ba yaabeduma malema zo'e zo'e boi mɛ ti ba dɔla wuu, ko'om wama, dugerɔ, la nyɔgum dugerɔ peere.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Ŋwana ti Farasiiduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum zahereba la ka dɔla tu sɔyaabe duma pa'alegɔ la, gee dita la galeŋɔ nu'uhi.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Nɔtɔgeha Azaaya yuum tɔgɛ yamam birekɔgenduma wa yele la asira, wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Nɛrebana wa na'ahɛ mam mɛ la ba nɔya, gee ba suya zaaɛ mɛ la mam.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Ba na'ahɛ mam la zaŋa, Ba pa'alegɔ dela asaala puti'irɛ, ti ba yɛla dɛna asaala mi'ilum.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Ya bahɛ Yinɛ nɔze'elego la mɛ, gee tugum gurɛ ya malema.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Ti a lebe yele ba yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, ya mi' la sore suŋɔ seko ti ya wan doohe zagehɛ Yinɛ nɔze'elego la gee gurɛ ya malema!.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Moose pa'alegɔ la yeti, Na'aha fum sɔ la fum ma. Fum se'em woo en tɔgɛ fum ma bii fum sɔ be'em ba ku hɔ.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Gee yamam yeti, nɛra han yele a sɔ bii a ma yeti, Suŋerɛ se'ere woo ti fum wan nyɛ ni mam zi'an la dela kaabegɔ bunɔ.

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Nɛra han diŋɛ yele ŋwana, a kan le suŋɛ a sɔ bii a ma yɛ'ɛha.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Ya bahɛ ti Yinɛ yelehum la ka tara vuurɛ, ya yaabeduma malema wa zuo. Ti ya ita yɛla zo'e zo'e en ani wuu yele ana wa.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Gee nyaa le yi' nɛrekuuŋɔ la na, yele ba yeti, Ya za'a kelehe ya mam suŋa, la ya bɔkɛ.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Sɛla en ze'eti yiŋa kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛra puan kan ta'am galum en, gee sɛla en ze'eti nɛra puan yɛha na en galeni en. .

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: En ta bahɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la gee kɛ̃' yire la, ti a zahereba la soke en makerɛ la vuurɛ.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam me ka bɔkɛ? Yamam ka mi' ti sɛla en ze'eti yiŋa kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛra puan na kan ta'am galum en?.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dia ka sigeri nɛra suuren gee a puuren nyaa ta yehe na. .

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Ti a yeti, Sɛla en ze'eti nɛra puan yɛhera na en tugum galena en.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra suure puan ti ti'ihɛ be'ero wuu yaluntuuma, nageyigum, nɛrekua.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: kabohi, nyika, putoo, pã'ahegɔ, dɛgerɔ, nintɛrega, yu'sageŋɔ, pa'ali mɛŋa, la gerum tuuma.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Yele ana wa za'a ze'eti la nɛra puan na galena en.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Dabeha aŋa mɛa puan daarɛ deyima ti nɛrekuuŋɔ le lagehɛ Yezu zi'an yuue, ti dia ka bɔna ti ba wan di. Ti a yi' a zahereba la yeti.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Ti Yezu ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ Taai timpaberɛ, ta paaɛ kɛ̃' yire puan, ka bɔta ti nɛra baŋɛ ti a boi bini. Gee a kelum ka ta'am piiɛ dɛ.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ wa tari mam la nimbu'ɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, ba boi ka'lam la mam la dabeha, ka tara sɛla wan di.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Pɔka bipugela ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a dɔla en. Ti pɔka wa wum Yezu yele, wa'am na kpa' duma a nɛŋan.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: N han bahɛ ba ti ba kule la kom buna wa, ba wan vibe sore. Basɛba me ze'ele la zi'an n ba' zaaɛ na.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Pɔka wa tɔgeri la Giriki gee dɛna Sarofonisiya buuri. Nyaa belum Yezu ti a sa'ɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'a la a poyua la puan.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la lerege en yeti, Nɛra yeti a iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am nyɛ dia muɔ wa puan diihe nɛrekuuŋɔ wa.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Bahɛ ti kɔma la di tigɛ yia se'ere en sɔi la, la dagi ma'a ti nɛra dikɛ kɔma dia bo' baahi.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Ya tari la boroboro ba lɛ? Ti ba yeti, Boroboro bayopɔi.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Gee pɔka wa lerege Yezu yeti, Naa, baahi la n ga' gee ti kɔma la dita ti sɛla zɔgera la, baahi la wan pi'ihe.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Ti a bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la zi'ire timpaberɛ. Gee nyaa to'e boroboro bayopɔi la, bu Yinɛ fara ba'ahɛ, gee fige boroboro la bo' a zahereba la ti ba tɔrɛ. Ti ba tɔrɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum en yele se'em wa zuo, kula gee Kulekpa'bɛ'a la yehe fum poyua la puan.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Ba me tari zim fii, ti a pu'uhɛ bahɛ zim la me zuo, gee yele a zahereba ti ba tɔrɛ bamam me.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Ŋwana ti pɔka wa kule ta paaɛ, nyɛ ti a poyua la ga' mɛ, ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a la pun tole.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la di tigɛ, gee ti a zahereba la doohe va nɛreba la en di gee bahɛ sɛla la pirɛ pitɔ tuyopɔi.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Yezu ze'ele la Taai timpaberɛ lɛbera, dɔla Sidɔn timpaberɛ sore, tɔɔra Galilii mɔgerɛ la, Dekapolise sɔ'ɔlum puan.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Nɛreba wa paaɛ wuu tuha anaahi. Ti Yezu nyaa keehe ba ti ba kiŋɛ.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Ti ba tari buraa en wam gee me ka ta'na tɔgera wa'am a zi'an na. Belum en ti a dikɛ a nu'o pagelɛ buraa la zuo.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: N keehe ba bã'ahi la, a kɔ'ɔm zom la uureŋɔ la a zahereba la ti ba kiŋɛ Dalemanuta timpaberɛ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Ti a tari buraa la yehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la kinkɛleŋa a ma'a, ta dikɛ a nudibehi tum buraa la tuban, gee tubehɛ nɔtuurum iŋɛ a nu'dibila zuo, dikɛ kalum buraa la zɛleŋa.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Ti Farasiiduma wa'am Yezu zi'an na pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la en, sɔkera en ti, a iŋɛ daalehum sebo en ze'ele saazuo na, ti ba makɛ en bihɛ.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: Gee gorege bihɛ saazuo, gee si'im paaɛ paaɛ gee yele buraa la yeti, Ɛfata de vuurɛ dela, Be'e.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Ti Yezu si'im a suure puan paaɛ paaɛ, gee yeti, Ŋwani ti zina beere nɛreba ɛɛra daalehum? N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ ti, daalehum ka boi wan bo' zina beere nɛreba wa.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Bilam bilam ti buraa la tuba be'ehe, ti ka zɛleŋa lorege ti ka tana tɔgera suŋa suŋa.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Gee bahɛ ba gee le zom uureŋɔ yakɛ yakenɛ deyima ha.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Ti Yezu kagɛ ba ti ba da yele nɛra nɛra. Gee en ka'am ba se'em la, ba tugum malum yele gaŋɛ la bala.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Yezu zahereba la tamɛ mɛ ka tari boroboro zo'e na, gee tara la boroboro deyima ma'a uureŋɔ la puan.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Ti la ba' iŋɛ nɛreba pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Ti ba yɛta yeti, A ita a sɛla woo la suŋa. A bahɛ ti waŋa wum ti gɔɔŋɔ ta'na tɔgera suŋa suŋa.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Ti Yezu ka'am ba yeti, Tari ya ya mɛhi suŋa, mini ya Farasiiduma la Hɛrɔd du'urɔ la yele.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Ti ba yele taaba yeti, Tumam en ka tari boroboro la zuo ti a yɛta ŋwana wa.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Ti Yezu baŋɛ ban tɔgeri sɛla la, yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya tɔgera ya ka tari boroboro yele? Yamam kelum ka nyɛta bii bɔkera? Ya puti'ira la ka lebegere?.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: Yamam tari la nini gee i ka nyɛta, yamam tari la tuba gee ti a ka wuna? Yamam ka tɛri?.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: Mam daa fige boroboro banuu la bo' tuha anuu la, pitɔ tu lɛ ti ya daa doohe va pirɛ, ban daa di tigɛ gee bahɛ sɛla la? Ti ba yele en yeti, Pia la tuyi'.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Gee mam daa le fige boroboro bayopɔi bo' tuha anaahi la, pitɔ tu lɛ ti ya daa va pirɛ, ban daa di tigɛ gee bahɛ sɛla la? Ti ba yele en yeti, Pitɔ tuyopɔi.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ti Yamam kelum ka bɔkɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Ti Yezu la a zahereba la wa'am Bɛtesaada. Ti nɛreba basɛba tari fua wa'am a zi'an na, belum en ti a kalum en la a nu'o.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Ti Yezu gurɛ buraa wa nu'o, tari ka yehe ha timbila la puan nyaa ta tubehɛ nɔtuurum ma' fua la nini, gee dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ka zuo, gee soke en yeti, Fum nyɛti sɛla.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Ti buraa la gorege bihɛ gee yeti, N nyɛti nɛreba, gee ba ani mɛ wuu tiihi n kinɛ la.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Ti Yezu le dikɛ a nu'o kalum buraa la nini la, ti ka kpa biha zii ti ka fɔɔlum la bahɛ en, ti ka nyaa kɔ'ɔm nyɛta sɛla woo fa'a.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Ti Yezu keehe en yeti, kula da pun le doohe timbila la puan.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Ti Yezu ɛɛra la a zahereba la kiŋɛ Kasariya Filipi timbibehi puan, ti a ta soke a zahereba wa yeti, Nɛreba yeti mam dela ani.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la, ti sɛba me yeti, Elia, ti sɛba me kelum yeti, fum dela nɔtɔgeheba la ayima.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Ti a soke ba yeti, Gee yamam me yeti mam dela ani? Ti Pita lerege en yeti, Fum de Kiresta la.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Ti Yezu ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ yeti, ba da yele nɛra nɛra eŋa en de se'em.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ pa'ala a zahereba la yeti, Asaala Dayua wan doohe namehegɔ kãtɛ puan, ti pu'uhegɔ yire kiinduma, la malema kiinduma, la gɔnmi'ineba zagehɛ en, gee ti ba ku en. Gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e kum la puan.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: A tɔgɛ yele ana wa la peelumen, ŋwana ti Pita tari en lebe kinkɛleŋa ha, ta yele en la ninmu'urɛ yeti, Yinɛ gu' ti yele ana da paaɛ hɔ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Ti Yezu ŋmɛregɛ bihɛ a zahereba la gee tahɛ Pita yeti, Lebe pooren ha Sagena wa. Fum puti'irɛ ka boi Yinɛ yɛla puan gee di boi la asaala yɛla puan.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Gee nyaa yi' nɛrekuuŋɔ la, la a zahereba la, yele ba yeti, ya han bɔta ti ya dɛna mam zahereba, ya zagehɛ ya mɛhi gee dikɛ ya pumpuuredɔɔ zi dɔla ma.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en boti ba faaɛ ba vom wan koŋe bu, sɛba en ka gagɛ ba koŋe ba vom mam la en faarɛ yelehum wa zuo wan nyɛ bɔ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Beni yɔɔrɛ ti ba wan nyɛ, ba han nyɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo lɔgerɔ za'a gee koŋe ba nyɔvore?.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Nɛra wan sirum dikɛ la bem tee a vom?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
