local: Sɛba en zoti valum la mam la n yelehum wa, yaalegɔ la be'em buuri saŋa wa puan, mam Asaala Dayua wa wan ta tara valum la ba, n han ta lɛbera na la n Sɔ suŋa la ti n Sɔ fa'a suŋereba la dɔla mam la.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, ti yan ze' ka'lam wa, ya sɛba kan ki' gura ti ya nini ta nyɛ ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa wa'am na la paŋa.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Dabeha ayoobe n tole, ti Yezu tari Pita, Yemesi, la Yɔn ti ba zom zoore zuo ha ba ma'a. Ba nyɛ la Yezu iŋa n tee ba nɛŋan.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: ti a futo la nyagera gee pelege yeri yeri pelegere se'ere taaba ti nɛra ka bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo wan ta'am dikɛ sɛla pee lɔkɔ ti kɔ yehe paaɛ bala.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Ti ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Elia la Moose n bala tɔgera la Yezu.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Ti Pita yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, ka'lam bɔŋa ani suŋa bo' tu. Bala bahɛ ti tu ti' suga atã ka'lam, fum deyima, Moose deyima, la Elia me deyima.

eng: He said this because he didnt know how to respond, for the three of them were terrified.
local: Pita yeen daa ɛ baarena mɛ ka mina n yeti sɛla se'ere en sɔi la, dabeem n ba' kɛ̃' ba.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Ti sagbanɛ zibege na pi' ba, ti kua ze'ele sagbanɛ la puan tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Mam mɛŋa Dayua n ŋwana, se'em ti mam nɔŋɛ, kelehe ya en.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Bilam bilam ti ba limehe kaaɛ, ka nyɛ nɛra nɛra gee gee la Yezu ma'a.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Ban ze'ele zoore la zuo sigera na la, ti a ka'am ba, ti ba da yele nɛra nɛra sɛla ti ba nyɛ gura ti Asaala Dayua wa ta vo'e a kum la puan na.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Bala ti ba tara yele wa ba ma'a, ba suya puan gee nyaa ti'iha a vo'e a kum la puan vuurɛ en de sɛla.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti gɔnmi'ineba la yeti, Elia wan wa'am yia na.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Eliya en sirum wan wa'am yia na, la a malum ze'ele sɛla woo suŋa. Ŋwani ti ba ye gulehɛ yeti, Asaala Dayua la wan doohe namehegɔ zo'e zo'e puan tole ti ba zeem en?.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Gee mam yeti ya ti Elia pun wa'am mɛ na, ti nɛreba iŋɛ en ban boti se'em wuu ban pun gulehɛ a yele se'em la.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Ban ze'ele zoore la zuo sige na paaɛ Yezu zahereba basɛba la zi'an la, ba nyɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ n kaaɛ ba mum, ti gɔnmi'ineba la basɛba ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la ba.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la n nyɛ en la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ, ti ba zɔ tu'uhɛ en, pu'uhɛ en.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Beni nɔkpe'ene ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛra la ba.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Ti Nɛra ayima ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan lerege en yeti, Pa'ala, mam tari la n dayua wa wa'am fum zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, Kulekpa'bɛ'a n daani en ti a pun kan ta'am tɔgɛ.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa han ni ta gbirege en, a ni zɛkɛ en mɛ fɔbɛ timpaberɛ, ti nɔtɔ-fulunfuka gala, ti a ɔbera nyɛna, gee kuhɛ kankaŋi. Ti mam yele fum zahereba wa ti ba sa'ɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa, gee ba ka nyaŋɛ.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yamam akataresakerɛ saŋa-duma wa, mam wan bɔna la ya yuue la ŋwani? Mam wan doohe ya paaɛ la ŋwani beene? Tari ya en na bo' mam.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Ti ba tari bia la paaɛ a zi'an. Kulekpa'bɛ'a la en nyɛ Yezu la, a fɔbɛ bia la la timpaberɛ ti ka bilena kaara ti nɔtɔ-fulunfuka gala.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Ti Yezu soke bia la sɔ yeti, La daam bia wa yuue la ŋwani? Ti a yeti, La pɔhɛ en la a bumbilemen ha.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa ni ta'am zɛ en mɛ lobe bugum bii ko'om puan, zigera a sagum en. Gee fum han wan nyaŋɛ iŋɛ sɛla, zɔ tu nimbu'ɔ gee suŋɛ tɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum han wan nyaŋɛ! Sɛla woo wan tum bo' se'em daana n tari sakerɛ.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Bilam bilam ti bia la sɔ la kaahɛ buki yeti, N sakɛ ya, suŋɛ mam en bu'uri wa.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Yezu en nyɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la zoti na la, ti a tahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'a la yeti, Fum Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa n bahɛ ti bia wa ka tɔgera gee me ka wuna wa, mam yeti fum ti fum yehe a puan gee da le malum kɛ̃' en yɛ'ɛha.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Ti Kulekpa'bɛ'a la kaahɛ supi gee zɛkɛ bia la fɔbɛ timpaberɛ, gee nyaa yehe, gee ti bia la gã'a wuu kum la, ti nɛreba la zo'e zo'e yeti, Bia la ki mɛ.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Ti Yezu paaɛ gurɛ bia la nu'o, ve'e en ihege, ti a ta'am ze'ele.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Yezu en ta kɛ̃' yire la, ti a zahereba la ba ma'a soke en yeti, Ŋwani ti tumam ka ta'am sa'ɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'a la.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Kulekpa'bɛ'a kana taaba kan ta'am yehe han dagena pu'uhegɔ la nɔlurɛ.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Ti ba yehe bilam yɛ'ɛha, dɔla Galilii tɔla. Ti Yezu ka bɔta ti nɛra baŋɛ.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, a pa'ali la a zahereba la. A yele ba yeti, Ba wan koohe Asaala Dayua wa bo' saaleba ti ba ku en, ba han ku en, dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e kum la puan.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: Gee a zahereba la ka bɔkɛ pa'alegɔ kuna wa vuurɛ gee ti dabeem me tara ba la ba soke en.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Ti ba wa'am Kapɛnum na, ti Yezu ta bɔna yire, soke zahereba la yeti, Beni nɔkpe'ene ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛri ni sore la zuo.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Gee ba yee la suram, se'ere en sɔi la, ban boni sore la zuo la, ba ŋmɛ'ɛri ni la nɔkpe'ene la taaba doohe la se'em en gani za'a ba puan.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Ti Yezu zi'ire timpaberɛ, yi' a zahereba pia la bayi' la na yeti, Fum se'em woo en boti fum dɛna yiadaana dela fum bɔna pooren gee dɛna yameŋa bo' nɛra woo.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Ti a yi' bia wa'am ba nɛŋan na, nyɔkɛ bia la zãla a nu'uhen, gee yele ba yeti.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Fum se'em woo n to'e bia wuu kana wa mam yu'urɛ wa zuo, to'e la mam. Se'em woo n to'e mam me , ka to'e la mam ma'a, gee se'em daana n tum mam la na.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Yɔn yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, tumam nyɛ la nɛra ayima ti a yi'ira fum yu'urɛ sa'aha Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, ti tumam zigera ti tu gu' en, se'ere sɔi la, a ka dole tu.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, La dagi ya gu' en, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre se'em n yi' mam yu'urɛ wa tum kirega, kan ta'am kɔ'ɔm ŋmɛregɛ nananawa bii beere ha tɔgɛ mam yu'urɛ wa be'em.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Fum se'em woo n ka zaberɛ la tɔ, boi mɛ la tɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti yɛta ya wa, ti se'em n bo' ya ko'ma'ahega ti ya nyu, yan de Kiresta dinɛ la zuo, a daana yɔɔrɛ kan malum bɔregɛ la fii me.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Ya se'em han dikɛ bɛregɔ biŋe mam kɔma wa ayima n sakɛ bo' mam wa ti a lu, la wan ani suŋa ti ba dikɛ nɛɛrɛ lu' a yugela za'aɛ en bahɛ ko'omen ti a muhi.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Fum nu'o han tara fum tuna be'em, ŋmaaɛ kɔ bahɛ. La ana suŋa ti fum ta kɛ̃' vom kamba'ahɛ la puan la nuyiŋɔ, la nyani fum ta kɛ̃' bugum timpaberɛ la nu'uhi siyi zi'an ti bugum la ka kiŋera la.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Fum na'arɛ han tara fum tuna be'em, ŋmaaɛ dɛ. La ani suŋa ti fum ta kɛ̃' vom kamba'ahɛ la puan la na'yinɛ, nyana fum ta kɛ̃' bugum timpaberɛ la puan la nama ayi', zi'an ti bugum la ka kiŋera la.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Fum niho han tara fum tuna be'em, kpikɛ bu bahɛ. La ani suŋa ti fum ta kɛ̃' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan la ninyimɔ, nyana fum ta kɛ̃' bugum timpaberɛ la nini i yi'.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Zi'an ti zunzuya sɛ'a n diti ba la ka kiira, ti bugum sebo la me n diti ba ka malum kiŋera.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Ba wan magehɛ nɛreba la bugum bihɛ.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Yaarum ani suŋa mɛ, gee bu za'ahum han yehe, fum wan iŋɛ bu la ŋwani ti bu le za'ahum? Bahɛ ya ti ya vom ana wuu yaarum sumɔ la, gee boni ya la taaba la suma'ahum.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yezu bahɛ bilam mɛ, gee kiŋɛ Yudiya sɔ'ɔlum puan ta tole tinse'ehi n boi Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha la. Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ lagehɛ kaaɛ en. Ti a pa'ala wuu en ni pun ita se'em la.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Ti Farasiiduma basɛba wa'am a zi'an na ti ba makɛ en bihɛ, nyaa soke en yeti, La de sore ti buraa bahɛ a pɔga.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Moose nɔze'elego la puan yeti bu?.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Ti ba yeti, Moose nɔze'elego la puan bo' sore ti buraa gulehɛ pɔgelebehego gɔŋɔ bo' pɔka la gee nyaa ta'am lebehe ka.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe yele ba yeti, Ya sukãberɛ la zuo ti a gulehɛ nɔze'elego kuna wa bo' ya.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Gee pi'ileŋo puan na la, Yinɛ naam ba la buraa la pɔka.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Dina wa zuo, buraa wan bahɛ ka sɔ la ka ma, gee dikɛ ka mɛŋa naaɛ la ka pɔga.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: ti ba bayi' la lebege inganɛ deyima. Ba ka le dɛna nɛreba bayi' gee ba nyaa dela inganɛ deyima.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Bala zuo la, sɛla ti Yinɛ dikɛ lagum taaba, se'em de yelege.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Ti ba ta bɔna yire, ti a zahereba la le soke en yele wa.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: A yele ba yeti, Se'em woo lebehe a pɔga gee tee di kayima tum la kabohi baha pɔgesɛka ti a bahɛ yia la zuo.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Pɔka me han bahɛ ka asira gee tee ele buraa kayima, pɔka la me tum la kabohi.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Ti nɛreba tara ba kɔmbibehi kina Yezu zi'an na, ti a dikɛ a nu'o pagelɛ ba zuo, ti a zahereba la tahera ba la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Gee Yezu en nyɛ ŋwana la, a suure n nyiie, ti a yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti kɔmbibehi wa wa'am n zi'an na. Da gu'uri ya ba. Se'ere en sɔi la,Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la dela nɛreba n ani wuu kɔmbibehi wa dinɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ ti fum se'em ka to'e Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la wuu bia la, kan malum kɛ̃' bini.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Gee nyaa kɔkɛ kɔma la, gee dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto kaaɛ, Purɛ sum bahɛ ba zuto.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Yezu bahɛ bilam mɛ, gee bɔna sore zuo yɛ'ɛha, ti buraa zɔ paaɛ en, kpa duma a nɛŋan, soke en yeti, Pa'alesunkɔ, beni en de n iŋɛ la n ta'am nyɛ vom kamba'ahɛ wa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum yi'ira mam nɛresunkɔ? Nɛra ka boi dɛna sunkɔ han dagena Yinɛ ma'a.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Fum mi' nɔze'elego la, Da ku nɛra, da tum kabohi, da zu, da dɛna pumparema aze'nyɛdaana, da fã nɛra sɛla, na'aha fum sɔ la fum ma. .

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ti buraa la yele en yeti, Pa'ala, mam pun dɔla yele ana za'a mɛ ninmu'urɛ n biilemen na.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ en, ti a suure kɛ̃' en, ti a yele en yeti, la gee la yele deyina ma'a ti fum iŋɛ, kiŋɛ ta koohe sɛlawoo ti fum tara la, gee dikɛ ligeri la bo' naheduma, gee fum wan nyɛ lɔgerɔ saazuo ha. Fum han iŋɛ bala, fum nyaa wa'am na dɔla mam.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Buraa wa n wum ŋwana la, la kɔ'ɔhɛ la a giila paaɛ paaɛ, ti a kiŋɛ la susageŋɔ paaɛ paaɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, a lɔgerɔ la ba' zo'e mɛ.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la kaaɛ gee yele a zahereba la yeti, Bihɛ la kpe'em se'em ti buntateba ta kɛ̃' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ti a zahereba la ya' nuurɛ la yetɔgebuna wa. Gee Yezu le yele ba yeti, N kɔma, la kpe'em mɛ ti nɛra nyaŋɛ kɛ̃' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan!.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: La de naana bo' yugenɛ ti a doohe fupiho yoko puan tole, gana buntata ta'am kɛ̃' Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Ti la ba' iŋɛ a zahereba la pakerɛ, ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, ani yi wan nyɛ faarɛ?.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ ba kaaɛ gee yeti, Asaala zi'an ti la kpe'em gee dagi Yinɛ zi'an. Yinɛ zi'an sɛla ka boi kpe'em.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Ti Pita pɔhɛ yɛta en yeti, Bihɛ, tumam bahɛ tu sɛla woo mɛ gee wa'am na dɔla fum.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, nɛra ka boi bahɛ a yire, a suurɔ, a ma, a sɔ, bii a kɔma bii a timpaberɛ mam zuo la faarɛ yelehum wa zuo.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: gee kan nyɛ ba kɔbehi kɔbehi timpaberɛ wa zuo. A daana wan malum nyɛ yiya, suurɔ, maduma, kɔma, la vatɔ gee me nyɛ namehegɔ pa'ahɛ, gee ti ba'ahegɔ saŋa han ta paaɛ, a daana wan nyɛ vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Gee ya mina suŋa ti a tole nɛŋa duma wan ta yee pooren ti a yee pooren ta tee tole nɛŋa.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Ba boi la sore zuo yɛ'ɛha zɔna Yerusalɛm, ti a zahereba la ta bihɛ ti Yezu boi la ba nɛŋa ha, ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ, ti dabeem kɛ̃' sɛba me en dole ba la. Ti a tari a zahereba pia la bayi' la lebe kinkɛleŋa pɔhɛ yɛta ba le yɛ'ɛha sɛla en wan iŋɛ en.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: A yele ba yeti, Biheri ya, tu ye' zɔna la Yerusalɛm wa, ba wan ta nyɔkɛ Asaala Dayua wa bo' malema nɛŋa duma, la gɔnmi'ineba la, ti ba gakɛ en ti a dela a ki. Gee nyaa dikɛ en bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: Ti ba ɛregɛ en gee tubehɛ nɔtuurum ma' en, gee ŋmɛ' en, gee ku en. Gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e a kum la puan.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Yemesi la Yɔn en de Zebedii dayɔɔhi la tole la nɛŋa na ze'ele Yezu nɛŋan yele en yeti, Pa'ala, tu boti fum iŋɛ la sɛla woo ti tu wan yele fum wa bo' tu.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam boti n iŋɛ la bem bo' ya.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Tumam boti la fum han ta zi'a fum suŋa na'am gɔŋɔ la zuo, fum bahɛ ti tumam ayima ta zi'a fum zuɔ, ti ayima me zi'a fum gɔbega.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Ya ka mi' sɛla ti ya sɔkera wa vuurɛ. Yamam wan ta'am nyu namehegɔ wanɛ se'ere puan ti mam yeti n nyu wa? Bii ya ta'am sɔ namehegɔ malema sua sɛka ti mam yeti n sɔ wa.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti Tu wan ta'am sɔ, ti Yezu yele ba yeti, ya sirum wan ta'am nyu namehegɔ wanɛ se'ere ti mam yeti n nyu wa, gee me wan sirum ta'am sɔ namehegɔ malema sua sɛka ti mam yeti n sɔ wa la.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: gee ya zi'ire mam zuɔ bii mam gɔbega nu'o bɔba, dagi mam n wan bo' ya sore, zɛ'sina dela ban maahum se biŋe sɛba la dinɛ.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Yezu zahereba pia la n wum ŋwana la, ti ba suya pɔhɛ nyiira la Yemesi la Yɔn.

eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Bala ti Yezu yi' ba, yele ba yeti, Ya mi' ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la puan, ba na'duma ni kɔ'ɔm dikɛ la ba mɛhi ɔ̃belɛ ba, ti sɛba me en de ba nɛrekãreba me tari ba vɛgena.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Gee la ka ani bala yamam puan. Gee se'em woo n boti a dɛna ya nɛrekãtɛ, a daana lebege ya za'a yameŋa.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: ya puan se'em me han bɔta ti a dɛna ya nɛŋa daana, a daana lebege ya za'a da'aberɛ.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Gee Asaala Dayua ka wa'am na ti ba tuna bɔ'ɔra en mɛ, gee a tugum tuna bɔ'ɔra nɛreba, pun ta dikɛ a vom kaabɛ faaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e bahɛ.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Ba wa'am la Yeriko na. Ti Yezu la a zahereba, la nɛrekuuŋɔ la en ta bahera Yeriko la, ti Timiyɔsi dayua Batimiyɔsi en de fua suhera la en zi' sore la nuuren.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: En wum ti Yezu en yehe Nazareti na n ye' tɔla la, ti a pɔhɛ nyabera yeti, Yezu, Deviti Dayua wa, zɔ n nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e tahɛ en la ninmu'urɛ yeti, go'e fum voole la. Ti a tugum voole gaŋɛ bala yeti, Deviti Dayua wa, zɔ n nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Ti Yezu teke su'u gee yeti, Yi' ya en bo' mam na. Ti ba yi' fua la yele yeti, bahɛ ti fum puurɛ pee, Ihege, a yi'iri fum mɛ.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Bala ti a lobe fuwoko la ti a yɛ la bahɛ, gee vaaɛ ihege kiŋɛ paaɛ Yezu.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Fum boti n iŋɛ la beni bo' fum? Ti fua wa yele en yeti, N pa'ala, bahɛ ti n nini wa pikɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Kina, fum sakerɛ wa n tibɛ hɔ. Bilam bilam ti buraa wa nini la pikɛ ti a nyaa dɔla Yezu sore la zuo.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Ban ta pɔra Yerusalɛm, nan bɔna Bɛtefege la Bɛtani tihi, lɛm la Olive zoore la, ti a tum a zahereba bayi'.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya timbila wa en boi ya nɛŋan ha wa puan, ya han ta kɔ'ɔm kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra timpaberɛ la puan, ya wan nyɛ ti bumbila lu' zɛ'a, ti nɛra ka tabelɛ ban ka, lorege ya ka na bo' mam.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Nɛra han soke ya yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ita bala? Yeen yele en yeti, Daana la n boti ka, gee ba wan tari ka lebe kalam na! .

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Ti a pɔhɛ tara mageha tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la yeti, Buraa n burɛ ka tiihi wala va'am, mɛ nangoone kaaɛ va'am la mum, tu' daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko ku puan, gee mɛ limehego zi'an va'am la puan, nyaa dikɛ bo' gureba, gee kiŋɛ timpaberɛ kayima n zaaɛ ha.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Ti ba kiŋɛ ta nyɛ ti bumbila ti ka lu' zɛ'a zanɔren samanɛ zuo. Ba n boi bini lɔregera buŋa wa la.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Ti dia la go'ohego saŋa ta paaɛ, ti a tum a yameŋa ti a kiŋɛ gureba la zi'an ta to'e a tɔtɛ na bo' en.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: ti sɛba n ze' gee nyɛ la basɛba soke ba yeti, Beni ti ya ita bala, ya loregere la buŋa la.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Ti gureba wa nyɔkɛ yameŋa la ŋmɛ' ka, gee sa'ɛ ka ti ka lebe la nu'bɛɛla.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la yele nɛreba la Yezu n yele ba se'em la, ti nɛreba la bo' sore ti ba tari buŋa la kiŋɛ.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Ti a le tum a yameŋa kayima ba zi'an ti ba gi' yameŋa kaŋa me, ŋmɛ' aaɛ ka zuo gee yaalɛ en.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Ba tari buŋa la wa'am na bo' Yezu, gee digehɛ ba futo pagelɛ buŋa la zuo, ti Yezu zom yagelɛ ka zuo.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Ti a le tum yameŋa kayima, ti ba gi' yameŋa kana kɔ'ɔm ku en. Ba iŋɛ yamehi se'ehi woo ti a tum la la bala, ba ŋmɛ' basɛba mɛ, gee ku basɛba.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Ti Nɛreba zo'e zo'e tara ba futo yarena sore la zuo, ti sɛba me aahum tiihi vɔtɔ seto woo ti ba nyɛ me tari ti Yezu nɛ tɔla.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Gee va'am daana wa kelum tara la nɛra yima en de a mɛŋa dayɔba'arɛ. Ba'ahegɔ za'a a tum la kaŋa gee yeti, Ena en de mam dayua la, ba wan na'ahɛ en.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Ti sɛba n boi nɛŋa, la sɛba n boi pooren la woo vɔɔla yeti, Hozaana! Sum bɔna la fum se'em n dikɛ Daana wa yu'urɛ wa'am na!.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Gee gureba wa en ta nyɛ ti va'am daana wa mɛŋa dayua la kini na la, ti ba yele taaba yeti, Ɛhɛɛ asooesɔna n bala kini na wa. Wa'am ya na ti tu ku en gee ti lɔgerɔ la dɛna tumam dinɛ.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Sum bɔna la tu sɔyaaba Deviti sɔ'ɔlum la n wa'am na la! Hozaana dɛna saa zuin zuin ha dinɛ.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Bala ti ba nyɔkɛ en, ku en dikɛ en lobe bahɛ va'am la yiŋa.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Ti Yezu paaɛ Yerusalɛm timpaberɛ, kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃' pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, bihɛ sɛla woo kaaɛ. Bala ma'a ti yinɛ pun lu mɛ. Ti a yehe la a zahereba pia la bayi', kiŋɛ Bɛtani Tiŋa.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Va'am daana la nyaa wan iŋɛ la ŋwani? A wan kiŋɛ ta ku gureba la gee dikɛ va'am la tee bo' basɛba.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Yiimbeere, ban ze'ele Bɛtani timpaberɛ lebe na la, ti kom tara Yezu.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Ya ka kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la ze'kana wa en yeti, Kugese'ere ti mɛta zagehɛ la kugedinɛ en ta dɛna ɛberɛ kugere.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Ti a bihɛ nyɛ ti kinkaŋa ze' zaaɛ zaarɛ ha la vuurɔ vipi, ti a yɛ'ɛha ka timpaberɛ ti a bihɛ a wan nyɛ sɛla ka zuo. Gee n paaɛ la, a ka nyɛ sɛla ka zuo han dagena vɔtɔ ma'a, se'ere en sɔi la, la dagi mu'uro waŋa saŋa.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Daana n tum de bala, ti la dɛna yelepakerɛ bo' tu..

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Ti a yele kinkaŋa la yeti, Nɛra kan malum di mu'uro fum zuo yɛ'ɛha. Ti a zahereba la wum n yele se'em la.

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Ban ta baŋɛ ti a makɛ makerɛ wa bo' la bamam la, ti ba bɔta ti ba nyɔkɛ en, gee dabeem n tari ba la nɛrekuuŋɔ la ti ba bahɛ en gee kiŋɛ.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Ti ba wa'am na paaɛ Yerusalɛm, kɛ̃' pu'uhegɔ yire la puan. Ti Yezu pɔhɛ sa'aha sɛba n koohe la sɛba n da'ari lɔgerɔ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la, a tulege Ligetɛɛhereba teebula kpabɛ gee lobe sɛba n zi' kɔɔha dayɛma la kugehi bahɛ.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Ti ba le tum Farasii la Hɛrɔd kpɛ'ɛnduma Yezu zi'an na, ti ba bɛ̃rɛ a nuurɛ yetɔgum puan.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Gee gu' ti se'em da zɛ' lɔkɔ doohe pu'uhegɔ yire la puan tole.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Ti ba wa'am na yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, tumam mi' ti fum dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, gee me ka yɛleha nɛreba, se'ere en sɔi la fum ka to'ohe nɛreba la ninkɔrɛ, gee fum pa'ali Yinɛ sore la me ti la dɔla la yelemɛŋerɛ. La de sore ti tu yɔɔra kuba bɔ'ɔra Siiza bii tu da yɔɔra bɔ'ɔra en?.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Ti a nyaa pa'ala nɛreba la yɛta yeti, La ka gulehɛ biŋe yeti, Mam yire wa wan dinɛ la pu'uhegɔ yire bo' tihi woo? Gee ti yamam dikɛ dɛ lebege fãareba zi'an.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Tu yɔɔra bii tu da yɔɔra? Gee Yezu en baŋɛ ba birikɔgenɛ la, a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya makera mam? Bo' ya mam lagekɔberɛ na ti n bihɛ.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Gee Malema kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la n wum ŋwana la, ba mu'ɛ la nini ɛɛra ban wan iŋɛ se'em ku en, gee ti dabeem me tara ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, la iŋɛ nɛreba la za'a la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ la a pa'alegɔ la.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Ti ba dikɛ lagekɔberɛ bo' en, ti a yele ba yeti, Ani naanɛ n ŋwana? La ani gulega n boi bu zuo? Ti ba lerege en yeti, Siiza.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Ti yinɛ ma'ɛ, ti Yezu la a zahereba la bahɛ timpaberɛ la puan.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Dikɛ ya sɛla en de Siiza dinɛ bo' en gee dikɛ sɛla en de Yinɛ dinɛ la me bo' en. Ti leregere la ba' iŋɛ ba pakerɛ.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Bulika ti ba yɛ'ɛha tɔla la, ba nyɛ ti kinkaŋa la ko'ɛ mɛ ta paaɛ ka nyɛga.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Sadusii sɛba n ka sakɛ ti nɛra han ki saŋa kini na ti a wan vo'e a kum la puan la, bamam basɛba n wa'am Yezu zi'an na soke en sokere yeti.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Ti Pita tiɛ nyaa yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, bihɛ! Kinkaŋa la ti fum pɔ bahɛ ka zuo la ki mɛ.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Pa'ala, Moose gulehɛ nɔze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan bo' tu yeti, Buraa suuɔ han ki gee gee a pɔga, ti ba me ka tara bia, a suuɔ la di pokuurɛ la dɔgɛ kɔma purɛ a suuɔ la yu'urɛ.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Yezu lerege ba yeti, Tari ya sakerɛ la Yinɛ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: La wa iŋɛ mɛ ti buraahi siyopɔi bɔna dɛna suurɔ, ti kiima la di pɔga, ka dɔgɛ gee ki.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ, ya han yele zoore wa yeti, ihege ka'lam, ta lu ateko wa puan, gee ka bu'ura ya suya puan, gee nam ti fum yele se'ere la wan tum, la sirum wan wa'am na tum.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Ti se'em n dole poore la sooe di pokuurɛ la, me ki gee me ka tara bia, ti se'em n tagelɛ butã la me iŋɛ bala mɛa.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Bala, n yeti ya mɛ ti sɛla woo ti ya suhɛ pu'uhegɔ puan, nam ya ti ya to'e ya, ti wan dɛna ya dinɛ.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: Suurɔ tuyopɔi wa za'a, ba se'em ka makɛ dɔgɛ la pɔka wa. Ti la bɔna bɔna ti pɔka la me wa ki.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Fum han ni yeti fum pu'uhɛ gee tara la se'ere la se'em di sugeri bo' en. Bala ti fum Sɔ la en boi saazuo la me wan di fum sugeri bo' fum me tuure. .

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Kum vo'ore wa han ta bɔna, ba ani en wan ta sɔna pɔka la? Se'ere en sɔi la ba za'a di ka soohum mɛ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Ti Yezu la a zahereba la le wa'am Yerusalɛm na. Ti Yezu n ye' kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra pu'uhegɔ yire puan la, ti Malema kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la, la pu'uhegɔ yire kiinduma wa'am Yezu zi'an na.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ɛhɛɛ! Ŋwana n pa'alɛ ti ya tuue mɛ gee me pun ka mina Yinɛ tuulum gɔŋɔ la puan bii Yinɛ paŋa la.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: yele en yeti, Beni kpe'eŋo ti fum tara tuna kirehi sina wa za'a? Ani n bo' fum sore ti fum tuna yele ana wa.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba han ta vo'e ba kum puan pɔgedire bii sire-ele kan ta bɔna. Nɛreba ta yeti ba ana mɛ wuu Yinɛ suŋereba en boi saazuo ha la.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Yezu yele ba yeti, N wan soke ya sokere deyima ti ya me lerege mam, ti mam me yele ya kpe'eŋo seko ti n tara tuna yele ana wa.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: La han le dɛna la kum vo'ore la yele ya ka kãalɛ sɛla en boi Moose nɔze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan, doohe tuntuo la n yuum taaɛ bugum la. Yinɛ yuum yele Moose yeti, Mam en de Aberaham Yinɛ, Aziki Yinɛ, la Yakɔbe Yinɛ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Yɔn ko'om sua la ze'ele la saazuo na bii asaala puti'irɛ? Yele ya mam na.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Yinɛ wa dela sɛba n vɔi la Yinɛ gee dagi sɛba ki Yinɛ. Bala ya ba' tuue mɛ.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Ti ba nan lebe ta ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la taaba yeti, Tu han yeti, la ze'ele la saazuo na, a wan yele tu yeti ŋwani ti tu ka sakɛ bo' Yɔn.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba la puan ayima wa'am na pɔra, wum ban ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la taaba gee me wum ti Yezu lebehe ba suŋa la ti a soke Yezu yeti, Yinɛ nɔze'elego la puan kuni en gani za'a?.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Gee tu me kan ta'am yeti, Bu dela asaala puti'irɛ. Dabeem n tari ba la nɛrekuuŋɔ la se'ere en sɔi la, nɛrekuuŋɔ la woo sakɛ ti Yɔn dela Yinɛ nɔtɔgeha yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Se'ere en gani za'a dela, Kelehe ya suŋa Izirale buuri wa. Daana la en de tu Yinɛ la dela ayima.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Bala ti ba lerege Yezu yeti, Tu ka mi'. Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam me kan yele ya kpe'eŋo seko ti mam tara tuna kirehi wa.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Nɔŋɛ fum Daana Yinɛ la fum suure za'a, la fum sia za'a, la fum puti'irɛ la fum paŋa za'a.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Se'ere en tagelɛ buyi' la dela, fum nɔŋɛ fum suuɔ wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛŋa se'em la. Nɔze'elego ka le bɔna gana ana ayi' wa.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Ti gɔnmina wa yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala fum tɔgɛ la asira ti Yinɛ dela deyina. Yinɛ se'ere ka boi han dagena dinɛ ma'a.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Gee me nɔŋɛ Yinɛ la fum suure za'a, la fum bɔkerɛ za'a, la fum paŋa za'a, gee nɔŋɛ fum suuɔ wuu fum mɛŋa la. Ŋwana tari tuulum gana daarɛ woo ti fum nyɔ'ɔra dunmuma gee kaabera.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Yezu en wum ti buraa wa lebehe la yɛm la, ti a yele en yeti, Fum ka zaaɛ la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la. Bilam pooren nɛra ka le sakɛ soke en sokere.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Yezu ta bɔna la pu'uhegɔ yire puan pa'ala, a yeti, Ŋwani ti gɔnmi'ineba la yeti, Kiresta dela Deviti Dayua?.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Fa'a Sia la yuum bahɛ ti Deviti purɛ yeti, Daana la yele mam Daana yeti, Zi'ire mam zuɔ nu'o bɔba wa na, gura ti n bahɛ ti fum dinduma lebege fum na'tagelega. .

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Deviti a mɛŋa yi' en la a Daana, ŋwani ti a nyaa wan dɛna Deviti dayua? Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la kɛleha en la pupeelum.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: Yezu en boi bini pa'ala la, a ta yele ba yeti, Mini ya gɔnmi'ineba wa, ba dela sɛba en nɔŋɛ ba yɛɛra fuwogero ɛɛra kaara, gee ti nɛreba pu'uhera ba gee na'ahera ba da'ahi puan.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Gee me nɔŋɛ ba zi'ita nɛŋa kugehi zuto zamehegɔ yiya la puan, la nɛrezura zi'irego zi'ihi de'enkãra puan.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba faarɛ la pokɔpa lɔgerɔ, gee pu'uha pu'uhewogero, ti nɛreba nyɛ ba. Ba wan to'e sageŋɔ seko n ka tari makerɛ.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Yezu zi' tɔ la bo'olum pi'ɔ la en ze zi'an la, gee biha nɛrekuuŋɔ la en tari ligeri kina ita pi'ɔ la puan la. Ti buntateba zo'e zo'e tara bɔbena kina ita pi'ɔ la puan.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Ti Yezu nyɛ ti pɔkɔ-naha paaɛ bini dikɛ a ligekɔba ayi' n wan dɛna wuu kɔbega tari na iŋɛ bini.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Ti Yezu yi' a zahereba la yele ba yeti, n yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, pɔkɔ-naha wa n bo' gana sɛba woo n tari ba bo'olum na iŋɛ pi'ɔ la puan la.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba za'a yehe la sɛla fifihe ba ligekãra la puan tari na bo' la, gee pɔka wa la a nɔŋɔ la za'a, ka bo' la sɛla ti ka tara en bala, ka bo' la sɛla za'a ti ka tara ti ka di n bala.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Yezu en ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan kɔ'ɔm yehe na la, ti a zahereba la ayima soke en yeti, Pa'ala, bihɛ kugekãra sɛ'a ti ba dikɛ mɛ dekãtɛ seko taaba wa.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Ya nyɛ dikãra wa? Kugere la deyina me ka yee paga de tadaana zuo, ba za'a wan ta lu.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Yezu en ta zi'a Olive Zoore zuo tɔ pu'uhegɔ yire la, ti Pita, Yemesi, Yɔn, la Anduru ta soke en ba ma'a.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Yele tu, ŋwani ma'a ti ŋwana wan ta iŋɛ, daalehum boni en wan pa'alɛ ti saŋa la paaɛ ya.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ yɛta ba yeti, Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ti se'em da pa'ahɛ ya.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan ta wa'am na dikɛ mam yu'urɛ tɔgera yɛta ti, Mam en de Kiresta la! Ba wan pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba bɔregɛ.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Ya han ta wuna zaba yɛla la zaba yelebibehi, da bahɛ ya ti ya paleŋa ɛgera, gee yele ana wan ba'ahum wa'am na gee dagi ba'ahegɔ saŋa la en bala.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, timpaberɛ kayima nɛreba wan ihege tɛ' pɛɛma la timpaberɛ kayima nɛreba, ti Na'duma zabera la taaba, tinmiiŋo wan sige tihi zo'e zo'e zuo, ti kom pɛɛlega sige, gee ŋwana wa woo nan dela too la saŋa dɔgum pɔhega en nan bala.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Gee han dɛna yamam mɛhi, mini ya suŋa suŋa gee ba wan ta nyɔgɛ ya kiŋɛ yele gakereba la zi'an, ba wan ŋmɛ' ya zamehegɔ yiya la puan, ya wan ta ze'ele tihi na'kãreba la na'duma nɛŋan mam zuo, ti la dɛna sɛla en iŋɛ gã'a.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Gee faarɛ yelehum wa wan diŋɛ nɛŋa muulɛ paaɛ tihi woo.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Ba han ta nyɔgɛ ya kiŋɛ yele gakereba zi'an da fabele ya, ya wan ta tɔgɛ se'em. Gee yeen yele ya se'ere woo n wa'am ya nuuren na sankaŋa puan la, se'ere en sɔi la, la dagi yamam n tɔgeri, gee la dela Fa'a Sia la.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Suuɔ wan ta koohe suuɔ tihɛ ti ba ku en, ti sɔ me koohe bia tihɛ, kɔma wan zabɛ la ba dɔgereba ta bahɛ ti ba ku ba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Nɛra woo wan ta yaaɛ ya yan dole mam la zuo. Gee fum se'em n ŋmibe ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ, wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Ya han ta nyɛ ti ba dikɛ galeŋɔ lɔkɔ la en tari sageŋɔ paaɛ la, ze'ele ze'sɛka ti ku ka suna ti ku zɛ'a la, , bala ma'a la, sɛba woo en boi Yudiya zɔ zom zɔya la zuto.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Se'em woo en yage gɔhegɔ da sige kɛ̃' bɔ'en ti a dikɛ sɛla.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Se'em en boi va'am me da lebe kule yire ti a dikɛ fuo.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Yele boi pɔgepuuheduma la pɔgese'ehi en tari bilɛɛhɛ ti si mɔgera dabeha ana puan!.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Pu'uhi ya mɛ ti yele dina da wa'am ɔɔrɔ saŋa na.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dabeha aŋa ta yeti ka dɛna la kumpiinɔ seto n ka tabelɛ bɔna Yinɛ n pi'ilum timpaberɛ wa maalegɔ na la, gee me pun kan le malum bɔna.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Daana la han ka ŋmaaɛ ni dabeha aŋa girega, nɛrebeeleho ka boi n wan vo'e. Gee nɛresunduma la zuo, sɛba ti a mɛŋa looe la zuo, ti a ŋmaaɛ dabeha aŋa girega.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Sankana wa puan, nɛra han yele fum yeti bihɛ Kiresta boi ke bii a boi ke ha, da sakɛ ya.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta pumpã'aheduma la nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma wan ta wa'am na tuna pa'ahegɔ kirehi ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba bɔregɛ. La han wan ta'am ni iŋɛ ni, ba wan pã'ahɛ ni sɛba ti Yinɛ pun looe la.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Gee bihɛ ya ya mɛhi suŋa gee mam pun yele ya sɛla woo ti didaarɛ nan ka paaɛ.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Gee dabeha aŋa puan, namehegɔ saŋa la han ta tole, wuntɛɛŋa wa wan lebege lika ti ŋmarega kan niiɛ.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: ti ŋmarebibehi wan ze'ele saazuo luta na, ti pahi se'ehi en boi zuo ha wan gbirege.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Bala ma'a la, nɛreba nyaa wan nyɛ ti Asaala Dayua la boi la sagbama puan kina na la paŋa en ka tari makerɛ la suŋa.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: A nyaa wan tum a suŋereba ti ba doohe timpaberɛ wa zuo luga anaahi la za'a lagehɛ nɛresɛba woo ti a lugehe ba la, ze'ele timpaberɛ wa tɛka beene ta paaɛ zuo ha.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Bala, zamehi ya yɛm kinkaŋa puan. Kinkaŋa han ta tara yilipaala, pɔhɛ puhera vɔpaala, ya mi' ti siiɔ pɔri.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Bala me, ti ya han ta nyɛta ti yele ana iti, ya mina ti n pɔri na, n kɔ'ɔm bɔna la ya kuleŋa nuuren.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, sankana nɛreba wa kan ki ba'ahɛ gee ti yele ana wa'am na tole.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuo la timpaberɛ wan tole gee mam yelehum wa la bu fii me kan malum tole.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Gee han dɛna dabeherɛ la bii saŋa la, nɛra nɛra ka mi'. Suŋereba n pun bɔna saazuo ha ka mi' bii Dayua la, gee Sɔ la ma'a n mi'.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Mini ya, gee maahum ya, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ka mi' saŋa la n ye' se'em ma'a na.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: La yeti la ta ana mɛ wuu nɛra n ye' sore gee dikɛ a yire gu'ule a yamehi, bo' nɛra woo la a tuunɛ a tuunɛ gee yele zanɔgura ti a biha yire la suŋa suŋa.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Dinɛ zuo la, da gihere ya, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ka mi' sansɛka ti yire daana la wan wa'am na, zaanuurɛ, yu'uŋɔ, nɔdɔɔ kaahega bii bulika.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: han dagena bala, ya wan ta giha ti a wa'am na di'e ya.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Mam yeti ya se'ere wa bala mɛa ti n yɛta bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo, Biheri ya.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: La wa gee la dabeha ayi' la azɔɛtole la boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la paaɛ. Malema kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la, ɛɛra ba wan iŋɛ se'em nyɛ Yezu tuure la ba ta'am nyɔkɛ en ku.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Ba yele taaba yeti, Tu kan nyɔkɛ en azɔɛtole daarɛ la, tu han iŋɛ bala nɛrekuuŋɔ la wan gbirege la tɔ.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Beere n ta yilegera la, bilam bilam ti malema kiinduma lagum la kiinduma za'a la gɔnmi'ineba la, maahum ba puti'irɛ, nyaa lu' Yezu nu'uhi tari en ta bo' Palete.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yezu en ta bɔna Bɛtani Simɔn se'em iŋa n wa tabelɛ sagum la yire la, ti Yezu lagum zi'a la nɛreba yire la puan, dia saŋa ti pɔka tara dugela la kaam en tari nyuunsuŋɔ ti bu da'a kpe'em, ti ba yi'ira bu alabasita kaam wa'am na ka'ɛ yeele a zuo.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ti Palete soke Yezu yeti, Fum n de Yuuduma naba la? Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, La dela fum yele se'em n bala.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Ti nɛreba basɛba boi bini ti ba suure nyiie, ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Ŋwani ti pɔka wa sagum kaam wa bahɛ bala?.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Ti Malema nɛheduma parum yɛla zo'e zo'e bo' Yezu.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Kaam wa wan ta'am dikɛ ni koohe ti ligeri la paaɛ wuu ŋmarehi sitã yɔɔrɛ, dikɛ ni bo' naheduma. Ti ba nyaa zɛregɛ pɔka la.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Bala ti Palete soke en yeti, Fum kan lerege? Bihɛ ban yele yɛla zo'e zo'e se'em bahɛ fum zuo.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya en bããlam ŋwani ti ya daana en? A tum la sɛla n ani suŋa bo' mam.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Gee Yezu ka lebehe en se'ere, ti la iŋɛ Palete pakerɛ.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, naheduma la wan kelum bɔna la ya saŋa woo, ti se'em ma'a ti ya bɔta ya ta'am iŋɛ sum bo' ba. Gee mam kan bɔna la ya saŋa woo.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Azɔɛtole de'eŋo saŋa woo, Palete ni yehe la nɛra ayima paka deo la bahɛ, ban soke en ti a yehe se'em woo.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Pɔka wa tum la sɛla ti ka wan ta'am tum, a te'ele bi'ihɛ la mam iŋa wa gura ka pi'a.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Bala buraa kayima yu'urɛ n wa yi'ira Barabase bɔna paka deo la a tuunfi'iha taaba, n tum tuunfi'iha ta ta' la nɛreba kua tingbiregere zaberɛ puan la.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, zi'a woo timpaberɛ wa zuo, ti ba ta muula faarɛ yelehum wan tiiha tɔgera sɛla ti ka iŋɛ wa yele.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Bala ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lagehɛ na pɔhɛ yɛta Palete yeti, tum fum ni tuunɛ se'em la bo' tu.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Ti Yuuduma Isikariyɔte se'em n lagum pa'ahɛ Yezu zahereba pia la bayi' la, kiŋɛ malema kiinduma zi'an ti a koohe Yezu bo' ba.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Ti Palete soke ba yeti, Ya boti n yehe la Yuuduma naba la bo' ya.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Ban wum Yudasi puti'irɛ la, ba puurɛ pee mɛ, ti ba biŋe nuurɛ ti ba bo' en ligeri. Bala ti Yudasi nyaa malum ɛɛra sose'ere ti a wan doohe koohe Yezu.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Palete mi' ti la dela nintɛrega zuo ti Malema kiinduma la nyɔkɛ Yezu wa'am na bo' en.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Boroboro zaŋa dia de'eŋo pɔhega daarɛ la, azɔɛtole pebila la kaabegɔ saŋa la, ti Yezu zahereba la yele en yeti, Bɛha ti fum bɔta ti tu kiŋɛ ta maahum bo' fum ti fum di azɔɛtole la.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Gee Malema kiinduma la nyaŋɛ pa'ahɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la mɛ ti ba yeti, ba boti la a tugum yehe Barabase bo' ba.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Bala ti a tum a zahereba bayi', yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya timpaberɛ la puan, ya wan ta nyɛ buraa n ziiri yoore la ko'om, dole ya en.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Palete le tɔgɛ la ba yeti, Gee ya boti n iŋɛ buraa wa ti ya yi'ira Yuuduma naba wa la ŋwani.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Ti en ta kɛ̃' yise'ere puan la, ya kɛ̃' bini la ya soke yidaana la yeti, Pa'ala la yeti, bɔ'ɔ kuni ti mam la n zahereba la wan kɛ̃' bini di azɔɛtole dia la.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Ti ba voole bahɛ na yeti, Kpa' ya en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: A wan pa'alɛ ya bɔkãtɛ en boi a yire la zuo, pun maahum zɛ'a maahum ya sɛla woo bo' tu.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Ti Palete soke ba yeti, Ŋwani, a iŋɛ la beni taalɛ? Ti ba malum voole gaŋɛ bala yeti, Kpa' ya en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Bala ti a zahereba la yehe kiŋɛ timpaberɛ la puan ta nyɛ sɛla woo wuu en pun yele ba se'em la. Ti ba nyaa maahum azɔɛtole dia la sɛla woo bilam.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Palete n boti a iŋɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la puurɛ pee la zuo ti a yehe Barabase bo' ba, gee bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ Yezu, gee nyaa dikɛ Yezu bo' ba ti ba tari ta kpa' en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Yinɛ ta ma'ɛ, ti Yezu la a zahereba pia la bayi' la wa'am yire la puan.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Ti Pɛntiireba la tari Yezu kɛ̃' naba zinzaka la puan gee nyaa yi' taaba lagehɛ, ba tansuga la za'a.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Ti ban zi'ire ba'ahɛ dita la, ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti ya puan ayima en yeti a koohe mam. Se'em en naaɛ la mam dita wa.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Ti ba dikɛ fumɔlegɔ yɛ Yezu gee pã'am gu'uhi muŋa vuge a zuo.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Ti ba suure sagum ti ba sɔkera en ayima ayima yeti, La dela mam yo'? Ti ayima me yeti, mam ka ti'ihɛ la dela mam.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Nyaa pɔhɛ ti bamam na'aha en mɛ nyaa yɛta yeti, Naa Yuuduma Naba wa! Bɔna wuuha.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, La dela ya pia la bayi' la puan ayima, se'em n fike boroboro kɔ'ɔm tuna laa puan la mam wa.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Gee ŋmɛ'ɛra a zuo la dɔya, tubeha nɔtuurum ma'ara a nɛŋa, kpa'ara duma wuu ba na'ahɛ en mɛ la.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: La de asira ti Asaala Dayua la wan ki doohe la ban gulehɛ a yele se'em beŋe la, gee yele bu fum se'em daana n koohe Asaala Dayua tihɛ la zuo. La ani ni suŋa ti ba pun da dɔgɛ ni en.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Ban ɛregɛ en ba'ahɛ la, ba yɛɛ fumɔlegɔ la mɛ, gee tee a mɛŋa futo yɛ en gee tari en kiŋɛ ti ba ta kpa' en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Ban boi bini dita la, ti Yezu dikɛ boroboro purɛ sum bahɛ de zuo gee fike de buyi' bo' a zahereba la, gee yele ba yeti, To'e ya la ya obe, mam iŋa n ŋwana.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Ban boi sore zuo yɛ' la, ba tu'uhɛ la buraa kayima n kinɛ ka kinɛ ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Simɔn, yehe Saarin timpaberɛ na, dɛna Alɛgezanda la Arufuse duma sɔ, ti ba pɛregɛ en ti a zi Yezu pumpuuredɔɔ la.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Ti Yezu le dikɛ wula la daam, n bo' Yinɛ fara ba'ahɛ la, a dikɛ bo' ba mɛ ti ba za'a nyu de puan.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Ba tari Yezu wa'am na paaɛ la zi'an ti ba yi'ira Gɔlegɔta .

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: A yele ba yeti, Mam nɔpu'ulegɔ ziim n ŋwana, wan ka'ɛ faaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e bahɛ.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Bilam ti ba dikɛ daam ti mɛɛ bɔna bini bo' en ti a zagehɛ.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, mam ka le makɛ nyu tiihi wala daam wa la ya gura daanse'ere daarɛ ti n wan ta nyu bu paalum paalum Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Ti ba kpa' en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo, gee nyaa ŋmɛ gbaŋa ti ba tɔrɛ a lɔgerɔ la.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ n tole la, ti Mɛɛri Magedalin la Yemesi ma Mɛɛri la Salome da' nyuunsuŋɔ kaam ti ba ta bi'ihɛ Yezu iŋa la.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Ban yuum pu'uhegɔ yuuma ba'ahɛ la, ti ba yehe kiŋɛ ta zom Olive Zoore zuo.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: La wa dɛna la gogo kaareŋa awai bulika ti ba kpa' Yezu.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Yiimbeere bulika ma'aherɛ ma'aherɛ dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ, ti suurɛ nan zɔna la, ti ba kiŋɛ yɔɔ la puan.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya za'a wan ta bahɛ mam, se'ere sɔi la, ba gulehɛ yeti, N wan ŋmɛ dunkiina la, gee ti piihi la yirum.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Ti ba nyaa gulehɛ yelese'ere zuo ti ba kpa' en la yeti, Yuuduma Naba.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Ban ye' la, ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, Ani n wan ta bilege kugere la yɔɔ la nuuren bo' tu.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Gee n han ta vo'e n kum la puan la, n wan diŋɛ ya nɛŋa kiŋɛ Galilii.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Gee ba me kpa' la fãareba bayi' la en, ti ayima bɔna a zuɔ ti ayima me a gɔbega.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Ban pɔri la, ba nyɛ ti yɔɔ kugere la bilege mɛ gã'a kinkɛleŋa ha. Kugere la ba' karegɛ mɛ ka tana ita.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Ti Pita yele Yezu yeti, Ba za'a han pun ta bahɛ hɔ, gee mam kan bahɛ hɔ.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Ti sɛba n dole tɔla, lɔbera ba zuto gee tuura yeti, Iiie! Fum se'em n wan gbirege pu'uhegɔ yire gee mɛ ku dabeha atã la n bala.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Ban kɛ̃' yɔɔ la puan la, ba nyɛ la budibele paalega n yɛ fuwoko pɛɛlega, zi'an zuɔ nu'o bɔba, ti dabeem kɛ̃' paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum, ti zina daarɛ wa, yu'ukuna wa, nɔdɔɔ ta kaahɛ buyi' la, ti fum pun ki'ihɛ mam la butã.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Ti budibele paalega wa yele ba yeti, Da zoti ya dabeem, mam mi' ti ya eere la Yezu n de Nazareti buraa la ti ba daa kpa' pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo la. A vo'e mɛ, a ka boi ka'lam. Bihɛ ya, ban daa dikɛ a iŋa la gã'arɛ zi'an ŋwana.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: to'e fum mɛŋa bahɛ gee ze'ele pumpuuredɔɔ wa zuo sigena.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Ti Pita lebehe Yezu la ninmu'urɛ yeti, La han pun dɛna la n ki la hɔ. Mam kan bahɛ fum. Ti a zahereba la me woo lagum yele bala.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Gee kiŋɛ ya, la ya ta yele a zahereba la Pita ti a diŋɛ nɛŋa yɛ'ɛha Galilii ha. Bilam ti ya wan ta nyɛ en wuu eŋa daa pun yele ya se'em la.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Ba kiŋɛ la zi'an kayima ti ba yi'ira Gɛtesemane, ti ban paaɛ la, Yezu yele ba yeti, Zi' ya ka'lam gee ti n kiŋɛ ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Bala mɛa ti Malema kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la ɛregera Yezu bɔ'ɔra taaba yeti, A faari la basɛba gee kan ta'am faaɛ a mɛŋa.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Ti ban yehe yɔɔ la puan na la, ti ba ɛkɛ kalam gee bahɛ yɔɔ la se'ere en sɔi la, dabeem la palen-ɛka n kɛ̃' ba. Ti ba kina gee ka sɔhera bɔ'ɔra taaba dabeem la zuo. En yele ba sɛla za'a la, ba yele de la girega girega bo' sɛba n boi la Pita la. Pooren ti Yezu mɛŋa tum ba ti ba kiŋɛ ze'a woo ta muulɛ ninmu'urɛ la faarɛ yelehum wa n ka sageni wa.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: A looe la Pita la Yemesi la Yɔn, ti ba kiŋɛ fii wa, ti a iŋa kɔ'ɔhɛ ti a suure sagum.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Bahɛ ya ti se'em n de Kiresta la, dɛna Izirale naba la ze'ele pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo sige nananawa na, ti tu nyɛ gee sakɛ. Ti sɛba ti ba kpa' ba la Yezu la me ɛregera en bala me.

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Yezu n vo'e a kum la puan la bulika ma'aherɛ ma'aherɛ, dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ la, a niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ la Mɛɛri yia, Mɛɛri Magedalin se'em ti a sa'ahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi siyopɔi a puan la.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, N suure n ba' sagum zo'e ana wuu n ki mɛ la. Ze' ya ka'lam gura mam, biheri ya da gihere ya.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Ti la ta paaɛ gogo muma pia la ayi', wuntɛɛŋa ti lika wa'am na pi' timpaberɛ la za'a gareti tara bala la gogo atã.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Ti pɔka wa yehe ta yele sɛba n daa lagum bɔna la Yezu. Bala ma'a la ti bamam nan kele mɛ gee fabela.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: En nyaa kiŋɛ nɛŋa fii la ti a lu timpaberɛ pu'uhɛ yeti, la han wan ta'am iŋɛ, bahɛ ti namehegɔ kuna tole gee bahɛ mam.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Gee ban ta wum ti Yezu vo'e ya ti pɔka la nyɛ en la, ba kelum ka sakɛ.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Gogo atã la puan ti Yezu kaahɛ supi yeti, Eloi Eloi lama sabakitani? De vuurɛ dela, N Yinɛ N Yinɛ, ŋwani ti fum bahɛ mam.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: A yeti, Aba, di vuurɛ dela n Sɔ, fum zi'an sɛla ka boi n kan ta'am tum, to'e namehegɔ wanɛ wa n zi'an gee dagi mam sumboolum gee fum sumboolum tum.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Ŋwana pooren ti a le niiɛ a mɛŋa sadɔɔ kuyima le pa'alɛ a zahereba bayi' ti ba boi la sore zuo yɛ'ɛha timpaberɛ la kinkɛleŋa ha.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: Gee lebe na paaɛ, nyɛ ti a zahereba la gihi mɛ, ti a yele Pita yeti, Simɔn, yamam gihere mɛ? Yamam kan ta'am piga paaɛ gogo mune ma'a?.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Ti nɛresɛba n ze' bini gee wum la yeti, Kelehe ya, a yi'ira la Elia.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Ti ba lebe ta yele ba taaba la, gee ba ka sakɛ bo' ba.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Niiɛ ya, la ya pu'uhɛ ti ya da lu pã'ahebe'o saŋa wa puan. Sia la kɔ'ɔm bɔta mɛ gee inganɛ la en tari tɔregɔ.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Ti nɛra ayima zɔ ta dikɛ soosoo tum dami'ihego puan, dikɛ vile dɔɔ, nyaa zɛkɛ dikɛ tuhera a nuuren, ti a bihɛ a wan nyu, Bahɛ ya ti bihɛ ya Elia wan wa'am na faaɛ en pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo sige na.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Pooren ti a zahereba pia la ayima la ta naaɛ taaba ti ba di. Ti Yezu niiɛ amɛŋa pa'alɛ ba gee nyaa nyɔna ba la ban n ka tari sakerɛ la ba tubegirega la doohe la ban ka sakɛ bo' sɛba n nyɛ ti eŋa vo'e ya gee yele ba la.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Gee le kiŋɛ lebe ta pu'uhɛ, le yele en pun yele ni se'em mɛa.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Bilam ti Yezu kaahɛ supi', gee vo'ohe a vo'ohum za'a.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo ta muulɛ yelesuma wa bo' nɛra woo.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Gee le lebe na paaɛ ti ba gihere mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, ba nini n vilegeri. Ti ba me ka mina ban wan yele en se'em.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Ti Yinɛ deo tanɛ la n beŋe fa'a saraa la ahegɛ buyi' ze'ele de zuo ta paaɛ timpaberɛ.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Se'em woo n sakɛ bo' yelehum la gee sɔ ko'om n wan nyɛ faarɛ, gee se'em woo me n ka sakɛ wan nyɛ sageŋɔ.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Ti a kiŋɛ gee le lebe butã puan na yele ba yeti, Ya kelum giha mɛ gee sũna? La seke ya! Saŋa la paaɛ na. Ba koohe Asaala Dayua la bo' la be'em duma.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Ti Pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana se'em n biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la n ze' gura Yezu la, n nyɛ ti a vo'ohum la ba'ahɛ se'em la ti a yeti, Asira, buraa wa dela Yinɛ dayua.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Daalehum buna wan dɔla sɛba n sakɛ la, ba wan dikɛ mam yu'urɛ sa'ahɛ Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, ba wan tɔgɛ zɛlempaala.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Ihege ya, bahɛ ya ti tu kinɛ ya, bihɛ ya, se'em n koohe mam la n ŋwana.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Ti Pɔgehi sise'ehi me zɛ'a zaaɛ zaaɛ ha gee biha na. Pɔge se'ehi n pa'ahɛ si puan dela Mɛɛri Magedalin, la Mɛɛri se'em n de Yemesi bila, la Yose ma, la Salome.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: Ba wan nyɔkɛ bunsɛlenduma la ba nu'uhi, gee ba han pun nyu la yabega se'ere kan iŋɛ ba, ba wan dikɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ bã'duma zuto ti ba nyɛ ba tibega.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Ban kelum bɔna bini tɔgera la, bilam bilam ti Yudasi en lagum pa'ahɛ pia la bayi' la en bala en paaɛ na la nɛrekuuŋɔ, ti ba zãla sɔ'ɔhi la dɔya ze'ele malema kiinduma, gɔnmi'ineba, la yizuto kiinduma la ze'a na.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Nɛreba wa daa ni dɔla en mɛ gee bihera en, se'em ma'a ti a bɔna Galilii, la pɔgehi kekeko n doohe en zom Yerusalɛm na.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Bala Daana Yezu n tɔgɛ bo' ba ba'ahɛ la, a zom la saazuo, ta zi'ire Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Ŋma'ara la bo' ba la daalehum buyina yeti, Se'em ti n ta kɔkɛ la, eŋa en bala, nyɔkɛ ya en bo' gureba la ti ba tari en kiŋɛ.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Yinɛ n ta ma'a la, ti daanse'ere n de maaheŋɔ daarɛ la, dabehe se'ere n gee bɔ'ɔra vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Bala, Yudasi n wa'am na paaɛ la, bilam bilam ti a tɔ Yezu, kiŋɛ paaɛ a zi'an yeti, Pa'ala! Gee kɔkɛ en.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Ti a zahereba la me yiregɛ zi'an woo ta muulɛ yelesuma la bo' nɛra woo, ti Daana la me tum nyalema la kirehi, ti la dɛna daalehum bo' yelehum la.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: ti Arimatiya buraa yu'urɛ n yi'iri Yosefe dɛna zuodaana pa'ahɛ kiinduma la puan gee me dɛna se'em n me daa guri ti a nyɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la. Eŋa n kãbɛ suure kiŋɛ Palete zi'an ta suhɛ ti a bo' en sore ti a dikɛ Yezu iŋa la laaɛ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Ti ba nyaa paaɛ Yezu zi'an nyɔkɛ en.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Ti Palete bu'ura nyaa Yezu pilum ki mɛ, bala ti a yi' Pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana se'em n biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la la n ze' ni gura Yezu la soke en nyaa Yezu ki ti la yuue?.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Ti nɛreba la n ze' la Yezu la, ayima nyaa fooe a su'a kɛ̃' malema kiinduma la ayima yameŋa tuberɛ bahɛ.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: Ti en wum Pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana n biheri se'em n biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la zi'an ti Yezu ki mɛ la, ti a bo' Yosefe sore ti a dikɛ kum la.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya yehe na la sɔ'ɔhi la dɔya ti ya nyɔkɛ mam wuu n dela nageyiga?.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
