local: Ti Yosefe nyaa da' tansunɛ nyaa kiŋɛ ta nyakɛ Yezu iŋa la pɔbɛ tansunɛ la ti a da la puan, tari ta laaɛ yɔɔ ti n kerehe kɔ taŋa puan gee bilege kugere lige yɔɔ la nuurɛ.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Daarɛ woo mam boi mɛ la ya pu'uhegɔ yire la puan pa'ala, ti ya ka nyɔkɛ mam. Gee ŋwana wa wan bahɛ ti gulehegɔ la dɛna asira.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Ti Mɛɛri Magedalin la Yosefe ma Mɛɛri nyɛ ba gã'arɛ Yezu zi'an.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Ba za'a bahɛ en mɛ gee zɔ.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Ti budibele paalega kayima n lobe a fugɔbesuŋa ma'a pi' a mɛŋa dɔla Yezu poore ti ba ta gurɛ en.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: ti a zugum pilege fugɔbesuŋa la bahɛ ba gee zɔ la beele.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Ti ba nyaa tari Yezu kiŋɛ Malema nɛŋadaana la zi'an, bala ma'a ti malema kiinduma, yizuto kiinduma, la gɔnmi'ineba pun lagehɛ mɛ maahum zi'an bini.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Ti Pita me bɔna pooren pooren ha dɔla Yezu fifihe, wa'am na lagum kɛ̃' malema nɛŋa daana la zinzaka puan, lagum zi'a la tuntuneba la ɔɔha bugum.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Ti malema nɛheduma la kiinduma la za'a nyaa biha se'em n wan ta'am parum yelese'ere bo' Yezu ti ba doohe bilam ku en. Gee ba ka nyɛ se'ere bala.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba zo'e zo'e yehe na parum mɛ gee ba pumpɔreŋɔ la ka naaɛ.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Basɛba yehe na parum bo' en yeti.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: Tumam wum n yeti N wan gbi' pu'uhegɔ yire wa ti asaala mɛ wa bahɛ gee malum mɛ kuyima ti asaala ka mɛ dabeha atã puan. .

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Gee bamam me n yele se'em la kelum ka naaɛ.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Lan de ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e maahum ti ba gulehɛ ti la doohe taaba, yele sɛ'a en wa'am asira na tumam tiŋasuka la.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Ti Malema nɛŋadaana la ihege saazuo ba nɛŋan soke Yezu yeti, Fum ka tari se'ere n wan lerege? Beni yɛla n bala ti ba tara bɔ'ɔra fum wa.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: wuu ban dikɛ a se'em bo' tumam la, bamam sɛba en daa boi pi'ileŋo puan na, dɛna aze'nyɛ duma gee dɛna yamehi bo' yelehum la.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Ti Yezu sina, ka yɛta se'ere. Ti malema nɛŋadaana la le soke n yeti, Fum en de Kiresta la? Fum en de sum za'a daana Dayua la.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Mam me iŋɛ n puti'irɛ, doohe mam ma'ɛ n mɛŋa viihɛ sɛla woo en pɔhɛ se'em la yia na la, ti n gulehɛ doohe taaba taaba bo' fum, zuodaana Tiyofilose.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Yezu yeti, Mam en bala, gee ya wan nyɛ Asaala Dayua la ti a zi'a paŋadaana la zuɔ nu'o bɔna saazuo sagbama puan kina na. .

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: ti la bahɛ ti fum mina yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere en boi yele sɛ'a woo ti ba pa'alɛ fum la puan.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Ti Malema nɛŋadaana la ihege aahum a mɛŋa futo gee yele nɛreba la yeti, Ŋwani ti tu kelum ɛɛra se'em ti a wa'am na yele se'ere.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Dabeha sɛ'a puan ti Hɛrɔd dɛna Yuda naba la, ti malema daana ayima en boi, ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Zekariya, yehe malema daana Abiya yizuo la puan na, ti a pɔga dɛna Aron yaaŋa, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Elizabɛte.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Ya wum n tu Yinɛ se'em, Yamam puti'irɛ dela deni? Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a tahera ti Yezu galum mɛ, nara a ki.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Ba bayi' la dela tuuntɔɔ duma Yinɛ nɛŋan, ti ba vom tigɛ Yinɛ nini, dɔla Daana la nɔze'elego la a kiha woo puan.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Ti sɛba pɔhɛ tubeha nɔtuurum ma'ara en, ti sɛba me pi' a nini gee ŋmɛ' en, gee yele yeti, Bukɛ ani n ŋmɛ fum wa. Ti gureba la mɛ sooe ŋmɛ en paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Gee ba ka tari bia, se'ere en sɔi la, Elizabɛte dela kundɔgerɛ, ti ba za'a me kuregɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Pita en kelum zi'a Malema nɛŋadaana la zinzakan la, ti Malema nɛŋadaana la tuntuna en de pugela wa'am zinzakan la na.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Saŋa kayima ti Zekariya wa tuna a malema daana tuuma Yinɛ nɛŋan la, bamam wele la kaabegɔ saŋa en paaɛ.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Eŋa n nyɛ ti Pita ɔɔhi bugum la, ti a bihɛ Pita zii gee yeti, Fum me daa lagum bɔna mɛ la Yezu en de Nazareti nɛra la.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: ti ba ŋmɛ' gbaŋa looe en, wuu malema duma la itegɔ en de se'em la, ti a kɛ̃ tuulum saraa la puan ta nyɔ' nyuuŋo suŋɔ zu'uhi bo' Yinɛ.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Ti Pita ki'ihɛ yeti, aayi. Mam ka mi' en, bii n bɔkɛ fum tɔgeri se'ere wa. Gee ihege kina tɔɔra zanɔren bɔba ha, ti nɔdɔɔ kaahɛ.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Se'em ma'a ti a ta nyɔ'ɔra nyuuŋo suŋa zu'uhi kaabegɔ la, ti lageŋɔ la nɛreba la za'a ze' la yiŋa pu'uhera.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Ti Pugela wa en de tuntuna wa la, le nyɛ Pita nyaa pɔhɛ yɛta sɛba n ze' bilam la yeti, Burakana wa dela Yezu nɛreba la ayima.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Ti Daana la suŋera niiɛ a mɛŋa a nɛŋan, zɛ'a zu'uhi nyɔ'a bimbine la zuɔ bɔba.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ti Pita le ki'ihɛ. Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti sɛba en ze' bini la ayima yele Pita yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ fum sirum dela ba puan ayima se'ere en sɔi la, fum dela Galiliiŋa.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: Zekariya en nyɛ en la, a paleŋa ɛkɛ mɛ, ti dabeem kɛ̃' en paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Ti Pita pɔhɛ pɔta gee yeti, Mam ka mi' buraa wa ti fum yɛta wa.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Gee suŋera la yele en yeti, Da zɔta dabeem Zekariya, Yinɛ wum fum pu'uhegɔ la. Fum pɔga Elizabɛte wan dɔgɛ bibudibila bo' fum, ti fum sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Yɔn.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Sankana ma'a la ti nɔdɔɔ le kaahɛ pa'ahɛ buyi'. Ti Pita tiɛ ti Yezu pun yele en yeti, Nɔdɔɔ ta kaahɛ pa'aha buyi' la, ti fum pun ki'ihɛ eŋa la butã. Ti a suure sagum ti a kele.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Fum wan tara pupeelum la nyɛɛma, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e me wan nyɛɛna bia wa dɔgum zuo.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, ka yeti ka dɛna la nɛre kãtɛ, Daana la zi'an. Ka da makɛ nyu daam bii sɛla en kpe'em. Ba ta dɔgera ka la, ti ka pun pirɛ mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Ka wan ŋmɛregɛ Izirale nɛreba zo'e zo'e suya lebe Daana la en de ba Yinɛ la ze'a na.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: A wan bɔna a nɛŋa la Sɛsɛka, la pansɛka en yuum boi Elia puan la, ti a ŋmɛregɛ dɔgereba suya lebe ba kɔma puan na, gee ŋmɛregɛ sɛba en ka nane suya lebe tuntɔɔnɛ duma yɛm puan na, gee maahum nɛreba bo' Daana la.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Zekariya yele suŋera la yeti, N wan iŋɛ la ŋwani baŋɛ ti la ti dɛna wa dela bala, mam kuregɛ, ti n pɔga me yuuma ka pɔ'ɛ wa.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Ti suŋera la lerege en yeti, Mam dela Gaberɛl. Mam ze' la Yinɛ nɛŋan, gee ti a tum mam na, ti n tari yelesunɛ wa na bo' fum.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Gee nananawa, fum en ka nam mam yelehum la en yeti bu wa'am na tum bu saŋa puan la zuo, fum wan maam kan tana tɔgera, gura daanse'ere ti yele ana wa wan tum.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Ŋwana woo la, ti nɛreba la guri Zekariya mɛ, ti'iha sɛla en bahɛ ti a yuura bala tuulum deo la puan ha.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: En ta yehe na la, a ka tã tɔgera wan tɔgɛ bo' ba. Ti nɛreba la mɛŋa baŋɛ ti a nyɛ la lilegere tuulum deo la puan, ti a tara a nu'uhi yibeha pa'ala ba, gee kan ta'am tɔgɛ.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: A malema tuuma dabeha la en ta ba'ahɛ la, a lebe kule la a yire.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Dabeha aŋa pooren ti a pɔga Elizabɛte yee sia, sugera a mɛŋa ŋmarehi sinuu. A yeti.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Daana en iŋɛ sɛla bo' mam en ŋwana. Dabeha ana puan, a bihɛ mam mɛ la zu'uhegɔ gee yehe nyanɛ se'ere ti n nyɛta n nɛreba wa puan bahɛ.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Yuunɛ puan, ŋmarehi siyoobe puan, ti Yinɛ tum a Suŋera Gaberɛl Galilii en boi Nazareti tiŋa puan.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: pɔgesa'kaherɛ ti ba maahum ti de ele buraa yu'urɛ en yi'ira Yosefe, en de naba Deviti yire puan. Pɔgesa'kaherɛ wa yu'urɛ yi'iri la Mɛɛri.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Ti Suŋera la wa'am a zi'an na, pu'uhɛ en yeti, Fum fara fara, zusuŋɔ daana wa, Daana la boi mɛ la hɔ.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Gee Mɛɛri maam mɛ la a yetɔgum la, ti'ihɛ pu'uhegɔ wa vuurɛ en de sɛla.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Ti Suŋera la yele en yeti, Da zɔta dabeem Mɛɛri, Yinɛ en zu'uhɛ hɔ.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: Fum yeti fum nyɔkɛ la puurɛ dɔgɛ budibela, sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Yezu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: Ka yeti ka ta dɛna la nɛre kãtɛ, ti ba yi'ira en Gani za'a Yinɛ wa Dayua. Daana la en de Yinɛ la, wan dikɛ ka sɔyaaba Deviti na'amkuka la bo' en.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: Eŋa en wan sɔna Yakɔbe sɔ'ɔlum la za'a bihera wuuha. Han dɛna ka sɔ'ɔlum la, bu ka tara tɛka.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Ti Mɛɛri soke suŋera la yeti, Ŋwana yeti la iŋɛ la ŋwani iŋɛ, gee ti mam dɛna pɔgesa'kaherɛ wa.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Ti Suŋera la yele en yeti, Fa'a Sia la wan sige fum zuo, ti Gani Za'a Daana paŋa pi' fum. Bala zuo, bia la ti fum yeti fum dɔgɛ la wan dɛna la Fa'a. Ba wan yi'ira ka la Yinɛ Dayua.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Nananawa, fum suuɔ Elizabɛte la a kuregerɛ la za'a, tari la puurɛ, ti bia la dɛna budibela, a ŋmarehi siyoobe en ŋwana. Se'em ti ba daa yi'ira kundɔgerɛ la n bala.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Sɛla ka boi kpe'em bo' Yinɛ.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Ti Mɛɛri yeti, Mam en ŋwana, mam dela Daana la yameŋa. Bahɛ ti la iŋɛ bo' mam doohe fum yelehum la. Ti Suŋera la kiŋɛ.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Dabeha aŋa mɛa puan, ti Mɛɛri yehe kabelɛ kiŋɛ Yudiya tiŋa en boi zoore zuo la.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: ta kɛ̃ Zekariya yire, pu'uhɛ Elizabɛte.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ti Elizabɛte en wum Mɛɛri pu'uhegɔ la, bia la damɛ mɛ a puuren ha. Ti Elizabɛte pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: kaahɛ supi yeti, Sum boi fum zuo gana pɔgehi za'a, sum boi mɛ la walɛ se'ere en boi fum puuren wa.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Gee beni ti la iŋɛ mam ŋwana, ti mam Daana ma wa'am mam zi'an na?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Mam en kɔ'ɔm wum fum pu'uhegɔ la, bihɛ, bia la en boi mam puuren la dam mɛ la nyɛɛma.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Sum bɔna la fum se'em en nam ti Daana la en yele se'em la wan sirum wa'am na tum la.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Ti Mɛɛri yeti, Mam suure wa pɛgɛ mam Daana la mɛ.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: ti mam sia me nyɛɛna Yinɛ en de mam Faara wa puan.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, a bihɛ mam mɛ la zu'uhɛ, a yameŋa wa en dagi sɛla wa. Asira, pɔhɛ zina yɛ'a paara buuri saŋa woo, nɛra woo wan yi'ira mam la Sum.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Gaŋɛ Za'a Daana la tum la yele kãra bo' mam. A yu'urɛ dela la Fa'a.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: A nimbɔ'zɔrɛ boi bɔ'ɔra la sɛba en zoti en buuri saŋa woo puan.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: A pa'alɛ la a paŋa la a nu'o. A yiregɛ pa'ali mɛŋa duma suya ti'ihegɔ la za'a bahɛ mɛ.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Eŋa en sigeheri paheduma, ba na'am zuo, gee zɛkera tarenduma.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Eŋa en piti sɛba ti kom tara ba, la lɔgesunɔ, gee bahera ti buntateba kina nu'bɛɛla.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: A suŋɛ a yameŋa Izirale mɛ, ti a tɛra a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la.

eng: just as he promised to our ancestors, to Abraham and to Abrahams descendants forever.
local: doohe la a nɔbiŋere ti a biŋe la tu sɔyaabeduma, Aberaham la a yaahi ti de ka tara ba'ahegɔ la.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Ti Mɛɛri yee kɛ̃ la en wuu ŋmarehi sitã, gee lebe kule.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Ti saŋa la ta paaɛ ti Elizabɛte dɔgɛ, ti a dɔgɛ budibela.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Ti a mabiihi, la a mɛŋa nɛreba za'a wum ti Daana la zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, ti ba za'a lagum nyɛɛna la en.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Dabeha anii daarɛ en de ba ŋma bia la buraanɛ la, ti ba maahena ti ba dikɛ bia la sigɛ ka sɔ Zekariya.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Ti ka ma yeti, Aayi, bia la yu'urɛ dela Yɔn.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Ti nɛreba la yele en yeti, Nɛra ka boi fum buuri wa puan tara yu'dina.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Gee nyaa pɔhɛ yibeha nu'uhi pa'ala bia la sɔ la ti ba baŋɛ yu'urɛ se'ere ti a bɔta ti ba dikɛ sigɛ bia la.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Ti a yibehɛ pa'alɛ ba ti ba bo' en sɛla ti a gulehɛ iŋɛ bini, ti ba bo' en, ti a gulehɛ yeti, Bia la yu'urɛ yi'iri la Yɔn. Ti la iŋɛ nɛreba la za'a pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
local: En ba'ahɛ bala la, bilam bilam ti a ya'ɛ nuurɛ ti a zɛleŋa lorege, ti a tana tɔgera, nyaa pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Ti dabeem kɛ̃ sɛba woo en lɛm ba la, ti ba tɔgera yele ana woo zoore zuo tiŋa la en boi Yuda puan la za'a.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Ti sɛba woo en wum yele ana wa la, ti'iha yeti, Beni nɛra ti bikana wa nyaa yeti ka zo'e na dɛna? Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela sira ti Daana la nu'o en page ka zuo.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Ti bia wa sɔ Zekariya, pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la, nyaa purɛ purega wa yeti.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Sum dɛna Izirale Daana en de Yinɛ la dinɛ, Se'ere en sɔi la, a zɔ a nɛreba la nimbu'ɔ mɛ, gee yɔ to'e ba.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Yinɛ bo' tu la faara en sɔi a mɛŋa, yehe a yameŋa Deviti yire puan na.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: wuu en tɔgɛ se'em doohe diimen diimen tuulum nɔtɔgeheba la nɔya puan se'em.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: yeti, tu wan to'e tu mɛhi tu dinduma, la sɛba woo en yaaɛ tu la nu'uhen.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: A pa'alɛ tɔ a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ se'ere ti a yuum tara bo' tu yaabeduma la, a tiɛ a tuulum nɔbiŋere la mɛ.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: en yuum biŋe a nuurɛ se'ere la pɔtɛ bo' tu yaaba Aberaham ti a wan bahɛ ti tumam sɛba.

eng: that we would be rescued from the power of our enemies so that we could serve him without fear.
local: ti a faaɛ tɔ tu dinduma nu'uhen bahɛ la, wan tuna bɔ'ɔra en; ti dabeem ka tara tɔ.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: ti tu dɛna tuulum nɛreba, tara tuuntɔɔ vom bɔna a nɛŋan tu vom dabeha woo puan la.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Gee fum bia wa, fum yu'urɛ wan yi'ira la Gaŋɛ Za'a Daana la Nɔtɔgeha. Se'ere en sɔi la, fum en wan diŋɛ Daana la nɛŋa maalɛ a sɔya la biŋe en.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: Fum bo' a nɛreba la faarɛ mi'ilum, doohe ba be'em la sugeri dia.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: tumam Yinɛ la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la zuo. Niihum wan ze'ele saazuo ha niiɛ bo' tumam na.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: Bo wan niiɛ bo' sɛba en zi' lika puan, la sɛba en boi Kum nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega puan. Bo wan niiɛ gu'ura tu nama suma'ahum sore la zuo.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Ti bia wa malum zɔ'ɔra Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan, bɔna muuen ha a ma'a, ta paaɛ daanse'ere ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ Iziraleduma la.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Dabeha aŋa puan la, ti Kaiza Agastus bo' nuurɛ ti ba gulehɛ Arome sɔ'ɔlum nɛreba za'a yu'ura.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Gulehegɔ kuna en de yia, ti ba iŋɛ, se'em ma'a ti Kurinos dɛna Siriya timbihega nɛŋadaana la.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Nɛra woo kule la a tiŋa a tiŋa ti ba ta gulehɛ ba yu'ura.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Ti Yosefe me ze'ele Nazareti en boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan la, yɛ'a Yuda, kula Deviti tinsɛka en yi'iri Bɛtelehɛm la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, Yosefe ze'ele la Deviti buuri puan na.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: A ye' kula ti a ta gulehɛ la a yu'urɛ, eŋa la Mɛɛri se'em ti ba maahena ti a di en, gee ti a me pun gee dɔgum la.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Ban paaɛ bini la, ti Mɛɛri saŋa paaɛ ti a dɔgɛ a bia la.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Ti Mɛɛri dɔgɛ a saresare bia en de budibela, pɔbɛ bia la la futo, dikɛ ka gã'arɛ duhi dia dɔɔ puan, se'ere en sɔi la, zi'an ka boi bo' ba ga'aregɔ yire la puan.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Tinkana wa puan, dunkiima basɛba en boi muuen ha la gu'ura ba piihi yu'ukuŋɔ mɛa puan.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: Ti Daana la suŋera en bala zɛ'a ba nɛŋan, ti Daana suŋa niiɛ bilam piim kaaɛ ba bahɛ tiŋasuka, ti ba pabegɛ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: Ti suŋera la yele ba yeti, Da zoti ya dabeem, bihɛ ya, mam tari la yelesunɛ en tari nyɛɛma kãtɛ yɛ'a ya zi'an na, ti n bo' nɛreba woo.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Zina dabeherɛ wa, Deviti sɔ'ɔlum wa puan, ba dɔgɛ faara la en de Kiresta la, dɛna Daana la.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Dina en wan dɛna daalehum bo' ya. Ya wan ta nyɛ bia la, ti ba pɔbɛ ka mɛ la futo, dikɛ ka gã'arɛ duhi dia dɔɔ zuo.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Bilam bilam ti suŋereba kekeko en bala en ze'ele saazuo na lagum suŋera ayima la, ti ba pɛgera Yinɛ yeti.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Pɛka paaɛ Yinɛ, se'em en boi saazuin zuin ha wa, gee ti timpaberɛ wa me zuo, suma'ahum bɔna la saaleba ti a zu'uhɛ ba la.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Suŋereba la en bahɛ dunkiima wa gee lebe zom saazuo la, ti dunkiima wa yele taaba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ Bɛtelehɛm nananawa, la tu ta bihɛ yele dina wa en iŋɛ, ti Daana la lilege pa'alɛ tu wa.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Ti dunkiima la ihege kabela yɛ'a, ta paaɛ nyɛ Mɛɛri la Yosefe, gee nyɛ bia la me ti ka gã'a duhi dia dɔɔ la puan.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Ban nyɛ ŋwana la, ti ba nyaa yele bamam en wum se'ere doohe bia wa puan.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: ti sɛba woo en wum dunkiima wa la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Gee Mɛɛri to'e ban yele sɛla woo la mɛ la nimu'urɛ, gee nyaa ti'iha a suuren.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Ti dunkiima wa lɛbera, gee na'aha gee pɛgera Yinɛ, la sɛla ti ba wum gee nyɛ, wuu suŋereba la en yele ba se'em la.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Dabeha anii en tole la, bia la buraanɛ ŋmaaɛ saŋa en bala, ti ba sigɛ ka yu'urɛ Yezu, yu'se'ere ti suŋera la yuum bo' en gee ti ba baŋɛ nyɔkɛ ka puurɛ la.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Peere malema saŋa la en ta paaɛ la, wuu Moose pa'alegɔ la en de se'em la, ti ba tari Yezu kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm, ti ba ta dikɛ en bo' Daana la.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: ti ba nyaa kaabɛ kaabegɔ doohe lan gulehɛ se'em bɔna Daana pa'alegɔ la puan la, Naduma dayɛma ayi', bii barebale'ero bibehi tuyi'.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Bala ma'a ti buraa kayima en boi Yerusalɛm ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Simeyɔn, dɛna tuuntɔɔ, kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' Yinɛ, gee gura Izirale sumbugelegɔ dabeherɛ la. Ti Fa'a Sia pirɛ en.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Fa'a Sia la tabelɛ lilege pa'alɛ en mɛ yeti, A kan ki, gura ti a nini ta nyɛ Daana la en de Kiresta la.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Ti Fa'a Sia la tari Simeyɔn wa wa'am pu'uhegɔ yire la puan na, ti Yezu dɔgereba tari Yezu kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan na, ti ba malum a malema wuu pa'alegɔ la en pa'alɛ se'em la.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: ti Simeyɔn to'e bia la a nu'uhen pɛgera Yinɛ yeti.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: N Daana, nyaa bahɛ mam fum yameŋa wa ti n vo'ohe bala la suma'ahum, wuu fum en yele se'em la.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam nini nyaa nyɛ fum faarɛ la.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: ti fum maahum dɛ nɛreba woo nɛŋan la.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Se'em en de niihum wan niiɛ ti sɛba n dagi Yuuduma nyɛta la. Se'em en wan tari pɛka wa'am fum nɛreba Iziraleduma zuo na la.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Ti bia la sɔ la ka ma yari zɛ'a yɛli, la ban tɔgeri bia la yele se'em la.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Ti Simeyɔn ka' sum bo' bia la dɔgereba la, gee yele bia la ma Mɛɛri yeti, bihɛ, Bikana wa zuo ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan lu ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan ihege, ka wan dɛna daalehum ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e kan sakɛ bo' ka Izirale puan.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: ti la bahɛ ti yele sɛ'a en sugɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e suya puan ha la niiɛ na, gee ti su'a lu' fum mɛŋa me.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Nɔtɔgeha ayima me en da boi ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Anna, dɛna Fanuyel en de Asere buuri la poyua. A wa ba' kuregɛ mɛ. A pɔgesa'aren na ti a ele ta paaɛ yuuma ayopɔi ti a sira a ka wa bɔna.

eng: She was now an -year-old widow. She never left the temple area but worshipped God with fasting and prayer night and day.
local: Ti en pun dɛna pokuurɛ en de woo yuuma pihinii la anaahi la, a ka baheri pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, a kɔ'ɔm bɔna la bilam la nɔlurɛ gee suha Yinɛ yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa za'a.

eng: She approached at that very moment and began to praise God and to speak about Jesus to everyone who was looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
local: Sankaŋa mɛa puan ti ka me kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan na, pɔhɛ pɛgera Yinɛ, gee tɔgera bia la yele bɔ'ɔra sɛba woo en guri Yerusalɛm faarɛ la.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Ban doohe malema la en de se'em Daana ze'elego la puan ba'ahɛ la, ba lebe la ba mɛŋa tiŋa Nazareti en boi Galilii puan.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Ti bia wa zɔ'ɔra, kpe'em, pirɛ la yɛm, ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bɔna ka zuo.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Nananawa,Yuunɛ woo, Yezu dɔgereba ni kiŋɛ la Yerusalɛm ta di azɔɛtole de'eŋo la.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Ti Yezu en ta dɛna yuumpia la ayi' la, ti eŋa la a dɔgereba la kiŋɛ zom de'eŋo la, wuu ban ni pun kina se'em la.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: De'eŋo la dabeha en ta ba'ahɛ la, ti a dɔgereba la yɛ'a lɛbera ba tiŋa la, Yezu en nan de bumbila la, yee la Yerusalɛm, ti a dɔgereba la ka baŋɛ.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: A dɔgereba la ti'ihɛ ti a lagum bɔna la nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan, gee kiŋɛ daanyinɛ kinɛ. Ti ba pɔhɛ ɛɛra bia la ba mabiihi la ba zɔduma zi'an.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Ti ban ka nyɛ bia la, ba lebe la Yerusalɛm, ta ɛɛra en.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: Ti la iŋɛ ti Dabeha atã ta tole, ti ba nyɛ en pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti a zi'a la pa'aleba la, kɛleha ba, gee sɔgeha ba yɛla.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Ti la iŋɛ sɛba woo en kelehe en la pakerɛ, la a bɔkerɛ, la a leregere la.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: A dɔgereba la en wa'am na nyɛ en la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ, ti a ma yele en yeti, Bia wa, ŋwani ti fum iŋɛ tu ŋwana? Bihɛ, fum sɔ la mam eere fum mɛ bala la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ɛɛra mam bala? Yamam ka mi' ti n suni ti n bɔna n Sɔ yire la puan.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Gee a dɔgereba la ka bɔkɛ en yele ba sɛla la vuurɛ.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Ti a doohe ba kule Nazareti, ta kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la a dɔgereba la, gee nana ba. Ti a ma dikɛ yele ana wa woo suŋa suŋa a suuren.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Ti Yezu zɔ'ɔra la yɛm, kahegerɛ, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la saaleba zu'uhegɔ.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Saazuo sɔ'ɔlum wan ana mɛ wuu. Pɔge sa'ara pia n dikɛ ba butolehi kiŋɛ ti ba tu'uhɛ pɔgedita.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Ba banuu dela geto, ti ba banuu dɛna yɛm duma.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Geto la n dikɛ ba butolehi la, ba ka lee kaam pa'ahɛ.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: Gee yɛm duma la lee la kaam iŋɛ sɛla puan pa'ahɛ ba butolela duma la puan.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Pɔgedita la en ta yuura la, ba za'a zɔgeri mɛ ta gihi.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Gee sunsua paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba wum voole yeti, Bihɛ ya, pɔgedita la n ŋwana, yehe ya na tu'uhɛ en.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Gee ti pɔge sa'ara pia la ihege zomehe ba butolehi.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Gee geto la yele yɛm duma la yeti, Iŋɛ ya kaam la bo' tu, gee tumam butolehi la kiŋeri mɛ.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Gee yɛm duma wa lerege ba yeti, Aayi bu ka zo'e wan paaɛ tumam la yamam, kiŋɛ ya sɛba n koohe la zi'an ta da bo' ya mɛhi.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Ban ye' ti ba ta da' kaam la, ti pɔgedita la n bala paaɛ na, ti sɛba n maahum la kɛ̃ la en pɔgedire la puan puan, ti ba yu' kuleŋa la.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Pooren ti pɔgesa'ara bana la en me bala, paaɛ yi'ira yeti, Daana, daana, yu'ɛ bo' tu.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Ti pɔgedita la lerege ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti en yɛta wa, mam ka mi' ya.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Bala la, Da giha ya, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ka mi' dabeherɛ la bii saŋa la.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: La ani me wuu, nɛra en ye' sore, yi' a a mɛŋa da'aba dikɛ a lɔgerɔ iŋɛ ba nu'uhen.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: A bo' ayima la talenta banuu, bo' ayima talenta bayi', bo' ayima me talenta ayima . A bo' ba doohe la ba mi'ilum paŋa en zuni se'em. Gee kiŋɛ a sore la.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Se'em en to'e talenta anuu la, kiŋɛ mɛ kalam ta leebe ta koohe la talenta banuu nyɛ anuu pa'ahɛ.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Bala mɛa ti se'em me en tari lagekɔba ayi' la me nyɛ lagekɔba ayi' pa'ahɛ.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Gee se'em en to'e ayima la, kiŋɛ ta tu' la boko, dikɛ a daana la lagehɔ pi' tiŋa.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Ti la ta yuue, ti da'aberɛ wa daana la lebe na yi' ba, ti a bihɛ nɛrawoo n tum paaɛ se'em.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Ti se'em n to'e anuu la tole na, tara anuu pa'ahɛ, gee yeti, Daana, fum dikɛ la talenta anuu bo' iŋɛ n nu'uhen, gee bihɛ mam leebe nyɛ la anuu pa'ahɛ.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ti a Daana la yele en yeti, Fum fara fara, fum dela da'aberɛ suŋa tari yelemɛŋerɛ. Fum tari yelemɛŋerɛ lɔgerɔ fii wa puan la, n nyaa wan dikɛ lɔgerɔ kekeko iŋɛ fum nu'uhen. Lagum pa'ahɛ fum daana nyɛɛma la puan.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Ti se'em n to'e talenta ayi' la me tole na yeti, N daana fum dikɛ la bayi' iŋɛ mam nu'uhen, gee bihɛ, mam leebe nyɛ ayi' pa'ahɛ.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Ti a daana la yele en yeti, Fum fara fara, fum dela da'aberɛ suŋa, tara yelemɛŋerɛ. Fum n tari yelemɛŋerɛ yɛla fii wa puan la, n nyaa wan dikɛ lɔgerɔ kekeko iŋɛ fum nu'uhen. Lagum pa'ahɛ fum daana nyɛɛma wa puan.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Ti se'em n to'e talenta ayima la me tole na yeti, Daana, mam mi' ti fum dela sukãberɛ daana, kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra zi'an ti fum ka burɛ, gee buna zi'an ti fum ka lobe bumbuura.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Bala ti dabeem tara mam, ti mam dikɛ fum talenta la ta pi' tiŋa bumɔ n ŋwana.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Ti a daana la lerege en yeti, Fum dela putɔya la zinyaka. Fum mi' ti mam kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la zi'an ti n ka burɛ, gee buna zi'an ti n ka lobege bumbuura la, vaara ze'a ti en ka lobege.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: fum yi nari ni ti fum dikɛ n lagehɔ la ta biŋe ligebiŋere zi'an, ti mam lebe ni na wa, n wan to'e ni n lagehɔ la, gee le nyɛ nyuurɔ pa'ahɛ.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Bala, to'e ya talenta la a zi'an, la ya dikɛ pa'ahɛ se'em n tari talenta pia la.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Sɛba n tari la, bamam n wan nyɛ pa'ahɛ. Sɛba n ka tari la, sɛla fii ti ba pun tara la, ba wan to'e la ba zi'an.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Han dɛna da'aberɛ yaalega kana wa, lobe ya en bahɛ lika puan, zi'an ti kaahega la nyɛna ɔbega bɔna la.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Asaala Dayua han ta wa'am la a suŋa la, ti suŋereba la za'a dɔla en la, a nyaa wan zi'ire a suŋa na'am kuka la zuo.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Tihi wa woo nɛreba za'a wan lagehɛ a nɛŋan, ti a welehi nɛreba yima yima wuu dunkiina en welehi a piihi la buuhi se'em la.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: A wan dikɛ piihi la lebe a zuɔ nu'o bɔba, gee dikɛ buuhi la me lebe a gɔbega nu'o.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Ti naba la nyaa wan yele sɛba n boi a zuɔ nu'o bɔba la yeti, Wa'am ya na, yamam sɛba ti mam Sɔ iŋɛ sum bo' ya wa, la ya sooe sɔ'ɔlum la ti ba maahum biŋe ya tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan ha sɔna.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, kom daa tari mam ti ya bo' mam dia ti n di, ko'nyuuro daa tara mam, ti ya bo' mam ko'om ti n nyu, mam daa dela saana ti ya to'e mam.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: Mam daa eere la beele ti ya bo' mam lɔgerɔ ti n bagɛ, mam daa bɛ̃'ɛri mɛ, ti ya daa bihɛ mam. N daa boi la paka deen, ti ya wa'am na bihɛ mam.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Ŋwana ti tuntɔɔduma la wan zɛ'a yɛli, gee lebe en yeti, Daana, bɛha ti tumam nyɛ fum ti fum kom duna, ti tu bo' fum dia, bii ko'nyuuro tara hɔ ti tu bo' fum ko'om ti fum nyu?.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Bɛha ti tu nyɛ hɔ, ti fum dɛna saana ti tu to'e fum, bii fum zɛ'a beele ti tumam bagɛ fum lɔgerɔ?.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Sankani tu fum bɛ̃'ɛra bii fum bɔna paka deen ti tumam wa'am na bihɛ hɔ.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Ti Naba la wan lerege ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yan daa iŋɛ se'em bo' se'em n pɔ'ɛ mam suurɔ wa puan la, ya daa iŋɛ bo' la mam.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Gee nyaa wan yele sɛba n boi a gɔbega nu'o bɔba la yeti, Yamam sɛba ti n nɔpɔrɛ bɔna ya zuo wa, fɔregɛ ya mam zi'an ta kɛ̃ bugum sebo n dita ka kiŋera, ti ba maahum biŋe Sagena la, la a suŋereba la.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, kom daa tari mam ti ya ka bo' mam dia. Ko'nyuuro tara mam ti ya ka bo' mam ko'om ti n nyu.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: Mam daa dela saana, ti ya ka to'e mam. Beele ti ya ka bagɛ mam fuo. Bɛ̃'ɛra, wa bɔna la paka deen, ti ya ka wa'am na bihɛ mam.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Ti ba me wan lerege yeti, Daana, bɛha ti tumam nyɛ fum ti fum kom duna bii ko'nyuuro tara hɔ, bii fum dɛna saana, bii ɛɛra beele, bii bɛ̃'ɛra, bii bɔna paka deen ti tu ka wa'am na suŋɛ hɔ.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Ti a nyaa wan lerege ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yamam n ka iŋɛ de bo' se'em en de bia mam suurɔ wa puan la, ya ka iŋɛ de bo' la mam n bala.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Ti bana wa wan kiŋɛ namehegɔ seko n ka tari ba'ahegɔ puan, gee ti tuuntɔɔ duma la me kɛ̃ vom kamba'ahɛ puan.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Ti Yezu pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la, ze'ele Yɔɔdan lebe na, ti Sia la tari en kɛ̃ kpaahenɛ puan ha.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: ti a ta bɔna bini dabeha pihinaahi, ti Sagena la makɛ en bihɛ. Dabeha pihinaahi wa puan a ka di sɛla, ti dabeha la en ta ba'ahɛ la, ti kom tara en.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Ti Sagena la yele en yeti, Fum han sirum dɛna la Yinɛ bia, bo' nuurɛ, ti kuga wa lebege boroboro.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, La gulehɛ yeti, Nɛra ka vɔi mɛ la dia ma'a. .

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Ti Sagena la tari en zom zi'an, ta ze'ele bilam pa'alɛ en sɔ'ɔlum la woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo la za'a bilam bilam.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Ti Sagena la yele en yeti, Fum ti en wan dikɛ bana woo, la ba pahi la suŋa la za'a bo'. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba dikɛ ba bo' la mam, ti mam me dikera bɔ'ɔra se'em ti n bɔta.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Fum han wan na'ahɛ mam, ba za'a dela fum dinɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, La gulehɛ yeti, Na'aha Daana la en de fum Yinɛ la, gee tuna bɔ'ɔra eŋa ma'a. .

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Ti Sagena la tari Yezu kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm, tari en ta zom pu'uhegɔ yire la zuin zuin ha, yele en yeti, Fum han sirum dɛna la Yinɛ bia, ze'ele ka'lam lobe fum mɛŋa bahɛ tiŋa ha.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Yinɛ wan bo' a suŋereba nuurɛ, fum zuo, ti ba te'ele fum.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: ti Ba wan te'ele fum la ba nu'uhi, ti fum na'arɛ kan ŋmɛ kugere. .

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, La tɔgɛ biŋe yeti, Da makɛ Daana la en de fum Yinɛ la bihɛ. .

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Sagena la n ba'ahɛ mageha la woo la, a bahɛ en mɛ, gee gura sansɛka ti a wan le nyɛ fole.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Ti Yezu pirɛ la Fa'a Sia paŋa, lebe Galilii, ti a yele to'e tinse'ehi en kaaɛ la za'a.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ pa'alegɔ ba zamehegɔ yiya la puan, ti nɛra woo pɛgera en.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Yezu en lebe Nazareti, zi'an ti a zo'e na la, ta kiŋɛ zamehegɔ yire la puan vo'ohego daarɛ, wuu en ni pun ita se'em la. Nyaa ihege ze'ele ti a kãalɛ.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: ti ba yehe Azaaya gɔŋɔ bo' en. Ti a yirege gɔŋɔ la nyɛ ze'sɛka n gulehɛ yeti.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Daana Sia la en boi la mam, Se'ere en sɔi la, a sɔ mam mɛ ti n tari faarɛ yelehum wa na bo' naheduma. A tum mam na ti n wa'am na muulɛ fai yɛla bo' sɛba en boi bobere puan, gee bihɛ ti fɔɔhi nini pigehɛ gee bahɛ ti sɛba n boi mugehego puan kiŋɛ fai.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: Ti n muulɛ Daana la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ yuunɛ la.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Gee nyaa kilum gɔŋɔ la, dikɛ bo' bituneŋa la, gee lebe zi'ire. Ti nɛra woo n boi zamehegɔ yire la puan la nini kɔ'ɔm kpa' bihera en ziki.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Ti a pɔhɛ yele ba yeti, Zina, gɔŋɔ wa zɛ'kana wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na ya tuban.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Ti nɛra woo pɛgera en, gee ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ la zu'uhegɔ yele sɛ'a n ze'eti a nuuren na la. Ti ba yɛta yeti, Dagi Yosefe dayua la n bala.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam ka bu'uri, ti ya wan dikɛ yetɔgum biŋere se'ere n yeti, Tibera, tibɛ fum mɛŋa yia la, yele mam. Ya me wan yele mam yeti, Tum ka'lam me, fum mɛŋa tiŋa wa puan, daalehum ti tu wum ti fum tum si Kapɛnum la. .

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta wa, ti nɔtɔgeha ka boi' ti a mɛŋa tiŋa nɛreba to'e en.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: De yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, pokɔpa kekeko yuum boi Izirale tiŋa puan mɛ Elia saŋa la, ti saazuo yuum yu', yuuma atã la ŋmarehi siyoobe la. Ti kompɛɛlega yuum to'e tiŋa la za'a la.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: Gee Yinɛ yuum ka bahɛ ti Elia kiŋɛ ba se'em zi'an, han dagena pokuurɛ se'ere n boi Zarefati tiŋa n boi Sidɔn tiŋa la.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Insageŋɔ duma me yuum zo'e mɛ Izirale tiŋa wa puan nɔtɔgeha Elisa saŋa la, gee ba ayima me yuum ka nyɛ tibegɔ han dagena Siriya tiŋa buraa Neeman ma'a.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Ban wum ŋwana la, ba woo n boi zamehegɔ yire la puan la suya nyiie mɛ.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Ti ba vaaɛ ihege sa'ɛ en, tari en yehe tiŋa la yiŋa ha, tari en ta paaɛ zoore la ti ba tiŋa mɛ yaga de zuo la zuleŋa ha, ti ba za'ɛ en bahɛ tiŋa ha.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Gee a lilum la ba puan yehe kiŋɛ.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Ti Yezu sige Kapɛnum n boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan la, ta pa'ala nɛreba vo'ohego daarɛ la.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ, la a pa'alegɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, a pa'ali mɛ ti paŋa bɔna bini.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Zamehegɔ yire la puan, buraa n tari sɛfi'iha boi bini, kaahɛ tagi tagi bahɛ na yeti.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Bahɛ tu bããlam, beni tu fum tara la tɔ, Nazareti buraa Yezu wa? Fum wa'am na ti fum sagum tu mɛ? Mam mi' fum n de se'em, fum dela Yinɛ Fa'a nɛra.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Ti Yezu tahɛ en yeti, Go'e suram gee yehe a puan na. Ti sɛfi'iha la fɔbɛ buraa wa tiŋa nɛreba la nɛŋan, gee nyaa yehe na, gee ka pɔgelum buraa la.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛra woo pakerɛ, ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Beni yele ŋwana? Kpe'eŋo la paŋa ti a tara tahɛ sɛfi'iha ti si yɛha.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Ti ba pɔhɛ tɔgera a yele tiŋa la zi'an woo puan.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Yezu n bahɛ zamehegɔ yire la, a kɛ̃ la Simɔn yire. Bala ma'a ti Simɔn dɛɛmpɔka n boi yire la puan ti ka iŋa tulegɛ, ti ba tɔgɛ ka yele bo' Yezu.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Ti Yezu paaɛ ze'ele ka zuin, tahɛ intulegerɛ la, ti bã'a la bahɛ pɔka la. Ti ka ihege bilam bilam bo' ba ko'om.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Zaanuurɛ n ta ma'ara la, ti sɛba woo n tari ba nɛreba ti ba bɛ̃'ɛra bã'ahi yima yima la, tari ba kina la Yezu zi'an na, ti a tara a nu'uhi pagela ba ayima ayima zuo, tibera ba.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi me zɛ'ɛta ba zo'e zo'e puan yɛhera na gee tahera yeti, Fum n de Yinɛ Dayua la. Gee ti a ni tahɛ ba, ka bahera ti ba tɔgera, se'ere n sɔi la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la mi' ti Yezu n de Kiresta la.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti a bahɛ bilam gee kiŋɛ zi'an ti nɛreba ka bɔna bini. Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ ɛɛra en, ban paaɛ en la, ba boti ba gu' en mɛ ti a da bahɛ ba.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, La dela n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la n de Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum yɛla la bo' tihi sise'ehi me. Se'ere n sɔi la ba tum mam la bala zuo na.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Bala ti a dɔla muula yelehum la Yudiya zamehegɔ yiya la puan.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Yuuma pia la anuu, Kaiza Tiberiyusi na'am kuka zuo saŋa la, ti Pɔntiyɔsi Palete dɛna timbihega nɛŋa daana Yudiya, ti Hɛrɔd me sɔna Galilii bihera, ti a suuɔ Filipi me sɔna Iturea la Tirakonitise tihi bihera, ti Lisaniyase me sɔna Abilene tiŋa bihera.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: ti Annas la Kaifase dɛna malema kiinduma la, ti Yinɛ dikɛ a yelehum doohe Zekariya dayua Yɔn puan, muɔ puan ha.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Ti Yɔn doohe tihi la woo en kaaɛ Yɔɔdan la, muula teere ko'om sua, la be'em sugeri dia, bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: wuu lan gulehɛ se'em nɔtɔgeha Azaaya gɔŋɔ la puan se'em yeti, Nɛra ayima kua en boi muuen kaaha baha na yeti, Maalɛ Daana la sore la, Maalɛ ya a sadɔtɔ la tuuntɔɔ.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Naherɛ woo Palehɛ, ti zoore la kunkune woo sirege, ti sadɔɔ ŋmɛleŋɔ woo tiɛ, ti sadɔtɔ-ɛ̃rema woo saalum.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: gee ti saala woo nyɛ Yinɛ Faarɛ la.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Yɔn yele nɛrekuuŋɔ la en yehe na ti a bo' ba ko'om sua la yeti, Kampɛbibehi wa, ani en kagɛ ya ti ya zɔ yehe Yinɛ sunsunyiim tuulɛ la en ye' na la puan?.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Bala zuo, wam ya wala sɛ'a en pa'alɛ ti ya tee mɛ. Gee da yɛta ya mɛhi ti, Aberaham en de tumam yaaba. N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti Yinɛ ta'am bahɛ ti kuga wa a sɛ'a lebege kɔma bo' Aberaham.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nananawa, liya la maahum mɛ gã'a tiihi nyɛga zuto, ti tisɛka woo en ka wani walesuma, ka ŋmaaɛ bahɛ, lobe ka bahɛ bugum puan.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la soke en yeti, Beni ti tu nyaa wan iŋɛ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Ti Yɔn lerege ba yeti, Se'em woo en tari futo tuyi', tɔrɛ bo' se'em woo en ka tari la. Fum se'em woo en tari dita, me iŋɛ bala mɛa.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Ti kubetɔ'ɔhereba me lagum wa'am na ti ba sɔ ko'om la, gee soke en yeti, Pa'ala, beni ti tumam me wan iŋɛ.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Da to'ohe ya kuba la gaŋɛ lan de se'em.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Ti pɛntiireba me soke en yeti, Gee tumam, beni ti tu wan iŋɛ? Ti a yele ba yeti, Da pɛregɛ ya se'em to'e ligeri, bii ya zuuhe se'em gee ka nyɛ, gee me bahɛ ti ya nini tigɛ la ya yɔɔrɛ ma'a.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Nɛreba suya en daa pun maahum gura la, ba daa nyaa sokere mɛ ba suya puan nyaa,Yɔn wan dɛna Kiresta la?.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Ti Yɔn lerege ba za'a yeti, Mam sɔɔri ya mɛ la ko'om wa, gee se'em en kpe'em gana mam kini na, mam ka nari n pun lorege a tagera mi'a. Eŋa wan sɔ ya mɛ la Fa'a Sia, la bugum.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: A zãli la a yeelego wanɛ, ti a yeele sɛla woo en boi a tooren la, la a mui la iŋɛ a bããrɛ puan, gee vagela la, a wan nyɔ' ba bugum sebo en ka kiŋeri puan.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Bala,Yɔn dikɛ la kpemehego yɛla zo'e zo'e tɔgɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' nɛreba la.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Gee Hɛrɔd en de naba ti Yɔn tahɛ en doohe en fa' Hɛrɔdiya en de a suuɔ la pɔga di la, la tuunfi'iha sɛ'a woo ti Hɛrɔd tum la.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: ta pa'ahɛ tuunfi'iherɛ se'ere en gani za'a la, dɛna en pagɛ Yɔn paka deen la.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Nananawa Nɛreba la za'a en ta to'e ko'om sua ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu me tɔ'ɔra a ko'om sua gee a pu'uheri la, ti saazuo yu'ɛ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Ti Fa'a Sia la sige a zuo na, la naanɛ en ani wuu dayene la. Ti kua ze'ele saazuo ha tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Fum en de mam Dayua ti n nɔŋɛ, mam nini tigɛ mɛ la fum.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Yezu ta dɛna wuu yuum pihitã, gee pɔhɛ a muulegɔ tuuma. A sɔ, dela Yosefe, en de Hɛli dayua.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: se'em sɔ en de Matati, se'em sɔ en de Levi, se'em sɔ en de Meliki, se'em sɔ en de Yanayi, se'em sɔ en de Yosefe.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: se'em sɔ en de Matatiyase, se'em sɔ en de Amɔsi, se'em sɔ en de Nahum, se'em sɔ en de Ɛsili, se'em sɔ en de Nagai.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: se'em sɔ en de Maati, se'em sɔ en de Matatiyase, se'em sɔ en de Sɛmin, se'em sɔ en de Yoseki, se'em sɔ en de Yoda.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: se'em sɔ en de Yowanan, se'em sɔ en de Aresa, se'em sɔ en de Zerubabɛle, se'em sɔ en de Siyotiyɛl, se'em sɔ en de Nɛri.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: se'em sɔ en de Meliki, se'em sɔ en de Adi, se'em sɔ en de Kosam, se'em sɔ n de Elimadam, se'em sɔ en de Eri.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: se'em sɔ en de Yosua, se'em sɔ en de Ɛliyaza, se'em sɔ en de Yorim, se'em sɔ en de Matati, sɔ en de Levi.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: se'em sɔ en de Simeyɔn, se'em sɔ en de Yuda, se'em sɔ en de Yosefe, se'em sɔ en de Yonam, se'em sɔ en de Eliyakim.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: se'em sɔ en de Meleya, se'em sɔ en de Mina, se'em sɔ en de Natan, se'em sɔ en de Deviti.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: se'em sɔ n de Yesi, se'em sɔ en de Ɔbɛd, se'em sɔ en de Boazi, se'em sɔ en de Sala, se'em sɔ en de Nason.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: se'em sɔ en de Aminadabi, se'em sɔ en de Adim, se'em sɔ en de Aram, se'em sɔ en de Hɛzirɔn, se'em sɔ en de Pɛrezi, se'em sɔ n de Yuda.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: se'em sɔ en de Yakɔbe, se'em sɔ n de Aziki, se'em sɔ n de Aberaham, se'em sɔ n de Tɛra, se'em sɔ n de Nahɔ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: se'em sɔ n de Seruge, se'em sɔ n de Aru, se'em sɔ n de Pelege, se'em sɔ n de Ɛbɛ, se'em sɔ n de Sɛla.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: se'em sɔ en de Keenan, se'em sɔ n de Afagezad, se'em sɔ n de Sɛm, se'em sɔ n de Nowa, se'em sɔ n de Lamɛki.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: se'em sɔ n de Mɛtusɛla, se'em sɔ n de Enɔki, se'em sɔ en de Yared se'em sɔ n de Mahalaleel, se'em sɔ n de Keenan.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: se'em sɔ n de Enose, se'em sɔ n de Sɛti, se'em sɔ n de Adam, se'em sɔ en de Yinɛ.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Saŋa kayima ti Yezu zɛ'a lɛm Genesarete ateko la ti nɛrekuuŋɔ mike za'ara en na ti ba wum Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: ti Yezu nyɛ ti uurenɔ tuyi' boi mɔgerɛ la ko'om la nɔgbene. Bala ma'a ti zim nyɔgereba la bahɛ uurenɔ la mɛ, gee pɛɛra ba yugehi.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Ti Yezu zom uureŋɔ la kuyima n de Simɔn dinɛ, gee nyaa yele Simɔn yeti, za'ɛ uureŋɔ wa ti ku kiŋɛ nɛŋa fii ha. Ti a nyaa zi'a uureŋɔ la zuo gee pa'ala nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: En tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ la, a yele Simɔn yeti, Za'ɛ fum uureŋɔ la bahɛ ko'kɔ'ɔlum la ha, lobe fum yuka la bilam, gee fum wan nyɔgɛ zim.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Ti Simɔn yele en yeti, Naa, tumam zaam tum la bala yu'uŋɔ na, gee ka nyɔkɛ la biileŋa me, gee fum han yele bala, n wan lobe yuka la.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Ban iŋɛ bala la, ba nyɔgɛ la zim zo'e zo'e, ti ba yuka la pɔhɛ aaheŋɔ.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Ti ba nyaa yi' ba taaba n me boi ba uureŋɔ puan ti ba wa'am na suŋɛ ba, ti ba wa'am na pirɛ uurenɔ tuyi' la za'a, ti uurenɔ la pɔhɛ muhera.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Gee Simɔn Pita n nyɛ ŋwana la, a lu la Yezu nama tiŋa yɛta ti, Kiŋɛ gee bahɛ mam n Daana, mam dela be'em Daana.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Pita la sɛba woo n boi la en la, la iŋɛ ba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ la zim la n ba' zo'e se'em ti ba nyɔgɛ la.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Bala mɛa ti la iŋɛ Yemesi la Yɔn n de Zebedii dayɔɔhi, gee dɛna taaba la Simɔn la. Ti Yezu yele Simɔn yeti, Da zɔta dabeem, yehe nananawa yɛ'a ha, fum yeti fum tara la nɛreba kina na bɔ'ɔra mam.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Ban tari ba uurenɔ la dooe doone na la, ba bahɛ sɛlawoo mɛ, gee dɔla Yezu.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Saŋa kayima, ti Yezu bɔna tiŋa kayima puan, ti insageŋɔ kɔ'ɔm to'e buraa kayima iŋa za'a. En nyɛ Yezu la, a suure la tiŋa paaɛ paaɛ, gee belum Yezu yeti, Daana, fum han bɔta, fum tibɛ mam.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Ti Yezu tiɛ a nu'o kalum en gee yeti, Mam boti. Nyɛ fum tibegɔ. Bilam bilam ti insageŋɔ la bahɛ en.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Ti Yezu bo' en nuurɛ ti a da yele nɛra nɛra se'ere. Kiŋɛ, la fum dikɛ fum mɛŋa ta pa'alɛ malema daana la, gee bo' tibegɔ bo'olum la wuu Moose n bo' ya nuurɛ se'em la, ti la dɛna aze' nyɛ daana bo' ba.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Gee saŋa kana wa la ti Yezu yele pun malum yiregɛ mɛ zi'an woo gana dinɛ n tabelɛ yiregɛ se'em. Nɛreba kekeko ni lagehɛ mɛ ti ba kelehe en, gee nyɛ ba bã'ahi tibega.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Gee a ni yehe mɛ, kiŋɛ zi'an ti nɛra ka bɔna bini a ma'a ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti Yezu pa'ala, ti Farasiiduma, la sɛba n pa'alɛ Moose ze'elego la zi'a bilam bɔba , ti Daana la paŋa bɔna la en, ti a tibera.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Sankaŋa mɛa puan ti buraahi sise'ehi ziira kɔɔreŋɔ suŋɔ puan kina na. Ba zigɛ mɛ ti ba tari ni en paaɛ Yezu nɛŋan biŋe.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: gee ban ka nyɛ fole wan tari en paaɛ Yezu nɛŋan nɛrekuuŋɔ la zuo la, ba zom la deo la zuin ha, ta ahegɛ fole bini, dikɛ en doohe bilam a suŋɔ la puan, wa'am na ze'ele nɛreba la tiŋasuka, Yezu nɛŋan.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yezu n nyɛ ba sakerɛ la, a yele kɔɔreŋɔ la yeti, Asaala wa, ba bahɛ fum be'em la bo' fum.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Ti gɔnmineba la, la Farasiiduma la pɔhɛ sɔkera yeti, Ani n de ena tɔgera tuura bala? Ani n wan ta'am bahɛ nɛra be'em han dagena Yinɛ ma'a.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Yezu n baŋɛ ban sokere se'ere ba suya puan la, ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya sɔkera sokere dina taaba ya suya puan?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: De deni n de naana ti n yele, N bahɛ fum be'em bahɛ la bii, ihege la fum kina?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Gee ŋwana n wan bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ ti, mam Asaala Dayua la tari paŋa tiŋa wa zuo, ti n bahɛ nɛra be'em bo' en. Gee nyaa yele kɔɔreŋɔ la yeti, Mam yeti fum mɛ Ihege, dikɛ fum suŋɔ kula.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Bilam bilam ti kɔɔreŋɔ wa ihege ze'ele ku nama zuo ba nɛŋan, vaaɛ a suŋɔ la ti a ni gã'a ku zuo la, kula gee pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Ti la pakɛ nɛra woo paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba pɛgera Yinɛ, gee ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba, ti ba yɛta yeti, Tu nyɛ yɛla en boi a tɔka zina.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ŋwana pooren, ti a yehe ta nyɛ ti Kuba tɔ'ɔha ti a yu'urɛ yi'iri Levi, zi'a kuba to'ohego zi'an. Ti a yele en yeti, Dɔla mam.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Ti a ihege, bahɛ sɛla woo gee dɔla en.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Ti Levi wa ta maahum de'eŋo kãtɛ yi' Yezu, ti kuba tɔ'ɔha duma zo'e zo'e, la nɛreba basɛba, wa'am na lagum zi'a dɛla taaba la ba, dia zi'an la.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ti Farasiiduma, la gɔnmi'ineba la nyɔna bɔ'ɔra Yezu zahereba la yeti, Ŋwani ti yamam dita gee nyuura la kubetɔ'ɔhereba, la be'em duma wa.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Sɛba n ka bɛ̃'ɛri, ka eere tiba, gee sɛba n bɛ̃'ɛri.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Mam ka wa'am na ti n yi' la tuntɔɔduma, gee be'em duma ti ba tee.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Ti ba nyaa yele Yezu yeti, Yɔn zahereba, la Farasiiduma zahereba la luti ba nuurɛ tabela mɛ, gee pu'uha, gee fum dɔleba wa kɔ'ɔm dita mɛ gee nyuura.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Fum kan ta'am iŋɛ ti pɔgedire saama lu' nuurɛ gee ti pɔgedita la nan bɔna la ba. Fum wan nyaŋɛ?.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Dabeha kini na, ti ba wan dikɛ pɔgedita la kiŋɛ gee bahɛ ba, bilam ti ba nyaa wa luta nɔya dabeha aŋa puan.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Ti Yezu tɔgɛ makerɛ bo' ba yeti, Nɛra ka anhegere fupaalega tanɛ pɛ'ɛra ligera fukɛka yoko. A han iŋɛ bala, tanpaalɛ la wan ahegɛ gee bahɛ fukɛka la. Le yɛ'ɛha, tanɛ paalɛ la pun kan naaɛ la fukɛka la.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: Nɛra me ka boi dikeri daam n kele, su'ura laŋa kɛka puan. A han iŋɛ bala, daam la n kele la wan tahɛ laŋa la, gee ka'ɛ, ti laŋa la me sagum.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Gee daam n nan kele, dela bu su' daam laŋa paalega puan.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Nɛra me ka boi nyu zaam daam n kele ba'ahɛ, gee le bɔta zina daam n ka kele. A wan yeti, Zaam daam la ani suŋa. .

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Yezu n tɔgɛ yele ana woo nɛreba la tuban ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ la Kapɛnum.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Bilam ti pɛntiira en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega tara a da'aberɛ en tari tuulum bo' en ti de bɛ̃'ɛra yɛ'a kum.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: En ta wum Yezu yele la, a tum la Yuuduma kiinduma basɛba a zi'an ti ba ta belum en ti a wa'am na tibɛ a da'aberɛ la.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yezu zi'an la, ba belum en la ninmu'urɛ yeti, Nɛra la nari mɛ ti fum tum yele dina bo' en.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a nɔŋɛ tu tiŋa wa mɛ, eŋa n mɛ zamehegɔ yire bo' tu.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Ti Yezu dɔla ba yɛ'a buraa la yire, gee ban ta pɔra buraa la yire la, ti pɛntiira en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega wa tum a zɔduma ti ba tu'uhɛ Yezu sore zuo na yele en yeti, Daana, da dagum daam fum mɛŋa yɛ'a mam yire na, gee mam ka nari fum wa'am n yire na.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Bala zuo ti mam ka ti'ihɛ ti n wa'am fum nɛŋan na n mɛŋa. Fum yeen tum fum yelehum la ma'a bahɛ na, gee mam dabeherɛ wa wan iŋɛ sum.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam me dela se'em ti ba bo' mam kpe'eŋo, ti n sɔna pɛntiireba bihera. N han yele ba ayima yeti, Kiŋɛ, a ye' mɛ. N han yele ayima me yeti, Wa'ana, a ye' me na. N han yele n da'aberɛ yeti, Iŋɛ ŋwana, da'aberɛ la iti mɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Yezu n wum ŋwana la, la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ la buraa la, ti a ŋmɛregɛ tɔ nɛrekuuŋɔ la n dole en la yeti, N yeti ya me wa, ti Izirale puan me, mam ka nyɛ sakerɛ kãtɛ dina taaba.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Kpɛ'ɛma wa n tum burase'ehi na la en lebe ta paara yire la, ba nyɛ ti da'aberɛ la de a mɛŋa.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Ŋwana n tole la, ti Yezu ta kiŋɛ tiŋa n yi'iri Nain, ti a zahereba la, la nɛrekuuŋɔ zo'e zo'e doohe en.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Bihɛ, en ta pɔra tiŋa la nayaŋa la, ti buraa n ki, ti ba ziira yɛhera. Buraa wa dela ka ma bia za'a za'a n bala, ti pɔka wa me dɛna pokuurɛ. Ti nɛreba kekeko yehe tiŋa la puan lagum bɔna la pɔka wa.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Daana la n nyɛ en la, a zɔ la a nimbu'ɔ, gee yele en yeti, Da kɛla.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Gee tole nɛŋa na, paaɛ kalum suŋɔ la, ti bayaahi la teke ze'ele. Ti Yezu yeti, Budibe paalega wa, N yeti fum ti fum ihege.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Ti budibela wa n ki la, ihege zi'ire, pɔhɛ tɔgera, ti Yezu dikɛ en bo' a ma la.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Ti dabeem kɛ̃ nɛra woo, ti ba dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ yeti, Yinɛ nɔtɔgeha kãtɛ n boi la tɔ. Gee le yeti, Yinɛ n kaaɛ a nɛreba wa bihɛ.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Ti Yezu yeledina wa to'e Yuda, la tinse'ehi woo n lɛm la puan za'a.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
