local: Ti Yɔn zahereba tari yele ana za'a ta yele Yɔn. Ti Yɔn yi' a zahereba bayi' na.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: tum ba Daana zi'an, ti ba ta soke en yeti, Fum n de se'em n wan wa'am na la bii, tu gura ayima ha.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Buraahi la n wa'am na paaɛ Yezu zi'an la, ba yele en yeti, Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la n tum tɔ fum zi'an na, ti tu wa'am na soke fum yeti, Fum de se'em n wan wa'am na la bii, tu gura ayima me ha? .

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Bala ma'a la ti Yezu nan kɔ'ɔm tibɛ la nɛreba kekeko bã'ahi, too, sa'ahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee pigehɛ fɔɔhi kekeko nini.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Bala ti a yele buraahi wa yeti, Ta yele ya Yɔn, yan nyɛ sɛla, la yan wum sɛla. Fɔɔhi nini pigehɛ mɛ, ti kɔɔrenɔ ihege kina, ti kunkɔma insageŋɔ bahɛ, ti wahi wuna, ti kum vo'e, ti tarenduma wum faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Sum boi mɛ la se'em daana n ka piti luta mam zuo.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Yɔn bitunehi la n tole la, ti Yezu pɔhɛ tɔgera Yɔn yele bɔ'ɔra nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Beni ti ya daa ni yehe kiŋɛ muɔ la puan ti ya ta bihɛ? Muɔ ti sabeeo tara vɛgelena?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Beni ti ya daa ni yehe kiŋɛ ti ya ta bihɛ? Nɛra n bagɛ banaahi? Bihɛ ya, sɛba n bagerɛ banaahi gee bɔna na'am puan kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la na'am yiya puan.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Beni ti ya nyaa daa ni yehe kɛ̃ muɔ la puan ha ti ya bihɛ? Nɔtɔgeha? Ɛ̃ɛ, N yi yele ya yeti, a dela se'em n gani nɔtɔgeha.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Eŋa n bala ti ba gulehɛ a yele yeti, Bihɛ, n tuni la n bituneŋa, ti ka diŋɛ fum nɛŋa na la, se'em n wan wa'am na maahum fum sore biŋe fum la.

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: N yeti ya me wa, ti pɔka en dɔgɛ saaleba woo puan, nɛra ka boi gana Yɔn, gee la bala za'a, se'em n de bia Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan la, gani en mɛ.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Ti sɛba woo n wum yelehum wa la, ta ta' la sɛba n to'ohe kuba la, baŋɛ Yinɛ buurɔ la mɛ, beni zuo, bamam n daa sakɛ to'e Yɔn ko'om sua la.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Gee Farasiiduma la, la yetɔge tɔgereba n ka sakɛ ti Yɔn sɔ ba ko'om la, zagehɛ la Yinɛ n tari se'ere bɔ'ɔra ba la.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Beni ti mam wan ta'am dikɛ zina beere nɛreba wa makɛ? Ba ŋwani mɛ la beni?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Ba ani me wuu kɔma n zi' da'a puan gee yi'ira taaba yeti, Tumam peebe la yia bahɛ hɔ, gee fum ka wa' Tumam fabele mɛ, gee ti fum ka kele.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la wa'am me na, Ka dita dia, ka nyuura, daam ti ya yeti, A tari la kulekpa'bɛ'a.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ti mam Asaala Dayua wa wa'am na, dita gee nyuura, ti ya yeti, Bihɛ, a dela diiha la dabugera. Nyɔgera zɔtɔ la sɛba n to'ohe kuba, la be'em duma.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Gee la kan dɛna bala. Yɛm kɔma vom n wan bo' en buurɔ.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Farasii ayima n yi' Yezu ti a wa'am na yire na di la en. Ti Yezu kiŋɛ Farasii la yire, ti ba zi'ire dɛla taaba dia saŋa la.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Ti pɔka n boi tiŋa la puan dɛna be'em daana, baŋɛ ti Yezu boi Farasii la yire ti a di, me tari a kaam n tari nyuuŋo suŋɔ la lila ti ba dikɛ kuga suma maalɛ mɛ'ɛ na.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: Paaɛ na zɛ'a a pooren kɛla, ti a nintam zɔgera a nama zuo, ma'alɛ a nama, ti a dɔbelɛ tiŋa dikɛ a zuo zonto duuhe a nama la, gee mɔgera a nama, gee tara kaam la sɔ̃ɔra Yezu nama la.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Farasii sɛka n yi' Yezu wa'am a yire na la n nyɛ ŋwana la, a yele a mɛŋa yeti, Buraa wa han deni nɔtɔgeha, a wan baŋɛ ni pɔka wa n de se'em, la en de pɔgesɛka taaba kalena en wa. A wan baŋɛ ni ti ka dela be'em daana.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Ti Yezu tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Simɔn, mam tari la se'ere ti n yele fum. Ti Simɔn lerege yeti, Pa'ala, yele mam na.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Lige pɛmeha ayima ti nɛreba bayi' tara a sanɛ. Ayima peŋe la dinaari kɔbehi pihinuu, ti ayima me peŋe dinaari pihinuu.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Ti ba za'a n ta koŋe ba yɔ la, ti lige pɛmeha la bahɛ bo' ba zaŋa. Ba ini n wan nɔŋɛ lige pɛmeha wa gaŋɛ za'a.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Ti Simɔn lerege yeti, Mam ti'ihɛ ti se'em sanɛ n ba' zo'e gaŋɛ la. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum kɔ'ɔm lerege la ma'a.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Gee nyaa ŋmɛregɛ tɔ pɔka la, gee yele Simɔn yeti, Fum nyɛ pɔka wa? Mam kɛ̃ fum yire wa na la, fum ka bo' mam ko'om ti n pee n nama, eŋa n pee mam nama wa la a nintam, duuhe la a zonto.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Fum ka mɔgɛ mam gee mam kɛ̃ na daarɛ, ka go'e mam nama mɔka.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: Fum ka so' mam zuo la kaam, gee kaŋa n so' mam nama la tuulum kaam.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Bala zuo, n yele fum ti, pɔka wa be'em la n zo'e se'em la, n bahɛ bo' n, bala zuo ti ka pa'ala nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ la wa. Gee fum se'em be'em n ka zo'e ti ba bahɛ bo' fum, fum daana nɔŋerɛ ani la fii wa.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Gee nyaa yele pɔka la yeti, Fum be'em la bahɛ bo' fum.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Ti sɛba n lagum zi'a dɛla taaba dia saŋa la la pɔhɛ nyɔna ba suya puan yeti, Ani n de ena pun baha nɛra be'em bahɛ.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Ti Yezu yele pɔka la yeti, Kina, fum sakerɛ n faaɛ fum bahɛ, kina la suma'ahum.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ deyima, ti Yezu dɔla ki va'am puan tɔla, ti a zahereba la kɔ'ɔra ki la ŋmiina pɛbeha ɔbera.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la basɛba nyɛ yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ita sɛla n dagi ma'a vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yamam ka kãalɛ sɛla ti Deviti yuum iŋɛ, eŋa la a taaba la ti kom yuum tara ba la?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: Deviti yuum kɛ̃ la Yinɛ yire la puan ta dikɛ Tuulum boroboro la n pa'ali ti Yinɛ boi la tu la, dɛna kihere ti nɛra nɛra ka ɔbera, han dagena malema daana la ma'a la? Deviti yuum obe de mɛ, gee bo' a taaba la.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Gee nyaa yele ba yeti, Asaala Dayua la n de vo'ohego dabeherɛ la Daana.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ deyima ti Yezu kɛ̃' zamehegɔ yire la puan ta pa'ala, ti buraa bɔna bini ti ka zuɔ nu'o ki.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasiiduma la ana kɛli gee biha nyaa a wan tibɛ en vo'ohego dabeherɛ la puan ti ba ta'am nyɛ se'ere zuuhe en.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: La Yezu n pun mina ban ti'ihɛ se'em la, a yele buraa la nu'o n ki la yeti, Wa'ana ze'ele ka'lam. Ti buraa la ihege ze'ele.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Ti Yezu yele nɛreba la n boi bini la yeti. N soke ya, la de ma'a ti nɛra tum sum vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan bii a tum be'em? A faaɛ vom bahɛ bii a sagum vom.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Ti Yezu bihɛ nɛreba la kaaɛ, gee yele buraa la yeti, Tiɛ fum nu'o la. Ti a tiɛ a nu'o la, ti nu'o la iŋɛ sum.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Gee lan iŋɛ bala la, la daam ba mɛ, ti ba suure nyiie, ti ba tɔgera la taaba ban wan iŋɛ Yezu se'em.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Dabeha aŋa mɛa puan ti Yezu yehe kiŋɛ zoore zuo ha ti a ta pu'uhɛ, ta bɔna bini ha yu'uŋɔ la za'a pu'uha Yinɛ.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Ti beere en ta yilegɛ la, ti a yi' a zahereba la za'a na, nyaa looe ba pia la bayi' sigɛ ba yu'urɛ tuntuneba.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Simɔn ti a sigɛ en Pita, la a suuɔ Anduru, Yemesi, Yɔn, Filipi, Batolomi.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: Matu, Tɔmasi, Yemesi dayua Alifeyusi Simɔn ti ba yi'ira Zɛlɔti la.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: la Yudasi en de Yemesi dayua, la Yudasi Isikariyɔte se'em en ŋma'ara la.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Ti a sige na la ba, ze'ele zi'an en naaɛ taaba la a zahereba la zo'e zo'e, la nɛrekuuŋɔ en ze'ele Yuda tiŋa bɔberɛ woo na, la Yerusalɛm bɔberɛ woo, ta ta' la Taai la Sidɔn tihi ateko nɔgbɛma tihi la.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Ba woo wa'am na ti ba kelehe la Yezu pa'alegɔ, gee ti a tibɛ ba bã'ahi, ti sɛba ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi daana ba me nyɛ ba tibegɔ.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a zigera ti ba kalum Yezu zi'an se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en kalum en, paŋa en ni ze'ele a puan yehe na tibɛ a daana.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Ti Yezu gorege bihɛ a zahereba la gee yeti, Sum boi mɛ la yamam sɛba n nameheri wa, Se'ere en sɔi la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la dela ya dinɛ.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Sum boi mɛ la yamam sɛba ti kom tara ya nananawa, Se'ere en sɔi Ya wan ta tigɛ. Sum boi mɛ la yamam sɛba en kele nananawa, Se'ere en sɔi la Ya wan ta la'.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Sum boi mɛ la ya, nɛreba han yaaɛ ya, ka dikera ya pa'ahɛ nɛreba puan, tuura ya gee Sagena ya yu'ura Asaala Dayua wa zuo.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: nyɛɛni ya dabeherɛ la puan, ɛgeri ya la nyɛɛma se'ere en sɔi la, yɔkãtɛ boi saazuo ha gura ya gee ba yaabeduma en yuum pun iŋɛ nɔtɔgeheba la se'em n bala.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Gee yele boi buntateba zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya pun diŋɛ nyɛ ya sumbugehum mɛ.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Yele boi yamam sɛba en tigɛ nananawa, se'ere en sɔi la, kom wan ta nyɔkɛ ya. Yele boi yamam sɛba en la'ari nananawa la, se'ere en sɔi la, ya wan ta kɛla gee si'ina.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Yele boi fum se'em daana ti nɛra woo tɔgera fum sum yɛla ma'a, se'ere en sɔi la ba yaabeduma yuum iti pumpã'ahum nɔtɔgeheba la la bala.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Gee mam yeti yamam sɛba en kelehere mam wa yeti, Nɔŋɛ ya dinduma, iti ya sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba en yaaɛ ya.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: ka'ari ya sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba n pɔti nɔya bɔ'ɔra ya, gee suha Yinɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en tuure ya.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Se'em han lobe fum sapɛka, ŋmɛregɛ ka yima la bo' en ti a lobe pa'ahɛ. Se'em han dikɛ fum daansika , dikɛ banaa la pa'ahɛ en.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Bo' se'em woo en suhɛ fum sɛla, ti se'em me han dikɛ fum lɔkɔ, da soke en ku yele.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Ita bɔ'ɔra sɛba wuu fum boti ba ita se'em bɔ'ɔra fum la.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Ya han nɔŋɛ sɛba en nɔŋɛ ya ma'a, beni nyuurɔ ti ya wan nyɛ? Be'em duma pun nɔŋɛ la sɛba en nɔŋɛ ba ma'a.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Ya han ita sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba n iti ya sum ma'a, beni nyuurɔ ti ya wan nyɛ? Be'em duma la pun iti bala me mɛ.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Ya han suŋera la sɛba ti ya mina ti ba wan lebehe bo' ya ma'a, beni nyuurɔ n wan bɔna bo' ya? Be'emduma pun bɔ'ɔra la be'emduma, ti ba malum nyɛ pa'ahɛ.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Gee nɔŋɛ ya dinduma, iti ya sum, gee bɔ'ɔra, gee da ti'ihɛ ya ti ya lebehe to'e sɛla. Ya yɔɔrɛ wan dɛna yɔkãtɛ, gee ti ya dɛna Gaŋɛ za'a Yinɛ wa kɔma. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ iti sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba en ka nane sɛla la putɔpa mɛ.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Bala, tari ya nimbɔ'zɔrɛ wuu ya Sɔ en tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ se'em la.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Da gakɛ, ti ba da gakɛ fum me, Da sagum ayima, ti ba da sagum fum me. Bahɛ ayima tuure bo' en, ti ba bahɛ fum me tuure bo' fum.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Bo' gee ba me wan lebehe bo' fum. Ba wan bo' ti la zo'e, di'ihe, zugehe zugehe, ti la pirɛ suŋa, yabera, ti fum to'e ɔ̃belɛ nyu'ɔ. Fum n makɛ se'em tihɛ la, bala mɛa ti fum wan lebehe to'e.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Ti Yezu le dikɛ makerɛ tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Fua wan ta'am tari fua ti ba kina? Ba za'a kan ta lu boko puan?.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Zahera ka gani a pa'ala. Gee se'em woo n zaŋɛ wan ana wuu a pa'ala la.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Ŋwani ti fum nyɛta sɔkɔ n boi fum suuɔ niho puan, gee ka mina ti dibega boi fum niho puan?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Bii fum wan iŋɛ la ŋwani yele fum suuɔ yeti, N suuɔ, bahɛ ti n dikɛ sɔkɔ wa n boi fum niho wa puan bahɛ, gee ka nyɛta dibega la n boi fum mɛŋa niho la puan la. Birekɔgehi wa, dikɛ dibega la n boi fum mɛŋa niho puan la bahɛ yia, gee la fum nyɛta suŋa la fum ta'am dikɛ sɔkɔ la n boi fum suuɔ niho la puan la bahɛ.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Tisuŋa ka boi wuna wala pu'uha, bii tia n ka ani suŋa wuna walesuma.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Ba mi' tia woo doohe la ka wala puan. Ba ka pi'ihe mu'uro zaahi tiŋa, bii ba pi'iha ta'ama tuntuuro tiŋa.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Nɛresuŋɔ la en tari yelesuma a suure puan la yeheri la yelesuma ma'a, ti nɛre be'o la me en tari be'em a suure puan la, me yeheri la yelebe'ero ma'a a puan na. La dela sɛla en zo'e suure la puan la ti nuurɛ tɔgera.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Ŋwani ti ya yi'ira mam Daana Daana, gee ka ita mam en yɛta?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: N wan pa'alɛ ya se'em daana n wa'am na mam zi'an na wum mam yelehum wa gee ita bu n ani se'em.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: Ena daana ani me wuu yire ti nɛra yeti a mɛ, tu' tiŋa sige nyaa iibɛ ɛ̃berɛ la yagelɛ taŋa zuo. Ko'om han ta ɔkɛ, ti kulaahi pirɛ di na, yire la ze' me kankaŋi ka miina, se'ere en sɔi la, de yage la ɛ̃berɛ sunɛ zuo.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Gee se'em n wum mam yelehum wa gee ka ita bu n yeti se'em la ani me wuu nɛra n kɔ'ɔm mɛ a yire tiŋanpu'a zuo, gee ka tu' mɛ ɛ̃berɛ. Ti kulaahi ta pirɛ di na, ti yire la kɔ'ɔm lu bilam bilam wuregam kɔ'ɔm saalum za'a za'a.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Dina wa n tole ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu dɔla tinkãra, la timbibehi puan muula gee tɔgera faarɛ yelehum la, n de Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum yelehum la. Ti a zahereba pia la bayi' la bɔna la en.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: la pɔgehi sise'ehi ti Yezu tibɛ si kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, la bã'ahi la. Mɛɛri, ti ba yi'ira Mɛɛri Magedalin, se'em ti Yezu sa'ɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi siyopɔi a puan la.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: Yowana, se'em sira en de Hɛrɔd tuntuna ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Kuza la, la Suzaana, la pɔgehi sise'ehi kekeko n lagum tara ba lɔgerɔ suŋera Yezu la a zahereba la.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ n ta lagehɛ Yezu zi'an na la, ti nɛreba ze'ele tihi tihi wa'am a zi'an na la, ti a makɛ makerɛ bo' ba yeti.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Kaara en yehe ti a ta burɛ a bumbuura. En buti la, ti bumbuura la i se'ehi zɔgɛ sore zuo, ti nɛreba niira ba zuo, ti niihe n ɛgeri wa'am na di ba.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Ti i se'ehi me lu taŋa zi'an, ti ban zo'ore na la, ba ki mɛ se'ere en sɔi la mu'uhegɔ ka boi tiŋa la zuo.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Ti i se'ehi me lu gu'uhi zi'an, ti gu'uhi la zo'e la ba na, kpãrɛ ba.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ti i se'ehi me lu pu'uhegɔ zi'an, zo'e na, wam zuto kɔbehi. Yezu n yeti ŋwana la, a yi' mɛ yeti, Fum se'em n tari tuba n wan bahɛ ti a kɛleha.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la soke en makerɛ wa vuurɛ n de se'em.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Han dɛna yamam, ba bo' yamam la bɔkerɛ ti ya mina Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la sugelum yɛla, gee basɛba, n tɔgeri la mageha bɔ'ɔra ba, ti ba Bihera gee kan malum nyɛ, ba kɛleha gee kan malum bɔkɛ.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Makerɛ la vuurɛ n ŋwana. Bumbuura la dela Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Se'ehi n lu sore zuo la dela nɛre sɛba n wum yelehum la, gee ti Sagena la wa'am na dikɛ yelehum la ba suya la puan kiŋɛ, ti ba da ta'am nam yelehum la la ba nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Bumbuura se'ehi n lu taŋa zi'an la dela, nɛre sɛba n ni wum yelehum la, to'e bu la nyɛɛma. Gee ba ka ba' nyɛga, ba nam yelehum la la saŋa fii wa, gee makerɛ saŋa, ba ni lu mɛ.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Han dɛna sɛba en lu gu'uhi zi'an la, bamam dela, sɛba en wum yelehum la, gee ban kini la, tiŋa wa zuo vom yɛla, la tarega, la nyɛlum, n ni kpãrɛ ba, ti ba wala la ka bi'ira.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Gee han dɛna sɛba n lu pu'uhegɔ zi'an la, bamam dela, sɛba n ni wum yelehum la, gurɛ bu kankaŋi la yelemɛŋerɛ, la susunɛ, tara sugeri la ŋmibega ta wam wala.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Nɛra ka boi laŋɛ butolela dikɛ pi'ɔ vuge ka, bii dikɛ ka sugɛ gɔregɔ leŋen, gee ka sugele la ka sugelego zi'an, niira bɔ'ɔra se'em woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri na.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, sɛla ka boi pi' kan ta pilege, bii sɛla bɔna sugɛ ti nɛreba kan ta baŋɛ, bii kan ta niiɛ peelumen na.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Bala la, bihɛ fum mɛŋa suŋa la fum kelehe yɛla se'em. Se'ere n sɔi la, sɛba n pun tara zo'e la, bamam ti ba wan bo' pa'ahɛ, ti sɛba n pun ka tara la, sɛla ti ba ti'ihɛ ba tari la, ba wan to'e la ba zi'an.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Ti Yezu ma la a yibehi wa'am a zi'an na, gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la zuo, fole ka boi ti ba wan paaɛ en.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Ti ba ta yele en yeti, Fum ma la fum yibehi ze' yiŋa ha bɔta ti ba nyɛ hɔ.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam ma, la mam yibehi dela sɛba n wum Yinɛ yelehum wa gee tara bu tuna.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti Yezu zom uureŋɔ la a zahereba la, ti a yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu yakɛ ko'om wa ke ha bɔba ha. Ti ba pɔhɛ yɛ'a.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: ti ban mɔni uureŋɔ la yɛ'a la, ti Yezu giha. Ti kusebego kiŋɔ lu ko'om la zuo, gbiregera, ti ko'om pita uureŋɔ la, ti ba bɔna nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega puan.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Ti a zahereba la paaɛ a zi'an, niiɛ en gee kaaha yeti, Daana, Daana, tu yeti tu ki mɛ. Ti Yezu ihege ze'ele tahɛ kusebego la, la ko'mi'ihi la n gbiregeri la, ti ba za'a go'e suram ti sɛla woo ma'ɛ lɔge lɔge.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Ya sakerɛ bɛɛ? Ti dabeem tara ba, gee ti la me iŋɛ ba pakerɛ, ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Ani nyaa de en wa, ti kusebego, la ko'om nana en.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Ti ba wa'am na paaɛ Gerasene n boi Galilii nɛŋan ha.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yezu n ze'ele uureŋɔ la zuo kɔ'ɔm sige na ti a na'arɛ kɔ'ɔm tagelɛ tiŋa la, ti buraa n boi tiŋa la puan ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla en, tu'uhɛ en. Buraa wa bahɛ futo yɛa ti la yuue mɛ, gee me ka kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra yire puan, gee a boi la yɔɔrɔ zuto ha.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: En nyɛ Yezu la, a lu mɛ a nɛŋan, gee kaahɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Fum tari la bem la mam Yezu? Gaŋɛ Za'a Yinɛ Dayua wa? N beleni fum mɛ, da mugehe mam.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yezu pun tahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a la mɛ yeti, a tole yehe buraa la puan.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa soke en yeti, Fum yu'urɛ? Ti a yeti, Tu zo'e mɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la zo'e mɛ dɔla buraa wa.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Ti ba belum Yezu ti a da sa'ɛ ba bahɛ lika boko la puan.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Bala ma'a ti zoore la tilen ha, kurikuri zo'e zo'e n ze' bini ɔbera. Ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la belum Yezu ti a bo' ba sore ti ba kɛ̃ kurikuri la puan. Ti Yezu bo' ba sore.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la yehe buraa la puan na, ta kɛ̃ kurikuri la, ti bamam me zɔ zulege ha, ta lu ko'om la puan, yee ko'om la puan.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Sɛba n guri kurikuri la n nyɛ ŋwana la, ba zɔ mɛ kiŋɛ tinkãra la timbibehi puan ta yele sɛla n iŋɛ la.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Ti nɛreba yehe na ti ba bihɛ sɛla n iŋɛ la. Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yezu la, ba nyɛ ti buraa la ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la yehe ka zi'an la, zi' la Yezu nɛŋan, yɛ futo, dɛna a mɛŋa. Ti dabeem ke ba paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Ti sɛba n boni gee nyɛ la nyaa yɛta bana la lan iŋɛ se'em ti buraa la ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla ka la nyɛ ka tibegɔ.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Ti nɛre sɛba woo n kaaɛ Gerasene la, n lagehɛ na la, ba za'a yele Yezu yeti, a bahɛ ba tiŋa la. Se'ere en sɔi la, dabeem kãtɛ n kɛ̃ ba. Bala ti Yezu lebe zom uureŋɔ lebe.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Ti buraa sɛka ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la yehe ka puan la, belum Yezu ti, a bahɛ ti a doohe en, gee Yezu bahɛ en ti a kiŋɛ, gee yele en yeti.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Lebe kule fum yire, la fum ta yele Yinɛ n tum tuunkãtɛ se'ere bo' fum. Ti buraa wa kiŋɛ, ta muulɛ kaaɛ tiŋa la za'a, Yezu n tum tuunkãtɛ se'ere taaba bo' en la.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Yezu n lebe ta paaɛ la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ tu'uhɛ en na, se'ere n sɔi la, ba pun guri ni en mɛ.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Bilam bilam ti buraa yu'urɛ n yi'iri Yaruse, dɛna nɛŋadaana zamehegɔ yire puan n bala paaɛ na. Lu Yezu nama tiŋa, gee belum en ti a wa'am a yire na.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: se'ere n sɔi la, a tari la poyua kayima ma'a n de woo yuumpia la ayi', n kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a kum. Yezu n ye' la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la mikera za'ara en na.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Bala ma'a la ti pɔka n bɔna bini ti ka ziim kɔ'ɔm ka'aha yuuma pia la ayi'. Ti ka dikɛ ka lɔgerɔ za'a doohe tibeduma, gee ba se'em ka nyaŋɛ tibɛ en.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Ti pɔka wa firegɛ na paaɛ Yezu pooren, kalum Yezu fuo la tiŋa, ti bilam bilam ti ka ziim la ŋmaaɛ.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Ti Yezu soke yeti, Ani n kalum mam wa? Ti nɛra woo n ta yeti ba ka mi' la, ti Pita yeti, Daana, nɛrekuuŋɔ wa n mikere fum wa, pun za'ara luta la fum zuo na.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Gee Yezu yeti, Aayi, nɛra n kalum mam. Mam baŋɛ ti paŋa n ze'ele n puan yehe.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Pɔka wa en ta bihɛ ti a kan ta'am sugɛ la, ti a yehe na, gee gbi'ira, lu tiŋa Yezu nɛŋan, gee muulɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a nɛŋan, se'ere zuo ti eŋa kalum en, la eŋa n nyɛ a tibegɔ bilam bilam se'em.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, N poyua, fum sakerɛ en bahɛ ti fum nyɛ fum tibegɔ wa. Kina la suma'ahum.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Yezu n boi bini tɔgera la, ti nɛra ayima n bala ze'ele Kpɛ'ɛma la yire paara na, ti a yele kpɛ'ɛma la yeti, Fum poyua la pun bahɛ tu mɛ, da dagum daam pa'ala la le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Yezu n wum ŋwana la, a yele kpɛ'ɛma la yeti, Da zɔ dabeem. Yeen sakɛ, gee fum poyua la wan vo'e.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Yezu n wa'am na paaɛ yire la puan la, a ka bahɛ ti se'em doohe en kɛ̃ yire la puan, han dagena Pita, Yɔn, Yemesi, la bia la sɔ la ka ma ma'a.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Ti nɛra woo kɛla gee fabela bɔ'ɔra bia la. Gee Yezu yele ba yeti, Da kele ya gee bia la ka ki, ka gihere mɛ.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Ti nɛreba la la'ara en, se'ere n sɔi la, bamam mi' ti bia la ki mɛ.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Gee Yezu paaɛ mɛ gi' bia la nu'o, gee yi' en yeti, Bia wa, ihege.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Ti bia la sia lebe kɛ̃ ka puan, ti ka vaaɛ ihege buyina. Ti Yezu yele ba ti ba bo' bia la sɛla ti ka di.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Ti la iŋɛ bia la dɔgereba la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, gee ti Yezu yele ba yeti ba da yele nɛra nɛra sɛla n iŋɛ la.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Sankana wa puan la, ti nɛreba basɛba boi nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yele Yezu, Palete en tabelɛ kɔrum Galiliihi, ka'ahɛ ba ziim lagum la ba kaabegɔ la se'em.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Ya ti'ihɛ ti Galiliihi sina wa en yuum namehɛ ŋwana wa la dela be'em duma gana Galiliihi la za'a yoi?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayi, n yeti ya mɛ wa, ti han dagena ya tee mɛ, ya me wan bahɛ yoo wuu lan yuum iŋɛ ba se'em la.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Bii nɛreba pia la banii la ti Silom dewoko la yuum lu di' ba ku la, ba yuum ba' galum mɛ gana nɛresɛba woo n yuum boi Yerusalɛm sankaŋa la yoi?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Aayi, mam yeti ya mɛ wa, ti han dagena ya tee mɛ, ya me wan bahɛ yoo wuu lan yuum iŋɛ ba se'em la.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Ti Yezu yi' a zahereba pia la bayi' la lagehɛ na, bo' ba paŋa la kpe'eŋo, ti ba tana kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi woo, gee tibera bã'ahi.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Ti Yezu bo' ba makerɛ dina yeti, Buraa n sɛ kinkaŋa a va'am puan. Nyaa ta wa'am na ɛɛra kinkama ka zuo, gee a ka nyɛ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Gee nyaa tum ba ti ba yehe ta muulɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yɛla, gee tibera bã'ahi.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Bala ti a yele va'am bihera la yeti, Bihɛ, yuuma atã n ŋwana ti n kina kinkaŋa wa tiŋa na ɛɛra kinkama wa'am na paaɛ zina, gee ka malum nyɛta sɛla. Ŋmaaɛ ka bahɛ. Ŋwani ti ka wan zɛ'a Sagena n samanɛ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: A yele ba yeti, Da dikɛ ya sɛsɛla ya kinɛ wa puan. Doore, bii tapɔɔ, bii dia, bii ligeri. Da pun dikɛ ya fuo pa'ahɛ.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ti a lerege yeti, Naa n daana, bahɛ ka yuunɛ ma'a pa'ahɛ. N wan tu' boko kaaɛ ka, bo' ka bugelego.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Yire se'ere woo puan ti ya kɛ̃, boni ya bilam gura ya ta ba'ahɛ.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: ti ka han wam yuunɛ se'ere en kini na la, la ani suŋa, gee ka han ka wam, fum ta'am ŋmaaɛ ka bahɛ. .

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Ze'sɛka woo ti ba ka to'e ya, ya han bahera tinkaŋa, kpa'ahɛ ya nama puuhego bahɛ ka puan ti la dɛna yele gã'a.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Yezu ta pa'ala mɛ zamehegɔ yire deyima puan Vo'ohego dabeherɛ.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Ti a zahereba la kiŋɛ, ta doohe timbibehi puan, yɛta ba faarɛ yelehum la, gee tibera bã'ahi zi'an woo.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Ti pɔka en bala wa'am na, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a kaarum ka, yuuma pia la anii. Pɔka wa kɔ'ɔm nyure mɛ uma, kan ta'am tiɛ ze'ele dɔ'ɔ.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Hɛrɔd en de naba la, n wum sɛlawoo en iŋɛ la, a siiɛ mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba basɛba en tɔgera ti, Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la n vo'e a kum puan.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Yezu n nyɛ pɔka wa la, a yi' ka mɛ tole nɛŋa na, yele ka yeti, Pɔka wa, fum nyɛ faarɛ fum bã'a wa puan.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: ti basɛba me yeti, Elia en lebena, ti sɛba me yeti, diimen diimen nɔtɔgeheba la ayima en vo'e kum puan na.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: En dikɛ a nu'o pagelɛ pɔka wa zuo la, bilam bilam ti pɔka wa tiɛ ze'ele dɔ'ɔ, pɔhɛ pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Ti Hɛrɔd yeti, Mam fike Yɔn zuo mɛ, gee ani me en ŋwana ti n wuna yele ana wa? Ti Hɛrɔd mu'ɛ nini ɛɛra a nyɛ Yezu.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Gee zamehegɔ yire nɛŋadaana la, suure nyiie mɛ la Yezu, la eŋa n tibɛ pɔka la vo'ohego daarɛ la, nyaa nyɔna yɛta nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Dabeha ayoobe en de tuuma dabeha, wa'am ya dabeha aŋa puan na to'e tibegɔ, gee dagi vo'ohego dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la ta lebena, yele en sɛla woo ti ba iŋɛ. Ti Yezu tari ba ma'a ti ba yehe kiŋɛ Bɛtesaada.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Ti Daana la lebehe ba yeti, Birekɔgenduma wa, Ya woo ka lorene ya lɔlehi bii bohi baha vo'ohego dabeherɛ la, ni tari ba ta nyuuhe?.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la n ta baŋɛ bala la, ba ihege mɛ doohe a poore, ti a me to'e ba, gee tɔgɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yɛla pa'alɛ ba, gee tibɛ sɛba woo en boti tibegɔ.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Ti Pɔka wa en de Aberaham yaaŋa ti Sagena bobe ka tara tara yuuma pia la anii wa la, ka nari ti ka nyɛ ka fai, vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Ti yinɛ ta luta, ti a zahereba pia la bayi' la paaɛ a zi'an yele en yeti, Bahɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ wa yiregɛ, la ba kiŋɛ timbibehi wa puan, la kinkɛlehi tihi wa puan ta ɛ ga'aregɔ zi'an, gee ɛ dia di, gee ka'lam dela muɔ puan.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: Yezu en yele ŋwana la, vi en kɛ̃ sɛba woo en ka sakɛ bo' en la. Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a nyɛɛna, la nyalema sɛ'a ti Yezu tuna la.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yamam bo' ba sɛla ti ba di. Ti ba yeti, Tumam n tari sɛla la ka gaŋɛ boroboro anuu la zim iyi', han dagena tu ye yeti tu kiŋɛ mɛ ta da' dia na bo' nɛrebana wa za'a.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa ani wuu bem? N wan dikɛ bɔ makɛ mɛ la bem?.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba la paaɛ wuu tuha anuu, buraahi ma'a kãlɛ en bala. Ti Yezu yele a zahereba la yeti, Bahɛ ya ti ba lagehɛ taaba wuu nɛre pihinuu pihinuu zi'ire.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: Bo ani mɛ wuu gungumbeeliho ti nɛra tari ta burɛ a va'am bila puan, ti biiliho la yehe lebege tia, ti niihe wa'am na tɛ' ba tugerɔ tia la yila zuo.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: Ti a zahereba la iŋɛ bala, bahɛ ti nɛreba la zi'ire.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Ti Yezu le yele pa'ahɛ yeti, N wan dikɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa makɛ mɛ la bem?.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: Ti Yezu to'e boroboro banuu la zim iyi' la, gorege bihɛ saazuo, gee purɛ sum bahɛ ba zuo, gee gi'ihɛ ba bo' a zahereba la, ti ba tɔrɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: Bu ani mɛ wuu pɔka n dikɛ dabelɛ gɛregɛ zom kunkura atã ŋmɛ ti zom la ta ihege se'em na la.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Ti ba za'a di tigɛ, gee ti sɛla ti ba obe tigɛ gee bahɛ la, ti ba doohe lagehɛ ba, ti ba pirɛ pitɔ pia la tuyi'.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Ti Yezu dɔla tinkãra, la timbibehi kayima kayima kaara, pa'ala bala yɛ'a Yerusalɛm.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Saŋa kayima ti Yezu ta bɔna a ma'a pu'uha, ti a zahereba la ma'a lɛm en, ti a soke ba yeti, Nɛreba kekeko yeti mam dela ani.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Ti nɛra ayima ta soke en yeti, Daana, nɛreba fii wa n wan ta nyɛ faarɛ wa? Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Luk . Ti ba lerege en yeti, Sɛba yeti fum dela Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la, ti sɛba me yeti fum dela Elia ti basɛba me yeti fum dela diimen diimen ha nɔtɔgeheba la ayima en vo'e a kum puan na.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Pɛregɛ ya la ya doohe zanɔrɛ mika la puan kɛ̃. Se'ere en sɔi la, n yele ya ti, nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan zigera ti ba kɛ̃, gee ba kan nyaŋɛ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Gee yamam mɛhi yeti n dela ani? Ti Pita lerege en yeti, Yinɛ Kiresta la.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Yidaana han diŋɛ ihege yu' kuleŋa la, ti fum zɛ'a yiŋa ha ŋmɛ'ɛra gee yi'ira yeti, N daana, yu'ɛ bo' tu, a wan lebehe ya yeti, Mam ka mi' yamam n ze'ele zi'an na.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Ti a ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da makɛ yele nɛra nɛra.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Ya wan lebehe yele en yeti, tumam la fum en lagum dita gee nyuura, ti fum ni bɔna tu sadɔtɔ zuo pa'ala la.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: sɛ:Gee yeti, Asaala Dayua la wan doohe namehegɔ kãtɛ puan tole, ti kiinduma, malema kiinduma, la gɔnmi'ineba la zagehɛ en, gee ti ba ku en, gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Gee a wan lebehe ya yeti, Mam ka mi' yamam en ze'ele zi'an na, fɔregɛ ya n zi'an, be'em duma wa.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Ti a nyaa yele ba woo yeti, Ya se'em han bɔta ti a dɛna mam dɔleba, a daana zagehɛ a mɛŋa, gee dikɛ a daare woo pumpuuredɔɔ zi dɔla mam.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Bilam ti ya wan ta kaaha, gee ɔbera nyɛna, ya han ta nyɛ ti Aberaham, Aziki la Yakɔbe la Yinɛ nɔtɔgeheba la za'a ti ba boi Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan, gee ti ba lobe yamam mɛhi la bahɛ yiŋa ha.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Sɛba woo en boti ba gu' ba vom, wan bɔregɛ bɔ bahɛ, ti sɛba woo me en zagehɛ ba vom mam zuo, wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Nɛreba wan ta ze'ele dapɔya, ti sɛba ze'ele zanɔya, ti sɛba ze'ele zuɔ ti sɛba ze'ele gɔbega bɔba na Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan dita.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Beni nyuurɔ ti nɛra wan nyɛ, nɛra eŋa han nyɛ tiŋa wan zuo lɔgerɔ za'a, gee bɔregɛ bii koŋe ba nyɔvore?.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛba yee la pooren, gee wan ta tole nɛŋa, ti sɛba me tole nɛŋa, gee wan ta yee pooren.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Se'em en tari vi la mam, la n yelehum wa la, eŋa ti Asaala Dayua wa wan ta tara vi la en, n han ta lɛbera na la n suŋa la puan la, Sɔ suŋa puan la, la Fa'a suŋereba suŋa la.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Gogo kaareŋa kaŋa mɛa puan, ti Farasiiduma basɛba wa'am na yele Yezu yeti, Bahɛ ka'lam, gee Faro eere fum mɛ ti a ku.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti sɛba boi ya puan kan makɛ lem kum gura ti ba nini ta nyɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta yele piihɔ wa bo' mam yeti, A Kɛleha, mam sa'ahɛ la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee tibera bã'ahi zina la beere, dabeha atã daarɛ ti n wan ba'ahɛ n tuuma.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: La iŋɛ wuu dabeha anii puan, en tɔgɛ yetɔge buna wa la, ti Yezu tari Pita, Yɔn la Yemesi zom zoore zuo ti a ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Gee zina, beere, la daarɛ, n wan kiŋɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, la kan ta'am iŋɛ ti ba ku nɔtɔgeha yiŋa, ti a ka bɔna Yerusalɛm puan.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: En pu'uheri la, ti a nɛŋa tee, ti a futo lebege pɛɛlega kerikeri.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Yerusalɛm, Yerusalɛm, tinsɛka en kuuri ka nɔtɔgeheba, gee lɔbera Yinɛ en tum sɛba na la la kuga. Saŋa woo mam zigeri mɛ ti n kã'ahɛ hɔ bɔbeyinɛ wuu nua en kã'ahɛ ka kɔma iŋɛ ka kelumpagehi puan se'em la, gee fum ka iŋɛ fum puti'irɛ bala.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ, ti buraahi siyi, Moose la Elia en bala zɛ'a tɔgera bɔ'ɔra en.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Bihɛ, Fum yire la nyaa boi la fum nu'uhen. N yeti fum mɛ wa, ti fum kan le malum nyɛ mam, gura sansɛka ti fum wan ta tiɛ mam yele yeti, Sum bɔna la se'em en wa'am Daana la yu'urɛ puan na la. .

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Ba niiɛ mɛ na la ba saazuo suŋa, tɔgera la en, a tiŋa wa zuo bahega la ti a bɔna bini ti a kɛ̃ ka puan Yerusalɛm la.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Pita la a taaba la nini kɔ'ɔm vilegera mɛ la guum, gee ban ŋmibe ka gihi la, ba nyɛ a suŋa la mɛ, la buraahi siyi la en ze' la en la.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Ti buraahi siyi wa en ta bɔna bini ti ba bahɛ Yezu gee kiŋɛ la, ti Pita yele Yezu yeti, Daana, la ani suŋa ti tu bɔna ka'lam. Bahɛ ti tu ti' suga atã, deyima fum, deyima Moose, deyima Elia. Gee Pita ka mi' en yeti sɛla la vuurɛ.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: En boi bini yɛta ŋwana wa la, ti sagbanɛ wa'am na pi' ba, ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba, ban nyɛ ti sagbanɛ la pi' ba la.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Ti kua ze'ele sagbanɛ la puan tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Mam dayua n ŋwana, mam dayɔba'arɛ n ŋwana, kelehe ya en.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Kua la en tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ la, ba nyɛ ti Yezu ze' la a ma'a. Ti ba ana bããlam dabeha aŋa la, ka yele nɛra nɛra ban nyɛ sɛla la, la yele yinɛ me.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Yiimbeere, ban ze'ele zoore la zuo sige na la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ tu'uhɛ en.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Bilam bilam ti buraa ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan tahɛ na yeti, Pa'ala, n beleni fum mɛ ti fum bihɛ n dayua wa bo' mam. N bia za'a za'a n bala.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a en ni gi' en, ti a ni kaahɛ bahɛ, a ni kɔ'ɔm nyuta, ni bahɛ ti a lu kilenki'ire, ti nɔtɔ-fulunfuka pirɛ a nuurɛ, ni bahɛ en ti a lu la tiŋa pɔgelena a mɛŋa ka baheri en tɔtɔ.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Mam belum fum zahereba wa mɛ ti ba sa'ɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a la, gee ba ka nyaŋɛ.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Yamam a sakerɛ ka boi, la ŋmɛlema duma saŋa wa, mam yeti n yuue la gee ŋmibe la ya paaɛ la ŋwani? Tari fum dayua la wa'am ka'lam na.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: En tari bia la kina na la, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a la fɔbɛ bia la tiŋa ti ka lige. Gee Yezu tahɛ sɛfi'iha la mɛ, gee tibɛ bia la, dikɛ ka bo' ka sɔ la.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ti la iŋɛ nɛra woo pakerɛ la Yinɛ pankãtɛ la. Nɛra woo puurɛ en pee la en tuni se'em la, a yele a zahereba la yeti.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Bahɛ ya ti yele dina wa kɛ̃ ya tuban. Ti la kini na, ti ba wan ta koohe Asaala Dayua wa bo' saaleba.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Gee a zahereba la ka bɔkɛ yetɔgebuna wa vuurɛ. Bo vuurɛ la sugɛ mɛ bo' ba, ti ba kan bɔkɛ dɛ. Gee ti dabeem tari ba, la ba soke Yezu a yetɔgum la vuurɛ.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Yele ana en tole la, tu ti Daana la looe a zahereba basɛba pihiyopɔi, tum ba bayi' bayi', ti ba diŋɛ a nɛŋa, ta kɛ̃ tihi se'ehi woo puan ti eŋa mɛŋa maahum ti a doohe si puan la.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Ti nɔkpe'ene lu Yezu zahereba la tiŋasuka doohe la se'em en gani za'a ba puan.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: A yele ba yeti, Hmm! Dia buŋa la zo'e mɛ, gee ti tuntuneba la pɔ'ɛ. Bala, suhɛ ya Daana la en sɔi va'am la, ti a tum tuntuneba a dia buŋa la puan na.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Gee Yezu en baŋɛ ban ti'ihɛ se'em ba suya puan la, a yi' la bibila na ze'ele a zi'an.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Kini ya. Bihɛ ya, ya ani mɛ wuu pebibehi la, ti n tuna ya sahi puan la.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Yezu ta bɔna la zi'an kayima pu'uha, ti en ba'ahɛ la, ti a zahereba la ayima yele en yeti, Daana, pa'alɛ tɔ ti tu pu'uha wuu Yɔn en pa'alɛ a zahereba la se'em la.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: gee yele ba yeti, Se'em woo en to'e bia wa mam yu'urɛ zuo, to'e la mam, ti se'em woo me en to'e mam, to'e la se'em en tum mam na la. Se'em en sigehe a mɛŋa paaɛ paaɛ ya puan eŋa en gani ya za'a.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Da tari ya ligeri tapɔtɔ, bii sore kinɛ tapɔtɔ, bii tagera. Da teke ya sore zuo pu'uhɛ nɛra ya kinɛ wa puan.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya han ni pu'uha, ni yele ya yeti, Sɔ, fum yu'urɛ wa sɔna na'ahegɔ. Fum sɔ'ɔlum wa'am na.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Ti Yɔn lerege en yeti, Daana, Tumam nyɛ la nɛra ayima ti a sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi fum yu'urɛ la puan, ti tumam kpeŋe tu mɛŋa gu' en, se'ere en sɔi la, a ka dole tu.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Yire se'ere woo ti ya yeti ya kɛ̃ de puan, yia ni yele ya yeti, Suma'ahum bɔna yire wa puan.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Bɔ'ɔra tɔ daarɛ woo tu nuurɛ dia.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya ka de ni ya gu' ni en. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em en ka zaberɛ la tɔ, boi mɛ la tɔ.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Ti suma'ahum daana han bɔna bini, ya suma'ahum la, wan sige a daana, gee se'em han ka bɔna bini dɛna suma'ahum daana, ya suma'ahum wan lebe ya zi'an na.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Bahera tu be'em bo' tu beni zuo, tumam mɛhi baheri bɔ'ɔra se'em woo en tari tu sama mɛ. Da bahɛ ti tu kɛ̃ pã'ahebe'ero puan.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Dabeha en ta pɔra ti ba zɛkɛ en zom saazuo la, a kɔ'ɔm ŋmɛregɛ a nɛŋa tɔ la Yerusalɛm.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Yee ya yire dinɛ puan la ya dita gee nyuura sɛla woo ti ba bo' ya, se'ere en sɔi la, tuntuna dela ba yɔ en. Da lomehe ya yiya kaara.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya se'em han tara a zɔ, kiŋɛ a zi'an yu'unsunsua ta yele en yeti, N zɔ, peŋe mam boroboro batã na.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Gee a tum bitunehi ti, ba diŋɛ a nɛŋa. Ti bitunehi la ta teke Samariyaduma timbila puan ti ba maahum gura en.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Sansɛka woo ti ya kɛ̃ tinsɛka woo puan ti ka nɛreba to'e ya, diti ya sɛla woo ti ba bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: gee n zɔ n nan kɔ'ɔm paaɛ n zi'an na, ti mam me ka tara sɛla wan dikɛ ze'ele a nɛŋan.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Gee Tiŋa wa duma zagehɛ en mɛ se'ere en sɔi la, a ye' tɔla la Yerusalɛm.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: Tiberi ya bã'duma sɛba woo en boi tiŋa la puan, gee yɛta ba yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la paaɛ ya na.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Ti fum zɔ la gã'a ha gee yeti, Da daana mam, gee mam pun diŋɛ yu' n kuleŋa mɛ, ti kɔma me gã'a la ma suŋɔ zuo giha. Mam kan ta'am ihege bo' fum sɛla.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Yezu zahereba, Yemesi la Yɔn n nyɛ nɛreba la en iti se'em la, ti ba yele Yezu yeti, Daana, fum boti tu yi' bugum ti bu ze'ele saazuo lu ba zuo na ba'ahɛ ba.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Gee sansɛka woo ti ya kɛ̃ tinsɛka puan ti ka nɛreba ka to'e ya, yehe ya ta ze'ele ka sɔya zuto la ya yeti.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti la en kan ihege bo' en sɛla ban pun dɛna zɔtɔ la, gee buraa la en sini gee zɛ'a ŋmɛ'ɛra la zuo, a wan ba'ahum ihege bo' en sɛla woo ti a bɔta la.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Gee Yezu ŋmɛregɛ tahɛ ba gee yele ba yeti, Ya ka mi' sia sɛka taaba ti ya tara?.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Ya tiŋa wa puuhego en puke tu nama la n bala ti tu kpa'aha baha wa, ti la dɛna yele gã'a gura ya. Gee la bala za'a, mini ya suŋa ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la lɛm ya mɛ na.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Bala, mam yeti ya yeti, soke ya, gee ba wan bo' ya. Ɛ ya gee ya wan nyɛ. Ŋmɛ' ya gee kuleŋa la wan yu'ɛ bo' fum.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la Asaala Dayua la ka wa'am na ti a sagum la vom gee faaɛ bahɛ. Ti ba ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ tole timbila kayima puan.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti dabeherɛ la daarɛ, la wan ana ma'ma' bo' Sodom nyana tihi sina taaba.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Beni zuo, Se'em woo en soke wan to'e, ti se'em woo en ɛ wan nyɛ, ti se'em woo en ŋmɛ, kuleŋa la wan yu'ɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Yele boi yamam Korazinduma wa zuo. Yele boi yamam Bɛtesaadaduma wa zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam paŋa n tum daalehum sebo ya puan wa han tum ni la Taai la Sidɔn tihi, ŋwana ma'a ba pun tee ni la wunse'eren ha, yɛ susageŋɔ futo, gã'a tiŋa bilena.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Ban kini sore zuo la, ti nɛra ayima ta yele Yezu yeti, Mam wan dɔla hɔ zi'an woo ti fum yɛ'a.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Se'em boi yamam wa puan ti a bia han suhɛ en ziho, a nyɔkɛ bunsɛla bo' en gee dagi ziho?.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Gakerɛ daarɛ la, la wan ana ma'ma' bo' Taai la Sidɔn tihi nyana yamam.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Piini tari yogero mɛ, ti niihe en ɛgeri saazuo, tara tugerɔ kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra, gee Asaala Dayua wa ka tari zi'an ti a wan dikɛ a zuo biŋe.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Bii a bia suhɛ en gele ti a nyɔkɛ naŋa bo' en?.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Yamam Kapɛnum tihi wa me, ya ti'ihɛ ti ba wan zɛkɛ yamam wan yagelɛ la saazuo bii? Aayi, ba wan sigehe ya tiŋa ki'intiŋa.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Ti Yezu mɛŋa yele ayima yeti, Dɔla mam. Ti a yeti, Daana, bahɛ ti n lebe ta pi' n sɔ yia.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Yamam en tugum dɛna be'em duma, han mina yan bo'ori ya kɔma bo'olum suma se'em, Ŋwani ti ya Sɔ en boi saazuo ha kan bo' sɛba en suhɛ en Fa'a Sia la gaŋɛ bala.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Se'em woo en kelehe ya, kelehe la mam. Se'em woo me en zagehɛ ya, zagehɛ la mam. Ti se'em woo me en zagehɛ mam, zagehɛ la se'em en tum mam na la.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, Bahɛ ti kum pi' ba mɛhi kum, gee han dɛna fum, kiŋɛ ta muulɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yɛla.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Yezu wa bɔna bini sa'ara la gɔɔnɛ kulekpa'bɛ'a nɛra zi'an, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a la en yehe la, ti nɛra la en de gɔɔŋɔ la tɔgɛ, ti la iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba pihiyopɔi la lebena la nyɛɛma, yɛta yeti, Daana, fum yu'urɛ la zuo, kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi nam tumam mɛ.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Gee nɛreba la basɛba yeti, A sa'ahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi wa mɛ la Beelezebule en de kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi nɛŋa daana la paŋa puan.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Ti ayima me yele en yeti, Mam wan doohe fum, gee n Daana, bahɛ ti n lebe ta keehe n yire duma yia.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam nyɛ Sagena la en ze'ele saazuo ha luta na wuu bugum pãrehi la.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Ti sɛba me en boti ba magehɛ en bihɛ la me kɔ'ɔm ɛɛra ti a tum saazuo daalehum.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Se'em woo n dikɛ a nu'o pagelɛ nii kuuri zuo gee limehe pooren ka nari a bɔna Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Bihɛ ya, n bo' ya paŋa ti ya niira bunsɛlenduma, nahi, la Sagena la pahi za'a zuto, ti sɛla sɛla kan iŋɛ ya.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Gee Yezu en mi' ban ti'ihɛ se'em la, ti a yele ba yeti, Tinsɛka woo en welege zabera la ka mɛŋa, wan yiregɛ. Yire me en welege zabera la de mɛŋa, wan lebege daboo.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Gee la bala za'a da nyɛɛni ya la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la n nam ya la, gee tugum tari ya pupeelum ti ya yu'ura gulehɛ gã'a saazuo ha.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Sagena la me han welege zabera la a mɛŋa, a sɔ'ɔlum la wan iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am ze'ele? Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam yeti mam tari la Beelezebule paŋa sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Sankaŋa mɛa puan, ti Yezu nyɛɛna Fa'a Sia la puan yeti, N pu'uhɛ ya n Sɔ, saazuo la tiŋa Daana, la fum en sugɛ yele ana wa yɛm la mi'ilum duma, gee tugum lilege a bo' kɔmbilɛɛhi wa. La dela sira n Sɔ, fum zu'uhegɔ la fum sumboolum n de se'em n bala.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Mam han tara Beelezebule paŋa sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani yu'urɛ sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la? Bala zuo, bamam en wa dɛna ya mɛŋa gakereba.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Mam Sɔ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la mam nu'uhen. Se'em ka mi' Dayua la en de se'em han dagena Sɔ la ma'a, bii se'em mina Sɔ la en de se'em, han dagena Dayua la ma'a, la sɛba ti Dayua la mɛŋa looe ba ti a niiɛ Sɔ la pa'alɛ ba la.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Gee la han dɛna la Yinɛ nu'dibila ti mam tara la sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi la, la pa'alɛ ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la paaɛ ya na.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Ti Yezu ŋmɛregɛ tɔ a zahereba la, gee yele ba bããlam yeti, Sum boi mɛ la nini se'ehi en nyɛ sɛla ti yamam nyɛ wa.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Paŋa daana han bobe a zaba lɔgerɔ gura a zi'an suŋa suŋa, a lɔgerɔ la boi la fai.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: N yele ya ti, nɔtɔgeheba, la na'duma zo'e zo'e boti ni ti ba nyɛ yamam n nyɛ sɛla wa mɛ, gee ba ka nyɛ. Ba wum ni yamam n wum sɛla wa, gee ba ka wum.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Gee se'em me en kpe'em gana en han kɔ' zaberɛ la en, nyaŋɛ en, a wan to'e a zaba lɔgerɔ la ti a dikɛ a mɛŋa delum la, a wan tɔrɛ sɛla woo ti a fã la.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Bilam bilam, ti yetɔge tɔgera ayima vaaɛ ihege ti a makɛ Yezu bihɛ, nyaa yeti, Pa'ala, beni ti n wan iŋɛ la n ta'am nyɛ vom kamba'ahɛ wa.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Se'em woo en ka boi la mam, zaberɛ mɛ la mam, se'em en ka lagum ka'aha la mam, yiregerɛ bahera mɛ.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la en ta lagehɛ tuha tuha na la, nyaa niira taaba la, ti Yezu pɔhɛ tɔgera bɔ'ɔra a zahereba la yia yeti, Mini ya Farasiiduma wa dabelɛ la en de ba birikɔgenɛ la yele.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Beni en gulehɛ bɔna ze'elego la puan? Fum kãalɛ la beni bini.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a han yehe nɛra puan, ka ni ɛɛra la kpaahenɛ puan tuuna ɛɛra vo'ohego zi'an, gee en kan nyɛ la, ka wan yeti, N wan lebe n yikɛka la ti n ze'ele de puan na la puan.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Sɛla ka boi pi' kan ta pilege, sɛla me ka boi sugɛ kan ta niiɛ na.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Ti a lebehe yeti, Fum nɔŋɛ Daana Yinɛ, la fum suure za'a, fum Sia za'a, la fum paŋa za'a, la fum puti'irɛ za'a, gee nɔŋɛ fum suuɔ wuu fum mɛŋa la.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Kulekpa'bɛ'a wa han lebena nyɛ ti yire la maalɛ mɛ gã'a filam.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Bala, sɛla woo ti fum sugɛ tɔgɛ lika puan, ba wan wum dɛ peelumen, ti sɛla woo ti fum waahum dɛ saraa puan, ba wan muulɛ dɛ gɔherɔ zuto.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum kɔ'ɔm lebehe la ma'a. Kɔ'ɔm ita bala gee fum wan vɔna.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: A wan lebe ta ɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi siyopɔi n fi'ɛ gana eŋa, tara ba na ti ba kɛ̃ nɛra la puan, bɔna bini ti nɛra la nyaa sagum gana en daa nan ani se'em la.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, n zɔduma wa, da zoti ya sɛba en wan ku inganɛ wa ma'a gee kan ta'am iŋɛ se'ere pa'ahɛ la.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Gee buraa wa en boti ka nyɛ buurɔ la, ti ka soke yeti, Ti ani n nyaa de n mabia.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Yezu en boi bini tɔgera ŋwana la, ti pɔka ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan tɔgɛ voole bahɛ na yele Yezu yeti, Sum bɔna la puse'ere en daa tari hɔ la, la bi'ihum sebo en uge fum la.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Gee mam wan yele ya la ninmu'urɛ, se'em en de ya zɔta. Zoti ya se'em en wan ku ya inganɛ la, gee tara paŋa ti a dikɛ ya sia bahɛ bugum puan la. Ɛ̃ɛ, n yeti ya mɛ wa, zoti ya eŋa.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Buraa kayima en ze'ele Yerusalɛm sigera Yeriko, ti fãareba ta gu' ka sore, sɛgehɛ ka gee ŋmɛ' ka, gee kiŋɛ gee bahɛ ka ti ka gee kum.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, Sum tugum bɔna mɛ la sɛba en wum Yinɛ yelehum wa gee gurɛ bu suŋa suŋa.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Niimbibehi sinuu ka koohere la kɔɔba ayi'? Gee la ban pun buge bala la,Yinɛ ka malum tamena ba ayima yele.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Ti la iŋɛ, ti malema daana ayima dɔla sore dinɛ mɛa sigera, en nyɛ buraa la, a lɔkɛ doohe la zi'an kayima ha tole.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la en malum pa'ahera la, ti Yezu pɔhɛ yɛta yeti, Sankana buuri wa dela be'em buuri saŋa. Ba eere la ba nyɛ daalehum, gee daalehum ka boi ti n wan iŋɛ bo' ba, han dagena daalehum sɛka en yuum iŋɛ Yona la ma'a.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Yinɛ mi' ya zuto zonto kãlɛ mɛ. Da tari ya dabeem, gee ya tari tuulum mɛ gana niimbibehi kekeko.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: Bala mɛa ti Levi wa'am na paaɛ zi'an la, nyɛ buraa la, gee me lɔkɛ doohe zi'an kayima ha tole.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti se'em woo en ze'ele nɛreba nɛŋan tɔgɛ mam yele suŋa suŋa , Asaala Dayua wa me ta ze'ele Suŋereba nɛŋan me tɔgɛ a yele suŋa suŋa.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: Wuu Yona en yuum de daalehum bo' Ninevɛ tiŋa duma se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam Asaala Dayua wa wan dɛna bo' sankana buuri wa.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Gee Samariya n ta yɛ'a tɔla, wa'am na pɔra buraa la, nyɛ buraa la, ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ en.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Gee Se'em woo en zagehɛ mam nɛreba nɛŋan, mam me wan ta zagehɛ en Yinɛ suŋereba la nɛŋan.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Tiŋanuurɛ bɔba pɔgenaba la wan ta ihege ze'ele gakerɛ daarɛ, tɔgɛ lubɛ sankana buuri wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Pɔgenaba wa yuum ze'ele la zi'an en ka yɔi' wa'am na ti a zi'ire naba Solomɔn nɛŋan kelehe a yɛm tugum la. Gee bihɛ ya, se'em en gani Solomɔn en bala bɔna ka'lam wa.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
