local: Ti ka sige paaɛ en, ka'ɛ kaam la daam duuhe en, gee pɔgeleŋɔ la lu', gee ɔ̃begɛ en pagelɛ a mɛŋa buŋa zuo, tari en paaɛ saama ga'aregɔ yire, bihɛ en.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Se'em woo en tɔgɛ se'ere pɔ'ɛ Asaala Dayua wa, wan nyɛ sugeri dia, gee se'em woo en tɔgɛ pɔ'ɛ Fa'a Sia wa, kan malum nyɛ sugeri dia.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Ninevɛ tiŋa nɛreba wan ta ihege ze'ele gakerɛ daarɛ la tɔgɛ lubɛ saŋa kana buuri wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Yona en yuum tɔgɛ bo' ba la, ba yuum tee mɛ. Gee bihɛ ya, se'em en gani Yona en bala bɔna ka'lam wa.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Ti yiimbeere ti a dikɛ ligeri wuu dabeha ayi yɔɔrɛ bo' se'em en biheri saama ga'aregɔ yire la, gee yele en yeti, Bihera buraa wa suŋa bɔ'ɔra mam, ti n han ta tulege na ti sanɛ se'ere woo bɔna, n wan yɔ.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Ba han ta nyɔgɛ ya kiŋɛ zamehegɔ yiya puan, na'duma la kpɛ'ɛnduma nɛŋan, da fabele ya yan wan ta po'e ya mɛhi se'em, bii yan wan ta tɔgɛ se'em.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Nɛra ka laŋeri butolela yire puan, dikɛ sɛla vugele ka, gee a wan tugum dikɛ ka ze'ele la ka ze'elego zi'an, ti se'em woo en ki'ire yire la puan na nyɛta.

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Ba ani batã wa puan, ti fum ti'ihɛ ti a dela buraa wa n lu fãareba nu'uhen wa mabia.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Fa'a Sia la wan bo' ya sankaŋa puan la, sɛla ti ya wan yele.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Fum niho en de fum iŋa wa niihum, bala, niho la han dɛna bu mɛŋa, fum iŋa la tari niihum mɛ, gee niho la han ka barɛ, fum iŋa la za'a boi la lika puan.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Ti yetɔge tɔgera la lerege yeti, Se'em n zɔ a nimbu'ɔ la. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Me kiŋɛ ta iŋɛ bala mɛa.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Ti nɛra ayima ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yele en yeti, Pa'ala, yele n suuɔ wa ti a tɔrɛ tu sɔ lɔgerɔ la mam la eŋa tiŋasuka.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Bala, bihɛ fum mɛŋa, ti niihum sebo en boi fum puan la dagena lika.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Ban boi sore zuo yɛ'a la, ba ta paaɛ la timbila kayima puan, ti pɔka bɔna bini ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Maata, yi' Yezu kule a yire.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Gee Yezu yele en yeti, N zɔ, ani n tum mam na ti n wa'am na dɛna gakera, bii yele maala bu fum.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Gee niihum han to'e fum iŋa la za'a ti lika ka bɔna, bu wan niiɛ piim wuu butolela en niiri se'em la.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Gee nyaa yele a zahereba la yeti, Tara ya mɛhi suŋa. Gu' ya mɛhi suŋa ti nyika sɛka da kɛ̃ ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra vom dagi la lɔgerɔ la tarega zo'e zo'e.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Maata wa me tari la a suuɔ yu'urɛ en yi'iri Mɛɛri, kɔ'ɔm zi'a Daana la nɛŋan kɛleha sɛla ti a tɔgera la.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Yezu en tɔgera la, ti Farasii ayima wa'am na yi' en ti a wa'am na yire na di la en. Ti Yezu kiŋɛ ta paaɛ zi'ire a zi'irego zi'an dia la saŋa.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba makerɛ dina wa yeti, Dakãarɛ deyima n wa kɔ a samanɛ ti dia buge.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Gee ti Maata yee kiileŋɔ la tuuma. Bala ti a wa'am na soke Yezu yeti, N Daana, Fum ka nyɛti n suuɔ wa bahɛ mam ti mam kɔ'ɔm kiilena la tuuma wa n ma'a? Yele en ti a ihege na suŋɛ mam.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Ti la iŋɛ Farasiiduma la pakerɛ, la Yezu en paaɛ na ka pee malema peere la yia gee zi'ire dia la puan la.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Ti a nyaa ti'ihɛ bo' a mɛŋa yeti, Beni ti n wan iŋɛ? Se'ere en sɔi la, n ka tari zi'an ti n wan biŋe n dia wa.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Gee Daana la yele en yeti, Maata, Maata, la daani fum mɛ, ti fum puti'irɛ ka gã'a tiŋa la tuuma kekeko la zuo.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Ti Daana la yele en yeti, Yamam Farasiiduma peere la laa, bii wanɛ la poore, gee ti ya puan ha pirɛ la nyika la putoo.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Ti a nyaa yeti, Mam n wan iŋɛ se'em n ŋwana. N wan gbi' n bã wa bahɛ, gee malum mɛ bunkãra, la n biŋe n dia, la n lɔgerɔ wa bini.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: gee yele deyina ma'a n pakɛ. Mɛɛri looe la yele suŋa ti nɛra kan ta'am to'e ka a zi'an.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Geto wa, dagi se'em en maalɛ yiŋa la n maalɛ puan la?.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Gee nyaa yele n sia yeti, N sia, fum tari lɔgerɔ zo'e zo'e wan di kɛ̃ yuuma zo'e zo'e. Bala, vɔ'ɔha fai, dita gee nyuura gee tara pupeelum. .

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Bala, dikɛ ya sɛla en boi ya puan la tihɛ, gee ya wan nyɛ ti ya sɛla woo ana filam bo' ya.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Gee Yinɛ yele en yeti, Gerego wa! Yu'uŋɔ kuna wa, n wan to'e fum vom la fum zi'an, Gee ti fum lɔgerɔ la ti fum lagehɛ biŋe la, tu bihɛ tumam n wan dɛna se'em dinɛ.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Gee yele boi yamam Farasiiduma wa zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya yɔɔri ya pia pia bo'olum la nima nima, ta ta' nangina la yɔgevuurɔ, la zɛvuurɔ woo ti ya yɔ' ti ya dugɛ, gee tugum zagehɛ yelemɛŋerɛ, la Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ. Yele ana n tugum dɛna ya ita, nyaa ita yan iti yele sɛ'a wa pa'aha.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Bala n wan iŋɛ sɛba woo n ka'aheri lɔgerɔ biŋera ba mɛhi, gee tugum dagena buntateba Yinɛ nɛŋan la.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, Yelesɛ'a n piti nɛra lubera wan ba'ahum bɔna mɛ, gee yele boi fum se'em zi'an ti yele ana wa ze'ele na la!.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Yele boi yamam Farasiiduma wa zuo, se'ere n sɔi la, ya nɔŋɛ la ya ni to'ohe nɛhi duma kugehi, Zamehegɔ yiya la puan, gee nɔŋɛ nɛreba dɔbeta pu'uha ya da'ahi puan.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Ti a yele a Zahereba la yeti, Bala, mam yeti ya yeti, da fabele ya vom wa yele, ti beni ti ya wan di, bii ya iŋa wa yele, ti beni ti tu wan yɛ.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: La wan ana suŋa bo' fum daana ti ba dikɛ nɛɛrɛ kugere lu' yule fum yugela, za'ɛ fum bahɛ ateko puan ti fum muhi, nyana fum bahɛ ti kɔmbibehi wa ayima pirɛ lu.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, ya ani mɛ wuu yɔɔrɔ en gã' ka tara daalehum la, ti nɛreba kina ba zuto gee ka mina.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa. Fum suuɔ han tuue, tahɛ ya en a tuure la puan, ti a han tee, ya di sugeri bo' en.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la vom wa gani dia mɛ, ti inganɛ wa gana futo.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Ti yetɔge tɔgera la ayima soke en yeti, Pa'ala, fum han tɔgera ŋwana, fum tu ta' tumam me mɛ n bala.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Nɛre-ina mɛa han pun tuue sugum nuuren buyopɔi daanyinɛ, gee lebe sugum nuuren buyopɔi na yele fum yeti, Mam tuue ya, la dela fum di sugeri bo' en.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Bihɛ ya guurɔ, Ba ka buti bii ba kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra. Ba ka tari deto bii bã biŋera dia, gee la bala za'a, Yinɛ en diiheri ba. Yamam tuulum gani niihe wa mɛ!.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Ti Yezu tuntuneba yele en yeti, Pa'ahɛ tu sakerɛ.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yele boi yamam yetɔge tɔgereba wa zuo! Se'ere en sɔi la, ya ni ziile la nɛreba zeero en tibegɛ gaŋɛ, gee kan dikɛ ya nu'dibila tebege zeero la suŋɛ ba.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Ya se'em fabelegɔ wan ta'am dikɛ gogo kaareŋa buyina ma'a pa'ahɛ a vom puan?.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Ti Daana la lerege ba yeti, Ya han tara sakerɛ ti de pun pɔ'ɛ wuu nanginɛ biileho la, fum wan ta'am yele sinsabega tia yeti, Vuuɛ fum mɛŋa ta sɛ fum mɛŋa ateko wa puan ha, ti tia la sirum nam hɔ.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Yele boi ya zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en mɛti la nɔtɔgeheba la ti ya yaabeduma yuum ku la yɔɔrɔ.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Fum han kan nyaŋɛ iŋɛ yele pika kana wa, ŋwani ti fum yi fabela yele sɛ'a n gee la yele?.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Ani ya puan, en wan yele ya da'aberɛ en nan kɔ'ɔm ze'ele ya nii kua puan na, bii a biheri ni la piihi sɛkan ha gee nan kɔ'ɔm kɛ̃ na, ti fum sakɛ yele en yeti, kɔ'ɔm wa'am na zi'ire la mam ti tu di?.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Bala, yamam en de aze' nyɛ duma, la sɛba n yuum pa' ya yaabeduma poore ti ba tum ba tuuma la. Bama n ku nɔtɔgeheba la, ti yamam me mɛ ba yɔɔrɔ.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Bihɛ ya mɔpuuro wa, bihɛ ya ban zo'ore se'em, ba ka tuni, bii ba wugera tama, gee n yele ya ti, la Naba Solomɔn la a suŋa la za'a ka paari mɔpuuro wa la kuyina me.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Fum tugum kan yele en yeti, Maahum n yu'uŋɔ dia na, yɛ fum duka lɔgerɔ, la fum maahum n yu'uŋɔ dia na ze'ele mam ti n di gee nyu ba'ahɛ, ti fum me nyaa wa'am na di gee nyu.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Bala ti Yinɛ mi'ilum la puan yeti, N wan tum n nɔtɔgeheba, la n tuntuneba na, gee ba wan ku basɛba, gee mɛlehɛ basɛba.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Gee Yinɛ han yɛɛlɛ samana zuto mɔpuuro n boi zina ma'a ti beere ti ba dikɛ ba bahɛ bugumen, a nyaa wan yɛɛlɛ yamam gaŋɛ gaŋɛ la ŋwani? Sakerɛ pɔ'ɛ ya duma wa.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Fum ni pu'uhɛ fum da'aba mɛ la fum bo' en nuurɛ ti a tum sɛla la?.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Bala ti buuri saŋa kana wa, wan tara taalɛ nɔtɔgeheba la za'a ziim n ka'ɛ, gee ti tiŋa wa baŋɛ naam la.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Da kɔ'ɔm pɛregeri ya ɛɛra yan wan di sɛla, la yan wan nyu sɛla, me da kɔ'ɔm fabele ya saŋa woo.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: La dela bala bo' yamam me, ya han tum sɛla ti ba bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya tum la ba'ahɛ, ni yele ya yeti, Tumam yeen dɛna la da'aba yooro, tum lan de tu tum se'em la ba'ahɛ.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: Ze'ele Abɛl ziim ka'arɛ, ta paaɛ nɔtɔgeha Zekariya ziim ka'arɛ la, se'em ti ba wa ku' en biŋe kaabegɔ daka la, la tuulum saraa la tiŋasuka la. Ɛ̃ɛ, bu taala wan dɔla la yamam zina beere nɛreba wa.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tiŋa wa zuo nɛreba n pɛregeri ba mɛhi ɛɛra yele ana woo, gee yamam Sɔ la mi' ti ya boti ba.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: En ye' Yerusalɛm la, a dole la tihi se'ehi en boi Samariya la Galilii tihi tiŋasuka la tɔla.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Yele boi yamam yetɔge tɔgereba wa zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en dikɛ mi'ilum kparehega la, ya kan kɛ̃ ya mɛŋa, gee me gu'ura sɛba en boti kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Gee tugum mu'ɛ ya nini la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, gee ba wan dikɛ yele ana za'a pa'ahɛ ya.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Yezu en tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ gee yehe na la, ti gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasiiduma la, iŋɛ suure la en, nyaa sɔgeha en yɛla zo'e zo'e doohe en tɔgɛ se'em la puan.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: A ta kɛ̃'ɛra la tiŋa kayima puan, ti kunkɔma pia tu'uhɛ en. Bɔna kinkɛleŋa ha.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Da zoti ya dabeem n pebibehi wa, la dela Sɔ la pupeelum ti a dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum la bo' ya.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: kɔ'ɔm pigera la ba wum ti ba bɛ̃rɛ nyɔkɛ en yele sɛla tuue ti ba nyɔkɛ en.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: gee yi' bahɛ na la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Yezu, Daana, zɔ tu nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Bala, koohe ya lɔgerɔ za'a dikɛ ligeri la bo' tarenduma. Maahum ya ligeri tapɔtɔ seto n ka aaheni bo' ya mɛhi, dikɛ ya ligeri biŋe saazuo ha, ze'sɛka ti nageyiga ka pɔra bini, bii mɔka wan paaɛ sagum.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: Yezu en nyɛ ba la, a yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta dikɛ ya mɛhi pa'alɛ malema daana la. Ti ban ye' la, ti ba kunkom la bahɛ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Fum lɔgerɔ en boi zi'an la, fum suure boi la bilam.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Ba puan ayima en ta nyɛ ti a nyɛ tibegɔ la, a ŋmɛregɛ mɛ lɛbera na, pɛgera Yinɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Bobe ya kankaŋi maahum tuuma. Bahɛ ti ya butolehi dita suŋa.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Wa'am na paaɛ lu tiŋa Yezu nɛŋan, bo' en fara. A me dela Samariya buuri.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: Ani ya wuu sɛba n ze' gura ba daana n kiŋɛ pɔgedire de'eŋo la, ti a han ta paaɛ na ŋmɛ', ba yu'ɛ nayaŋa la bo' en.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Ŋwana ti Yezu soke yeti, Ya dagi ni la pia en nyɛ ya tibegɔ? Gee bana bawai' la bɛɛ?.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Sum wan bɔna la da'aba se'ehi ti ba kpɛ'ɛma paaɛ na nyɛ ba ti ba kɔ'ɔm maahum mɛ zɛ'a. Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti kpɛ'ɛma la wan lu' a sɛganɛ, gee bahɛ ti a da'aba la tee zi'a, dɛla taaba ti a wa'am na tuta ba ti ba dita.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Bama la ayima me ka lebe na ti a dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ, han dagena fum saana wa ma'a.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: A han wa'am la yu'unsunsua na, bii bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ, nyɛ ba ti ba maahum ya, sum wan bɔna la da'aba sina taaba.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele en yeti, Ihege kina, fum sakerɛ en bahɛ ti fum dɛna fum mɛŋa.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Gee mini ya dina wa, ti yidaana han mina gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti nageyiga wan wa'am a yire na, a kan bahɛ ti a kɔ' kɛ̃ a yire la puan.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la le soke Yezu, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la en ye' se'em ma'a na. Ti a lerege ba yeti, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la ka tari naanɛ yɛ'a na ti fum wan nyɛ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Yamam me maahum, se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala Dayua lɛbega na la ye' la gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti ya kan baŋɛ.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: bii se'em yele fum yeti, Bihɛ, bumɔ en ŋwana bii Bumɔ en ŋwana ke ha. Lan de se'em la dela, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la pun diŋɛ bɔna mɛ la ya.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: Ti Pita yeti, Daana, fum makere dina wa bɔ'ɔra la tumam bii nɛra woo.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Gee nyaa yɛta a zahereba la yeti, Dabeha kini na, ti ya wan boti ni ti ya nyɛ ni mam Asaala Dayua dabeha wa deyina ma'a, gee ya kan nyɛ.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Ti Daana la yeti, Ani n nyaa de yameŋa n tari yɛm, gee mina tuuma, ti ka kpɛ'ɛma dikɛ Da'aba la woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen, ti a bɔ'ɔra ba dia ligeri de saŋa?.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Ba wan ta yɛta ya yeti, Bihɛ ya, en ŋwana ke ha! Bii Bihɛ ya, en ŋwana ke ha! Gee da kiŋɛ ya. Da doohe ya yele ana pooren.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Sum wan bɔna la Da'aberɛ sɛka ti ka kpɛ'ɛma ta lebena nyɛ ti ka tuni bala.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Wuu saa en ni nyakɛ saazuo sagbanɛ zuo, ze'ele bɔberɛ deyima ta paaɛ bɔberɛ deyima se'em la, bala mɛa ti a saala dayua wa lɛbega na wan ta ana a dabeha la puan.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta wa, ti a wan dikɛ la da'aberɛ kana ti a bihera a lɔgerɔ za'a.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Gee yia, la dela a ŋmibe namehɛ kãtɛ puan, ti zina beere nɛreba wa zagehɛ en.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Gee da'aberɛ wa han yele ka suuren yeti, N kpɛ'ɛma wa yuure mɛ, gee nyaa pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra yamehi sise'ehi, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a, gee dita en boti se'em, nyuura, ta nyuura bugera.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Wuu lan daa ani se'em Nowa saŋa la, bala mɛa ti lan wan ta ana Asaala Dayua dabeha la puan.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: da'aberɛ wa kpɛ'ɛma wa wan wa'am la daan se'ere ti a ka ti'iha ya, la gogo kaareŋa sɛka puan ti a ka mina. A wan ŋma en zɔra zɔra, dikɛ en ta pa'ahɛ sɛba en ka nane la puan.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Nɛreba yuum diti mɛ gee nyuura, gee dita pɔgeba gee ɛleha ba kɔma, ta paaɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti Nowa kɛ̃ daka la puan la, ti ko'om la uue na di ba za'a.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
local: Da'aberɛ sɛka en mi' ka kpɛ'ɛma n boti sɛla gee ka maahum a mɛŋa bii a iŋɛ sɛla ti ba bɔta ti a iŋɛ la, wan to'e ŋmɛ'kãtɛ.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: La ta yeti la ana mɛ wuu Lɔti dabeha la puan la. Nɛreba la yuum diti mɛ gee nyuura, da'ara, gee kɔɔha, buta gee mɛta.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Gee se'em n ka mi' gee iŋɛ sɛla n de ba ŋmɛ' en, eŋa ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ kan zo'e. Se'em woo ti ba bo' en zo'e, ba eere la zo'e zo'e a zi'an. Se'em ti ba dikɛ kekeko iŋɛ a nu'uhen, ba boti gana bala mɛ a zi'an.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: gee daan se'ere daarɛ ti Lɔti yehe Sodom la, ti Saazuo ni bugum, la tahi bugum bahɛ na sagum ba za'a.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Mam wa'am na ti n tari la bugum wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na. N boti ni ti ŋwana ma'a bu pun diti ni mɛ!.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: La ta yeti la ana la bala Asaala Dayua la niirɛ daarɛ la.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: N tari la sua ti n sɔ. Gee mam boi la wuŋerɛ se'ere puan gura de ba'ahɛ.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: De daarɛ, fum se'em en boi gɔhegɔ, gee ti fum lɔgerɔ bɔna deen, da le sige deen ti fum dikɛ. Bala mɛa ti se'em en boi va'amen, da lebe yire na.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Ya ti'ihɛ ti mam wa'am na ti n tari la suma'ahum wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na bii? Aayi, n yeti ya mɛ wa, mam tugum tari la welehego na.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Tɛri ya Lɔti pɔga la.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Pɔhɛ nananawa yɛ'a ha, nɛreba banuu n boi yire puan wan welehi, ti batã tugela la bayi' ti bayi' me tugela la batã.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Se'em en ti'ihɛ ti a wan ta'am gu' a vom, wan bɔregɛ bu bahɛ, gee se'em en wan bɔregɛ a vom bahɛ wan tara vom.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Ba wan welehe, sɔ wan tulege la ka dayua, ti dayua tulege la ka sɔ, ma wan tulege la a poyua, ti poyua tulege la ka ma, danyaama wan tulege la a saampɔga, ti saampɔga tulege la danyaama.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti de daarɛ, nɛreba bayi' wan ta lagum gã'a suŋɔ kuyina zuo, ti ba dikɛ ayima gee bahɛ ayima.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Ti Yezu yele nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Ya han nyɛ ti sagbanɛ biŋere nɛŋa bɔba zɔna na, ya ni yeti, Saa n yeti ka ni; ti la ni sirum dɛna bala.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Pɔgehi siyi wan ta bɔna nɛɛren nɛɛna, ti ba dikɛ ayima gee bahɛ ayima. .

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Ya han le nyɛ ti kusebego ze'ele tiŋanuurɛ lɔbera na, ya ni yeti, Zina wuntɛɛŋa wan ŋmɛ' paaɛ paaɛ; ti la ni sirum dɛna bala.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Ŋwana ti a zahereba la soke en yeti, Daana, bɛha ti la wan iŋɛ? Ti a yele ba yeti, sɛla en ki zi'an la, ziiro tiiheni la bilam.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Yamam birikɔgenɛ duma wa. Ya tã bihera tiŋa wa, la sagbanɛ wa baŋera sɛla n wan iŋɛ, gee ŋwani ti ya kan ta'am pa'alɛ sɛla en iti sankana wa puan?.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Ŋwani ti ya ka gakera sɛla en de yelemɛŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra ya mɛhi?.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Bala, fum la se'em han nyɛ tugum, yɛ'a tugum zi'an, yan boi sore zuo la, tiregɛ la fum ŋmɛ' nupu'ɔ la en, han dagena bala, a wan dikɛ fum bo' gakera la, ti gakera la la me dikɛ fum iŋɛ kpɛ'ɛma nu'uhen, ti kpɛ'ɛma la tari fum ta lobe bahɛ paka deo puan.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: N yele ya yeti, fum kan malum veehe na, han dagi na fum ta yɔ la za'a ti kɔbega ka gee.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Zi'an kayima ti Yezu ta yɛ'a, Farasiiduma nɛŋa daana ayima yire ti a ta di, vo'ohego dabeherɛ. Ti ba kɔ'ɔm pigera en nima nima.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Bala ma'a la ti buraa iŋa za'a n pɔgelum en ze' Yezu nɛŋan.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Ti Yezu ta soke yetɔge tɔgereba la la Farasiiduma la yeti, La de sore ti fum tibɛ nɛreba vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan bii la dagi.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ti ba sina. Bala ti Yezu yi' buraa la na, tibɛ ka gee bahɛ ti ka kiŋɛ.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Ti a nyaa yele ba yeti, Ya se'em han tara bombila bii lɔlega ti ka lu bulega puan, ya kan yaaɛ ka kalam bulega la puan vo'ohego dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ti ba ka ta'am wan lerege yele ana wa.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Yezu en nyɛ ti saama la woo demehe nɛŋa duma kugehi la se'em la, ti a makɛ makerɛ dina wa bo' ba yeti.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: Nɛra han yi' fum a pɔgedire de'eŋo puan ti fum kiŋɛ, da paaɛ zi'ire nɛŋa duma kugehi la zuo, daanse'ere nɛrezure boi ha gana fum ti ba yi' en.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: Se'em daana en yi' yamam bayi' la ta'am ta wa'am me na yele fum yeti, ihege ka'lam gee dikɛ bahɛ ti ena tee zi'ire bini. Bala ma'a la, fum nyaa ihegere mɛ la fagehum, ta zi'ita zi'an en boi tiŋa.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Gee ba han ni yi' ya zi'an ti fum ta paaɛ bini, kiŋɛ ta zi'ire kuge se'ehi en yeen zɛ'a bini la, ti se'em en yi' fum la han wa'am na, a wan yele fum yeti, n zɔ wa, ihege zom saazuo kugehi wa puan. Bala ti fum wan tara zuo sɛba woo en zi' la fum bimbine la zuo la.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba woo en zɛkeri ba mɛhi, ba wan sige ba tiŋa, ti sɛba woo en sigehe ba mɛhi tiŋa, ba wan zɛkɛ ba.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Gee nyaa yele se'em en yi' en la me yeti, Fum han yi'ira nɛreba ti ba wa'am fum yire na di wuntɛɛŋa, bii zaanuurɛ, da ni yi' fum zɔduma bii fum suurɔ bii fum nɛreba, bii kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛba en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri lɛm fum ma'a, ti ba me wan ta yi' fum me lerege zuo, la ba lebehe yɔ hɔ.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Gee fum han yi'ira nɛreba ti ba wa'am na di la hɔ, yi' naheduma, kɔɔrenɔ, wɔbehi la fɔɔhi la na.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: Ŋwana ti fum wan nyɛ sum, se'ere en sɔi la, bamam kan ta'am lebehe yɔ hɔ. Fum wan ta to'e fum yɔɔrɛ la tuntɔɔduma vo'ore la.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Saama la ayima n lagum bɔna dia la puan la n wum ŋwana la, ti a yele Yezu yeti, Sum wan bɔna la sɛba n wan ta bɔna Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan dita la.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Nɛra ayima en daa iŋɛ de'eŋo kãtɛ nyaa yi' nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Ti dia la saŋa ta paaɛ ti a tum a da'aba ti a kiŋɛ ta yele sɛba woo ti a yi' la yeti, Wa'ana gee sɛla woo maahum ba'ahɛ.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Gee ti nɛreba la za'a pɔhɛ suhera sore. Yia daana la yeti, Mam nan kɔ'ɔm da' la samanɛ, ti la dɛna n kiŋɛ de puan zina wa. Bala n beleni fum mɛ ti n kan ta'am wa'am na.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Ti a yima me yeti, Mam nan kɔ'ɔm da' la n nii kɔɔri i kɔɔhum bunuu, bɔta ti n dikɛ i kɔ bihɛ zina, bala n beleni fum mɛ, ti n kan ta'am wa'am na.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Ti ayima me yeti, Mam nan kɔ'ɔm di la pɔgesaaŋa bala zuo n kan ta'am wa'am na.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Ŋwana ti da'aberɛ wa lebe ta yele a kpɛ'ɛma wa sɛla en iŋɛ. Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa suure nyiie, ti a yele a da'aberɛ wa yeti, Yehe kiŋɛ sadɔkãra la sadɔbibehi woo zuto, la fum yi' naheduma, kɔɔrenɔ, fɔɔhi la wɔbehi woo ti ba la fum tari ba ka'lam na.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Ti Da'aberɛ wa ta lebena yele ka daana wa yeti, Fum yeti n iŋɛ se'em la tum ya, gee fole kelum bɔna.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa le yele da'aberɛ wa yeti, Le yehe sadɔkãra la, la sadɔbibehi la zuto, la fum darɛ nɛreba wa'am na pirɛ n yire wa.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: N yeti fum mɛ wa, ti sɛba woo ti mam yi' ba yia la, ba se'em nuurɛ kan vɛkɛ mam dia wa la fii me. .

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ n kini la Yezu, ti a ta ŋmɛregɛ yele ba yeti.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Se'em woo en wa'am mam zi'an na gee ka yaaɛ a sɔ la ma, a pɔga la a kɔma, a kiinduma la a tapa, Ɛ̃ɛ, pun ta ta' la a mɛŋa vom, kan ta'am dɛna mam zahera.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Se'em woo n ka zi a pumpuuredɔɔ dɔla mam, kan ta'am dɛna mam zahera.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ya ani en wan yeti ya mɛ dewoko, ka zi'ire yia bihɛ sanɛ se'ere n boi bini la a baŋɛ nyaa a tari zo'e wan mɛ deo la ba'ahɛ yoi?.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Han dagena bala, a han iibɛ biŋe gee ka ba'ahɛ, se'em woo en nyɛ ɛberɛ la wan ɛregera en.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: yeti, de bɛɛ, Nɛra wa daa pɔhɛ la mɛa, gee ka nyaŋɛ ba'ahɛ.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Bii beni naba n wan yɛ'a ti a ta tɛ' pɛɛma la naba kayima, kan zi'ire yia ti'ihɛ bihɛ nyaa a pɛntiireba tuha pia la wan ta'am nyaŋɛ naba kayima la n tari pɛntiireba tuha pihiyi' la?.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: A han bihɛ ti a kan nyaŋɛ, bala ma'a ti naba kana la nan zaaɛ ha la, a wan tum a kima, ti ba tu'uhɛ ba lu en ti suma'ahum wa'am na.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Bala zuo, ya se'em kan ta'am dɛna mam zahera han dagena ya zagehɛ la sɛla woo.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Yaarum ani suŋa mɛ, gee yaarum han bɔregɛ bu za'ahum bahɛ, bu za'ahum la wan le iŋɛ la ŋwani lebe bu puan na?.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: Bo ka tari tuunɛ bo' samanɛ, bii tampugerɛ. Ba ni kɔ'ɔm lobe yaarum bumɔ bahɛ mɛ. Bahɛ ti se'em n tari tuba ti a wuna, kɛleha.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Sɛba woo en to'ohe kuba la, la be'emduma za'a du'uri en mɛ ti ba kelehe en.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Ti Farasiiduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la gɔgena yeti, Nɛre-ina wa to'ohe be'em duma mɛ, gee dita la ba.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Ti Yezu makɛ makerɛ dina wa bo' ba yeti.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Ya ani en boi, tara piihi kɔbega, ti kuyima bɔregɛ, ti a daana kan bahɛ piihi pihiwai' la siwai' la sɛkan la, gee ɛɛra peyiŋɔ seko en bɔregɛ la tara tara ta nyɛ kɔ?.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: A han nyɛ kɔ, a wan nyɔkɛ ku buke, gee nyɛɛna.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: A han wa'am na paaɛ yire, a wan yi' a zɔduma, la sɛba en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri lɛm en, yele ba yeti, Wa'am ya na nyɛɛm la mam, se'ere en sɔi la, n nyɛ n pehego la n bɔregɛ la.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: Bala mɛa ti n yɛta ya wa, ti nyɛɛma kãtɛ en ni bɔna saazuo ha, be'em daana ayima han tee a puti'irɛ, nyana tuuntɔɔ duma pihiwai' la bawai' en pun tee gee ka le bɔta teere.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Bii beni pɔka n tari ka lage kuba pia, ti bu buyima han bɔregɛ, ti ka kan laŋɛ butolela, piihɛ bɔ'ɔ la za'a, gee ma'ɛ a mɛŋa bihɛ suŋa ta nyɛ a lagehɔ la?.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: A han ta nyɛ bɔ, a wan yi' a zɔduma, la a mabiihi woo n ki'ire la en la yeti, Wa'am ya na nyɛɛm la mam, se'ere n sɔi la, n nyɛ n lagehɔ la n bɔregɛ la.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: Bala mɛa ti n yɛta ya wa, Ti nyɛɛma kãtɛ n ni bɔna Yinɛ suŋereba tiŋasuka, be'em daana ayima han ni tee a puti'irɛ.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yeti, Buraa n wa bɔna tara a dayɔɔhi siyi.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Ti bɛ'a la ta yele ba sɔ la yeti, N sɔ, bo' mam n tɔtɛ n de se'em fum lɔgerɔ wa puan. Ti sɔ wa tɔrɛ a lɔgerɔ la bo' ba bayi' la.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Ti la iŋɛ dabeha fii wa, ti dayɔbila la kã'ahɛ a tɔtɛ la za'a nɔgɛ kiŋɛ tiŋa n zaaɛ, ta mɔgɛ a lɔgerɔ la za'a bilam bura bura.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: En ta di sɛla woo ti a tara la ba'ahɛ la, ti kom pɛɛlega me sige tiŋa la puan zi'an woo, ti la nyaa pɔhɛ wuna en.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Ti a dikɛ a mɛŋa ta bo' tiŋa la puan buraa kayima, ti kaŋa me tari en kiŋɛ va'amen puan ha ti a ta biha a kurikuri.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Ti a suure ba' boti ni ti a pita a puurɛ la tipɔgerɔ la ti kurikuri la dita la, gee nɛra ka bo' en sɛla.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: A puti'irɛ en ta lebe na la, a yele a mɛŋa yeti, Mam sɔ tara ba tuna la za'a diti tigera mɛ gee zɛ'ɛta. Gee mam ŋwana ti kom kuura ma wa!.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: N wan ihege lebe n sɔ wa zi'an, ta yele en yeti, N sɔ, mam tum la be'em bo' saazuo, la fum.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: Mam ka le nara ti ba yi' mam fum bia. Ita mam wuu fum sɛba ti fum tara tuna wa ayima.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Gee nyaa ihege to'e sore yɛ'a lɛbera a sɔ la zi'an. Gee en nan ta bɔna zaaɛ zaarɛ ha kina na la, ti a sɔ la nyɛ en baŋɛ, ti la ve'e en, ti a ihege zɔ tu'uhɛ en, gi' en mɔra, mɔgɛ en.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Ti a dayua la yele en yeti, N sɔ, mam tum la be'em bo' saazuo, la fum nɛŋan. Mam ka le nara ti fum yi' mam fum dayua.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Gee sɔ la yi' la a da'aba la yeti, Iŋɛ ya kalam tari fuo na, fuseko en ani suŋa gana futo la za'a la na yɛɛlɛ en. Tari ya salema nu'tua na pire a nu'dibila, gee tari tagera na pire en.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Gee la ya nyɔkɛ nagepole sebo en karegɛ gana nii la za'a la na ku, ti tu obe gee de'em.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam dayua wa daa ki mɛ gee le vo'e, ka daa bɔregɛ mɛ, gee lebe na! Bala ti ba pɔhɛ de'eŋo.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Bala ma'a la ti kiima la boi la va'amen ha, en ta kula na pɔra yire la, a wum la yuuma la wa'ahi.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Ti a yi' da'aberɛ de yima na soke en yeti, Beni en iti.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Ti da'aberɛ la lerege en yeti, Fum suuɔ la n lebena, ti fum sɔ ku nagepole sebo en karegɛ gana nii la za'a la, se'ere en sɔi la, sɔ la to'e en mɛ la inma'ahum.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Ti kiima wa iŋɛ suure, ti eŋa kan kɛ̃. Ti sɔ la wum, yehe na bɛlena en.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Ti a me zagehɛ, gee yele a sɔ la yeti, N sɔ, kɛleha. Yuuma kekeko ti mam tuni bɔ'ɔra fum me wuu da'aberɛ la, ka tabelɛ zagehɛ fum nuurɛ. Gee la bala za'a, fum ka tabelɛ bo' mam bubila, ti n me lagehɛ n taaba na ti tu de'em.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Gee fum dayɔkana wa en to'e fum lɔgerɔ la kiŋɛ ta mɔgɛ la yalunduma ba'ahɛ gee lebe na la, fum nyɔkɛ la nagepole n karegɛ gana za'a ku bo' en.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Ti a sɔ la yele en yeti, N dayua, fum boi la mam saŋa woo, mam en tari sɛla woo wa dela fum bunɔ.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Gee la dela tu de'em gee nyɛɛm, se'ere en sɔi la, fum suuɔ wa daa ki mɛ gee vo'e. A daa bɔregɛ mɛ gee ti tu nyɛ en. .

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Ti Yezu yele a zahereba la yeti, Buntata ayima en tari a lɔgebehera kpɛ'ɛma, ti nɛreba ta yɛta en yeti, buraa wa kɔ'ɔm Sagena a lɔgerɔ la mɛ.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Ti a yi' en na soke en yeti, Beni en bala ti n wuna ti fum iti me wa? Wa'am na bo' mam fum bihɛ n lɔgerɔ la paaɛ se'em beene, gee fum kan le dɛna mam tuuma wa nɛŋa daana yɛ'ɛha.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Ti lɔgebehera kpɛ'ɛma wa nyaa sɔkera a mɛŋa yeti, N nyaa wan iŋɛ la ŋwani, n kpɛ'ɛma wa en ku a puti'irɛ ti a to'e kpe'eŋo wa n zi'an wa? N ka kpe'em wan ta'am tu' N me zoti la vi ti n suhɛ.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: N mi' mam wan iŋɛ se'em, ti n kpɛ'ɛma wa han pun sa'ɛ mam n kpe'eŋo wa puan, nɛreba wan kelum tɔ'ɔha mam ba yiya puan.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Ti buraa wa ihege yi' sɛba en tari a daana la sama la ayima, ayima lagehɛ na, soke yia daana la yeti, Fum tari n daana sanɛ la ŋwani.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Ti a yeti, Kaam dugelehi kɔbega. Ti a yele en yeti, To'e fum sanɛ gɔŋɔ en ŋwana, zi'ire ka'lam gulehɛ dugelehi pihinuu kalam iŋɛ bini.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Gee le soke ayima me yeti, Fum tari n daana sanɛ la ŋwani? Ti a yeti, Ki pitɔ kɔbega. Ti a yele en yeti, To'e fum sanɛ gɔŋɔ n ŋwana, zi'ire ka'lam gulehɛ pitɔ pihinii kalam iŋɛ bini.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: A daana la en ta baŋɛ la, a pɛgɛ ŋmɛlema lɔgebehera kpɛ'ɛma wa mɛ, la kaŋa en nyaŋɛ tarehɛ ka yele la se'em la yɛm. La dela bala mɛa, zina beere wa nɛreba tã zãla ba mɛhi saŋa wa yɛla la yɛm gana niihum puan nɛreba.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Bala, n yeti ya mɛ wa, ti ya dikɛ ya ŋmɛlema lɔgerɔ nyɔgɛ zɔtɔ, ti lɔgerɔ la han ta ba'ahɛ, ba wan bo' ya bɔŋa zi'an ti fum bɔna wuuha.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ daana yele pigehi puan, wan dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ daana yelekãra puan. Se'em daana en pini yele pika puan me wan pina yele kãtɛ puan.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Fum han ka tara yelemɛŋerɛ ŋmɛlema lɔgerɔ puan, ani en wan sakɛ dikɛ yelemɛŋerɛ lɔgerɔ iŋɛ fum nu'uhen?.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Fum han ka tara yelemɛŋerɛ la ayima bunɔ, ani en wan dikɛ sɛla en de fum mɛŋa bunɔ bo' fum?.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Yameŋa ka boi wan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi'. Yameŋa la wan ba'ahum yaaɛ ayima, gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' ayima, gee bahɛ ayima. Fum kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, gee tuna bɔ'ɔra ligeri.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Farasiiduma la en de lige bɔteba la en wum ŋwana za'a la, ba ɛregeri Yezu mɛ.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya dela nɛreba en bo'ori ya mɛhi buurɔ nɛreba nɛŋan, gee Yinɛ mi' ya suya puan en ani se'em mɛ. Sɛla en de tuulum lɔkɔ nɛreba nɛŋan, fi'ɛ mɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Ze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba la tugum la woo tum mɛ, tara tara ti Yɔn ta wa'am na. Yehe bilam, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yɛla kɔ'ɔm muula la bala, ti nɛra woo zigera ba pɛregɛ kɛ̃ bu puan.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: La ka tɔi ti saazuo la tiŋa wa mɛlum, gee la kpe'em mɛ ti gulehegɔ pika pa'alegɔ wa puan saalum.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Se'em woo en lebehe a pɔga gee tee di kayima, tum la kabohi, ti se'em woo me en di pɔgesɛka ti ka sira lebehe ka, me tum la kabohi.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Buraa kayima en wa bɔna dɛna buntata, bagera ka mɛŋa saŋa woo la banaahi la doogila, ti dia, la de'eŋo bɔna ka yire daare woo.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Gee a nayaŋa nuuren, buraa kayima en de naha, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Lazarus, ti nɔra to'e ka iŋa za'a, n gã' bini.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: ti ka suure ni bɔta ti ka pirɛ ka puurɛ la disɛka ze'ele buntata dia zi'an la zɔgɛ la; gee baahi kina lɛɛla ka nɔra la.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Ti naha wa ta ki, ti Yinɛ suŋereba wa'am na tari en kiŋɛ Yinɛ yire, ta bɔna Aberaham zi'an. Ti buntata la me ta ki, ti ba pi' en.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: Ti a kiŋɛ bugum tiŋa puan, ta bɔna namehegɔ puan, gee gorege a zuo, nyɛ ti Aberaham boi zaaɛ zaarɛ ha, ti Lazarus gã'a a nyu'ɔ zuo bilena en.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Ti a yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, N sɔ Aberaham, zɔ n nimbu'ɔ gee bahɛ ti Lazarus dikɛ a nu'dibila ti'ɛ ko'om puan na yɔɔlɛ n nuuren, gee n kunkɔya n kãberi. Se'ere en sɔi la, la wuni mam mɛ bugum peelum wa puan.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Gee Aberaham yele en yeti, N bia, tɛra ti fum yuum boi vom puan tiŋa zuo la, fum yuum nyɛ fum sum mɛ za'a, gee ti Lazarus me bɔna mɛlehegɔ puan. Gee nananawa, a boi la sum zi'an ka'lam, gee ti fum me bɔna mɛlehegɔ puan.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Gee pun dagena bala ma'a, gee tumam la fum tiŋasuka, boko kãtɛ en beŋe, ti sɛba en wan bɔta ti ba ze'ele ka'lam yakɛ bilam na kan ta'am iŋɛ bala, ti se'em me kan ta'am ze'ele bilam yakɛ tumam zi'an na.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Ti buntata wa yeti, N sɔ, n ye beni fum mɛ ti fum tum Lazarus ti a kiŋɛ n sɔ yire.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, mam tari la n suurɔ tunuu ha. A kiŋɛ ta tɔgɛ bo' ba la ninmu'urɛ, ti ba da ta wa'am namehegɔ kuna wa puan ze'kana wa puan na.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Ti Aberaham lebehe en yeti Moose, la nɔtɔgeheba la woo boi ha. Ba kɛleha ba.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Ti buraa wa lebehe yeti, Aayi n sɔ Aberaham, se'em n ki la han kiŋɛ ba zi'an, ba wan tee.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ti Aberaham lebehe en yeti, Ba han kan kelehe Moose la nɔtɔgeheba la, bala mɛa ti se'em n ihege kum puan han pun lebe ta tɔgɛ bo' ba, ba pun kan tee ba puti'irɛ. .

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Ti Yezu wa'am na kɛ̃ Yeriko tiŋa nyaa kina yɛ'a.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Ti buraa boi bini ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Zakiyɔsi, dɛna kubetɔ'ɔhereba la nɛŋadaana, pãrɛ.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Ti buraa wa zigera ti ka nyɛ Yezu en de se'em, gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la zuo, a ka nyɛti Yezu, doohe la buraa wa ba' dɛna la nɛre pika a naanɛ puan la zuo.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Bala ti a zɔ diŋɛ nɛŋa ta zom kinkaŋa zuo ti a nyɛ Yezu, se'ere en sɔi la Yezu dole la bilam tɔla.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Yezu en wa'am na paaɛ bilam la, a ze'ele mɛ bihɛ tia la zuo ha, gee yele Zakiyɔsi, Zakiyɔsi, kabelɛ kalam sige na, gee zina, mam kɔ'ɔm yeti n dɛna fum yire saana zina.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Ti Zakiyɔsi kabelɛ sige na, ti a puurɛ pee ti a to'e Yezu a yire.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Ti sɛba woo en nyɛ ŋwana la pɔhɛ gɔgeleŋɔ la ninmu'urɛ yeti, A ye' ti a ta dɛna la be'em daana wa yire saana.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Gee Zakiyɔsi ze'ele la bilam yele Daana la yeti, Bihɛ n Daana, mam wan puregɛ n lɔgerɔ wa za'a zinzimɔ bo' naheduma, ti se'em me ti n daa pim en sɛla puan, en wan yɔ a daana sugum nuuren bunaahi lebehe bo' en.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Zina, faarɛ wa'am yire dina wa puan na, se'ere en sɔi la, buraa wa me dela Aberaham dayua.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam Asaala Dayua wa wa'am na ti n yehe ɛ mɛ, la n faaɛ sɛba en bɔregɛ la bahɛ.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Nɛreba la en kelehe ŋwana la, ti a yele ba makerɛ dina wa, en pɔri Yerusalɛm la zuo, la ban me en ti'ihɛ ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la sunti bu wa'am nananawa na la.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Ti a yele yeti, Zuodaana ayima en wa kiŋɛ tiŋa en zaaɛ ti a ta to'e sɔ'ɔlum buyima muŋa bo' a mɛŋa lebena.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Gee nyaa yi' a da'aba pia na, bo' ba ligekɔba pia, gee yele ba yeti, Tari ya tuna gura ti n ta lebena.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Gee buraa wa tiŋa nɛreba ka nɔŋɛ a yele, gee tum nɛreba a pooren, ti ba ta yele tiŋa la duma yeti, Tumam ka boti buraa kana wa biha tɔ.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Buraa wa en ta lebena la muŋa la, a bo' la nuurɛ ti ba yi' da'aba se'ehi ti a bo' ba ligekɔba la na, ti a baŋɛ ban dikɛ ligeri la tum nyɛ nyuurɔ se'em.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Ti yia daana la wa'am na yeti, N Daana, fum lagekɔberɛ la tum nyɛ la ligekɔba pia pa'ahɛ.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Ti zuodaana wa yele a da'aba wa yeti, Fu tuuma tuuma, fum dela da'abesuŋa. Beni zuo, fum en de yelemɛŋerɛ daana la lɔgerɔ fii wa la, tara tihi pia bihera.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Ti yi'i daana la me wa'am na yeti, N Daana, fum ligekɔberɛ la tum nyɛ la ligekɔba anuu.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Ti Zuodaana wa yele en yeti, Fum me, tara tihi sinuu bihera.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Ti ayima me wa'am na yeti, N Daana, fum lage kɔberɛ la en ŋwana. Mam daa pɔbɛ bu mɛ suŋa la tanɛ biŋe.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: se'ere en sɔi la dabeem en daa tari mam la hɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, fum dela nɛre wurebere. Fum digehɛ la sɛla ti fum ka biŋe, gee me kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra zi'an ti fum ka burɛ.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Ti Zuodaana wa yele da'aberɛ wa yeti, N wan gakɛ hɔ la fum mɛŋa nuurɛ yetɔgum wa. Yameputua wa. Fum mi', fum pun mina ti mam dela nɛra wurebere, digeha sɛla ti n ka biŋe, gee kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra zi'an ti n ka burɛ?.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Ti ŋwani ti fum ka tari n lagehɔ la ta biŋe ligeri biŋere zi'an? Ti mam lebe ni na wa, n wan to'e ni n lagehɔ la ti dɔgelega pa'ahɛ.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Gee nyaa yele sɛba en ze' la yeti, To'e ya lage kɔberɛ la a zi'an bo' se'em en tari ligekɔba pia la.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti se'em woo en tari la, ba wan bo' en kekeko pa'ahɛ, gee se'em en pun ka tara sɛla la, sɛla fii wa ti a tara la, ba wan to'e la a zi'an.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Gee han dɛna mam dinduma sɛba en ka boti ni ti mam dɛna naba sɔna ba biha la, tari ya ba wa'am ka'lam na, la ya kɔrum ba n nɛŋan wa. .

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yezu en yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a le to'e la sore yɛ'a zɔna Yerusalɛm.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: En ta pɔra Bɛtefege la Bɛtani tihi la, ze'sɛka ti ba yi'ira Olive Zoore la, ti a tum a zahereba bayi'.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya kɛ̃ timbila wa en boi ya nɛŋan wa puan. Yan ta kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ka puan la, ya wan nyɛ ti ba lu' buŋa ze'ele, ti nɛra nan ka tabelɛ ba' ka. Lorege ya ka tari na bo' mam.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Se'em han soke ya yeti, Ya loregere buŋa la bu? Yeen yele ya en yeti, Daana la en boti ka. .

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Bala ti sɛba ti a tum la kiŋɛ, ta paaɛ nyɛ dɛ wuu Yezu en pa'alɛ ba se'em la.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Ti ban boi bini lɔregera buŋa la, ti ka daana soke ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya lɔregera buŋa la.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Ti ba yeti, Daana la en boti ka.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Ti ba tari buŋa la wa'am Yezu zi'an na. Ti ban yɛɛhɛ ba futo yaregɛ buŋa la zuo ba'ahɛ la, ba nyaa te'ele Yezu mɛ ti a zom yagelɛ ka zuo.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: Yezu en ba' buŋa la kina la, ti nɛreba tara ba futo yarena sɔya la zuo.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: En ta pɔra Olive Zoore la zuleŋa sore la, Ti a zahereba kuuŋɔ la za'a pɔhɛ pɛgera Yinɛ la nyɛɛma vɔɔla yɛta paŋa tuuma sɛ'a ti ba nyɛ.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: nyaa yɛta yeti, Sum boi mɛ la naba la en dikɛ Daana la yu'urɛ wa'am na la. Suma'ahum boi saazuo, Ti na'ahegɔ bɔna saazuin zuin ha.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Ŋwana ti Farasiiduma la basɛba en lagum bɔna nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan la yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, yele fum dɔleba la ti ba go'e.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Ti a me yele ba yeti, Mam yeti ya me wa, ti bamam han pun sina, kuga wa wan voole mɛ bahɛ na.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Yezu en ta pɔra na nyɛ Yerusalɛm tiŋa la, a kele mɛ ka zuo.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: yeti, Fum wa, fum wa fum han mini zina beere wa, Yele sɛ'a en tari suma'ahum kina na la. Gee nananawa yele aŋa sugɛ mɛ fum ninin, ti fum ka nyɛta.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, dabeha a sɛ'a ye' fum zuo na, dabeha sɛ'a ti fum dinduma wan ti' dɔtɔ kaaɛ fum nangoone la za'a, mum zabera la fum luga woo.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Ba wan wɔ̃regɛ fum za'a sige tiŋa, sagum fum la fum kɔma biŋe fum puan. Ba kan gee kugere deyina dɔgelɛ de tadaana zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, fum daa ka baŋɛ Yinɛ limehego saŋa.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Ti Yezu kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la, pɔhɛ sa'aha sɛba woo en koohe lɔgerɔ bini la.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: gee yɛta yeti, La gulehɛ yeti, Mam yire wa dela pu'uhegɔ yire; gee bihɛ, yamam dikɛ de lebege la fãareba suka zi'an.

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Daarɛ woo, Yezu boi la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan pa'ala. Gee malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la, la yizuto kima la me mu'ɛ nini ɛɛra sadɔseko ti ba wan doohe ku' en.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: Gee ba ka malum nyɛ sɛla ti ba wan iŋɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba la za'a nɔŋɛ, gee bɔta ba kɛleha a yelehum la mɛ.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Ti Yezu makɛ makerɛ bo' ba, ti ba pu'uha, saŋa woo gee ti ba giila da kɔ'ɔha.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: A yeti, Tiŋa kayima puan ti gakera ayima bɔna bini, ka zɔta Yinɛ, bii a dikɛ nɛra yi' sɛla.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Tinkaŋa mɛa puan, pokuurɛ me en boi bini, kina gakera wa zi'an daare woo yɛta en yeti, Gakɛ n yele wa bo' mam buurɔ, mam la se'em en tɔgeri wa tiŋasuka.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Ti gakera wa zagehɛ ti la yuue fiiwa. Gee pooren ti a ti'ihɛ bo' a mɛŋa yeti, La mam pun ka zɔta Yinɛ, benti n dikɛ nɛra yi' sɛla.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: gee pokuurɛ wa en kini daana mam se'em wa la, n wan tɔgɛ bo' en buurɔ, ti a da kina daana mam bala saŋawoo. .

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Ti Daana la nyaa yeti, Wum ya ŋmɛlema gakera wa en yele se'em.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Gee Yinɛ en tugum kan bo' sɛba ti a mɛŋa looe la en kaahi bɔ'ɔra en bulika la yu'uŋɔ la? A wan yuue mɛ gee suŋɛ ba yoi?.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: N yele ya ti, Yinɛ kan gu'um, a wan kabelɛ bo' ba buurɔ. Gee la bala za'a, Asaala Dayua wa han ta wa'am na, a wan nyɛ sakerɛ duma tiŋa wa zuo.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Ti Yezu le makɛ makerɛ dina wa bo' sɛba en nam ba mɛhi ti bamam dela tuuntɔɔ duma, gee zɛɛna basɛba la.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: Nɛreba bayi' en kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire ti ba pu'uhɛ, ba ayima dela Farasii ti ayima dɛna kuba tɔ'ɔha.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Ti Farasii la dooe zɛ'a, gee pu'uha yeti, Yinɛ, mam bo' fum fara, la mam en ka ani wuu nɛreba basɛba, fãareba, nɛreŋmɛlema, bii sɛba en tuni kabohi, pun ta paaɛ kuba tɔ'ɔha wa.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Gee mam luti la nuurɛ sugum nuuren buyi' dabeha ayopɔi woo puan, mam yɔɔri n pia pia bo'olum mɛ n sɛla woo puan.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Gee kubetɔ'ɔha la ze' zaaɛ mɛ, pun kan gorege bihɛ saazuo, gee sura lɔbera a zuo gee yɛta yeti, Yinɛ, zɔ mam be'em daana wa nimbu'ɔ.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: N yele ya yeti, burakana en wan kule yire la buurɔ gee dagi yia daana la. Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba woo en zɛkeri ba mɛhi, ba wan sigehe ba tiŋa. Gee sɛba woo en sigehe ba mɛhi, ba wan zɛkɛ ba.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Ti nɛreba tara ba kɔma, ta ta' la kɔmbilɛɛhi kina Yezu zi'an na, ti a han wan dikɛ a nu'o pagelɛ ba zuto. Yezu zahereba la en nyɛ ŋwana la, ba yele nɛreba la mɛ la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Gee Yezu yi' ba mɛ wa'am a nɛŋan na yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti ba tari kɔmbibehi wa wa'am n zi'an na, da gu'uri ya ba, se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en ani wuu kɔma wa, ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la dɛna ba dinɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti se'em woo en ka to'e Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa wuu bia, kan malum kɛ̃ bu puan.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Nɛŋa daana ayima en wa soke Yezu yeti, Pa'ala sunkɔ, Beni en de n iŋɛ la n to'e vom kamba'ahɛ wa.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum yi' mam ti sunkɔ? Nɛra ka boi dɛna sunkɔ, han dagena Yinɛ ma'a.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Fum mi' nɔze'elego la, Fum da tum kabohi, Fum da ku nɛra, Fum da zu, Fum da dɛna aze' nyɛ pumparema, Na'aha fum sɔ la fum ma.

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Ti buraa wa lebehe yeti, Mam dole yele ana za'a mɛ n bumbile paalemen na.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yezu en wum ŋwana la, a yele en yeti, Yele deyima n kelum pɔ'ɛ hɔ. Koohe fum tari sɛla woo la, la fum dikɛ ligeri la tɔrɛ naheduma, ti fum tarega la nyaa bɔna saazuo, gee ti fum wa'am na dɔla mam.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Gee buraa wa en wum ŋwana la, a suure sagum mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, a ba' pãrɛ mɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Ŋwana ti Yezu bihɛ en zii gee yeti, La kpe'em mɛ bo' sɛba en de buntateba ti ba kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la de naana bo' yugenɛ ti de doohe fupiho yoko puan tole, gana se'em en tari kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Ti sɛba en kelehe en la yɛta yeti, Ti ani en nyaa wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe ba yeti, Sɛla en kpe'em bo' saaleba, ka kpe'em bo' Yinɛ.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita nyaa yeti, Bihɛ, tumam bahɛ tu yiya mɛ gee dɔla fum wa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, se'em ka boi bahɛ a yire, a pɔga, bii a suurɔ bii a dɔgereba, bii a kɔma Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa zuo.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: gee kan malum nyɛ gaŋɛ bala saŋa kana vom wa puan, la buuri sansɛka en kini na la vom kamba'ahɛ la en kini na la puan.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Ti Yezu tari a zahereba pia la bayi' la lebe kinkɛleŋa ba ma'a, ta yele ba yeti, Bihɛ ya, tu ye' zɔna la Yerusalɛm wa, zi'an ti sɛla woo ti nɔtɔgeheba la gulehɛ doohe mam Asaala Dayua wa puan la wan tum.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Ba wan nyɔkɛ en bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma, ti ba ɛregɛ en, wan iŋɛ en sɛla en ka ani suŋa tu en, gee tubehɛ nɔtuurum ma' en.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: Ba han ta ŋmɛ' en ba'ahɛ, ba wan ku' en, gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan ihege.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Gee Yezu zahereba la pun ka bɔkɛ sɛla sɛla yetɔgebuna wa puan. Yelemɛŋerɛ, en yele ba sɛla la sugɛ mɛ bo' ba, ti ba ka gurɛ sɛla ti a yele la.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Yezu en ta pɔra Yeriko la, ti fua en zi' sore nuuren suha.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: Fua wa en wum ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la kini tɔla la, ti a soke ba sɛla en iti.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Yezu en de Nazareti buraa la en ye' tɔla.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ti fua wa kaahɛ supi yeti, Yezu, Deviti Dayua wa, zɔ n nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Ti sɛba en boi nɛŋa la tahɛ en ninmu'urɛ yeti, ana bããlam. Gee fua wa tugum malum voole gaŋɛ la bala yeti, Deviti Dayua wa, zɔ n nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Ti Yezu teke ze'ele su'u gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari buraa la wa'am na a zi'an na, ti ba tari buraa la wa'am na nɛŋan na. Ti buraa la en ta pɔra na la, ti Yezu soke en yeti.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: Beni ti fum bɔta ti n iŋɛ bo' fum? Ti fua wa yeti, N Daana, bahɛ ti n le nyɛta.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Nyɛta, fum sakerɛ en faaɛ hɔ.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Bilam bilam ti buraa la le nyɛta, nyaa dɔla Yezu poore pɛgera Yinɛ, ti nɛreba la za'a n nyɛ ŋwana wa la, me pɛgɛ Yinɛ.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti Yezu pa'ala nɛreba pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, gee yɛta ba faarɛ yelehum la, ti malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la doohe la yizuto kima la wa'am a zi'an na.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: yele en yeti, Yele tu, beni paŋa ti fum tara tuna tuun-ana wa? Ani en bo' fum sore.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Ti a lebehe ba yeti, Mam me wan soke ya sokere ti ya lebehe mam.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: Yɔn ko'om sua la ti a daa sɔɔra la yehe la saazuo ze'a na bii asaala zi'an.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Ti ba lebe ta suhɛ la taaba yeti, Tu han yeti, Bu ze'ele la saazuo na, a wan soke tu nyaa, Beni ti tu daa ka sakɛ bo' en.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Tu me han yeti, Bu ze'ele la asaala zi'an na, nɛreba wa woo en ze' wa wan lobe tu la kuga. Se'ere en sɔi la bamam woo mi suŋa suŋa ti Yɔn dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Bala ti ba lebe ta yele en yeti, Bamam ka mi' bu n daa ze'ele zi'an na.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Ti Yezu me yele ba yeti, Bala ti mam me kan yele ya se'em en bo' mam sore ti n tuna tuun-ana wa za'a la.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Ti Yezu pɔhɛ yɛta nɛreba la makerɛ dina yeti, Buraa n burɛ a tiihi dia va'am, dikɛ bo' bihereba ti ba bihera, gee kiŋɛ tiŋa kayima n zaaɛ ta yuue.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Ti go'ohego saŋa ta paaɛ, ti a tum a da'aberɛ bihereba la zi'an ti ba to'e a tɔtɛ bo' n na. Gee Bihereba wa en nyɛ va'am daana wa da'aberɛ la wa la, ba nyɔkɛ en mɛ ŋmɛ' gee bahɛ en la nu'bɛɛla ti a lebe.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Ti va'am daana wa le tum a da'aberɛ ba zi'an, ti ba nyɔkɛ kaŋa me ŋmɛ' gee iŋɛ en sɛla en ka suŋa gee bahɛ en ti a lebe nu'bɛɛla.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Ti a le kelum tum ta'a daana, ti ba ŋmɛ' eŋa me pɔgelum dige en bahɛ yiŋa.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Ti va'am daana wa nyaa yele a mɛŋa yeti, Beni ti n nyaa wan iŋɛ? N nyaa wan tum la n mɛŋa dayɔba'arɛ wa, daanse'ere ba wan na'ahɛ eŋa.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Gee sɛba en biheri va'am wa la n ta nyɛ ti va'am daana Wa dayua la kini na la, ti ba sɔhɛ bo' taaba yeti, Se'em en wan ta sooe sɔna la en bala kina na wa. Bahɛ ya ti tu ku en gee ti lɔgerɔ wa nyaa dɛna tumam dinɛ.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Ŋwana ti ba gurɛ en ve'e yehe va'am la yiŋa, ku en. Beni ti va'am daana wa wan dikɛ iŋɛ sɛba en biheri a va'am wa?.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: A wan wa'am na sagum sagum bihereba bana wa za'a gee dikɛ va'am la tee bo' basɛba. Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana la, ba kaahɛ me buki yeti, Yinɛ da sakɛ ti ŋwana iŋɛ.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
