local: Ti Yezu bihɛ ba zii, gee yeti, Tɔgebuna wa vuurɛ dela deni? Kugese'ere ti mɛteba zagehɛ la, kugedinɛ mɛa en de ɛ̃berɛ la zuo?.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Se'em woo en lu kugedina wa zuo wan ŋma zɔra zɔra, kugedina wa me wan mɛrehɛ se'em daana woo ti de lu a zuo.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: Gɔnmi'ineba la, la malema kiinduma la en baŋɛ ti a makɛ makerɛ dina wa bahɛ la bamam zuo la, ba maahum mɛ ti ba nyɔkɛ en gogo kaareŋa kaŋa puan la, gee zɔ nɛreba la dabeem.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Ti ba lebe pooren ta ana bããlam, gee tum viihereba en ani wuu ba dela nɛresuma a zi'an na, ti ba ta'am bɛ̃rɛ en doohe a nuurɛ yetɔgum puan, ti ba ta'am nyɔkɛ en ta bo' yele gakereba la timbihega kpɛ'ɛnduma la.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Bala ti nɛrebana wa soke Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, tu mi' ti sɛla ti fum yɛta bii fum pa'ala, dela ma'a, fum ka lugehe nɛreba tɔgera, gee fum pa'ali la Yinɛ sore la en de se'em la la yelemɛŋerɛ la puan.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: La de sore bo' tu ti tu yɔɔra tu kuba bɔ'ɔra Kaiza bii la dagi de sore.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Gee Yezu baŋɛ ba nyaleŋɔ la za'a mɛ, gee yele ba yeti.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Pa'alɛ ya mam dinaari, ani zuo, la ani yu'urɛ en boi bu zuo la? Ti ba yeti, Kaiza.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Bala, yi dikeri ya sɛla en de Kaiza dinɛ bɔ'ɔra Kaiza, gee dikera sɛla n de Yinɛ dinɛ, bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Ba ka nyaŋɛ nyɛ la yeleyinɛ mɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la nɛŋan ti ba wan ta'am bɛ̃rɛ en la en tɔgɛ se'em la. Ti a lebehego la me iŋɛ ba pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba yari yee suram.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Ti Sadusii duma, sɛba en yeti ihegere ka boi la, wa'am Yezu zi'an na.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: soke en sokere yeti, Pa'ala, Moose yuum gulehɛ bo' tu yeti, buraa suuɔ han ki gee bahɛ a pɔga ti ka ka tara bia, buraa la suuɔ di pokuurɛ la dɔgɛ kɔma purɛ a suuɔ la yu'urɛ.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Ŋwana ti suurɔ tuyopɔi bɔna, ti yia daana la di a pɔga, ki gee ka tara bia.

eng: The second.
local: Ti se'em en tagelɛ la sooe di.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: ti ta'a daana me wa sooe di. La iŋɛ la bala ti ba bayopɔi la di pɔka la soohum, ki gee ka tara bia.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, ti pɔka la me ta ki.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Ihegere saŋa la puan, ba ani n wan ta sɔna pɔka la, gee ti ba bayopɔi la za'a di ka soohum la.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Sɛba en boi vom buna wa puan sankana wa puan la, en diti pɔgeba gee ɛleha pɔgehi.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: Gee sɛba ti ba bihɛ ti ba nari ti ba tara zi'an sansɛka en kini na la, la ihegere saŋa la, kan ta dita pɔgeba bii ɛla sireba.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ba kan le ta kiira, se'ere en sɔi la ba ta yeti ba ana mɛ wuu Yinɛ Suŋereba la, dɛna Yinɛ kɔma en yehe, ihegere la puan.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Han le dɛna ihegere la puan, Moose mɛŋa pun pa'alɛ mɛ ti la boi mɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la tuntuo la n wa taaɛ dita bugum la, zi'an ti Moose tɔgɛ a Daana la yele yeti, a dela Aberaham Yinɛ, Aziki Yinɛ la Yakɔbe Yinɛ la.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Yinɛ me kan ta'am dɛna ki'induma Yinɛ, gee a dela vɔpa Yinɛ. Yinɛ zi'an, ba woo vɔi mɛ.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Ŋwana ti gɔnmi'ineba basɛba lebehe yeti, Pa'ala, fum kɔ'ɔm tɔgɛ la ma'a.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en soke en la ka le tara nuurɛ ti ba le soke en se'ere pa'ahɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ba wan ta'am yeti, Kiresta la dela Deviti dayua?.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Deviti mɛŋa yele a Yuuma Gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, Daana la yele mam Daana la yeti, Zi'ire n zuɔ nu'o wa.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: gura ti n ta bahɛ ti fum dinduma lebege fum nama dafeere. .

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Deviti yi' en la a Daana, bala ŋwani ti ba wan yi'ira en Deviti dayua.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Ti Yezu yele a zahereba la nɛreba za'a tuban yeti.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Mini ya gɔnmi'ineba wa n nɔŋɛ ba ɛɛra kaara la fuwogero wa. Ba nɔŋɛ la nɛreba dɔbeta pu'uha ba da'ahi puan, gee nɔŋɛ ba zi'an nɛheduma kugehi zuto, pu'uhegɔ yiya puan, la nɛŋa duma kugehi zuto de'eŋo kãra puan.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Ba saari la pokɔpa yiya. Ban boti nɛreba nyɛta ba la zuo, ba pu'uhi la pu'uhi wogero. Ba wan ta to'e sageŋɔ kãtɛ en gani za'a.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Ti Yezu nyɛ ti buntateba tara ba bo'olum kina ita bo'olum pi'ɔ la puan.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: ti a nyɛ ti pɔkɔ-naha tari a lagekɔba ayi' ta iŋɛ pi'ɔ la puan.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Ti a yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, pɔkɔ-naha wa en bo' gana ba za'a.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, bana woo yehe la sɛla ba tarega la puan bo', gee eŋa la a nɔŋɔ la za'a, a bo' la en tari sɛla za'a wan di ni en bala.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Nɛreba basɛba en tɔgeri pu'uhegɔ yire la yele, ban gulɛ ku se'em la kuga suma, la bo'olum sumɔ sebo taaba ti nɛreba tara bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ ku puan la. Ti Yezu yele ba yeti.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Lɔgetuna wa woo ti ya nyɛta wa, dabeha kini na, ti kugere kan gee paga de tadaana zuo. Ba wan gbirege ba za'a sige tiŋa.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Ti nɛreba la soke en yeti, Pa'ala, ŋwani ma'a ti yele dina wan iŋɛ, la daalehum boni n wan pa'alɛ ti yele wa pɔri.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mini ya suŋa ti se'em da pã'ahɛ ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba zo'e zo'e wan wa'am mam yu'urɛ puan na yeti, Mam n de en. Sankana wa pɔri mɛ. Da dole ya ba poore.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Ya han ta wuna pɛntɛrɛ yɛla la gbiregeri yɛla, da bahɛ ya ti ya paleŋa ɛgera, Se'ere en sɔi la, yele ana woo wan ba'ahum iŋɛ yia, gee ba'ahegɔ saŋa la kɔ'ɔm kan tagelɛ yele aŋa pooren.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Ti a nyaa yele ba yeti, Tiŋa wan ihege zabera la tiŋa, ti sɔ'ɔlum ihege zabera la sɔ'ɔlum.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Tinmiiŋo kãra wan wa'am na, ti zi'ihi sise'ehi me kom pɛɛlega la bã'ahi kãra me lu. Palen-ɛka yɛla la daalehum kãra wan zɛ'ɛta saazuo wa na.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Gee ti yele ana wa za'a wan pɔhɛ la, ba wan pɔhɛ nyɔgɛ ya, namehɛ ya, gee dikɛ ya tihɛ ba zamehegɔ yiya puan la paka deto puan. Ba wan tari ya ta ze'ele naduma la timbihereba nɛheduma nɛŋan mam yu'urɛ wa zuo.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ŋwana wa wan dɛna fole yu'ɛ bo' ya ti ya muulɛ mam yele.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Bala, da maahum ya puti'ira ti ya te'ele ta gu' ya mɛhi.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, mam mɛŋa wan ta bo' ya yelesɛ'a la yɛm sebo ti ya dinduma la kan ta'am ze'ele la ya bii tɔgɛ nyaŋɛ ya.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Dɔgereba, la ya suurɔ, la yireduma, la ya zɔduma wan pun ta koohe ya tihɛ, ti ba ku ya sɛba.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Nɛreba wan yaaɛ ya za'a mam yu'urɛ wa zuo.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Gee ya zuo zonko la kuyina me kan bahɛ bɔregɛ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Ya ŋmibega puan ti ya sɛɛhi wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Ya han ta nyɛ ti pɛntiireba kaaɛ Yerusalɛm tiŋa wa mum, mini ya ti Yerusalɛm sageŋɔ la pɔri.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Bala, yamam sɛba en boi Yuda tiŋa la, zɔ zom zɔya zuto, ti sɛba en boi tiŋa puan la, me bahɛ ka, ti sɛba n boi yiŋa ha me da kɛ̃ ka puan na.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dabeha ana dela leregere dabeha, ti la doohe gulehegɔ la en gulehɛ yele se'em la.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Yele boi pɔgepuuhi duma, la sɛba en tari kɔmbilɛɛhi ti ba mɔgera zuo, dabeha ana puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ wan bahɛ ti susagekãtɛ sige tiŋa wa zuo, ti a sunsunyiim taaɛ bahɛ nɛreba wa zuo.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Su'a wan kɔrum ba, gee ti ba nyɔgɛ basɛba tari kiŋɛ ti ba dɛna da'aba tihi zo'e zo'e puan, gee ti nɛresɛba en ka dole Yinɛ wan nɛ Yerusalɛm tiŋa wa taalɛ tiŋa, gura ti sɛba en ka mi' Yinɛ la paŋa saŋa la ta wa'am na tum ba'ahɛ.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Daalehum wan ta bɔna yintule wa puan, ŋmarega wa puan la ŋmarebibehi puan. Tiŋa wa zuo, daalehum la wan ta dɛna la susageŋɔ en wan paaɛ tihi doohe la tihi n wan tulum ba mɛhi se'em doohe la ateko ko'om wa dameŋa, la bu ko'mi'ihi.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Nɛreba wan lige la dabeem, doohe la ban nyɛ sɛla n ze'ele saazuo sigera ba zuo na la. Se'ere en sɔi la, saazuo wa ha pahi la han ta yeti la miim mɛ.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Bilam ti ba nyaa wan nyɛ Ti Asaala Dayua boi sagbanɛ zuo sigera na, la paŋa la suŋa kãtɛ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Yele ana wa han ta pɔhɛ itegɔ, ihege ya saazuo la ya gɔra biha saazuo, se'ere en sɔi la ya ayɔto'e la pɔri na.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Ti a nyaa yele ba makerɛ yeti, Bihɛ ya kinkaŋa wa la tiihi wa za'a.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Ba han ni ta puhera vɔpaala, ya mɛhi wan nyɛ ge mina ti siiɔ pun paaɛ mɛ n bala.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Bala mɛa, ya han ta nyɛta ti yele ana iti, ya mina ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la pɔri na.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti zina beere nɛreba wa kan ba'ahɛ gee ti yele ana wa wa'am na tum.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Saazuo la tiŋa wan ba'ahɛ, gee mam yelehum wa kan malum ba'ahɛ.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Gurɛ ya ya mɛhi suŋa, han dagena bala ya suya wan tibegɛ la nyɛlumkãra la buka la vom wa puti'ira ti daandina wa la ta wa'am na di'e ya wuu bɛregɔ.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la dabedina wa wan sige se'em woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Bala, maahum ya ya mɛhi saŋa woo, Pu'uheri ya ti ya wan tara paŋa nyaŋɛ piiɛ yele ana wa woo n wan iŋɛ la, gee ta'am ze'ele Asaala Dayua la nɛŋan.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Daare woo Yezu ni bɔna la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan pa'ala, gee ti yu'uŋɔ woo ti a ni yehe kiŋɛ ta bɔna zoore se'ere ti ba yi'ira Olive Zoore la zuo.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ti nɛreba la za'a ni ihege bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ, kiŋɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ti ba ta kelehe a yetɔgum la.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la ti ba yi'ira azɔɛtole la ta pɔra mɛ.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: ti malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la nyaa ɛɛra sose'ere ti ba wan doohe ku Yezu, se'ere en sɔi la, dabeem en daa tari ba la nɛreba la.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Ti Sagena la kɛ̃ Yudasi ti ba yi'ira Isikariyɔte, dɛna Yezu zahereba pia la bayi' la ayima la.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Ti a welege kiŋɛ ta naaɛ la malema kiinduma la, la pu'uhegɔ yire kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛba en biheri pɛntiireba la, nyaa yele ba eŋa en wan koohe Yezu bo' ba se'em.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Ti ba puurɛ pee gaŋɛ, ti ba ku nuurɛ ti ba bo' en ligeri.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: ti a me sakɛ, gee nyaa ɛɛra sore ti a koohe Yezu bo' ba, sansɛka ti nɛrekuuŋɔ kan ta bɔna la Yezu.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la ti ba ni kaabɛ azɔɛtole pebila la saŋa la en bala paaɛ.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Bala ti Yezu tum Pita la Yɔn, yele ba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta maahum azɔɛtole dia bo' tu, ti tu wa'am na di.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Bɛha ti fum bɔta ti tu ta maahum azɔɛtole dia la ti tu di.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Kelehe ya, ya han ta kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, ya wan tu'uhɛ buraa ti ka zi ko'om la yoore. Doohe ya ka, ta kɛ̃ yire se'ere puan ti a wan kɛ̃ la.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: la ya yele yire la daana yeti, Pa'ala la yeti, Saama bɔ'ɔ la bɛɛ ti n zi'ire di azɔɛtole dia wa la n zahereba wa? .

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Yidaana la wan pa'alɛ ya bɔ'yaleŋa ti ba pun maahum kɔ. Maahum ya azɔɛtole dia la bilam bo' tu.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Bala ti a zahereba la kiŋɛ, sirum ta nyɛ sɛla woo wuu Yezu en yele ba se'em la, nyaa maahum azɔɛtole dia la bilam.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Saŋa la en ta paaɛ la, ti Yezu zi'ire a zi'irego zi'an dia la zi'an, ti a tuntuneba la zi'a la en dɛla taaba.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam ba' boti ni ti n di azɔɛtole wa mɛ la ya gee nyaa kɛ̃ n namehegɔ wa puan.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: gee n yeti ya mɛ wa, ti n kan di, gura ti dia la vuurɛ en de se'ere la tum tole Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Gee nyaa dikɛ wula la daam, bo' Yinɛ fara ba'ahɛ, gee yele ba yeti, To'e ya nyu gee bo' taaba.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: Mam yeti ya mɛ wa ti, ze'ele sankana yɛ'a, mam kan nyu tiihi wala daam wa, gura ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la wa'am na.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Gee dikɛ boroboro, en bo' fara ba'ahɛ la, a fike boroboro la mɛ bo' ba gee yeti, Mam iŋa en ŋwana dikera bɔ'ɔra ya wa. Iti ya ŋwana tɛra n yele.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: A iŋɛ daam la wula la me la bala mɛa ban di ba'ahɛ la, a yeti, Wula wa en ka'ari bɔ'ɔra ya wa dela mam nɔpu'ulepaalɛ ti n pu'ula la ya, la n ziim.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Gee bihɛ ya, se'em en koohe mam la en bala zi'a la mam wa, ti a nu'o bɔna laa wa puan wa.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: La dela sira ti Asaala Dayua la wan kiŋɛ wuu lan gã'arɛ se'em na la, gee yele boi se'em en wan koohe en la zuo.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Ti a zahereba la pɔhɛ sɔkera taaba, ba se'em n wan sakɛ tum tuun-dina wa.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Ti nɔkpe'ene lu Yezu zahereba la tiŋasuka, ba se'em n gani za'a zuo.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Tiŋa wa zuo na'duma dikɛ ba mɛhi di' la ba nɛreba, ti sɛba en boi kpe'eŋo puan me dikɛ ba mɛhi di' nɛresɛba en boi ba nu'uhen la.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Gee la kan ana bala bo' yamam. La tugum dɛna se'em la dela la, se'em en gani za'a ya puan la, tugum dɛna bia ya za'a puan. Ti se'em en de nɛŋadaana ya puan, tugum lebege ana wuu se'em en tɔti la.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Ti'ihɛ ya de bihɛ, ani n gani za'a? Se'em en zi' dɛla la bii se'em en zi' tɔta la? Dagi se'em en zi' dɛla la? Gee mam boi la ya mɛ wuu se'em en ze' tɔta la.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Yamam en de sɛba en ze' la mam kankaŋi n mageha wa woo puan.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Bala ti mam dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum bo' ya, wuu Sɔ la n dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum la iŋɛ mam nu'uhen se'em la.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: ti ya ta ta'am di gee nyu la mam n dia zi'an n sɔ'ɔlum la puan, gee zi'a na'am kugehi zuo bihera Izirale buuri pia la bayi' la.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Ti Yezu yeti, Simɔn, Simɔn, kɛleha suŋa, Sagena la to'e la yɔ'ɔ ti a yeele ya za'a wuu ki uto la bahɛ.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Gee mam pu'uhɛ bo' fum mɛ ti fum sakerɛ da koŋe. Gee fum, fum han ta ŋmɛregɛ lebe na, kpemehe fum suurɔ wa.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Ti Pita yele Yezu yeti, N Daana, mam maahum ya ti mam la fum lagum kɛ̃ paka deen, la han pun dɛna la kum me, n ki la hɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Ti Yezu yeti, N yeti fum Pita, ti nɔdɔɔ kan kaahɛ zina, han dagena fum ta ki'ihɛ butã ti fum ka mi' mam.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam daa tum ya ti ya yehe gee ka tara ligeri dãhi, la sore kinɛ tapɔtɔ,bii tagera la, ya daa koŋe la sɛla? Ti ba yeti, Aayi, sɛla sɛla.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Gee nananawa, se'em en tari ligeri daŋa a dikɛ, fum se'em me en tari sore kinɛ tapɔɔ fum dikɛ, ti se'em me n ka tari su'a, a koohe a fuo da' sua.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti gulehɛ kuna dela ku wa'am sira na mam puan. La gulehɛ yeti, Ba kãalɛ en pa'ahɛ la nageyigehi puan. La sirum dela sira ti ban gulehɛ mam yele se'em la sirum wa'am na tum.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, Daana, bihɛ, sɔ'ɔhi siyi en ŋwana. Ti a yele ba yeti, La seke ya.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Ti Yezu yehe na yɛ'a Olive Zoore zuo wuu en ni pun ita se'em la, ti a zahereba la doohe en.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Yezu en ta paaɛ en ye zi'an la, a yele ba yeti, Pu'uhi ya mɛ ti makerɛ be'o saŋa la da nyaŋɛ ya.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Ti Yezu vurege wuu nu'o kugelobete lua zi'an, gee bahɛ ba, nyaa kpa' duma, pu'uhɛ yeti.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: N Sɔ, fum han bɔta, da bahɛ ti n nyu namehegɔ wanɛ dina wa puan. Gee dagi mam suboolum, gee fum suboolum en de se'ere la tum. .

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Ti Suŋera ze'ele saazuo, wa'am na zi'an na kpemehe en.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Ti a ze'ele a susageŋɔ la puan pu'uhɛ tuulɛ tuulɛ, ti a iŋa muregere ana wuu ziim la tɔgeha tiŋa. Diimen diimen gulehegɔ tuseto ka tari ka'lam.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: En ihege a pu'uhegɔ la puan la, a lebe la a zahereba la zi'an na, paaɛ na nyɛ ba ti ba gã' giha mɛ, susageŋɔ zo'e zo'e la zuo.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya giha? Ihege ya la ya pu'uhɛ ti pã'ahebe'o saŋa la da nyaŋɛ ya.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: En boi bini tɔgera la, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ en bala bibigera na, ti se'em ti ba yi'ira Yudasi, dɛna Yezu zahereba pia la bayi' la puan ayima la, en boi ba nɛŋa. Ti a paara Yezu zi'an na ti a mɔgɛ en;.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: ti Yezu yele en yeti, Yudasi, fum yeti fum koohe Asaala Dayua wa me la mɔka wa yoi.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Sɛba en boi la en en nyɛ nɛreba la en kini na la, ba soke yeti, Daana, ti kɛ̃' ba la sɔ'ɔhi.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la ayima fooe a su'a kɛ̃' malema nɛŋadaana ayima da'aberɛ tuberɛ fike bahɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Da le pa'ahɛ dɛ. Gee kalum tuberɛ la, ti de lebe iŋɛ sum.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yele malema kiinduma la, la pu'uhegɔ yire pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛnduma, la yizuto kima la en wa'am na ti ba nyɔkɛ en la yeti, Yamam yehe mɛ na la sɔ'ɔhi la dɔya wuu mam dela nageyiga?.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Mam ni bɔna la ya daare woo pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, gee ya ka nyɔkɛ mam. Gee ya saŋa en ŋwana, lika puan paŋa saŋa en ŋwana.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Ti ba nyaa nyɔkɛ Yezu, tari en kiŋɛ, tari en na kɛ̃ malema nɛŋadaana la yire. Ti Pita bɔna pooren ha dɔla na.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Ti ba ta tuurɛ bugum zinzaka la tiŋasuka ɔɔha, ti Pita me wa'am na zi'ire la ba.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: Ti pugela en de da'aberɛ nyɛ Pita bugum la zi'an, bihɛ en gee yeti, Buraa kana wa ni dɔla la Yezu.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Ti Pita ki'ihɛ yeti, Pɔka wa, mam ka mi' en.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa, ti nɛra ayima me bihɛ en gee yeti, Fum dela ba ayima. Ti Pita yeti, Buraa wa, mam ka pa'ahɛ ba puan.

eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Ti la le iŋɛ wuu gogo kaareŋa, ti nɛra ayima me kelum yeti, aayi, Yelemɛŋerɛ, Buraa kana wa me ni bɔna mɛ la Yezu, se'ere en sɔi la a dela Galiliiŋa.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Ti Pita yeti, Buraa wa, mam ka mi' fum yeti sɛla wa. Sankaŋa puan la, Pita en nan boi bini tɔgera la, ti Nɔdɔɔ kaahɛ.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Ti Daana la ŋmɛregɛ bihɛ Pita. Ti Pita tiɛ Daana la yelehum la, en daa yele en yeti, Gee ti nɔdɔɔ ta kaaha zina, ti fum ki'ihɛ butã ti fum ka mi' mam.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Ti Pita yehe yiŋa ta kele ninmu'urɛ.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Ti buraahi se'ehi en gurɛ Yezu la pɔhɛ kɛhera en gee ŋmɛ'ɛra en.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: dikɛ sɛla lu' a nɛŋa gee sɔkera en yeti, Bukɛ ani n ŋmɛ' fum wa.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Gee kɔ'ɔm tuura en tuunyooro yima yima.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti yizuto kima, la malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la lagehɛ taaba, ti ba nyaa tari Yezu wa'am ba kiinduma tugum zi'an la na.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Fum han dɛna Kiresta la, yele tu. Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam han pun yele ya, ya kan pun nam.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: n me han soke ya sokere, ya me pun kan lebehe.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Gee yehe nananawa, Asaala dayua la wan zi'ire la Yinɛ Paŋa zuɔ nu'o.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Ti ba za'a soke yeti, Fum nyɛ dela Yinɛ dayua? Ti a yele ba yeti Ya yele la bala wa.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Ti ba yele taaba yeti, Beni aze'nyɛ le yɛ'ɛha ti tu le ɛɛra ti tu nyɛ? Tu wum de la tu mɛhi a mɛŋa nuuren.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Ti tiŋa la tiiheŋɔ la naaɛ nuurɛ, tari Yezu wa'am Palete zi'an na.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Nyaa pɔhɛ zuuha Yezu bɔ'ɔra naba la yeti, Tu nyɛ buraa wa mɛ ti ka gbiregeri tu tiŋa wa, gu'ura tumam ti tu da yɔɔra kuba bɔ'ɔra Kaiza wa, gee yɛta yeti, Eŋa en de Kiresta la, dɛna naba.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Ti Palete soke en yeti, Fum en de Yuuduma naba la? Ti Yezu lerege yeti, Fum yele la bala wa.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Ti Palete yele malema kiinduma la, la nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Mam ka nyɛ se'ere ya zuuhego la woo puan ti ya zuuhe buraa wa la.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Gee ba malum tahera mɛ baha na yeti, A gbiregeri la nɛreba la a pa'alegɔ la Yuda puan za'a, pɔhɛ Galilii tara tara la ka'lam na la.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Palete en wum ŋwana la, a soke ba me nyaa buraa wa dela Galiliiŋa.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: En ta baŋɛ ti Yezu yehe la Hɛrɔd sɔ'ɔlum puan na la, a bo' la sore ti ba tari en kiŋɛ Hɛrɔd zi'an, bala ma'a ti Hɛrɔd mɛŋa pun wa'am la Yerusalɛm na.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Hɛrɔd en ta nyɛ Yezu la, a puurɛ pee mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, a gu'uhi ti a nyɛ Yezu ti la yuue mɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, a wuni la a yele, bɔta ti a nyɛ en ti a tum daalehum ti a nyɛ.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Ti a soke Yezu sɔgeha ta paaɛ beene deyima, gee Yezu ka lebehe en sese'ere.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Ti malema kiinduma la, la gɔnmi'ineba la kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ nini zuuha Yezu.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Ti Hɛrɔd la a pɛntiireba la pun ta ita en sɛla en ka ani suŋa, gee kɛha en, dikɛ na'am fuo yɛɛlɛ en, gee bahɛ ti ba tari en lebe Palete zi'an.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Daan dina daarɛ en de Palete la Hɛrɔd nyɔgɛ zɔtɔ, gee ba ni dɛna la dataahi la taaba.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Ti Palete yi' malema kiinduma, la Yizuto kima la, la nɛreba la woo lagehɛ na.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: yele ba yeti, Ya tari buraa kana wa na bo' mam ti a dela nɛra en pã'ahɛ nɛreba, bihɛ ya ti mam me soke en ya nɛŋan, gee n ka nyɛ tuure, yele sɛ'a woo ti ya dikɛ zuuhe en la puan.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Hɛrɔd me ka nyɛ tuure se'ere a puan, gee le tulege en bo' tu na wa. Yelemɛŋerɛ, a ka iŋɛ sɛla ti la nara a ki.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Bala zuo, n wan bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' en, gee ti n bahɛ en. .

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Bala ti nɛreba la naaɛ nuurɛ voole bahɛ na yeti, Tari ya burakana wa kiŋɛ ha. Gee yehe ya Barabase bahɛ bo' tu.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Ti Palete en boti a yehe Yezu bahɛ la zuo, ti a tɔgɛ la nɛreba wa le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: gee ba kɔ'ɔm taha baha me na yeti, Kpa' ya en, Kpa' ya en.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Ti Palete le tɔgɛ bo' ba sugum nuuren butã yeti, Ŋwani, Beni be'em ti a tum? Mam ka nyɛ en tuue se'ere ti tu wan ta'am ze'ele de zuo ku en. Bala n wan bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' en gee bahɛ en.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Gee nɛreba la mu'ɛ la nini gaŋɛ bala, tahera baha na yeti, A dela ba kpa' en, ba voole la bala ta nyaŋɛ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Ti Palete ta ku yeti, bamam en boti se'em la tum.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Gee yehe buraa sɛka ti ba bɔta ti a yehe bahɛ la bahɛ. Buraa sɛka ti ba pagɛ ka doohe gbiregeri, la nɛrekua yɛla la, gee dikɛ Yezu bo' ba wuu bamam en boti se'em la.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Ban tari en yɛ'a la, ba gurɛ la buraa kayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Simɔn, yehe Saarin tiŋa, kɔ'ɔm ze'ele bo' en kina na, ti ba gurɛ en. To'e pumpuuredɔɔ la ziile en, ti a ziira ku dɔla Yezu poore.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ en ka ta'am kãalɛ dɔla poore, ti pɔgehi bɔna bini kaaha gee nyɔna zɔ'ɔra.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Gee Yezu ŋmɛregɛ mɛ bihɛ ba gee yeti, Yerusalɛm poyɔɔhi wa, da kele ya bɔ'ɔra mam, gee tugum kele ya bɔ'ɔra ya mɛhi la ya kɔma.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Bihɛ ya, se'ere en sɔi la, dabeha sirum kini na, ti ba wan ta yeti, Sum boi mɛ la kundɔgereba puya sɛ'a en ka tabelɛ dɔgɛ, sɛba en ka makɛ dɔgɛ, la sɛba en ka tabelɛ mɔgehɛ bia.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Nɛreba wan ta pɔhɛ yɛta zɔya wa yeti, Lu ya tu zuo na; gee yɛta zɔbibehi me yeti, Pi' ya tu na.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Ba han ita dɔma'aherɛ ŋwana, la nyaa yeti la ana la ŋwani bo' dɔkɛ'ɛŋa.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Nɛreba bayi me en de tuunfi'iha duma, ti ba tari ba yɛ'a ti ba ku ba la en.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Ti ba wa'am na paaɛ zi'an ti ba yi'ira zuwama zi'an la, ti ba kpa' Yezu bilam, la tuunfi'iha duma la, ti ayima bɔna Yezu zuɔ, ti ayima bɔna a gɔbega. .

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yeti, N Sɔ, di sugeri bo' ba gee ba ka mi' ban iti sɛla. Ti pɛntiireba la ŋmɛ' gbaŋa ti ba tɔrɛ Yezu en yɛ lɔge seto la.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Ti nɛreba zɛ'a zaaɛ zaarɛ gee biha na. Gee ti nɛheduma la tuura yeti, A ka tã faara la sɛba baha, bahɛ ya ti a faaɛ a mɛŋa bahɛ, a han sirum dɛna Yinɛ Kiresta la, se'em ti a looe la.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Ti pɛntiireba la me kɛha en, tari daam mi'ihego zom ta bɔ'ɔra en.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: gee yɛta en yeti, Fum han sirum dɛna la Yuuduma naba, faaɛ fum mɛŋa.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Ba me daa gulehɛ la gulehegɔ kpa' dɔɔ la zuo Yezu zuin yeti, Yuuduma naba la en ŋwana.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Ti tuunfi'iha duma bayi la ayima ti ba lagum kpa' la kɔ'ɔm tuura Yezu gee yɛta yeti, Fum dagi la se'em ti Yinɛ looe la, Faaɛ fum mɛŋa, gee faaɛ tumam me.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Ti tuunfi'iha duma la ayima la tahɛ ena la yeti, Fum ka zoti Yinɛ, fum la eŋa en lagum to'e sageŋɔ namehegɔ kuyina wa?.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Tumam namehegɔ wa dela de sore, se'ere en sɔi la, tumam to'ore la tu tuuma yɔɔrɛ en bala, gee buraa kana wa ka tuue se'ere.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Gee nyaa yeti, Yezu, ta tɛra n yele, fum han ta bɔna fum sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, ti zina, fum wan bɔna la mam ze'suŋa puan.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Wuntɛɛŋa en wa bɔna wuu zuo tiŋasuka la, ti lika wa'am na pi' tiŋa la za'a gbareti, tara tara la gogo kaareŋa atã wuntɛɛŋa.

eng: while the sun stopped shining. Then the curtain in the sanctuary tore down the middle.
local: Ti wuntɛɛŋa iŋɛ lim, ti tanɛ se'ere en yule beŋe pu'uhegɔ yire puan la ahegɛ buyi'.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Ti Yezu kaahɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, N Sɔ, fum nu'uhen ti n bahera n sia. En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a vo'ohe la a ba'ahegɔ vo'ohego.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Ti pɛntiira wa en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega wa la en ze' bini gee nyɛ sɛla woo en iŋɛ se'em la, ti a dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛra wa ka tari tuure.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en lagehɛ bilam na gee nyɛ sɛla woo en iŋɛ se'em la, lɛbera kula gee ɔbera nyɛna.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Gee sɛba en lɛm la Yezu la, la pɔgehi la en doohe la en ze'ele Galilii na la woo ze' la kinkɛleŋa ha gee biha yele ana woo.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Buraa kayima en me boi, dɛna nɛretuntɔɔ la nɛresuŋɔ ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Yosefe. La en pun lagum dɛna tiŋa la kima ayima la.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: a ka lagum sakɛ bo' ba puti'irɛ la, la ba itegɔ la. Buraa wa yehe la Yuda tiŋa kayima en yi'iri Arimatiya, daa gura la ninmu'urɛ ti Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la wa'am na.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Buraa kana wa en kiŋɛ Palete zi'an ta yele en Yezu iŋa la yele.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Gee nyaa yakɛ Yezu iŋa la, pɔbɛ ka la tansaalega en de pɛɛlega, tari ta biŋe yɔɔ ti ba kibegɛ taŋa puan, ti ba nan ka tabelɛ biŋe kum ku puan.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Didaarɛ dela maaheŋɔ saŋa, ti vo'ohego la me pɔhera.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Ti pɔgese'ehi en doohe Yezu ze'ele Galilii na la, doohe ta nyɛ yɔɔ la, la ban biŋe Yezu iŋa la se'em.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Gee nyaa lebe kule, ta maalɛ kaam en tari nyuuŋo suŋɔ. Vo'ohego daarɛ la, ba vo'ohe mɛ wuu nɔze'elego la en pa'alɛ se'em la.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Pi'ileŋo puan na la, ti se'em en de Yelehum la pun bɔna mɛ. Yelehum wa boi mɛ la Yinɛ, gee ti Yelehum wa dɛna Yinɛ.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Yelehum buna wa boi mɛ la Yinɛ pi'ileŋo puan na.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Sɛla woo en boi wa, naam doohe la eŋa puan. Han dagi eŋa puan, zugɔ la ayima me ka boi sɛla woo en boi wa puan, wan boni.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Eŋa puan ti vom bɔna, vom buna wa en de niihum bo' timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Niihum wa niiri baha la lika la puan, ti lika la kan ta'am lile bɔ.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Buraa kayima ti Yinɛ daa tum ka, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Yɔn.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: Buraa kana wa wa'am na dɛna la Aze'nyɛ daana, ti a dɛna aze'nyɛ daana bo' niihum la, ti nɛra woo doohe eŋa puan sakɛ.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Yɔn mɛŋa yuum dagi niihum la, gee wa'am na ti a dɛna la aze'nyɛ daana bo' niihum la.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ niihum la en niiri bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo la en ye' timpaberɛ wa zuo na.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: A pun bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo mɛ na, ti timpaberɛ wa naam doohe la eŋa puan. Gee la bala za'a, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka mi' en.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: A wa'am la sɛla en de a mɛŋa bunɔ puan na, gee a mɛŋa nɛreba la ka to'e en.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Gee sɛba woo en to'e en la, sɛba en sakɛ bo' a yu'urɛ la, bamam ti a bo' ba paŋa ti ba dɛna Yinɛ kɔma.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Bama sɛba dɔgum en yeen dagena ziim en lagum taaba dɔgum, bii inganɛ boolum, bii asaala suboolum puan, gee Yinɛ suboolum puan.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Ti se'em ti ba yi'ira Yelehum wa, ta lebege asaala, bɔna la tɔ, ti tu nyɛ a suŋa, sunsɛka en pa'ali ti a dela Sɔ dayɔba'arɛ, pirɛ la zu'uhegɔ, la yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Yɔn en yuum tɔgeri Yelehum wa yele la, a yuum kaahɛ bahɛ me na la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Ena en bala ti mam tɔgɛ a yele yeti, Nɛra ayima n boi mam pooren, gee gana mam, se'ere en sɔi la, a pun bɔna mɛ na gee ti mam nyaa wa'am na. Yɔn .

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: Eŋa en seke paaɛ paaɛ la, nɛra woo wan nyɛ zu'uhegɔ, malum nyɛ zu'uhegɔ.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: La de asira ti Ze'elego la doohe la Moose puan na, gee zu'uhegɔ, la yelemɛŋerɛ ze'eti la Yezu Kiresta puan na.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Nɛra nɛra ka tabelɛ nyɛ Yinɛ. Gee la dela Yinɛ biba'arɛ la, en de Yinɛ la bɔna a Sɔ la suure la en dikɛ en pa'alɛ.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Yɔn en yele se'ere en iŋɛ n ŋwana, Yuuduma la en tum malema duma la Levi duma ze'ele Yerusalɛm na ti ba soke en yeti, Fum dela ani la.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Yɔn yele ba mɛ, ka zagehɛ, gee kɔ'ɔm yele ba yelemɛŋerɛ yeti, Mam dagi Kiresta la.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Gee fum nyaa dela ani? Fum dela Elia? Ti a lerege ba yeti, Aayi mam dagi. Gee fum dela nɔtɔgeha? Ti a yeti, Aayi.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Gee fum dela ani? Lerege tɔ se'ere ti tu tari lebe ta bo' sɛba en tum tu na la. Fum mɛŋa ti'ihɛ ti fum dela ani?.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam dela kua sɛka en boi kpaahenɛ puan kaahera baha na yeti, Maalɛ ya Daana la sore la tuuntɔɔ, wuu nɔtɔgeha Azaaya en purɛ se'em la.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Farasiiduma la en tum nɛreba wa na.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Bala ti ba le soke Yɔn yeti, Gee beni ti fum sɔɔra nɛreba wa ko'om gee dagena Kiresta la, bii Elia, bii nɔtɔgeha.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Ti Yɔn lerege ba yeti, Mam sɔɔri mɛ la ko'om wa. Gee ya puan, nɛra ayima boi la ya ti ya ka mina en.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: se'em en wan wa'am mam pooren na la. Mam pɔ'ɛ mɛ ti n lorege a tagera.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Yele dina wa iŋɛ la Bɛtani timpaberɛ, Yɔɔdan kulaa la yakenɛ ha ti Yɔn bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua la.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Yiimbeere ti Yɔn nyɛ ti Yezu kini tɔɔra en na, ti a muulɛ yeti, Bihɛ ya,Yinɛ Pebila la n ŋwana, se'em n to'e timpaberɛ wa zuo be'em la!.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Eŋa en bala ti mam wa yɛta a yele yeti, Nɛra ayima en dole mam pooren, gee gana mam, se'ere en sɔi la, a pun bɔna mɛ na, gee bo' mam la.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Mam mɛŋa ka mi' en, gee mam wa'am na bɔ'ɔra ko'omsua wa la dela, n niiɛ en pa'alɛ Iziraleduma wa.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Ti Yɔn yele pa'ahɛ yeti, Mam nyɛ mɛ ti Fa'a Sia la ana wuu dayene la, ze'ele saazuo ha sigera na, wa yagelɛ a zuo.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mam mɛŋa la ka mi' en, gee se'em en tum mam na ti n bo' nɛreba ko'omsua la en yele mam yeti, Se'em ti fum nyɛ ti Fa'a Sia la sige na yagelɛ a zuo la, eŋa en de se'em n sɔɔri nɛreba la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Mam mɛŋa nini en nyɛ, gee ti n yɛta ya wa, ti eŋa en de Yinɛ dayua la.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Ti yiimbeere, ti Yɔn le zɛ'a la a zahereba bayi'.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: a yeti a bihɛ, ti Yezu en bala dɔla tɔla, ti a muulɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Bihɛ ya, Yinɛ pebila la en wa'am na.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: Ti zahereba bayi' wa en wum ŋwana la, ihege doohe Yezu poore.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Yezu en ŋmɛregɛ nyɛ ti ba dole a poore la, ti a soke ba yeti, Ya boti la bem? Ti ba lerege en yeti, Rabi , Fum ki'ire la bɛ.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Wa'am ya na baŋɛ mam ki'ire zi'an. Ti ba doohe en ta baŋɛ en ki'ire zi'an la, bɔna la en dabeherɛ la za'a. Ŋwana wa iŋɛ la gogo anaahi zaanuurɛ bɔba.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Ba bayi' la en wum ti Yɔn tɔgɛ, ti ba doohe Yezu la, ba ayima dela Anduru, Simɔn Pita suuɔ.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Eŋa en ta nyɛ se'em yia dela a suuɔ Simɔn, yele en yeti, Tumam nyɛ Mɛzaaya la .

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Ti a tari Simɔn wa'am Yezu nɛŋan na, ti Yezu bihɛ Simɔn zii, gee yele en yeti, Fum yu'urɛ yi'iri la Simɔn, fum dela Yɔn dayua. Fum yu'urɛ nyaa wan yi'ira la Sefase .

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Ti yiimbeere ti Yezu iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a kiŋɛ Galilii. Nyaa ta nyɛ Filipi yele en yeti, Dɔla mam.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipi yehe la Bɛtesaada, tinkãtɛ sɛka ti Anduru la Pita yehe na la.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Ti Filipi nyɛ Nataniyɛl, yele en yeti, Tumam nyɛ se'em ti Moose tɔgɛ a yele pa'alegɔ gɔŋɔ la la nɔtɔgeheba gulehɛ a yele yeti, a dela Yezu, dɛna Yosefe dayua yehe Nazareti na la.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Ti Nataniyɛl soke Filipi yeti, Bunsula boi wan ta'am yehe Nazareti na? Ti Filipi yele en yeti, Wa'ana ti fum nini nyɛ.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Yezu en nyɛ ti Nataniyɛl kini tɔɔra en na la, a yele en yeti, Fum ka nyɛ, Izirale buuri paaɛ paaɛ n ŋwana, ti ŋmɛlema ka bɔna a puan.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Ti Nataniyɛl soke Yezu yeti, Fum mi' mam la bɛha? Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Mam nyɛ fum la se'em ma'a ti fum zi'a kinkaŋa la timpaberɛ gee ti Filipi wa'am na yi' fum la.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Ti Nataniyɛl lerege en yeti, Rabi fum sirum dela Yinɛ dayua la. Fum en de Izirale naba la.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Fum nyaa nam mɛ la mam yele fum ti mam nyɛ hɔ kinkaŋa timpaberɛ la zuo? Fum wan nyɛ yelekãra en gani ŋwana.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Gee yele en yeti, N yeti fum la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti fum nini wan ta nyɛ ti saazuo ha yu'ɛ, ti Yinɛ suŋereba sigera mam Asaala Dayua wa zi'an na, gee lɛbera zɔna.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Yezu en ta baŋɛ ti Farasiiduma la wum ti, Yezu nyaŋeri nɛreba zo'e zo'e gee bɔ'ɔra ba ko'om sua gana Yɔn la.

eng: although Jesus disciples were baptizing, not Jesus himself.
local: gee la dagi Yezu a mɛŋa, gee a zahereba la en bo'ori nɛreba la ko'om sua la.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: ti a bahɛ Yudiya, gee yɛ'ɛha lɛbera Galilii.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: Gee la dela a doohe Samariya tiŋa puan.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Ti ba wa'am na paaɛ Samariya tinkãtɛ ti ba yi'iri Sika la, lɛm la samanɛ se'ere ti Yakɔbe yuum dikɛ bo' a dayua Yosefe la.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Yakɔbe bulega n boi bini, ti Yezu n kiŋɛ taregɛ la, paaɛ zi'a bulega la zi'an. La wa dɛna la wuntɛɛn-sigelika.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Ti Samariya tiŋa pɔka sige na ti ka akɛ ko'om, ti Yezu yele ka yeti, Bu' mam ko'om ti n nyu.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Ti Samariya tiŋa pɔka wa yele en yeti, Ŋwani ti fum Yuu nɛra zuhera ko'om mam Samariya pɔka wa zi'an.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti. Fum han mi' ni Yinɛ bo'olum la n de sebo, la se'em n de' en yɛta hɔ ti, Bu' mam ko'om ti n nyu wa, fum wan zuhɛ ni en, ti a bo' ni fum vomko'om.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Ti pɔka la yele Yezu yeti, N dɛɛma wa, fum ka tari gu'a, bulega wa me kɔ' mɛ ti fum wan nyɛ vom ko'om buna wa la biha?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Fum gani tumam yaaba Yakɔbe n bo' tumam bulega wa la mɛ, ti a dayɔɔhi la a duhi za'a nyuura ka puan la.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, Se'em woo n nyu ko'buna wa, ko'nyuuro wan le tara en.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: Gee sɛba woo n nyu ko'sebo ti mam wan bo' ba la, ko'nyuuro kan le malum tara bamam duma. Gee ko'sebo ti mam wan bo' ba la, wan lebege la bulega ba puan bula vom ko'om yɛ'ɛha vom en kamba'ahɛ puan.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Ti pɔka wa yele Yezu yeti, N dɛɛma wa, yi bo' mam ko'buna wa, ti ko'nyuuro da le makɛ tara mam, bii n kina ka'lam na agera ko'om.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Kiŋɛ ta yi' fum sira na.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Ti pɔka wa lerege en yeti, Mam ka tari sira. Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum tɔgɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ, la fum yeti, N ka tari sira la.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum ele la sireba banuu, ti se'em ti fum bɔna la en nananawa, pun dagena fum sira. Sɛla ti fum yele la dela asira.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Ti pɔka wa yele en yeti, Naa, mam bihɛ ti fum dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Tumam yaabeduma pu'uheri Yinɛ la zɔya wa zuto, gee yamam yeti, zi'an ti nɛreba wan pu'uha Yinɛ dela Yerusalɛm puan ma'a.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Pɔka wa, nam mam, ti saŋa kini na ti ya kan pu'uha Sɔ la zoore wa zuo, bii Yerusalɛm puan.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Yamam pu'uheri la sɛla ti ya ka mina, gee tumam pu'uheri la sɛla ti tu mina. Se'ere n sɔi la, faarɛ ze'eti la Yuuduma zi'an na.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Gee saŋa kini na, kaŋa n ŋwana, ti sɛba n de yelemɛŋerɛ pu'uhereba la, wan pu'uhera Sɔ la, Sia la yelemɛŋerɛ puan. Se'ere n sɔi la, Sɔ la eere la nɛrebana taaba ti ba pu'uha en.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Yinɛ dela Sia, ti sɛba n pu'uheri en la dela ba pu'uha en Sia la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Ti pɔka la yele Yezu yeti, Mam mi' ti Mɛzaaya la, kini na. Eŋa han wa'am na, a wan pa'alɛ tɔ sɛla woo.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Ti Yezu yele pɔka wa yeti, Mam n bala. Mam se'em en tɔgera la fum wa.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Bilam, ti a zahereba la n bala paara na, ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ la en tɔgeri la pɔka, gee ba se'em ka lakɛ nuurɛ soke Pɔka wa nyaa Beni ti fum bɔta? Bii ba soke Yezu nyaa Beni ti fum tɔgera la en.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Ti pɔka la bahɛ ka yoore la bilam, gee lebe tinkãtɛ la puan, ta muulɛ yele nɛreba yeti.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Wa'am ya na bihɛ ya, Buraa kayima n yele mam yele sɛ'a woo ti mam tum! Eii!, Ka wan dɛna Kiresta n bala, bii ka kan dɛna.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Ti nɛreba la yehe tinkãtɛ la puan, to'e sore yɛ'ɛha Yezu zi'an.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Bala ma'a la ti Yezu zahereba la beleni en mɛ yeti, Rabi, di dia fiiwa.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam tari dia mɛ wan di ti ya ka mina.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Ti a zahereba la yɛta taaba yeti, Nɛra n tari dia na bo' en ti a di bii.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam dia dela n tuna se'em n tum mam na la sumboolum, la n ba'ahɛ a tuunɛ la.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Ya ka tari yetɔgum busebo ni yeti, Ŋmarehi sinaahi n gee la tu bum dia? Gee mam yeti ya mɛ yeti, Bihɛ ya kaaɛ kaaɛ, la ya nyɛ samana la zuto dia n bi'ɛ se'em, bɔta buŋa.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Buna la pun pɔhɛ tɔ'ɔha a yɔɔrɛ mɛ, gee ka'aha dia la yɛ'ɛha vom kamba'ahɛ puan, ŋwana ti buta, la buna la wan lagum nyɛɛna la taaba.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Ya yetɔgebuna wa dela asira. Ayima n buti, ti ayima buna.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Mam tum ya ti ya kiŋɛ ta bum la zi'an ti ya ka tum. Basɛba n namehɛ, ti yamam kiŋɛ ta dita ba nyuurɔ.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Ti Samariyaduma en yehe tinkãtɛ la puan na la kekeko nam bo' Yezu, doohe la pɔka la n yele se'em yeti, A yele mam la mam tum yele sɛ'a woo la.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Bala zuo, Samariya tinkãtɛ nɛreba wa n wa'am na na la, ba belum en mɛ ti a kɛ̃ la ba. Ti a sakɛ, kɛ̃ bilam dabeha ayi'.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam en, doohe a yelehum la puan.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Gee nyaa yele pɔka la yeti, La le dagena fum n yele tu se'em la zuo ti tu nam wa, gee tu mɛhi tuba n wum, ti tu baŋɛ ti eŋa n sirum de timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a faara la.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Dabeha ayi' n tole la, ti Yezu ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ Galilii.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: En paaɛ Galilii la, Galiliihi la to'e en mɛ, doohe la ban pun nyɛ sɛla woo ti Yezu tum Yerusalɛm de'eŋo la puan la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa lagum bɔna de'eŋo la puan mɛ.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Ti Yezu le kiŋɛ Kana n boi Galilii puan, zi'an ti a tabelɛ bahɛ ti ko'om lebege daam la. Kpɛ'ɛma ayima dayua n wa bɛ̃'ɛra, bɔna Kapɛnum.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa n ta wum ti Yezu ze'ele Yudiya wa'am la Galilii na la, ti a kiŋɛ ta belum en ti a wa'am na tibɛ a dayua wa, se'ere en sɔi la, a dayua la kɔ'ɔm yɛ'ɛha la yɔɔen.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Han dagena ti ya nyɛ la daalehum la nyalema, ya kan malum nam.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Ti kpɛ'ɛma wa belum Yezu yeti, Naa, wa'am kalam na tibɛ n dayua wa gee ka ye' la kum.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Kina gee fum dayua la kan ki. Ti buraa wa nam yelehum la, Yezu n yele en se'em la, gee sige sore yɛ'ɛha kula.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: En boi sore zuo kula la, ti a yamehi zɔ tu'uhɛ en na yele en yeti, Fum bia la vo'e ya.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Ti a soke ba sansɛka puan ti bia la ban'an la bahɛ ka la, ti ba yeti, Zaam, wuntɛɛŋa gogo kaareŋa kayina wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti ka ɔɔrɔ la bahɛ en.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Bala ti buraa wa baŋɛ ti sankaŋa mɛa puan ti Yezu yele en yeti, Kina gee fum bia la kan ki la. Ti buraa wa nam Yezu, eŋa la a yire za'a.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Daalehum kana pa'ahɛ la daalehum buyi' ti Yezu tum, eŋa n ze'ele Yudiya wa'am Galilii na la.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Yele ana pooren ti Yuuduma malema de'eŋo kuyima saŋa paaɛ, ti Yezu kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Yerusalɛm puan la, nayaŋa kayima ti ba yi'ira ka piihi nayaŋa, ti bɔka gã'a ti ba yi'ira ka Heburu puan ti Bɛti-zata, tara bɔpagehi sinuu.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Zɛ'sina sinuu wa puan ti bã'duma kekeko, fɔɔhi, wɔbehi la kɔɔrenɔ ni wa'am na gã'a gura.

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Ti buraa kayima bɔna bini bɛ̃' yuuma pihitã la anii.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yezu n nyɛ en ti a gã' bini gee me mina ti a bɛ̃' yuue mɛ la, ti a soke en yeti Fum boti tibegɔ.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Ti buraa wa lebehe en yeti, Naa, Suŋera la ni han sige na damɛ ko'om la, mam ka tari se'em ni wan suŋɛ mam ti n lu ko'om la puan yia. Mam ni ta maahena la, ti ayima n pun lu bini.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Ihige kilum fum suŋɔ kiŋɛ.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Bilam bilam ti buraa la nyɛ ka tibegɔ, vaaɛ ihige saazuo, kilum ka suŋɔ tu' kina. Yele dina wa iŋɛ la vo'ohego daarɛ puan.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Bala ti Yuuduma la yele buraa wa n nyɛ ka tibegɔ wa yeti, Zina dela vo'ohego dabeherɛ, la dagi ma'a ti fum ziira suŋɔ.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Gee buraa wa lerege ba yeti, Buraa sɛka n tibɛ mam la n yele mam ti, Kilum fum suŋɔ la kina. .

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Buraa kani n bala yele fum yeti, Kilum fum suŋɔ kiŋɛ la.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Buraa wa n nyɛ ka tibegɔ wa me ka mi' Yezu n de se'em. Ti Yezu me doohe nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yehe.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Pooren ti Yezu ta nyɛ buraa wa pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, yele en yeti, Bihɛ, fum ban'an la bahɛ ya. Da le tum be'em ti la tari sɛla n gani ŋwana le wa'an fum zuo na.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Ti buraa wa kiŋɛ nyaa ta yele Yuuduma la yeti, la dela Yezu n daa tibɛ mam la.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti Yuda duma la ihige dɔla Yezu dɔlega, eŋa en tuni tuun-ana wa taaba vo'ohego dabeherɛ puan la.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam Sɔ n tuni la, mam me tuni mɛ.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yuuduma wa malum mu'ɛ la nini ɛɛra ba ko' en, beni zuo, a ka galum la vo'ohego daarɛ kiha la ma'a, gee a galum mɛ la eŋa en yi'iri Yinɛ ti a mɛŋa Sɔ, tara a mɛŋa makera la Yinɛ la.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti Dayua la kan ta'an tuna a mɛŋa tuuma, han dagena sɛla ti a nyɛ ti Sɔ la tuna la. Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la tuna la, bala mɛa ti Dayua la tuna.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Sɔ la nɔŋɛ Dayua la mɛ, gee pa'ala en sɛla woo ti eŋa n de Sɔ la tuna. A me wan pa'alɛ en yele kãra n gani yele ana wa, ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: La dela asira ti, wuu Sɔ la n ihigere kum bɔ'ɔra ba vom se'em la, bala mɛa ti Dayua la me bɔ'ɔra nɛreba vom en boti se'em.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Sɔ la mɛŋa ka gakeri nɛra, gee a dikɛ gakerɛ paŋa la za'a bo' la Dayua la.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: ti bala ti nɛra woo wan na'aha dayua la wuu ban na'ahɛ Sɔ la se'em la. Fum se'em woo n ka na'ahɛ Dayua la, ka na'ahɛ sɔ se'em n tum en na la.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa. Se'em woo n wum mam yelehum wa, gee nam se'em daana n tum mam na la, tari la vomkanba'ahɛ. Ena daana ka boi gakerɛ puan. A daana tee mɛ kum puan wa'am vom puan na.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa ti, saŋa kini na, ka pun paaɛ, ti ki'induma wan wum Yinɛ dayua wa kua, ti sɛba n wum wan vɔna.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Wuu sɔ la mɛŋa n tari vom a puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti a bo' Dayua la me sore ti a tara vom a puan.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: gee me kelum bo' Dayua wa paŋa ti a gakɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, a dela Asaala Dayua.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ la yele ana, gee saŋa la kini na ti sɛba ti ba pi' ba yɔɔrɔ puan wan wum Dayua la kua.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: ihige ba yɔɔrɔ la puan yehe na, ti sɛba n tum sum wan vo'e ihige kɛ̃ vomkanba'ahɛ puan, ti sɛba n tum be'em me vo'e ihige kɛ̃ namehegɔ puan.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Mam ka tuni tuunɛ n ma'a. Mam n wum se'em la, mam gakeri dɔla la bala mɛa. Bala zuo, mam gakerɛ la dela tuuntɔɔ se'ere n sɔi la, mam ka tuni n mɛŋa sumboolum, gee se'em n tum mam la sumboolum.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Mam han muula n mɛŋa yele, n muulegɔ la kan dɛna asira.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: Ayima n boi ha muula mam yele, ti mam me mina ti en tɔgeri n yele se'em la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Ya daa tum nɛreba mɛ Yɔn zi'an, ti Yɔn me yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: Mam yeti sɛla wa dagi la mam to'ere la asaala aze'nyɛ yetɔgum, gee mam yeti ŋwana la yamam nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Yɔn n daa bala ana wuu butolila la, dita niira, ti ya daa maahum ya suya ti ya lagum nyɛɛm a bugum la puan dabeha ayi.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Gee mam tari la yelehum n gani Yɔn. Tuunsɛ'a ti n Sɔ dikɛ bo' mam ti n tum ba'ahɛ la, tuun aŋa ti n tuna wa. Ŋwana wa pa'alɛ mɛ ti, Sɔ la n tum mam na.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Mam Sɔ se'em n tum mam na la mɛŋa pun tɔgɛ mam yele mɛ. Yamam ka tabelɛ wum a kua bii ya nyɛ a naanɛ.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: ya me ka tari a yelehum la ya puan se'ere n sɔi la, ya ka nam se'em ti a tum na la.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Ya viiheri pa'alegɔ gɔnɔ la puan la daare woo, se'ere n sɔi la ya ti'ihɛ ti vom kamba'ahɛ yɛla n boi bini, gee gɔnɔ tuna mɛa n bala tɔgera mam yele la.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Gee la bala za'a ya zagehɛ mɛ ti ya kan wa'an mam zi'an na la ya nyɛ vom la.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: Mam ka eere nɛreba na'ahegɔ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: Gee mam mi' ya ti ka tari Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ ya puan.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Han dɛna mam, mam wa'am la n Sɔ yu'urɛ puan na, gee ya ka to'e mam. Gee se'em han wa'am a mɛŋa na, ya to'ore en mɛ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ya yeti ya iŋɛ la wani nam mam. Ya han tɔ'ɔha taaba na'ahegɔ, gee ka ɛɛra la ninmu'urɛ, na'ahegɔ seko n ze'eti se'em ma'a n de Yinɛ la zi'an na la?.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Da ti'ihɛ ya ti mam n wan ta ze'ele Sɔ la nɛŋan zɛregɛ ya. Se'em n zɛregɛ ya dela Moose, se'em ti ya dikɛ ya puti'irɛ delum la.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Ya han sirum nam ni Moose, ya wan nam ni mam. Se'ere n sɔi la, Moose gulehɛ la mam yele.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Gee ya han ka nam Moose n gulehɛ sɛla la, ya yeti ya iŋɛ la ŋwani nam mam yeti ya sɛla wa.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Gee dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ, bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ, ti pɔgehi wa ihege kiŋɛ yɔɔ la zi'an, tara ba nyuuŋo suŋɔ kaam la ti ba maalɛ la.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Ba paaɛ nyɛ ti yɔkugere la bilege mɛ gã'a kinkɛleŋa ha.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Gee ban kɛ̃ la, ba ka nyɛ Daana Yezu iŋa la.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: Ti ba yari zɛ'a yɛli la yele dina wa, ba yeti ba bihɛ ti buraahi siyi en bala zɛ'a ba zi'an, ti si futo nyagera.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Ti dabeem kɛ̃ pɔgehi la ti si suure si zuto tiŋa, gee buraahi wa yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ɛɛra vɔpa ki'induma puan.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: A ka boi ka'lam a ihege mɛ.Tɛri ya en daa yele ya se'em en daa nan boi Galilii la.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: ti ba wan dikɛ Asaala Dayua la iŋɛ be'emduma nu'uhen, ti ba ku en, gee dabeha atã daarɛ, a wan vo'e.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Ti ba nyaa tiɛ Yezu tɔgebuna wa.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: ti ban ze'ele yɔɔ la puan lebe na la, ba yele a zahereba pia la ayima la mɛ la bana la za'a sɛla woo en iŋɛ.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: La dela Mɛɛri Magedalin, Yowana, Mɛɛri en de Yemesi ma la, la pɔgese'ehi woo en doohe ba na la en yele Yezu tuntuneba wa.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Gee yeledina wa ani mɛ wuu solene la bo' tuntuneba la, ti ba ka sakɛ bo' ba.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Gee Pita ihege mɛ guu zua yɛ'a yɔɔ la puan, ta paaɛ teke veehe bihɛ ku puan ha, nyɛ ti tanpeelesunɛ la gã' de ma'a, ti a lebe kula, ti la iŋɛ en pakerɛ la sɛla en iŋɛ la.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Daan dinɛ daarɛ mɛa, ti Yezu zahereba bayi' yɛ'a timbila ti ba yi'ira Emayose, en de wuu miila ayopɔi fum ze'ele Yerusalɛm yɛ'a bini.
