eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Ti ba kina gee suhera la taaba yele se'ere en iŋɛ la.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Ban tɔgera gee sɔheri la, ti Yezu en bala wa'am na paaɛ ba, naaɛ la ba kina.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: gee la pi' la ba nini ti ba da baŋɛ en.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Ti Yezu tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Yamam kini gee suhera la beni la taaba wa? Ti ba ze'ele bããlam la nimbu'ɔ.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Ti ba ayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Kelopase la lebehe en yeti, Fum ma'a en de saana Yerusalɛm wa ka mina yelesɛ'a en iŋɛ ka'lam dabeha ana wa puan.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Ti a soke ba yeti, Beni yɛla? Ti ba lerege en yeti, Yezu en yehe Nazareti na la, dɛna nɔtɔgeha, pirɛ la paŋa, la yelehum la Yinɛ la nɛreba woo nɛŋan la.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: ti malema kiinduma, la yizuto kiinduma nyɔkɛ en tihɛ ti ba gakɛ en ti la dela a ki, ti ba kpa' en la.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Gee tumam me daa biheri la eŋa ti eŋa en de se'em en wan yɔ to'e Izirale bahɛ. Yele ana woo pooren, zina dela dabeha atã, ti yele ana wa iŋɛ.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Sɛla en le pa'ahɛ dela, pɔgehi sise'ehi en pa'ahɛ tumam puan en maam tumam nɔya. Si kiŋɛ ni la yɔɔ la puan zina bulika ma'aherɛ ma'aherɛ.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: ka nyɛ a iŋa la, gee lebe na yele tumam ti bamam nyɛ la suŋereba, ti ba yele bamam yeti, a vo'e mɛ.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Ti tu puan basɛba kiŋɛ yɔɔ la puan lebe na yele tu yeti la kɔ'ɔm dɛna la pɔgehi la en yele se'em la, gee bamam ka nyɛ en.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ya gerege mɛ, ti ya bɔkerɛ me tɔna ti ya nam sɛla woo ti nɔtɔgeheba la muulɛ ti la wan iŋɛ la.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: La dagi la de sore ti Kiresta la namehɛ yele ana woo puan gee nyaa kɛ̃ a suŋa la puan.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: Gee nyaa pɔhɛ Moose, doohe nɔtɔgeheba la woo puan pa'alɛ ba eŋa yele en de se'em bɔna gulehegɔ la puan.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Ban wa'am na pɔra timbila la puan ti ba yɛ'a la, ti a kina tɔla ba nɛŋa wuu a ye' tɔla mɛ la.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: Ti ba belum en ninmu'urɛ yeti, Yee la tu ka'lam, gee la ye' la zaanuurɛ n bala, ti yinɛ me yɛ'a lua. Bala ti a yee bilam la ba.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: En ta zi'a la ba ti ba di la, ti a dikɛ boroboro purɛ sum bahɛ de zuo, gee fike dikɛ bo' ba.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Bilam ti ba nini pikɛ, ti ba nyaa baŋɛ en, ti a me mɛlum ba nɛŋan.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Bihɛ, tu paleŋa ka ɛgeri ni mɛ, en tɔgeri ni la tɔ sore la zuo la, se'em ma'a ti a lakeri ni gɔŋɔ la yelehum la pa'ali ni tu la.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Gogo kaareŋa kaŋa mɛa puan ti ba ihege lebe Yerusalɛm, ta paaɛ nyɛ zahereba pia la ayima la, ti ba lagehɛ taaba.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: Ba tɔgeri me yeti, Daana la sirum vo'e mɛ, a niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' Simɔn mɛ.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Ti ba nyaa yele ba sɛla en iŋɛ sore la zuo la, la en niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' bamam se'em boroboro figehego saŋa la.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Ban boi bini tɔgera ŋwana la, ti Yezu en bala zɛ'a la ba, gee yele ba yeti, Suma'ahum bɔna la ya.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Ti ba gɛ̃rɛ zɛ'a, ti dabeem sãɛ ba, ti ba ti'iha ti ba nyɛti la kukɔ.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwani ti dabeem sãɛ ya bala, ŋwani ti bumbu'um bɔna ya suya puan bala?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Bihɛ ya n nu'uhi la n nama, la ya baŋɛ ti la dela mam en bala. Kalum ya mam bihɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, kukɔ ka tari inganɛ la kɔba wuu yan nyɛ mam ti en tari se'em wa.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a pa'alɛ ba la a nu'uhi la a nama la.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: La ba nyaa pun nyɛɛna la, ba kelum ka sakera, gee me bɔ'ɔra. Ti a yele ba yeti, Ya tari sɛla ka'lam ti n di.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Ti ba bo' en ziho fiiwa ti ba sɛ'.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: ti a to'e bu, ze'ele ba nɛŋan obe.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Mam yelehum n ŋwana ti n daa tɔgɛ bo' ya, mam daa nan boi la ya la, yeti, Sɛla woo ti ba gulehɛ doohe mam yu'urɛ puan, Moose nɔze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba la, la yuuma puan la za'a dela la wa'am na tum.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Ti a nyaa yu'ɛ ba puti'irɛ ti ba bɔkɛ gɔnɔ la yɛla.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: Ti a le yele ba yeti, La pun gulehɛ mɛ ti, Kiresta la wan namehɛ gee vo'e a kum la puan dabeha atã.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: ti a yu'urɛ puan ti ba wan muula teere en bo'ori , la be'em sugeri dia, pɔhera Yerusalɛm, la tihi za'a puan.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Yamam en de aze'nyɛduma yele ana wa woo puan.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: Nyaa bihɛ ya, n yeti n tum la mam Sɔ la n biŋe nuurɛ ti a iŋɛ se'em la. Bala, boni ya tinkana wa puan gura ti ba ta pirɛ ya la saazuo paŋa.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa tari ba yehe ti ba kiŋɛ zaaɛ ta paaɛ Bɛtani tiŋa, ti a zɛkɛ a nu'uhi saazuo, purɛ sum bahɛ ba zuo.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: En boi bini puta sum baha ba zuo la, ba nyɛ ti a zɛkera la saazuo, nyaa zɔna zuin ha ta kɛ̃ ha.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Ti ba na'ahɛ en, gee tulege lebe Yerusalɛm na la nyɛɛmkãtɛ.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Ti ba naaɛ taaba bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la puan pɛgera Yinɛ saŋa woo.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Dabeha atã puan ti pɔgedire bɔna Kana en boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan, ti Yezu ma kiŋɛ bini.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Ba me tum yi' Yezu la a zahereba la mɛ, ti ba me bɔna pɔgedire de'eŋo la puan.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Pɔgedire daam la en ta ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu ma wa'am na waaɛ en yeti, Ba daam la ba'ahɛ mɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Pɔka wa, bala pakɛ fum la mam la ŋwani? Mam saŋa nan ka paaɛ.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Ti a ma la yele tuntuneba la yeti, Iŋɛ ya sɛla woo ti Yezu wan yele ya.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Bala ma'a la ti dugerɔ kãra tuyoobe en ze' zi'an la puan, dɛna Yuuduma malema ko'om sua dugerɔ, ti dukɔ woo ko'om wan paaɛ yɔkãra pihiyi' yɛ'ɛha pita' ko'om.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Ti Yezu bo' tuntuneba la nuurɛ yeti, Akɛya ko'om pirɛ dugerɔ wa. Ti ba akɛ ko'om pirɛ dugerɔ la ta paaɛ nuuren na.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Nyaa kabegɛ ya ko'om la ta bo' se'em en biheri de'eŋo la yɛla la. Ti ba kabegɛ ko'om la ta bo' en.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Ti se'em en biheri de'eŋo wa yɛla la en lem ko'om la en nyaa lebege daam la, a ka baŋɛ daam bumɔ en ze'ele zi'an na . Ti se'em en biheri de'eŋo wa yi' pɔgedita wa na.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: yele en yeti, Nɛra woo ni tugum tɔrɛ la daam sumɔ la yia, gee ti pooren ba nyaa dikɛ daam be'ero toŋe, bala ma'a ti saama la han nyu tigɛ, gee fum tugum dikɛ ni daam sumɔ la ze'ele mɛ, tara tara la ŋwana ma'a.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Yezu en tum dina wa. A yia daalehum n ŋwana, Kana n boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan la, niiɛ a suŋa, ti a zahereba la nam en.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Dina n tole la, ti Yezu kiŋɛ Kapɛnum, la a ma, a suurɔ, la a zahereba la, ti ba ta kɛ̃ bilam dabeha fii wa.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Yuuduma azɔɛtole malema la wa pɔra mɛ, ti Yezu kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Ti pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti Yezu nyɛ ti nɛreba tari nii, piihi la dayɛma kɔɔha, ti sɛba me zi'a ba teebula zuto tɛɛhena ligeri.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Ti Yezu pi'ihe mi'ihi maalɛ kpa'aherɛ, sa'ahɛ sɛba woo en iti ŋwana Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la bahɛ yiŋa, la ba piihi, la ba nii la. Gee ka'ahɛ lige tɛɛhereba la lige kuba la bahɛ;.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Gee yele sɛba en koohere dayɛma la yeti, Tari ya bana wa yehe ka'lam. Da dikɛ ya mam Sɔ yire wa lebege da'a.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Bilam ti a zahereba la tiɛ yeti, la gulehɛ bɔna ze'elego la puan yeti, Fum yire wa nɔŋerɛ en kuuri mam.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Ti Yuuduma la soke en yeti, Fum tari la daalehum boni ti fum pa'alɛ se'ere zuo ti fum ita ŋwana wa.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Gbi' ya Pu'uhegɔ yire wa bahɛ, gee n wan lebehe mɛ dɛ ze'ele dabeha atã puan.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Ti Yuuduma wa yele en yeti. Bihɛ, yire wa mɛa dikɛ la yuuma pihinaahi la ayoobe, ti fum yeti fum wan lebehe mɛ dɛ dabeha atã.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: Gee Yezu dikeri la a mɛŋa iŋa, makera la Pu'uhegɔ yire la.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: En nyaa wa ihege a kum la puan la, ti a zahereba la tiɛ ti a tabelɛ tɔgɛ tugum wa, ti ba malum nam gulehegɔ la, la yelehum la ti Yezu tɔgɛ bo' ba la.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Yezu en daa ta bɔna Yerusalɛm azɔɛtole de'eŋo saŋa la, nɛreba zo'e zo'e en daa nam bo' a yu'urɛ la, doohe la daalehum sɛ'a ti a tuna la.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Gee Yezu daa ka dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' ba, se'ere en sɔi la, a mi' asaala woo en ani se'em mɛ.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: dagi se'em en wan yele en se'em yele. A mi' sɛla en boi nɛra woo puan mɛ.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Buraa kayima n boi Farasii lageŋɔ la puan, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Nikodemose, dɛna Yuuduma nɛŋadaana ayima.

eng: He came to Jesus at night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could do these miraculous signs that you do unless God is with him.
local: Ti a wa'am Yezu zi'an yu'uŋɔ kuyima na, soke en yeti, Rabi, tu mi' ti fum dela pa'ala en ze'ele Yinɛ mɛŋa zi'an na. Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛra ka boi wan ta'am tum daalehum sɛ'a ti fum tuna wa han dagena Yinɛ n boi la a daana.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, nɛra ka boi wan bɔna Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan han dagena ba dɔgɛ en la saazuo na.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Ti Nikodemose yele en yeti, Ŋwani ti ba wan ta'am le malum dɔgɛ nɛra a bunkutɛ puan? Nɛra wan le ta'am kɛ̃ a ma puuren ti ba le dɔgɛ en buyi' puan.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta hɔ wa, ti nɛra ka boi wan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan, han dagena ba malum dɔgɛ en mɛ doohe ko'om, la Sia la puan.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Sɛla ti inganɛ dɔgɛ dela inganɛ, ti sɛla ti Sia dɔgɛ, dela Sia.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Bala, da bahɛ ti la iŋɛ fum pakerɛ la mam yele fum yeti, Fu dela ba malum dɔgɛ fum saazuo dɔgum la.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Sabeeo lobere yɛ'ɛha la kuŋɔ en boti zi'an. Fum yeen wuna la ku dameŋa, gee fum ka mi' kuŋɔ en ze'ele zi'an na, bii kuŋɔ n ye'ehe zi'an. La ani la bala bo' se'em woo ti Fa'a Sia dɔgɛ en.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Ti Nikodemose yele en yeti, Yele ana yeti a iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am tum.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Aa, Fum dagi la Iziraleduma pa'ala, gee ka bɔkera yele ana.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, tumam yeti la sɛla ti tu mina, gee tɔgera sɛla ti tu nini nyɛ, gee ya ka sakɛ bo' tɔ.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: Mam han yele fum timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla ti fum ka nam, fum nyaa yeti fum iŋɛ la ŋwani nam, n han yele fum saazuo yɛla?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Nɛra ka tabelɛ zom saazuo wa ha, han dagena se'em daana en ze'ele de zuo sige na la, dɛna Asaala Dayua la.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Wuu Moose n wa zɛkɛ bunsɛla la muɔ la puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti ba wan ta zɛkɛ Asaala Dayua wa saazuo.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: Ti se'em woo en nam en, wan tara vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Yinɛ nɔŋɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ paaɛ paaɛ zuo, ti a dikɛ a dayua kayima wa bo' na, ti se'em woo n nam en, kan bahɛ yoo, gee a wan tara vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ ka tum a dayua wa ti ka wa'am na gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee timpaberɛ wa tugum nyɛ faarɛ doohe a dayua wa puan.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Sɛba en nam en kan nyɛ sageŋɔ, gee sɛba en ka nam en la, pun diŋɛ bɔna la sageŋɔ puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka nam Yinɛ dayua kayima wa yu'urɛ la.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Gakerɛ en de se'em en ŋwana. Niihum wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na, gee nɛreba nɔŋɛ la lika gana niihum la, beni zuo, ba tuni la be'em.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Sɛba woo n tuni be'em yaaɛ niihum mɛ, kan wa'am bu puan na, ti ba be'em la da niiɛ na.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Gee sɛba n tuni yelemɛŋerɛ kini niihum la puan me na, ti ba tuuma la niiɛ na, pa'alɛ ti ba tuni ba tuuma dɔla la Yinɛ n boti se'em.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Dina pooren ti Yezu la a zahereba kiŋɛ Yudiya kinkɛleŋa ha, ti eŋa la bamam ta kɛ̃ bilam dabeha fii wa, bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Bala ma'a ti Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la boi la Ɛnɔn n lɛm Salim la, bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua, se'ere en sɔi la, ko'kãra n boi zɛ'sina wa puan, ti nɛreba kina a zi'an na tɔ'ɔha ko'om sua.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Bala ma'a ti ba nan ka pagɛ Yɔn Ko'sɔɔra wa.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Malema peere nɔkpe'ene n daa wa lu Yɔn zahereba la, la Yuuduma nɛra tiŋasuka.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Yɔn zahereba basɛba n wa wa'am Yɔn zi'an na yele en yeti, Rabi, se'em n daa wa'am fum zi'an na Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha la, se'em ti fum daa tɔgɛ a yele la n bala ŋmɛregɛ bɔ'ɔra nɛreba ko'om sua, ti nɛreba la za'a nyaa bibigera a zi'an la.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Ti Yɔn lerege ba yeti, Se'em ka boi wan to'e sɛla, han dagena saazuo n dikɛ bo' a daana.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Yamam mɛhi en de mam aze'nyɛduma, ti n yɛta ti Dagi mam en de Kiresta la, gee Yinɛ tum mam mɛ na, ti n diŋɛ a nɛŋa na.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Pɔgedita la en sɔ'e pɔka la, gee a zɔ la n zi' la en la, puurɛ pee mɛ la en wuni pɔgedita la kua la. La ani mam la bala, ŋwana zuo ti mam puurɛ ba' pee paaɛ paaɛ la.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Eŋa wan malum pa'ahera, gee ti mam sigera.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Se'em n ze'ele saazuo na la, en gani za'a. Se'em n ze'ele timpaberɛ wa zuo na, dela timpaberɛ wa zuo bunɔ, tɔgera la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla. Se'em n ze'ele saazuo na la n gani za'a.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Eŋa tɔgeri la sɛla ti a nyɛ, gee wum, gee nɛreba kelum ka nam bo' a yelehum la.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Gee se'em woo n nam bo' a yelehum la nam la yele dina wa, ti Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Se'em daana ti Yinɛ tum la, tɔgeri la Yinɛ nuurɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ba' pirɛ en mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Sɔ la nɔŋɛ dayua la mɛ, gee dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Se'em woo n nam dayua la tari la vom kamba'ahɛ. Se'em me n zagehɛ dayua la ka tari vom, gee Yinɛ sunsunyiim n boi a daana zuo.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Ŋwana en tole la, ti Yezu yakɛ Galilii tiŋa ko'kãtɛ la yakenɛ deyima la ti ba yi'ira Tiberiyasi la ha.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ bibigera dɔla en se'ere zuo, ba nyɛ daalehum sɛ'a ti a tuna bɔ'ɔra bã'duma la.

eng: Jesus went up a mountain and sat there with his disciples.
local: Ti Yezu zom zoore zuo, ta zi'an bilam la a zahereba la.

eng: It was nearly time for Passover, the Jewish festival.
local: Azɔɛtole la en de Yuuduma malema de'eŋo la pɔri mɛ.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Ti Yezu n gorege zuo nyɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ kini tɔɔra en na la, ti a soke Filipi yeti, Bɛha ti tu wan nyɛ dia da' bo' nɛreba wa ti ba di.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: A soke ŋwana ti a makɛ en bihɛ mɛ, gee a mɛŋa mi' en yeti a iŋɛ se'em.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Ti Filipi lerege en yeti, Ŋmarehi siyoobe yɔɔrɛ pun kan ta'am da' dia bo' nɛrebɔbegɔ wa ti nɛra woo yeen paaɛ fiifiihe.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Ti a zahereba la ayima, Simɔn Pita suuɔ la Anduru, yele Yezu yeti.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Bumbilepaalega boi ka'lam ziira boroboro banuu la zim iyi'. Gee bana yeti ba iŋɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ wa la wani.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti nɛreba la zi'ire tiŋa. Muɔ me n zo'e zi'an la puan, ti nɛreba la zi'ire, nɛreba n en paaɛ wuu tuha anuu la zi'ire tiŋa.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Ti Yezu to'e boroboro la, bo' Yinɛ fara ba'ahɛ, gee bahɛ ti ba tɔrɛ bo' nɛreba la n zi' la. Gee to'e zim la iŋɛ bala mɛa, ti nɛra woo to'e paaɛ en boti se'em.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Nɛreba la n di tigɛ la, ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, Doohe ya va ban tigɛ gee bahɛ sɛla woo la za'a, ti sɛla da gee.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Bala ti ba doohe lagehɛ sɛla woo, zɔra la ti n yee boroboro anuu la ti nɛreba la obe tigɛ gee bahɛ la puan la, pirɛ pitɔ pia la tuyi'.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Nɛreba la n nyɛ daalehum kana wa ti Yezu tum la, ti ba pi'ilum yɛta yeti, Ena n sirum de nɔtɔgeha se'em n wan wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na la.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yezu n baŋɛ ti nɛreba la maahum mɛ ti ba wa'am na pɛregɛ bobe en na'am la, a bahɛ ba mɛ gee kiŋɛ zom zoore la zuo ha a ma'a.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: Yinɛ n ta lu la, ti a zahereba la kiŋɛ paaɛ ateko la nɔgbene.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: paaɛ zom uureŋɔ ti ba yakɛ Kapɛnum tiŋa. Bala ma'a ti la nyuulum mɛ, gee ti Yezu nan ka lebe ba zi'an na.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: Ti komi'ihe pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra, se'ere n sɔi la, sabeeo kiŋɔ n lobere ko'om la zuo.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Bama n mɔm uureŋɔ la kiŋɛ wuu miila atã anaahi la, ba nyɛ ti Yezu yagɛ la ko'om la zuo kina paara uureŋɔ la na, ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Gee a yele ba yeti, La dela mam. Da zoti ya dabeem.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Ti ban yeti ba ze'ele uureŋɔ la ti a zom la, ba bihɛ ti ba pun bɔna la doone, zɛ'sɛka ti ba ye' ni la.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Yiimbeere, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n zaam boi ateko la yakenɛ deyima ha la, tiɛ ti uureŋɔ zaam ka boi gee gee la uureŋɔ kuyina ma'a n zaam boi . Ba me zaam nyɛ ti Yezu zaam ka doohe a zahereba la uureŋɔ la, gee a zahereba la ma'a n zaam kiŋɛ.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Ti uurenɔ tuseto ze'ele Tiberiyasi tiŋa wa'am na paaɛ zi'an ti Daana la zaam bo' fara boroboro la zuo gee ti nɛreba la zaam obe la.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Gee nɛrekuuŋɔ la n nyɛ ti Yezu, bii a zahereba la ka boi zi'an la puan la, ti ba zom uurenɔ la mɛhi kiŋɛ Kapɛnum tiŋa ta ɛɛra en.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Ban ta nyɛ en ateko la yakenɛ deyima la ha la, ba yele en yeti, Rabi, wani ma'a ti fum wa'an ka'lam na.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, yan eere mam wa dagi la daalehum la ti ya nyɛ la, gee yan zaam obe boroboro la tigɛ la zuo.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Da tuni ya ɛɛra disɛka n wan ba'ahɛ, gee tuni ya ɛɛra disɛka n wan bɔna wuuha, bo' ya vom kamba'ahɛ, sebo ti mam Asaala Dayua wa wan bo' ya la. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ma'a ti Yinɛ n de Sɔ la daalehum bɔna a puan.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Beni en de tu iŋɛ, ta'am ita sɛla ti Yinɛ tuuma.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yinɛ en boti ya tuna se'ere dela, ya nam mam se'em ti a tum na wa.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Beni daalehum ti fum wan tum, ti tu nyɛ gee nam bo' fum. Beni tuunɛ ti fum wan tum.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Tumam yaabeduma yuum di la maana muɔ puan la ha. Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, A bo' ba la boroboro n ze'ele saazuo na ti ba di la. .

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, Moose n yuum bo' ya boroboro sɛka la dagi saazuo boroboro, gee la dela mam Sɔ n bo'ori ya boroboro se'ere n sirum ze'ele saazuo na.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ boroboro dela boroboro n ze'ele saazuo sige na, bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa za'a vom.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Ti ba lebehe en yeti, Naa, yi bɔ'ɔra tɔ boroboro kana wa saŋa woo.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam n de vom boroboro la. Se'em woo n wa'am mam zi'an na, kan malum nyɛ kom. Se'em woo n nam mam, ko'yuuro kan malum tara en.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Gee mam pun yele ya yeti, Ya nyɛ mam mɛ, gee la bala za'a ya ka nam bo' mam.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la dikɛ bo' mam, wan wa'am mam zi'an na, ti se'em woo me n wa'an mam zi'an na, mam kan makɛ dige en bahɛ.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Se'ere n en sɔi la, mam ze'ele saazuo sige na dagi la n tuna n mɛŋa sumboolum, gee se'em daana n tum mam na la sumboolum.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Ti dina wa n de se'em daana n tum mam na la sumboolum yelemɛŋerɛ. Ti n da bɔregɛ se'em, sɛba woo ti a dikɛ bo' mam la puan bahɛ, gee n ihige ba ba'ahegɔ daarɛ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Dina wa n de mam Sɔ la suboolum yelemɛŋerɛ, ti sɛba woo n nyɛ Dayua la gee nam bo' ka la, wan tara vomkanba'ahɛ, ti n ta vo'e ba ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: Ti Yuuduma la pɔhɛ nyɔna en la en yeti, Mam n de boroboro n ze'ele saazuo sige na la.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Ba nyɔni yeti, Dagi ena n de Yezu la,Yosefe dayua la, se'em sɔ la a ma ti tu mina wa? Ti la iŋɛ la wani ti a ŋmɛregɛ yɛta ti, Mam ze'ele la saazuo sige na.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Da gugeleni ya la taaba.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Nɛra kan ta'am wa'am mam zi'an na, han dagena Sɔ la n tum mam na la mɛŋa n ve'e en na. Ti n me wan vo'e nɛre eŋa daana ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: La gulehɛ bɔna nɔtɔgeheba gɔŋɔ puan yeti, Yinɛ mɛŋa wan pa'alɛ ba za'a. Se'em woo n wum Sɔ la kua, gee zamehɛ Sɔ la zi'an n ye' mam zi'an na.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Dagi ti se'em n tabelɛ nyɛ Sɔ la, han dagena se'em n ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na la. Eŋa daana n nyɛ sɔ la.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti se'em woo n nam tari la vomkanba'ahɛ.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Mam n de vom boroboro la.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Ya yaabeduma yuum di maana dia la mɛ muɔ la puan ha, gee ba za'a ki ya.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Boroboro en ze'ele saazuo sige na n ŋwana, ti se'em woo n obe de, kan ki.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Mam n de vom boroboro la, ze'ele saazuo sige na la. Se'em woo en obe boroboro dina wa wan vɔna wuuha. Boroboro se'ere ti mam yeti n dikɛ bo' timpaberɛ wa ti ba tara vom la dela mam inganɛ wa.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Ŋwana ti Yuuduma la pɔhɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene tuulɛ la ba mɛhi yeti, Ŋwani ti buraa kana wa wan dikɛ ka inganɛ bo' tumam ti tu obe.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta wa, ti han dagena ya obe la mam asaala dayua wa inganɛ, gee nyu mam ziim, ya kan tara vom ya puan.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Se'em en obe mam inganɛ, gee nyu mam ziim la n wan tari vomkanba'ahɛ, ti n ihige en ba'ahegɔ daarɛ.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Mam inganɛ wa dela dia yelemɛŋerɛ, ti mam ziim wa dɛna sɛla en nyuuri yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Se'em en obe mam iŋa wa, gee nyu mam ziim la boi la mam puan, ti mam me bɔna la en.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Wuu Sɔ la n de vom daana tum mam na ti mam vɔna Sɔ la zuo la, bala mɛa ti se'em woo n obe mam wan vɔna mam zuo.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Boroboro dina n ze'ele saazuo sige na, de ka ani wuu ya yaabeduma n yuum di boroboro gee kelum ki la. Gee fum se'em en boroboro dina wan vɔna wuuha.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: A tɔgɛ yetɔgebuna wa la en daa boi Kapɛnum tiŋa Zamehegɔ yire la puan pa'ala la.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yezu zahereba la kekeko n wum ti Yezu tɔgɛ yele ana la, ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Pa'alegɔ kuna ba' kpe'em mɛ. Ani n wan to'e ku.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Dina pooren, ti Yezu nyaa ɛɛra Galilii tiŋa puan. A ka boti a ɛɛra Yudiya tiŋa puan, se'ere n sɔi la Yuuduma la mu'ɛ la nini ɛɛra yɔ'ɔ ti ba ku en.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Gee Yezu en daa pun mina a mɛŋa puan ti a zahereba la gugeleni mɛ la, ti a yele ba yeti, Yetɔgebuna daam yamam me mɛ?.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Gee Yuuduma sugerɔ ti'an de'eŋo la wa pɔra mɛ.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Gee ya nyaa han ta nyɛ ti asaala dayua wa lebe zɔna zi'an ti a daa pun bɔna la ya wan yeti bu?.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Ti Yezu suurɔ la yele en yeti, Bahɛ ka'lam gee kiŋɛ Yudiya, ti fum zahereba wa me ta nyɛ tuunsɛ'a ti fum tuna wa.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: La dela Fa'a Sia la n bo'ori vom, gee inganɛ la ka bo'ori sɛla sɛla. Yelehum wa ti mam tɔgɛ bo' ya wa dela Sia, gee dɛna vom.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, se'em ka boi bɔta ti a yu'urɛ yehe yiŋa gee sugera a tuuma. Fum han ita yele an, dikɛ fum mɛŋa pa'alɛ timpaberɛ wa.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Gee sɛba boi ya puan ka nam bɔ. Se'ere n sɔi la, pɔhega na la, Yezu pun mina ba puan sɛba n wan nam bo' en, la se'em n de en wan ta koohe en mɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam saŋa paaɛ paaɛ la nan ka paaɛ na, gee yamam saŋa kɔ'ɔm bɔna mɛ saŋa woo.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Bala ti a yele ba yeti, Ŋwana zuo ti mam yele ya yeti, se'em kan ta'am wa'am mam zi'an na, han dagena mam Sɔ n bo' sore.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Tiŋa wa zuo ka yaaɛ yamam, gee ba yaaɛ mam mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la mam kɔ'ɔm dɛna la aze'nyɛ daana ti ba tuuma dela be'em.

eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: Yetɔge buna wa zuo, Yezu zahereba kekeko lebe la pooren ka le dɔla en.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Yamam mɛhi kiŋɛ de'eŋo la, gee mam, ka ye' bini, se'ere n sɔi la, mam saŋa paaɛ paaɛ la nan ka paaɛ za'a na.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Ti Yezu soke a zahereba pia la bayi' la yeti, Yamam me boti ya kiŋɛ mɛ bii.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: En tɔgɛ ŋwana bo' ba ba'ahɛ la, a yee la Galilii tiŋa dabeha fiiwa.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita lerege en yeti, Daana, to yeti tu kiŋɛ ta paaɛ la ani? Fum n tari vomkanba'ahɛ yelehum la.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: Yezu suurɔ la n tole de'eŋo la puan ba'ahɛ la, ti la iŋɛ dabeha fiiwa, ti eŋa me kiŋɛ bini bããlam, kɔ'ɔm ka niiɛ a mɛŋa bala, gee lan de se'em la dela a sugeri mɛ.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Tumam nam mɛ, gee mina ti fum en de FA'A Daana ze'ele Yinɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Yuuduma la ɛɛra en mɛ de'eŋo la puan, sɔkera taaba yeti, A boi la bɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Dagi mam mɛŋa n looe yamam pia la bayi wa? Gee la bala za'a, ya ayima dela bumbɛ'a.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Ti gugeleŋɔ bɔna nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan Yezu yele. Ti sɛba yɛta ti, A dela nɛresuŋɔ, ti sɛba me yɛta ti, Aayi, a pã'ahɛ la nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: Yezu tɔgeri la Simɔn Isikariyɔte dayua Yudasi yele n bala. Eŋa n pun dɛna pia la bayi' la puan la, gee a wan koohe Yezu.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Gee la bala za'a se'em ka boi tɔgera vɔɔla, ban zoti Yuuduma la zuo.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: De'eŋo la tiŋampuuŋan, ti Yezu yehe kiŋɛ kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ta pɔhɛ pa'ala.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Ti la iŋɛ Yuuduma la pakerɛ, ti ba yɛta yeti, Buraa wa nyɛ mi'ilum buna la bɛ, gee ti ba ka makɛ zamehɛ en.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam pa'alegɔ wa dagi mam mɛŋa pa'alegɔ, gee se'em n tum mam na la.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Se'em woo n maahum ti a tum Yinɛ sumboolum, wan baŋɛ nyaa pa'alegɔ kuna wa ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na bii, mam tɔgeri la n mɛŋa puti'irɛ.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Sɛba n tɔgeri ba mɛhi puti'ira puan la eere la ba mɛhi na'ahegɔ, gee se'em n eere se'em daana n tum en na la na'ahegɔ, dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, ti sɛla en de ŋmɛlema ka bɔna a puan.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: Dagi Moose n bo' ya pa'alegɔ la? Gee la bala za'a, ya se'em ka dole pa'alegɔ la. Beni zuo ti ya ɛɛra ya ku mam.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lebehe en yeti, Fum tari la kulekpa'arega! Ani n eere ti a ku hɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Ti Yezu le lerege ba yeti, Mam tum la daalehum buyina ma'a, ti la iŋɛ ya za'a pakerɛ.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Moose bo' ya la buraanɛ ŋmaa ze'elego. , ti la dagena kihere ti ya ŋmata ya buraahi vo'ohego dabeherɛ la.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Ba han ŋma buraa vo'ohego dabeherɛ ti la da galum Moose pa'alegɔ la, wani ti yamam iŋɛ suure la mam ti n tibɛ nɛra iŋa za'a vo'ohego daarɛ la?.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Da yeen gakeri ya sɛla ti ya nyɛ, gee gakeri ya la tuntɔɔnɛ.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Ti Yerusalɛm nɛreba basɛba yɛta yeti, Dagi buraa kana wa n bala ti ba ɛɛra ti ba ku ka la?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Gee ti a zɛ'a ka'lam tɔgera fai ti se'em ka lakera nuurɛ? La dela nɛheduma wa za'a sirum baŋɛ mɛ ti eŋa n de Kiresta la?.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Gee la bala za'a, tu me yi mi' buraa kana wa en yehe zi'an me na. Gee Kiresta la han ta wa'am na, se'em kan mina en ze'ele zi'an na.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Ti Yezu kaahɛ bahɛ na en boi bini pa'ala pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la yeti, Yamam mi' mam, la mam yehe zi'an na? Mam ka wa'an la n mɛŋa na. Gee se'em n tum mam na la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, ya me ka mi' en.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Mam en mi' en, se'ere n sɔi la, mam ze'ele la a zi'an na. Eŋa n tum mam na.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Ti ba ɛɛra ti ba nyɔkɛ en, gee se'em ka dikɛ nu'o kalum en, se'ere n sɔi la a saŋa la ka paaɛ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Gee la bala za'a, nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan nam en mɛ, gee yɛta yeti, Kiresta la han pun wan wa'am na, a wan tum la daalehum boni le gaŋɛ buraa kana wa n tum se'em wa.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Ti Farasii duma la wum ti nɛreba la gugeleni yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra Yezu la, ti malema kiinduma la, la Farasii duma la tum pu'uhegɔ yire gureba basɛba, ti ba kiŋɛ ta nyɔkɛ Yezu.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa yeti, Mam wan bɔna la ya mɛ dabeha fiiwa, gee lebe se'em n tum mam na la zi'an.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Ya wan ta ɛɛra mam, gee ya kan nyɛ mam. Mam me n ta bɔna zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am na.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Ti Yuuduma la sɔkera taaba yeti, Bɛha ti buraa wa maaheni ti ka kiŋɛ, ti tumam kan nyɛ en? A maaheni ti a kiŋɛ la tu nɛreba sɛba n kɛ'eri Giriki tihi puan ha la zi'an ta pa'ala ba bii?.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Eŋa n yeti, Ya wan ta ɛɛra mam, gee ya kan nyɛ mam, la Mam n ta bɔna zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am bini na la vuurɛ dela dini.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: De'eŋo la ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la, dabehekãtɛ daarɛ la, ti Yezu boi bini, muulɛ la kukɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Fum se'em woo ti ko'yuuro tara hɔ, wa'am n zi'an na.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Fum se'em woo n nam mam, wa'am na nyu wuu lan gulehɛ se'em gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, Se'em woo n nam, vom ko'om kulaahi wan tahɛ a suure puan bula na la. .

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yezu n yeti ŋwana la dela, Fa'a Sia la ti sɛba n nam en la wan to'e la. Saŋa kana wa puan la, ti Fa'a Sia la nan ka wa'am na se'ere n sɔi la, Yezu nan ka to'e a suŋa.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Nɛreba la n wum yele ana wa la, nɛreba basɛba nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan yeti, Ena sirum dela nɔtɔgeha la.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Gee basɛba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, Kiresta la n bala. Ti basɛba me soke yeti, Kiresta la kan ze'ele Galilii na, bii a wan yehe la bilam na?.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Gulehegɔ la puan la ka yeti, Kiresta la yeti a dɛna la Deviti yaaŋa, yehe Bɛtelehɛm tiŋa, ze'ele zi'an ti Deviti mɛŋa kɛ̃'ɛra.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Ti welegere sige nɛrekuuŋɔ la tiŋasuka, Yezu zuo.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: Ti basɛba bɔta ti ba nyɔkɛ en, gee se'em ka boi dikɛ nu'o kalum en.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Ti pu'uhegɔ yire gureba la lebe malema kiinduma la, la Farasii duma la zi'an, ti ba soke ba yeti, Ŋwani ti ya ka nyɔkɛ en.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Ti gureba la lerege ba yeti, Wai, Nɛra ka tabelɛ tɔgɛ wuu ŋwana.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Ti Farasii duma la lebehe ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ tumam ka sakɛ ti a pa'ahɛ yamam me mɛ, bii a pa'ahɛ yamam me mɛ?.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Se'em boi kpɛ'ɛnduma bii Farasii duma wa puan nam bo' en?.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Gee nɛrekuuŋɔ wa, sɛba n ka mi' ze'elego la. Yinɛ nɔpɔrɛ en dole ba.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Ti Nikodemose se'em n tabelɛ kiŋɛ Yezu zi'an la, gee me bɔna ba puan la, soke ba yeti.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Tu tiŋa wa ze'elego puan, tu ka gakeri nɛreba, gee ka wuna ba nuurɛ yetɔgum yia, la tu baŋɛ ban iti sɛla. Bii la dagi bala.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ fum me ka yehe Galilii na, bii fum yehe la bilam na? Ɛ' gɔnɔ la puan gee fum wan baŋɛ ti nɔtɔgeha ka boi wan yehe Galilii tiŋa na.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Ti ba za'a yiregɛ kule Ba yiya.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Gee ti Yezu zom Olive zoore la zuo ha.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Ti yiimbeere bulika, ti a le wa'am Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan na. Ti nɛreba la za'a wa'am a zi'an na, ti a zi'ire pɔhɛ ba pa'alegɔ.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Ti gɔnmi'ineba la, la Farasii duma la tari pɔka ti ba nyɔkɛ ka kabohi puan na, bahɛ ka ti ka zɛ'a ba woo nɛŋan.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: ti ba nyaa yele Yezu yeti, Pa'ala, pɔka wa kɔ'ɔm bɔna bini mɛ tuna kabohi tuuma ti tu nyɔkɛ ka.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Pa'alegɔ la puan, Moose bo' tu nuurɛ ti tu lobe pɔge kaŋa taaba la kuga. Gee fum yeti bu.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Ba yele ŋwana ti ba makɛ Yezu mɛ, la ba ta'am dikɛ yele pagelɛ a zuo. Ti Yezu suure tiŋa tara a nu'dibila buura tiŋa.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Ti ban sini gee zɛ'a sɔkera en la, ti a dooe zi'ire, yele ba yeti, Bahɛ ya ti se'em n boi ya puan ka tabelɛ tum be'em, diŋɛ yia lobe pɔka wa.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Gee le suure le bɔ'ɔra tiŋa.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Ban wum lebehego kuna wa la, ba foohe mɛ ayima ayima, pɔhɛ kiinduma la, ta gee Yezu ma'a, ti pɔka la zɛ'a a nɛŋan.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Ti Yezu le dooe zi'ire, yele pɔka la yeti, Pɔka wa, ba bɛɛ? Ba se'em ka gakɛ hɔ.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Ti pɔka wa yeti, Naa, aayi, ba se'em se'em. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam me kan gakɛ hɔ ti fum tuue ya. Kina, gee yehe nananawa yɛ'a ha, da le tum be'em.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Ti Yezu le tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, Mam n de niihum bo' timpaberɛ wa. Se'em woo n dole mam kan malum kina lika puan, gee a wan tara vom niihum la.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Ti Farasii duma la yele en yeti, Fum nyaa tɔgeri la fum mɛŋa yele. Fum yetɔgum la woo ka le tara vuurɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam han pun nyaa tɔgera la n mɛŋa yele, mam yelehum la kelum dɛna la yelemɛŋerɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, mam mi' mam n ze'ele zi'an na, la mam n ye' zi'an. Gee yamam ka mi' mam n ze'ele zi'an na, bii mam n ye' zi'an.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Yamam gakeri la nɛresaalenɛ, gee mam ka gakeri se'em.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Gee la bala za'a, mam han pun gakera, mam gakerɛ la ani suŋa mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, dagi mam ma'a n gakeri, gee mam la n Sɔ se'em n tum mam na la.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ya ze'elego la puan, la gulehɛ yeti, Aze'nyɛ yelemɛŋerɛ duma ba yi' han tɔgɛ, la ba'ahɛ ya.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Mam tɔgɛ la n mɛŋa yele, ti Sɔ se'em n tum mam na la, me tɔgɛ n yele.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Fum Sɔ wa bɛɛ? Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Ya pun ka mina mam, benti mam Sɔ la. Ya han mini mam, ya wan mini mam Sɔ la me.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: A tɔgɛ tɔgebuna wa la se'em ma'a ti a bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, lɔgerɔ biŋere bɔ'ɔ la puan pa'ala nɛreba la. Ti se'em ka nyɔkɛ en, se'ere n sɔi la, a saŋa la ka paaɛ.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ti Yezu le yele ba yeti, Mam ye' mɛ, gee ya wan leen ɛɛra mam, gee ya wan ki ya be'em la puan. Mam n ye' zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am bini na.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Ti Yuuduma la yɛta ba mɛhi yeti, A yeti a ku la a mɛŋa bii? De vuurɛ n bala ti a yɛta yeti, Mam n ye' zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am bini na la bii.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yamam yehe la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, gee mam yehe la saazuo wa ha. Yamam dela timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, gee mam dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Mam yele ya ti ya wan ki ya be'em la puan. Ya sirum wan ki ya be'em la puan han dagena ya nam mɛ bo' mam ti mam dela mam n de se'em la.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Fum dela ani? Ti Yezu lebehe ba yeti, Beni ti n pun le tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya wa?.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: Gee mam tari ya yɛla zo'e zo'e wan yele ni ya, gee gakɛ ya. Gee Se'em daana n tum mam na la dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa la mam wum sɛla a zi'an la.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Ti ba ka bɔkɛ ti Yezu tɔgeri la a Sɔ la yele bɔ'ɔra ba'.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Ŋwana ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya han ta zɛkɛ Asaala Dayua wa saazuo ba'ahɛ, ya wan baŋɛ ti mam n bala, gee me baŋɛ ti mam ka iti sɛla n ma'a, gee mam tɔgeri yele ana wa dɔla la Sɔ la n pa'ali mam se'em.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Se'em daana n tum mam na la, boi mɛ la mam. A ka bahɛ mam n ma'a, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tuni pɛɛra la a puurɛ paaɛ paaɛ saŋa woo.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: En yeti yele ana wa woo la, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam bo' en.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Ti Yezu yele Yuuduma sɛba n nam bo' en la yeti, Ya han gurɛ la mam yelehum wa, ya sirum dela mam zahereba.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Ya wan mina yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti yelemɛŋerɛ la bahɛ ti ya bɔna fai.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Ti ba lebehe yele en yeti, Tumam dela Aberaham yaahi, tu ka tabelɛ dɛna se'em da'aba. De vuurɛ dela deni ti fum yeti, Tu wan bɔna fai.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Se'em woo n tum be'em dela be'em da'aberɛ.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Da'aberɛ ka boi wuuha yire la puan. Gee dayua la, kɔ'ɔm bɔna yire la puan mɛ wuuha.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Bala zuo, dayua la han faaɛ fum bahɛ, fum yeti fum sirum bɔna la fai.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: Mam mi' ti ya dela Aberaham yaahi, gee la bala za'a, ya eere la fole ti ya ku mam, se'ere n sɔi la ya suya ka boti mam yelehum la.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Mam yeti ya la sɛla ti mam bɔna Sɔ la zi'an gee nyɛ, gee han dɛna yamam, yamam iti la sɛla ti ya wum ya sɔ la zi'an.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Ti ba lerege Yezu yeti, Aberaham n de tumam sɔ Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Ya han deni Aberaham kɔma, ya wan iti ni Aberaham itegɔ.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: gee nananawa, ya zigera mɛ ti ya ku mam, mam se'em n yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere ti n wum Yinɛ zi'an. Dagi bala ti Aberaham iŋɛ.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Yezu n kini la, a nyɛ la buraa n de fua ka biilimen na.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Yamam kɔ'ɔm sirum ita la tuunsɛ'a ti ya sɔ la ita la. Ti ba yele en yeti, Tumam dagi kɔm-yooro, Tumam sɔ dela ayima, Yinɛ wa.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Ti a zahereba la soke en yeti, Rabi, ani n tum be'em? Buraa wa bii ka dɔgereba be'em zuo ti ba dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna fua wa.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yinɛ han sirum dɛna yamam Sɔ, ya wan nɔŋɛ ni mam, se'ere n sɔi la, mam ze'ele la a zi'an na bɔna ka'lam wa. Dagi mam mɛŋa n wa'am na, eŋa n tum mam na.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, La dagi buraa wa, bii ka dɔgereba be'em zuo, gee ka de fua ti Yinɛ tuuma n niiɛ ka puan na.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Wani ti ya ka bɔkera mam yeti se'em wa? La dela, ya ka maahum ti ya nam mam yelehum wa zuo.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: La dela tu tuna n Sɔ la n tum mam na la tuuma, ti wuntɛɛŋa nan boi wa, gee yu'uŋɔ kini na, ti nɛra kan ta'am tum.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Ya kɔ'ɔm ze'ele la ya sɔ Sagena la zi'an na, gee me looe ti ya tum ya sɔ tuunfi'iha sumboolum la. A dela nɛre kuura pi'ileŋo puan na, ka bɔta yelemɛŋerɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, yelemɛŋerɛ ka boi a puan. A han parum, a tɔgeri dɔla la a naanɛ n bala, se'ere n sɔi la, a dela pumparema, gee dɛna pumpɔreŋɔ woo sɔ.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Mam n nan boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa, mam de timpaberɛ la niihum.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Gee mam n tɔgeri yelemɛŋerɛ la zuo, ya ka sakɛ bo' mam.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a tubehɛ la nɔtuurum iŋɛ tuntɔnɔ puan, nɔm tuntɔnɔ la la nɔtuurum la, dikɛ buuɛ buraa la nini la.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Ya ani n wan ta'am pa'alɛ mam n tum be'em sebo? Mam han yɛta ya yelemɛŋerɛ, wani ti ya ka nam mam?.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: gee yele en yeti, Kiŋɛ Silom bɔka la puan ta wire. . Ti buraa wa kiŋɛ ta wire a nɛŋa la. Ti a nini la pikɛ ti a kina lɛbera na.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Se'em woo en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, wuni Yinɛ yelehum mɛ. Se'ere zuo ti yamam ka wuna yelehum la dela, ya ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: ti a mabiihi, la nɛresɛba n ni nyɛta en ti a zi'an suhera la, pɔhɛ sɔkera taaba yeti, Ena dagi buraa la n ni zi'an suha la.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Ti Yuuduma la lebehe en yeti, Tu ka tari la buurɔ la tu yele fum yeti, fum dela Samariya, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a yaga fum zuo la.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Ti sɛba yeti, en bala, ti sɛba me yeti, Aayi, a yeen wuna mɛ la en. Ti buraa wa mɛŋa muula yeti, Mam n bala, mam n bala.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, kulekpa'bɛ'a ka yage mam zuo. Gee mam na'ahɛ n Sɔ la mɛ, gee ti yamam ka na'ahɛ mam.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Ti ba sɔkera en tabela yeti, Ti la nyaa iŋɛ la wani tu fum nini la pikɛ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: Gee la bala za'a, mam ka eere n mɛŋa pɛka. A yima n boi nara pɛka la. Eŋa n de gakera la.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Buraa la yu'urɛ n yi'ire Yezu la, n bum tuntɔnɔ yakɛ buuɛ mam nini la, gee yele mam yeti, Kiŋɛ Silom bɔka la puan ta wire. Bala ti mam kiŋɛ ta wire nyaa nyɛta wa.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, Ti se'em woo n gurɛ mam yelehum wa kankaŋi, kan malum ki.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Ti ba soke en yeti, Ka bɛɛ? Ti a yeti, Mam ka mi'.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Ti Yuuduma la yele en yeti, Tu nyaa baŋɛ ya ti kulekpa'bɛ'a n sirum yage fum zuo. Aberaham ki mɛ, bala mɛa ti nɔtɔgeheba la woo ki, gee la bala za'a, fum yeti mɛ ti, Se'em woo n gurɛ mam yelehum wa kankaŋi kan malum ki.

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Ti nɛreba la tari buraa wa n daa de fua wa wa'am Farasii duma la zi'an na.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: Fum gani tumam sɔ Aberaham n ki la mɛ? Nɔtɔgeheba la me ki mɛ. Fum tugum yeti fum dela ani.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: La dela vo'ohego daarɛ ti Yezu nɔm tuntɔnɔ la buuɛ buraa la nini ti i pikɛ la.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam han na'ahera n mɛŋa, n mɛŋa na'ahegɔ kuŋɔ dela yoo. La dela mam Sɔ la n na'aheri mam, Eŋa n bala ti ya yeti, Yinɛ ma'a n de tumam Yinɛ la.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Bala ti Farasii duma wa me pɔhɛ sɔkera buraa la, lan iŋɛ se'em ti a nini la pikɛ. Ti a yele ba yeti, A nɔm la tuntɔnɔ buuɛ mam nini, ti mam kiŋɛ ta wire nyaa nyɛta wa.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: la yamam n pun ka mina en la. Gee mam mi' en. Mam han yeti mam ka mi' en, n pareni mɛ wuu yamam wa la. Gee mam sirum mi' en mɛ, gee gurɛ a a yelehum la kankaŋi.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Ti Farasii duma la basɛba yeti, Burasɛka n iŋɛ ŋwana wa ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, a ka na'aheri vo'ohego dabeherɛ la. Gee basɛba me yeti. Ŋwani ti nɛra n de be'em daana wan ta'am tuna daalehum buna taaba? Ti ba welehi taaba.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: Ya yaaba Aberaham yuum nyɛɛni mɛ ti a nyɛ mam kinɛ dabeherɛ la. En yuum nyɛ mam kinɛ la, a puurɛ yuum pee mɛ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Ti Farasii duma wa le soke fua wa yeti, Fum ti'ihɛ ti buraa wa dela beni? Fum nini ti a pikɛ. Ti buraa la yele ba yeti, A dela nɔtɔgeha.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Ti Yuuduma la yele en yeti, Fum nan ka paaɛ yuuma pihinuu, gee yuum bɔna la bɛ nyɛ Aberaham.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Gee Yuuduma kpɛ'ɛnduma la pun ka sakɛ ti buraa la tabelɛ fɔ mɛ gee nyaa nyɛta, han dagena ban yi' buraa wa dɔgereba na la.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Aberaham n wa bɔna la, ti mam pun bɔna mɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: ti ba soke ba yeti, Yamam dayua n ŋwana, ti ya yeti yamam dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna la fua ka biilimen na la? La nyaa iŋɛ la wani ti ka nyɛta wa.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: Ŋwana ti nɛreba la pi'ihe kuga ti ba lobe en, ti a lile nɛreba la puan yehe pu'uhegɔ yire la puan.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Ti ka dɔgereba lerege yeti, Tumam mi' ti tumam dayua n bala, tu me dɔgɛ en ti a dɛna la fua.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: gee tumam ka mi' lan iŋɛ se'em ti a nyaa nyɛta wa, bii tu mina se'em daana n pikɛ a nini la. Soke ya en gee a dagi bia, a mɛŋa wan tɔgɛ bo' a mɛŋa.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: A dɔgereba la n yele ŋwana la dela, ba zoti la Yuuduma la dabeem, Se'ere n sɔi la, ba ku la nuurɛ yeti, fum se'em woo n sakɛ ti Yezu dela Kiresta la, ba wan dige fum daana ti fum kan le kɛ̃ zamehegɔ yire la puan.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Bala zuo ti a dɔgereba la yele ba yeti, A dagi bia, soke ya en la.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Ti ba le yi' buraa wa n tabelɛ dɛna fua wa buyi' puan na yele en yeti, Pɛgɛ Yinɛ! Tumam mi' ti burasɛka n tibɛ fum wa dela be'em daana.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Mam ka mi' a de be'em daana, bii a dagi be'em daana. Yele yinɛ ti mam mina dela, mam daa fɔ mɛ, gee nyaa nyɛta.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Ti Yuuduma wa le soke en yeti, A iŋɛ fum la wani? A iŋɛ la wani pikɛ fum nini la.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Ti a lerege ba yeti, Mam pun yele ya bala mɛ, gee ya ka boti ya wum. Wani ti ya le ɛɛra ya wum le yɛ'ɛha? Ya nyaa boti ya lebege la a zahereba bii.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Ti ba taaɛ en la tuurɛ, yɛta en yeti, Fum n de a bura kaŋa wa zahera, gee tumam dela Moose zahereba.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Tumam mi' ti Yinɛ yuum tɔgɛ mɛ bo' Moose, gee han dɛna bura kana wa, tumam ka mi' kaŋa n yehe zi'an na.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Ti buraa la lebehe ba yeti, Yele pakerɛ yaa! Ya ka mi' kaŋa n yehe zi'an na, gee la bala za'a, kaŋa me n pikɛ mam nini wa.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Tumam mi' ti Yinɛ ka kelehe be'em duma pu'uhegɔ, gee a kelehe la se'em n na'ahɛ en, gee nana a suboolum.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Tu ka tabelɛ wum dɛ, timpaberɛ wa n pɔhɛ na wa paaɛ zina, ti nɛra n pikɛ nɛra n de fua a biilimen na nini.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Buraa wa han ka ze'ele ni Yinɛ zi'an na, ka kan ta'am ni looe yele.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Fum ti ba dɔgɛ hɔ kɔ'ɔm bahɛ be'em zo'e zo'e puan na wa en zigeri ti fum pa'alɛ tumam? Gee nyaa sa'ɛ buraa la ti ka kiŋɛ.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Ti Yezu en ta wum ti ba sa'ɛ buraa la mɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti a ta nyɛ ka, nyaa soke ka yeti, Fum nam asaala dayua la.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Ti buraa la soke en yeti, Naa, a dela ani? Pa'ali mam ti n nam bu n.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum pun nyɛ en mɛ. Se'em n ze' tɔgera la fum wa n bala.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Ti a yeti, N daana, mam nam ya. Gee na'ahɛ en.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Ti Yezu yeti, Mam wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na ti n gakɛ mɛ, ti sɛba n fɔ la nyɛta, ti sɛba n nyɛti la tee fɔ.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Ti Farasii duma basɛba zɛ'a lɛm en, wum en yele se'em la, yele en yeti , Tumam ka fɔ, bii fum ti'ihɛ tu fɔ mɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Ya han fɔ ni mɛ, ya kan tari ni be'em, gee yamam n yeti, Tumam nyɛti me wa la, ya be'em la yeere la ya puan.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Mam yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ, yelemɛŋerɛ ti se'em woo n ka doohe nayaŋa puan kɛ̃ piihi zɔŋɔ la puan, gee doohe la ze' sɛka sige, dela nageyiga, la se'em n faari.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Se'em n doohe nayaŋa puan kɛ̃ la n de piihi la dunkiina.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Nayangura la ni yu'ɛ bo' en mɛ ti a kɛ̃. A piihi la wuni a kua mɛ. A yi'ire a mɛŋa piihi la mɛ yu'ura, bɔna si nɛŋa tara si yɛha.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: A han tari si yehe na, a boi la nɛŋa ti si dɔla en, se'ere n sɔi la, piihi la mi' a kua mɛ.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Piihi la kan doohe saana, si tugum wan zɔ mɛ gee bahɛ en se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' saana la kua.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Yezu dikɛ la tɔgɛ biŋere buna wa tɔgɛ bo' ba, gee nɛreba la ka bɔkɛ en yeti ba sɛla la.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Ti Yezu le yele ba yeti, N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ'e wa, Mam n de piihi la nayaŋa.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Sɛba woo n diŋɛ mam nɛŋa na la dela nageyigehi, la fãareba, ti piihi la ka kelehe ba.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Mam n de nayaŋa la, se'em woo n doohe mam zi'an kɛ̃ wan nyɛ faarɛ. A wan kɛ̃, gee yehe ta nyɛ yaama obe.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Nageyiga ye' na yoo gee a ye' na ti a zu mɛ, gee ku, gee sagum. Gee han dɛna mam, mam wa'am na ti ba nyɛ la vom, vom sebo en seke yabera .

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Mam dela dunkiinsuŋa la. Dunkiinsuŋɔ la dikeri la a vom biŋera bɔ'ɔra a piihi.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Gee se'em ti ba dikɛ la, ti a dagena piihi la dunkiina la, ka sɔna piihi la, han nyɛ ti saheŋa kini na, a bahɛ piihi la mɛ gee zɔ, ti saheŋa la wa'am na nyɔgɛ piihi la gee yiregɛ si bahɛ.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: Se'em ti ba dikɛ la n wan zɔ la dela, piihi la yele ka pakɛ en.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Mam n de dunkiinsuŋɔ la. Mam mi' sɛba n de n dinɛ, ti mam dinɛ la me mina mam.

eng: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I give up my life for the sheep.
local: wuu Sɔ la n mi' mam ti mam me mina Sɔ la se'em la. Mam dikɛ n vom mɛ biŋe bo' n piihi la.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: Mam tari n piihi sise'ehi mɛ ti si ka bɔna piihi zɔŋɔ wa puan. N wan ba'ahum ɛ si na ti si me wum n kua. Ti piihi la za'a dɛna buyina, dunkiina ayima.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: Dina wa zuo ti Sɔ la nɔŋɛ mam la, mam n maahum ti n dikɛ n vom biŋe gee le dikɛ bɔ la.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Nɛra kan ta'am dikɛ mam vom wa, gee mam mɛŋa n dikɛ bu biŋera, mam boti se'em. Mam tari paŋa ti n dikɛ n vom wa biŋe, gee tara paŋa ti n lebehe dikɛ bɔ. Mam sɔ la n bo' mam nuurɛ ti n wan ta'am iŋɛ bala.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Ti Yuuduma la le welehi taaba yetɔgebuna wa zuo.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Ti ba zo'e zo'e yɛta ti, Kulekpa'bɛ'a n dole en, ti a zuo ka tuna. Wani ti tu wan zɛ'a kɛleha en.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Ti basɛba me yɛta ti, Yetɔgesebo ti a tɔgera wa dagi nɛra n tari kulekpa'bɛ'a yetɔgum. Kulekpa'bɛ'a wan ta'am pikɛ fua nini.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Saŋa kana wa puan la, ti pu'uhegɔ yire yu'urɛ de'eŋo la saŋa paaɛ mɛ Yerusalɛm puan. La wa dɛna ko'oro ŋmarega.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: ti Yezu kina kaara pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, zɛ'sɛka ti ba yi'ira Solomɔn paka la puan.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Ti Yuuduma la lagehɛ taaba wa'am na kaaɛ en bahɛ tiŋasuka, sokere en yeti, Fum boti tu gu'uhi ta paaɛ la wani? Fum han dɛna la Kiresta la, kɔ'ɔm tiɛ nɛŋa yele tu.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam pun yele ya, gee ya ka nam. Tuunsɛ'a ti mam tuna a n Sɔ yu'urɛ puan la pa'alɛ mɛ mam n de se'em.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: gee ya ka nam, se'ere n sɔi la, ya ka pa'ahɛ mam piihi la puan.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: Mam piihi la wuni mam kua mɛ. Ti mam me mina si, ti si dɔla n poore.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: Mam bo'ori si la vomkanba'ahɛ, ti si kan malum bahɛ yoo. Se'em me ka boi wan ta'am zɛ piihi wa mam nu'en faaɛ.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: Mam Sɔ n bo' mam sɛla la n gani sɛla woo. Se'em ka boi wan ta'am faaɛ la Sɔ la nu'uhen.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Sɔ la, la mam dela buyina.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Ti Yuuduma wa le pi'ihe kuga ti ba lobe en.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam tum la tuunkãra zo'e zo'e ya ninin n Sɔ la yu'urɛ puan. Yele ana wa dinɛ zuo ti ya yeti ya lobe mam wa.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
