local: Ti Yuuduma la lerege en yeti, La dagi la fum tuunsunɛ se'ere zuo, gee tuurɛ la. Beni zuo, fum wa n kɔ'ɔm dɛna asaala gee dikera fum mɛŋa lɛbegera Yinɛ la zuo.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, La ka gulehɛ bɔna ya pa'alegɔ la puan yeti, Mam yeti, ya dela yina?.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Ti a han yi'ira sɛba ti a dikɛ Yinɛ yelehum wa'am ba puan na yina, ti tu me kan ta'am sagum gulehegɔ la.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: wani ti ya wan ta'am yeti, Mam se'em ti Sɔ la bi'ihe mam, gee tum mam timpaberɛ wa zuo na la tuurɛ la Yinɛ la mam yeti, mam dela Yinɛ dayua la?.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Mam han ka tuna la n Sɔ la tuuma, da nam ya bo' mam.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Gee mam han tuna a, ti ya pun ka nam bo' mam, nam ya tuuma la, la ya ta'am mina, gee baŋɛ ti Sɔ la boi mɛ la mam, ti mam me bɔna la Sɔ la.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: Ti ba maahum ti ba le nyɔkɛ en, ti a pee ba nu'uhen.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Ti a le kiŋɛ yakɛ Yɔɔdan kulega la, zi'an ti Yɔn yuum bɔna sɔɔra nɛreba ko'om la, ta bɔna bilam.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'an a zi'an na gee yɛta taaba yeti, Yɔn yuum ka tuni daalehum, gee sɛla woo ti yele doohe buraa wa puan za'a dela asira. .

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam bo' Yezu bilam.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Buraa kayima n wa lu bã'a, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Lazarus, yehe Bɛtani timpaberɛ, timbila sɛka n de Mɛɛri la a suuɔ Maata timpaberɛ la puan.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Mɛɛri kana n tabelɛ dikɛ tuulum kaam ka'ɛ yeele Yezu nama, dikɛ a zonto viilum kaam la Yezu nama la. Pɔge kana wa n bala ti ka taya dɛna Lazarus bɛ̃'ɛra wa.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Ŋwana ti pɔgehi wa en de suurɔ wa tum sɔɔleŋa bo' Yezu yeti, Daana, se'em ti fum nɔŋɛ la bɛ̃'ɛri mɛ.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Gee Yezu n wum sɔɔleŋa wa la, a yeti, Ba'an wa kan ku kum, gee ka tugum wan bo' la Yinɛ pɛka, ti Yinɛ dayua wa doohe bini nyɛ pɛka.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Lan de se'em la dela, Yezu nɔŋɛ Maata la a suuɔ la Lazarus mɛ.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: gee en wum sɔɔleŋa la ti Lazarus bɛ̃'ɛri mɛ la, a yee la eŋa n boi zi'an la dabeha ayi'.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Pooren, ti Yezu yele a zahereba la yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu lebe ya Yudiya tiŋa le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, Rabi, daarɛ wa ti Yuuduma daa zigera ti ba lobe fum la kuga la, ti fum le yeti tu kiŋɛ bini.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Dagi gogo kaareŋa pia la ayi' n ni dɛna wuntɛɛŋa? Sɛba n kini niihum saŋa la ka ŋmɛ'ɛri nama, se'ere n sɔi la, ba nyɛti timpaberɛ wa zuo niihum la.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Gee sɛba duma n kini yu'uŋɔ la, ŋmɛ'ɛri la nama, se'ere n sɔi la, niihum la ka boi ba puan.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Ti en yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a yele ba yeti, Tu zɔ Lazarus gã' giha mɛ, gee mam ye' la bini ti n ta niiɛ en.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, Daana, a han giha, se'ere ka daani en.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Gee Yezu tɔgeri la Lazarus kum n bala, gee bamam ti'ihɛ ti a yeen tɔgera la gɔɔm yele.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa anregɛ bo' ba yeti, Lazarus ka boi.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: Gee yamam zuo, la ani mam suŋa la mam n ka boi bini ha wa. Ŋwana ti ya wan nam. Gee yeen bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ a zi'an.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Ti Tɔmasi se'em ti ba yi'ira liiberɛ la yele a zahereba taaba la yeti, Bahɛ ya ti tu doohe en kiŋɛ, ta lagum kɔ'ɔm ki la en.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Yezu n ta paaɛ Yudiya tiŋa la, a wum ti ba pi' Lazarus la dabeha anaahi zina.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Bɛtani timpaberɛ wa ka zaaɛ la Yerusalɛm, la ani wuu miila ayi' la.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: ti Yuuduma timpaberɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am na ti ba lagum pã'ahɛ Maata la Mɛɛri, ba taya kum la puan.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Maata n wum ti Yezu kini na la, a yehe tu'uhɛ en mɛ sore, gee ti Mɛɛri yee yire.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Ti Maata yele Yezu yeti, Daana, fum daa han boni ka'lam, mam taya wa kan ki ni.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: La gee la dabeha ayoobe la azɔɛtole de'eŋo la paaɛ, ti Yezu wa'am Bɛtani na, Lazarus la ti a daa ihige en kum puan la yire.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Gee la nananawa wa me, mam mi' ti fum han soke Yinɛ sɛla woo, a wan bo' fum.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Ti ba dugɛ zaanuurɛ dia bo' en. Ti Maata tɔta dia la, ti Lazarus lagum zi'an kaaɛ ti ba dɛla taaba, bamam sɛba woo n boi bini ti ba di la Yezu la.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum taya la wan vo'e a kum la puan.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Ti Mɛɛri dikɛ tuulum kaam n tari yuuŋo suŋɔ, tari na ka'ɛ yeele Yezu nama, dikɛ a zonto viilum kaam la sɔ Yezu nama. Ti yire la za'a to'e la kaam la nyuuŋo suŋɔ la.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Ti Maata lebehe Yezu yeti, Mam mi' ti a wan ta vo'e la ihigere daarɛ la n de ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Gee Yudasi Isikariyɔte en de Yezu zahera ayima la, , yeti.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam n de ihigere la, la vom la. Se'em n nam mam, la han pun ki, gee ba wan vo'e.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Ŋwani ti fum ka dikɛ kaam wa koohe ligekɔba kɔbehi sitã ti ba dikɛ ligeri la suŋɛ naheduma.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: ti se'em woo n vɔi gee nam mam kan malum ki. Fum nam dina wa.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Ti a yele Yezu yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ n Daana, mam nam ya ti fum de Kiresta la, Yinɛ dayua la, se'em n wan wa'an timpaberɛ wa zuo na la.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Bahɛ pɔka wa bããlam. Pɔka wa da' kaam wa biŋe mɛ gura mam laarɛ daarɛ.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Maata n tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a kɛ̃ mɛ ta waahum bo' Mɛɛri a suuɔ la ma'a bããlam yeti, Pa'ala la boi ka'lam gee yi'ira hɔ.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Naheduma wan bɔna la ya mɛ daarɛ woo, gee mam kan bɔna la ya bala.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mɛɛri n wum bala la, a ihige kalam kiŋɛ la Yezu zi'an.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Yuuduma nɛrekuuŋɔ la n ta baŋɛ ti Yezu boi Bɛtani timpaberɛ puan la, ba zo'e zo'e wa'am me na, dagi la Yezu ma'a zuo, gee ba nyɛ Lazarus la ti Yezu vo'e en kum puan la.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Bala ma'a ti Yezu nan ka kɛ̃ timpaberɛ la puan na, gee a kelum bɔna la zi'an ti Maata tu'uhɛ en la.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti malema kiinduma la ku nuurɛ mu'ɛ nini ti ba ku Lazarus pa'ahɛ.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Ti Yuuduma la n wa'am na zi'an yire la puan pã'aha Mɛɛri la n nyɛ ti a ihige bala kalam yɛ'a la, ti ba me ihige dɔla a poore tɛ'ɛha ti a ye' la yɔɔ la puan ti a ta kele.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa vo'ore la zuo ti Yuuduma la zo'e zo'e zageha Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ la gee nam Yezu.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Ti Mɛɛri wa'am na paaɛ zi'an ti Yezu bɔna la, nyɛ Yezu, kpa' duma Yezu nɛŋan, gee yele en yeti Naa, Fum daa han boni ka'lam, mam taya wa kan ki ni.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Ti yiimbeere ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en ka tã ita en wa'am azɔɛtole de'eŋo la na la, wum ti Yezu ye' Yerusalɛm na.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yezu n nyɛ ti Mɛɛri kɛla la, ti Yuuduma sɛba me n doohe Mɛɛri poore na la me kɛla la, ti la ve'e Yezu paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Ti nɛreba la aahum kunkɔɔreŋɔ yehe tu'uha en gee vɔɔla yeti, Hozaana! Yinɛ sum boi la se'em n wa'am Daana la yu'urɛ puan na la. Izirale naba la.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Ti a soke yeti, Ya biŋe en la bɛ? Ti ba yele en yeti, Daana, wa'am na bihɛ.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Ti Yezu nyɛ bompaalega, zom yaga ka zuo, wuu lan gulehɛ gɔŋɔ la puan yele se'em yeti.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Ti Yezu kele.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Da zɔta dabeem Zaayɔn poyua wa, Bihɛ, fum naba la n bala kina na la, a yaga la bumpaalega zuo.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Ti Yuuduma la basɛba yɛta taaba yeti, Bihɛ en nɔŋɛ Lazarus se'em.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Yezu zahereba la ka bɔkɛ yele ana wa yia yia ha la, gee Yezu n da ta to'e a suŋa ba'ahɛ la, ti ba nyaa tiɛ ti yele ana wa woo gulehɛ bɔna gɔŋɔ la puan mɛ doohe Yezu puan, ti ba dikɛ a za'a iŋɛ en.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Gee ti basɛba me yeti, Eŋa se'em n daa pikɛ fua nini la, kan ta'am gu' ni ti buraa wa da ki ni.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la n daa boi la Yezu ti a yi' Lazarus yɔɔ la puan la, gee vo'e en kum la puan la, daa kelum kɔ'ɔm tɔgera yele la mɛ.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Ti la le ve'e Yezu, ti a kiŋɛ paaɛ yɔɔ la zi'an, ti ku dela yoko, ti ba bilege taŋa lige ku nuurɛ la.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: La me dela, nɛreba wum mɛ ti a tum la daalehum kana zuo, ti nɛrekuuŋɔ yehe bibigera tu'uha en.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Ti Yezu bo' nuurɛ yeti, Bilege ya kugere la yɔɔ la nuuren. Ti Maata n de Lazarus wa n ki wa taya ayima la, yele Yezu yeti, Daana, la wan ba' fi'ira, se'ere en sɔi la, ban pi' en, dabeha a naahi n ŋwana zina.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Ti Farasii duma la nyaa yɛta taaba yeti, Ya nyɛti, tu kan ta'am iŋɛ se'ere. Bihɛ ya, timpaberɛ wa za'a n bala bibigere dɔla en wa.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam ka yele fum ti fum han nam, fum wan nyɛ Yinɛ paŋa n ani se'em.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Nɛresɛba n wa'am na ti ba na'ahɛ azɔɛtole de'eŋo la saŋan la, basɛba dela Giriki duma.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: Ti nɛreba la bilege kugere la. Ti Yezu gorege tɔ saazuo gee yeti, N sɔ, mam bo' fum fara, la fum n kelehe mam.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Ti ba wa'am na nyɛ Filipi n yehe Bɛtisaada tiŋa n boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan la yele en yeti, Naa, tu boti ni ti tu nyɛ la Yezu wa.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: Mam mi' ti saŋa woo, fum kelehe mam mɛ, gee n yeti ŋwana wa la nɛrekuuŋɔ wa n ze' ka'lam wa zuo, ti ba ta'am nam ti fum n tum mam na.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Bala ti Filipi kiŋɛ ta yele Anduru, ti Anduru la Filipi nyaa paaɛ Yezu zi'an yele en.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: En tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a tahɛ mɛ la kukɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Lazarus, yehe na.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Saŋa la paaɛ ya ti asaala dayua la nyɛ pɛka.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Ti buraa wa n ki wa vaaɛ ihige yehe na, ti a nu'uhi la a nama bobe la tama, ti tanɛ bobe a zuo. Ti Yezu yele nɛreba la yeti, Lorege ya en bahɛ ti a kiŋɛ.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti beeleho han ka lu tintɔnɔ puan pu'ɛ, bu kelum dɛna la beeleho. Gee bu han lu timpaberɛ pu'ɛ yehe na, bu wan wam biili zo'e zo'e.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Ti Yuuduma la n doohe la Mɛɛri yehe tu'uhɛ Yezu la, n nyɛ Yezu en iŋɛ sɛla woo la, ti ba zo'e zo'e nam Yezu.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Se'em en nɔŋɛ a vom wan bɔregɛ bɔ bahɛ, ti se'em n ka gagɛ a vom zuo timpaberɛ wa zuo, a n tugum wan tara vomkanba'ahɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Gee ti ba puan basɛba me kiŋɛ Farasii duma la zi'an ta yele ba sɛla woo ti Yezu iŋɛ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Se'em n wan dɛna mam yameŋa dela, a dɔla mam. Mam n boi zi'an la, n yameŋa la me wan bɔna la bilam. Se'em woo n de mam yameŋa tuna bɔ'ɔra mam la, Sɔ la wan zɛkɛ en.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Ti malema kiinduma la, la Farasii duma la yi' kiinduma lagehɛ ti ba sɔhɛ. Nyaa yele ba yeti, Tu yeti tu iŋɛ la wani la buraa wa? Ka ba' tuna la daalehum zɔ'ɔra.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Nananawa, mam suure sagum mɛ. Gee n wan yeti bu? N yeti N Sɔ, yehe mam namehegɔ saŋa wa puan bii? Aayi n kan yele bala, namehegɔ wa zuo ti mam wa'am na paaɛ saŋa kana puan wa.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Tu han bahɛ en ti a ita ŋwana yɛ'a ha, nɛra woo wan nam en, ti bala me wan bahɛ ti Aromduma wa'am na gbirege tu tuulum yire wa, la tu timpaberɛ wa bahɛ.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: N Sɔ, bo' fum yu'urɛ wa pɛka. Ti kua ze'ele saazuo ha tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Mam bo' n yu'urɛ la pɛka gee wan le bo' de pɛka le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Gee ba puan, ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Kaifase, dɛna malema kiima yuum dinɛ puan la, n yele ba yeti, Ya ka mi' sɛla la fii me.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n ze' la en gee wum kua la, basɛba yeti, Saa n damɛ, ti basɛba me yeti, suŋera n tɔgɛ bo' en na.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Ya ka mi' ti la ani suŋa ti nɛreyinɛ ki bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ, gana timpaberɛ la nɛreba za'a n ki.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Kua la wa'an la yamam zuo na gee dagi mam.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: A daa ka yele dina wa la a mɛŋa paŋa, gee en de malema kiima la yuum dinɛ puan la, ti a purɛ ti Yezu wan ki bo' timpaberɛ la.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Tiŋa la gakerɛ paaɛ ya, se'em n sɔi timpaberɛ wa bihera la dika saŋa paaɛ ya.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: dagi bamam timpaberɛ la ma'a, gee ba lagehɛ sɛba woo n de Yinɛ kɔma, yiregɛ la wa'am bɔbeyinɛ na.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Gee han dɛna mam, ba han ta zɛkɛ mam saazuo timpaberɛ wa zuo, n wan ve'e nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am n zi'an na.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Yehe daan dinɛ daarɛ, Yuuduma kiinduma wa daa ku la nuurɛ ti ba ku Yezu.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Yezu yele ŋwana pa'ala la kum sebo taaba ti a wan ki.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Bala zuo la, Yezu ka le ɛɛra bala fai Yuuduma la puan, gee a bahɛ a timpaberɛ la mɛ, gee kiŋɛ timpaberɛ kayima ti ba yi'ira Efraim n boi goo bɔba ha la, ta bɔna bilam la a zahereba la.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lebehe en yeti, Tumam wum nɔze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan ti la yeti, Kiresta la wan bɔna la wuuha kan ki. Wani ti fum yɛta yeti, Ba wan ta zɛkɛ asaala dayua la saazuo? Ani n de asaala dayua wa.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Ti Yuuduma azɔɛtole de'eŋo la pɔra, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e yehe timbibehi puan yɛ'ɛha Yerusalɛm ti ba ta pee ba mɛhi gee gura azɔɛtole de'eŋo la.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Niihum la wan bɔna la ya la dabeha fii wa, bala biheri ya kina ti niihum la nan boi wa, ti lika la da ta wa'am na pi' ya. Ya han kina lika puan, ya ka mi' yan ye' zi'an.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Ti nɛreba la ta ɛɛra Yezu, gee nyaa sɔkera taaba pu'uhegɔ yire la puan yeti, yamam ti'ihɛ la ŋwani? A wan sakɛ wa'am de'eŋo wa puan na, bii a wan kan sakɛ.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Niihum wa n nan boi la ya wa, nam ya bo' niihum la, la ya dɛna niihum la kɔma. Yezu n yele ba ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ mɛ ta sugɛ a mɛŋa ti ba da nyɛta en.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Bala ma'a la, ti malema kiima duma la, la Farasii duma la pun bo' la nuurɛ yeti, Fum se'em woo n nyɛ Yezu zi'an, fum yele tu, ti tu nyɔkɛ en.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: La Yezu n tum daalehum kekeko nɛreba la ninin la, ba zo'e zo'e ka nam bo' en.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Gee ŋwana wa iŋɛ ti la doohe yelese'ere n tɔgɛ doohe nɔtɔgeha Azaaya puan se'em yeti, N Daana, ani n nam tu yelehum la, gee ani ti Daana pa'alɛ a kinkpaŋa.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Ba kan ta'am nam, se'ere n sɔi la Azaaya daa purɛ pa'ahɛ yeti.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Yinɛ mɛŋa n pi' ba nini, gee kãbɛ ba suya, ti ba nini da nyɛta, ti ba suya me da bɔkera yɛla, ti ba da tee ba puti'ira lebena ti n tibɛ ba.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Azaaya n yele dina wa la dela a yuum nyɛ la na'ahegɔ seko n de Yezu dinɛ, bala ti a tɔgɛ a yele la.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Gee la bala za'a, pun ta ta' la kpɛ'ɛnduma la zo'e zo'e, nam bo' en mɛ. Gee Farasii duma la zuo, ba ka ta'am muulɛ dɛ se'ere n sɔi la, ba zoti la dabeem ti ba wan sa'ɛ ba zamehegɔ yire la puan bahɛ.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, ba ba' nɔŋɛ la asaala pɛka, gana pɛka sɛka n ze'eti Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Ti Yezu kaahɛ bahɛ na yeti, Se'em woo n nam bo' mam, ka nam bo' la mam ma'a gee a nam bo' la se'em n tum mam na la.

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Se'em woo me n nyɛ mam, a nyɛ se'em daana n tum mam na la mɛ.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Mam wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo me na woo niihum la, ti fum se'em woo n nam bo' mam kan yee lika puan.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Mam ka gakeri se'em n wum mam yelehum wa gee ka tara bɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka wa'am na ti n gakɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ, gee n tugum faaɛ ka bahɛ.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Se'em n zagehɛ mam gee ka to'e mam yelehum la, tari se'em n wan gakɛ en mɛ. Ba'ahegɔ daarɛ la, yelehum sebo ti mam tɔgɛ wa n wan dɛna gakera.

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka tɔgɛ la mam mɛŋa n boti se'em, gee Sɔ la n tum mam na la n bo' mam nuurɛ, mam wan tɔgɛ sɛla, la mam wan yele sɛla.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: Mam me mi' suŋa ti eŋa n boti n yele se'ere dela vomkanba'ahɛ yele. Bala zuo, mam n tɔgeri sɛla la dela Sɔ la n kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti n tɔgɛ se'em n bala.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Nananawa, gee ti azɔɛtole de'eŋo la wan paaɛ la, ti Yezu pun baŋɛ ti a saŋa la paaɛ ya ti a bahɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee lebe a Sɔ zi'an. Bala ma'a ti Yezu nɔŋerɛ pun kɛ̃ sɛba en de a dinɛ la n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo la mɛ paaɛ paaɛ. A nɔŋɛ ba mɛ ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Ba zaanuurɛ dia saŋa, ti Sagena la pun diŋɛ kalum Yudasi en de Simɔn Isikariyɔte dayua la puti'irɛ mɛ, ti a koohe Yezu.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Ti Yezu n pun mina ti a Sɔ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la eŋa Yezu nu'uhen, gee me mina ti eŋa ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, gee me lɛbera la Yinɛ zi'an la.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: ti a ihige a zi'irego zi'an la, yɛɛ a fuo seko ti a yɛ dɔgelɛ la biŋe, gee dikɛ duuhega wooe a sia.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Nyaa ka'ɛ ko'om iŋɛ peere lɔkɔ puan, pɔhɛ pɛɛra a zahereba la nama, gee duuha ba la duuhega la ti a wooe la.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: Ti a wa'am na paaɛ Simɔn Pita, ti a yele en yeti, Daana, fum yeti fum pee la mam nama yoi.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Nananawa, fum kan bɔkɛ mam iti sɛla wa, gee pooren fum wan ta bɔkɛ.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, Fum kan makɛ pee mam nama. Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Han dagena mam pee fum mɛ, fum ka tara tɔtɛ la mam.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Ŋwana ti Simɔn Pita yele en yeti, Daana, yi da pee n nama ma'a, gee pee n nu'uhi la n zuo pa'ahɛ.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Se'em n diŋɛ sɔ iŋa ka le sɔɔra pa'aha han dagena a nama ma'a ti a wan duuhe, a iŋa la za'a ani la filam. Yamam ani la filam, gee dagi ya za'a.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Yezu mi' ba puan se'em n wan koohe en. Bala zuo ti a yeti, Dagi ya za'a n ani filam la.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: En pee ba nama la ba'ahɛ la, dikɛ a fuo la yɛ, nyaa lebe zi'ire la ba la, a yele ba yeti, Ya mi' sɛla ti n iŋɛ ya wa vuurɛ?.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Ya yi'iri mam la Pa'ala la Daana, Ya ka tuue, se'ere en sɔi la, mam n de se'em n bala.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Bala, mam en de ya Daana, la ya Pa'ala la han pee ya nama, yamam me dela ya pee taaba nama.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Mam iŋɛ biŋe ya mɛ, ti ya me ita wuu mam n iŋɛ se'em la.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti yameŋa ka gani ka daana, bii bitunehi gana se'em n tum ba la.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Ya han mina yele ana wa, sum wan bɔna, ya han ita a.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: N ka tɔgeri ŋwana bɔ'ɔra la ya za'a. Mam mi' sɛba ti N looe la mɛ. Gee la dela la doohe gulehegɔ la n yeti, Mam la se'em n lagum tuna laa kayina puan la n dikɛ mam koohe la.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: N yeti ya dina wa nananawa ti de nan ka tum wa, de nyaa han ta tum, ya wan nam ti mam n bala.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti se'em woo n to'e se'em ti mam tum, to'e la mam, se'em woo me n to'e mam, to'e la se'em n tum mam na la.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yezu n tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a suure sagum mɛ ti a kaahɛ yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa. Yamam puan ayima n yeti a koohe mam.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Ti a zahereba la biha taaba kaara, ka mina se'em ti Yezu yɛta la.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Ti a zahereba la ayima, se'em ti a ba' nɔŋɛ la zi'an dɛla en.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: ti Simɔn Pita ɛbegɛ en yeti, a soke Yezu, se'em n de en ti a tɔgera wa.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Bala ti a zahera wa n zi' dɛla a nyu'ɔ zuo la soke en yeti, Daana, ani n de en.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, A dela se'em ti n wan dikɛ boroboro wa suke laa wa puan bo' en la. Bala, en suke boroboro la, a dikɛ bo' la Yudasi n de Simɔn Isikariyɔte dayua la.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Ti en to'e boroboro la ba'ahɛ la, ti Sagena la kɛ̃ en. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Tum fum yeti fum tum se'ere la kalam.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Gee se'em ka boi ban zi' la puan mina se'ere zuo ti Yezu yele en bala.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Basɛba ti'ihɛ ti, Yudasi n zãli ba bo'olum tapɔɔ la, ti Yezu yɛta en ti, Da' lɔgeseto ti ba wan bɔta azɔɛtole de'eŋo la puan; bii a bihɛ sɛla bo' naheduma.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Bala, en to'e boroboro la ba'ahɛ la, bilam bilam ti a ihige yehe. Bala ma'a ti la dela yu'uŋɔ.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Yudasi n yehe la, ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Nananawa ti asaala dayua wa nyɛ pɛka, ti Yinɛ me nyɛ pɛka a puan.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Yinɛ han nyɛ pɛka Dayua wa puan, Yinɛ mɛŋa wan bo' Dayua la me pɛka Dayua la mɛŋa puan, gee me iŋɛ de kalam.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Kɔmbibehi wa, mam yeti n bɔna la ya la dabeha fiiwa. Ya wan ta ɛɛra mam, wuu mam daa yele Yuuduma la se'em la, bala ti n nyaa yɛta yamam me. Mam n ye' zi'an la, ya kan ta'am wa'am bini na.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: N bo'ori ya la ze'elego paalega ti, ya nɔŋɛ taaba. Wuu mam nɔŋɛ ya se'em la, me nɔŋɛ taaba bala.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Ya han nɔŋɛ taaba ŋwana, nɛra woo wan baŋɛ ti ya dela mam zahereba.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita yele en yeti, Daana, fum yeti fum kiŋɛ la bɛ? Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam ye' zi'an la, fum kan ta'am doohe mam nananawa, gee pooren, fum wan doohe.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, Daana, beni zuo ti mam kan ta'am doohe fum nananawa? Mam wan dikɛ n vom biŋe bo' hɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum wan dikɛ fum vom biŋe bo' mam? Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa, ti nɔdɔɔ kan kaaha gura ti fum ta ki'ihɛ mam sugum nuurɛ butã.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti ya suya daana, nam ya Yinɛ, gee nam mam me.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: Mam Sɔ yire la ha, deto zo'e mɛ. La han dagi ni bala, mam wan yele ni ya ti n ye' ti n ta maalɛ la bɔŋa zi'an biŋe ya?.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: N han kiŋɛ ta maalɛ zi'an la biŋe ya ba'ahɛ, n wan le lebe n mɛŋa na, tari ya kiŋɛ, ti zi'an ti mam bɔna la, bilam ti yamam me wan bɔna.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Ya me pun mina sore se'ere n dole yɛ'ɛha zi'an ti mam yɛ'ɛha la mɛ.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Ti Tɔmasi yele en yeti, Daana, tu ka mi' fum ye' zi'an, tu yeti tu iŋɛ la wani baŋɛ sore la.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam n de sore la, la yelemɛŋerɛ la, la nyɔvore la. Se'em kan ta'am paaɛ Sɔ la zi'an, han dagena a doohe la mam puan.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Ya han mina mam, ya wan mina mam Sɔ la me. Gee nananawa yɛ'ɛha ha, ya mi' en, gee me nyɛ en.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Ti Filipi yele en yeti, Daana, pa'alɛ tɔ se'em n de Sɔ la, gee bala wan tigɛ tɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Mam n boi la ya saŋa kana wa woo wa Filipi, ti fum kelum ka mina mam? Se'em woo n nyɛ mam, nyɛ Sɔ la mɛ. Wani ti fum le yɛta mam yeti, Pa'alɛ tɔ Sɔ la?.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: Fum ka nam ti mam boi mɛ la Sɔ la, ti Sɔ la me bɔna la mam? Yelehum wa ti mam tɔgera wa dagi mam mɛŋa yelehum, gee bu dela Sɔ se'em n boi la mam la n tuni a tuuma mam puan.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Nam bo' mam ti mam boi mɛ la n Sɔ la, ti n Sɔ la bɔna la mam. Gee fum han pun ka nam, tuuma la mɛŋa zuo, fum dela fum nam bo' mam.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti se'em woo n nam mam, me wan tuna tuuma sɛ'a ti mam tuna wa, a wan tum a pun gaŋɛ bala, beni zuo, mam ye' la Sɔ la zi'an.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: N wan iŋɛ sɛla woo ti ya soke bɔta mam yu'urɛ puan, ti Sɔ la nyɛ pɛka dayua la puan.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Ya han soke mam yu'urɛ la puan bɔta sɛla woo, n wan iŋɛ dɛ.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Ya han nɔŋɛ mam, ya wan gurɛ mam pa'alegɔ kankaŋi.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: Bala ti n wan soke n Sɔ la, ti a wan bo' ya Nu'pu'ŋmɛ'ɛra ayima en kpe'em paaɛ paaɛ ti a bɔna la ya wuuha.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Eŋa en de Fa'a Sia la n de yelemɛŋerɛ la, se'em ti Timpaberɛ wa zuo kan to'e en, Se'ere n sɔi la, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka nyɛti en, bii ba mina en. Gee yamam mi' en se'ere n sɔi la, a boi mɛ la ya, gee wan kelum bɔna la ya.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: N kan bahɛ ya ti ya ana wuu kiibehi la. N lebere ya zi'an me na.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: La han iŋɛ fiiwa, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba kan le nyɛ mam, gee yamam wan nyɛ mam. Mam vɔi la, yamam me wan vɔna.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: De daarɛ la, ya wan baŋɛ ti mam boi mɛ la n Sɔ la, ti yamam bɔna la mam, ti mam me bɔna la ya.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Se'em n mi' mam Pa'alegɔ duma la, gee dɔla a n de se'em n nɔŋɛ mam. Se'em en nɔŋɛ mam la, mam Sɔ la me wan nɔŋɛ en, ti mam mɛŋa me nɔŋɛ en, gee niira n mɛŋa pa'ala en.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Ti Yudasi ayima, yele Yezu yeti, Daana, wani ti fum wan ta niiɛ fum mɛŋa pa'ala tumam, gee dagi timpaberɛ wa.

eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Sɛba en nɔŋɛ mam la, wan tara mam yelehum la, ti mam Sɔ wan nɔŋɛ ba, ti mam la n Sɔ la wan wa'am na iŋɛ tu bɔŋa zi'an ba puan.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Se'em n ka nɔŋɛ mam, ka guti mam yelehum. Yelehum wa ti ya wuna wa me dagi mam yelehum, bu dela Sɔ la n tum mam na la dinɛ.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: N yeti ya yele ana wa woo ti n nan boi la ya wa.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: Gee Tɛ'ɛla la kini na, Fa'a Sia la. Se'em ti Sɔ la wan tum en na mam yu'urɛ puan la. Eŋa wan pa'alɛ ya sɛla woo, gee tiiha ya sɛla woo ti mam yele ya la.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Suma'ahum ti mam dikɛ baha ya. Mam mɛŋa suma'ahum ti mam dikɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. Mam ka bo'ori n suma'ahum wa wuu timpaberɛ zuo nɛreba n bo'ori ba suma'ahum se'em la. Da bahɛ ya ti ya suya daana, gee me da bahɛ ya ti dabeem tara ya.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Ya wum mam pun yele ya yeti, N yeti n kiŋɛ mɛ, gee le lebe ya zi'an na. Ya han nɔŋɛ mam, ya wan nyɛɛna, la mam n ye' Sɔ la zi'an la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Sɔ la gani mam mɛ.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Nananawa, n pun yele ya mɛ, gee ti yɛla la baŋɛ wa'am na, bala ti yele ana wa han ta wa'am na tum, ya wan nam mam.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: N kan le ta'am tɔgɛ la ya yuue, se'ere en sɔi la, Be'em daana se'em n pɛregeri timpaberɛ wa zuo kini me na. A me ka tari n paŋa.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: gee mam iti la sɛla ti Sɔ la bo' mam nuurɛ ti n iŋɛ la, ti timpaberɛ nɛreba baŋɛ ti mam nɔŋɛ Sɔ la mɛ. Ihige ya ti tu kiŋɛ.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Mam de yelemɛŋerɛ sinsabega, ti mam Sɔ bihera sinsabega la.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: A ŋmati yile-se'ere woo n naaɛ la mam gee ka wuna baha mɛ. Gee yɔreha yile-se'ere woo n naaɛ la mam gee wuna wala ti de wam zo'e zo'e.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Yamam pun diŋɛ ana la filam, doohe la yelehum la ti n tɔgɛ bo' ya la.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Boi ya la mam, ti mam me bɔna la ya. Wuu yile n kan ta'am wam wala de ma'a se'em, han dagena de naaɛ mɛ la ta'aŋa la, bala mɛa ti yamam me kan ta'am wam, han dagena ya naaɛ mɛ la mam.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Mam n de ta'aŋa la, ti yamam dɛna yila. Sɛba n naaɛ la mam, ti mam me naaɛ la ba la wani zɔ'ɔra mɛ, se'ere zuo, la han dagena la mam puan, ya kan ta'am iŋɛ sɛla.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Se'em woo n ka naaɛ la mam, ba lobere en baha mɛ wuu yile ti ba ŋmaaɛ bahɛ ti a ku'e se'em la. Yile aŋa, ba ni lagehɛ ba mɛ bahɛ bugum puan ti ba di.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Ya han bɔna la mam, ti n yelehum la bɔna la ya, soke ya yan boti sɛla, gee la wan tum bo' ya.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Mam Sɔ pɛka boi la, ya wam zo'e zo'e. Ŋwana ti ya wan dɛna mam zahereba.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Wuu Sɔ la n nɔŋɛ mam se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam nɔŋɛ ya. Boni ya n nɔŋerɛ la puan.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ya han gurɛ mam pa'alegɔ duma la kankaŋi, ya wan bɔna n nɔŋerɛ la puan, wuu mam n gurɛ n Sɔ pa'alegɔ duma la se'em kankaŋi la, bɔna a nɔŋerɛ la puan se'em la.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: N yeti ya yele ana wa woo, ti mam nyɛɛŋɔ wan bɔna la yamam puan, ti ya nyɛɛma pirɛ.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: Mam Pa'alegɔ n ŋwana. Ti ya nɔŋɛ taaba, wuu mam n nɔŋɛ ya se'em la.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Se'em ka tari nɔŋerɛ n karegɛ gana dina wa, ti nɛra dikɛ a vom bo' a zɔduma.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Yamam dela mam zɔduma, ya han ita mam bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya ita sɛla la.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: Mam ka yi'iri ya n yamehi yɛ'ɛha, se'ere n sɔi la, yameŋa ka mi' ka daana n iti sɛla. Gee mam yi'iri ya la n zɔduma se'ere n sɔi la, mam bahɛ ti ya mina sɛla woo ti mam wum n Sɔ la zi'an mɛ.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Yamam ka looe mam, gee mam n looe ya, gee tum ya ti ya kiŋɛ ta wam wala. Ya wam wala sɛ'a n kan pu'ɛ. Bala ti Sɔ la wan bo' ya sɛla woo ti ya soke en mam yu'urɛ puan.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: . N bo'ori ya pa'alegɔ kuna, ti ya nɔŋɛ la taaba.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Timpaberɛ wa zuo han yaaɛ ya, mini ya ti ka yaaɛ la mam yia, gee nyaa yaaɛ yamam.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Ya han deni timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, timpaberɛ wa zuo wan nɔŋɛ ya wuu a dinɛ la. Gee yan dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ la, gee mam n looe ya yehe timpaberɛ wa puan la, bala zuo ti timpaberɛ wa zuo yaaɛ ya la.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Tɛri ya yelehum la ti mam tɔgɛ bo' ya yeti, Yameŋa ka gana ka daana la. Ba han mɛlehɛ mam, ba wan mɛlehɛ yamam me. Ba han gurɛ mam yelehum la kankaŋi, ba wan gurɛ yamam yelehum la me kankaŋi.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Gee ba wan iŋɛ ya yele ana woo mam yu'urɛ wa zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' se'em n tum mam na la.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Mam han ka sige ni na tɔgɛ ni bo' ba, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba ka tari tu ka baŋɛ mɛ, ba be'em la puan.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Se'em woo n yaaɛ mam, yaaɛ mam Sɔ la me mɛ.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Mam han ka ze'ele ni ba nɛŋan tum daalehum sebo taaba ti nɛra kan ta'am tum wa, ba ka tari ni tuure. Gee nananawa, ba pun nyɛ mɛ, gee ti ba sina gee yaaɛ mam la n Sɔ la.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Gee ŋwana woo iŋɛ ti la doohe la gulehegɔ la n boi ba pa'alegɔ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, Ba yaaɛ mam la yoo la.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Gee Tɛ'ɛla la han wa'am na, se'em ti mam wan tum en ti a ze'ele Sɔ la zi'an na la, se'em n de Fa'a Sia la en de yelemɛŋerɛ, ze'ele mam Sɔ la zi'an na la, a wan tɔgera la mam yele bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Yamam me mɛhi dela ya tɔgera mam yele, se'ere n sɔi la, ya boi la mam mɛ n pa'alegɔ wa pi'ileŋo puan na.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: N yele ya yele ana wa, ti a gu' ya ti ya da pita luta.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Kelehe ya, Ba wan dige ya zamehegɔ yiya la puan bahɛ. Saŋa kini na, ti sɛba n ku ya, wan ti'ihɛ ti ban iŋɛ bala la, ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la Yinɛ.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Ba wan iŋɛ ŋwana doohe la ban ka mi' Sɔ la, bii mam la zuo.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Gee mam diŋɛ yele ya yele ana wa, ti a saŋa han ta paaɛ, ya wan tɛra ti n pun yele ya a yele. N daa ka yele ya yele ana wa pi'ileŋo puan na la, doohe la mam nan boi la ya la zuo.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Gee nananawa, mam ye' lɛbera la se'em n tum mam na la zi'an, gee ya se'em ka soke mam nyaa Fum ye' la bɛha.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Gee mam n nyaa yele ya yele ana wa la, susageŋɔ n pirɛ ya suya.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Gee la bala za'a, mam yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yamam sum zuo, la ani suŋa ti mam kiŋɛ. Se'ere n sɔi la, mam han ka kiŋɛ Tɛ'ɛla la kan wa'an ya zi'an na. Gee mam han kiŋɛ, n wan tum en ya zi'an na.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: A han wa'am na, a wan bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba baŋɛ ti ba tuue mɛ la bamam n ti'ihɛ ti be'em, tuuntɔɔ vom, la gakerɛ n de sɛla la.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: Ba ka mi' be'em n de sɛla, beni zuo ba ka nam mam.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Ba ka mi' tuuntɔɔ vom n de sɛla, beni zuo, mam ye' la Sɔ la zi'an, ti ya kan le nyɛ mam.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Ba me ka mi' gakerɛ n de sɛla, beni zuo, ba pun gakɛ be'em daana la en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo la mɛ.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: Yɛla zo'e zo'e boi ti n wan yele ya, gee nananawa la, la wan zo'e gaŋɛ ya tarega.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Gee se'em en de Sia yelemɛŋerɛ la han ta wa'am na, a wan gu'ura ya ti ya mina yelemɛŋerɛ la za'a. Se'ere n sɔi la, a ka yeti a tɔgera la a mɛŋa yetɔgum, gee a wan tɔgɛ la sɛla woo ti a wum Yinɛ zi'an, ti a wan muulɛ bo' ya yele sɛ'a n wan wa'am na.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: A wan bo' mam pɛka, se'ere n sɔi la, a wan dikɛ la sɛla n de mam dinɛ, muulɛ bo' ya.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Sɛla woo ti Sɔ la tara dela mam dinɛ. Bala zuo ti mam yeti, a wan dikɛ la sɛla n de mam dinɛ, muulɛ bo' ya la.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: La han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han le iŋɛ fiiwa ya wan nyɛ mam.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Ti a zahereba la basɛba yɛta taaba yeti, De vuurɛ dela deni ti a yele tu yeti, La han iŋɛ fiiwa ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han iŋɛ fiiwa ya wan le nyɛ mam. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam lebere la Sɔ la zi'an.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Ti ba yɛta taaba yeti, Yetɔgebuna wa vuurɛ dela dinɛ La han iŋɛ fiiwa? Tu ka mi' en tɔgeri sɛla yele.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Yezu baŋɛ mɛ ti, ba boti ba soke en la se'ere, bala ti a yele ba yeti, Ya sɔheri mɛ la taaba, de vuurɛ n de se'em, la mam yeti, La han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya kan le nyɛ mam, gee la han iŋɛ fiiwa, ya wan le nyɛ mam la.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, ti ya wan kele, gee si'im, gee ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba nyɛɛna. Ya suure wan sagum, gee ya susageŋɔ la wan ta lebege nyɛɛma.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Pɔka han virege, la ni wuna ka mɛ, beni zuo, a dɔgum saŋa n paaɛ. Gee bia la han diŋɛ lu na, a ka le tɛra too la, beni zuo, a nyɛɛni mɛ la eŋa n tari asaala wa'an timpaberɛ wa zuo na la.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: Bala, la wuni ya mɛ nananawa, gee n wan le lebe ya zi'an na, ti ya suya pirɛ la nyɛɛma, ti nɛra ka bɔna wan to'e ya nyɛɛŋɔ la ya zi'an.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: De daarɛ, ya kan soke mam se'ere. Yelemɛŋerɛ yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta ya wa, n yeti ya ti, ya han soke sɛla mam Sɔ la zi'an mam yu'urɛ puan, a wan dikɛ la bo' ya.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Wa'am na na paaɛ zina, ya ka soke en sɛla mam yu'urɛ wa puan. Soke ya gee ya wan to'e, ti ya nyɛɛma la pirɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Mam yeti ya yele ana wa me la yetɔgum biŋere puan. Gee saŋa la kini na, ti n kan le tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya yetɔge biŋere puan, gee n wan kɔ'ɔm tɔgera Sɔ la tuuntɔɔ bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: De daarɛ ya wan soke sɛla woo la mam yu'urɛ puan. Mam ka yeti mam wan ze'ele ya na'arɛ soke sɛla Sɔ la zi'an bo' ya.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, Sɔ la mɛŋa nɔŋɛ ya mɛ, la yamam n nɔŋɛ mam, gee nam ti mam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na la.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: Mam ze'ele la Sɔ la zi'an sige timpaberɛ wa zuo na, gee le yeti n bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee lebe n Sɔ la zi'an.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Ti a zahereba la yele en yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, fum nyaa tɔgeri la tuuntɔɔ, fum ka le tɔgera biŋera.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Nananawa, tu mi' ti fum mi' sɛla woo, ka ɛɛra nɛra ti a soke fum se'ere. Ŋwana wa ti tumam nam ti fum ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Ti Yezu soke ba yeti, Ya nyaa nam ya?.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Saŋa la kini na, ka pun paaɛ mɛ, ti ba wan dige ya ti ya yiregɛ, ti nɛra woo kule a yire a yire, gee bahɛ mam n ma'a. Gee la bala za'a, n kan bɔna n ma'a, beni zuo, Sɔ la n boi la mam.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: N yeti ya dina wa, ti ya tara suma'ahum mam puan. Han dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo, ya wan nyɛ mɛlehegɔ. Gee da bahɛ ya ti dabeem tara ya, gee mam pun nyaŋɛ timpaberɛ wa mɛ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yezu n tɔgɛ yele ana ba'ahɛ la, a gorege tɔ la saazuo, gee yeti, N Sɔ, saŋa la paaɛ ya. Pɛgɛ fum Dayua wa, ti Dayua la me pɛgɛ hɔ.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Fum n bo' mam kpe'eŋo ti n sɔna asaala woo, la n dikɛ vomkanba'ahɛ bo' sɛba woo ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Ŋwana n de vomkanba'ahɛ la, ti saaleba baŋɛ fum, fum se'em n de yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ wa ma'a, gee me baŋɛ Yezu Kiresta, se'em ti fum tum na la.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Mam pɛgɛ hɔ timpaberɛ wa zuo, la mam ba'ahɛ tuunse'ere ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: Bala nananawa n Sɔ, bahɛ ti n nyɛ pɛka fum nɛŋan. Pɛka sɛka ti n daa pun tara fum nɛŋan gee ti timpaberɛ wa baŋɛ naam la.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Mam dikɛ fum yu'urɛ wa pa'alɛ sɛba ti fum looe ba bo' mam timpaberɛ wa zuo la. Ba dela fum dinɛ, gee ti fum dikɛ ba bo' mam, ti ba me gurɛ fum yelehum la kankaŋi.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Nananawa, ba mi' suŋa ti sɛla woo ti fum dikɛ bo' mam sirum ze'ele la fum zi'an na.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, yelehum la ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la, mam dikɛ bo' ba mɛ, ti ba me to'e bu, gee mina yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti mam sirum ze'ele fum zi'an na. Ba me nam ya ti, fum n tum mam na.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: Mam pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra la bamam, Gee mam ka pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba. Mam pu'uhi bɔ'ɔra la sɛba ti fum dikɛ bo' mam, ban de fum dinɛ la zuo.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: Mam bunɔ za'a dela fum bunɔ. ti fum bunɔ dɛna mam bunɔ. Bama puan ti mam nyɛ pɛka.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Nananawa, mam kan le bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee bamam kelum bɔna la ka puan. Gee ti mam yɛ'ɛha fum zi'an na. N Sɔ Fa'a daana, gura ba fum yu'urɛ se'ere ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la puan, ti ba dɛna buyina wuu tumam n de buyina se'em la.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Mam n boi la ba la, mam gu'uri ba mɛ la yu'urɛ la ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la. Mam gu'uri ba mɛ, ti ba ayima ka bɔregɛ, han dagena se'em ti la gã'a na ti a bɔregɛ ti gulehegɔ la wa'an asira na la.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Gee nananawa, mam ye' la fum zi'an na, gee tɔgera yele ana timpaberɛ wa zuo, ti ba tara mam nyɛɛma la ti a pirɛ ba paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Mam bo' ba fum yelehum la, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo yaaɛ ba, la bamam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, wuu mam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ se'em la.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: Mam ka pu'uheri ti fum fooe ba yehe timpaberɛ wa zuo, gee mam pu'uheri ti fum gu' ba suŋa suŋa be'em daana wa zi'an.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Ba dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, wuu mam n dagi timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ se'em la.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: Pee ba ze'ele la yelemɛŋerɛ. Fum yelehum la n de yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Wuu fum n tum mam timpaberɛ wa zuo na se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam me tuna ba timpaberɛ wa puan.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Bama zuo ti mam pee n mɛŋa, ti bamam me nyɛ peere yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: N ka pu'uhɛ bɔ'ɔra la bamam ma'a, gee sɛba woo me n wan ta nam mam doohe ba yelehum muulegɔ puan la.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: Ba za'a me dɛna buyina, wuu fum Sɔ wa n boi la mam se'em la, ti mam me bɔna la fum se'em la, bamam me bɔna la tu bala mɛa, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba nam ti fum n tum mam na.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: Pɛka sɛka ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la, kaŋa mɛa ti mam dikɛ bo' ba, ti ba dɛna buyina wuu tumam n de buyina se'em la.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: Mam bɔna la ba, ti fum bɔna la mam, ti bamam me naaɛ taaba bi'ɛ kahegɛ dɛna buyina, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba baŋɛ ti fum tum mam na, gee me nɔŋɛ ba wuu fum nɔŋɛ mam se'em la.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: N Sɔ, mam le bɔta ti, nɛresɛba ti fum dikɛ bo' mam wa ta lagum bɔna la mam n bɔŋa zi'an la puan, la ba nyɛ na'ahegɔ seko ti fum mɛŋa dikɛ bo' mam, doohe la fum nɔŋɛ mam se'em gee ti timpaberɛ wa baŋɛ naam la zuo.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: N Sɔ tuntɔɔdaana, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka mi' hɔ, gee mam mi' hɔ. Bana me mi' ti fum n tum mam na.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: Mam tɔgɛ fum yu'urɛ la yele pa'alɛ ba, gee wan kelum tɔgɛ de yele pa'alɛ ba, ti nɔŋerɛ se'ere ti fum nɔŋɛ mam la wan bɔna ba puan, ti mam me bɔna la ba.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Ti Palete to'e Yezu, bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' en paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Ti pɛntiireba la pa'am gu'uhi muŋa vugele a zuo, gee yɛ' en fu mɔlegɔ.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Gee kina a nɛŋan na ɛrigera en yeti, Naa,Yuuduma naba wa! Gee ni lobe a pɛka.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Ti Palete le yehe na yele ba yeti, Bihɛ ya, n wan tari en yehe na bo' ya, ti ya mina ti mam ka nyɛ tuure se'ere a puan.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Ti Yezu yehe na, vuga gu'uhi muŋa la, gee yɛ fumɔlegɔ. Ti Palete yele nɛreba la yeti, Bihɛ ya, buraa la n ŋwana.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Malema kiinduma la, la gureba la n nyɛ Yezu la, ba taheri mɛ yeti, Kpa' ya en, kpa' ya en. Ti Palete yele ba yeti, Ya mɛhi tari en ta kpa', gee mam ka nyɛ a tuure.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Ti Yuuduma la lerege naba wa yeti, Tumam tari la tu ze'elego, ti ze'elego la puan, a dela a ki, la eŋa n yeti eŋa dela Yinɛ dayua la.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Palete n wum dina la, dabeem n malum kɛ̃ en gana yia la.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Ti a lebe kɛ̃ a yire la puan ha, le ta soke Yezu yeti, Fum yehe la bɛha na? Ti Yezu ka lerege en se'ere.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Ŋwana ti Palete yele en yeti, Fum ka boti fum tɔgɛ bo' mam? Fum ka mi' ti mam tari paŋa wan to'e fum bahɛ, gee le tari paŋa wan bahɛ ti ba kpa' hɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum kan ta'am iŋɛ mam sɛla, han dagena saazuo ha n bo' fum sore. Bala, se'em daana n dikɛ mam iŋɛ fum nu'uhen la, tum la be'em kãtɛ.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: Yehe bilam, Palete kɔ'ɔm zigera mɛ ti a to'e Yezu bahɛ fai, gee ti Yuuduma la me kɔ'ɔm tahera bahɛ na yeti, Naba, fum han bahɛ buraa kana wa, fum dagi Siiza zɔ. Se'em woo en iŋɛ a mɛŋa naba, pageri mɛ la Siiza.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Palete n wum yetɔgebuna wa la, a tari Yezu yehe me na, yiŋa na, gee zi'ire gakerɛ kuka la zuo, zɛ'sɛka ti ba yi'ira kuga zinzaka la, bii Heburu puan, Gabata la.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: La wa dɛna la azɔɛtole maaheŋɔ daarɛ, wuntɛɛn-sigelika bɔba, ti Palete yele Yuuduma la yeti, Ya naba la n ŋwana.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Ti ba kaahɛ bahɛ na yeti, Tari ya buraa kana wa kiŋɛ ha, tari ya buraa kana wa kiŋɛ ha, Kpa' ya en. Ti Palete soke ba yeti, N kpa' ya naba la? Ti malema kiinduma la lerege Palete la yeti, Tumam ka tari naba sɛka, han dagena Siiza ma'a.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Ti Palete dikɛ Yezu iŋɛ ba nu'uhen, ti ba tari en ta kpa'. Ti ba nyaa to'e Yezu.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: ti a zi a pumpuuredɔɔ to'e sore kiŋɛ bala ta paaɛ zi'an ti ba yi'ira zuwama la, gee yi'ira ka Heburu puan Golgota la.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Bilam ti ba kpa' Yezu, gee kpa' nɛreba basɛba me, kayima a zuɔ, kayima me a gɔbega, ti Yezu bɔna ba tiŋasuka.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Ti Palete bahɛ ti ba gulehɛ sɛla iŋɛ ta kpa' labelɛ Yezu pumpuuredɔɔ la zuin yeti, Yezu, Nazareti buraa,Yuuduma naba la.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Ti Yuuduma la zo'e zo'e kãalɛ gulehegɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, zi'an ti ba kpa' Yezu la lɛm mɛ la tinkãtɛ puan, ti gulehegɔ la gulehɛ Heburu, Laatin la Giriki yetɔgum puan.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Ti Yuuduma malema kiinduma la kiŋɛ ta yele Palete yeti, Gulehegɔ la ka suni ti ku yeti, Yuuduma naba, gee Buraa kana wa yeti kaŋa dela Yuuduma naba. .

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Ti Palete lerege ba yeti, Sɛla ti mam gulehɛ la, n gulehɛ ya.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Pɛntiireba la n kpa' Yezu ba'ahɛ la, ba dikɛ futo la mɛ tɔrɛ sugum nuuren bunaahi, ti pɛntiira woo dikɛ fuo, fuo. Gee nyaa dikɛ a fuwoko la. Fuwoko wa dela mune, ti pɛ'a ka bɔna ku puan, gee ba dikɛ la tanɛ kɔ'ɔm wugɛ kɔ ze'ele zuin ta paaɛ tiŋa.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Ti pɛntiireba la yele taaba yeti, Da bahɛ ya ti tu ahegɛ fukuna wa, gee bahɛ ya ti tu ŋmɛ' gbaŋa la tu bihɛ se'em n wan di. Dina wa iŋɛ ti la doohe mɛ la gulehegɔ la n yeti, Ba dikɛ la mam futo tɔrɛ bo' ba mɛhi, gee ŋmɛ' gbaŋa mam fuo zuo la.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Lan bala ti pɛntiireba wa iŋɛ la. Ban iti ŋwana woo la, ti Yezu ma, la a ma suuɔ, Mɛɛri n de Kalopase pɔga, la Mɛɛri Magedalin ze' mɛ lɛm pumpuuredɔɔ la ti ba kpa' Yezu ku zuo la.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yezu n nyɛ a ma la, la a zahera se'em ti a ba' nɔŋɛ la n ze' lɛm a ma la, ti a yele a ma la yeti, Pɔka wa, fum dayua n bala.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Gee yele a zahera la me yeti, Fum ma n bala. Yehe de daarɛ, ti Yezu zahera wa tari Yezu ma la kule a yire.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Dina pooren, Yezu n baŋɛ ti sɛla woo tum ba'ahɛ la, , ti a yeti, Ko'nyuuro n tari mam.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Bala ma'a la ti dugela n ze' bilam la daam kɛka, ti ba zɔ kalam ta dikɛ nyunhunyuuŋa yile tum daam kɛka la puan, dikɛ vile dibega, nyaa zɛkɛ ta ita a nuuren.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Lan ta kalum Yezu nuurɛ la, ti Yezu yeti, La ba'ahɛ ya. Gee nyaa suure a zuo, vo'ohe a vo'ohum za'a.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: De daarɛ n de azɔɛtole maaheŋɔ daarɛ la zuo, Yuuduma la ka boti ba bahɛ ti ihi la yula pumpuure-dɔɔrɔ la zuo vo'ohego daarɛ la, yele yele wuu vo'ohego dabeherɛ dinɛ n boi de tɔka la. Bala ti ba kiŋɛ Palete zi'an ta suhɛ ti ba kɔ' ban kpa' sɛba la nama gee yagehɛ ba pumpuure-dɔɔrɔ la zuto.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Bala ti pɛntiireba la wa'am na, kɔ' buraahi la yia daana la nama, loŋe kɔ' kayima la ti ba kpa' la en la nama.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Gee ban paaɛ Yezu zi'an la, ba nyɛ ti Yezu pun ki mɛ, ti ba ka kɔ' eŋa nama.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Gee pɛntiireba ayima n tugum dikɛ a kanɛ lu' Yezu lugerɛ, ti bilam bilam ti ziim la ko'om ka'ɛ na.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Se'em daana n daa ze' bini gee nyɛ ti yele ana wa iŋɛ la n bala n yeti hɔ, ti fum nam. En yeti se'ere la dela asira. A mi' ti a yeti la yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Yele ana wa woo iŋɛ ti gulehegɔ la n wa'am asira na. La gulehɛ yeti, A kɔberɛ la deyina me kan kɔ'ɛ.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Gee le gulehɛ zi'an kayima yeti, Ba wan kaaɛ biha la se'em ti ba lu' a lugerɛ la kanɛ la.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Yele ana woo n tole la, ti Arimatiya buraa yu'urɛ n yi'iri Yosefe, se'em me n de Yezu zahereba la ayima gee sugera a mɛŋa Yuuduma la dabeem zuo la, en kiŋɛ ta suhɛ Palete ti a bahɛ ti a yakɛ Yezu iŋa la kiŋɛ ta pi'. Ti Palete bo' en sore, ti a nyaa kiŋɛ ta yakɛ Yezu iŋa la.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Ti Nikodemose, se'em n tabelɛ kiŋɛ Yezu zi'an yu'uŋɔ la, tari sɛla n tari yuuŋo suŋɔ ti ba yi'ira ba mɛɛ la alose wa'am na, ti ba tibehum dɛna wuu kugetambiliŋa.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Ti ba kiŋɛ ta yakɛ Yezu iŋa la, pɔbɛ iŋa la, la yuuŋo suŋɔ lɔgerɔ la tanɛ pɛɛlega n ani suŋa puan, wuu Yuuduma kum pi'an malema n de se'em la.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Bala ma'a la ti va'am bila n lɛm zi'an ti ba kpa' Yezu la, ti yɔɔ bɔna va'am bila wa puan ti ba nan ka tabelɛ laaɛ' nɛra ku puan.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Didaarɛ la n de Yuuduma azɔɛtole maaheŋɔ dabeherɛ la zuo, ti yɔɔ la me lɛm la, ti ba tari Yezu paaɛ bilam biŋe.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ,Dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ, ti la nan ka yilegɛ ba'ahɛ, ti Mɛɛri Magedalin wa'am yɔɔ la puan, nyɛ ti ba yehe tansɛka n dikɛ yu' yɔɔ la nuurɛ la mɛ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ti a zɔ kiŋɛ Simɔn Pita, la Yezu zahera ayima la, se'em ti Yezu ba' nɔŋɛ la, zi'an ta yele ba yeti, Ba dikɛ Daana iŋa la yɔɔ la puan mɛ. Tu me ka mi' ban tari en ta biŋe zi'an.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Ti Pita la Yezu zahera ayima la yehe yɛ'ɛha yɔɔ la puan.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Ti ba bayi' la zɔta, ti a yima la zɔ gaŋɛ Pita wa'am na paaɛ yɔɔ la puan yia.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Paaɛ suure bihɛ yɔɔ la puan ha, nyɛ ti tanɛ pɛɛlega la n dikɛ ni pɔ̃bɛ kum la, ga' yɔɔ la puan ha, gee a ka kɛ̃ yɔɔ la puan.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita me paaɛ pooren na, tole kɛ̃ yɔɔ la puan, nyɛ ti tanɛ pɛɛlega la ga' mɛ.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: Ti tanɛ se'ere n dikɛ vile Yezu zuo la me ka gã'a lɛm tanpɛɛlega la, gee de me kilum de mɛŋa gã'a la kinkɛleŋa ha.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Pooren ti Yezu zahera se'em n zɔ paaɛ yɔɔ la puan yia na la, me kɛ̃ yɔɔ la puan ha, me nyɛ bala mɛa, gee nam.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Gee ŋwana ma'a wa la, ti ba ka bɔkɛ gulehegɔ la n yeti, a wan ihige a kum puan la.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Ti zahereba wa lebe kule ba yiya.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Gee Mɛɛri Magedalin yee zɛ'a la yɔɔ la yiŋa kɛla. En kele la, ta suure bihɛ yɔɔ la puan ha.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: nyɛ ti suŋereba bayi' yɛ fupɛɛla, zi'an zi'an ti Yezu iŋa ga' ni la, ayima zi'an a zuin bɔba, ti ayima zi'an a nama bɔba.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Pɔka wa, beni ti fum kɛla? Ti a yele ba yeti, Ba dikɛ n Daana iŋa la mɛ, n me ka mi' ban dikɛ ka ta biŋe zi'an.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a ŋmɛregɛ mɛ, ti Yezu n bala zɛ'a bini, gee a ka baŋɛ ti Yezu n bala.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Ti Yezu soke en yeti, Pɔka wa, beni ti fum kɛla? Fum eere la ani? Ti pɔka la ti'ihɛ ti a dela va'am la daana, nyaa yele en yeti, Naa, la han dɛna la fum n dikɛ iŋa la, pa'alɛ mam fum malum biŋe ka zi'an, ti n kiŋɛ ta dikɛ ka kiŋɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Mɛɛri Ti a ŋmɛregɛ yele en Heburu yetɔgum puan yeti, Araboni! .

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Da nan gi' mam, se'ere n sɔi la n nan ka zom sɔ la zi'an. Gee kiŋɛ ta yele n suurɔ la yeti, N yeti n kiŋɛ la n sɔ la n de ya sɔ la, la mam Yinɛ la n de ya Yinɛ la zi'an. .

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Ti Mɛɛri Magedalin zɔ kiŋɛ ta muulɛ bo' Yezu zahereba la yeti, Mam nyɛ Daana la. Gee yele ba en yele en sɛla woo la.
