eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ zaanuurɛ, ti Yezu zahereba la lagehɛ bɔna yire deyima puan, gee yu' yire la nayahi za'a, dabeem n tari ba Yuuduma la zuo. Ba ta yeti ba bihɛ ti Yezu kɛ̃ na zɛ'a la ba, pu'uhɛ ba yeti, Suma'ahum bɔna la ya.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: En pu'uhɛ ba ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a tiɛ la a nu'uhi pa'alɛ ba, gee pa'alɛ ba a lugerɛ la me. Ti a dɔleba la nyɛɛna la ban le nyɛ Daana la.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Suma'ahum bɔna la ya. Wuu sɔ la n tum mam na se'em la, bala mɛa ti mam me tuna ya.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: En yele ba ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a pebele la sabeeo tuule ba, gee yele ba yeti, To'e ya Fa'a Sia la.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Ya han bahɛ se'em woo be'em bo' en, la dela bala bo' a daana. Ya han ka bahɛ se'em be'em bo' en, la dela bala mɛa bo' a daana.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Gee ŋwana woo la, ti Tɔmasi , dɛna Yezu zahereba la ayima la, daa ka boi la ba, Yezu wa'an ba zi'an na la.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: Bala zuo, zahereba bana la en ta yele en yeti, Tumam nyɛ Daana la mɛ la. A yele ba yeti, Han dagena ti mam nyɛ la kpa'ahi yiha la a nu'uhen, la n dikɛ n nu'dibila doohe kpa'ahi la yiha la, gee le dikɛ n nu'dibila sa'abɛ a lugerɛ la ti kanɛ la daa lu' la, Mam kan nam.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Ti la le paaɛ dabeha ayopɔi, ti Yezu zahereba la le lagehɛ yire la puan, ti Tɔmasi bɔna bini. Ti ba yu' yire la kulehi la za'a. La ban yu' kulehi, Yezu wa'am me na, zɛ'a la ba, gee yele ba yeti, Suma'ahum bɔna la ya.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Gee nyaa yele Tɔmasi yeti, Dikɛ fum nu'dibila doohe n nu'o wa puan bihɛ, gee tiɛ fum nu'o doohe n lugerɛ wa. Da bu'ura, gee nam.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Ti Tɔmasi lerege en yeti, Mam Daana la mam Yinɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Fum nyaa nam ya la fum nyɛ mam la zuo? Sum boi mɛ la sɛba n ka nyɛ gee kelum nam.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yezu tum nyalema mɛ kekeko a zahereba la ninin, ti ba ka gulehɛ yele aŋa pa'ahɛ gɔŋɔ wa puan.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Gee ba gulehɛ yele ana wa, ti la bahɛ ti fum wa'am na nam ti, Yezu n de Kiresta la, Yinɛ Dayua la. Ti naŋa la puan, ya wan tara vom a yu'urɛ la puan.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Yele ana woo n tole la, ti Yezu le niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a zahereba la, Tiberiyasi Ateko la nuuren. En niiɛ a mɛŋa se'em n ŋwana.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: Zahereba la lagehɛ la taaba bɔna zi'an kayina, Simɔn Pita, Tɔmasi ti ba yi'ira liiberɛ la, Nataniyɛl n yehe Kana tinkãtɛ n boi Galilii sɔ'ɔlum puan la, Zebedii dayɔɔhi, la Yezu zahereba bayi'.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita yele ba yeti, Mam ye' la zim nyɔka. Ti bana la me woo yele en yeti, Tumam wan doohe hɔ. Ti ba za'a yehe ta zom uureŋɔ, sige ko'om la puan. Gee yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ la, ba ka nyɔkɛ sɛ'sɛla.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Beere n ta yilegɛ la, ti Yezu wa'am na zɛ'a ko'om la nɔgbene ha, gee ba ka baŋɛ ti eŋa n bala.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Ti Yezu yi' ba na soke yeti, N kɔma wa, ya ka nyɔkɛ sɛla, bii ya nyɔkɛ ya? Ti ba lerege en yeti. Aayi.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Lobe ya yuka la bahɛ uureŋɔ la zuɔ bɔba, gee ya wan nyɛ ziim. Ti ba sirum lobe ba yuka la bala, koŋe ba ve'e, se'ere n sɔi la, zim la ba zo'e mɛ yuka la puan.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Ti zahera se'em ti Yezu ba' nɔŋɛ la yele Pita yeti, Daana n bala. Simɔn Pita n wum ti Daana n bala la, ti a dikɛ fuo yɛ, se'ere n sɔi la a ze' ni la beele. Nyaa ɛkɛ lu ko'om la puan.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Gee ti a zahereba bana la yee uureŋɔ la puan dɔɔra na, vɛ'ɛra yuka la ti ka pirɛ la zim, bala ma'a ti ba ka zaaɛ la doone. La wan dɛna wuu mi' ihe kila pia.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ doone la, ba nyɛ ti bugum saana n ga' la bugum, ti zim paga bu zuo, ti boroboro me gã'a.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, tari ya zim se'e ti ya nan nyɔgɛ la i se'e na pa'ahɛ.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita lebe uureŋɔ la puan ta lagum ve'e yuka la dooe na, ti ka pirɛ la zim kãra ma'a n de zim kɔbega, la pihinuu la i ta'. Gee zim la n pun zo'e bala la, yuka la ka ahegɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Wa'am ya na sãɛ nuurɛ. A zahereba la ayima me ka soke en yeti, Fum dela ani? Se'ere n sɔi la, ba za'a baŋɛ ya ti a dela Daana la.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Ti Yezu nyaa dikɛ boroboro la bo' ba, gee dikɛ zim la me bo' ba bala mɛa.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Butã n ŋwana ti Yezu niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a zahereba la, en ihige kum puan na la.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Ban sãɛ ba nuurɛ la ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu yele Simɔn Pita yeti, Simɔn,Yɔn dayua wa, fum nɔŋɛ mam gana n zahereba bana wa? Ti Pita lerege yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, n Daana, fum mi' ti mam nɔŋɛ hɔ. Ti Yezu lebehe en yeti, Yi bihera n pebibehi wa.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Buyi' puan ti Yezu le yele Pita yeti, Simɔn, Yɔn dayua wa, fum nɔŋɛ mam? Ti Pita yele en yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, n Daana, fum mi' ti mam nɔŋɛ hɔ. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Tara n piihi wa suŋa.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Ti Yezu le yele en butã puan yeti, Simɔn,Yɔn dayua wa, fum nɔŋɛ mam? Ti Pita suure sagum, se'ere zuo, Yezu n yele en butã puan yeti, Fum nɔŋɛ mam? Ti a yele Yezu yeti, N Daana, fum mi' sɛla woo, fum mi' ti n nɔŋɛ hɔ. Ti Yezu yele en yeti, Bihera n piihi wa.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ti n yɛta fum wa. Fum daa nan ihigere na la, fum mɛŋa n ni bobe fum sɛganɛ yɛ'ɛha fum boti zi'an. Gee fum han ta kuregɛ, fum wan tiɛ fum nu'uhi, ti nɛre se'em wan lu' fum sɛganɛ, tari fum kiŋɛ zi'an ti fum kan bɔta ti fum kiŋɛ.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a yele Pita yeti, Dɔla mam.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Ti Pita ŋmɛregɛ nyɛ zahera sɛka ti Yezu ba' nɔŋɛ la n dole ba. Eŋa n daa zi' lɛm Yezu, zaanuurɛ dia saŋa la, daa soke yeti, Daana, ani n de' en yeti a koohe fum la.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Pita n nyɛ en la, a yele Yezu yeti, Daana, gee ena me.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Ti Yezu yele en yeti, La han dɛna mam suboolum ti a vɔna gura ti n ta lebe na, la nyɛ fum la bɛ? Dɔla mam.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: Ti la to'e zi'an woo nɛreba puan ti, Yezu zahera se'em ti a ba' nɔŋɛ la kan ki. Gee ti Yezu me ka yele en ti a kan ki, gee a yeti, La han dɛna mam sumboolum ti a vɔna gura ti n ta lebe na, la nyɛ fum la bɛ.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Yezu zahera ena n bala n tɔgeri yɛla wa, gee gulehɛ a wa. Tu mi' ti yele ana wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Gee yɛla a sɛ'a zo'e zo'e me boi ha, ti Yezu tum, ti ba han yele ni ti, ba gulehɛ a za'a yele, yele ta ba'ahɛ, mam mi ti timpaberɛ wa tari ka ni gɔnɔ seto ti ba wan gulehɛ ni la.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yezu n tɔgɛ yele ana wa ba'ahɛ la, a yehe mɛ la a zahereba la yakɛ Kidiron bɔka la n zoti dɔla bɔ'seko puan ti va'am pika bɔna bini la, ti eŋa la a zahereba la ta kɛ̃ de puan.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Yudasi se'em n pun koohe Yezu la mi' zi'an wa puan mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, Yezu tabeli kina bini mɛ la a zahereba la za'a.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Bala ti Yudasi wa doohe la pɛntiireba tansugere, la gureba ti malema kiinduma la, la Farasii duma la tum la, ti ba wa'am va'am la puan na, la butolelehe, bugum, la zaba lɔgerɔ.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Ti Yezu n pun mina sɛla woo n yeti la iŋɛ la, tole nɛŋan na ze'ele, soke ba yeti, Ya eere la ani.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Ti ba lerege yeti, Yezu, Nazareti buraa la. Ti Yezu yele ba yeti, Mam ŋwana. Ti Yudasi se'em n koohe en la, ze' mɛ la ba.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yezu n yele ba yeti, Mam n ŋwana la, ba za'a gɛ̃rɛ mɛ apovura ta lu ha turururu.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Ti Yezu le soke ba yeti, Ani ti ya ɛɛra? Ti ba yeti, Yezu, Nazareti buraa la.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, Mam yele ya yeti, mam n ŋwana. Bala, ya han ɛɛra la mam, bahɛ ya ti nɛreba wa kiŋɛ.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: Dina wa dela la bahɛ ti a yetɔge sebo n yeti, N ka bɔregɛ sɛba ti fum dikɛ bo' mam la la ayima me, wa'an asira na.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita, se'em n tari su'a a zi'an la, fooe ka na, kɛ̃' malema kiima ayima yameŋa zuɔ tuberɛ fike. Yameŋa wa yu'urɛ yi'ire la Malekus.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Ti Yezu yele Pita yeti, Dikɛ fum su'a la lebehe su' ka su'a zi'an. N dagi la n nyu mɛlehegɔ wanɛ se'ere puan ti mam Sɔ dikɛ bo' mam la.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Ŋwana ti pɛntiireba la, la ba kpɛ'ɛnduma, la Yuuduma gureba la nyaa yɔkɛ Yezu lu' en.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: Yia, ba tari en kiŋɛ la Anase n de Kaifase dɛɛma la nɛŋan. Kaifase wa dɛna la malema kiima yuum dinɛ puan.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Kaifase kana n bala tabelɛ ka'am Yuuduma la yeti, La ani suŋa ti nɛre yinɛ ki bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Ti Simɔn Pita, la Yezu zahereba la ayima, dɔla Yezu poore. Yezu zahera ayima ti malema kiima la mina en, eŋa en doohe la Yezu ta kɛ̃ malema Kiima wa zinzaka la puan.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: gee ti Pita zɛ'a yiŋa, nayaŋa la nuuren. Ti Yezu zahera se'em ti malema kiima la mina la, yehe na tɔgɛ bo' pɔka n guri nayaŋa la, gee tari Pita kɛ̃ zinzaka la puan na.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Ti pɔka la soke Pita yeti, Fum dagi Yezu zahereba la ayima, bii fum de mɛ? Ti Pita yeti, Mam dagi ba puan.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Yamehi la la gureba la dikɛ la bugum saana na tuurɛ bugum, se'ere n sɔi la la ma'arɛ mɛ, ti nɛra woo nyaa kaaɛ bugum la ɔɔha. Ti Pita me zɛ'a la ba ɔɔha.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Ti malema kiima la nyaa soke Yezu a zahereba la, la a pa'alegɔ la yele.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Mam tɔgera la peelumen bɔ'ɔra timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba woo. Saŋa woo, mam ni bɔna zamehegɔ yiya, la pu'uhegɔ yiya puan mɛ, zi'an ti Yuuduma za'a ni lagehɛ taaba la. Mam ka sugɛ yele se'ere.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Wani ti fum sɔkera mam? Soke sɛba n wum sɛla ti n yele ba la. Ba mi' mam n yele ba sɛla.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yezu n yele ŋwana wa la, ti pu'uhegɔ yire gureba la ayima n ze' lɛm en, lobe Yezu pɛka gee yeti, Fum lebehe la malema kiima la bala.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Mam han tɔgɛ tuue mɛ, pa'alɛ mam n tuure la, gee n han tɔgɛ la tuuntɔɔ, beni zuo ti fum ŋmɛ' mam.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Ti Anase bahɛ ti ba bobe Yezu bala, tari en ta bo' Kaifase, malema kiima la.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Ti Pita zɛ'a bugum la zi'an ɔɔha, ti ba soke en yeti, Fum me dagi Yezu zahereba la ayima bii fum de' mɛ? Ti Pita ki'ihɛ gee yeti, Aayi, mam dagi.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Ti malema kiima la yameŋa kayima, se'em suuɔ ti Pita kɛ̃' a tuberɛ fike la, soke Pita yeti, Mam ka nyɛ ni hɔ va'am la puan la en.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Ti Pita le ki'ihɛ. Ti saŋa kaŋa mɛa puan la, ti nɔdɔɔ kaahɛ.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Ti ba tari Yezu ze'ele Kaifase zi'an, kiŋɛ Palete yikãtɛ la puan. Bala ma'a ti la dela bulika yu'uŋɔ yu'uŋɔ. Ban paaɛ la, Yuuduma mɛhi ka kɛ̃ naba yire la puan, ti ba da galum ba mɛhi, gee la ba ta'am di azɔɛtole de'eŋo la.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Bala ti Palete yehe paaɛ ba, soke ba yeti, Beni tuure ti ya tara la buraa wa.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Buraa wa han dagi ni tuunfi'iha daana, tu kan tari ni ka na bo' hɔ.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Ti Palete yele ba yeti, Ya mɛhi tari en kiŋɛ, ta gakɛ en ya mɛhi, doohe ya ze'elego la puan. Ti Yuuduma wa lebehe yeti, Tumam ka tari sore ti tu ku nɛra.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Ti Palete le kɛ̃ a yire la puan ha, gee yi' Yezu paaɛ a nɛŋan na soke en yeti, Fum dela Yuuduma wa naba.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum soke dina wa la fum mɛa bii, nɛreba en yele fum mam yele.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Ti Palete lebehe en yeti, Mam dagi Yuuduma nɛra, bii n de mɛ? Fum mɛŋa tiŋa wa nɛreba, la malema kiinduma n tari fum na bo' mam. Fum iŋɛ la bem.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Mam sɔ'ɔlum la ka boi timpaberɛ wa zuo. Mam sɔ'ɔlum han boi ni timpaberɛ wa zuo, mam dɔleba wan zaberɛ ni ti Yuuduma wa kan nyɔkɛ ni mam na bo' fum. Gee yelemɛŋerɛ la dela mam sɔ'ɔlum la ka boi timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Ti Palete soke en yeti, Bala, Fum dela naba? Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, Fum yele ya ti mam dela naba. Yele dina wa zuo ti ba dɔgɛ mam, yele dina wa zuo ti mam sige timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti n tɔgɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la yele. Se'em woo n de yelemɛŋerɛ la nɛra, kelehe mam yetɔgum mɛ.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Ti Palete soke en yeti, Beni n de yelemɛŋerɛ? Palete n tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a le yehe la Yuuduma la zi'an na, yele ba yeti, Mam ka nyɛ nɛra la tuure.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Gee ya tari la ya malema kayima, ti n ni yehe nɛra ayima paka deen bo' ya azɔɛtole saŋa woo. Bala, ya boti n yehe Yuuduma naba wa bahɛ bo' ya.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Ti Yuuduma la za'a kaahɛ bahɛ na yeti, Aayi, dagi burakana wa, yehe Barabase bahɛ! Barabase fãari mɛ.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Yia gɔŋɔ la puan, Tiyofilose, n daa tɔgɛ yele sɛ'a woo ti Yezu iŋɛ la, gee pa'alɛ, pɔhera sansɛka ti a pɔhɛ a tuuma la.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: ta paaɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti a zom saazuo la. Gee ti a zom saazuo la, a daa bo' la pa'alegɔ Fa'a Sia la puan, bo' a tuntuneba la ti a mɛŋa looe la.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: A namehegɔ la pooren, Yezu niiɛ a mɛŋa la vore, yelemɛŋerɛ sadɔtɔ zo'e zo'e puan pa'alɛ a tuntuneba la, niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ ba dabeha pihinaahi puan, tɔgɛ la ba Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yele.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: En wa vo'e na bɔna la ba la, a bo' ba la nuurɛ yeti, da yehe ya Yerusalɛm wa puan, gee guri ya nɔbiŋere se'ere ti Sɔ biŋe la. A yeti, Dina wa n bala ti ya wum ti n tɔgera de yele yeti.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ Yɔn sɔɔri mɛ la ko'om, gee la han dɛna yamam, ba wan sɔ yamam mɛ la Fa'a Sia la, la kan yuue, yehe zina.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Ban daa ta lagum taaba la, ti ba soke Yezu yeti, Daana, sankani ti fum yeti fum wan le malum sɛ sɔ'ɔlum la bo' Izirale.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Ti a lebehe ba yeti, La dagi ya tuunɛ ti ya mina saŋa, bii sɛla n wan iŋɛ. Sɔ la mɛŋa en ze'ele yele aŋa la a mɛŋa kpe'eŋo.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Gee yamam wan ta to'e paŋa, Fa'a Sia la han ta sige ya zuo na, ti ya dɛna mam aze' nyɛ duma, Yerusalɛm, la Yudiya za'a puan, la Samariya, la timpaberɛ wa luga za'a.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: En yele ŋwana ti ba biha en la, ba nyɛ la en pɔhɛ zɛkera saazuo, ti sagbanɛ wa'am na pi' en ti ba ka le nyɛta en.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Ban kelum zɛ'a biha saazuo ha zii la, ti a me zɔna la, ba yeti ba bihɛ ti buraahi siyi en yɛ fuwogero pɛɛla n bala zɛ'a la ba.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: Yele ba yeti, Galilii buraahi wa, ŋwani ti ya zɛ'a gɔra biha saazuo bala? Yezu wa ti ba dikɛ ya zi'an en ze'ele ya zi'an zom saazuo ha wa, wan wa'am la bala mɛa na wuu yan nyɛ en ti a zɔna saazuo ha se'em wa la.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Ti ba ze'ele Olive Zoore la n lɛm la Yerusalɛm wuu vo'ohego dabehere kinɛ la, lebe Yerusalɛm.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Ban ta paaɛ kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan la, ba zom la bɔ'seko n boi saazuo ha zi'an ti ba kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la. Pita, Yɔn, Yemesi, Anduru, Filipi, Tɔmasi, Batolomi, Matu, Alifeyusi dayua Yemesi, Simeyɔn ti ba yi'ira Zɛlɔti la, Yemesi dayua Yudasi.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bana za'a lagehɛ la taaba dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' pu'uhegɔ daarɛ woo, la pɔgehi sise'ehi, ti Yezu ma Mɛɛri lagum bɔna bini, la Yezu suurɔ la.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Dabeha aŋa mɛa puan ti Pita ihege ze'ele sɛba n nam bo' Yezu la n lagehɛ la tiŋasuka yele ba yeti.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: N suurɔ, gulehegɔ la dela ku wa'am asira na. Gulehegɔ seko ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgɛ doohe Deviti puan doohe Yudasi se'em n wan bɔna sɛba n yeti ba yɔkɛ Yezu la nɛŋa.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la a daa pa'ahɛ la tu puan, tara a tuunɛ lageŋɔ wa puan.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Buraa kana wa daa dikɛ a tuumbe'o ligeri la da' la zi'an, gee lu a liko tahɛ a tiŋasuka ti a puuren lɔgerɔ za'a ɔlegɛ yehe na.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Yerusalɛm nɛreba za'a mi' yele wa mɛ, bala zuo ti ba yi'ira a zi'an la ti a da la ba yetɔgum puan yeti, Hakɛledama, ti de vuurɛ dela la Ziim sɔlega.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, la gulehɛ bɔna Yuuma Gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, Bahɛ ti a yire la lebege daboo, Bahɛ ti nɛra nɛra da kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra de puan. Bahɛ ti nɛra ayima sooe a kinɔ kpe'eŋo la.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Bala zuo la, se'em daana n daa dole tu saŋawoo ti tu Daana Yezu daa bɔna la tɔ tu yɛhega la tu lɛbega na la.

eng: beginning from the baptism of John until the day when Jesus was taken from us. This person must become along with us a witness to his resurrection.
local: pɔhɛ Yɔn ko'sɔɔra la, tari bala ta paaɛ Yezu n wan zom saazuo la. Bana puan ayima n wan lagum pa'ahɛ tu puan lagum dɛna Yezu vo'ore la aze' nyɛ daana.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Bala zuo ti ba ku nuurɛ yehe buraahi siyi, kayima Yosefe, se'em ti ba yi'ira Basabase la, ti a yu'urɛ deyima dɛna Yusetuse la, la Matiyase.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Gee nyaa pu'uhɛ yeti, Daana, fum mi' nɛrawoo suure puan. Pa'alɛ tɔ buraahi siyi wa, ba se'em ti fum looe.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: ti a sooe ze'ele fum tuntuneba tuuma la fole la n gã' doohe la Yudasi n bire a poore bahɛ gee kiŋɛ a mɛŋa n boti zi'an la.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Ti ba bahɛ maanɛ kuga, ti la looe Matiyase, ti ba dikɛ en pa'ahɛ Yezu tuntuneba pia la ayima la puan.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti Pita la Yɔn yɛ'ɛha pu'uhegɔ yire la pu'uhegɔ saŋa, woo gogo kaareŋa butã wuntɛɛŋa bɔba.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Ti nɛreba zi buraa ti ba dɔgɛ ka ti ka dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ ka bilɛɛmen ha tari na. Nɛreba n ni zi buraa wa tari na ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la nayaŋa sɛka ti ba yi'ira ka Nayansuŋa la nuuren, ti a zi'an bilam la a ta'am suha sɛba n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la daare woo.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Buraa wa n ta nyɛ ti Pita la Yɔn boi bini ti ba kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan la, ti a suhɛ ba ti ba bo' en sɛla.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Ti Pita kpa' en biha zii, ti Yɔn me biha en bala, ta yele buraa la yeti, Biha tu na.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Ti Buraa wa kpa' biha, ba la ninmu'urɛ gee ti'iha la a wan to'e sɛla ba zi'an.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Gee Pita yele en yeti, Mam ka tari salempɛɛlega, bii salema, gee mam n tari sɛla n ŋwana ti mam dikera bɔ'ɔra fum wa. Yezu Kiresta n de Nazareti Buraa la yu'urɛ puan, ihege la fum kina.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Gee nyaa gurɛ kɔɔreŋɔ wa zuɔ nu'o ve'e en ihege ze'ele, ti bilam bilam ti paŋa wa'am kɔɔreŋɔ wa nama la a kigela puan na.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: ti a ɛkɛ saazuo lebe ze'ele, nyaa pɔhɛ kinɛ, nyaa doohe la Pita la Yɔn kɛ̃ Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, kina kaara, gee ɛgera la pupeelum gee pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Ti nɛreba la woo n nyɛ en ti a kini, gee pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Ba baŋɛ ti buraa kaŋa n bala ni zi'a Pu'uhegɔ yire la nayansuŋa la nuuren suhera la. Ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ ti ba zɛ'a yɛli la sɛla n iŋɛ buraa wa la.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Buraa wa kɔ'ɔm gi' Pita la Yɔn la bala kankaŋi, gee ti nɛreba la za'a zɔta yɛ'a ba zi'an, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ze'sɛka ti ba yi'ira Solomɔn zinzaka la puan, ti la dɛna yele pakerɛ bo' ba.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Pita n nyɛ nɛreba wa bala la, a yele ba yeti. Yamam Izirale buuri wa, ŋwani ti yele dina wa iŋɛ ya pakerɛ, bii ŋwani ti ya biha tumam wuu la dela tumam paŋa bii tu Yinɛ zoolum puan ti buraa wa ihege kina wa?.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: La dela Aberaham Yinɛ la, Aziki Yinɛ la, la Yakɔbe Yinɛ la. Tu sɔyaabeduma Yinɛ la en na'ahɛ a dayua Yezu, se'em ti ya daa yehe en tihɛ, ze'ele Palete nɛŋan zagehɛ en, la Palete n daa pun iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a bahɛ en la za'a.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: Yamam zagehɛ la Fa'a la tuuntɔɔ daana la gee tugum soke ti ba bo' ya nɛrekuura.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: ya tugum ku la vom daana la se'em ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum puan la. Yele dina wa ti tumam dɛna aze' nyɛ duma.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: La doohe la sakerɛ a yu'urɛ puan ti buraa wa ti ya nyɛta gee mina ka wa dɛna ka mɛŋa wa. Sakerɛ a yu'urɛ puan la en tibɛ en za'a za'a ya woo ninin wa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Nananawa n suurɔ, mam mi' ti ya daa tum la yɛmka'alum, wuu ya nɛhi duma la me n daa tum yɛmka'alum se'em la.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Ŋwana puan ti Yinɛ nɔtɔgeheba la en yuum purɛ yeti, a Kiresta la wan namehɛ la wa'am asira na.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Bala zuo la, tee ya, la ya ŋmɛregɛ ya mɛhi bo' Yinɛ, ti a piiɛ ya be'em woo bahɛ.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: ti fula fula saŋa ze'ele Daana mɛŋa zi'an sige ya zuo, ti a tum Kiresta se'em ti a looe bo' ya la, ti a wa'am ya zi'an na, se'em n de Yezu la.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Yezu se'em n wan bɔna saazuo gura sɛla woo maalegɔ seko ti Yinɛ tɔgɛ dɛ yuuman yuuman ha doohe a tuulum nɔtɔgeheba puan la.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Moose yuum yeti, Ya Daana Yinɛ la a mɛŋa wan yehe nɛra ya mɛŋa puan, ti a dɛna a nɔtɔgeha wuu mam wa. Kelehe ya sɛlawoo ti a wan yele ya.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: La wan ta dɛna se'em la dela, fum se'em woo n ka ta kɛleha nɔtɔgeha kaŋa la, ba wan vuuɛ n nɛreba la puan bahɛ.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Nɔtɔgeheba wa woo n zo'e se'em la, ze'ele Samuwɛle saŋa ta paaɛ sɛba n wa'am a pooren na la woo yuum purɛ dabeha wa yele mɛ.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Ya dela nɔtɔgeheba wa yaahi, la nɔpu'ulegɔ la ti Yinɛ yuum pu'ulɛ la ya yaabeduma la Aberaham yeti, Fum yaŋa puan ti buuri woo n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a wan nyɛ sum.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Yinɛ n wa looe a yameŋa wa la, a pɔhɛ tum en la yamam zi'an ti ya nyɛ sum, doohe la en wan bahɛ ti yamam ayima ayima tee ya suya gee yehe ya be'em tuuma la puan la.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Gee buraa kayina yu'urɛ n yi'iri Ananiya naaɛ la ka pɔga Safira, koohe ba samanɛ.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: ti a pɔga la mina, ti a welege ligeri la i se'ehi biŋe gee tari se'ehi n gee la na biŋe tuntuneba la nɛŋan.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Ti Pita soke Ananiya yeti, Ŋwani ti Sagena la pirɛ fum suure paaɛ bala ti fum parum bo' Fa'a Sia, doohe fum aaɛ samanɛ la ti fum koohe la ligeri i se'ehi biŋe la?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Samanɛ la n nan ka koohe ni la, de dagi ni la fum mɛŋa dinɛ, fum le ta koohe de la, ligeri la ka boi la fum nu'uhen? La iŋɛ la ŋwani ti fum ta'am ti'ihɛ yele dina wa fum suuren? Fum ka parum bo' la asaala, gee Yinɛ.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiya n wum yetɔgebuna wa la, a kɔ'ɔm lu mɛ ki. Ti dabeem kãtɛ sãɛ sɛba woo me n wum la.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Ti budibe paalehi la paaɛ pɔbɛ en, zi en yehe ta pi'.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Ti la iŋɛ wuu gogo kaareŋa atã la, ti a pɔga la me kiŋɛ kɛ̃ na, ka baŋɛ sɛla en iŋɛ.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Ti Pita soke en yeti, Yele mam, fum la fum sira la koohe ya samanɛ la ligeri la ŋwani la ŋwani. Ti a yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, tumam n koohe de se'em n bala.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, La iŋɛ la ŋwani ti yamam ku nuurɛ la taaba ti ya magehɛ Daana la Sia wa bihɛ? Bihɛ, sɛba n pi' fum sira nama n bala bɔna kuleŋa nuuren wa, bamam mɛa n wan zi fum yehe.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Bilam bilam ti a lu Pita nɛŋan ki, budibe paalehi la n kɛ̃ nyɛ en ti a ki mɛ la, ti ba zi eŋa me yehe ta pi' en lɛm asira la.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Ti dabeem kãtɛ sãɛ lageŋɔ la, la sɛba woo n wum yele ana wa.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Daalehum la nyalema la kekeko en tuni nɛreba la puan, doohe tuntuneba la puan. Ti ba za'a lagehɛ bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la ze'sɛka ti ba yi'ira ka Solomɔn zinzaka la puan.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Gee nɛreba la, la ayima me ka makɛ dikɛ a mɛŋa pa'ahɛ tuntuneba la puan, gee nɛreba la za'a na'aheri ba mɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Gee nɛreba n tugum malum nam bo' Daana la, ti buraahi la pɔgehi kãlɛ zo'e.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: ti nɛreba ni zi bã'duma ta biŋe sore nuuren la ba kugehi bii ba sunɔ zuto, ti Pita en tole la a nyilenyileho wan doohe ba zuto tole ti ba nyɛ ba tibegɔ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e me ni ze'ele tinse'ehi n boi Yerusalɛm bɔba, tari bã'duma la sɛba ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi mɛleha ba, ti ba za'a nyɛ ba tibega.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Ti malema kiima la vaaɛ ihege, eŋa la sɛba woo n boi la en la n pirɛ la nyuure la.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: nyɔgɛ tuntuneba la pagɛ ba paka deto puan.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Gee yu'uŋɔ, ti Yinɛ suŋera wa'am na yu'ɛ paka deo kulehi la tari ba yehe na, gee yele ba yeti.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Kiŋɛ ya ta ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la ya yele nɛreba la vom bɔna wa yelehum la za'a.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Ban wum ŋwana la, ba kiŋɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la la bulika, ta kɛ̃ pɔhɛ ba yelehum pa'alegɔ. Malema nɛŋa daana la, la sɛba woo n boi la en la n paaɛ na la, ba yi' la kiinduma la za'a, la sɛba woo n de kima Izirale buuri puan la lagehɛ na, gee nyaa tum ti ba kiŋɛ paka deo la puan ta tari tuntuneba la na.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Gee sɛba n guri pu'uhegɔ yire la n kiŋɛ paka deo la puan la, ba ka nyɛ ba bini, ti ba lebena yele yeti.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: Tumam paaɛ ti paka deo kulehi la yu' mɛ kankaŋi, ti gureba la me zɛ'a kulehi la nuuren, gee tumam n yu'ɛ kulehi la, tumam ka nyɛ se'em bini.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Sɛba n biheri pu'uhegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma la, la malema kiinduma la n wum ŋwana wa la, ba maam mɛ la ba, ti'ihera nyaa beni n tugum ita wa.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Ti nɛra ayima n bala paaɛ na muula yeti, Bihɛ ya, buraahi la ti ya pagɛ ba la n bala zɛ'a pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ha pa'ala nɛreba la.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Ti sɛba n guri pu'uhegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma la pu'uhegɔ yire gureba la kiŋɛ ta tari ba bããlam wa'am na, se'ere n sɔi la, ba zoti mɛ ti nɛreba la wan lobe bamam.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Ban tari ba paaɛ na la, ba bahɛ ti ba ze'ele la malema duma kima la nɛŋan. Ti malema nɛŋa duma la soke ba.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: yeti, Tu daa bo' ya nuurɛ la ninmu'urɛ yeti, ya da le tara yu'urɛ wa pa'ala, gee bihɛ yamam n bala pirɛ Yerusalɛm wa la ya pa'alegɔ wa, kɔ'ɔm lu' ti ya dikɛ buraa wa ziim ka'arɛ pagelɛ tumam zuo.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Gee Pita la tuntuneba la lebehe ba yeti, Tumam wan nam la Yinɛ nyana saala kpe'eŋo.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tu yaabeduma Yinɛ la n vo'e Yezu a kum la puan, se'em ti ya daa yule en tia la zuo ku la.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Yinɛ zɛkɛ en mɛ ta zi'ire a zuɔ nu'o ti a dɛna Nɛŋa Daana, la Faara, ti a bo' Izirale suteere la yelebe'ero sugeri.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Ti tumam n dɛna aze' nyɛ duma bo' yele ana wa, la Fa'a Sia ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' sɛba n nane en la.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Ban wum ŋwana la, ba suya n nyiie gaŋɛ, ti ba ku nuurɛ la taaba ti ba ku ba.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Gee Farasii ayima n lagum bɔna kiinduma la puan ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Gamaliyɛl, pa'ala Moose pa'alegɔ la, ti nɛreba za'a na'ahera en. Eŋa n ihege ze'ele bo' nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti tuntuneba la yehe yiŋa fii wa.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Ti a nyaa yele ba yeti, N suurɔ Izirale taaba wa, ti'ihɛ ya suŋa yan yeti ya iŋɛ nɛreba wa se'em.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Saŋa n tole ha, Tiyodase yuum ihege mɛ, yeti eŋa dela sɛla, ti buraahi yuum paaɛ wuu kɔbehi sinaahi dɔla en. Gee ba yuum ku en mɛ, ti a dɔleba la za'a yiregɛ, mɛlum.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Eŋa pooren, ti Yudasi n de Galiliiŋa me yuum ihege na, nɛreba kãalegɔ saŋa la, ti nɛreba me yuum dɔla en, ti eŋa me ta ki, ti a dɔleba la za'a yiregɛ.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Bala, nananawa, yeledina wa puan, mam yeti ya me wa, ti ya fooe ya nu'o buraahi wa puan gee bahɛ ba ba ma'a. Se'ere n sɔi la, yele dina wa bii tuun-dina wa han dɛna la asaala puti'irɛ, de wan bahɛ yoo.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Gee de han dɛna la Yinɛ puti'irɛ yamam kan ta'am gu' ba. Ya han yeti ya iŋɛ bala, ya wan ta nyɛ ti ya zaberɛ mɛ la Yinɛ mɛŋa! Ti ba bɔkɛ a yetɔgum la.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Ti ban yi' tuntuneba la kɛ̃ na la, ba bahɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' ba mɛ, gee ti ba nyaa bo' ba nuurɛ yeti ba da le tɔgɛ se'ere Yezu yu'urɛ la puan, gee nyaa bahɛ ba ti ba kiŋɛ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Ban baheri kiinduma la zi'an la, ba puurɛ pee mɛ ti bamam me ti ba diihe ba vi Yezu yu'urɛ la zuo.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Ti daarɛ woo, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan bii ba mɛhi yiya puan, ba ka go'ore pa'alegɔ, bii ba muula ti Yezu n de Kiresta la.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Azɔɛtole dapihinuu de'eŋo la, daarɛ la n ta paaɛ la, ba za'a lagehɛ bɔna la bɔbeyinɛ.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Ba ta yeti ba bihɛ ti sɛla n ze'ele saazuo damena na wuu kusebego kãtɛ la, wa'am na pi' yire se'ere ti ba bɔna bini la tapi.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Ti sɛla ana wuu bugum zɛlehi la welehi yima yima wa'am na yagelɛ nɛra woo zuo.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba za'a, ti ba pɔhɛ tɔgera yetɔgesaahi yima yima, wuu Fa'a Sia n bo' ba paŋa se'em la.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Saŋa kana puan la, ti sɛba woo n de Yuda buuri yelemɛŋerɛ la ze'ele la ban boi tihi se'ehi woo puan timpaberɛ wa zuo lagehɛ Yerusalɛm na.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Ti dameŋa kana wa bahɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la en lagehɛ na la, la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ se'ere n sɔi la, nɛrawoo n boi bini wuni ban tɔgeri la a tiŋa yetɔgum.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Ti la iŋɛ pakerɛ gee ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba ti ba sɔkera taaba yeti, Nɛrebana wa za'a dagi la Galiliihi?.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: Gee ti la iŋɛ la ŋwani ti tumam woo n boi ka'lam wuna ti ba tɔgera a mɛŋa yetɔgum?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Sɛba n ze'ele Patiya, Mɛdise, Ɛlamduma, la sɛba woo n de Mɛsopotemiya, Yudiya la Kapadosia, Pɔntusi la Esiya.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: Firigiya la Pamfiliya, Igipiti la Libiya ze'se'ehi n boi Saarin sɔ'ɔlum puan la, la saama sɛba woo n ze'ele Arome wa'am na, Yuuduma, la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri gee lebege Yuuduma.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Kereteduma la Arebiyaduma za'a yɛta yeti, tu wuni ban tɔgeri tu mɛhi tiŋa yetɔgum puan pa'ala Yinɛ tuunkãra yɛla.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ ti ba ze'a yɛli yɛta taaba yeti, Yele dina wa vuurɛ dela bem.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Gee ti nɛreba basɛba me giila ba gee yɛta yeti, Daam paalum ma'a n bala ti ba buge bala.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Gee Pita n ze' la zahereba pia la ayima la n wum bala la, a zɛkɛ la a kua tɔgɛ bo' nɛrekuuŋɔ la yeti, Yudiya nɛreba wa, la sɛba woo n boi Yerusalɛm wa, bahɛ ya ti n yele ya yele dina wa. Kelehe ya mam yeti se'ere wa.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛreba wa ka buge wuu yan yeti se'em la. La nan dela gogo kaareŋa awai bulika.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Aayi, ŋwana wa dela yele se'ere ti Nɔtɔgeha Yowɛle yuum purɛ biŋe yeti.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Yinɛ yeti, Ba'ahegɔ dabeha la puan, N wan ka'ɛ Sia la bahɛ saaleba woo zuo, ti ya dayɔɔhi la poyɔɔhi wan purɛ mam yelehum la, ti ya bumbile-paalehi nyɛta lilegere, ti ya buranya'ahi zaahena zaaheŋɔ.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Pun ta ta' la mam da'aba, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a dabeha aŋa puan, n wan ka'ɛ n Sia ti ba puta.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Gee n me wan tum daalehum saazuo wa ha, gee tum saŋa la daalehum timpaberɛ wa zuo, Ziim, la bugum la zu'uhi wan pi' tapi.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Wuntɛɛŋa wan sobege ti ŋmarega dɛna ziim, gee ti Daana la dabehekãtɛ la, en de tɛrega dabeherɛ la nyaa paaɛ.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: La wan wa'am na tole ti fum se'em woo n yi' Daana la yu'urɛ la wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Yamam sɛba n de Izirale buuri wa, kelehe ya mam n tari se'ere ti n yele wa. Yezu n de Nazareti buraa la, dela buraa n tari tuunɛ ti Yinɛ pun maahum en, gee tum daalehum a puan doohe paŋa, nyalema la daalehum ya tiŋasuka, ti la niiɛ pa'alɛ ya ti Yinɛ n sirum looe en wuu ya mɛhi n mi' se'em la.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Buraa kana wa daa lu ya nu'uhen doohe la Yinɛ mɛŋa n pun maahum a mina de se'em la, ti ya daa kpa' en gee ku en, doohe la yan dikɛ en bu sɛba n ka mi' kiha la ti ba ku en la.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Gee Yinɛ n vo'e en, gee yehe en kum puan bahɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, kum paŋa kan ta'am bobe en sɔna.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la Deviti yuum purɛ a yele yeti, Mam nyɛti n Daana la saŋa woo ti a bɔna n nɛŋan. Se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa n boi zuɔ nu'o ti n ka gbɛ'ɛra.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Bala zuo, mam suure wan nyɛɛna, gee ti mam zɛleŋa tara pɛka. Se'ere n gani za'a dela, mam iŋa wa wan vo'ohe gee tara beere puti'irɛ.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, fum Yinɛ kan zagehɛ n Sia bo' ki'intiŋa, bii fum bahɛ ti fum Fa'a daana wa wuu'ɛ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: Fum pa'alɛ mam vom sore la mɛ, Fum wan bo' mam nyɛɛma doohe la fum n boi la mam la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: N suurɔ Izirale buuri wa, mam wan yele ya gee ti dabeem ka tara mam yeti, tu sɔyaaba Deviti me yuum ki mɛ ti ba pi' en, ti a yɔɔ kelum gã'a la zina.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: Se'em ma'a ti a dina nɔtɔgeha la, ti a pun mina mɛ ti Yinɛ pɔ mɛ la pɔtɛ ti a wan bahɛ ti a yaaŋa kayima ta wa'am na sooe zi'ire na'am kuka la zuo.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Deviti n nyɛti dina la zuo ti a tɔgɛ Kiresta vo'ore yele yeti, A kan ta zagehɛ eŋa bo' ki'intiŋa la. Bii eŋa kɔba ta lagum wuu'ɛ.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Yezu kana wa, Yinɛ n vo'e en, yelese'ere ti tu za'a dɛna aze' nyɛ duma ti de sirum iŋɛ mɛ.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: En nyaa paaɛ zi'ire Yinɛ na'am paŋa kuka la zuo la, a to'e Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ n de Sɔ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan bahɛ ti a wa'am na la, bala ti a ka'ɛ ŋwana wa bahɛ tumam zuo, ti ya za'a nini nyɛ ti ya tuba wum.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la Deviti yuum ka ihege zom saazuo, gee eŋa mɛŋa en yeti, Daana la yele mam Daana la yeti, Zi'a n zuɔ nu'o wa.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: gura ti n bahɛ ti fum dinduma za'a ta lebege fum nama dafeere.

eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Bala zuo la, Izirale yire za'a, mina ya suŋa ti Yinɛ n iŋɛ en ti a dɛna Daana la Kiresta. Yezu sɛka ti ya kpa' la.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana la, la ve'e ba mɛ ba suya puan ha, ti ba soke Pita la tuntuneba basɛba la yeti, Tu suurɔ wa, tu nyaa wan iŋɛ la ŋwani.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Ti Pita yele ba yeti, Tee ya suya, ya za'a ayima ayima to'e ko'om sua Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan, ti Yinɛ di sugeri bo' ya be'em la, gee ti ya to'e Fa'a Sia la bo'olum la.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ n biŋe a nuurɛ ti a iŋɛ se'em la dela ya dinɛ la ya kɔma, ta ta' se'em woo n zaaɛ gee ti Daana la yi' en.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Ti Pita dikɛ yɛla kekeko magehɛ pa'alɛ ba gee kpemehe ba yeti, Faaɛ ya mɛhi be'em buuri saŋa wa puan bahɛ.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Ŋwana ti sɛba woo n to'e a yelehum la za'a to'e ko'om sua, ti didaarɛ mɛa, nɛreba n paaɛ wuu tuha atã n wa'am na pa'ahɛ.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Ti nɛrebana wa kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' tuntuneba pa'alegɔ la, la lageŋɔ la taaba, lagum ɔbera boroboro, la pu'uhegɔ.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Ti nɛra woo yari ya' nɔya, la daalehum la nyalema sɛ'a ti tuntuneba la tuna la.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Ti sɛba woo n nam la za'a boi la bɔbeyinɛ ita sɛla woo buyina.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Ba wan ni koohe ban tari sɛla, la ba lɔgerɔ, tari ligeri la wa'am na tɔrɛ bo' nɛra woo doohe la n boti sɛla.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Beere en yilegeri woo, ba tari la saŋa zo'e zo'e bɔna pu'uhegɔ yire la puan zameha, tɔta boroboro la la taaba, gee dɔla taaba yiya dita la taaba la pupeelum la bu'a suya la taaba.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: pɛgera Yinɛ, ti ba puya pee la nɛra woo n nɔŋɛ ba se'em la. Ti beere n yilegeri woo, Daana la tara nɛreba pa'aha basɛba n nyɛ faarɛ wa lageŋɔ wa puan.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Pita la Yɔn n ze' tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la, ti Pu'uhegɔ nɛŋa daana, la kpɛ'ɛma se'em n biheri pu'uhegɔ yire la, la Sadusiiduma wa'am ba zi'an na.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: ti ba suure nyiie la ban pa'ali nɛreba la gee muula yeti Yezu puan a ki gee ihege boi mɛ la.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti ba nyɔgɛ ba ta pagɛ yiimbeere se'ere n sɔi la yinɛ daa pun lu mɛ.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Gee sɛba n wum yelehum la zo'e zo'e nam bu mɛ, ti ba kãlɛ dɛna wuu buraahi tuha anuu.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Ti la iŋɛ ti yiimbeere la, ti naduma, kiinduma la gɔnmi'ineba la lagehɛ taaba Yerusalɛm puan.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: ti Annas n de malema kiima, la Kaifase, Yɔn, la Alɛgezanda la sɛba woo n de malema kiima yireduma la za'a wa'am na.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Ban yu'ɛ ba na ti ba zɛ'a ba nɛŋan la, ti ba soke ba yeti, Beni paŋa, bii yu'deni puan ti ya nyaŋɛ tum ŋwana wa.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita n pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la yele ba yeti, Naduma n sɔi nɛreba, la kiinduma wa.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: Ya han sɔkera tumam mɛ zina wa doohe la yelesunɛ en tum bo' nɛra n bɛ̃'ɛri, ti ya sɔkera lan iŋɛ se'em ti buraa wa nyɛ ka tibegɔ.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: baŋɛ ya zina, yamam za'a, la Izirale buuri za'a, ti buraa wa n ze' ya nɛŋan dɛna ka mɛŋa wa, dela Yezu Kiresta n de Nazareti buraa la yu'urɛ la puan. Se'em ti yamam yuum kpa', gee ti Yinɛ vo'e en a kum la puan la.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Yezu wa dela Kugere se'ere ti yamam mɛteba la zagehɛ la, n nyaa de ɛ̃berɛ kugere kãtɛ.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Faarɛ ka boi se'em puan, se'ere n sɔi la, yu'se'ere ka boi sagbanɛ wa tiŋa bo' nɛresaaleba ti ba wan yi' de nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Nɛreba wa n nyɛ Pita la Yɔn sukpe'ene la, gee me baŋɛ ti ba dagi gɔnmi'ineba gee ba yeen dɛna la nɛreba la, ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ, gee ti ba le baŋɛ ti ba ni bɔna mɛ la Yezu.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Ban nyɛ ti buraa la n nyɛ ka tibegɔ la ze' la ba la, ba ka tari se'ere ti ba wan yele de puan.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Bala ti ba bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba yehe kiinduma la nɛŋan fɔregɛ gee ti bamam suhɛ yele la la taaba bihɛ.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Ti ba nyaa yele taaba yeti, Beni ti tu wan dikɛ iŋɛ ba? Se'ere n sɔi la, la ka sugɛ nɛrawoo n boi Yerusalɛm wa ninin ti ba tum la daalehum n de yelemɛŋerɛ. Tu kan ta'a sugɛ dɛ.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Gee tumam n ka boti ti de yiregɛ zi'an woo la, bahɛ ya ti tu ka'am ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da le dikɛ yu'dina wa tɔgɛ zi'an, nɛreba tiŋasuka.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Ŋwana ti ba nyaa yi' Pita la Yɔn lebena, yele ba la ninmu'urɛ ti ba da le tɔgɛ bii pa'alɛ Yezu yu'urɛ la puan le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Gee Pita la Yɔn yele ba yeti, Lebehe ya bo' ya mɛhi nyaa, la ani suŋa Yinɛ nɛŋan ti tu kɛlehi la yamam bii Yinɛ? Gakɛ ya.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, tumam kan nyaŋɛ mum tu nɔya kan tɔgɛ sɛla ti tu nini nyɛ, ti tu tuba wum.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Ti ba vuuhe ba le yɛ'ɛha, gee bahɛ ba ti ba kiŋɛ. Ban ka nyɛ yele se'ere ti ba wan ze'ele namehɛ ba la, gee me le nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la me. Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a pɛgeri la Yinɛ la tibegɔ daalehum la n tum la.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, buraa wa ti daalehum tum ka puan la gani yuumpihinaahi mɛ.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Ban bahɛ ba la, ti ba kiŋɛ ba pu'uhi taaba la zi'an, ta yele ba malema kiinduma la, la kiinduma la en yele ba se'em.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Ba taaba la n wum la, ba za'a zɛkɛ la ba kuuhi buyina, kaahɛ bo' Yinɛ yeti, Gaŋeza'a Daana se'em n naam saazuo la timpaberɛ wa, ateko la sɛla woo n boi bu puan.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: la dela fum n doohe Fa'a Sia la puan, doohe tu yaaba Deviti n de fum yameŋa la puan yeti, Ŋwani ti sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa suya nyiie, ti nɛreba la ti'iha yeleyooro?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Tiŋa wa zuo na'duma vaaɛ ihege mɛ, Tiŋa wa zuo kpɛ'ɛnduma maahum mɛ zɛ'a ti ba zabɛ la Daana la, ti ba zabɛ la a Kiresta la.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, tinkãtɛ wa puan, Hɛrɔd la Pɔntiyɔsi Palete ta ta' la sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la, la sɛba en de Izirale nɛreba la lagehɛ la taaba ti ba zabɛ la fum Fa'a yameŋa Yezu, se'em ti fum bi'ihɛ en;.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: ti a tum sɛla woo ti fum nu'o la fum puti'irɛ pun diŋɛ maahum ti la tum la.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Nananawa tu Daana, bihɛ ba vuuhego wa, gee la fum bo' fum yamehi wa sore ti ba muulɛ fum yelehum la sukpe'ene.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: gee ti fum mɛŋa tiɛ fum nu'o la fum tibɛ, gee ti nyalema la daalehum tuna Fa'a yameŋa yu'urɛ Yezu wa puan.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Ban pu'uhɛ la, zi'an la ti ba lagehɛ bɔna la miim mɛ, Ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba za'a ti ba tɔgɛ Yinɛ yelehum la la sukpe'ene.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Nananawa, Lan de se'em la dela, sakereba la za'a tari la suyinɛ la putɛ'yinɛ la taaba, ti ba se'em ka sɔna a lɔgeseko bii en tari sɛla, gee ba se'em woo n tari sɛla dela ba za'a bunɔ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la nyɛ pankãtɛ, muulɛ ban nyɛ sɛla la doohe Daana Yezu ihegere la, ti zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ bɔna ba za'a zuo.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Naha ka boi ba puan, se'ere n sɔi la, basɛba zo'e zo'e n tari samana bii yiya koohe a mɛ tari ligeri la na.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: Ba tari i na biŋe la Yezu tuntuneba la nɛŋan, ti ba tari i tɔta bɔ'ɔra nɛrawoo n ka tari sɛla.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Ti Levi buuri nɛra n wa bɔna ba puan, yehe Sapuruse na ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Yosefe, gee ti tuntuneba la sigɛ en Banabase, .

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Eŋa koohe la a samanɛ, tari ligeri la za'a na biŋe tuntuneba la nɛŋan.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Ŋwana ti malema nɛŋadaana la soke Siteven yeti, Alaa, yele ana wa za'a dela asira ti fum yele la bala.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Ti Siteven lerege yeti, N suurɔ la n sɔduma, kelehe ya mam. Yinɛ en de suŋa Yinɛ la yuum niiɛ la de mɛŋa bo' tu sɔyaaba Aberaham, se'em ma'a ti a bɔna Mɛsopotemiya, se'em ma'a ti a nan ka nɔgɛ kiŋɛ Haran la, yeti.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Dabeha aŋa puan la, zahereba la kãlɛ n wa pa'aha la, ti Hiburuduma sɛba n tɔgeri Giriki la la nyɔni mɛ la ba Hiburuduma la mɛŋa, doohe la ban ka bo'ori Hiburuduma sɛba n tɔgeri Giriki la pokɔpa la ba daare woo dia tɔtɛ la.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Bahɛ fum tiŋa wa la fum nɛreba, gee kiŋɛ tiŋa kayima ti n yeti n pa'alɛ hɔ.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Ti zahereba pia la bayi' la yi' sɛba n nam la za'a lagehɛ, yele ba yeti, La dagi ma'a bo' tumam, ti tu bahɛ Yinɛ yelehum la muulegɔ gee yee dia tɔrega.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Bala ti Aberaham bahɛ Kalediya duma tiŋa wa, gee kiŋɛ ta zi'ire Haran la. Ti a sɔ en ta ki la, ti Yinɛ ihege en bilam wa'am tinsɛka ti ya bɔna ka puan zina wa puan na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Bala tu suurɔ wa, looe ya buraahi siyopɔi ya puan n de tuuntɔɔ, ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ ba, dɛna yɛmduma, tari na bo' tu, ti tu bo' ba sore ti ba biha yele ana wa.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Yinɛ yuum ka dikɛ tinkana wa zi'an bɔba bo' Aberaham ti ka dɛna a dinɛ, pun dagena wuu na'arɛ tagelegɔ beene. Gee Yinɛ yuum biŋe la nuurɛ ti a wan dikɛ ka bo' eŋa la a yaahi se'ehi n wan ta wa'am na, la Aberaham n yuum pun ka tara bia la.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: gee tumam me, wuu tu tuni n de se'em la, tu wan kaabɛ tu mɛhi saŋa woo bo' pu'uhegɔ, la yelehum muulegɔ.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Yinɛ yuum tɔgɛ bo' en yeti, A yaahi wan ta dɛna saama bɔna nɛreba basɛba tiŋa, ti ba dikɛ ba lebege ba da'aba, mugehe ba yuuma kɔbehi sinaahi.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
local: Ti ban yele se'em la pee nɛreba la puurɛ, ti ba looe Siteven, n de buraa n pirɛ la sakerɛ la Fa'a Sia la, gee me looe Filipi, Porokoruse, Nikano, Timɔn, Pamenase, la Nikolayuse se'em n yehe Antiyoke se'em n dagi Yuda buuri gee lebege Yuda buuri la.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Gee n wan gakɛ tinse'ehi ti ba dɛna ba da'aba la. Yinɛ n yele se'em n bala. N han ta gakɛ tihi la ba'ahɛ fum yaahi la nyaa wan ze'ele si puan yehe na, la ba na'aha mam tinkana wa puan.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Ti ba bahɛ ti buraahi sina wa ze'ele tuntuneba la nɛŋan, ti ba pu'uhɛ, gee dikɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Ti Yinɛ ku ŋmaa nuurɛ la n. Bala zuo ti Aberaham n ta dɔgɛ Aziki la, a ŋmaaɛ bia la dɔgum dabeha anii daarɛ mɛ, ti Aziki me n ta dɔgɛ Yakɔbe, ti Yakɔbe me ta dɔgɛ ba yaabeduma pia la bayi' la.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Ti Yinɛ yelehum la kɔ'ɔm yiregera, ti zahereba la kãlɛ malum pa'ahera Yerusalɛm puan, ti malema duma la zo'e zo'e to'e sakerɛ la.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Ti yaabeduma wa looe Yosefe ba puan nyuuna la en, ta koohe en bu Igipiti. Gee Yinɛ boi la en mɛ.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Siteven pirɛ me la zu'uhegɔ la paŋa, tuna daalehum la nyalema kãra bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: gee faaɛ en pɔgeleŋɔ woo puan bahɛ, gee bahɛ en ti a nyɛ zu'uhegɔ, gee bo' en yɛm Igipiti naba Faro nɛŋan, se'em n wa looe Yosefe ti a sɔna Igipiti za'a la eŋa naba la yire za'a biha.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Ŋwana ti sɛba n boi zamehegɔ yire la puan ti ba yi'ira a boi fai pa'alegɔ yire la puan la , Sereniduma, Alɛgezanda duma, la sɛba n me yehe Kilikiya, la Esiya na la, daa vaaɛ ihege la nɔkpe'ene la Siteven.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Ti la bɔna bɔna ti kom pɛɛlega ta sige Igipiti la Keenan tihi puan, la namehegɔ kãtɛ, ti tu yaabeduma ka nyɛ dia zi'an zi'an.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Gee ba ka nyaŋɛ ze'ele la yɛmsebo la Sɛsɛka n boi la en ti a tɔgera la.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Gee Yakɔbe n ta wum ti ki boi Igipiti la, a tum la tu yaabeduma wa ti ba kiŋɛ ba yia kinɛ.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Ti ba iŋɛ bããlam ɛbegɛ buraahi sise'ehi ti ba yeti, Tumam wum mɛ ti a tɔgɛ tu' Moose la Yinɛ.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Ba yi'i puan la ti Yosefe niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a kiinduma wa, ti naba Faro me baŋɛ Yosefe yire buuri wa.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la gbirege, ta ta' kiinduma, la gɔnmi'ineba la, ti la ka yuue ti ba kaaɛ Siteven mum, nyɔkɛ en, tari en wa'am kiinduma zi'an na.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Ŋwana ti Yosefe nyaa tum yi' a sɔ Yakɔbe, la Yosefe suurɔ woo wa'am a zi'an Igipiti na, ti ba za'a dɛna nɛreba pihiyopɔi la banuu.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Gee nyaa maahum aze' nyɛ pumparenduma, ti ba yehe na yeti, Buraa wa kan malum bahɛ ka tɔgera tuura tuulum zi'an wa la Ze'elego la.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Lan iŋɛ se'em ti Yakɔbe la tu yaabeduma basɛba la kiŋɛ Igipiti n bala. Yakɔbe ta ki la bilam, la tu yaabeduma basɛba la me.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wum ti a yeti, Yezu n de Nazareti buraa la wan wa'am na sagum ze'kana wa, gee malum teehum tu malema sɛ'a ti Moose dikɛ bahɛ tu la.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Ti ba zi ba lebe Sɛkɛm na, pi' ba yɔɔ seko puan ti Aberaham yuum da' la salema pɛɛlega Hamɔn dayɔɔhi la zi'an Sɛkɛm puan la.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Ti sɛba woo n boi kiinduma suhega zi'an la puan la biha Siteven nɛŋa zii, ta nyɛ ti a nɛŋa la tee me ana woo Suŋera nɛŋa la.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Gee saŋa la n ta pɔra ti Yinɛ tum a nɔbiŋere la ti a biŋe la Aberaham la, ti nɛreba wa en boi Igipiti bala ma'a la ba' wi'ɛ mɛ.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: tara tara ti naba ka yima n ka mi' Yosefe yele, wa'am na zi'ire Igipiti na'am kuka la zuo.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: tara yɛm kirega dugera tu buuri wa, pɛregɛ tu yaabeduma ti ba han ni dɔgɛ bia, ba bahɛ ka ti ka ta ki.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Sankana puan ti ba dɔgɛ Moose, ti a dɛna bia n sum Yinɛ Nɛŋan. Ŋmarehi sitã ti bia wa bɔna ka sɔ yire.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: ti ban daa ta tari en ta bahɛ la, Faro mɛŋa poyua n nyɛ en, dikɛ en bihera, ti a ta zo'e na dɛna a mɛŋa dayua.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Bala zuo la, ba yuum pa'alɛ Moose mɛ la Igipiti yɛm la za'a, ti a zo'e na, tara paŋa a yetɔgum puan la a tuuma puan.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Moose n ta dɛna yuuma pihinaahi la, ti la wa'am a suuren na, ti a kiŋɛ ta kaaɛ a nɛreba la n de Izirale buuri la.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: En wa'am na nyɛ ti ba tari ba ayima ŋmɛ'ɛra la, a lerege en mɛ, ŋmɛ' Igipiti nɛra la ku.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Moose ti'ihɛ ti a buuri la wan baŋɛ ti Yinɛ wan doohe eŋa puan faaɛ ba bahɛ, gee a buuri la ka bɔkɛ de bala.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: Yiimbeere ti a le kiŋɛ na paaɛ ti a buuri la bayi' zaberɛ, ti a zigɛ ti a sugelum ba, nyaa yɛta ba yeti, N suurɔ wa, mini ya ti ya dela suuɔ. Ŋwani ti ya pɔgelena taaba.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Ti se'em n ŋmɛ'ɛri ni a tadaana la za'ɛ Moose bahɛ ha, gee yele en yeti, Ani n iŋɛ hɔ ti fum biha tumam, gee gakera tɔ?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: Fum boti fum ku mam me mɛ wuu fum zaam ku Igipiti nɛra la se'em la.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Moose n wum ŋwana la, a zɔ mɛ loŋe Midiya, ta dɛna saana bilam. Bilam ti a dɔgɛ dayɔɔhi siyi.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
