local: Ti yuuma pihinaahi ta tole, ti Yinɛ Suŋera ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ en Muɔ puan ha, Sinai Zoore la ha, ti tuntuo dita gee ka taara.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Moose n nyɛ ŋwana la, la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ la sɛla ti a nyɛta la, ti en kiŋɛ paara ti a bihɛ la, ti Daana la kua yi' en yeti.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Mam n de fum yaabeduma Yinɛ la. Aberaham Yinɛ, Aziki la Yakɔbe Yinɛ la. Ti Moose iŋa pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra, gee ti a ka le sakɛ ti a le bihɛ.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Ti Daana la yele en yeti, Pirege fum tagera la ti fum pire la, se'ere n sɔi la, ze'sɛka ti fum zɛ'a wa dela Tuulum zi'an.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Mam nini sirum nyɛ mɛlehegɔ seko ti mam nɛreba wa bɔna Igipiti mɛleha la, gee me wum ba uhega, bala ti n sige na ti n faaɛ ba. Wa'am na nananawa na, n wan tum hɔ Igipiti.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Moose kana n bala ti ba daa ka nam gee soke en yeti, Ani n iŋɛ hɔ ti fum biha tumam gee gakera tu la? Moose kaŋa n bala ti Yinɛ tum en ti a sɔna biha gee dɛna faara, doohe Suŋera la n niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ en muuen ha la.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Ti a tari ba yehe na, se'em ma'a ti a damɛ Igipiti, la mɔgemɔlegɔ la puan, la kpaahenɛ la puan la daalehum nyalema yuuma pihinaahi.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Moose kana n bala yuum yele Izirale buuri wa yeti, Yinɛ wan looe a nɔtɔgeha ya mɛŋa buuri wa puan bo' ya wuu en looe mam se'em la.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Eŋa n bala wa bɔna Izirale lageŋɔ la puan muuen ha la, la suŋera se'em n yuum tɔgɛ bo' Moose Sinai Zoore la zuo la, gee daa bɔna la tu yaabeduma, ti a to'e vom pa'alegɔ na bo' tu.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Tu yaabeduma la yuum ka maahum ti ba nana en, gee tugum za'ɛ en bahɛ kinkɛleŋa, gee ti ba suya puan, ba yuum lebe la Igipiti puan.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: doohe la ban yuum yele Aron yeti, Maalɛ baga bo' tu ti a pa'alɛ tu sore, gee han dɛna Moose wa n tari tu ze'ele Igipiti yehe na wa, tu ka mi' sɛla n iŋɛ en.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Sankaŋa ti ba kure nagebila, kɔrum duhi kaabɛ ka, gee na'ahɛ sɛla ti ba dikɛ ba nu'uhi kure la.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Ŋwana ti Yinɛ ŋmɛregɛ gee bahɛ ba, gee dikɛ ba bo' saazuo ŋmarebibehi wa kaabegɔ, wuu lan gulehɛ nɔtɔgeheba gɔŋɔ la puan yele se'em la. La yeti, Yamam yuum kɔreni ya duhi la gee kaabera la mam yuuma pihinaahi muɔ la puan ha la, Izirale yire duma wa?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Aayi, yan daa yeheri la, ya daa tari Mɔlɛki bagerɛ la mɛ la ya, gee tara ŋmarebila bagerɛ la n yi'iri Arafan la, bage se'ere ti ya kure na'aha la. Bala la, n wan bahɛ ti ya kiŋɛ zaaɛ gana Babilɔn.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Tu sɔyaabeduma la yuum ziiri tuulum sugerɛ la mɛ la ba muuen la ha, wuu Yinɛ n yuum pa'alɛ Moose se'em la, gee yuum bo' en nuurɛ ti a maalɛ tuulum sugerɛ la wuu eŋa n pa'alɛ en se'em la.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Tu yaabeduma la me yuum tari tuulum sugerɛ wa doohe la Yosua kɛ̃ na, se'em ma'a ti ba yuum ta nyaŋɛ tihi se'ehi woo ti Yinɛ yuum dige si nɛreba tu yaabeduma wa ninin la. Ti tuulum sugerɛ wa yuum bɔna la bilam tara tara ta paaɛ Naba Deviti saŋa.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: se'em ti Yinɛ yuum zu'uhɛ en ti a yuum soke Yinɛ ti a bahɛ ti a mɛ Yinɛ bɔŋa zi'an, Yakɔbe Yinɛ wa yu'urɛ purega zi'an.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: Gee Solomɔn en yuum ta wa'am na mɛ yire bo' en.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Gee Gaŋɛ Za'a Daana wa ka ki'ire yiya ti saaleba nu'uhi mɛ puan, wuu nɔtɔgeha la n yele se'em la.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Saazuo n de mam na'am kuka zi'an, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo dɛna mam nama dafeere Yire deni taaba ti fum wan ta'am mɛ bo' mam, Daana n yele. Bii ze'kani n wan dɛna mam vo'ohego zi'an?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Dagi mam nu'o n maalɛ sɛla woo en boi wa.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Tube-kpirega duma wa, ya suya digelum mɛ ti ya tuba li', saŋawoo yamam kan malum nam bo' Fa'a Sia la, ya kɔ'ɔm ita mɛ wuu ya yaabeduma en yuum iti se'em la.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Nɔtɔgeha kani ti ya yaabeduma ka mɛlehɛ? Ba yuum ku sɛba woo en yuum puti Fa'a Daana wa kinɛ yele la mɛ, ti nananawa, yamam nyaa lebege ŋma'areba la kuureba.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Ya dela sɛba n to'e ze'elego la, ti ku ze'ele la Yinɛ Suŋereba ze'a na, gee ya ka dole ku.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Nɛreba la n wum yele ana wa la, ba suure n nyiie, ti ba ɔbera nyɛna Siteven nɛŋan.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Gee ti Fa'a Sia la pirɛ en, ti Siteven gorege bihɛ saazuo ha, nyɛ Yinɛ suŋa, ti Yezu zi'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Ti a yele nɛreba la yeti, Bihɛ ya, mam nyɛti la saazuo n yu'ɛ, ti Asaala Dayua la zɛ'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: Ti nɛreba la li' ba tuba, gee kaaha tagurega, lagum zɔ uue en ha.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: Nyaa vuue en yehe tiŋa la kinkɛleŋa ha, pɔhɛ lɔbera en la kuga. Ti aze' nyɛ duma la yɛɛhɛ ba futo biŋe budibele paalega ti ba yi'ira ka Sɔɔl nɛŋan.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Ban lobere Siteven la, ti a pu'uhɛ yeti, Daana Yezu, to'e n sia wa.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Gee kpa' duma kaahɛ supi yeti, Daana, da dikɛ be'em buna wa bahɛ ba zuo. En yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a ki mɛ.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Ti Sɔɔl to'e kua la ti ba ku la. De daarɛ mɛa ti mɛlehegɔ kãtɛ sige Yezu lageŋɔ la zuo Yerusalɛm puan, ti han dagena tuntuneba la ma'a, sɛba n nam la za'a zɔ yiregɛ mɛ kiŋɛ Yudiya la Samariya kinkɛlehi tihi puan.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Ti buraahi en zoti Yinɛ dikɛ Siteven ta laaɛ, gee kele en, si'im paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Gee Sɔɔl mu'ɛ la nini ba'ahera Lageŋɔ la, doohe la en dole yire yire vɛ'ɛra sɛba n nam la, buraahi la pɔgehi, tara kina ti ba pagera ba.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Lan iŋɛ bala la, sɛba en nam la nyaa zɔ yiregɛ mɛ, lomehe zi'ihi, muula yelehum la.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Filipi kiŋɛ sige la Samariya tinkãtɛ puan, ta muulɛ Kiresta yele bo' ba.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Ban wum gee nyɛ daalehum zo'e zo'e sebo ti Filipi tum la, nɛrekuuŋɔ la naaɛ nuurɛ kelehe a yelehum la suŋa suŋa.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛfi'iha kaahi kaahedɔɔrɔ gee zɛ'ɛta nɛreba kekeko en tari ba la puan yɛhera gee baha ba, ti kɔɔrenɔ la wɔbehi zo'e zo'e ihegera kina.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Ti nyɛɛma kãtɛ sige tinkãtɛ la puan, yele ana zuo.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Buraa kayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Simɔn se'em n ni tuna a tiim tuuma Samariya tiŋa la puan, yuum Samariya nɛreba la, yɛta ti eŋa bi'e mɛ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Ti Samariya tiŋa puan, bia ta paara bunkutɛ woo kelehe en mɛ suŋa suŋa, gee yɛta yeti, Burakana wa dela yinɛ se'ere ti ba yi'ira Paŋa Yinɛ la, n sige na.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Ti ba ni ana kɛli gee kɛleha n suŋa suŋa se'ere n sɔi la, saŋa zo'e zo'e ti a ni ita a tiim tuuma la yɔɔna ba.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Gee lan nyaa wa'am na ti nɛreba la nam Filipi en muuli faarɛ la doohe Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, ba to'e la ko'om sua, buraahi la pɔgehi za'a.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Ti Simɔn la mɛŋa lagum nam yelehum la gee me to'e ko'om sua la. En to'e ko'om sua la ba'ahɛ la, a kɔ'ɔm du'ura Filipi la bala saŋa woo, bihera daalehum kãra la, nyalema kãra kɔ'ɔm sigera ti la iŋɛ en pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Nananawa, Yezu tuntuneba la n boi Yerusalɛm la n ta wum ti Samariyaduma to'e Yinɛ yelehum la me la, ba tum la Pita la Yɔn ba zi'an.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Ti ba bayi' la sige Samariya, ta paaɛ pu'uhɛ bo' ba, ti ba ta'am to'e Fa'a Sia la.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Ti Pita la Yɔn digehɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto ti ba to'e Fa'a Sia la.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Gee Simɔn n ta nyɛ ti tuntuneba la dikɛ la ba nu'uhi pagelɛ ti Fa'a Sia la sige nɛreba zuo la, a tiɛ la nu'o ti a bo' tuntuneba la ligeri.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: gee yeti, Bu' ya mam me pankana wa, ti se'em woo ti n dikɛ n nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo, Fa'a Sia sige a daana zuo.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Gee Pita yele en yeti, Fum la fum ligeri la bahɛ yoo, la fum ti'ihɛ ti fum wan ta'am da' Yinɛ bo'olum wa la ligeri la.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: Fum ka tari zi'an bii tɔtɛ yele ana wa puan se'ere n sɔi la fum suure dagi tuuntɔɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Bala zuo la, tee yehe fum tuunfi'iha wa puan, gee suhɛ Yinɛ, ti la han wan ta'am iŋɛ, a di sugeri bo' fum suure ti'ihegɔ la.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la mam nyɛ ti fum tari la nintɛrega, en tɔi wuu ya'am la ti tuunfi'iha zɔregɔ bobe fum.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Ti Simɔn lerege en yeti, Suhɛ Daana la bo' mam ti sɛla wuu fum yele se'em wa la da sige n zuo.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Pita la Yɔn n yele bamam n mi' Yezu yele se'em, gee muulɛ Daana la yelehum la ba'ahɛ la, ba lebe la Yerusalɛm, ban ye' lɛbera la, ba muuli yelehum la bala bɔ'ɔra timbibehi zo'e zo'e se'ehi n kaaɛ Samariya tiŋa la nɛreba.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Ti Daana la Suŋera yele Filipi yeti, Ihege la fum dikɛ tiŋanuurɛ bɔba sore wa, ta yibegɛ sore se'ere n ze'ele Yerusalɛm yɛ'a Gaza la.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Ti Filipi ihege kiŋɛ. Bala ma'a la ti Itoopia bulɛ n de kpɛ'ɛnkãtɛ, n de ba yetɔgum kpɛ'ɛma, tuna bɔ'ɔra Itoopia Pɔgenaba Kandase, sɔna a pɔgenaba wa tarega za'a biha. A wa'am Yerusalɛm na ti a na'ahɛ la Yinɛ.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: gee nyaa lɛbera kula, nyaa yaga a yiiri toto zuo, gee kãala nɔtɔgeha Azaaya gɔŋɔ la puan.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Ti Fa'a Sia la yele Filipi yeti, Zɔ paaɛ yiiri toto wa, la fum zom.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Bala ti Filipi zɔ ta paaɛ yiiri toto la, wum ti buraa wa kãalɛ la Azaaya gɔŋɔ la puan, ti a soke en yeti, Fum bɔkɛ fum kãalɛ sɛla wa.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Ti buraa wa lerege en yeti, N yeti n iŋɛ la ŋwani bɔkɛ, han dagena nɛra n suŋɛ mam? Gee nyaa yi' Filipi ti a kɛ̃ na zi'ire lɛm en.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Gulehegɔ la ze'sɛka ti a kãala n yeti se'em n ŋwana. Ba nyɔkɛ en mɛ wuu pehego la ta bo' se'em n kuuri duhi, a ani la bããlam wuu pehego en ani bããlam se'em gee ti ba kɛɛra ku kɔberɔ la, bala mɛa ti a ana bããlam ka ya'ɛ a nuurɛ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Ba diihe en la nyanɛ, ka bo' en buurɔ. Ani n wan ta'am pa'alɛ a nɛreba? Se'ere n sɔi la ba yehe a vom tiŋa wa zuo mɛ.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Ti Bulɛ wa soke Filipi yeti, N soke fum se'ere, Nɔtɔgeha wa tɔgeri la ani yele? A tɔgeri la a mɛŋa yele n bala bii ayima me ha.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Ti Filipi ya'ɛ a nuurɛ pɔhɛ la gulehegɔ kuŋɔ mɛa puan, muulɛ Yezu faarɛ yelehum la bo' en.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Ti ban boi sore zuo yɛ'a la, ti Bulɛ wa ta yeti, Bihɛ, ko'om n ŋwana ha. Beni n wan gu' mam ti n kan to'e ko'om sua.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Ti buraa wa bo' nuurɛ ti yiiri toto la ze'ele. Ti Filipi la Bulɛ la za'a lagum sige na, kiŋɛ paaɛ sige ko'om la puan, ti Filipi sɔ en ko'om.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Ban ze'ele ko'om la puan kɔ'ɔm dooe na la , ti Daana Fa'a Sia la zɛɛ Filipi kiŋɛ ti Bulɛ wa ka le nyɛ en, gee to'e a sore kula gee nyɛɛna.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Gee Filipi ta yeti a bihɛ la ti a boi la Azotuse, ti n dole tiŋa la puan tɔla la, a muuli la faarɛ yelehum la bɔ'ɔra tihi la puan tara tara ta paaɛ Kasariya tiŋa.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Ŋwana woo en daa iti la, Sɔɔl me daa kelum mu'ɛ la nini tara nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega la kua yɛla dɔla Daana la zabera la, nyaa pun kiŋɛ malema nɛŋadaana la zi'an.

eng: seeking letters to the synagogues in Damascus. If he found persons who belonged to the Way, whether men or women, these letters would authorize him to take them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
local: ta yele en ti a bo' en gɔnɔ ti a tari kiŋɛ Damasekuse zamehegɔ yiya la puan ta bihɛ a han nyɛ ti se'em boi bini dɔla Sore la, dɛna buraahi bii pɔgehi, a nyɔgɛ ba bobe ba lebe Yerusalɛm na.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Nananawa, en ye' ta pɔra Damasekuse la, a yeti a bihɛ ti bugum en bala ze'ele saazuo nyakɛ kaaɛ en mum.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Ti a lu tiŋa, gee wum ti kua yi'ira en yeti, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, ŋwani ti fum dɔla mɛleha mam.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Ti Sɔɔl lebehe soke yeti, Fum dela ani n Daana? Ti leregere wa'am na yeti, Mam dela Yezu, se'em ti fum dɔla mɛleha la.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Gee ihege kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, gee nɛra wan yele fum sɛla en de fum iŋɛ.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Ti burase'ehi n dole en kina la, maam zɛ'a se'ere en sɔi la, ba wum la kua gee ka nyɛ se'em.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Ti Sɔɔl ihege tiŋa la, ti a nini piga, gee a ka nyɛti sɛla. Bala ti ba gi' a nu'o vɛ'ɛra en ta kɛ̃' Damasekuse.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: Dabeha atã ti Sɔɔl fɔ ka nyɛta, gee me ka di bii nyu sɛla.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Ŋwana woo la, ti Yezu dɔla ayima n boi Damasekuse la puan ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Ananiya. Ti a nyɛ lilegere, ti Daana la tɔgɛ bo' lilegere la puan yeti, Ananiya, ti a lerege yeti, Bihɛ Mam n ŋwana n Daana.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Ti Daana la yele en yeti, Ihege kiŋɛ sadɔɔ seko ti ba yi'ira kɔ tuuntɔɔ la, la fum ɛ Yudasi yire, kɛ̃ de puan soke buraa n yehe Tasuse na ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Sɔɔl. Bihɛ, Nananawa, buraa wa boi la pu'uhegɔ puan.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: ti la lilege bo' en ti a nyɛ ti buraa yu'urɛ en yi'iri Ananiya kɛ̃ a zi'an na, dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo ti a lebe nyɛta.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Gee Ananiya lerege en yeti, Daana, Mam wum nɛreba zo'e zo'e , be'em zo'e zo'e sebo taaba ti buraa kana tum fum nɛresunduma en boi Yerusalɛm.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: gee nyaa to'e kpe'eŋo ka'lam, malema kiinduma la zi'an ti a pagɛ sɛba woo n yi'iri fum yu'urɛ la.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Gee Daana la yele en yeti, Kiŋɛ, gee a dela lɔkɔ ti mam looe ti a tara mam yu'urɛ wa bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, ba naduma, la Izirale buuri la.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Mam mɛŋa wan pa'alɛ en, en de a namehɛ namehɛ kãra sɛ'a mam yu'urɛ wa muulegɔ zuo.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ŋwana ti Ananiya kiŋɛ, ta paaɛ kɛ̃ yire la puan. Dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo, gee yele en yeti, N suuɔ Sɔɔl, Daana Yezu se'em n niiɛ a mɛa pa'alɛ hɔ fum boni sore zuo yɛ'a ka'lam na la, n tum mam ti n wa'am na ti fum nini la le nyɛta, gee ti Fa'a Sia la me pirɛ hɔ.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Bilam bilam ti sɛla ana wuu zim pɔgerɔ la ze'ele a nini la puan lu na, ti a nini la le nyɛta. Ti Sɔɔl nyaa ihege ti a bo' en ko'om sua.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: Ti en di dia la, ti a paŋa le lebe na. Dabeha zo'e zo'e ti a bɔna la Yezu zamehereba la Damasekuse.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Bilam bilam ti Sɔɔl pɔhɛ muula Yezu yele zamehegɔ yire la puan yeti, Yezu sirum dela Yinɛ Dayua.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Ti la iŋɛ sɛba woo en kelehe la pakerɛ, ti ba sɔkera yeti, Dagi buraa kana n bala n pɔgelum nɛreba Yerusalɛm ha ti ba yi'iri yu'dina wa zuo la? A ka wa'am ka'lam na bala zuo na? Ti a nyɔgɛ ba mɛ bobe ta bo' malema kiinduma la.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Ti Sɔɔl malum pa'ahera la paŋa, tɔgɛ nyaŋɛ Yuuduma la en ki'ire Damasekuse la, doohe la eŋa en nyaŋɛ pa'alɛ ba sira sira ti Yezu sirum dela Kiresta la.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Lan iŋɛ dabeha fii la, ti Yuuduma la ɛɛra sose'ere ti wan doohe ku en.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: gee Sɔɔl baŋɛ ba maaheŋɔ la za'a mɛ, ba guri tiŋa la nayahi la za'a mɛ pigera bulika la yu'uŋɔ za'a ti ba wan nyɛ Sɔɔl ku.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: gee zahereba la basɛba tari en la yu'uŋɔ bahɛ ti a sige pi'ɔ puan dikɛ doohe nangoone zuo sigehera pi'ɔ la fifihe ti ku ta ze'ele tiŋa.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Sɔɔl n wa'am na paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, maahum a mɛŋa ti a lagum Yezu zahereba la, gee ba za'a me zoti en me, se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka nam ti a dela Yezu zahera.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Gee ti Banabase to'e en, tari en wa'am tuntuneba la zi'an na, nyaa pa'alɛ ba sore la zuo ti a nyɛ Daana la se'em, ti a tɔgɛ bo' en se'em, la en ze'ele Damasekuse muulɛ la buraanɛ Yezu yu'urɛ la puan se'em.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Bala ti Sɔɔl bɔna la ba, ti ba lagum kina gee lɛbera na Yerusalɛm puan, tɔgera la buraanɛ Daana yu'urɛ la puan.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Ti Sɔɔl tɔgera gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la Hiburuduma sɛba en tɔgeri Giriki la, ti ba maahum ti ba ku en.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Ti sakereba la n ta baŋɛ la, ba tari en kiŋɛ la Kasariya, ta bahɛ ti a tole Tasuse.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Gee ŋwana ma'a saŋa la, ti Lageŋɔ la n boi Yudiya, Galilii, la Samariya tihi puan la za'a nyaa tari la suma'ahum, gee ze'ele suŋa. Ti ba kina la Daana la zoolum Fa'a Sia la sumbugehum puan, ti ba zɔ'ɔra ba kãlɛ puan.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Pita n daa dole kaara la, a ta kiŋɛ la nɛresunduma sɛba n boi Lida la.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Bilam ti a nyɛ buraa ti ka yu'urɛ n yi'iri Aniyase, n bɛ̃' gã'a ka suŋɔ zuo yuuma anii, dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Ti Pita yele en yeti, Aniyase, Yezu Kiresta tibɛ hɔ mɛ. Ihege la fum kilum fum suŋɔ la. Ti bilam bilam, ti a ihege.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Ti nɛresɛba woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri Lida la Saron tihi puan n nyɛ buraa wa la, ba ŋmɛregɛ la ba suya bo' Daana la.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Yopa puan, Yezu zahera ayima n boi bini ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Tabita ti de vuurɛ dɛna la Dɔkasi. A pirɛ itesuŋɔ la bu'a.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Sankaŋa puan la, ti a lu bã'a, ki. Ban sɔ en ba'ahɛ la, ba tari en ta biŋe la bɔ'ɔ n tugelum saazuo ha puan.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Lida tiŋa wa n ka zaaɛ la Yopa la, ti zahereba sɛba n boi la n wum ti Pita wa'am bini na la, ti ba tum buraahi siyi a zi'an na suhera en yeti, Wa'am tu zi'an kalum na, da gu'um.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Bala ti Pita sirum ihege doohe la buraahi la ti ba wa'am na paaɛ. Ti ba tari en zom bɔ'ɔ la n tugelum bɔna saazuo la. Ti pokɔpa la woo zɛ'a la en kɛla, gee pa'ala lɔge kãra la lɔge bibehi seto ti Dɔkasi pɛ en daa nan boi la ba la.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Ti Pita bahɛ ti ba za'a yehe, gee ti a kpa' duma pu'uhɛ, gee ŋmɛregɛ tɔ kum la gee yeti, Tabita, ihege. Ti kum la pikɛ bu nini, ti en nyɛ Pita la, ti a ihege zi'ire.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Ti Pita tiɛ a nu'o gurɛ en, suŋɛ en ti a ihege ze'ele. Ti a nyaa yi' nɛresunduma la la pokɔpa la, dikɛ en voto pa'alɛ ba.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Ti yele dina wa to'e Yopa za'a, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam Daana la.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Ti Pita kɛ̃ Yopa puan dabeha kekeko la buraa kayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Simɔn n de gbanzaba.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Buraa kayima n boi Kasariya puan, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'iri Koneliyose, bihera pɛntiireba kɔbega ti ba yi'ira ba Itali tansugere.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: A dela nɛra suŋɔ paaɛ paaɛ, dɛna Yinɛ zɔta eŋa la a yire za'a, gee bɔ'ɔra nɛreba yabi yabi gee pu'uha Yinɛ saŋa wuu.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Ti daarɛ deyima wuntɛɛŋa gogo kaareŋa atã bɔba, ti a nyɛ ti lilegere ti de yu'ɛ, bo' en, ti a nyɛ ti Yinɛ Suŋera ayima kini na gee yɛta en yeti, Koneliyose.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Ti a bihɛ Suŋera la ti dabeem kɛ̃ en paaɛ paaɛ, gee soke en yeti? La dela beni Daana? Ti la lerege en yeti, Fum pu'uhegɔ, la fum bu'a la za'a zom paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan mɛ dɛna tuulum tiihegɔ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Bala zuo la, tum nɛreba Yopa ti ba ta ɛ nɛra ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Simɔn ti ba yi'ira Pita la zi'an.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: A kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la Simɔn se'em n de gbanzaba la yire n boi ateko nuuren la.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Suŋera la n tɔgɛ bo' en ba'ahɛ gee kiŋɛ la, a yi' la a yire la yamehi siyi, la pɛntiira suŋɔ se'em kpe'eŋo en zuni la sɛba en biheri en daare woo la.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: yele ba sɛla woo, gee tum ba Yopa.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Ti yiimbeere wuu wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti ban boi sore zuo pɔra tiŋa la puan la, bala ma'a ti Pita me zom gɔhegɔ zuo ti a pu'uhɛ.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Sankaŋa puan la, ti a kom duna ti a bɔta sɛla ti a di, ti ban boi bini dugera la, ti a zɛɛ guum zaahum.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: A nyɛ mɛ ti saazuo yu'ɛ ti sɛla ana wuu ganɛ n karegɛ, yirege ti sɛla gurɛ a nɔya anaahi la ze'ele saazuo sigehera en fiifiihe tiŋa wa zuo na.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Ti duhi nama anaahi anaahi, bunvuula, la saazuo niihe en boi ku puan.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ti a wum ti kua yɛta en yeti, Pita, ihege la fum ku ba obe.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Gee Pita yeti, La kan ta'am iŋɛ bala n Daana, se'ere n sɔi la, mam ka tabelɛ di galeŋɔ bii kihere sɛla.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Ti kua le la yele en yi'i yeti, Sɛla ti Yinɛ pee, da yi' la kihere bunɔ.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Lilegere dina wa iŋɛ la bala sugum nuuren butã, ti n yeti a bihɛ la, ti ganɛ la zom la saazuo.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Pita n ze' yɛli ti'iha lilegere vuurɛ en de se'em la, a yeti bihɛ la ti buraahi la ti Koneliyose tum la n bala paara na, zɛ'a Simɔn yire zanɔren, gee sɔkera de yele.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Nyaa yi'ira baha na ti ba soke nyaa Simɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Pita la kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la yire la puan.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Pita n kelum bɔna bini ti'iha lilegere la yele la, ti Fa'a Sia la yele en yeti, Bihɛ, buraahi sitã n eere fum.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Nananawa, ihege sige tu'uhɛ ba, la fum doohe la ba kiŋɛ, da bɔ'ɔra, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tum ba.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita sige ta paaɛ buraahi la yele ba yeti, Bihɛ ya, Mam n bala ti ya ɛɛra la, beni zuo ti ya kina na.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Ti ba lerege yeti, Koneliyose n de pɛntiireba kɔbega nɛŋadaana, dɛna nɛre tuuntɔɔ, dɛna Yinɛ zɔta la, ti Yuuduma la za'a yi'ira en yu'sunɛ la, ti Fa'a Suŋera pa'alɛ en ti a tum yi' fum ti fum wa'am a yire na ti a wum fum tari se'ere ti fum yele.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita nyaa tari ba kɛ̃, ta bo' ba ga'aregɔ zi'an. Ti yiimbeere ti a ihege doohe la ba, ti Yopa suurɔ basɛba, lagum kɔlegɛ en.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Yiimbeere ti ba wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya puan. Koneliyose n pun gura ba la zuo, a yi' a mabiihi la sɛba n ba' dɛna a zɔduma lagehɛ na.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Pita n wa'am na paaɛ la, ti Koneliyose tu'uhɛ en, dɔbelɛ a nɛŋan na'ahɛ en.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Gee Pita yele en mɛ yeti, Ihege, mam me yeen dɛna la asaala.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Ti Pita n tɔgɛ la en la, a kɛ̃ deen ta nyɛ ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e n lagehɛ.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: ti a yele ba yeti, Ya mɛhi mi' ti la dela kihere bo' Yuda nɛra, ti a lagum bii a wa'am na kaaɛ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, gee Yinɛ n lilege pa'alɛ mam yeti, n da yi' asaala ti kihere bii galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Bala zuo ban tum yi' mam la, n ka de n zagehɛ. Gee nyaa bahɛ ti n soke se'ere zuo ti ya tum yi' mam la.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Ti Koneliyose lerege yeti. Dabeha anaahi n ŋwana zina, ti mam wa pu'uha n yire, wuu sankana wa, gogo kaareŋa atã bɔba, mam yeti n bihɛ ti buraa n yɛ lɔgerɔ ti tu nyagera bala zɛ'a mam la.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: Yele mam yeti, Koneliyose, fum pu'uhegɔ la fum bu'a la paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan mɛ dɛna tuulum tiihegɔ.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Bala, tum nɛreba Yopa, ti ba ta soke Simɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Pita la yele. A kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la Simɔn la n de gbanzaba yire n lɛm la ateko la puan.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti n kɔ'ɔm tum ba fum zi'an na, ti fum me iŋɛ sum zo'e zo'e wa'am na wa. Bala zuo la, tu za'a ze' la Yinɛ nɛŋan ka'lam ti tu kelehe sɛla woo ti Daana bo' fum nuurɛ ti fum yele tu.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Ti Pita pɔhɛ ya'ɛ a nuurɛ tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba yeti, Mam nananawa yelemɛŋerɛ bɔkɛ ya ti Yinɛ ka gaheri nɛreba.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: gee tiŋa woo puan, se'em n zoti en, gee tuna sɛla n de ma'a ti Yinɛ bɔta.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Ya mi' yelehum sebo ti a yuum tum bo' Izirale buuri la, muula suma'ahum yelehum Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa n de sɛla woo Daana la.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Ya mi ti Yelehum buna wa to'e Yuda za'a mɛ, pɔhɛ Galilii, Yɔn en yuum muulɛ ko'om sua yelehum ba'ahɛ la.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Yinɛ n bi'ihɛ Yezu en yehe Nazareti na la, la Fa'a Sia, la paŋa se'em, la en yuum eere tuna sum se'em, gee tibera sɛba woo ti Sagena la mugeha ba, se'ere n sɔi Yinɛ en boi la en zuo.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Tumam dela aze' nyɛ duma bo' en tum yele sɛ'a woo Yudiya la Yerusalɛm tihi puan, ti ba ku en doohe la ban yule en tia zuo la;.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: gee ti Yinɛ vo'e en dabeha atã daarɛ, gee bo' en fole ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: A yuum ka niiɛ a mɛŋa bo' la nɛra woo, gee tumam sɛba ti Yinɛ pun looe ti tu dɛna aze' nyɛ duma bo' en la ma'a, tumam sɛba n di gee nyu la en, en ihege a kum la puan la.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Ti a bo' tu nuurɛ ti tu tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba, gee muulɛ ti eŋa n de se'em ti Yinɛ looe en ti a dɛna vɔpa la ki'induma gakera.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Nɔtɔgeheba la woo yuum yele la eŋa yele ti, se'em woo n nam en, a be'em wan saalum Yezu yu'urɛ la zuo.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Pita n boi bini tɔgera ŋwana la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige sɛba woo n boi bini wum yelehum la zuo na.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Ti sakereba la en ŋma en doohe Pita na la zɛ'a yɛli, la Fa'a Sia bo'olum la n lagum sige sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zuo.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, ba wum nɛreba la n tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri, gee na'aha Yinɛ se'em la. Ŋwana ti Pita yeti.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Se'em boi wan ta'am gu' nɛreba wa ti ba da sɔ ko'om, sɛba n to'e Fa'a Sia la bo'olum wa wuu tumam wa.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Ŋwana ti Pita nyaa bo' sore ti ba sɔ nɛreba la ko'om Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ puan. Ti nɛreba yele Pita ti a kɛ̃ la ba yuue fii wa.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Nananawa,Yezu tuntuneba la, la sakereba la za'a n boi Yuda tiŋa la wum ti, sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la me nam Yinɛ yelehum la mɛ.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Bala, Pita n lebe zom Yerusalɛm la, sakereba sɛba n ŋma la zuuheri en mɛ.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: yeti, Ŋwani ti fum kiŋɛ sɛba n ka ŋma la zi'an, pun ta di la ba.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Ti Pita pɔhɛ yele yele pa'ala ba yeti.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: Mam wa bɔna la Yopa puan pu'uha, ta zɛɛ guum, nyɛ lilegere. Lilegere la puan, mam nyɛ sɛla n karegɛ wuu ganɛ la ti ba dikɛ sɛla lu' ku nɔya anaahi la ze'ele saazuo ha bahera ku fiifiihe na, wa'am na lɛm mam.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Ti mam n bihɛ ku zuo suŋa suŋa la, mam nyɛ duhi en tari nama anaahi anaahi, la muuen duhi, bãhi buuri, la niihe wa en ɛgeri wa.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Ti n wum kua, ti ka yɛta mam yeti, Ihege, Pita; la fum ku ba obe.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ti mam yeti, La kan ta'am iŋɛ n Daana, se'ere n sɔi la, sɛla n de galeŋɔ bii kihere lɔkɔ ka tabelɛ tole mam nuurɛ wa puan.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Gee yi'i puan, kua la ze'ele saazuo lerege mam na yeti, Sɛla ti Yinɛ pee, da yi' la galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Bunɔ wa iŋɛ la butã gee ti ba lebehe ve'e sɛla woo zom saazuo ha.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Bihɛ, sankaŋa mɛa puan, ti buraahi sitã ti ba tum ba ti ba ze'ele Kasariya na, paara tumam n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri yise'ere puan la.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Ti Sia la yele mam yeti n doohe en kiŋɛ gee da gahera bamam la tumam tiŋasuka. Ti tu suurɔ tuyoobe wa n doohe mam ti tu kiŋɛ ta paaɛ kɛ̃ buraa la yire.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Ti buraa wa, yele tu eŋa n nyɛ Suŋera ti a zɛ'a a yire la puan gee yele eŋa yeti, Tum nɛreba Yopa, ti ba kiŋɛ ta tari Simɔn, se'em yu'urɛ n yi'iri Pita la na.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: a wan bo' ya yelehum, ti fum la fum yire wa za'a wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Ti mam n pɔhɛ tugum bini la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige ba zuo na, wuu kaŋa n yuum sige tumam zuo se'em pɔhega saŋa la.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Ti mam tiɛ tu Daana yelehum la, en daa wa yeti, Yɔn sɔɔri ya mɛ la ko'om, gee ba wan ta sɔ yamam mɛ la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: Bala zuo,Yinɛ mɛŋa han bo' ba bo'olum bumɔ mɛa, sebo ti a daa tumam n daa nam bo' Daana Yezu Kiresta la, mam dela ani wan ta'am gu' Yinɛ.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana wa la, ba yee la suram. Gee pɛgera Yinɛ yeti, Yinɛ mɛŋa n bo' sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la me teere se'ere n bo'ori vom la.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Nananawa, sɛba woo n zɔ yiregɛ doohe la ban mɛlehɛ Siteven se'em la zuo la, yiregɛ mɛ kiŋɛ zaaɛ zaarɛ ta paaɛ Fonisiya, Sapuruse, la Antiyoke tihi, ka muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' nɛra nɛra han dagena Yuda buuri la ma'a.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Gee ba puan, buraahi sise'ehi n de Sapuruse, la Saarin tihi wa'am Antiyoke na tɔgɛ bo' sɛba n de Hiburuduma gee tɔgera Giriki la, me muulɛ Daana Yezu bo' ba.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Ti Daana la nu'o paga ba zuo, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e sakɛ, dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Daana la.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Ti yele ana wa woo lu Yerusalɛm lageŋɔ la woo tuban, ti ba tum Banabase Antiyoke.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Banabase n wa'am na paaɛ nyɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la n sige nɛreba la zuo se'em la, a puurɛ n pee, ti a kpemehe ba za'a yeti, ba bɔna ba sakerɛ la puan la Daana la, la ba kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' en saŋa woo;.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: se'ere n sɔi la, Banabase wa dela nɛresuŋɔ, pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la, la sakerɛ, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am Daana la zi'an na.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Ti Banabase ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ Tasuse ti a ta ɛ Sɔɔl.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: En nyɛ Sɔɔl la, a tari en wa'am la Antiyoke na. Ba boi bini la bala yuum mune, lagena la Lageŋɔ la, pa'alɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ. Antiyoke puan ti nɛreba pɔhɛ yi' Yezu zahereba la yu'urɛ ti Kiresta kɔma.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Sankaŋa puan, ti nɔtɔgeheba ze'ele Yerusalɛm sige Antiyoke na.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Ti ba puan ayima yu'urɛ n yi'ira Agabuse, ti Sia la bo' en paŋa ti a vaaɛ ihege ze'ele purɛ yeti, kompɛɛlega wan sige timpaberɛ zuo zi'an woo. Ti la sirum wa'am na iŋɛ bala Kulodeyose Kaiza saŋa puan.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la kɔ'ɔm lu' ba puti'irɛ ti ba woo wan bo' Sakereba la en boi Yuda puan la suŋɛ doohe la nɛra woo paŋa n wan ta'am se'em bo'.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Ti ba sirum iŋɛ yele dina wa, dikɛ ba suŋɛ bo' Banabase la Sɔɔl ti ba tari ta bo' kiinduma.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Antiyoke sakereba lageŋɔ la puan, nɔtɔgeheba la pa'aleba n boi bini. Banabase, Simeyɔn se'em ti ba yi'ira Naiga la, dikɛ Lusiyuse n yehe Saarin, Manayin n de Naba Hɛrɔd kiima ayima la, nyaa dikɛ Sɔɔl.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti ba bɔna Daana la na'ahegɔ puan la nɔlurɛ, ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgɛ yeti, Yehe ya Banabase la Sɔɔl ze'ele bo' tuunse'ere ti mam yi' ba ti ba tum la.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Ti ban ta ba'ahɛ nɔlurɛ la pu'uhegɔ la, ba digehɛ la ba nu'uhi pagelɛ Banabase la Sɔɔl zuo gee bahɛ ba ti ba kiŋɛ.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Bala, Fa'a Sia la n tum ba ti ba yehe la, ba kiŋɛ Kilikiya, ze'ele bilam zom uureŋɔ kaaɛ Sapuruse.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Ban ta paaɛ Salamise tiŋa la, ba muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la mɛ Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire la puan. Ti Yɔn bɔna lagum suŋɛ ba.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Ban doohe tiŋa la n boi ko'om tiŋasuka la za'a ta paaɛ Pafose la, ba tu'uhɛ la tiim daana ayima n de Yuda buuri nɔtɔgehe pumparema, ti ba yi'ira en Baa-Yezu.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: A boi mɛ la nɛŋa daana Segiyose Pɔɔlusi, en de mi'ilum daana yi' Barabase la Sɔɔl, bɔta ti a wum Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Gee tiindaana Elymas , eŋa ka sakɛ bo' ba, gee zigera ti a tee nɛŋadaana wa puti'irɛ yehe sakerɛ la puan.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Gee Sɔɔl me, se'em ti ba tee yi'ira Pɔɔl la, pirɛ la Fa'a Sia la bihɛ en zii.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: gee yele en yeti, Sagena dayua wa, fum se'em n zaberɛ la tuuntɔɔ vom wa. Fum pirɛ mɛ la pa'ahegɔ la ŋmɛlema ma'a. Fum kan bahɛ fum dole ŋmɛlena Daana tuuntɔɔ sɔya wa?.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Nananawa kelehe, Daana la nu'o n zaberɛ la hɔ, fum wan fɔ pun kan nyɛ yintule wa ta paaɛ saŋa kayima. Bilam bilam ti puuhego la lika pi' en ti a tu'una, ɛɛra nɛra gurɛ a nu'o.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Gee nɛŋadaana wa n nyɛ sɛla n iŋɛ la ti a nam yelehum la se'ere n sɔi la Daana la pa'alegɔ la iŋɛ en la pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Ti Pɔɔl la a tuntuna tadaana wa zom uureŋɔ Pafose wa'am na paaɛ Piga en boi Pamfiliya puan. Gee Yɔn bahɛ ba mɛ gee lebe Yerusalɛm.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: gee ti bamam ze'ele Piga wa'am na paaɛ Antiyoke n boi Pisidiya puan. Ti vo'ohego daarɛ ti ba kɛ̃ zamehegɔ yire la puan, ta zi'ire.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Ban kãalɛ Moose la nɔtɔgeheba ze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan ba'ahɛ la, ti zamehegɔ yire la puan kpɛ'ɛnduma la tum yele ba na yeti, Tu suurɔ wa, Ya han tara kpemehego seko ti ya bo' nɛreba wa, bo' ya na.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl ihege ze'ele suŋa, gee pɔhɛ tugum yeti, Yamam Izirale buuri, la yamam sɛba woo n zoti Yinɛ, kelehe ya.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Yinɛ se'ere n sɔi Izirale buuri wa la, yuum looe la tumam sɔyaabeduma, gee iŋɛ Izirale buuri wa ti ba dɛna nɛrekãreba ban yuum nan boi Igipiti puan la. Ti Yinɛ mɛŋa zɛkɛ a nu'o tari ba ze'ele Igipiti la puan yehe na.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: bɔna la ba muuen di'iha suure la ba, ta paaɛ wuu yuum pihinaahi.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Ti Yinɛ ta sagum tihi siyopɔi nɛreba za'a, Keenan sɔ'ɔlum puan, gee nyaa dikɛ ba tihi la bo' Izirale buuri wa ti la dɛna ba sɔ'ɔlum.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: yele ana woo n pi'ilum wa'am na paaɛ zina de wuu yuuma kɔbehi sinaahi la yuum pihinuu. Bilam n tole la, ti Yinɛ wa bo' ba kima ti ba biha ba, tara tara ta paaɛ nɔtɔgeha Samuwɛle saŋa.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Saŋa kana puan ti Izirale buuri soke ti bamam boti la Naba, ti Yinɛ yehe Sɔɔl n de Kis dayua, n yehe Benyamin buuri puan bo' ba, ti a bihɛ ba yuuma pihinaahi.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Yinɛ n ta fooe Sɔɔl la, a tee dikɛ la Deviti zi'ire na'am kuka la zuo. Yinɛ n wa yeti a tɔgɛ Naba Deviti yele la, a yeti, Mam nyɛ Yesi dayua Deviti wa ti a dɛna la se'em ti mam suure kɛ̃ paaɛ paaɛ, a wan ta'am tum mam sumboolum n de se'em la.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Buraa kana wa yaahi puan ti Yinɛ doohe tari faara la n de Yezu wa'am Izirale puan na wuu en yuum biŋe a nuurɛ se'em la.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Faara la kinɛ na la, ti Yɔn pun diŋɛ nɛŋa wa'am na tɔgɛ suteere ko'om sua yele bo' Izirale buuri la mɛ za'a.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Yɔn n wa pɔra a tuuma la ba'ahegɔ la, a yele a nɛreba la yeti, Yamam ti'ihɛ mam dela ani? Mam dagi se'em ti ya gura la. Aayi, eŋa ye' la mam pooren na. Mam ka seke n zãla a nama tagera.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: N suurɔ wa, Aberaham yireduma yaahi wa, la yamam sɛba n zoti Yinɛ wa, Yinɛ tum faarɛ yelehum buna wa bo' la tumam na.

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Gee sɛba n ki'ire Yerusalɛm la la ba nɛheduma la daa ka baŋɛ en, bii nɔtɔgeheba la yelehum la ti nɛreba daa kãala pa'ala ba vo'ohego dabeherɛ woo puan la. Ba tugum daa bahɛ ti yelehum buna wa niiɛ la asira na, doohe la ban gakɛ en gee ku en la.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: La ban daa pun ka nyɛ yele se'ere ti ba wan ze'ele de zuo ku en la, ba daa kelum yele Palete mɛ ti a bahɛ ti ba ku' en.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Ban doohe tum sɛla woo n gulehɛ a yele se'em ba'ahɛ la, ba yakɛ en mɛ tia la zuo, tari en ta laaɛ yɔɔ puan.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Gee Yinɛ vo'e en mɛ kum la puan.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: Ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa dabeha kekeko pa'alɛ sɛba n doohe la en ze'ele Galilii ta paaɛ Yerusalɛm, bamam n nyaa de aze' nyɛ duma boi a nɛreba.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Tumam tari la yelesuma la wa'am na, ti sɛla ti Yinɛ yuum biŋe a nuurɛ la tu yaabeduma la.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: a tum dɛ bo' la tumam n de ba kɔma la, doohe la n ihege Yezu a kum la puan wuu lan gulehɛ se'em bɔna yuuma gɔŋɔ zuo buyi' puan se'em la. La gulehɛ yeti, Fum dela mam Dayua, Zina mam dɔgɛ hɔ.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: En tɔgɛ se'em doohe en wan vo'e en a kum la puan ti a kan le lebe pu'urɛ puan. N wan bo' fum tuulum yɛla la sum sebo ti n biŋe nuurɛ la Deviti ti n bo' la.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Bala zuo la, a le yele yuuma gɔŋɔ la puan ze'a kayima yeti, Fum kan bahɛ ti fu tuulum nɛra la pɔ'ɛ.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Naba Deviti n daa ta tum a Yinɛ la n boti a tum a mɛŋa saŋa la puan ba'ahɛ la, a ki mɛ, ti ba pi' en lɛm a yaabeduma, ti a pɔ'ɛ.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Gee eŋa se'em daana ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum puan la ka makɛ pɔ'ɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Bahɛ ti n yele ya ti ya baŋɛ zina n suurɔ, ti la doohe la buraa kana Yezu Kiresta wa puan, ti tu muula sugeri dia yelehum wa bɔ'ɔra ya wa la.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Doohe la Yezu wa zuo se'em woo n nam wan nyɛ faarɛ a be'em sebo woo puan. Be'em sebo woo ti Moose pa'alegɔ la daa kan ta'am faaɛ ya bu puan la.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Bala zuo, bihɛ ya suŋa ti sɛla ti nɔtɔgeheba la purɛ biŋe la da sige ya zuo. La yeti.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Bihɛ ya, Yamam sɛba duma n ka dikɛ Yinɛ yelehum yi' sɛla wa, Bahɛ ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ ti ya bahɛ yoo, se'ere n sɔi la, mam tuni mɛ ya vom dabeha wa puan. N tuni la tuunsɛ'a ti ya kan malum sakɛ, nɛra han pun yele ya. .

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Pɔɔl la Banabase en ta yɛ'a yɛha la, ti nɛreba la bɛlena ba yeti, ba daan tɔgɛ yele ana mɛa bo' ba vo'ohego dabeherɛ se'ere n kini na la.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Ban ta ba'ahɛ lageŋɔ la zamehegɔ yire la puan la, Ti Yuda buuri la zo'e zo'e la sɛba n kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' Yuda pu'uhegɔ la dɔla Pɔɔl la Banabase poore, ti ba tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba gee kpɛmeha ti ba kelum bɔna Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la puan.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ la en le ta paaɛ la, la ŋwani ti tiŋa la za'a nɛreba n wa'am na ti ba kelehe Daana la yelehum.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Gee Yuuduma la n nyɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la, nyuure n kɛ̃ ba, ti ba pɔhɛ tuura, gee zɛregera Pɔɔl n tɔgɛ se'em la.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Ti Pɔɔl la Banabase tɔgɛ la buraanɛ yele ba yeti, La nari mɛ ti ba muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' yamam yia. Gee yamam n zagehɛ ka nam bu la, gee gakɛ ya ti yamam ka nari vom kamba'ahɛ wa la, tu nyaa ŋmɛregera lɛbera la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zi'an.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Ŋwana wa la zuo, Daana pun bo' tumam a nuurɛ mɛ yeti, Mam iŋɛ ya ti ya dɛna la niihum bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, Ti ya bahɛ ti faarɛ paaɛ timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo. .

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la n wum ŋwana la, ba pee puurɛ mɛ, ti ba pɛgera Daana la yelehum la. Bama sɛba ti ba pun maahum ti ba nyɛ vomkan ba'ahɛ la bamam en nam.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Yinɛ yelehum la n yiregɛ zi'an la puan se'em n bala.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Gee Yuuduma la doohe mɛ dɔrɛ pɔge se'ehi en kaabɛ si mɛhi bo' pu'uhegɔ la, mina si nɛŋa si poore la, la buraahi se'ehi n de zuto duma tiŋa la puan la, ti ba gbirege Pɔɔl la Banabase, sa'ɛ ba yehe tiŋa la puan.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl la Banabase kpa'ahɛ ba nama puuhego bahɛ ti la dɛna yele bo' tiŋa la, gee nyaa kiŋɛ Ikonium tiŋa.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Ti Yezu zahereba la pirɛ la nyɛɛma la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Saŋa kaŋa mɛa puan ti naba Hɛrɔd tiɛ a nu'uhi la gbirege pagelɛ lageŋɔ nɛreba basɛba.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Bahɛ ti ba kɔregɛ Yemesi en de Yɔn la la su'a.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: En nyɛ ti la pee Yuuduma la puurɛ paaɛ paaɛ la, a kiŋɛ la nɛŋa, bahɛ ti ba nyɔkɛ Pita me. Ŋwana ma'a wa dela boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la saŋa la.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: En bahɛ ti ba nyɔkɛ en la, a tari en me na pagɛ la paka deo puan, dikɛ en bo' pɛntiireba tansuga anaahi n tari pɛntiireba ba anaahi banaahi ti ba gura en. Gee iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti Azɔɛtole de'eŋo la han tole, a yu'ɛ en yehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la puan na.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Ban pagɛ Pita paka deo la puan la, lageŋɔ la mu'ɛ la nini la pu'uhegɔ saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ a zuo.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Hɛrɔd n wa ti'iha ti a tari en yehe na la, yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ mɛa ti ba ba' Pita mɛ la bãhi, ti a giha pɛntiireba bayi' tiŋasuka gee ti pɛntiireba basɛba me zɛ'a yiŋa kuleŋa gura paka deo la.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ ti Daana suŋera n bala paka deo la puan, ti bugum niiɛ bini piim. Ti a paaɛ ŋmɛ' Pita lugerɛ yele en yeti, Ihege kalam. En tɔgɛ bala la, ti bãhi la n ba' Pita nu'uhi la zɔgɛ tiŋa.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Ti Suŋera la yele en yeti, Lu' fum sɛganɛ, lu' fum tagera la. Ti Pita iŋɛ bala. Ti a nyaa yeti, Lobe fum fuo la lu' suŋa la fum dɔla mam na.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Ti Pita kiŋɛ yehe na dɔla en, gee ka mina ti sɛla n iti eŋa la Suŋera la tiŋasuka la dela sira, a ti'ihɛ ti a nyɛti la lilegere.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Ban kiŋɛ gaŋɛ gura se'em n ze' yia la, le wa'am na kiŋɛ gaŋɛ yi'i gura la, ba wa'am na na paaɛ la kuto nayaŋa la n dole yɛha yɛ'a tiŋa la puan la. Ti kuto nayaŋa la mɛŋa yu'ɛ ka mɛŋa bo' ba, ti ba kiŋɛ yehe sige sore zuo kina, Pita ta yeti a bihɛ la ti suŋera la mɛlum ya gee bahɛ en.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Bilam ti Pita yɛm nyaa lebena, ti a yeti, Nananawa Mam mi' ti yelemɛŋerɛ Daana n tum a Suŋera ti a wa'am na faaɛ mam Hɛrɔd nu'uhen, la Yuuduma wa woo n ti'ihɛ ni ti yele se'ere paaɛ ni mam la.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: Pita n baŋɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ Mɛɛri n de Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n de Maki la ma Mɛɛri yire, zi'an ti sakereba zo'e zo'e lagehɛ pu'uha la.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: En paaɛ ŋmɛ' yire la yiŋa kuleŋa la, pɔgesɔɔ yu'urɛ en yi'iri Aroda n wa'am na sakɛ ŋmɛ'a la.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: En baŋɛ ti kua la dela Pita la, ti a puurɛ ba' pee gaŋɛ, ti en wan yu'ɛ ni kuleŋa la, a tugum bahɛ mɛ gee zɔ lebe ha ta muula yeti, Pita ze' kuleŋa nuuren ha.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Ti ba yele en yeti, Fum zuo ka tuni. Ti pugela wa sina gee sɛ zɛ'a kankaŋi ti la dela bala. Ti ba yele en yeti, Fum ye nyɛ la a suŋera.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Sankana woo la, ti Pita kelum ŋmɛ'ɛra kuleŋa la mɛ, ti ban yu'ɛ la, ba nyɛ en mɛ. Ti la iŋɛ ba pakerɛ paaɛ paaɛ .

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Ti Pita yibehɛ ba la nu'uhi ti ba da vɔɔla, gee nyaa pa'alɛ ba Daana la en iŋɛ se'em tari eŋa paka deo la puan yehe na. Gee pa'ahɛ yeti, Yele ya Yemesi yele dina wa suurɔ la za'a me. Gee nyaa bahɛ bilam gee tee kiŋɛ zi'an.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Beere n ta yilegɛ la, ti gbiregeri kãtɛ lu pɛntiireba la, doohe la beni n iŋɛ Pita.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Hɛrɔd bo' sore ti ba ɛ Pita la ninmu'urɛ ti ba ka nyɛ en la, ti a malum viihɛ gureba la, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba ku ba. Gee nyaa ze'ele Yuda kiŋɛ sige Kasariya, ta bɔna bilam.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Ti Hɛrɔd suure ba' nyiie la Taya la Sidɔn tihi nɛreba. Bala ti ba naaɛ taaba la nɔyinɛ tum ba nɛreba na ti ba wa'am na ŋmɛ' nu' pu'ɔ. Ti ba paaɛ na nyaŋɛ tɔgɛ ti Balastuse se'em en biheri naba bungã'ala la bɔkɛ ba. Ti ba nyaa suhɛ suma'ahum, se'ere n sɔi la, ba tihi la dele la naba tihi la nyɛta dia.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Ti suhega la dabeherɛ ta paaɛ, ti Hɛrɔd bagɛ a na'am lɔgerɔ, tole zi'ire a na'am kuka la zuo, nyaa tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba la woo n lagehɛ na la.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: Ti nɛreba la vɔɔla yeti, Ɛhɛɛ, yinɛ de mɛŋa kua n ŋwana gee dagi asaala.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Ti bilam bilam doohe la Naba wa n ka dikɛ na'ahegɔ wa bo' Yinɛ la zuo, ti Daana Suŋera lobe en la ba'a ti a gã'a ti zunzuya dita en tara tara ti a ta ki.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Gee Yinɛ yelehum la zo'ore mɛ yiregera.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Ti Banabase la Sɔɔl n ta ba'ahɛ tuunɛ se'ere ti ba wa'am bini na ti ba tum la, ba lebe la Yerusalɛm, ta tari Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n yi'ira Maki la lebena.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Pɔɔl la Silase n wa'am na gaŋɛ Amfipolisi la Apolonia tihi tole la, ba wa'am na paaɛ la Tɛsalonika, zi'an ti Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire bɔna bini.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃ bini wuu en ni pun ita se'em la, vo'ohego dabeha atã ti eŋa la Yuuduma la tɔgera yɛla gulehegɔ la puan.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: ti a pa'ala ba, gee niira bɔ'ɔra ba ti la de la Kiresta la namehɛ gee ihege kum puan. Gee nyaa yele ba yeti, Yezu kana wa, se'em ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa en de Kiresta la.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Ti ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la, dikɛ ba mɛhi pa'ahɛ Pɔɔl la Silase puan, ti sɛba me n de Giriki sɛba en de Yinɛ zɔteba la, me zo'e zo'e ta ta' la pɔgese'ehi n de zuo duma la me ka pɔ'ɛ.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Gee nyuure n kɛ̃ Yuuduma la basɛba, ti ba me nyɛ nɛreyaalehi sise'ehi n boi da'a, ti ba pa' ba poore, ti ba nyaa lagehɛ taaba bahɛ ti gbiregeri kãtɛ sige tiŋa la puan. Ban eere Pɔɔl la Silase ti ba tari ba na ze'ele nɛrekuuŋɔ tiŋasuka la, ba kiŋɛ ta zabɛ mɛ la Yason yire.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Ban ka nyɛ Pɔɔl la Silase bini la, ti ba vɛ' Yason la sakereba basɛba wa'am tiŋa la kpɛ'ɛnduma nɛŋan na, gee vɔɔla yeti, Nɛreba wa en dole tuleha timpaberɛ wa kpabera la, nyaa paaɛ ka'lam me na.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: ti, Yason to'e ba ki'ihɛ a yire. Ba za'a iti la sɛla n ka dole Kaiza ze'elego la. Ba yeti, naba ayima me n boi ha ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Yezu.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la kpɛ'ɛnduma la n wum ŋwana la, la daam ba mɛ.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Ti ban to'e bahega ligeri Yason la sɛba ti ba lagum nyɔgɛ ni la zi'an ba'ahɛ la, ba bahɛ ba mɛ ti ba kiŋɛ.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ mɛa puan ti sakereba la tari Pɔɔl la Silase ta bahɛ sore ti ba kiŋɛ Beroya tiŋa. Ban ta paaɛ bini la, ba kiŋɛ la Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire puan.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Yuuduma bana to'e ba gana Yuuduma sɛba n boi Tɛsalonika puan la mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, ba to'e yelehum la ti ba muulɛ bo' ba la mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, gee viihɛ gulehegɔ la puan daarɛ woo ti ba bihɛ nyaa yɛla la ani la bala bini.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Ŋwana zuo la, ba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la mɛ ta ta' la Giriki pɔgehi la buraahi zo'e zo'e n de zuoduma.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Gee Yuuduma sɛba n boi Tɛsalonika la n ta wum ti Pɔɔl le muulɛ yelehum la me, ba ɛ Beroya tiŋa puan mɛ, ti ba ihege wa'am bilam me na, ti ba gbirege gee tu'ule nɛrekuuŋɔ la.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Bilam bilam ti sakereba la tari Pɔɔl kalam kiŋɛ ateko ko'om nuuren, gee ti Silase la Timoti yee pooren.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Sɛba n tari Pɔɔl kina la, tari en kiŋɛ la zaaɛ zaarɛ ta paaɛ Atense, ti en pa'alɛ ba ba wan iŋɛ se'em bahɛ ti Silase la Timoti wa'am na paaɛ en ka'lam ba'ahɛ la, ti ba keehe en gee lebe.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Pɔɔl n boi Atense gee gura Silase la Timoti la, a suure sagum mɛ paaɛ paaɛ, la tinkãtɛ la n pirɛ la baga la.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Bala a pɔhɛ yetɔgum mɛ la zamehegɔ yire la puan la Yuuduma la la Yinɛ zɔteba la, la da'ahi puan daare woo la sɛba en wan bɔna bini.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Ti Epikureya la Sitoyiki yɛmduma wa'am na ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la en. Ti sɛba yeti, Gerego kuna me boti ku pa'alɛ la bem? Ti sɛba me yeti, A tɔgeri la tihi sise'ehi tingama yɛla.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Ti ba tari en wa'am Areopagus tugum zi'an la puan na, nyaa soke en yeti, Bahɛ ti tu baŋɛ pa'alesaaŋa wa ti fum pa'ala vuurɛ en de se'em?.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum tari la pa'alesaanɛ wa'am tu tuba puan na, bala ti tu bɔta ti tu baŋɛ ku vuurɛ.

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Atenseduma woo, la saama n ki'ire ka puan, ka tari ba saŋa ita sɛla gana ba yɛta bii ba kɛleha sɛla n de yele paalɛ.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Ti Pɔɔl ihege ze'ele Areopagus wa nɛŋan yeti, Atenseduma wa, mam nyɛ ti ya ba dɛna la Yinɛ pu'uheba sadɔseko woo puan paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, mam n doohe ya tiŋa wa za'a kaaɛ mum la, gee ma'ɛ n mɛŋa bihera ya lɔgeseto ti ya na'aha la, mam nyɛ la kaabegɔ bimbine deyima ti gulehegɔ gulehɛ iŋɛ de zuo yeti, Yinɛ se'ere ti tu ka mina. Bala zuo la, yinɛ se'ere ti ya na'aha gee ka mina la, Yinɛ dinɛ n bala ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Yinɛ se'ere n naam timpaberɛ wa la sɛla woo n boi de puan la. Yinɛ se'ere n de Saazuo la timpaberɛ wa zuo Daana la ka kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri zɔnɔ ti saaleba dikɛ nu'uhi mɛ puan.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Bii nɛreba tuna bɔ'ɔra en wuu a boti la sɛla, se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa en bo'ori saala woo vom, la sɛla woo vo'ohum.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Yinɛ wa naam la nɛra ayima ma'a, ti nɛre-ina wa puan ti nɛreba buuri buuri wa pirɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, eŋa en bu ba woo ba bɔŋa saŋa ba bɔŋa saŋa. Eŋa n bo' ba za'a ba beene ba beene la ba bɔŋa ze'a ba bɔŋa ze'a.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Yinɛ iŋɛ ŋwana wa, ti nɛreba wan ɛɛra en. Ba wan tu'una ɛɛra en, ta nyɛ en, gee ti Yinɛ me pun ka zaaɛ la tu se'em.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, Eŋa zuo ti tu vɔna, gee kina gee dɛna nɛreba wa, wuu ya mɛŋa mageha guleheba en yele se'em yeti, Se'ere n sɔi la, tumam me dela a kɔma la.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Tumam n yi de yinɛ kɔma la, la dagi ti tu ti'iha ti Yinɛ naanɛ wan ani mɛ wuu salema, bii salempɛɛlega bii kugere bii naanɛ se'ere ti ba gulɛ bii naanɛ bii ti'ihegɔ seko en yehe asaala na.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
