local: Saŋa daa boi ti Yinɛ daa ka dikɛ saaleba yɛmka'alum ti la dɛna daaŋɔ. Gee nananawa, a bo' nɛrawoo n boi ze'sɛka woo la nuurɛ ti ba tee.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, a looe la dabeherɛ biŋe, ti daan dinɛ daarɛ ti a wan gakɛ nɛrawoo n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo tuuntɔɔ. A wan doohe la buraa kayima ti a mɛŋa looe la, doohe eŋa puan ti putɛ'kɔ'ɔrɛ bɔna, eŋa n ihege en kum puan la.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Ban wum ti a purɛ a ki gee ihege la, ti basɛba pureha, gee basɛba yeti, Tu boti fum malum tɔgɛ yele ana wa pa'ahɛ mɛ ti tu kelehe.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Lan paaɛ ŋwana beene la, Pɔɔl bahɛ ba mɛ.

eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Gee ba puan basɛba yehe me na pa' Pɔɔl poore dɛna sakereba, Diyonisuse n de Areopagus lageŋɔ nɛra la, la pɔka ti ba yi'ira Damaris la basɛba n lagum bɔna la ba lagum sakɛ.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Bala mɛa n le iŋɛ, Ikonium puan, zi'an ti Pɔɔl la Banabase kiŋɛ ta kɛ̃' Yuuduma zamehegɔ yire la puan, muulɛ Yinɛ yelehum la paaɛ paaɛ ti Yuuduma la Girikiduma zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Gee Yuuduma sɛba n ka nam yelehum la dɔrɛ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la, tu'ule ba puti'ira la suurɔ la.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti Pɔɔl la Banabase yee tiŋa la puan yuue, tɔgera Daana la yelehum la ninmu'urɛ, ti Daana la mɛŋa bahɛ ti a tuntuneba la tuna daalehum la nyalema la ba nu'uhi ti la pa'alɛ ti zu'uhegɔ yelehum la dela eŋa yelehum.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Gee tiŋa la nɛreba zo'e zo'e welehi mɛ buyi, ti basɛba pa' Yuuduma la n ka sakɛ yelehum la poore, ti basɛba me pa' Yezu tuntuneba la poore.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Ti sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la Yuuduma la ba kima za'a, la n ta maahum ti ba iŋɛ ba sɛla en ka ani suŋa gee lobe ba la kuga la.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: ti Yezu tuntuneba la baŋɛ de yele, zɔ kiŋɛ Lisetira la Dɛɛbi n de Likoniya tinkãtɛ la puan, la tihi se'ehi n boi kɔrum kɔrum la puan.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Bilam ti ba ta bɔna gee muula faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Lisetira puan, buraa n boi bini dɛna kɔɔreŋɔ ka dɔgum puan na, ka malum kiŋɛ.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Ti buraa kana mɛa wa zi'a kɛleha Pɔɔl n tɔgeri la kpiri. Ti Pɔɔl ta biha en zii, ta baŋɛ ti buraa wa tari sakerɛ ti a wan nyɛ a tibegɔ.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: ti Pɔɔl yele en paŋa paŋa yeti, Ihege ze'ele fum nama zuo. Ti buraa wa ɛkɛ ze'ele, pɔhɛ kina kaara.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Nɛrekuuŋɔ la n nyɛ Pɔɔl en tum se'em la, ti ba woole kpim Likoniduma yetɔgum puan yeti, Tingama n lebege nɛreba wa'am tu zi'an na.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Ti ba sigɛ Barabase ti Zeyusi, gee sigɛ Pɔɔl ti Hɛmese, se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa n boi nɛŋa la tugum la.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Ti tindaansɛka n kaaberi Zeyusi wa la bagerɛ bɔ'ɔ la kɔ'ɔm pɔra la tiŋa la kinkɛleŋa, ti a tari lɔlehi la mɔpuuro na, ti eŋa la nɛrekuuŋɔ la maahum ti ba kaabɛ ba.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Yezu tuntuneba Banabase la Pɔɔl n ta wum ŋwana la, ba aahum la ba futo, gee zɔ gɛregɛ nɛreba la puan ha, tahera yeti.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: Tu zɔduma wa, ŋwani ti ya ita ŋwana? Tumam dela nɛresaaleba wuu yamam la, tara la yelesuma wa'am na muula bɔ'ɔra ya ti ya ŋmɛregɛ ya puti'ira yehe yeleyotuna wa puan, gee wa'am Yinɛ vua wa ze'a na, se'em n naam saazuo la tiŋa wa, la ateko wa la sɛla woo n boi ba puan la.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Buuri saŋa se'ehi n tole ha la, ti Yinɛ yuum bahɛ ti tihi woo ita ban boti se'em.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: gee la bala za'a, aze' nyɛ duma boi mɛ Yinɛ itesuŋɔ seko ti a ita doohe n baheri ti saa zɛ'ɛta saazuo na niira bɔ'ɔra ya, ti yuuma dɛna yuumsuma, ti a tigeha tɔ tu dia ti ya suya pirɛ la nyɛɛma.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: La Pɔɔl duma n pun tɔgɛ yetɔge buna za'a la, bu ka nyaŋɛ la pika pika me, ta'am gu' nɛrekuuŋɔ la ti ba da kaabɛ ba.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Gee Yuuduma basɛba n ze'ele Antiyoke la Ikonium tihi puan na, tɔgɛ nyaŋɛ ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la ti ba pa' ba poore. Nyaa lobe Pɔɔl, vuue en yehe tiŋa la yiŋa ha ta bahɛ ti'iha ti a ki mɛ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Gee zahereba la n lagehɛ kaaɛ en la, a ihege mɛ kiŋɛ kɛ̃ tiŋa la puan, ti yiimbeere ti eŋa la Banabase kiŋɛ Dɛɛbi.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Ban muulɛ yelesuma la tiŋa la puan ba'ahɛ la, ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e nam yelehum la, ba lebe la Lisetira, ze'ele bilam tole Ikonium nyaa ta tole Antiyoke.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Ba doohe malum kpe'em zahereba la gee kpemehe ba ti ba bɔna ba sakerɛ la puan, yɛta ba yeti, Tu wan doohe la namehegɔ zo'e zo'e puan gee kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Ban doohe lageŋɔ la woo puan lu' nuurɛ gee pu'uhɛ gee loohe kiinduma bahɛ lageŋɔ woo puan ba'ahɛ la, ba dikɛ ba woo iŋɛ la Daana se'em ti ba sakɛ bo' en la nu'uhen.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Ti ba nyaa ze'ele bilam doohe Pisidiya tole, wa'am na paaɛ Pamfiliya.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Ban tɔgɛ yelehum la Pɛga puan la, ba ze'ele bilam kiŋɛ sige la Atalia.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: Ba ze'ele la bilam le zom uureŋɔ lebe Antiyoke, ze'a ti ba daa lagehɛ dikɛ ba iŋɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ puan la tuuma la ti ba tum ba'ahɛ la.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Ban wa'am na na paaɛ la, ba yi' lageŋɔ la za'a mɛ lagehɛ na, nyaa yele sɛla woo ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba tum la ba, la Yinɛ n yu'ɛ sakerɛ kuleŋa se'em bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la se'em.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Ŋwana pooren, ba yee mɛ la Yezu zahereba la yuue fiiwa.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Ti nɛreba basɛba ta ze'ele Yudiya wa'am na pa'ala sakereba la yeti, Han dagena fum ŋma mɛ wuu Moose pa'alegɔ la n yele se'em la, fum kan ta'am nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Ban ta ba'ahɛ la, Pɔɔl la Banabase ŋmɛ' la nɔkpe'en tuulɛ la ba, Pɔɔl la Banabase la basɛba ti ba looe ba ti ba tari yele ana wa kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm ta sɔhɛ la Yezu tuntuneba la la kiinduma la.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Ti lageŋɔ la tum ba, ti ban dole Fonisiya la Samariya la, ba yeti sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la teere mɛ, ti sakereba la za'a n boi ze'se'ehi la puurɛ pee.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, lageŋɔ la za'a la Yezu tuntuneba la, la kiinduma la za'a to'e ba mɛ, ti ba me yele ba Yinɛ n doohe bamam puan tum yele sɛ'a woo.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Gee sakereba la basɛba n boi Farasiiduma wele la ihege ze'ele mɛ yeti, La dela de sore ti ba ŋma ba, gee ti ba me bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba dɔla Moose ze'elego la.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la, la Lageŋɔ la Kiinduma la lagehɛ taaba zi'ire ti ba bihɛ sɔhega wa puan.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Ban ta ŋmɛ' nɔkpe'ene la zo'e la, ti Pita ihege ze'ele saazuo yele ba yeti, N suurɔ wa, ya mi' ti kuremin ha la, Yinɛ yuum looe la loore ya puan, ti mam n wan dɛna se'em puan ti Sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la wan doohe wum yelesuma wa wa gee lebege sakereba.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Gee Yinɛ se'em n mi' asaala suuren la, pa'alɛ yeti ba nari mɛ doohe la n bo' ba a Fa'a Sia la, wuu en yuum bo' tumam se'em la.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: En duuhe ba suya la bahɛ doohe ban nam la puan la, pa'alɛ yeti, yima yima ka boi tumam la bamam tiŋasuka.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Bala zuo la, ŋwani ti ya makera Yinɛ bihera, doohe la yan le dikera yɔgehega yɔgera a zahereba wa, yɔgelehi, yɔgehega sɛka ti tumam yaabeduma bii tumam mɛhi kan ta'am zi wa?.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Lan tugum dɛna se'em dela, tumam sakɛ ya ti tu wan nyɛ faarɛ doohe la zu'uhegɔ seko n doohe Yezu puan na la, wuu bamam me n wan sakɛ se'em la.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Ti lageŋɔ la za'a yee suram, gee kɛleha sɛlawoo ti Banabase la Pɔɔl yɛta ba doohe daalehum la nyalema sɛ'a ti Yinɛ tum doohe ba puan, sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la tiŋasuka la.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Ban ta tɔgɛ ba'ahɛ la, ti Yemesi lerege yeti, N suurɔ wa, kelehe ya mam.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simeyɔn . pun pa'alɛ Yinɛ en pɔhɛ zɔ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri wa nimbu'ɔ se'em, gee yeti a yehe nɛreba ba puan ti ba yi'ira a yu'urɛ la.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Dina wa doohe mɛ la nɔtɔgeheba tugum la ti ba gulehɛ yeti.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Yele ana pooren n wan lebe na, la n maalɛ Deviti bɔŋa zi'an la lu la. N wan vaaɛ nangoone gira la le mɛ en, la n sɛ di ze'ele saazuo.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: ti nɛrawoo han nyɛ bala, ba wan ɛ Daana la. Sɛba n pun dagena Yuda buuri gee ti mam yi' ba la za'a. Daana la n yele se'em n baheri ti.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: nɛreba bɔkera yele ana yuuma yuuma ha la.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Bala zuo, mam n gi' putɛ'se'ere dela, tu da daam sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri gee ŋmɛregɛ yɛ'a Yinɛ zi'an na wa.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Gee tu tugum gulehɛ bo' ba yeti, ba yehe ba mɛhi baga dia puan, la yaluntuuma, la sɛla woo ti ba kigɛ ku, la ba da dita ziim.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, tihi wuu puan, buuri sansɛka n tole, Moose tari sɛba n muuli a yele me, se'ere n sɔi la, ba kãalɛ a yele vo'ohego dabeherɛ wuu zamehegɔ yiya la puan.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Ti Yezu tuntuneba la lageŋɔ kiinduma la, la lageŋɔ la za'a ku nuurɛ ti ba looe nɛreba ba puan pa'ahɛ Pita la Banabase puan tum ba ti ba kiŋɛ Antiyoke, ba tum Yudasi se'em ti ba yi'ira Barabase, la Silase se'em n de suurɔ la nɛŋa daana la.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: ba gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa yeti, Tu suurɔ wa, Yezu tuntuneba la, la Kiinduma la n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam sakereba sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri ya tuuhum puan na, bɔna Antiyoke, Siriya la Kilikiya tihi puan la, tu pu'uheri ya na.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Tumam n wum ti nɛreba basɛba ze'ele tumam zi'an yehe, gee me ka to'e kã'aŋɔ tumam ze'a la, wa'am na yele ya yɛla a sɛ'a ti daam ya suya, gee gbirege ya puti'ira ti a ka gã'a bɔbeyinɛ la zuo.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: ti tumam ku nuurɛ deyina tum nɛrebana wa ya ze'a na, ti ba doohe la tu nɔŋerɛ suurɔ Banabase la Pɔɔl.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: sɛba n pun pɔ̃hɛ ba nyuurɔ tu Daana Yezu zuo.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Tu tum la Yudasi la Silase sɛba n wan tɔgɛ yele sɛ'a n boi gɔŋɔ wa puan la ba nuurɛ bo' ya.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ la dagi Fa'a Sia la, la tumam me puti'irɛ ti tu pɛregɛ zeero le pa'ahɛ zeseto n pun bɔna ya zuo wa, han dagi yele sɛ'a en pakɛ wa.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Ya da dita sɛla woo n dikɛ kaabɛ bo' bagerɛ, ya da dita ziim, bii sɛla ti ba kigɛ ku gee yehe ya mɛhi yaluntuuma puan. Ya han dɔla ŋwana, la ba'ahɛ mɛ, Yinɛ suŋɛ ya.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Ŋwana ti ba tum nɛreba wa, ti ba kiŋɛ Antiyoke. Ti ba ta paaɛ, lagehɛ lageŋɔ la za'a, ba bo' ba gɔŋɔ la.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Lageŋɔ la nɛreba la n kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la, ba puurɛ pee mɛ la kpemehego seko n boi bini la.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Ti Yudasi la Silase en de nɔtɔgeheba la tɔgɛ yele sɛ'a n wan kpemehe gee sɛ sakereba la ze'ele suŋa.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Ban kɛ̃ bini yuue fii la, sakereba la bahɛ ba mɛ la suma'ahum ti ba lebe sɛba n tum ba na la zi'an. .

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Bala ti Pɔɔl la Banabase yee Antiyoke, la ba nɛreba basɛba zo'e zo'e, pa'ala nɛreba la, gee muula Daana la yelehum la bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Dabeha n iŋɛ fii wa la, ti Pɔɔl yele Banabase yeti, Wa'ana ti tu lebe doohe bihɛ sakereba sɛba woo ti tu doohe tihi la woo puan pa'alɛ gee muulɛ Daana la yelehum bo' ba la, la tu bihɛ ban ani se'em.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Ti Banabase bɔta ti Yɔn se'em yu'urɛ deyima n yi'iri Maki la doohe ba.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: Gee Pɔɔl ku a puti'irɛ mɛ ti ba kan bahɛ ti se'em n tabelɛ zagehɛ ba Pamfiliya gee ka doohe ba kiŋɛ tuni la doohe ba.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Ti ba nɔkpe'ene la ba' karegɛ gaŋɛ ti ba welehi taaba, ti Banabase tari Maki, ti ba zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Sapuruse.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: Gee ti Pɔɔl me tari Silase ti bamam me yeti ba yehe, Ti Sakereba la pu'uhɛ dikɛ en iŋɛ Daana la zu'uhegɔ puan.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Ti Pɔɔl doohe Siriya la Kilikiya tihi puan za'a puan, sɛɛra Lageŋɔ duma la woo zɛ'ɛta.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Apolos n boi Korinti la, ti Pɔɔl doohe la tinkãra zo'e zo'e puan tole wa'am na paaɛ Ɛfisu, zi'an ti a nyɛ zahereba basɛba.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: Ti a yele ba yeti, Yan lebege sakereba la, ya to'e Fa'a Sia la? Ti ba lerege yeti, Aayi, tumam pun ka wum ti Fa'a Sia boi.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Ti Pɔɔl soke ba yeti, Gee ba daa sɔ yamam ko'om la la ani puan? Ti ba lerege yeti, Yɔn ko'om sua la puan.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Ti Pɔɔl yele ba yeti, Yɔn ko'om sua la dela suteere ko'om sua. A daa yeti nɛreba la yeti, ba nam se'em n wan wa'am eŋa pooren na la. Yezu n bala.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Nɛreba la n wum ŋwana la, ba nyaa sɔ ba ko'om la Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Ti Pɔɔl n dikɛ a nu'uhi pagelɛ ba zuto la, ti Fa'a Sia la sige ba zuo, ti ba tɔgɛ zɛlempaala, gee purɛ.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Ba za'a de wuu nɛreba pia la ba yi'.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Ti Pɔɔl kina zamehegɔ yire la puan tara tara ŋmarehi sitã, tɔgera yelehum la la ninmu'urɛ, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene se'ere n wan bahɛ ti nɛreba la bɔkɛ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum yɛla la.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Gee basɛba kɔ'ɔm kãbɛ suure zagehɛ yelehum la, gee yaala Sore la nɛreba la nɛŋan la, ti Pɔɔl bahɛ ba, gee tari sakereba la kina zamehegɔ yire ti ba yi'ira Tairanuse la puan, ta pa'ala pa'ali seko ti nɛreba wan bɔkɛ ba daare woo.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: A iti la bala tara tara la yuuma ayi', ti la ŋwana wa bahɛ sɛba woo n boi Esiya dɛna Yuda buuri bii Giriki buuri la woo wum Daana yelehum la.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Ti Yinɛ dɔla Pɔɔl puan tuna paŋa tuunkãra sɛ'a n boi si tɔka.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: ti ba nyaa tara Pɔɔl nɛnduuhega bii a fuo tanɛ n kalum a iŋa kina pagela bã'duma zuo ti ba bã'ahi bahera ba, ti kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi ɛgera yɛhera na gee bahera nɛreba.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Ti Yuda buuri la puan sɛba eere sa'aha sɛbɛ'ɛhi me zigɛ ti ba dikɛ Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la sa'ahɛ kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi n boi sɛba puan la, yɛta yeti, N sa'ari fum mɛ Yezu se'em ti Pɔɔl muula a yele la puan.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Budibeto tuyopɔi n de Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ malema nɛŋadaana ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Seva, en zigeri ita ŋwana wa.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Gee Kulekpa'bɛ'a la lerege ba yeti, Han dɛna Yezu mam mi, la Pɔɔl mam mi' gee yamam dela bani.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Ti Buraa la ti kulekpa'bɛ'a la bɔna ka puan la ɛkɛ ba' ba, gbirege la ba ta nyaŋɛ ba za'a, ti ba zɔ yire la puan yehe na bɛɛla gee pɔgelum.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Yele dina wa n lu sɛba woo n boi Ɛfisu puan tuban la, Yuda buuri la Giriki buuri la woo n boi Ɛfisu puan ani la suram la dabehekãtɛ, gee ti ba zɛkera Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Ti zo'e zo'e la n lebege sakereba la me purɛ ba be'em gee pa'alɛ bamam n ni tuna se'em la.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Ti sɛba me n ni tuna tiim tuuma la me kekeko me va ba tiim gɔnɔ la na nyɔ' tɔ nɛreba nɛŋan. Ban magehɛ gɔnɔ la bihɛ la, tu wan koohe ni paaɛ wuu salempɛɛlega tuha pihinuu.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Ŋwana ti Daana yelehum malum zɔ'ɔra la paŋa gee tɔ'ɔra zi'an woo.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Yele ana wa woo n iŋɛ ba'ahɛ la, Pɔɔl maahum mɛ Sia la puan ti a doohe Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi puan gee nyaa tole Yerusalɛm. A yele bo' a mɛŋa yeti, N han paaɛ bilam, n wan ba'ahɛ paaɛ Arome.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Bala ti a tum a suŋereba bayi', Timoti la Erastuse, ti ba kiŋɛ Masedoniya, gee ti eŋa mɛŋa yee Esiya yuue fii wa.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Sankaŋa puan la ti gbiregeri kãtɛ en sige, doohe Sore la zuo.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Buraa yu'urɛ n yi'iri Demetereyose, en de salempɛɛlega kuta, nyaa dikɛ salempɛɛlega kure Atemis tinganɛ la, ti tuun-dina wa tari ligeri tuunkãtɛ na bo' tuntuneba.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Ti a kã'ahɛ nɛrebana wa woo, la nɛresɛba woo n lagum tuna tuun-ana taaba lagehɛ taaba na yele ba yeti, N taaba wa, ya mi' ti tu tarega wa yehe la tuun-ana wa puan.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Ya me nyɛ ya, gee me wum ti, dagi Ɛfisu ma'a puan, gee la ŋwani ti Esiya wa puan za'a, Pɔɔl wa doohe pã'ahɛ mɛ gee vɛ' nɛrekuuŋɔ yehe, doohe la en tɔgeri yeti, yina sɛ'a ti ba dikɛ nu'uhi maalɛ la dagi yina.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Ŋwana wa tari la nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega kina na, dagi la tu tuuma wa ma'a n wan bahɛ yoo, gee tinganɛ kãtɛ Atemis en de bage kãtɛ zɔŋɔ la wan lebege kɛhega lɔkɔ, ti ba faaɛ a pansɛka ti a tara ta'am lagehɛ Esiya tiŋa wa, la timpaberɛ wa za'a ti ba kina na'aha en la.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Ban wum ŋwana wa la, la wuŋɛ ba mɛ ti ba suya nyiie, ti ba kaahɛ tagurega yeti, Ɛfisuduma Atemis n gani za'a.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Ti gbiregeri sige tiŋa la puan, ti nɛreba zɔ kalam kiŋɛ de'eŋo yire la puan, gee vɛ'ɛra Gayɔse la Aristakuse n de Masedoniya, dole Pɔɔl ɛɛra la.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Ti Pɔɔl bɔta ti a gɛregɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ la ha, gee ti a zahereba la ka sakɛ bo' en.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Ti Esiya kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛba n de Pɔɔl zɔduma la tum sɔɔleŋa bo' en na ti a da makɛ nɛ na'arɛ de'eŋo yire la puan.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Gee bala ma'a la ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la nyaa yabera bura bura. Bana yɛta sɛla, ti bana me yɛta sɛla ha, se'ere n sɔi la, ba ka mi' ban boi zi'an, gee ti ba zo'e zo'e me ka mina se'ere zuo ti ba lagehɛ na.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la basɛba ka'ana Alɛgezanda la ti Yuuduma la za'ɛ en bahɛ na. Ti Alɛgezanda wa doohe yibehɛ nɛreba la ti nɛreba la go'e, gee nyaa zigera en wan iŋɛ se'em tɔgɛ suŋɛ a mɛŋa nɛreba la nɛŋan.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Gee nɛreba la n ta baŋɛ ti a dela Yuda buuri la, ti ba dikɛ gogo kaareŋa ayi' la, kaahɛ la nɔyinɛ baha na yeti, Ɛfisu yinɛ Atemis n gani za'a.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Gee tiŋa la gɔnguleha en ta bahɛ ti nɛreba la go'e la, a yele ba yeti, Ɛfisu tiŋambiihi wa, ani n gee ka mina ti Ɛfisu wa n biheri Atemis se'em n gani za'a la zɔŋɔ la, la bage se'ere saazuo ha lu na la?.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Yele ana wa n kan ta'am sugɛ la zuo, la dela ya ana bããlam gee da kabelɛ tum tuunse'ere yoo.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Yan tari buraahi wa wa'am na ka'lam na wa, si ka fa' tu zɔŋɔ wa puan sɛla bii tu' tu bagerɛ wa.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Bala zuo la, Demetereyose la a kuteba taaba la n naaɛ la en wa han tara la se'ere la nɛre se'em, tugum deto la gã' mɛ, ti sɛba n gakeri yɛla bɔna. Bahɛ ti ba paaɛ bilam la ba tɔgɛ ban tari se'ere la taaba la.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Ya han le bɔta ti ya baŋɛ la se'ere pa'ahɛ, ba paaɛ sɛba n biheri nɛreba yɛla la zi'an.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka zaaɛ ba kɛɛ tɔ zina ti tu gbiti mɛ. Se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka tari se'ere ti tu wan yele doohe gbiregeri dina puan.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: En tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, a yele nɛreba la mɛ ti ba yiregɛ.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta paaɛ Dɛɛbi la Lisetira tihi puan, bilam ti ba nyɛ zahera ayima yu'urɛ en yi'iri Timoti, ti a ma dɛna Yuda buuri puan pɔka, dɛna sakereba la ayima, gee ti a sɔ dɛna Giriki nɛra.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Timoti wa dela se'em ti sakereba la n boi Lisetira la Ikonium la tɔgera a yele suŋa.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Ŋwana ti Pɔɔl bɔta ti Timoti wa doohe en, nyaa bahɛ ti a ŋma, Yuuduma la n boi zɛ'se'ehi puan la zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, ba daa mi' ti Timoti sɔ dela Giriki buuri.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Ti ban dole kaara tiŋa tiŋa la, ba yeti nɛreba la ze'elego seko ti tuntuneba la kiinduma la en boi Yerusalɛm ku nuurɛ bu ba ti ba dɔla a suŋa la.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Ba doohe se'em kpemehe lageŋɔ la nɛreba ba sakerɛ puan n bala, ti nɛreba me malum kɛ̃ lageŋɔ la pa'ahɛ daarɛ woo.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Ti ba doohe Firigiya la Galatia sɔ'ɔlum la puan za'a kaaɛ, doohe la Fa'a Sia la n ka bo' ba sore ti ba muulɛ yelehum la Esiya tiŋa puan la zuo.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Misiya tiŋa beene zuo la, ba iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ ti ba kɔ'ɔm kɛ̃ Bitiniya tiŋa puan, gee Yezu Sia la ka bo' ba sore.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: bala ti ba doohe Misiya kinkɛleŋa sige, ta paaɛ Torowase.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Ti yu'uŋɔ ti Pɔɔl ta nyɛ lilegere, ti buraa n yehe Masedoniya tiŋa n bala zɛ'a la en bɛlena yeti, Wa'am Masedoniya na suŋɛ tɔ.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Eŋa n nyɛ lilegere dina wa la, ti tu ihege bilam bilam, zigera ti tu yakɛ kiŋɛ Masedoniya, se'ere n sɔi la tu sakɛ ya ti Yinɛ n yi' tu ti tu kiŋɛ ta muulɛ faarɛ la bo' ba .

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Ti tu zom uureŋɔ, ze'ele Torowase, kɔ'ɔm kiŋɛ tuuntɔɔ ta paaɛ Samotirasi, yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Niyapolise.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: ze'ele bilam tole Filipi, kaŋa n de tinkãtɛ Masedoniya tiŋa la puan, me dɛna Aromduma sɔ'ɔlum. Tu yee la tinkana wa puan dabeha fii wa.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Ti vo'ohego dabeherɛ la ta paaɛ, ti tu yehe tiŋa la yiŋa, kiŋɛ ta pɔra kulaa la, zi'an ti tumam ti'iha ti pu'uhegɔ ze'a wan bɔna la. Tu zi'ire la bilam na la, pɔhɛ tɔgera bɔ'ɔra pɔgehi n lagehɛ na.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Ti pɔka bɔna ba puan kɛleha tɔ, ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Lidiya, dɛna pɔka dɛna Yin-zɔta, yehe Tayatira na, kɔɔha fumɔlɔ. Ti Daana la yu'ɛ ka suure ti ka kɛleha Pɔɔl en yeti sɛla la suŋa suŋa.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Kaŋa la ka yire nɛreba la za'a n ta to'e ko'om sua la, ka bɛlena tumam yeti, Ya han sirum nyɛ mam ti mam dela sakera Daana la puan, wa'am ya na kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra mam yire. Ka tɔgera la bala tara tara ta nyaŋɛ tɔ.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Daarɛ deyima ti tu yɛ'a pu'uhegɔ zi'an la, tu tu'uhɛ la pugela n de yameŋa, ti kulekpa'bɛ'a dɔla ka, ti ka dɛna sɔputega, tari ligeri kina na bɔ'ɔra a daana la doohe la en yeti nɛreba ba yɛla la.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Pugela wa n dole Pɔɔl la tumam la, ka ni ta kaahɛ mɛ bahɛ na yeti, Nɛrebana wa dela Gaŋɛ za'a Yinɛ la Yamehi, muula faarɛ sore la bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: A iti ŋwana wa la dabeha zo'e zo'e. Ti a itegɔ la ba' nyiie Pɔɔl susuya, ti a ŋmɛregɛ yele kulekpa'bɛ'a la yeti, Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan ti n bo' fum nuurɛ yɛta fum tole yehe a puan na. Ti sankaŋa mɛa puan la, ti sɛbɛ'a yehe pugela la puan.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Gee pugela la daan duma la n nyɛ ti ban tari puti'irɛ ti ba wan nyɛ ligeri la tole ya la, ti ba nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl la Silase, vɛ' ba kɛ̃ da'a puan na, tari ba wa'am kpɛ'ɛnduma nɛŋan na.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Ban tari ba paaɛ yelegakereba nɛŋan na la, ba yeti, Buraahi sina wa ba' daani la tu tiŋa wa, ba me dela Yuuduma.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: gee wa'am na pa'ala tu nɛreba wa kiha sɛ'a n dagi ma'a bo' tumam n de Arome duma wa ti tu to'e a dɔla.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la lagum pa' ba poore, zabera la Pɔɔl duma, ti yelegakereba yɛɛhɛ ba lɔgerɔ, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' ba.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Ti ban ŋmɛ ba sagum sagum ba'ahɛ la, ba vu ba ta bahɛ la paka deo puan, gee bo' sɛba n biheri paka deo la nuurɛ ti ba gura ba suŋa.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Doohe la ban bo' en nuurɛ se'em la zuo, ti eŋa me tari ba kɛ̃ paka deo la saraa puan ha, ta dikɛ kuto ba' ba nama tabelɛ dɔkãtɛ.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: La ta paara me woo yu'uŋɔ sunsua bɔba, ti Pɔɔl la Silase pu'uha gee yuuma bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, ti sɛba ti ba pagɛ la ba la kɛleha.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Ba yeti ba bihɛ, ti tiŋa la tinmiiŋo kãtɛ n miim, ti paka deo la ɛ̃berɛ la gbiregera, ti bilam bilam ti paka kulehi la za'a yu'uhɛ, ti zɔtɔ la n ba' nɛreba la za'a lorehe.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Gura la en ta ihege nyɛ ti paka deo kulehi la yu'ɛ mɛ gã'a la, ti a fooe a su'a ti a ku a mɛŋa se'ere n sɔi la, a ti'ihɛ ti sɛba ti ba pagɛ ba la za'a yehe zɔ mɛ.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Gee Pɔɔl tahɛ en mɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Da pɔgelum fum mɛŋa gee tu woo boi mɛ.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Ti gura wa yi' ti ba tari bugum kɛ̃ na, ti a lu tiŋa Pɔɔl la Silase nɛŋan gbi'ira.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Gee nyaa tari ba yehe yiŋa na yele ba yeti, Naa, Beni ti n wan iŋɛ la n nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Ti ba lerege en yeti, Nam Daana Yezu, gee fum wan nyɛ faarɛ, fum la fum yire za'a.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Ti ba nyaa tɔgɛ Daana la yelehum la bo' eŋa la sɛba woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri a yire la puan la.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Ti yu'uŋɔ kuŋɔ sankaŋa mɛa puan, ti gura la tari ba kiŋɛ ta di'ihe ba nɔra la, gee ti eŋa la a nɛreba za'a to'e ko'om sua kalam ka gu'um.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Ti a nyaa tari ba wa'am a yire na, bo' ba dia, gee ti eŋa la a nɛreba za'a nyɛɛna la bamam n lebege sakereba bo' Yinɛ.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Beere n ta yilegɛ la, ti sɛba n gakeri yɛla la tum gureba yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta bahɛ ti buraahi la kiŋɛ.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Ti gura la to'e sɔɔleŋa la ta yele Pɔɔl yeti, Yelegakereba la tum na yeti, tu bahɛ ti ya kiŋɛ. Bala zuo la, ya ta'am yehe nananawa la inma'ahum.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Gee Pɔɔl lerege en yeti, Ba ŋmɛ tumam mɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ nɛŋan, gee ka nyɛ tu tuure, gee ti tu me dɛna Arome tiŋa wa nɛreba, ti ba le pagɛ tu, gee nyaa bɔta ti ba bahɛ tɔ bããlam? La kan iŋɛ bala. Bahɛ ti ba mɛhi wa'am na tari tu yehe.

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Ti gureba la tari yetɔgum la ta bo' yelegakereba la, ti dabeem ke ba, la ba wum ti ba dela Arome tiŋa duma la.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Ti ba nyaa wa'am na belum ba, gee nyaa tari ba yehe, gee nyaa yele ba ti ba bahɛ tiŋa la puan.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Ban yehe paka deo la puan na la, ba kiŋɛ la Lidiya yire. Ban paaɛ nyɛ gee kpemehe sakereba la ba'ahɛ la, ti ba bahɛ tiŋa la puan.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Ŋwana wa n tole la, ti Pɔɔl bahɛ Atense gee kiŋɛ Korinti.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Bilam ti ba ta nyɛ Yuda buuri la nɛra ayima ti ba ba yi'ira en Akuila, yehe Pɔntusi tiŋa na, nan kɔ'ɔm ze'ele Itali lebena la a pɔga Pirisila, se'ere n sɔi la, Kaiza Kulodeyose bo' la nuurɛ ti Yuuduma woo bahɛ Arome. Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ti a ta bihɛ ba.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: gee ba za'a n tuni tuunɛ deyina la zuo ti Pɔɔl kɛ̃ la ba, ti ba lagum tuna. Ba za'a lagum maala la gana pagehi.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Vo'ohego dabeherɛ woo, Pɔɔl ni wan kɛ̃ zamehegɔ yire la puan, zigera ti a nyaŋɛ Yuda la Girikiduma la.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Silase la Timoti n ta ze'ele Masedoniya paaɛ en na la, ti Pɔɔl boi la yelehum la muulegɔ puan mimi, yɛta Yuuduma la ti Kiresta la dela Yezu.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ban ka sakɛ bo' en, gee tuura en la, ti a kpa'ahɛ a futo puuhego bahɛ, gee yele ba yeti, Ya taala dɔla ya mɛhi, mam ka tari taalɛ. Yehe zina yɛ'a ha, n nyaa wan kiŋɛ la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la zi'an.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Ti Pɔɔl bahɛ zamehegɔ yire la puan, gee yehe kiŋɛ buraa ka yima zi'an ti ka yu'urɛ yi'iri Taitusi Yusetuse, dɛna se'em n na'aheri Yinɛ, ti a yire kɔ'ɔm du' la zamehegɔ yire la.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Kirisipuse n de kpɛ'ɛma zamehegɔ yire la puan la, to'e yelehum la lebege Daana la sakera, eŋa la a yire nɛreba za'a, ti Korintiduma sɛba n wum Pɔɔl yelehum muulegɔ la zo'e zo'e to'e lebege sakereba, to'e ko'om sua.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Yu'uŋɔ kuyima ti Daana la yele Pɔɔl lilegere puan yeti, Da tara dabeem, tɔgera, da sina.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, mam boi mɛ la hɔ, ti nɛra ka bɔna wan dikɛ a nu'o kalum hɔ bii a pɔgelum hɔ. Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛreba zo'e mɛ tinkana wa puan mɛ dɛna mam nɛreba.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Ti Pɔɔl yee tiŋa la puan yuunɛ la ŋmarehi siyoobe, pa'ala nɛreba la Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Gee Galiyo n wa biha Akaiya tiŋa la, ti Yuuduma wa lagehɛ taaba gbirege la Pɔɔl, tari en wa'am tugum yire puan na.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Nyaa yeti, Burakana wa dole pa'ala nɛreba paaɛ paaɛ ti ba pu'uha Yinɛ sadɔɔ seko n ka dole la ze'elego la.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Pɔɔl n boi bini ti a ya'ɛ nuurɛ la, ti naba Galiyo pun yele Yuuduma la yeti, La han deni la a tuue la se'ere bii a galum la se'ere n wan tari ni sore ti n to'e yamam Yuuduma wa suhega la.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Gee kan de tugum la yu'ura la yamam mɛŋa ze'elego wa la, ya mɛhi bihɛ tɔgɛ dɛ, gee mam ka boti n dɛna gakera yele ana wa puan.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Gee sa'ɛ ba tugum deo la puan ti ba yehe.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Ti Yuuduma la ŋmɛregɛ gurɛ Sositenese n de zamehegɔ yire la kpɛ'ɛma ayima la, ŋmɛ' en gakereba la nɛŋan, gee la bala za'a Galiyo ka be'e bihɛ ba yɛla wa puan.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Pɔɔl en kɛ̃ bilam yuue fii la, a keehe sakereba la za'a mɛ, gee zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Siriya tiŋa, ti Pirisila la Akuila doohe en. Ti a wa'am na paaɛ Sinkireya, bahɛ ti ba kɛ̃ a zuo bilam se'ere n sɔi la a biŋe la nuurɛ.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Ban wa'am na na paaɛ Ɛfisu la, a bahɛ ba la bilam, gee yia la, a kɛ̃ la zamehegɔ yire puan, ta tɔgɛ yɛla la Yuuduma la.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Gee ban yele en ti a yee la ba dabeha ayi la, a ka sakɛ.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: gee en bahɛ ba gee yɛ'a la, a yele ba yeti, Yinɛ han bɔta de bala, n wan ta lebe ye zi'an na. Gee zom uureŋɔ Ɛfisu la yɛ'a.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: En ta paaɛ sige Kasariya puan la, a kiŋɛ zom la Yerusalɛm ta pu'uhɛ lageŋɔ la, ze'ele bilam kiŋe sige Antiyoke.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: En kɛ̃ bilam yuue fii wa la, a bahɛ bilam mɛ gee dɔla kaara tihi la n boi Galatia la Firigiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la, kpɛmeha zahereba la za'a en boi bini la.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Nananawa, Yuda buuri la nɛra ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Apolos, wa'am Ɛfisu na. Ba dɔgɛ en la Alɛgezandiriya, ti a dɛna buraa n mi' tugum, gee me mina gulehegɔ la suŋa.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: A zamehɛ bi'ɛ mɛ Daana sore la puan, gee tɔgera la ninmu'urɛ, gee me pa'ala Yezu yɛla la ma'a ma'a. Gee a mi' la Yɔn ko'om sua la ma'a yele la.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Ti buraa wa pɔhɛ tɔgera la buraanɛ zamehegɔ yire la puan, gee Pirisila la Akuila n kelehe en ba'ahɛ la, ba tari en yehe kiŋɛ la kinkɛleŋa ha, ta pa'alɛ en Yinɛ sore la ma'a ma'a.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Buraa wa ta maahum ti a kiŋɛ Akaiya la, suurɔ la za'a daa pa' la a poore kpɛmeha en ti a kiŋɛ, gee nyaa gulehɛ gɔŋɔ bo' zahereba la n boi bilam la yeti ba to'e en suŋa . En ta paaɛ la, a nyaŋɛ kpemehe sɛba woo ti zu'uhegɔ zuo ba lebege sakereba la mɛ.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la a kɔ'ɔm tɔgɛ mɛ la buraanɛ nyaŋɛ Yuuduma la, dɔla gulehegɔ la puan pa'alɛ ba ti Kiresta la dela Yezu.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Gbiregere la n ma'ɛ la, ti Pɔɔl tum yi' a zahereba la na, kpemehe ba, gee keehe ba, gee yɛ'a Masedoniya.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: En ta paaɛ bilam doohe kaaɛ tihi la za'a kpemehe sakereba la suŋa suŋa ba'ahɛ la, a kiŋɛ la Girise.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: Bilam ti a ta kɛ̃ ŋmarehi sitã. A ta maahum mɛ ti a kiŋɛ Siriya, ti Yuuduma la maahum gbirege la en, ti bala la zuo, ti a iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a lebe Masedoniya.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Ti Faruse dayua Sopata n ze'ele Beroya la, kɔlegɛ en, nyaa dikɛ Aristakuse, Sekunduse n yehe Tɛsalonika na, Gayɔse n yehe Dɛɛbi, la Timoti nyaa dikɛ Takikuse la Tirofimuse n yehe Esiya.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Bana woo diŋɛ la nɛŋa ta gura tu Torowase.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: gee ti tumam zom uureŋɔ kiŋɛ Filipi tiŋa, ti boroboro zaŋa de'eŋo la tole, ti dabeha anuu daarɛ ti tu paaɛ ba Torowase, ti tu kɛ̃ bilam dabeha ayopɔi.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Dabeha ayopɔi pɔhega daarɛ, ti tu lagehɛ na ti tu di Daana tuulum dia la, ti Pɔɔl nyaa suhera suhega la ba, la eŋa n maahum ti a bahɛ yiimbeere la. A tɔgɛ la bala ta paaɛ yu'uŋɔ sunsua.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Ti ba lamehɛ bugum zo'e zo'e bɔ'ɔ la n boi saazuo ha ti ba bɔna ku puan suhera la puan.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Ti budibele paalega ti ka yu'urɛ yi'ira Yutikuse, zi'a lɛm takole nuuren, ti guum zɛɛ en ti a giha gee ti Pɔɔl kelum tɔgera wɔgera. Ti budibela wa gihi tole nyaa ta ze'ele yitugema butã puan la, lu ta paaɛ tiŋa, ti ba ɔ̃begɛ en ti a ki mɛ.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Gee Pɔɔl sige mɛ, ta nyigele bia la zuo, ɔ̃begɛ en a nu'uhen, gee yeti, Da bahɛ ya ti la dɛna daaŋɔ gee a vom ka kiŋɛ zi'an.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Ti Pɔɔl le zom saazuo la ha, ti ban ta di Daana tuulum dia la ba'ahɛ la, ti a le pɔhɛ suhega la ba tara tara ti beere ta yilegera, ti a nyaa bahɛ ba gee kiŋɛ.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Gee budibela la n lu na la, vo'e mɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ la pupeelenkãtɛ.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Ti tu kiŋɛ ta zom uureŋɔ yɛ'a Asɔsi, ti'iha ti tu wan paaɛ Pɔɔl bilam dikɛ en, se'ere n sɔi la, a pun iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a kan doohe ko'om puan.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: En tu'uhɛ tu Asɔsi la, ti tu dikɛ en pa'ahɛ kiŋɛ Mitilene.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Tu ze'ele la bilam dikɛ uureŋɔ, ti yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Kiyose. Ti yiimbeere le yɛ'ɛha ti tu paaɛ Samose, ti dabehe se'ere n tagelɛ la, ti tu paaɛ Militusi.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, Pɔɔl iŋɛ a suure ti a mɔm zo'e Ɛfisu mɛ la a da gã'arɛ Esiya la, a nini ba' mu'ɛ la a paaɛ Yerusalɛm, la han wan ta'am ni iŋɛ, a paaɛ ni dabeha pinuu de'eŋo daarɛ la.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Militusi puan, ti Pɔɔl bɔna gee tum sɔɔleŋa bo' Ɛfisu, yɛta ti a boti a zi'ire mɛ la lageŋɔ la kiinduma la.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Kiinduma la n wa'am na paaɛ en la, ti a yele ba yeti, Yamam mɛhi mi' mam daa boi la ya se'em, ta paaɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti mam pɔhɛ nɛ Esiya puan.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: N tuni bɔ'ɔra Daana la la sigehe mɛŋa la nintam, ŋmibera mageha sɛ'a ti Yuuduma wa ni maahum ti a paaɛ mam.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: N ka zagehɛ ti n tum se'ere n wan tara suŋɛ. N muuli yelehum la mɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, gee pa'ala ya nɛrekuuŋɔ puan, dɔla yire yire.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: gee yɛta Yuuduma la, la Girikiduma ti ba tee ba suya lebe Yinɛ zi'an, gee sakɛ bo' Yezu.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Ti nananawa, mam Fa'a Sia la da'aberɛ wa ye' la Yerusalɛm, n ka mi' sɛla n wan ta iŋɛ mam bini.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: han dagena Fa'a Sia la mɛŋa n yeti mam tiŋa woo ti n paara ka puan yeti, ti paka la mɛlehegɔ en ga' gura mam bini.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Gee mam ka biheri n vom la ti bu tara vuurɛ bo' mam, n han wa nyaŋɛ ni ba'ahɛ n tuunɛ wa, la tuunse'ere ti n to'e tu Daana Yezu ze'a ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa, n de Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ yɛla wa.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Nananawa, mam mi' ti yamam sɛba woo ti n tari ya doohe muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum wa yɛla la, ya ayima kan le nyɛ mam la ya niho.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Bala zuo ti mam yɛta ya zina wa la ninmu'urɛ ti ya se'em han bɔregɛ, ya taala ka dole mam.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: se'ere n sɔi la, mam daa ka zagehɛ ti n yele la ninmu'urɛ, Yinɛ puti'irɛ za'a n de se'ere.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Biheri ya, ya mɛhi suŋa, gee me biha piihi la ti Fa'a Sia la dikɛ ya ti ya biha la. Ya biha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la ti a dikɛ a mɛŋa Dayua ziim to'e ku la.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: Mam mi' ti mam han tole ba'ahɛ, sahi n obere wan wa'am na lagum ya, kan bahɛ piihi la.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Basɛba wan pun yehe yamam mɛŋa puan gɛ̃ta yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti ba ta'am pã'ahɛ zahereba la basɛba ti ba doohe ba poore.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Bala zuo la, ani ya wire, gee tɛra ti yuuma atã ti mam ka go'e n tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, ka'ana ya ayima ayima la ninmu'urɛ la nintam.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Gee nananawa, mam dikɛ ya ita la Yinɛ nu'uhen la a zu'uhegɔ yelehum la puan. Yelehum sebo n wan ta'am mɛ ya ze'ele, gee bo' ya tɔtɛ la sɛba n pee ba mɛhi la.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: N ka fã nɛra salempɛɛlega, bii salema bii futo.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Ya mɛhi la mi' ti mam daa tuni mɛ la n nu'uhi suŋera n mɛŋa la sɛba n tuni la mam.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Yele ana wa woo puan la, mam iŋɛ mɛ pa'ali ya, ti tu han tuna bala tu wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba n ka kpe'em la, doohe la tumam n tɛri tu Daana Yezu yelehum la. Se'ere n sɔi la, eŋa yeti, Sunkãtɛ boi mɛ la se'em n bo'ori, gana se'em n to'ohe. .

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: En tɔgɛ ŋwana woo ba'ahɛ bo' ba la, a kpa' la duma la ba za'a suhɛ bo' ba.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Ti ba kaahɛ la taaba zo'e zo'e, gee ti ba nyaa doohe kɔkɛ Pɔɔl kɔkɛ kɔkɛ.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: ti ba suya sagum mɛ yele yele woo en yele ba yeti ba pun kan le malum nyɛ en la niho le yɛ'ɛha la. Ti ba nyaa tari en na ki'ihɛ uureŋɔ.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: N suurɔ la n sɔduma wa, kelehe ya nananawa, mam me buurɔ n de se'em ti n yeti n dikɛ biŋe ya nɛŋan wa.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Ban wum ti a tɔgeri la Hiburu yetɔgum puan bɔ'ɔra ba la, ti ba malum iŋɛ bããlam pa'ahɛ. Ti a nyaa yeti:.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: Mam dela Yuda buuri, ti ba dɔgɛ mam Tasuse tiŋa n boi Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la, gee mam zo'e la tinkana puan, ti mam zi'ire Gamaliyɛl nama tiŋa, ti a pa'alɛ mam gee, zamehi mam nima nima ti yaabeduma pa'alegɔ la, ti mam mu'ɛ la nini boi Yinɛ woo yamam za'a n ana se'em zina wa.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Mam pɔgelum Sore wa mɛ, pun ta paaɛ n kuura ba, doohe la mam yuum ni bobe sɛba n boi Sore wa puan ba buraahi la ba pɔgehi, tari ba ta pagɛ paka deto puan.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: malema nɛŋadaana la la pu'uhegɔ yire kiinduma la woo wan tɔgɛ mam yele pa'alɛ ya. Bama zi'an ti mam to'e gɔŋɔ ta bo' tu suurɔ n boi Damasekuse. N kiŋɛ bini ti n ta bobe la sakereba la n boi bini, gee tari ba lebe Yerusalɛm ti ba mɛlehɛ ba.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Mam n boi sore zuo kina ta pɔra Damasekuse la, wuu wuntɛɛŋa zuo tiŋasuka, ti mam ta yeti n bihɛ la, ti bugum kãtɛ n ze'ele saazuo ha nyakɛ kaaɛ mam ze'ele.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Ti mam lu tiŋa, gee wum ti kua yi'iri mam yeti, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, beni zuo ti fum mɛleha mam.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Ti mam lerege yeti, Fum dela ani n Daana? Ti kua la yele mam yeti, Mam dela Yezu n yehe Nazareti na la, Mam n bala ti fum dɔla mɛleha la.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Lan iŋɛ ŋwana la, yelemɛŋerɛ sɛba n dole la mam la nyɛ bugum la, gee ka wum se'em n tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra mam la kua.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Ti mam soke yeti, Beni ti n nyaa wan iŋɛ n Daana? Ti Daana la yele mam yeti, Ihege la fum kɛ̃ Damasekuse. Bilam ti ba wan ta yele fum, sɛla woo ti mam looe fum ti fum tum.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Mam ka nyɛti, doohe la bugum la n ba' nyakɛ ku n nimbaahi la zuo, ti sɛba n dole mam la yari gurɛ n nu'o ve'e mam ta kɛ̃ Damasekuse puan.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Buraa kayima n de Ananiya kaabɛ ka mɛŋa bo' ze'elego la, ti Yuda buuri la woo n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri Damasekuse puan la yi'ira ka yu'sunɛ la.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Kaŋa n wa wa'am na, zɛ'a lɛm mam gee yeti, N suuɔ Sɔɔl, lebe nyɛta. Sankaŋa ti mam lebehe nyɛta gorege saazuo nyɛ en.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Ti a nyaa yele mam yeti, Ti yaabeduma Yinɛ n looe fum ti fum baŋɛ a suboolum, fum nyɛ Tuntɔɔ Daana wa, gee wum a kua.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, fum yeti fum dɛna la a Aze' nyɛ daana bo' timpaberɛ wa za'a nɛreba la fum n nyɛ gee wum se'ere wa.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Bala, nananawa, ŋwani ti fum gu'una? Ihege ze'ele, la fum sɔ ko'om, ti ba saalum fum be'em bahɛ, fum yi'iri a yu'urɛ la.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Mam lebe Yerusalɛm na ba'ahɛ la, ti daarɛ deyima ti n bɔna bini pu'uha, pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti guum zɛɛ mam.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: ti n nyɛ en ti a yɛta mam yeti, Iŋɛ kalam la fum yehe Yerusalɛm wa tɔtɔ, se'ere n sɔi la, ba kan to'e fum yeti fum tɔgɛ mam yele se'em wa.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Ti mam yeti, N Daana, ba mɛhi mi' ti, mam doohe zamehegɔ yiya woo puan me, ŋmɛ'ɛra gee pagera sɛba woo n nam bo' fum la mɛ.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Ti sansɛka ti ba ta ka'ara fum Aze' nyɛ daana Siteven ziim bahera la, mam mɛŋa n ze' bini pa' ba poore ti ba ku, gee to'ohe ba n lobere en kuura la futo zãla.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Ti a yele mam yeti, Kiŋɛ, gee n yeti n tum fum la zaaɛ zaarɛ, sɛba n dagi Yuuduma zi'an. .

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Ŋwana wa beene woo la, ti nɛreba la kelehe en mɛ, gee kɔ'ɔm malum tahera baha na yeti, Ba'ahɛ ya nɛre-ina taaba yehe timpaberɛ wa zuo. Se'ere n sɔi la, a pun dagena ba bo' en sore ti a vɔna.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Ban taheri bala la, gee yɛɛha ba futo la fɔmbera tiŋa gee vaara tintɔnɔ lɔbera saazuo la.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la bo' sore ti ba tari en lebe pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la puan, la ba viihɛ en gee ŋmɛ' en ti a baŋɛ se'ere zuo ti kaahi dɔɔrɔ tuna sigera a zuo wa.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Gee ban lu' en ta' la gu'uhi la, Pɔɔl yele pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la yeti, La de de sore ti fum ŋmɛ' Arome tiŋambia sɛka ti ba ka nyɛ ka tuure.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega wa wa n wum ŋwana la, a kiŋɛ la pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la zi'an ta soke ba yeti, Fum boi beni ti fum iŋɛ la bem n bala? Buraa wa dela Arome wa tiŋambia.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la wa'am na soke Pɔɔl yeti, Alaa, yele mam, fum dela Arome tiŋambia? Ti Pɔɔl yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la yeti, Mam yɔ la kɔɔba zo'e zo'e gee nyaŋɛ to'e n tiŋambiine gɔŋɔ wa. Ti Pɔɔl yele en yeti, Ba nuun dɔgɛ mam la Arome tiŋa wa.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: Bilam bilam ti sɛba woo n ze' ni ti ba viihɛ en la fɔ lebe pooren gee bahɛ en, ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la me zɔ dabeem, se'ere n sɔi la, a baŋɛ ti Pɔɔl dela Arome tiŋambia gee ti a sina gee ba' en.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Pɛntiireba kɛ'ɛma la n boti a baŋɛ sɛla ti Yuuduma tara zuuha Pɔɔl la zuo la, ti yiimbeere ti a yu'ɛ en yehe na, gee bo' malema kiinduma la ba kima la za'a nuurɛ ti ba lagehɛ. Ti a nyaa tari Pɔɔl sige ba ze'a na, bahɛ ti Pɔɔl zɛ'a ba nɛŋan.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Tumam n bahɛ nɛreba la gee zom tu uureŋɔ yɛ'a la, tu kɔ'ɔm kiŋɛ la tuuntɔɔ ta paaɛ Kose, ti yiimbeere ti tu paaɛ Arodisi, ze'ele bilam tole Patara.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Ti tu nyɛ ti uureŋɔ boi bilam maahum ti ku kiŋɛ Fonisiya, ti tu zom, ti ku yɛ'a.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Ti tu ta nyɛta Sapuruse, ti tu bahɛ tiŋa la tu gɔbega bɔba gee kiŋɛ ta paaɛ Siriya kiŋɛ ta sige Taai, se'ere n sɔi la uureŋɔ la zɛ la lɔgerɔ bɔta ti ba yehe lɔgerɔ la bilam.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Ti tu nyaa ɛ zahereba n boi ze'a bilam, kɛ̃ bilam dabeha ayopɔi. Ti Sia la tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba bilam, ti ba yele Pɔɔl ti a da kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Tu kɛ̃'a dabeha la bilam n ba'ahɛ la, tu bahɛ bilam mɛ gee le to'e tu sore. Ti sakereba la za'a, ba pɔgeba la ba kɔma beele tu ta yehe tiŋa la puan. Bilam ti tu za'a kpa' duma mɔgerɛ la nuuren pu'uhɛ.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: gee keehe taaba, ti tumam kiŋɛ ta zom uureŋɔ ti bamam me lebe kula ba yiya.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Tumam n yehe Taai tiŋa na daarɛ la, tu kelum kina la bala ta paaɛ Pitolemayese, pu'uhɛ suurɔ gee kɛ̃ la ba daanyinɛ.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: Yiimbeere ti tu wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya, ta kɛ̃ Filipi n de ayehemuulɛ daana, se'em n de ba bayopɔi ayima la yire, kɛ̃ la en.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: Filipi wa tari la pɔgesa'kaha anaahi, en puti.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Tumam n wa bɔna la ba dabeha zo'e zo'e la, ti nɔtɔgeha ayima yu'urɛ n yi'iri Agabuse ze'ele Yudiya wa'am na.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Wa'am tumam zi'an na, paaɛ na dikɛ Pɔɔl sɛganɛ lu' a mɛŋa nama la a nu'uhi, gee yeti, Fa'a Sia la yeti, Ŋwana ti Yuuduma la n boi Yerusalɛm la wan ta lu' burasɛka n sɔi sɛganɛ wa, dikɛ en bu sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Tumam n wum ŋwana la, tumam la sakereba la n boi bilam la tɔgɛ mɛ la en ninmu'urɛ ti a da kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ti Pɔɔl lerege ba yeti, Ya iti la bem n bala, ya kele mɛ Sagena mam suure? N yele ya ti, mam ka maahum ti ba bobe mam ma'a, gee n pun ki Yerusalɛm, Daana Yezu yu'urɛ wa zuo.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: En ka maahum ti a tee a puti'irɛ la, tu yee la suram, han dagena tu yeti, Daana la sumboolum tum.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Dabeha ana n ta tole la, ti tu maahum, to'e sore yɛ'a zɔna Yerusalɛm.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Ti zahereba la basɛba n yehe Kasariya la, lagum doohe tu, tari tu na paaɛ Neeson n yehe Sapuruse tiŋa la yire, se'em en de yia yia zahera la ayima la. Se'em ti tu wan kɛ̃ en la en la, en bala.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Tumam n paaɛ Yerusalɛm la, suurɔ la n boi bini la to'e tu mɛ suŋa.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: Yiimbeere ti Pɔɔl tari tu kiŋɛ ti a ta bihɛ Yemesi, a ta paaɛ la ti kiinduma la za'a boi mɛ.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Ti en pu'uhɛ ba ba'ahɛ la, a pɔhɛ mɛ lɔɔha yele yele doohe sɛla ti Yinɛ tum bo' sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la eŋa tuuma la puan.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Ban wum yɛla la ba'ahɛ la, ba pɛgɛ la Yinɛ. Gee nyaa yele en yeti, Fum nyɛ ya, tu suuɔ wa, bihɛ sakereba en de tuha tuha bilam se'em nyaa pa'ahɛ Yuuduma la puan, gee ti ba za'a me nini kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la ze'elego la.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ba pun yele ba mɛ, fum yele, ti fum pa'ali Yuuduma sɛba woo n boi la sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la mɛ ti ba zagehɛ Moose, gee le yeti fum yeti ba ti ba da ŋmata ba kɔma, gee me da dɔla malema la.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Beni n de tu iŋɛ? Ba wan ba'ahum wum ti fum wa'am na.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Bala zuo la, doohe sɛla ti tu wan yele la. Tu tari la buraahi sinaahi n biŋe ba nuurɛ la pɔta.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Bala, pa'ahɛ ba puan la fum lagum malum peere malema la la ba, gee yɔ ti ba siie ba zuto la. Bala nɛrawoo wan baŋɛ ti sɛla ka boi nɛreba n yele fum yele se'em la, gee ba wan nyɛ ti fum mɛŋa dole gee nana ze'elego la mɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Gee han dɛna sɛba n dagi Yuuduma gee lebege sakereba la, tumam tum gɔŋɔ bo' ba ha yeti, ba da dita sɛla ti n kaabɛ bo' bagerɛ, ba da dita ziim, la ba da dita sɛla ti ba kɛge ku, gee yehe ba mɛhi yaluntuuma puan.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: Ti Pɔɔl tari buraahi wa, eŋa n pee a mɛŋa ba'ahɛ la yiimbeere, kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la ba, ta malum peere malema dabeha la za'a nɛrekuuŋɔ la nɛŋan, ta paaɛ se'em ma'a ti ba wan kaabɛ kabegɛ bo' ba woo ayima ayima la.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Dabeha ayopɔi la n pun ta wuna ti a ba'ahɛ me la, ti Yuda buuri sɛba n ze'ele Esiya na, gee nyɛ Pɔɔl pu'uhegɔ yire la puan la, gbirege nɛrekuuŋɔ la za'a bahɛ. Ti ba nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: gee tahera yeti, Tu Izirale taaba wa, suŋɛ ya mam. Burakana wa n bala pa'ala nɛrawoo zi'an woo ti ba da nana tumam nɛreba, tu ze'elego, la ze'kana wa. Sɛla n pun gana bala dela a tari la Giriki nɛreba wa'am na kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan, galum tuulum zi'an wa.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Se'ere n sɔi ŋwana wa la dela, nɛreba la pun diŋɛ nyɛ la Tirofimuse n de Ɛfisu nɛra la ti Pɔɔl dɔla la en tiŋa puan ha la, ti ba kɔ'ɔm ti'ihɛ ti a tari en kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan me na.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Ti tiŋa la za'a gbirege, ti nɛreba la zɔ lagehɛ taaba kalam. Gurɛ Pɔɔl vuue en yehe pu'uhegɔ yire la yiŋa, gee yu' kulehi la bilam bilam.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Ban zigeri ti ba ku en la, ti sɔɔleŋa paaɛ pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana la yeti, Yerusalɛm za'a gbiregeri mɛ.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Ti bilam bilam ti a yehe pɛntiireba la ba nɛheduma en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega kɔbega zɔ paaɛ zi'an la puan. Nɛrekuuŋɔ en nyɛ kpɛ'ɛma la la a pɛntiireba la, ti ba go'e Pɔɔl ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Ti pɛntiireba nɛŋa daana wa paaɛ nyɔkɛ Pɔɔl gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba lu' en la zɔtɔ tuyi' Gee nyaa viiha eŋa n de se'em la en eŋa sɛla.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la basɛba vɔɔla ti ŋwana, ti basɛba me vɔɔla ti ŋwana ha. Ti eŋa n kan baŋɛ sɛla n iŋɛ doohe la voole la zuo la, ti a bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari en wa'am pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la na.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Pɔɔl n ta zɔna kura la zuo la, nɛreba la malum gbirege gaŋɛ la bala, ti pɛntiireba la ɔ̃begɛ Pɔɔl buke.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ la n dole la kɔ'ɔm tahera mɛ yeti, Ba'ahɛ ya en ha Ba'ahɛ ya en.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Ban ta pɔra ti ba tari Pɔɔl kɛ̃ pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la, ti Pɔɔl yele nɛŋadaana la yeti, N wan ta'am tɔgɛ se'ere bo' fum? Ti nɛŋadaana la soke en yeti, Fum tã tɔgera Giriki mɛ?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Fum ye dagi Igipiti tiŋa nɛra la n gbirege, tari zabe-zabereba tuha anaahi yehe kɛ̃ muuen ha la.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Ti Pɔɔl lerege en yeti, Mam dela Yuda buuri, yehe Tasuse n boi Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan la. Mam dela tiŋa n en tari yu'kãtɛ tiŋambia. N beleni fum mɛ bo' mam sore ti n tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba wa.
