eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Ti nɛŋadaana wa bo' en sore, ti Pɔɔl zom yagelɛ kura la zuo, yele nɛreba la yeti, ba yeen go'e, ti ban iŋɛ bããlam la, a tɔgɛ bo' nɛreba la mɛ Hiburu yetɔgum puan yeti:.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Ŋwana wa dabeha anuu puan, ti malema nɛŋadaana Ananiya sigena la kiinduma basɛba, la nɛra ayima en de yetɔgetɔgera ti ba yi'ira en Tetulose, ti ba dikɛ Pɔɔl yele biŋe timbihega nɛŋadaana la nɛŋan.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Ban sam Pɔɔl ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, ti Tetulose wa pɔhɛ Pɔɔl zɛregerɛ, yɛta yeti: Naa sukpe'ene, Fum zuo, suma'ahum boi tu mɛ wa'am na paaɛ zina, ti teere tee bo' tiŋa wa nɛreba wa, fum mi'ilum la zuo.

eng: Always and everywhere, most honorable Felix, we acknowledge this with deep gratitude.
local: Tu nini ba' tigɛ la yele ana wa ti tu pɛgera a sadɔɔ woo puan la zi'an woo, la pupeelum.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Gee mam ka boti n gu'um la zuo, mam beleni fum mɛ ti fum kelehe tu suhega wa gi'a gi'a la fum zu'uhegɔ la.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Tumam n nyɛ buraa wa, yelemɛŋerɛ, a dela nɛrebe'o n de nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega bo' tiŋa wa. A dela se'em n baheri ti gbiregeri sigera Yuda buuri wa n boi timpaberɛ wa zuo zi'an woo, dɛna lageŋɔ seko ti ba yi'ira ba Nazarenduma la nɛŋadaana.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: A pun zigɛ mɛ ti a galum Pu'uhegɔ yire la, ti tumam me nyɔkɛ en. .

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: Gee yamam mɛhi la han viihɛ bihɛ, ya wan wum a mɛŋa zi'an, yele sɛ'a woo ti tumam dikɛ zɛregera en wa.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Ti Yuuduma la me pa'ahɛ ba nuurɛ yeti, sɛla woo ti ba yele la n kɔ'ɔm bala ti ba tɔgɛ la.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Timbihega nɛŋadaana la ta yele Pɔɔl ti a me tɔgɛ a nuurɛ la, ti Pɔɔl yeti: Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la fum bo' mam sore ti n tɔgɛ n nuurɛ wa. Mam mi' ti fum bihɛ tiŋa wa la ka nɛreba dagi zina.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Gee fum mɛŋa n wum se'em la, la ka gaŋɛ dabeha pia la ayi' n ŋwana, ti mam kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm ti n ta pu'uhɛ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Ba ka nyɛ mam ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛri nɔkpe'ene la se'em pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, bii n gbirege nɛreba zamehegɔ yire puan bii tiŋa la puan.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Ba me ze' nananawa, ba pun kan tara se'ere ti ba pa'alɛ yeti bamam n zuuheri mam se'ere zuo wa la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Gee se'ere n de yelemɛŋerɛ ti mam wan yele fum dela, la doohe la Sore la ti bamam yeti, de dela be'em tigere, gee mam pu'uhi la tu yaabeduma Yinɛ la, gee nam sɛla woo en gulehɛ biŋe doohe ze'elego la nɔtɔgeheba gulehɛ biŋe la.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: Mam tari la beere puti'irɛ Yinɛ zi'an. Beere puti'irɛ se'ere ti bamam mɛhi pun sakɛ ti, tuntɔɔduma la be'em duma ihegere kum puan la wan tum.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Ŋwana zuo la, mam kpeŋere la n mɛŋa saŋawoo ti n tara putɛ'sunɛ bo' Yinɛ, la nɛreba za'a.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: Yuuma n tole ha, ti mam yuum tari n bo'olum wa'am na ti n bo' n tiŋa nɛreba, gee me tara n kaabegɔ bo'olum na ti n kaabɛ.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: Mam wa'am na bɔna malema ana puan la, ti ba nyɛ mam Pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti n boi la peere malema la ba'ahegɔ puan. Nɛreba daa ka zo'e bini, bii daaŋɔ seko sige bini.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Gee Yuuduma basɛba n daa ze'ele Esiya wa'am na, bamam me pun suni ti ba me boni fum nɛŋan ka'lam, la ba yele bamam me n tari se'ere la mam.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Bii bahɛ ti nɛrebana wa yele fum n tuure n daa de se'ere ban daa tari mam kiŋɛ kiinduma la nɛŋan la.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Han dagena yele dina wa ma'a ti n daa zɛ'a ba nɛŋan gee yele la. De dela nɛreba han ki ba wan le ihege la. Yele dina wa zuo ti mam zɛ'a fum nɛŋan zina wa.

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Gee Felikise n pun mina Sore la yele la, malum dikɛ yetɔgum la ze'ele la dabeherɛ se'ere yeti, Lasiya n de pɛntiira kiima la han sige na, n wan bihɛ fum yele wa puan n de se'em.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Gee bo' pɛntiira nɛŋa daana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la nuurɛ yeti, guri ya en, gee bahɛ ya en ti a tara fai fii wa, da gu'uri ya a zɔduma ti ba da kina na bihera en gee bɔ'ɔra en sɛla ti a bɔta.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Ti la iŋɛ dabeha fii wa, ti Felikise doohe la a pɔga Durusila n de Yuda buuri la wa'am, tum ti ba yi' Pɔɔl na, ti a bahɛ en ti a tɔgɛ bu en ti wum a sakerɛ Kiresta Yezu puan n ani se'em.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Ti Pɔɔl n ta tɔgera pa'ala tuntɔɔnɛ, tana fum mɛŋa tara, la gakerɛ la n kini se'em na la, ti dabeem kɛ̃ Felikise, ti a yele Pɔɔl yeti, Kiŋɛ ti la iŋɛ fii, n han le ta nyɛ fole, n wan tum ti ba yi' fum na.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Gee ŋwana woo la, a puti'irɛ dela Pɔɔl wan bo' en ligeri. Yele dina wa me zuo ti a ni makɛ yi' Pɔɔl na zi'a la en sɔha.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Yuuma ayi' n ta wa'am na tole, ti Pɔkiyose Fesetuse sooe Felikise, gee Felikise wa de gakɛ n boti a iŋɛ ti Yuuduma la puurɛ pee la, a bahɛ ti Pɔɔl kelum bɔna la paka deo puan.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Naba Fesetuse n wa sooe tiŋa la, dabeha atã n tole, ti a ze'ele Kasariya kiŋɛ zom Yerusalɛm.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: ta paaɛ ti malema kiima la, la Yuda kiinduma la lagehɛ yele en Pɔɔl yele, ba belum en.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: gee suhɛ ti a bahɛ Pɔɔl, la a bahɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl lebe Yerusalɛm na. Gee ba pun maahum mɛ ti ba wan sugɛ gura en la ba ku en sore la zuo.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Ti Fesetuse lerege ba yeti, ba pagɛ Pɔɔl la Kasariya tiŋa puan, la me kan yuue la eŋa mɛŋa n kiŋɛ bini.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Bala zuo a yele ba yeti, Ya nɛheduma la sige paaɛ mam Kasariya na, ti ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la, ba wan zɛregɛ ka.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Fɛsetuse ka kɛ̃ la ba gaŋɛ dabeha anii bii pia, a bahɛ bilam me gee kiŋɛ sige Kasariya, ti yiimbeere ti a kɛ̃ gakerɛ yire la puan ta zi'ire gakerɛ kuka la zuo, ti a gakɛ, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl na.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Pɔɔl n paaɛ na la, Yuuduma n ze'ele Yerusalɛm sigena la kɔ'ɔm kaaɛ en bahɛ la tiŋasuka, kɔ'ɔm parena yɛla zo'e zo'e pagela a zuo, gee kan ta'am pa'alɛ ti la dela asira.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Ti Pɔɔl me tɔgɛ a nuurɛ yeti, Mam ka tuue Yuuduma ze'elego la, bii Pu'uhegɔ yire la, bii Kaiza la fii me.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Gee Fɛsetuse n boti a pee Yuuduma la puurɛ la zuo ti a soke Pɔɔl yeti, Fum wan bɔta ti ba tari fum lebe Yerusalɛm ti n ta zi'ire bilam gakɛ hɔ yele sɛ'a ti ba zuuha fum wa.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Ti Pɔɔl lerege yeti, Mam ze' la Kaiza gakerɛ kuka wa nɛŋan. Ka'lam n de mam yetɔgum wa zi'an. Mam ka tum se'ere tuue Yuuduma wa wuu fum mɛŋa n mi de se'em suŋa suŋa la.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: La han deni la mam tum la se'ere tuue ti la dɛna n ki, mam ka zigeri ti n li'ihɛ kumbumɔ. Gee n han ka tara tuure bɔna ba zuuhego la woo puan, nɛra ka boi me wan ta'am dikɛ mam iŋɛ ba nu'uhen. Mam n yeti se'em wa dela, n bahɛ ti Kaiza gakɛ n yele wa.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Ti Fesetuse tɔgɛ la a gakereba kiinduma la, gee lerege Pɔɔl yeti, Fum beleni la Kaiza ti a bihɛ fum yele la bo' fum, la me han dɛna Kaiza, fum ye' la Kaiza.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Ti dabeha zo'e zo'e ta tole, ti naba Agiripa la Benise ta wa'am Kasariya na, ti ba pu'uhɛ Fesetuse.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Ban boi bini dabeha kekeko la, ti daarɛ deyima ti Fesetuse dikɛ Pɔɔl yele wa biŋe naba wa nɛŋan yeti, Buraa kayima n boi ka'lam ti naba Felikise yuum bahɛ ka paka deen.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: Mam daa ta bɔna Yerusalɛm la, ti malema kiinduma la la Yuuduma kiinduma la tɔgɛ ka yele bo' mam, gee yele mam ti ka dela ka ki. Gee yele mam ti n dela n gakɛ en ti a tuue mɛ.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Ti mam yele ba yeti, la dagi tumam Arome duma tuunɛ ti tu dikɛ se'em iŋɛ zuuhereba la nu'uhen gee ti zuuha la ka ze'ele la zuuhereba la, ti ba bo' en sore ti a tɔgɛ a nuurɛ yetɔgum n de se'em.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Bala, ban daa wa'am na la, mam daa ka gu'um, yiimbeere mɛa ti mam kɛ̃ zi'ire la gakerɛ kuka la zuo, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari buraa wa na.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Sɛba n zuuheri en la n ihege ze'ele la, ba ka nyaŋɛ yele buraa la tuure n de se'ere wuu mam n boti ni de se'em la.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Ba daa tugum dɔla ɛ tɔgera la bamam la buraa la n ka sakɛ bo' taaba se'em ba pu'uhegɔ yɛla puan, la Buraa kayima ti ba yi'ira ka Yezu n ki, gee ti Pɔɔl la sakɛ ti buraa la vɔi mɛ.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Mam n bɔregɛ ba yetɔgum la puan pun ka mina mam wa soke sɛla bii viihɛ bu se'em la, mam soke Pɔɔl wa yeti, a wan bɔta ti a kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm ti ba ta gakɛ en ban zuuhe en yele sɛ'a wa?.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
local: Gee Pɔɔl ka sakɛ gee belum yeti, ti ba bahɛ en ti a bɔna paka deen la gura Kaiza puti'irɛ. Ti mam tara en bala gura ti n ta dikɛ en bo' Kaiza.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Ti naba Agiripa yele Fesetuse yeti, Mam wan bɔta ti n wum buraa wa tugum n mɛŋa. Ti Fesetuse yele naba Agiripa yeti, Beere fum wan wum a tugum.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Bala zuo, yiimbeere ti naba Agiripa la Benise wa'am na na, ti de'em kãra taaɛ ba poore, ti ba wa'am na kɛ̃ nɛrekuuŋɔ deo la puan, ti pɛntiireba yele gakereba la tiŋa la puan nɛrekãreba. Ti Fesetuse nyaa bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl wa'am na.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Ti Fesetuse ihege yeti, Naba Agiripa la sɛba woo n boi ka'lam la tu wa, ya nyɛ buraa wa, ti Yuuduma la za'a ku nuurɛ Yerusalɛm puan la ka'lam, tahera ti a ka nari a vɔna la.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Gee mam ka nyɛ a tuure n de se'ere ti la dɛna a ki. Ti en belum la ti a boti Kaiza en bihɛ a yele la, ti mam me , iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n tari en kiŋɛ.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Gee mam ka nyɛ yele vua ŋwana mɔpi ti n gulehɛ a yele dikɛ bo' sɛba me n gani mam la. Bala zuo ti mam tari en wa'am ya za'a na, yele yele wuu fum naba Agiripa, ti tu za'a han viihɛ en bihɛ ba'ahɛ n ze'em wan nyɛ a yele se'ere gulehɛ.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, la ka tari vuurɛ ti fum dikɛ nɛra n boi paka deen ta'aha zi'an gee ka pa'alɛ a tuure n de se'ere.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Pɔɔl n ze' biha kiinduma zii la, a ta yeti, N suurɔ, wa'am na paaɛ zina, mam vɔi n vom mɛ tuuntɔɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Ŋwana ti Ananiya n de malema nɛŋadaana la kiima la, tahɛ sɛba n ze' lɛm Pɔɔl ti ba ŋmɛ' a nuurɛ la.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: A yetɔgebuna ti Pɔɔl me yele en yeti, Yinɛ wan ŋmɛ' fum nangoonpeele wa. Fum zi' bilam ti fum gakɛ mam doohe ze'elego la, gee la bala za'a fum galum ze'elego mɛ nyaa ta bo' nuurɛ ti ba ŋmɛ' mam.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Ti sɛba n ze' lɛm Pɔɔl la yele en yeti, Fum dela ani tuura Yinɛ malema kiima.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Ti Pɔɔl yeti, Mam ka baŋɛ n suurɔ, ti a dela malema kiima. Se'ere n sɔi la, gulehegɔ la yeti, Da tɔgɛ fum nɛreba nɛŋa daana be'em. .

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Pɔɔl n ta baŋɛ ti nɛreba la basɛba dela Sadusii duma ti basɛba dɛna Farasiiduma la, ti a yi' kiinduma la buki yeti, N suurɔ, mam dela Farasii, mam dela Farasii dayua. Mam boi tugum wa puan wa dela, beere puti'irɛ n ihegere la puan.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Pɔɔl n yele ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, ti nɔkpe'ene lu Farasiiduma la, la Sadusiiduma la tiŋasuka, ti lageŋɔ la welege buyi'.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Ti nɔkpe'en kãtɛ ihege, ti Farasii gɔnmi'ineba basɛba ihege yeti, Tumam ka nyɛ tuure se'ere buraa wa puan. Gee la han dɛna la sira ti Sia bii Suŋera n sirum tɔgɛ bo' en.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Nɔkpe'ene la n nyaa ta lebege zaberɛ la, ti dabeem kɛ̃ pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma doohe la ba ka gagɛ ba aahum Pɔɔl zɔra zɔra la, ti a bo' pɛntiireba la nuurɛ ti ba sige ta pɛregɛ Pɔɔl wa'am pɛntiireba bɔŋa ze'a la puan na.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Yu'ukuŋɔ puan la, ti Daana la wa'am na zɛ'a lɛm Pɔɔl, gee yele en yeti, Tara sukãberɛ, wuu fum n muulɛ mam yele se'em Yerusalɛm puan la, bala mɛa muulɛ mam yele Arome wa puan.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: Ti bulika, ti Yuda buuri la maahum la taaba, gee bobe taaba la nɔpɔrɛ, ti ba kan di bii ba nyu han dagena ba ku la Pɔɔl.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Ba me gaŋɛ nɛreba pinaahi n ku nɔdina wa.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Ti ba kiŋɛ malema kiinduma la yizuto kiinduma la zi'an ta yele ba yeti, Tumam kɔ'ɔm pɔ la nuurɛ ti dia kan tole tumam nuuren han dagena tu ku la Pɔɔl.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Bala la, nananawa, yamam la kiinduma la bahɛ ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma mina ŋwana, la a bahɛ ti ba tari Pɔɔl wa'am ya zi'a na, wuu ya boti ya malum viihɛ la a yele la paaɛ paaɛ la. Gee tumam pun maahum mɛ ti tu wan ba'ahɛ en ti a pun kan paaɛ yamam nɛŋan na.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Gee Pɔɔl taya dayua wum ban yeti ba sugɛ se'em gura Pɔɔl, me kiŋɛ ta nyaŋɛ nyɛ sore kɛ̃ pɛntiireba bɔŋa zi'an la, dikɛ ŋwana yele Pɔɔl.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Ti Pɔɔl yi' pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega yele en yeti, Tari budibele paalega wa kiŋɛ pɛntiira kpɛ'ɛma wa zi'an gee a tari se'ere ti a ko'ohe en.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Bala ti kpɛ'ɛma wa pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega pɛntiireba wa, to'e budibela wa, tari en kiŋɛ pɛntiireba kɛ'ɛma la ze'a na yele en yeti, Pɔɔl n boi paka deo puan la n yi' mam, yele mam yeti n tari budibela wa wa'am fum ze'a na, gee ka tari se'ere ti ka yele bo' fum.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Ti pɛntiira kpɛ'ɛma gi' budibela wa nu'o, tari ka lebe kinkɛleŋa ba ma'a, ta soke en yeti, Beni n de la ti fum tara ti fum yele mam.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Ti budibela la lerege yeti, Yuubuuri la ku la nuurɛ ti ba beere tum nɛreba fum zi'an ti fum bahɛ ti Pɔɔl wa'am ba zi'an wuu ba boti ba malum viihɛ la a yele la paaɛ paaɛ la.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Gee da to'e ba yetɔgum la. Se'ere n sɔi la, ba gaŋɛ buraahi pinaahi, n sugɛ sore gura. Ba woo pɔ la nuurɛ, ti ba kan di bii ba nyu han dagena bamam ku la Pɔɔl. Ba kɔ'ɔm maahum mɛ bɔna ha nananawa gee gura fum puti'irɛ.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Ŋwana ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma wa bahɛ budibela wa ti ka kiŋɛ, gee yele en yeti, Da pun yele zugɔ yiŋa me ti fum yele mam yele dina wa.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Ti pɛntiira kɛ'ɛma wa nyaa yi' pɛntiireba nɛheduma bayi' en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega kɔbega yele ba yeti, Maahum ya la bahɛ ka'lam gogo kaareŋa awai' puan yu'uŋɔ, la pɛntiireba kɔbehi siyi, sɛba, sɛba en tari yire zabera pihiyopɔi, sɛba n tari kana zabera kɔbehi siyi, ti ya kiŋɛ Kasariya.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Maahum yire me bo' Pɔɔl ti a ba', ti fum tari en la inma'ahum ta bo' timbihega nɛŋadaana Felikise.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Gee nyaa gulehɛ yele la n de se'em, gɔŋɔ yeti:.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Mam Kulodeyose Lisiyase n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra fum zuo daana, timbihega nɛŋadaana Felikise na. N pu'uhi fum na.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Buraa wa ti Yuuduma la n boi ka'lam la nyɔkɛ ka, zigera ti ba ku ka, gee mam n baŋɛ ti ka dela Arome tiŋambia la, ti mam doohe la pɛntiireba ta to'e ka bahɛ.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: Mam zigeri ti n baŋɛ yelese'ere ti ba dikɛ zuuha buraa wa, mam bahɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ ba kiinduma la nɛŋan mɛ.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Mam nyɛ se'ere dela, ba zuuhe en mɛ doohe ba ze'elego yɛla puan, gee ba mam ka nyɛ sɛla ti a tuue seke ba ku' en bii ba pagɛ en.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: Mam wam ti ba maaheni mɛ ti ba ku en, ti mam ta'ahɛ en fum zi'an nananawa na, gee me bo' sɛba n zuuheri en la nuurɛ yeti, ba wa'am fum ze'a na, la ba yele fum ban tari se'ere la buraa wa la.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Bala ti pɛntiireba la n to'e a nuurɛ la, tari Pɔɔl bala wa'am na paaɛ Antipatirise tiŋa yu'uŋɔ.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Ti Yiimbeere, ti ba bahɛ ti pɛntiireba sɛba n tari yire zabera la tara Pɔɔl yɛ'a gee ti basɛba la tulege lebe pɛntiireba ba bɔŋa ze'a la.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Kasariya puan la, ba dikɛ gɔŋɔ la mɛ bo' timbihega nɛŋadaana la, gee dikɛ Pɔɔl me iŋɛ a nu'uhen.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Zuo daana wa n kãalɛ gɔŋɔ la ba'ahɛ la, a soke Pɔɔl en yehe lugese'ere na, ti en baŋɛ ti Pɔɔl yehe la Kilikiya na la.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: ti a yele en yeti, Sɛba n zuuheri fum la han paaɛ na, n wan bahɛ ti fum tɔgɛ fum nuurɛ. Gee nyaa bo' nuurɛ ti ba tari en kiŋɛ Hɛrɔd yikãtɛ la puan ti a ta bɔna bilam gee ti ba gura en.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Ti naba Agiripa yele Pɔɔl yeti, Sore boi ti fum tɔgɛ po'e fum mɛŋa. Ti Pɔɔl zɛkɛ a nu'uhi pɔhɛ mageha gee tɔgera pɔ'ɔra' a mɛŋa.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Mam bihɛ n mɛŋa ti mam dela zusuŋɔ daana, ti la dela fum naba Agiripa nɛŋan ti n tɔgɛ po'e n mɛŋa zina, zuuhego seto woo ti Yuuduma wa zuuhe mam la.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum dela se'em en mi' suŋa suŋa, Yuuduma malema la za'a, la yima yima se'em en boi Yuuduma wa tiŋasuka. Bala zuo, mam beleni fum mɛ ti, fum kelehe mam la sugeri:.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Yuuduma woo mi' mam vom n ani se'em n bumbile-paalumen na, ba mi' mam en yuum ihegere na vɔna vom sebo la n nɛreba, la Yerusalɛm puan mɛ.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Ba mi' ti la yuue mɛ, ba han wan tɔgɛ sira, ti mam yuum pun dɛna la pu'uheba wele se'ere en yuum ba' mu'ɛ ninin la, vɔna n Farasiinɛ.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Gee nananawa mam ze' la ka'lam ti ba gakɛ mam doohe la, mam tari beere puti'irɛ la nɔbiŋere se'ere ti Yinɛ yuum biŋe la tu yaabeduma la zuo.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Nɔbiŋere se'ere ti tumam Izirale buuri pia la ayi' la mu'ɛ nini na'aha Yinɛ bulika la yu'uŋɔ woo, gee tara puti'irɛ ti tu beere wan to'e la. Naa naba, la dela putɛ'dina wa zuo ti Yuuduma wa zuuha mam wa.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Ŋwani ti la kpe'em bo' ya se'em ti ya sakɛ ti Yinɛ vo'ore kum?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ mam mɛŋa mi' suŋa suŋa ti mam daa maahum mɛ ti n tum yɛla kekeko zabɛ la yu'urɛ wa en de Yezu en de Nazaareŋa wa.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Mam daa sirum tuni se'em Yerusalɛm puan n bala. Mam daa to'e kpe'eŋo seko malema kiinduma la zi'an la, mam daa ka pagɛ nɛresunduma ma'a, gee mam dela se'em en ni pa' ba poore ti ba ku ba ba han ta yeti ba ku.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Mam daa dole zamehegɔ yiya la woo puan mɛ mɛleha ba, gee pɛregera ba ti ba tu yu'urɛ. Mam susuya n ba' nyiie la ba ti mam kiha ba la zuo, mam dole ba nama la bala yageha tihi.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: N daa tari la putɛ'dina mɛa yɛ'a Damasekuse, ti malema kiinduma la me bo' mam kpe'eŋo gee tum mam.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Naa, Ti wuntɛɛn-sigelika ti mam bɔna sore zuo yɛ'a la, ti bugum ze'ele saazuo niiɛ gana wuntɛɛŋa la, nyakɛ kaaɛ mam la n nɛreba la mum .

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Naa, tu za'a n lu' tiŋa gã'a la, mam wum la kua n yi'iri mam Hiburu yetɔgum puan yeti, Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, beni zuo ti fum mɛleha mam? La wan bi'ɛ fum ti fum tara fum nama tɛbera kuto.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Ti mam soke yeti, Fum dela ani n Daana? Ti Daana la lerege mam yeti, Mam dela Yezu. Mam bala ti fum dɔla mɛleha la.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Bala, ihege la fum ze'ele suŋa. Se'ere n sɔi la, mam n niiɛ n mɛŋa bo' fum wa dela yele dina wa zuo. N looe fum, ti fum dɛna n tuntuna, la fum tɔgɛ fum n nyɛ mam yele sɛ'a, la yele sɛ'a ti n niiɛ a pooren.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: N wan faaɛ hɔ fum mɛŋa nɛreba Yuuduma wa nu'uhen bahɛ, la sɛba n dagi Yuuduma ti n tuna fum ba zi'an.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: ti fum pikɛ ba nini ti ba ta'am ŋmɛregɛ lika la puan lebe niihum la puan na, ba yehe Sagena la bobere la puan wa'am Yinɛ ze'a na, la ba to'e ba be'em sugeri, gee me lagum pa'ahɛ sɛba n pee ba mɛhi doohe ban tari sakerɛ bo' mam se'em la.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Ŋwana n tole la, naba Agiripa, mam daa ka zagehɛ saazuo ligeri wa n lilege bo' mam wa.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: gee n daa yele de bo' la sɛba n boi Damasekuse puan yia, gee nyaa tagelɛ Yerusalɛm, nyaa tagela Yudiya kɔrɔmkɔrɔm, nyaa dikɛ sɛba n dagi Yuda buuri la me, ti bamam me tee la ba ŋmɛregɛ lebe Yinɛ zi'an, la ba tuna tuunsɛ'a n pa'alɛ ti ba tee ya.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Yele dina wa zuo ti Yuuduma wa ta gi' mam pu'uhegɔ yire la puan zigera ti ba ku mam.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Wa'am na paaɛ zina, Yinɛ n suŋɛ mam, ti mam ta'am zɛ'a ka'lam wa, tɔgera bɔ'ɔra nɛrekãreba la tarenduma, dagi la yele se'ere ha, gee sɛla ti nɔtɔgeheba la Moose yeti la wan wa'am na tum la zuo.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: Ba yuum purɛ ti Kiresta wan nyɛ mɛlehegɔ, gee dɛna yia daana ti a ihege a kum puan, la a muulɛ niihum yele wa bo' tumam nɛreba, tumam nɛreba wa yia gee nyaa bo' sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔl n boi bini tɔgera ŋwana gu'ura a mɛŋa la, ti Fesetuse tahɛ buki yeti, Pɔɔl, fum yirege mɛ. Fum nyɛ ya, zamehegɔ zo'e zo'e la n bahɛ ti fum zãla wa.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Ti Pɔɔl me yeti, Mam ka yirege, zuo Daana Fesetuse, mam tɔgeri la yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, Naba mi' yele ana mɛ bala ti mam tɔgera a bala fai bɔ'ɔra en la. Se'ere n sɔi la, mam mi ti a ka tamɛ yele ana wa la deyina me, se'ere n sɔi yele ana wa la deyina me daa ka boi iŋɛ yoko puan.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Naba Agiripa, fum nam nɔtɔgeheba la? Mam mi' ti fum nam ba.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Ti naba Agiripa yele Pɔɔl yeti, Fum kɔ'ɔm zigera mɛ ti mam lebege Yezu zahera kalam.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Ti Pɔɔl lerege en yeti, Kalam bii bãla bãla, mam sɔheri Yinɛ ti pun dagena fum ma'a, gee sɛba woo n kelehe mam zina me wa'am na dɛna wuu mam n de se'em zina wa, mam bãhi wa ma'a yeen da ba' ba.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: Ti naba Agiripa vaaɛ ihege, la timbihega nɛŋa daana la, la Benise, la sɛba woo n lagum zi'a la ba la.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: ti ban ye; yɛha la, ba yeti taaba yeti, Burakana wa ka iti sɛla n de ba ku bii ba pagɛ ka.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Ti naba Agiripa yele Fesetuse yeti, Tu wan bahɛ ni buraa wa, a mɛŋa han ka yele ni ti a boti a paaɛ la Kaiza zi'an.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Tumam n ku puti'irɛ ti tu yeti tu mɔm uureŋɔ la kiŋɛ la Itali la, ti ba yehe Pɔɔl la sɛba en boi paka deen la basɛba iŋɛ Yuliyuse nu'uhen, se'em en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega en de tiŋambihega daana Agastus pɛntiireba tansugere la.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Ti tumam n zom Adramitum uureŋɔ n maahum ti ku kiŋɛ ko'om nɔgbɛma tihi se'ehi woo n kaaɛ Esiya la, ti uureŋɔ la pɔhɛ kinɛ, ti se'em n dole tu yɛ'a la dɛna Aristakuse n de Masedoniya yehe Tɛsalonika na la.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Yiimbeere, ti tu paaɛ Sidɔn, ti Yuliyuse iŋɛ Pɔɔl sum, bo' en sore ti a kiŋɛ a zɔduma zi'ihen ta vo'ohe fii.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Ti tu ze'ele bilam, kiŋɛ ta paaɛ Sapuruse, se'ere en sɔi la, kusebego en ŋmɛ'ɛri tu'uha tɔ.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Tumam n page ateko la zuo ta gaŋɛ Kilikiya la Pamfiliya tihi ba'ahɛ la pooren, tu wa'am na paaɛ la Maira n boi Lisiya sɔ'ɔlum puan.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Bilam ti pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma wa Alɛgezandiriya uureŋɔ en yeti ku kiŋɛ Itali, ti a bahɛ ti tu tee kɛ̃ kuŋɔ.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Gee sabeeo la en lobere tu'uha tu na la, ti tu kina fiifiihe dabeha kekeko la wuŋerɛ, wa'am na paaɛ Kiniduse, Gee kusebego la en kelum tu'uha tu la, ti tu kan le nyaa kiŋɛ bilam bɔba la, ti tu ŋmɛregɛ doohe Kerete gulengukɔ la en lɛm Salemone la.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Ti tu mɔm la wuŋerɛ gaŋɛ bilam, wa'am na paaɛ zi'an en yi'iri uurenɔ ze'ele suŋɔ zi'an la, lɛm la Lasiya la.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Saŋa en diŋɛ gu'um, gee ti mɔŋa bala ma'a me dɛna nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔlurɛ saŋa la pun tole mɛ, ti Pɔɔl ka'am ba.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: yeti, Kpɛ'ɛnduma wa, mam nyɛ ti tu ko'om wa zuo kinɛ wan dɛna nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega, ti tu lɔgerɔ zo'e zo'e n boi uureŋɔ wa puan wa wan yee ko'omen wa. Dagi lɔgerɔ la la uureŋɔ wa la ma'a, gee tu vom me.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Gee pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma wa en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega wa, tugum ba' kɛleha la se'em n mɔni uureŋɔ la, la se'em en sɔi uureŋɔ la gana sɛla ti Pɔɔl yele la.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Gee ze'elego zi'an la n tari dabeem ti tu kan ta'am bɔna bini ti ɔɔrɔ la ta tole la, zo'e zo'e la pa' la ba pɔhɛ kinɛ bilam. Daanse'ere ba wan paaɛ Fonisiya, zi'an ti wan ta'am bɔna bilam ti ɔɔrɔ saŋa la tole. Ze'kana wa dela Kerete uurenɔ ze'elego zi'an en tɔ gɔbega zanɔya bɔba la zuɔ zanɔya.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Kusebego en lobere bããlam en ze'ele gɔbega bɔba lɔbera na, ti ba ti'ihɛ ti ku wan suŋɛ ban n boti se'em la, bala ti tu pɔhɛ kinɛ, wa'am na gaŋɛ Kerete, nyaa pɔra ko'om la nuuren.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Gee la ka yuue, ti kusebekãtɛ ti ba yi'ira kɔ zanɔya dapɔya bɔba kusebego en bala ze'ele Kerete puan na.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Kusebego wa n lure uureŋɔ la ti ku kan ta'am ŋmɛregɛ tu'uhɛ ku la, tu yari bahɛ mɛ ti kusebego la za'ara tu yɛ'a.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Ti tu dɛ ko'om la zuo bala doohe ko'om la tiŋasuka tinpika kayima en boi gulengukɔ puan ti ba yi'ira ka Kawuda. Ti tu mɛ gee nyaŋɛ uureŋɔ la uurenɔ bibehi la en yule ku la.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Ban nyaŋɛ ve'e uurembibehi kɛ̃ uureŋɔ la puan na, gee dabeem en tari ba ti uureŋɔ la wan wɛgɛ la, ba dikɛ la mi'ihi lu' uureŋɔ pulin na ta taaba. Gee ve'e sɛla en ze'eti uureŋɔ la dooe na, gee ti kusebego la kelum za'ara uureŋɔ kina.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Ti kusebego wa ba vɛgelum tu bala ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti ba pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra lɔgerɔ la lɔbera baha ko'om la puan.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: ti dabeha atã daarɛ la, ti ba dikɛ ba mɛhi nu'uhi ɔ̃bɛ lɔgeseto en de uureŋɔ la lɔgerɔ la lobe bahɛ ko'om la puan.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Wuntɛɛŋa bii ŋmarebibehi en ka le niiɛ na dabeha kekeko la, ti ko'mi'ihi la me ka malum sigeha la, tumam vo'ore puti'irɛ woo nyaa kɔ'ɛ mɛ.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Ban ka di dia ti la yuue la, ti Pɔɔl nyaa ihege saazuo ba puan yeti, Buraahi wa, la daa nari ni ti ya kelehe ni mam n daa yele ya se'em la, la daa dagi ni tu ze'ele Kerete la kiŋɛ ni, sageŋɔ la sɛla woo en bɔregɛ wa kan boni.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Mam yeti ya nananawa, ti nɛrawoo kpeŋe a suure, gee ya puan se'em kan koŋe a nyɔvore, gee uureŋɔ la ma'a n wan sagum.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, zaam yu'uŋɔ, Yinɛ se'ere ti mam dɔla gee na'aha wa suŋera en zaam wa'am na ze'ele mam sakpikɔ.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: gee yele mam yeti, Da zɔta dabeem Pɔɔl, gee fum wan ba'ahum ze'ele Kaiza nɛŋan; la dela asira, ti Yinɛ bo' sɛba woo me n boi uureŋɔ wa puan la fum wa piirɛ.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Bala zuo, kpemehe ya ya mɛhi, buraahi wa, se'ere en sɔi la, mam tari sakerɛ Yinɛ puan, ti la yeti la iŋɛ mɛ wuu eŋa n yele mam se'em la.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Gee tu wan ba'ahum kiŋɛ ta ŋmɛ' ko'om puan tiŋa ka yima doone.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Yinɛ en le ta lu pa'aha dabeha pia la anaahi la, bala ma'a ti kusebego la kelum za'ara tɔ dɔla Adria tɔla la, yu'unsunsua saŋa, ti sɛba n mɔni uureŋɔ la ta ti'ihera ti ba pɔri la doone.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Ti ba dikɛ magehega ti tibehegɔ yula ka, bahɛ ko'om la puan ha ti ba bihɛ ko'om la n kɔ' se'em, nyɛ ti ko'om la kɔ' wuu santira kɔbega la pihiyi'. Ti ba le kiŋɛ fii wa, le bahɛ magehega la ko'om la puan, nyɛ ti ko'om la kɔ' wuu santira pihiwai'.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Ti dabeem en tari tɔ ti uureŋɔ la wan ta ŋmɛ' tahi la zuo, ti ba dikɛ ze'elego tibehum la n lu' la mi'ihi la banaahi bahɛ ko'om la puan, gee kɔ'ɔm pu'uha ti beere yilegɛ kalam.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Gee bala ma'a la, sɛba n mɔni uureŋɔ la maaheni mɛ ti ba zɔ gee bahɛ uureŋɔ la, pun dikɛ uureŋɔ bila bahɛ ko'om la puan la, gee ita wuu ba yeti ba dikɛ la uureŋɔ ze'elego tibehum la bahɛ uureŋɔ la nɛŋa bɔba ko'om la puan la.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: ti Pɔɔl waaɛ pɛntiireba nɛŋadaana la en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la a pɛntiireba la yeti, Nɛreba wa han ka yee uureŋɔ wa puan ya kan piiɛ.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Ti pɛntiireba la ŋmaaɛ mi'se'ehi en gurɛ uureŋɔ bila la bahɛ, ti ko'mi'ihi la tari uureŋɔ pika la kiŋɛ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Gee ti beere baŋɛ yilegɛ la, Pɔɔl kpemehe ba za'a mɛ yeti, Zina pa'ahɛ la tu dabeha pia la anaahi en bala ti tu puga ko'om wa zuo, la kom, ti buum ka tole ya nɔyan.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Bala zuo la, mam beleni ya woo mɛ ti nɛra woo di sɛla la a nyɛ paŋa. Se'ere n sɔi la, ya se'em zonko kan bɔregɛ ya zuin.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: En tɔgɛ ŋwana ba'ahɛ la, gee looe boroboro, bo' Yinɛ fara ba za'a nɛŋan, gee gi'ɛ dɛ pɔhɛ ɔbega.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Ti la kpemehe ba za'a, ti ba woo me di dia.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Ba woo en di ma'ɛ ba kom ba'ahɛ la, ba pɔhɛ gbɛ'ɛra la mui bɔra en boi uureŋɔ la puan la lɔbera baha ko'om la puan, ti la bahɛ ti uureŋɔ la ana faga faga.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Beere en ta yilegɛ la, sɛba n mɔni uureŋɔ la ka baŋɛ ti doone n bala gã'a la, gee tugum ti'ihɛ ti la yeen dɛna la ko'om la kinkɛleŋa, bala ti ba iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ yeti, ba wan kɔ'ɔm bahɛ ti uureŋɔ la ze'ele la bilam, ba han wan nyaŋɛ.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Ŋwana ti ba ɔ̃begɛ tibehegɔ seko en ze'eti uureŋɔ la, bahɛ ko'om la puan, gee lorum mi'se'ehi en lu' tara mɔna uureŋɔ la me bahɛ, gee nyaa bire uureŋɔ la poore bahɛ kusebego la, ti sabeeo la nyaa za'ara ba yɛ'a doone na.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Gee uureŋɔ la wa'am na ŋmɛ' la kulenkãtenɛ, zɔ ta sãɛ tiŋa ha bɛgerɔ puan ha, yari firɛ zɛ'a bilam kan ta'am vurege, ti ko'mi'ihi kãra la me ŋmɛ uureŋɔ la paŋa paŋa ti ku tahɛ.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Ti pɛntiireba la me maahum ti ba ku sɛba woo en boi paka deen bɔna uureŋɔ la puan la, ti ba se'em da bum piiɛ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Gee pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛma la en biheri pɛntiireba kɔbega la n boti a faaɛ Pɔɔl bahɛ la zuo, a ka bo' sore ti pɛntiireba la tum ban ti'ihɛ ba tum se'em la, gee a tum bo' la a nuurɛ yeti, sɛba en ta' ko'om la, diŋɛ nɛŋa lu ko'om la puan bum dooe yia.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: gee ti sɛba n gee la me digehɛ uureŋɔ la dɔparebalagehi la zom tu zuo me bum yakɛ. La iŋɛ la ŋwana, ti ba tari nɛrawoo dooe doone na la inma'ahum.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Gee tumam n paaɛ doone suŋa ba'ahɛ la, ti tu nyaa baŋɛ ti timbila la en boi ko'om tiŋasuka la yu'urɛ en yi'iri Malita.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Ti tiŋa la nɛreba pa'alɛ tɔ ba itesuŋɔ en ka tari makerɛ. Lan wa dɛna sanire saŋa ti saa pɔhɛ niia ti la ma'ara la, ba tuurɛ la bugum gee yi' tu za'a ti tu zi'ire kaaɛ bɔ ɔɔha.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Ŋwana zuo la, beni yɔ'ɔ ti Yuda nɛra tara? Bii beni tuulum n boi ŋmaa puan?.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Ti Pɔɔl me lagehɛ dɔpu'uha na biŋe lɔɔha ita bugum la puan, ti kampia ɛkɛ yehe na, bugum la wɔlegɔ la zuo, vile Pɔɔl nu'o kankaŋi yula.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: La zo'e mɛ zi'an woo. Yia dela, Yuuduma la en pɔhɛ to'e Yinɛ yele sɛ'a en sugɛ la.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Tiŋa la nɛreba en nyɛ ti bunsɛla wa yule a nu'o la, ti ba waahena bɔ'ɔra taaba yeti, Burakana wa ba'ahum dɛna la nɛrekuura; bala en sɔi ti ka pun piiɛ ko'om puan kum la, buurɔ ka bo' ka sore ti ka vɔna.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Gee Yuuduma wa basɛba yuum han ka tara sakerɛ? Ban ka tari sakerɛ la wan gu' Yinɛ en tari sakerɛ se'ere la ba la me yoi?.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Gee ti Pɔɔl me mɛhegɛ kampia la bahɛ bugum la puan gee ti be'em ka paaɛ en.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Sore ka boi! La asaala woo n pun dɛna pumparema la, Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la, Bala ti fum wan tara buurɔ fum tugum puan, gee di mi'a gakerɛ woo puan.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Ti nɛreba la guri mɛ ti a iŋa fuuhum, bii a lu ki bilam bilam. Gee ban gu'uhi yuue, ta nyɛ ti sɛla sɛla ka ita Pɔɔl la, ti ba tee ba puti'ira le yeti a dela bagerɛ.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Gee tumam ŋmɛlema la han bahɛ ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niiɛ na, tu wan yeti bu? Yinɛ ka tari buurɔ a han taaɛ a sunsunyiim bahɛ tu zuo?.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Nananawa zɛ'kana ti nɛreba kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra la, mɛa puan, samana zo'e zo'e en boi bini dɛna tiŋa la n boi ko'om tiŋasuka la naba yu'urɛ en yi'ira Pabiliyuse dinɛ, se'em n to'e tumam, bihɛ tumam suŋa dabeha atã.

eng: Absolutely not! If God werent just, how could he judge the world?.
local: Sore ka boi! La han dɛna bala, Yinɛ wan iŋɛ la ŋwani gakɛ tiŋa wa?.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Ti la iŋɛ, ti Pabiliyuse wa sɔ lu bã'a gã'a la ɔɔrɔ la nyɛ'a. Ti Pɔɔl kiŋɛ ta bihɛ en, gee tibɛ en doohe la a dikɛ la a nu'uhi pagelɛ a zuo.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Gee la han doohe la mam pumpɔreŋɔ la zuo ti Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la niiɛ na bɔ'ɔra en suŋa, beni zuo ti ba wan sagum mam ti n dela be'em daana?.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: Dina en iŋɛ tole la, ti tiŋa la puan nɛresɛba woo en gee tara bã'ahi yima yima la, me wa'am na, ti Pɔɔl tibɛ ba.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: Ŋwani ti tu yi kɔ'ɔm kan yele wuu nɛreba basɛba n sageni tumam yu'ura se'em ti tumam yeti, Tu kɔ'ɔm tuna be'em ti la bahɛ ti sum wa'am na? Nɛrebana duma wa sageŋɔ gã' gura ba mɛ.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Ti nɛreba wa na'ahɛ tumam paaɛ paaɛ la ba bo'olum. Pooren ti tumam ta yeti tu kiŋɛ la, ti ba tari tumam en boti sɛla woo la na iŋɛ uureŋɔ seko ti tu yeti tu zom la puan.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: La nyaa dela ŋwani wa? Tumam Yuda buuri wa nyani basɛba me bii? Aayi, la fii wa me. Tumam pun diŋɛ yeti, nɛra woo, Yuuduma la Giriki buuri za'a boi la be'em paŋa puan.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Ŋmarehi sitã en tole, ti tumam ihege la uureŋɔ la. Ku wa yee la tiŋa la en boi ko'om tiŋasuka la puan. Ku wa bɔna bini la ɔɔrɔ saŋa la puan, dɛna Alɛgezandiriya uureŋɔ, ti liiba paga la ti ba na'aha la bɔna ku puan.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Se'em ka boi dɛna tuuntɔɔ, la ayima me.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Tu pɔhɛ la Sirakuse, ze'ele bilam dabeha atã;.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Ba se'em ka bɔkeri, Se'em ka boi ɛɛra Yinɛ.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Gɔlum na paaɛ Aregium, dikɛ daarɛ deyima bilam, ti kusebego ze'ele tiŋanuurɛ bɔba vuke na, ti yi'i daarɛ la ti tu wa'am na paaɛ Puteyoli.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Ba woo bire la ba pɔya, ba naaɛ la taaba bahɛ yoo. Se'em ka boi ita itesuŋɔ, Ayima me ka boi.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Bilam ti tu nyɛ suurɔ tuseto, ti ba me yi' tu ti tu wa'am na kɛ̃ la ba dabeha ayopɔi. Tumam n doohe se'em wa'am na paaɛ Arome na n bala.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ba koohe dela yɔɔrɔ kãra, ti ba tari ba zɛlehi pã'aha. Kampia yabega en boi ba nɔya puan.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Ti suurɔ la n boi Arome n wum ti tu kini na la, yehe la zaaɛ zaarɛ Apiyosi Da'a bɔba ha la, la Saama Vo'ohego Zi'ihi Sitã la puan ha tu'uhɛ tu na. Pɔɔl en ta nyɛ ba la, a bo' la Yinɛ fara gee ti a iŋa kpeŋe.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Ba nɔya pirɛ mɛ la pɔrɛ la yele tooro.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Tumam n wa'am na na kɛ̃ Arome puan la, ba bahɛ Pɔɔl mɛ ti a bɔna a ma'a, la pɛntiira sɛka en guri en la.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Ba nama kabelɛ ɛɛra la ziim ka'ahegɔ.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: Dabeha atã daarɛ, ti Pɔɔl yi' kiinduma la en biheri Yuda buuri wa la. Ban lagehɛ na ba'ahɛ la, ti a yele ba yeti, N suurɔ, la mam en pun ka iŋɛ se'ere tuue tu nɛreba Yuuduma wa, bii tu yaabeduma kiha wa la, gee la bala za'a, ba nyɔkɛ mam mɛ Yerusalɛm, dikɛ mam ta'ahɛ Arome duma ha.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: sageŋɔ la nɔyalegerɛ en ni yee ban doohe zi'an woo tole.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Ban ta viihɛ mam bihɛ la, Arome duma wa daa boti ba bahɛ mam mɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, vuurɛ daa ka boi kua la ti ba daa bɔta ti ba ku' mam la puan.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: gee suma'ahum sore la, ba ka mi.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Gee Yuuduma la en ka sakɛ la zuo, la daa pɛregɛ mam mɛ, ti n suhɛ timbihega kpɛ'ɛma la ti a bahɛ ti n paaɛ timbihega nɛŋadaana Kaiza nɛŋan, la mam pun ka tari zɛregerɛ se'ere la n mɛŋa tiŋa duma la.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Yinɛ zoolum la fii wa ka boi ba ninin.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Yele dina wa zuo, ti mam suhɛ sore ti n nyɛ ya, la n tɔgɛ la ya, lan de Izirale beere puti'irɛ wa zuo ti ba ba' mam la bãhi wa.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Tumam mi' ti ze'elego la n yeti sɛla woo la, yeti bɔ'ɔra la sɛba n dole ku la, ti nɛra da ta tara n ka baŋɛ mɛ, tiŋa wa za'a wan ta yele ban daa iŋɛ sɛla la sɛla.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Ti ba lebehe en yeti, Tumam ka to'e gɔŋɔ Yudiya, doohe fum puan. Tu suuɔ la kuyina me n kini na saŋa woo la me ka tabelɛ yele fum yele be'o ti tu wum.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Nɛra ka boi wan ta to'e buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan doohe la yele sɛ'a en boi ze'elego la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la puan ti be'em puti'irɛ bɔna.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Gee tumam wan bɔta ti tu wum la fum mɛŋa zi'an, fum ti'ihɛ se'em doohe sadɔkuna wa ti tu mina ti zi'an woo ba zɛregera ku me wa.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Gee nananawa, ze'elego la pooren, Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niiɛ mɛ, ti nɔtɔgeheba la pa'alegɔ la tɔgɛ la yele.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Ban daa ta ze'ele dabeherɛ ti ma le tu'uhɛ la ena la, ba wa'am la a ga'aregɔ zi'an na, ba nɛrekuuŋɔ. Ze'ele bulika tara tara la zaanuurɛ, ti a tu' yele la n ani se'em bo' ba, yɛta ba Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la n ani se'em, zigera ti a tee ba puti'ira la Yezu, ze'ele Moose ze'elego la, la nɔtɔgeheba la puan.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ dole la sakerɛ Yezu Kiresta puan, bɔ'ɔra sɛba woo en nam, ti yima yima ka bɔna.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Ti basɛba sakɛ doohe la Pɔɔl n yele se'em la, gee basɛba me zagehɛ ti bamam kan nam.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: saaleba za'a diŋɛ tuue mɛ, kan le paaɛ Yinɛ suŋa la.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Bala, ba ka sakɛ bo' taaba, bala zuo la, ban yiregerɛ la, Pɔɔl tɔgɛ yele deyima ma'a yeti: Fa'a Sia la yuum tari la buurɔ la a yele ya yaabeduma doohe nɔtɔgeha Azaaya puan yeti.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: Ba nyɛ buurɔ doohe la zu'uhegɔ la en de bo'olum la, doohe faarɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Kiŋɛ nɛrebana wa zi'an ta yele ba yeti, Ya sirum wan kelehe, gee ya kan malum bɔkɛ, ya me sirum wan bihɛ, gee ya kan malum nyɛ.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: se'em ti Yinɛ dikɛ en diilɛ ti a dɛna kaabegɔ seko en lilege Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ doohe a ziim la, en tuni dɔla sakerɛ puan la. Yinɛ iŋɛ ŋwana pa'alɛ la a tuntɔɔnɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, a sugeri la puan ti a bahɛ saaleba be'em sebo ti ba tabelɛ tum.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Se'ere n sɔi la, nɛrebana wa suya ba' bukɛ mɛ, ti ba tuba li' ka wuna, ti ba mum ba nini, ti ba da biha la ba nini la, gee me da kɛleha la ba tuba, gee me da bɔkera la ba suya la ba ta'am ŋmɛregɛ na ti n tibɛ ba'.

eng: during the time of Gods patient tolerance. He also did this to demonstrate that he is righteous in the present time, and to treat the one who has faith in Jesus as righteous.
local: A iŋɛ dina wa pa'alɛ la zina beere wa, ti Yinɛ dela tuuntɔɔ daana, gee me bɔ'ɔra se'em woo n tari sakerɛ Yezu puan buurɔ.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Bala, ye mini ya zina dabeherɛ wa yeti, Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum buna wa muulɛ bo' Sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la mɛ, ti ba me wan kelehe bu. .

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Beni zuo ti tu nyaa ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ la? Sɛla ka boi. Ze'elego seko bii? Bii tuunsɛ'a? Aayi. La dela sakerɛ, gee dagi itegɔ.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Pɔɔl kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la bilam yuuma ayi', bihera a mɛŋa, gee tɔ'ɔha sɛba woo en kini a zi'an na la.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam n gurɛ se'ere dela, Nɛra nyɛti buurɔ, la sakerɛ puan gee dagi a dole la kiha en gulehɛ bɔna ze'elego la puan.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: gee muula Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la yele, gee pa'ala Yezu Kiresta yele la sukpe'ene, ti sɛla ka gu'ura en.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: Abunɔ, bii Yinɛ dela Yuuduma ma'a Yinɛ? A dagi sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la me Yinɛ? Ɛ̃ɛ, la dela bala, a dela sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la me Yinɛ.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Yinɛ dela ayina, gee wan bo' sɛba en ŋma la buurɔ ba sakerɛ zuo, gee bo' sɛba en ka ŋma la buurɔ ba sakerɛ me zuo.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Ŋwana zuo, tu lobe ze'elego la bahɛ sakerɛ la zuo? Sore ka boi bala la fii me. Gee lan tugum dɛna se'ere dela, tumam en tugum zãla ze'elego la.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Beni ti tu wan ta'am yeti Aberaham en de tu yaaba inganɛ puan la, yuum nyɛ a mɛŋa tuuma puan?.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Aberaham yuum han di buurɔ la a tuuma puan, a tari sɛla ti a wan ta'am dikɛ ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, gee dagi Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Gee Gɔŋɔ la yeti boo? Aberaham nam la Yinɛ, ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ti a dɛna tuuntɔɔ.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Se'em woo en tum, ba ka bihɛ nyɛ a yɔɔrɛ la ti de dɛna bo'olum, gee la dela sɛla ti a tum to'e.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Gee se'em en ka tum, gee yeen nam la se'em n bo'ori be'em duma buurɔ la, sakerɛ dinɛ taaba ti ba bahɛ nyɛ ti a dela tuuntɔɔ daana.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Bala mɛa ti naba Deviti tɔgɛ doohe sum sebo en boi bo' sɛba ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ti ba dɛna tuuntɔɔ duma gee dagi la Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ la ba tuuma la yeti.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba duma ti ba bahɛ ba tuure bo' ba, la sɛba ti Yinɛ pi' ba be'em bahɛ la.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Sum boi mɛ la se'em ti Daana la ka bihɛ nyɛ a be'em la.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Sum buna wa purɛ bo' la sɛba en ŋma la ma'a, bii bu ta' sɛba en ka ŋma la me mɛ? Se'ere en sɔi la tu yeti,Sakerɛ zuo ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ Aberaham ti a dɛna tuntɔɔdaana.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Yinɛ daa bihɛ nyɛ Aberaham ti a de tuuntɔɔ nɛra la ŋwani? La dela en daa nan ka ŋma la bii en daa ŋma ba'ahɛ la? La dela en daa nan ka ŋma la, gee dagi en daa ŋma ba'ahɛ la.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Aberaham daa to'e ŋmaa daalehum la mɛ ti la dɛna sɛla n pa'alɛ a tuntɔɔnɛ la ti a to'e la sakerɛ la, ti a nan ka ŋma. Iti kuna wa dela, la iŋɛ ti a dɛna yaaba bo' sɛba woo en nam gee ka ŋma la, n yeti la sɛba ti Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ ba ti ba dɛna tuuntɔɔ duma la.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Gee me kelum dɛna yaaba bu sɛba en ŋma la, dagi ba ŋmaa la ma'a, gee sɛba en tari sakerɛ se'ere taaba ti tu yaaba Aberaham yuum tara gee nyaa baŋɛ ŋma la.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nɔbiŋere la n biŋe ti Aberaham la a yaahi ti ba wan sɔna timpaberɛ wa za'a la yuum ka tɔgɛ bo' Aberaham bii a yaahi la la doohe la ze'elego la puan, gee la doohe tuntɔɔnɛ en ze'eti sakerɛ puan.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: La han dɛna la sɛba en dole ze'elego la n wan ta dɛna asooesɔna duma, sakerɛ yi dela zaŋa, ti nɔbiŋere la me bahɛ yoo.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la tari la Yinɛ sunsunyiim kina na. Gee zi'an ti ze'elego ka bɔna, galeŋɔ me pun ka bɔna.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Ŋwana zuo, la dela sakerɛ en bahɛ ti nɔbiŋere la, doohe zu'uhegɔ puan paaɛ Aberaham yaahi woo, dagi la sɛba ma'a n dole ze'elego la, gee sɛba me en tari Aberaham sakerɛ la taaba la. A dela tu za'a sɔ.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: wuu lan gulehɛ gɔnɔ la puan se'em yeti, Mam iŋɛ hɔ ti fum dɛna la tihi zo'e zo'e sɔ — Yinɛ se'ere ti Aberaham nam la nɛŋan. Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori kum vom, gee baha ti sɛla en ka boi bɔna la.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: La en pun bɔna bɔnsɛka en ka tare beere puti'irɛ la, gee doohe beere puti'irɛ puan a nam mɛ ti eŋa wan ta wa'am na dɛna tihi zo'e zo'e sɔ, wuu eŋa en wum se'em yeti, Fum yaahi yeti ba wi'ɛ la bala la.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: A sakerɛ la ka kɔ'ɛ, la en yuum bihɛ ti a mɛŋa inganɛ la ba'ahɛ la za'a yɛ'a yɔɔen la, . Bii eŋa en yuum bihɛ Saara kundɔgenɛ la en paaɛ se'em la.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Bumbu'um yuum ka boi bahɛ ti a miim la Yinɛ nɔbiŋere la, gee a tugum malum kpe'em mɛ la a sakerɛ, pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Kɔ'ɔm lu' a suure kankaŋi, ti Yinɛ wan ta'am tum en biŋe nuurɛ ti a tum se'em la.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Bala zuo, Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ a sakerɛ la mɛ ti a dɛna tuuntɔɔ daana.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Gulehegɔ en yeti, Yinɛ bihɛ nyɛ en mɛ la, ka gulehɛ la eŋa ma'a zuo.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: gee tumam me. Yinɛ wan bihɛ nyɛ tumam me ti tu dɛna tuntɔɔduma, tumam sɛba woo en nam se'em daana en vo'e Yezu tu Daana kum puan la.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Se'em ti Yinɛ dikɛ en tihɛ na ti a ki tumam tuure zuo, gee daa vo'e en tumam buurɔ zuo la.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, Yezu Kiresta da'aberɛ, ti Yinɛ ayi' mam ti n dɛna tuntuna, yehe mam ze'ele bo' Yinɛ Faarɛ yelehum la ma'a.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: yelehum sebo ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ wunse'eren ha, doohe a nɔtɔgeheba puan, Fa'a gɔnɔ la puan la.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Faarɛ yelehum sebo en de Yinɛ dayua, se'em tuuhum en ze'ele Naba Deviti puan na la, wuu en de asaala la.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Doohe tuuntɔɔ sia puan, ba looe en mɛ ti a dɛna Yinɛ dayua, la paŋa, doohe en ihege a kum puan la, Ena daana dela Yezu Kiresta, tu Daana la.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Se'em puan ti tumam to'e zu'uhegɔ, la tuntuneba yɔ'ɔ, ti tu bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a me nam, gee tara sakerɛ a yu'urɛ la puan.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Yamam mɛhi ti Yinɛ yi' ti ya dɛna Yezu Kiresta dinɛ wa pa'ahɛ bini mɛ.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Pɔɔl n gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba woo ti Yinɛ nɔŋɛ ya, ti ya bɔna Arome tiŋa puan, ti Yinɛ yi' ya ti ya dɛna nɛresunduma la. Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, ti suma'ahum en ze'eti Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ, la Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an la dɛna ya dinɛ.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Yia, mam bo'ori Yinɛ fara Yezu puan ya za'a zuo, beni zuo, ya sakerɛ la yele to'e tiŋa wa zuo za'a mɛ.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Yinɛ wa ti mam tuna bɔ'ɔra dɛ la n sia za'a, doohe mam muuli a dayua Yezu yelehum wa, en de mam aze'nyɛdaana, ti mam ka malum tamena ya n pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Sɔkera ti Yinɛ suboolum en de se'em la, n wan ba'ahum nyɛ sore ba'ahegɔ puan ta'am wa'am ya zi'an na.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: N ba' boti ni n nyɛ ni ya mɛ, la n tɔrɛ zu'uhegɔ puan bo'olum la ya, ti la kpemehe ya Sia puan.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Bii tu lagum bugelɛ taaba la tu sakerɛ. Yamam sakerɛ, la mam sakerɛ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: Mam boti ya mina dina wa n suurɔ, ti saŋa zo'e zo'e ti n ni maahum ti n wa'am ya zi'an na, . N boti ni ti n wa'am ni na, la n bum sɛla ya puan bo' Yinɛ, wuu mam n bum se'em tihi sise'ehi nɛreba puan bo' Yinɛ la.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Mam tari la Giriki, la sɛba nini n ka yu'ɛ sanɛ, tara yɛmduma la sɛba en nan ka bɔkeri sanɛ.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Gee me le mu'ɛ nini ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' yamam sɛba me n boi Arome tiŋa wa.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Vi ka tari mam la faarɛ yelehum wa. Beni zuo, bu dela Yinɛ faarɛ paŋa, bo' se'em woo en tari sakerɛ, Yuuduma yia, gee nyaa bɔ'ɔra Girikiduma.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Bo puan ti Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la niira, dɔla sakerɛ puan, wuu lan pun gulehɛ se'em yeti, Se'emdaana en de tuuntɔɔ wan vɔna mɛ la sakerɛ la.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Yinɛ sunsunyiim ze'ele la saazuo ha niiɛ bahɛ nɛre sɛba be'em la ba tuunfi'iha en gu'uri yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ban suni ti ba mina Yinɛ yele sɛ'a la, niiɛ bo' ba mɛ kpaii. Beni zuo, Yinɛ mɛŋa en niiɛ a bo' ba.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Pɔhɛ Yinɛ n naam tiŋa wa se'em, a paŋa la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ, la a naanɛ la, la ban pun sugɛ la, gee asaala bɔkɛ a mɛ, gee nyɛ ba kpaii, doohe sɛla woo ti Yinɛ naam la puan. Ŋwana zuo, asaala ka tari a yeti eŋa ka baŋɛ mɛ.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la ban pun mina Yinɛ wa la, ba ka na'aheri en wuu a dela Yinɛ, bii ba bɔ'ɔra en fara fara, gee ba tugum lebege la nɛrefɔgerɔ ba ti'ihegɔ puan. Ti ba putɛ'vɔya la en ti'ihɛ yele yaalehi la pirɛ la lika.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Ba yeti bamam dela yɛm duma, gee ba tugum lebege la geto.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: nyaa tugum tee dikɛ na'ahegɔ seko en de Yinɛ vua wa na'ahegɔ la bo' naanɛ sɛ'a en ŋwani la asaala en kiiri, bii niihe, bii duhi en tari nama anaahi, bii bunvuula.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' ba kunkoma'ahum sebo ti ba suya bɔta la, dɛgerɔ tuuma la, ti ba tuna zufagehum tuuma la taaba.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Ba tee Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la en de se'em la mɛ bo' pumpɔreŋɔ, nyaa tugum na'aha gee malena malema bɔ'ɔra naanɛ, gee bahɛ naana la mɛŋa, se'em en nari pɛka saŋa woo la. Taari.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' zufagehum kunkoma'ahum, ti ba pɔgehi tee pɔka la buraa en naari taaba se'em la, bo' sadɔɔ seko en ka nari.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Bala mɛa ti buraahi me tugum bahɛ ba naara la ba pɔgeba, gee tugum tara kunkoma'ahum la taaba, ti buraahi tuna zufagehum tuuma la ba burataaba, lageha namehegɔ kãtɛ biŋera ba mɛhi zuto ba tuure wa puan.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Ban pun kelum ka bihɛ nyɛ ti la nari ba baŋɛ Yinɛ la, ti Yinɛ dikɛ ba bo' putɛ'ŋmɛlema dikɛ ba bo' yele yaalehi se'ehi en dagi ba tuna ma'a.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Ba kɔ'ɔm pirɛ mɛ la tuunfi'iha yima yima, be'em, fãa, ti'iha be'em bɔ'ɔra nɛreba. Pirɛ la nintɛrega, kua, zaba, pã'ahegɔ, sɔtuuma, ba dela yelebibehi duma.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: Sagena nɛreba yu'ura, yaaɛ Yinɛ, tuura, ukerɛ, pa'ali mɛŋa, be'em maaheŋɔ, ka nana dɔgereba.

eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: geto, nɔkɔ'ɔreba sukãberɛ, ka tara nɛreba giile.
