eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Ba mi' ti Yinɛ yeti, sɛba woo en iti yele ana wa dela ba ki, gee ba pun ka ita a ba ma'a, gee ba pɛgeri la sɛba me en ita a la.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bala zuo, tumam en to'e buurɔ doohe sakerɛ puan la, tu tari suma'ahum mɛ la Yinɛ, suma'ahum buna wa ze'ele la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan na.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Se'em puan ti tu doohe nyɛ yɔ'ɔ doohe puan ti tu tara zu'uhegɔ seko ti tu yaga ku zuo wa, gee me ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la tumam beere wan lagum nyɛ Yinɛ suŋa la.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: La dagi bala ma'a, gee tu me ŋmɛ'ɛri nyu'urɔ mɛ la tu namehegɔ la, gee mina ti namehegɔ wa bo'ori tu la ŋmibega.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: ti ŋmibega bɔ'ɔra tu aŋa en bi'ɛ, ti aŋa en bi'ɛ la bɔ'ɔra tɔ beere puti'irɛ.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: ti beere puti'irɛ wa me kan diihe tu vi, beni zuo, Yinɛ ka'ɛ la nɔŋerɛ bahɛ tu suya puan, doohe Fa'a Sia la ti a bo' tu la puan.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Se'em ma'a ti tumam daa nan dɛna tɔregɔ duma la, sansɛka en daa nari la puan, ti Kiresta ki be'em duma wa zuo.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la kpe'em mɛ ti tu ti'ihɛ ti nɛra wan ki bo' nɛre tuuntɔɔ. Han dɛna la nɛresuŋɔ, daanse'ere nɛra ta'am sirum maahum ti a ki.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Gee Yinɛ pa'alɛ tumam la a nɔŋerɛ, la tumam en daa kelum bɔna tu be'em puan la, ti Kiresta ki bo' tu.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Sɛla en gani bala dela, tumam en nyaa to'e buurɔ Kiresta ziim la puan la, tu nyɛ la faarɛ a puan, yehe Yinɛ susuntuulega la puan.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam nyaa daa han tugum dɛna Yinɛ dinduma, gee ti a dikɛ tu naaɛ la a mɛŋa, doohe a dayua kum la puan, sɛla en nyaa gani bala dela, en dikɛ tu naaɛ a mɛŋa la, tu wan nyɛ faarɛ a vom la puan.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Gee sɛla en pun gana bala dela, tu ŋmɛ'ɛri la nyu'ɔ Yinɛ puan, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zuo, se'em zuo ti tu nyaa nyɛ naarɛ la Yinɛ wa.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Bala zuo, wuu be'em en wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, doohe buraa kayima puan na se'em, ti kum me doohe be'em na, ti kum taaɛ nɛra woo se'em la, beni zuo, nɛra woo tum la be'em.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Be'em pun bɔna tiŋa wa zuo mɛ gee ti ze'elego la baŋɛ wa'am na, gee ba ka bihɛ nyɛta be'em zi'an ti ze'elego ka bɔna.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Gee la bala za'a, kum pa'alɛ a paŋa mɛ ze'ele Adam, ta paaɛ Moose, pun ta ta' sɛba be'em en ka ani wuu Adam tuure la, Adam kana wa ani wuu se'em en ye' na la.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Gee se'em ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu zaŋa la ka ani wuu se'em en tari tuure la. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba kekeko la han ki doohe la nɛre yinɛ tuure la zuo, yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la, la a bo'olum ti a bo' zaŋa la, doohe la zu'uhegɔ seko ti a zu'uhɛ buraa kayima Yezu Kiresta wa la, zu'uhegɔ kuna wan bɔna zo'e zo'e bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Bo'olum la ti a bo' tu zaŋa la, ka tari too seko taaba ti nɛre yinɛ tuure la tara la. Se'ere en sɔi la, gakerɛ la doohe la tuure deyina zuo tari sageŋɔ wa'am na, gee zaŋa bo'olum la en bo' doohe tuure zo'e zo'e zuo la, tari la buurɔ wa'am na.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: La han dɛna la nɛre yinɛ tuure la zuo ti kum doohe bilam sɔna sɛla woo la, yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla en gani bala dela, sɛba en to'e zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ la, la tuuntɔɔ zaŋa bo'olum kãtɛ la tari la vom paŋa, doohe buraa kayima Yezu Kiresta wa puan.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Bala zuo, wuu nɛre yinɛ tuure en tari sageŋɔ wa'am saaleba woo zuo se'em na la, bala mɛa ti nɛre yinɛ tuuntɔɔ tuunɛ la me bo' nɛreba woo buurɔ, la vom.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Wuu nɛre yinɛ pobiriga zuo ti zo'e zo'e dɛna be'em duma se'em la, bala mɛa ti nɛre yinɛ naŋa la zuo, ti zo'e zo'e wan dɛna tuntɔɔduma.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Gee ze'elego wa'am na ti ku bahɛ ti tuure kekeko en malum niiɛ pa'aha , gee zi'an ti tuure malum niira pa'aha la, zu'uhegɔ me malum niira pa'aha gana la bala.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: bala, be'em n pa'ali a paŋa kum puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti zu'uhegɔ wan pa'alɛ a paŋa doohe buurɔ, yu'ura sore bɔ'ɔra vom kamba'ahɛ sebo en boi Yezu Kiresta tu Daana puan la.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Bala zuo, fum ka tari fum yeti n ka baŋɛ mɛ, fum de nɛre se'em woo, fum han gakɛ basɛba. Fum n gakɛ ayima la, fum sagum la fum mɛŋa n bala. Beni zuo, fum se'em en de gakera la, iti la yele aŋa mɛa.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Gee tu mi' ti, Yinɛ en gakeri nɛre sɛba en iti yele aŋa la, dole la yelemɛŋerɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Fum bihɛ de la ŋwani, fum de se'em za'a, ti fum gakera nɛreba en iti yele sɛ'a, gee ita yele aŋa mɛa fum mɛŋa, fum ti'ihɛ ti fum wan piiɛ Yinɛ gakerɛ la?.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Bii fum tugum ka nam la Yinɛ itesuŋɔ la n ka tari tɛka la, la a ŋmibega, la a sugeri la? Fum ka baŋɛ ti Yinɛ itesuŋɔ la dela la bahɛ ti fum tee?.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Gee fum sukãberɛ la, la fum n ka malum tɛɛra lagehɛ la sunsunyiim pagela fum zuo, gura sunsunyiim dabeherɛ la ti Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ gakerɛ la wan niiɛ fum zuo na.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yeti a yɔ nɛra woo la en tum se'em.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Sɛba en ŋmibe tuna sum la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba nini mu'ɛ mɛ ɛɛra suŋa, na'ahegɔ, la sɛla en ka pu'uri. Yinɛ wan bo' ba vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Sɛba me en eere ba mɛhi en boti se'em la, ka nana yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee nana la tuunfi'iha la, Yinɛ sunsunyiim, la sunsunyiim tuulɛ en de ba dinɛ.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, wuŋerɛ en ka tari makerɛ la susageŋɔ wan bɔna bo' se'em woo n tuni be'em, pɔhera Yuuduma, gee nyaa paara Girikiduma.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Gee suŋa la na'ahegɔ, la suma'ahum wan bɔna bo' se'em woo en tuni sum la, pɔhera Yuuduma, gee nyaa paara Girikiduma me.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka lugehe.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Sɛba woo en tum be'em gee ka mina ze'elego la, ba wan bɔregɛ ti ze'elego la ka pa'ahɛ, ti sɛba woo me en tum be'em gee mina ze'elego la, ba wan gakɛ ba mɛ la ze'elego.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: La dagi sɛba en kelehe ze'elego yɛla la en de tuuntɔɔ duma Yinɛ nɛŋan, gee sɛba en tuni ze'elego la en boti se'em la ti Yinɛ wan bo' ba buurɔ.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Sɛba n dagi Yuda nɛreba la, ka mina Yinɛ ze'elego la han tuna ze'elego la en boti se'em la ma'a ma'a, la ban pun ka mina ze'elego la, ba mɛhi en de ze'elego bo' ba mɛhi.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ba pa'alɛ yeti, ze'elego la en boti se'em la, gulehɛ iŋɛ la bamam suya puan, ti ba mɛhi puti'ira dɛna ba aze'nyɛduma, ti ba mɛhi itegɔ la me wan ta zuuhe ba, bii bahɛ ba fai.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: daan se'ere daarɛ, wuu vom yelehum la en yele se'em yeti, Yinɛ wan doohe Kiresta Yezu puan gakɛ sɛla woo en sugɛ nɛre woo suuren la.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Gee fum han yi'ira fum mɛŋa Yuda nɛra, dɛla Ze'elego la, ŋmɛ'ɛra fum nyu'ɔ la fum en lɛm Yinɛ se'em.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: gee mina Yinɛ sumboolum se'em, gee mina sɛla en de ma'a se'em, doohe la ban pa'alɛ hɔ Ze'elego la puan yɛla la zuo.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: gee han mina ti fum pa'ali la fɔɔhi, dɛna niihum bo' sɛba en boi lika puan.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: dɛna se'em n pa'ali geto, dɛna se'em n pa'ali kɔmbilɛɛhi, bi'ihɛ fum mɛŋa la mi'ilum la yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere n boi Ze'elego la puan.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Bala la, fum se'em n pa'ali basɛba la, fum ka pa'ali fum mɛŋa? Fum han muula ti zua ka ani suŋa, gee ti fum mɛŋa la zuura?.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Fum se'em n yeti kabohi dela kihere la, gee ti fum mɛŋa la tuna kabohi? Fum se'em n yaaɛ baga paaɛ paaɛ la la, gee tara kunkoma'ahum la pu'uhegɔ yire lɔgerɔ?.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Fum se'em n ŋmɛ'ɛri fum nyu'ɔ la Ze'elego la, gee nyaa diihe Yinɛ vi la fum galum ze'elego la?.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Wuu lan pun gulehɛ se'em yeti, Yamam zuo, ti nɛreba basɛba tuura Yinɛ yu'urɛ la.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Buraahi ŋmaa tari tuulum dela, fum nane Ze'elego la. Gee fum han galena Ze'elego la, fum ŋmaa la lebe ta dɛna la se'em n ka ŋma.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Bala, sɛba n ka ŋma la han dɔla Ze'elego la n boti se'em la, la bamam pun ka ŋma la, ba kan biha nyɛta ba ti ba dɛna sɛba n ŋma?.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Ŋwana ti sɛba n pun ka ŋma ba inganɛ, gee nana Ze'elego la, wan zɛregɛ yamam sɛba n ŋma, zãli Ze'elego la ti ba gulehɛ la, gee galena ku yɛla la.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Nɛra dagi Yuda buuri doohe la a yiŋa, bii yelemɛŋerɛ ŋmaa dɛna yiŋa bii ingɔŋɔ yele.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ se'em n de Yuda buuri paaɛ paaɛ dela puan ha. Ŋmaa yelemɛŋerɛ dela suure puan. La dela Sia puan, dagi inganɛ. Nɛre-ina taaba ka to'ore pɛka nɛreba zi'an, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa zi'an.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Beni ti tu nyaa wan yele? Tu kɔ'ɔm kelum tuna be'em, ti la bahɛ ti zu'uhegɔ malum pa'aha?.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Sore ka boi bala la fii me! Ŋwani ti tumam sɛba en ka le bɔna be'em tuuma puan la, wan le lebena vɔna bu puan?.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Ya ka mi' ti tumam sɛba woo en sɔ tɔ Kiresta Yezu puan la, ba sɔ tɔ ki'ihɛ la a kum la puan?.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Bala zuo, ba pi' tu mɛ la Kiresta, doohe ban sɔ tɔ ki'ihɛ a kum la puan la, ti Kiresta en ihege kum la puan a Sɔ paŋa suŋa puan se'em la, bala mɛa ti tumam me wan kina la vom paalum.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Tumam han naaɛ la en mɛ a kum buna taaba wa puan, tu wan ba'ahum ta naaɛ la en a ihegere dina taaba wa me puan.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Tu mi' ti ba daa kpa' tu naanɛ kɛka la mɛ la Kiresta, ti tu be'em naanɛ la sagum, ti tu da le dɛna da'aba bo' be'em.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en ki, boi la fai ti be'em ka le tana en.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Gee tumam han ki mɛ la Kiresta, tu nam ya ti tu me wan ta vɔna la en.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Tu mi' ti Kiresta en vo'e kum puan na la, kan le makɛ ki. Kum paŋa ka le ta'na en.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: En ki kum sebo la, a ki keehe la be'em, buyina za'a. Gee vom sebo ti a vɔna wa, a vɔi bo' la Yinɛ.

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Bala, yamam me biha nyɛta ya mɛhi ti, ya ki keehe be'em mɛ, gee nyaa vɔna bo' Yinɛ Kiresta Yezu puan.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Bala, da bo' ya fole, ti be'em sɔna ya ingana wa en ki la, ita ya ti ya nana ya kɔrehi la.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Da le dikɛ ya ingana la ze'sɛka bo' be'em, ti la dɛna be'em lɔkɔ tara tuna be'em tuuma. Gee tugum dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' Yinɛ, wuu nɛreba ti ba tari ya kum puan wa'am vom puan na la. Gee dikɛ ya ihe la bo' Yinɛ, ti si dɛna tuuntɔɔ vom tuuma lɔgerɔ.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em kan le sɔna ya, yan ka boi ze'elego la puan, gee bɔna zu'uhegɔ puan la.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Beni en gee? Tu tuna be'em ti tu ka dole ze'elego la, gee tu boi la zu'uhegɔ puan zuo? Sore ka boi la fii me!.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Ya ka mi' ti ya han dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' se'em ti ya wan nana en, ya dela se'em ti ya nana la da'aberɛ? La dɛna be'em en tari ya yɛ'a kum puan, bii la dɛna naŋa en tari ya yɛ'a tuuntɔɔ vom puan.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Gee pɛka dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ, ti yamam sɛba en tabelɛ dɛna be'em da'aberɛ wa, nyaa dela sɛba en nane ya suya puan ha, doohe la pa'alegɔ seko taaba en dikɛ bo' ya la.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: la yan nyɛ faarɛ be'em puan la, ya nyaa dela tuuntɔɔ vom da'aberɛ.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: N tari la asaala yɛla mageha, ya bɔkerɛ en ka zo'e la zuo. Wuu yan tabelɛ dikɛ ya ihe wa lɔgerɔ bo' dɛgerɔ tuuma se'em la, malum galena kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra puan puan se'em la. Bala, nananawa, dikɛ ya ihe la za'a tihɛ, ti si dɛna da'aberɛ bo' tuuntɔɔ vom, ti Yinɛ pee ya.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Yan daa de be'em da'aberɛ la, ya daa ka tari sɛla ita la tuntɔɔnɛ.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Bala, beni sum ti ya nyɛ yele sɛ'a en nyaa tari ya vi wa puan? Yele aŋa za'a ba'ahegɔ dela kum.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Gee nananawa ti ba nyaa faaɛ ya be'em puan bahɛ fai, gee ti ya dɛna Yinɛ da'aberɛ wa, sum sebo ti ya nyɛ dela sakerɛ. Ti de ba'ahegɔ dɛna la vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em yɔɔrɛ dela kum, gee Yinɛ zaŋa bo'olum la ti a bo' la, dela vom kan ba'ahɛ, Kiresta Yezu tu Daana la puan.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Bala zuo, nananawa, sageŋɔ ka boi sɛba en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la zuo.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Beni zuo, ze'elego seko en boi Fa'a Sia la en de vom Kiresta Yezu puan la, faaɛ ya bahɛ la fai, ze'elego seko en tari be'em la kum la nu'uhen.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ tum sɛla en daa kpe'em bo' ze'elego la ti ku ka nyaŋɛ tum, doohe la inganɛ wa en tari tɔregɔ la, a iŋɛ dina doohe la en tum a mɛŋa dayua ti ka tara inganɛ en ani wuu asaala be'em inganɛ wa, ti ka zabɛ be'em, sagum be'em paŋa, la inganɛ wa.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: ti ze'elego la en boti yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere la tum tu puan suŋa suŋa, tumam sɛba en ka vɔi wuu tu inganɛ la en boti se'em, gee Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em la.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Sɛba en vɔi ba inganɛ en boti se'em la, dikɛ ba puti'ira delum la inganɛ wa yɛla, gee sɛba en vɔi Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em la, dikɛ ba puti'ira delum la Fa'a Sia la en boti se'em.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Fum dikɛ fu puti'irɛ delum inganɛ wa la dela kum, gee fum dikɛ fum puti'irɛ delum Fa'a Sia la dela vom, la suma'ahum.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Ŋwana zuo, puti'irɛ se'ere woo en dele inganɛ wa, zaberɛ mɛ la Yinɛ. De ka sigehe de mɛŋa la Yinɛ ze'elego la. Yelemɛŋerɛ, de pun kan nyaŋɛ sigehe de mɛŋa la Yinɛ .

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: Bala zuo, sɛba en dele ba inganɛ la kan ta'am tigɛ Yinɛ nini.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Gee yamam wa la ka boi inganɛ puan. Ya boi la Fa'a Sia la puan, Yinɛ Sia la han sirum bɔna la ya. Gee se'em woo en ka tari Kiresta Sia la a puan, dagi Kiresta dinɛ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Gee Kiresta han bɔna ya puan, la ya ingana la en pun ki be'em zuo la, gee Sia la en bo'ori vom doohe tuntɔɔnɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ bo' tu la zuo.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Se'em daana Sia en vo'e Yezu kum puan la han kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ya puan, Se'em daana en vo'e Kiresta kum puan mɛa wan bo' ya vom ya ingana wa n wan ki wa puan , doohe a Sia la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la ya la.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Bala zuo, n sakereba taaba, tu tari la sanɛ, gee de dagi inganɛ wa sanɛ, ti la dɛna ti tu vɔna wuu inganɛ la en boti se'em.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Ya han vɔna inganɛ la en boti se'em la, ya wan ki. Gee ya han vɔna Fa'a Sia la puan, ku inganɛ tuuma la, ya wan vɔna.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Sɛba woo ti Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la gu'ura ba ti ba kina tuuntɔɔ la, en sirum de Yinɛ kɔma.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Ŋwana zuo la Ya ka to'e la Sia sɛka en bahɛ ti ya dɛna da'aba, le lu dabeem puan, gee ya to'e la Sia sɛka en bahɛ ti ya dɛna sɛba ti ba to'e ya bihera. Bala n bahɛ ti tu ni kaahɛ yeti Aba Tu Sɔ suŋɔ.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Fa'a Sia kana mɛa en de tu aze'nyɛdaana la tumam sia la, ti tu dela Yinɛ kɔma.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: ti tu han dɛna Yinɛ kɔma, tu dela sɛba en wan sooe sɔna, tu dela Yinɛ kɔma wan sooe Yinɛ lɔgerɔ sɔna. Tu naaɛ mɛ la Kiresta sooe sɔna Yinɛ lɔgerɔ, tu han sirum namehɛ la en, tu me wan ta lagum nyɛ suŋa la en.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: Mam mi' n suure puan suŋa suŋa ti namehegɔ seko ti tu nameha saŋa kana wa puan wa la, ti ku dagi sɛla en wan dikɛ makɛ la sunsɛka en maahum ti ka niiɛ bo' tu na wa.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Timpaberɛ wa za'a ani la kɛli, gee gura Yinɛ kɔma wa niirɛ na.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Timpaberɛ wa za'a bɔberɛ se'ere en ka tari vuurɛ puan wa, dagi la ka sumboolum, gee se'em n iŋɛ ka bala ti la zi'im wan ba'ahɛ suŋa.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: ti timpaberɛ wa za'a wan faaɛ ka mɛŋa fai yehe ka bobere wa en ye' pu'urɛ puan wa, gee nyaa to'e Yinɛ kɔma suŋa fai bɔŋa wa.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Tu mi' ti timpaberɛ wa za'a uheri mɛ viregere too wa'am na paaɛ zina.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Dagi la timpaberɛ wa ma'a, gee tumam mɛhi en tari Fa'a Sia didiina tu puan la me uheri mɛ puan ha, gee gura ti Yinɛ to'e tu bihera, gee bo' tu inganɛ wa faarɛ.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Tu beere puti'irɛ zuo tu tari faarɛ. Puti'irɛ se'ere en diŋɛ niiɛ na, le dagena puti'irɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, ani en tari putɛ'se'ere en diŋɛ niiɛ na?.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Gee tu beere puti'irɛ han dɛna la sɛla ti tu nan ka nyɛ, tu wan gura dɛ suŋa suŋa la sugeri.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: Bala mɛa ti Fa'a Sia la suŋera tɔ tu tɔregɔ puan, se'ere en sɔi la, tu ni pun ka mina tu wan suhɛ Yinɛ lan suni se'em, gee Fa'a Sia kaŋa mɛa en ni ŋmɛ'ɛra nupu'ɔ bɔ'ɔra tɔ, la si'iŋɔ kãra en gani yetɔgum.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Ti Yinɛ en biheri suure puan la, mi' sɛla en boi Fa'a Sia la puti'iren mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Fa'a Sia la sooe ŋmɛ'ɛra la nupu'ɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la, dɔla Yinɛ sumboolum.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Tu mi ti sɛla woo tuni naara taaba tara la sum bɔ'ɔra la sɛba en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' doohe a puti'iren la.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba ti a pun mina la, a pun maahum ba ze'ele mɛ, ti ba tara a dayua la naanɛ, ti a dayua la ta'am dɛna zuodaana suurɔ kekeko puan.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Sɛba ti a maahum ba ze'ele la, bamam ti a yi', ti sɛba ti a yi' la, a bo' ba la buurɔ, ti sɛba ti a bo' ba buurɔ la, bamam ti a pirɛ ba la suŋa.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Bala zuo, beni ti tu wan yele, yele ana wa puan? Yinɛ han sɔna tɔ, ani en wan ta'am zabɛ tɔ?.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Yinɛ se'ere en daa ka bahɛ a mɛŋa dayua, gee dikɛ ka tihɛ tumam za'a zuo la, a kan doohe a dayua mɛa puan bo' tu sɛla woo ti tu bɔta me yoi?.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Ani n wan ta'am tari se'ere na dɔgelɛ sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la zuo? La dela Yinɛ mɛŋa en bo' tu buurɔ.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Ani en wan ta'am sagum tɔ? La dela Kiresta Yezu en ki, ɛ̃ɛ, se'em ti Yinɛ vo'e en la, ti a bɔna Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o la. Eŋa en sirum de nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra bɔ'ɔra tumam.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Ani en wan ta'am bɔkɛ tumam Kiresta nɔŋerɛ wa puan? Wuŋerɛ bii, susageŋɔ bii, namehegɔ bii, kom bii, beele bii, nyɔ̃pɔ̃hega bii sɔ'a?.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la, Yamam zuo, ba kuuri tumam la daare woo. Ba bihɛ nyɛ tumam ti tu ana mɛ wuu piihi ti ba tara yɛ'a kɔregerɛ la.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Gee yele ana woo puan, tumam gani nyaŋereba mɛ, doohe se'em daana en nɔŋɛ tu la puan.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam sakɛ n suure puan suŋa suŋa, ti kum bii vom, bii suŋereba, bii bihereba, bii sɛla n boi zina , bii sɛla n wan wa'am beere na, bii pahi.

eng: or height or depth, or any other thing that is created.
local: bii sɛla en boi zuin zuin ha, bii sɛla en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha, bii sɛla bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a wan ta'am bɔkɛ tumam Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ wa n boi Kiresta Yezu tu Daana puan wa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Ya ka mi, n suurɔ wa, ti mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la sɛba en mi' ze'elego la. Ti ze'elego la ta' nɛra la a vom saŋa ma'a?.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Bala, pɔka en ele, ze'elego la lu' pɔka la mɛ ta' la ka sira la, en nan vɔi la. Gee ka sira la han ki, ba yeheri ka sire-ele ze'elego la puan bahera mɛ.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Lan de se'em dela, ba wan yi' pɔka kabohi daana dela, ka han naaɛ la buraa kayima, gee ti ka sira la nan vɔna. Gee ka sira la han ki, ba yeheri en ze'elego kuŋɔ puan mɛ, ti ka han ele buraa kayima, la dagi kabohi ti ka tum.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: La dela bala mɛa n zɔduma wa, yamam ki mɛ keehe ze'elego la, Kiresta inganɛ la puan, ti ya dɛna ayima dinɛ, ya dɛna se'em daana ti ba ihege en kum puan, ti tu ta'am wam wala bo' Yinɛ la.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Tumam en daa nan boi tumam en boti se'em puan la, tu be'em kɔrehi la en boi tu puan la daa ihegere mɛ, ze'elego la zuo, tuna tu inganɛ wa puan, wuna kum wala.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Gee nananawa, ba yehe tumam ze'elego la puan mɛ. Tumam ka le vɔna sɛla en yuum tari tumam ti tu dɛna yamehi la puan, ti tu da dɛna yamehi bo' diimen diimen gulehegɔ la, gee vom paalum la en de Sia puan vom la.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Tu wan yeti bu? Ti ze'elego la dela be'em bii? Sore ka boi la fii me! Gee la bala za'a, la han dagi ni la ze'elego la zuo, mam kan mini be'em. Mam kan mini ti piŋa dela sɛla, ze'elego la han ka yele yeti, Da pim.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Gee be'em la en nyɛ yɔ'ɔ nuurɛ bu'a la puan la, nyaa tari la piŋa yɛla kekeko kina n puan na. Ze'elego la han ka boni, be'em ki ni mɛ gã'a.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Saŋa ka yima, mam tabelɛ vɔna mɛ, ti ze'elego la ka boi. Gee nuurɛ bu'a la n wa'am na la, be'em vo'e mɛ na.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: ti mam ki, ti nuurɛ bu'a de mɛŋa en wan bo' ni mam vom la, nyaa lebege dɛna kum bo' mam.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em en nyɛ yɔ'ɔ nuurɛ bu'a la puan la, bu pã'ahɛ mam mɛ, doohe bilam ku mam.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Bala, ze'elego la dela fa'a ti nuurɛ bu'a la dela Fa'a dɛna dɔ'ɔ, ana suŋa.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Bala, sɛla en ani suŋa la, en tari kum na bo' mam yoi? Sore ka boi la fii me! La dela be'em en tuni, ti a ku mam, dɔla sɛla en ani suŋa la puan, ti la pa'alɛ ti be'em dela be'em, gee wan doohe nuurɛ bu'a la puan yalegɛ nuurɛ gaŋɛ bala.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Bala zuo, tu mi' ti ze'elego la dela Sia puan tuunɛ, gee mam en ŋwana dɛna asaala, ti ba koohe mam ti n dɛna da'aberɛ bo' be'em.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: N ka bɔkeri n mɛŋa itegɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, n ka iti mam boti n ita sɛla la, gee n tugum ita la yele sɛ'a ti n ka bɔta la.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Bala, mam han ita sɛla ti n ka bɔta la. Mam sakɛ ya ti ze'elego la ani suŋa mɛ.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, la dagi la mam en iti bala, gee be'em la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri n puan la en iti bala.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: Mam mi' ti yelesunɛ ka boi n puan. N yeti la n inganɛ wa puan n bala. Mam wan ti'ihɛ sɛla en ani suŋa bo' n mɛŋa, gee n kan ta'am tum dɛ.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka tuni yelesuma sɛ'a ti n bɔta la, gee tuunfi'iha sɛ'a ti n ka bɔta la, en de sɛla ti mam tuna.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Bala, mam han tuna sɛla ti n ka bɔta, la yi dagi mam en tuni, gee be'em la en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri n puan la.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Bala, mam biheri dɛ ti la nyaa dɛna la ze'elego en boi mam puan dɛna ti, n han bɔta ti n tum sum, tuunfi'iha la en ba' lɛm mam.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Mam nyɛ ti mam boti Yinɛ ze'elego la mɛ zo'e zo'e n puan puan ha.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: gee mam nyɛti la ze'elego kuyima n inganɛ wa puan en zaberɛ la ze'elego seko en boi mam puti'irɛ puan la, bahɛ ti mam dɛna da'aberɛ bo' be'em ze'elego la en boi mam iŋa wa puan la.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Bihɛ mam n de nɛra vom n tari nimbaalega se'em. Ani en wan faaɛ mam insɛka n tari kum kina wa puan bahɛ?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: Gee Yinɛ n sɔi fara doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Daana la puan. Bani zuo, mam suure puan, mam dela da'aberɛ bo' Yinɛ ze'elego la, gee n inganɛ puan, mam dela da'aberɛ bo' be'em ze'elego la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: N suurɔ, mam suure en boti sɛla, la mam pu'uhegɔ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ ba zuo dela, ba nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: Mam wan ta'am yeti, ba ba' tara ninmu'urɛ mɛ paaɛ paaɛ bo' Yinɛ, gee la ka dole bɔkerɛ.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la ba ka mi' tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na la, gee zigera ti ba tum ba mɛŋa tuntɔɔnɛ la, ba ka dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Yinɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta en de ze'elego la ba'ahegɔ, ti tuuntɔɔ vom la nyaa bɔna bo' se'em woo en nam en.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Moose gulehɛ tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en dole ze'elego malema puan na la yele yeti, Se'em en iti ze'elego malema la dela a vɔna ze'elego la yɛla la za'a.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Gee tuuntɔɔ vom sebo en ze'ele sakerɛ puan na la, a yeti, Da yele ya suya puan yeti, Ani en wan zom saazuo ha?.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Bii ani en wan sige tiŋa tiŋa ha? ?.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Gee gulehegɔ la yeti boo? Yelehum la lɛm ya mɛ, kɔ'ɔm bɔna ya nɔgbamen la ya suyan.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Beni zuo, ya han purɛ la ya nɔya, ti Yezu en de Daana, gee nam la ya suya ti Yinɛ vo'e en kum la puan mɛ, ya wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ni nam mɛ la a suure, ti Yinɛ bo' en buurɔ, gee me ni purɛ la a nuurɛ nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Gɔŋɔ la puan yeti yaa, Se'em ka boi nam Yezu, ti vi di a daana.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Yima yima ka boi Yuda la Girikiduma tiŋasuka. Daana ayima en de ba za'a Daana, gee me bɔ'ɔra ti la sɛkera se'em woo en yi' en.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Se'em woo en yi' Daana la yu'urɛ wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Gee nɛreba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani yi' se'em ti ba ka nam en? Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani nam se'em ti ba ka tabelɛ wum a yele? Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani wum, se'em han ka muulɛ bo' ba?.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Gee ba yeti ba iŋɛ la ŋwani muulɛ, han dagena ba tum ba mɛ? Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Bihɛ sɛba en tari faarɛ yelehum la kina na la nama en ani se'em suŋa suŋa.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Gee dagi nɛra woo en nam bo' faarɛ yelehum la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Azaaya yeti, Daana, ani en nam tu yelehum wa.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Bala zuo, sakerɛ ze'eti la wuŋa puan na, ti sɛla ti ba wum la me ze'eti la Kiresta yelehum la puan na.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Gee mam sokere dela, Ba ka wum? Yelemɛŋerɛ, ba wum ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti, Ba kua yehe paaɛ timpaberɛ wa zi'an woo mɛ, ti ba yelehum la paaɛ timpaberɛ wa luga za'a.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: Ti mam le soke yeti, Izirale yuum ka bɔkɛ me bii? Yia, Moose yeti, N wan bahɛ ti ya nini tɛta la sɛba en pun ka zo'e paaɛ tinmune. N wan bahɛ ti ya suure nyiie la tinyaalega.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Azaaya me yuum kãbɛ la suure yele ya yeti, Sɛba en ka eere mam la, en tugum nyɛ mam. N niiɛ n mɛŋa bo' la sɛba en ka soke n yele.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Gee a tɔgɛ Izirale yele yeti, Daarɛ woo, mam tiɛ n nu'o la bala bo' nɛreba en ka nane, sɛba en ka dole mam n boti sɛla la.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mam tɔgeri la yelemɛŋerɛ Kiresta puan, Mam ka pareni. Fa'a Sia la yele mam puti'irɛ puan ti la dela bala.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Mam tari la susageŋɔ kãtɛ, la daaŋɔ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ n suuren wa.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ yeti, nɔpɔtɛ wan pɔ ni bahɛ mam mɛŋa zuo, ti ba ŋmaaɛ ni mam Kiresta zi'an bahɛ ni, mam mɛŋa nɛreba wa zuo. Mam suurɔ inganɛ puan wa zuo.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Ba dela Izirale buuri wa. Bama ti Yinɛ to'e ba biha, pa'alɛ ba a suŋa, nɔnaarɛ, ze'elego, na'ahegɔ, la a nɔbiŋere la dɛna ba dinɛ.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bama en de Hiburuduma kansanhi yaahi, gee ti inganɛ puan, Kiresta la yehe ba puan na, se'em en gani za'a la. Sum dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: La dagi ti Yinɛ yelehum la koŋe mɛ. Dagi Izirale buuri la za'a en de Izirale buuri yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: ti la me dagena Aberaham kɔma la za'a en de a yaahi yelemɛŋerɛ. Gee la dela Aziki yaahi en wan dikɛ sigɛ hɔ.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Ŋwana pa'alɛ ti, dagi kɔma woo ti ba dɔgɛ ba inganɛ puan en de Yinɛ kɔma, gee nɔbiŋere kɔma la ti ba kãala ba yaahi.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɔbiŋere la en yele se'em en ŋwana, Saŋa kana wa puan n wan lebe na, ti Saara wan pɛga bia en de budibela.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Gee dagi za'a en bala. Sɛla en ani wuu ŋwana wa me yuum iŋɛ Arebɛka mɛ. Eŋa en yuum tari puurɛ la a sira Aziki en de tu sɔyaaba la.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Gee ti ba baŋɛ dɔgɛ kɔma la, bii kɔma la baŋɛ tum sɛla en de be'em bii sum la, La wan iŋɛ se'em ti se'ere zuo ti Yinɛ lɔɔha a loohego wa wan kelum kina.

eng: It wasnt because of what was done but because of Gods call. This was said to her: The older child will be a slave to the younger one .
local: ti la dagena la nɛra tuuma zuo, gee Yinɛ yi'a la, ti ba yuum yele en yeti, Kiima la wan tugum dɛna bɛ'a la yameŋa.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Mam nɔŋɛ la Yakɔbe, Gee yaaɛ Esɔ la.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Beni ti tu wan yele? Ŋmɛlema en boi Yinɛ puan bii? Sore kai' boi fii me.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yele Moose yeti, N wan zɔ nimbu'ɔ bo' la se'em ti mam bɔta ti n zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, gee tara giile bo' se'em ti mam bɔta ti n tara a giile.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Bala, la dagi la asaala suboolum, bii a tiregerɛ, gee la dela Yinɛ mɛŋa se'em n tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Gulehegɔ la gulehɛ Faro yele yeti, Mam yɛɛlɛ fum na'am la yele dina wa zuo, ti n pa'alɛ n paŋa fum puan, ti muulɛ n yu'urɛ wa timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Bala zuo, Yinɛ tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra la se'em woo ti a looe, gee me kãbera se'em woo suure ti a bɔta.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Ya wan yele mam yeti, Ŋwani ti Yinɛ sina gee bihera saaleba tuure? Ani en wan ta'am zagehɛ Yinɛ suboolum.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: Gee ani en de fum, asaala, wan ta'am ŋmɛ' nɔkpe'ene la Yinɛ? Yagumbaherɛ wan ta'am soke yagumbaha la yeti, Ŋwani ti fum yagum mam ŋwana.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Yagumbaha la ka tari yɔgerɔ la paŋa, wan ta'am dikɛ yɔgerɔ tunɔ mɛa yagum lɔkɔ kuyima, ti kuŋɔ dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ, gee yagum kuyima ti kuŋɔ dɛna tuunse'ere yoo lɔkɔ?.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Ŋwani, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa han bɔta ti a pa'alɛ a sunsunyiim, ti a paŋa niiɛ, gee me bɔta gee ŋmibera la sugeri, la lɔgeseto ti a suure nyiie la tɔ, lɔge seto en maalɛ bo' sageŋɔ la.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Gee ŋwani, a han le iŋɛ bala ti a pa'alɛ a suŋa bo' sɛba en de a zɔ ba nimbu'ɔ la, sɛba ti a pun maahum biŋe a ti a pa'alɛ ba a suŋa kãtɛ la.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: ta' la tumam sɛba ti a yi tu, dagi la Yuu buuri puan ma'a, gee sɛba en dagi Yuu buuri la me la?.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: Wuu lan yele Hozeya gɔŋɔ la puan se'em yeti, Sɛba en daa dagi mam nɛreba la, n wan yi' ba n nɛreba. Eŋa se'em ti ba yuum ka nɔŋɛ la, n wan yi' en ti n nɔŋerɛ bunɔ. .

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Zɛ'se'ehi ti ba daa yi' ya ti, Ya dagi mam nɛreba la. Bilam mɛa ti ba wan yi'ira ya Yinɛ vua la kɔma.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Azaaya yuum kaahɛ Izirale yele yeti, La Izirale buuri la kɔma en zo'e wuu kulega tintɔnɔ la, ba fii wa ma'a en wan nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Daana la wan tum a gakerɛ tuunɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo la ninmu'urɛ, gee ba'ahɛ de ŋwana kalam, iŋɛ de ŋwana girega girega.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: Ŋwana ti Azaaya tɔgɛ biŋe yeti, Pɛntiireba nɛŋa Daana wa han ka bahɛ ni tu buuri wa fii wa bo' tu, Tumam me wan piiɛ ni wuu Sodom la. Tu wan ani ni wuu Gomora la.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Beni ti tu nyaa wan yele? Sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la en daa ka zigeri tuuntɔɔ vom wa yele la, nyɛ tuuntɔɔ vom la mɛ. Tuntɔɔ vom sebo en dole sakerɛ puan la.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Gee Izirale duma, sɛba en daa zigeri ti ba nyɛ tuntɔɔnɛ sebo en dole ze'elego la, daa koŋe mɛ, ba ka nyaŋɛ doohe ze'elego la kiha la.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Ŋwani ti ba ka nyaŋɛ? Ba daa ka zigeri ɛɛra dɛ sakerɛ puan, gee ba daa eere de mɛ wuu la dela tuuma puan la. Ba ŋmɛ' ba nama mɛ la kuge se'ere en piti la.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: wuu lan gulehɛ yele se'em la. Bihɛ ya, n biŋere la kugere Zaayɔn, en wan pita nɛreba, Taŋa en wan bahɛ ti nɛreba luta, Gee fum se'em woo en nam en, nyanɛ kan di fum.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: Mam soke nyaa, Yinɛ zagehɛ a nɛreba la mɛ n bala bii? Sore ka boi la fii me. Mam mɛŋa dela Izirale buuri, Aberaham yaaŋa, yehe Benyamin yizuo puan na.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Yinɛ ka zagehɛ a nɛreba sɛba ti a pun mina ba na la. Ya ka mi' Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la en tɔgɛ Elia yele se'em, en yuum ŋmɛ'ɛri nupu'ɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan Izirale yele se'em la?.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Daana, ba ku fum nɔtɔgeheba la la za'a, ba gbirege kaabegɔ zi'ihi la mɛ, ti mam ma'a en gee, ti ba ɛɛra mam me ti ba ku.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Gee Yinɛ lebehego yuum dela kuni bo' en? Mina ti mam tari nɔtɔgeheba tuha ayopɔi en ka makɛ na'ahɛ Baal bagerɛ la.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Bala mɛa, saŋa kana wa puan, fii wa boi ti Yinɛ looe ba a zu'uhegɔ la zuo.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: Gee la yi han dɛna la zu'uhegɔ zuo, la le dagena tuuma, han dagena bala, zu'uhegɔ yi dagi zu'uhegɔ.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Beni en nyaa boi? Izirale koŋe ba to'e sɛla ti ba ɛɛra la mɛ. Sɛba ti Yinɛ pun looe la ma'a en nyɛ dɛ, gee bana la woo kãbɛ la ba suya.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Yinɛ bo' ba la sia sɛka en ka bɔkeri, nini en ka nyɛti la tuba en ka wuni wa'am na paaɛ zina beere wa.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Naba Deviti yeti, Bahɛ ti ba dia zi'ihi ta lebege bɛregɔ nyɔgɛ ba, gee dɛna pirega pita ba, gee dɛna namehegɔ, nameha ba.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Bahɛ ti ba nini pãarum, ti ba da nyɛta, bahɛ ti ba uma da malum ihegera.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: Ti mam soke yeti, bala ba gɛ̃rɛ mɛ ta lu? Sore ka boi la fii me. Gee ba gɛrega la bahɛ ti faarɛ la paaɛ la sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, ti la bo' Iziraleduma la nintɛrega.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Bala, ba gɛrega la han bahɛ ti tarega paaɛ tiŋa wa zuo, ti ba tɔregɔ la bahɛ tarega paaɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, gee ba nyaa han lebehe ze'ele suŋa suŋa la nyaa wan ana la ŋwani?.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Nananawa mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa, se'ere en sɔi la, mam de Yinɛ tuntuna bo' yamam. Mam na'aheri n tuunɛ la mɛ.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: ti daanse'ere n bahɛ ti nyuure kɛ̃ mam mɛŋa nɛreba la, ti n ta'am faaɛ ba basɛba bahɛ.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Bama en zagehɛ Yinɛ la han bahɛ ti naarɛ wa'am tiŋa wa zuo, la Yinɛ tiŋa suka na, gee ba Yinɛ to'ore nyaa wan ana la ŋwani, gana kum vo'ore.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Zom sebo en dikɛ kaabera yia, dɛna yia wala bo'olum la, han dɛna tuulum bunɔ, zom la za'a yi dela tuulum bunɔ. Nyɛga la han dɛna tuulum bunɔ, yila la me dela tuulum bunɔ.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Gee yila la a sɛ'a daa han kɔ' mɛ, ti ba dikɛ yamam en de olive tiyɛɛhɛ tubera na wa wa'am na toŋe yila la zi'ihi la, ti ya lagum nyuura olive nyɛga la ko'sumɔ la.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: ya da ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ la yila sɛ'a en kɔ' la. Ya han ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ, ya tɛra ti, dagi yamam en suŋeri nyɛgerɛ la, gee tile la en tugum suŋera yamam.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Ya ta'am yeti, Yila en aahum, ti ba ta'am dikɛ fum toŋe.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Bala dela asira. Gee yila la kɔ' doohe la ba bumbu'um zuo, gee yamam ze' doohe la sakerɛ zuo. Bala, da ŋmɛ'ɛra ya nyu'urɔ gee tum ze' ya la dabeem.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ daa han ka bahɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ yila la, daanse'ere a kan bahɛ bo' yamam me.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: Bala, mini ya Yinɛ itesuŋɔ, la a kpe'eŋo seko ti a tara la. A nini mu'ɛ mɛ la sɛba en lu la, gee tara Yinɛ itesuŋɔ dɔla yamam, wuu ya han bɔna a sum la puan, bii a ŋmaaɛ yamam me bahɛ.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Izirale nɛreba la han ka kelum bɔna ba bumbu'um la puan, Yinɛ wan dikɛ ba naaɛ le yɛ'ɛha. Se'ere en sɔi la,Yinɛ tari paŋa ti a le dikɛ ba naaɛ le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa han ŋmaaɛ yamam olive tiyɛa puan na naaɛ la timɛnka puan, ŋwani ti bana en tugum dɛna timɛnka yila wa kan le ta'am dikɛ naaɛ ba mɛŋa olive mɛnka yila la puan yɛ'ɛha.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: Ŋwana ti ya kan ta'am yɛta ti, yamam ba' mina mɛ, n suurɔ. N boti ya bɔkɛ yele se'ere en sugɛ wa mɛ, ti Sukãberɛ en lu Izirale buuri la basɛba zuo, gura ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la kãlɛ en de se'em wan nyɛ faarɛ la ta kɛ̃ na ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Bilam ti Izirale za'a nyaa wan nyɛ faarɛ wuu lan gulehɛ yele se'em ti, Zaayɔn puan, ti Faara la wan yehe na, A wan sagum tuunfi'iha za'a Yakɔbe buuri la puan.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Mam nɔnaarɛ en ŋwana ti en naaɛ la ba wa, en han ta saalum ba be'em la za'a bahɛ.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Han dɛna la yelehum la, ba dela Yinɛ dinduma yamam zuo. Gee han dɛna loohego, bamam ti Yinɛ nɔŋɛ, ba yaabeduma zuo.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo'olum, la a yi'a ka lukere.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Wuu yamam en daa nan ka nane Yinɛ, gee nananawa nyaa nyɛ Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bamam ka nane la zuo la.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: bala mɛa ti bamam me ka nana, ti la bahɛ ti Yinɛ en zɔ yamam nimbu'ɔ se'em la, bamam me to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ dinɛ mɛa.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ pagɛ ba za'a la akanane puan, ti a ta'am zɔ ba za'a nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Bihɛ,Yinɛ tarega, la a yɛm la a mi'ilum pirɛ paaɛ mɛ ka tara tɛka. A gakerɛ gaŋɛ bɔkerɛ mɛ, ti a sɔya la ba' dɛna nyalema gaŋɛ bɔkerɛ!.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Ani en tabelɛ mina Daana la puti'irɛ? Bii ani en de a ka'ana.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Bii ani en tabelɛ bo' Yinɛ bo'olum, Bii tabelɛ bo' en sɛla, ti a to'e gee lebehe en.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, la eŋa zi'an ti sɛla woo zɛ'ɛta na, gee dɛna a dinɛ. Eŋa ti na'ahegɔ dɛna a dina saŋa woo. Taari.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: Bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo sigehe a mɛŋa bo' timbihega kpɛ'ɛnduma la, se'ere en sɔi la kpe'eŋo ka boi han dagena Yinɛ. Kpe'eŋo seko woo en boi wa, Yinɛ en sɛ ba ze'ele.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Bala zuo, fum se'em en zaberɛ kpe'emseko, zaberɛ la Yinɛ en looe sɛla, sɛba en zaberɛ la kpe'eŋo seko wan nyɛ la gakerɛ.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛnduma dagi dabeem lɔkɔ bo' sum, gee be'em. Fum ka boti fum tara dabeem la kpɛ'ɛnduma? Yi ita sɛla en de ma'a, gee ba wan pɛgɛ hɔ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a dela Yinɛ yameŋa fum sum zuo. Gee fum han ita sɛla en dagi ma'a, fum dela fum zɔta dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛma la ka tari a paŋa daalehum la la yoo. La dela Yinɛ yameŋa la tuunɛ ti a namehɛ se'em en tuni tuunyooro.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Bala zuo, nɛra dela a sigehe a mɛŋa, dagi la ba wan namehɛ fum la ma'a, gee fum suure la en gakeri fum la me.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Bala mɛa zuo ti fum me yɔɔra kuba la. Se'ere en sɔi la, kpɛ'ɛnduma la dela Yinɛ yamehi, mu'ɛ nini la tuun-ana wa.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Yɔ ya sɛla woo en de ba dinɛ bo' ba.Yɔ ya kuba bo' se'em en de a to'e kuba, gee yɔ ya lɔgeseto woo en de ya yɔ tu kuba la, bo' se'em en to'ohe kuba aŋa la, nane ya sɛba en nari ba nana, ti na'ahegɔ bɔ'ɔra se'em en de ya na'aha.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Da tara se'em sanɛ, han dagena ya nɔŋɛ taaba ma'a. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em en nɔŋɛ a tadaana, tum ze'elego la en boti se'em woo ba'ahɛ mɛ.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ze'elego seko en yeti, Da tum kabohi, da ku nɛra, da zu, da pim; la ze'elego seto woo en boi la za'a, kilehe bɔna la yele dina wa puan, Nɔŋɛ fum mabia wuu fum nɔŋɛ fum mɛŋa se'em la.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Nɔŋerɛ ka tuni mabia be'em. Bala, se'em en tari nɔŋerɛ, doohe la ze'elego la za'a.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: Dina pooren, ya mi' sansɛka puan ti tu bɔna nananawa, la nyaa paaɛ sansɛka en de ya niiɛ ya guum la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, faarɛ nyaa lɛm tu nananawa gana tumam n daa nan nam Yezu la.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Yu'uŋɔ la pun tole mɛ, ti beyiine pɔra. Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu biŋe lika puan yɛla kinkɛleŋa, gee dikɛ niihum zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la yɛ.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Bahɛ ya ti tu vɔni ya na'ahegɔ vom, wuu tumam n de niihum kɔma la, gee dagi tu dɛna sɛba en dole dita dikãreyooro la de'em yooro, bugera, tuna dɛgerɔ tuuma, la yetɔgeyooro, dagi zaba la nintɛrega yɛla.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Gee tugum dikɛ ya Daana Yezu Kiresta yɛ wuu fuo la gee da bo' ya inganɛ wa sore ti de dikɛ tu doohe tu kunkoma'ahum la yɛla la.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: To'ohe ya sɛba en ka kpe'em ba sakerɛ puan la, gee dagi ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkɛ'ɛma la ba, yɛla zuo.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Sɛba nam la ba dita ban paaɛ sɛla woo, ti sɛba me en ka kpe'em la, dita vuurɔ ma'a.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Sɛba en diti sɛla woo la, da biha sɛba en ka diti bala la yoo, ti sɛba en ka diti la me da gakera sɛba en diti sɛla woo la, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ to'ore ba woo mɛ.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Fum dela ani gakera ayima yamehi? Ba mɛŋa Daana la nɛŋan ti ba wan ba zɛ'a bii ba lu. Ba me wan zɛ'a, se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la wan bahɛ ti ba zɛ'a.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Sɛba bihɛ la dabeherɛ deyima ti de dɛna tuulum dabeherɛ gana dabeherɛ deyima, ti sɛba me biha dabeha la za'a ti a dɛna buyina. Bahɛ ya ti ba woo tari ba suya puan ban ti'ihɛ se'em la.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Sɛba en looe dabeherɛ se'ere la, iti bala bɔ'ɔra la Daana la, ti sɛba me en diti sɛla woo la me diti na'aha la Daana la, doohe la ba ni bo' Daana la fara gee di la. Ti sɛba me en ka diti sɛla woo la me lɔkeri na'aha la Daana la, la ban ni bo' Yinɛ fara de zuo la.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Tu ka vɔi bo' la tu mɛhi, tu ka kiiri tu mɛhi kum.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Tu han vɔna, tu vɔi bo' la Daana la, tu han le ki mɛ, tu dela Daana la dinɛ. Bala, tu vɔi mɛ, bii tu ki mɛ, tu dela Daana la dinɛ.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti Kiresta ki, gee le vo'e, ti a dɛna Daana bo' ki'induma la vɔpa.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Ŋwani ti fum gakera fum suuɔ? Bii ŋwani ti fum zɛɛna fum suuɔ? Tu za'a wan ze'ele Yinɛ gakerɛ kuka la nɛŋan mɛ.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Mam en vɔi wa, Daana la n yeti, dune woo wan kpa' tiŋa bo' mam, ti zɛleŋa woo wan dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Bala zuo, tu za'a wan ta yele tu yele tu yele bo' Yinɛ .

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ya ti tu gakera taaba, gee tu ku puti'irɛ ti tu kan dikɛ sɛla beŋe, bii gu' se'em sore.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Mam mi', gee ti Daana Yezu me bahɛ ti n baŋɛ ti, sɛla ka boi dɛna galeŋɔ lɔkɔ ku mɛŋa, gee ku de galeŋɔ lɔkɔ bɔ'ɔra la se'em daana en ti'ihɛ ti ku dela galeŋɔ lɔkɔ la.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Fum suuɔ han nyɛ pɔgeleŋɔ doohe fum en diti sɛla puan, fum ka le kina la nɔŋerɛ. Da bahɛ ti fum diti sɛla sagum se'em ti Kiresta ki bo' en la.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ti sɛla en ani suŋa bo' fum la, ba ta tɔgera bu be'em sore.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan dagi la dia la nyua, gee tuuntɔɔ vom, suma'ahum la nyɛɛma Fa'a Sia la puan.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Se'em woo en tuni dɔla sadɔɔ kuna bɔ'ɔra Kiresta, Yinɛ nini tigɛ la a daana mɛ, ti saaleba me to'e en .

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu dole ya la ninmu'urɛ yele sɛ'a en bo'ori tu suma'ahum, gee mɛta tɔ.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti dia zuo, sagum Yinɛ tuuma. Sɛla woo sirum dela tuulum, gee la me dela tuure bo' ya ti ya bahɛ ti nɛra lu doohe yan diti sɛla la.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: La ani suŋa ti tu da di dia, bii nyu daam, bii iŋɛ sɛla en wan bahɛ ti fum suuɔ lu.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Sakerɛ se'ere ti fum tara la, tara dɛ fum ma'a, fum la Yinɛ tiŋasuka. Sum boi mɛ la sɛba en ka nyɛ se'ere zuo ti ba wan gakɛ ba mɛhi doohe bamam en bihɛ ti sɛla dagi be'em.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Gee sɛba en bu'uri la, sageni la ba mɛhi, ba han di diihe sise'ehi, beni zuo, ba ka tuni la sakerɛ. Sɛla woo en ka ze'ele sakerɛ puan na, dela be'em.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Bala zuo, n yeti ya mɛ n suurɔ, ti Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la zuo, ya dikɛ ya ihi la bo' wuu kaabɛ vua la, dɛna tuulum kaabegɔ, gee tigɛ Yinɛ nini, dina en de ya sia puan na'ahegɔ.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Da dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, gee tugum malum sɛ ya mɛhi, ya puti'ira teere puan, la ya viihɛ baŋɛ sɛla en de Yinɛ sumboolum, la sɛla en ani suŋa, nara gee ba'ahɛ za'a.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yinɛ zu'uhɛ mam la zuo, mam yeti se'em woo en boi ya puan yeti, se'em da biha nyɛta a mɛŋa gana en sunti a ti'iha a mɛŋa yele se'em la, gee a sigeha a mɛŋa a ti'ihegɔ puan, ti nɛra woo bɔna wuu Yinɛ en makɛ a sakerɛ se'em bo' en la.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Wuu iŋa en tara luga yima yima se'em, ti luga la za'a me ka lagum tuna tuunɛ deyina se'em la.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: bala mɛa ti tumam en zo'e se'em wa, dɛna iŋa kayima Kiresta puan. Gee la tu yima yima mɛa, tu dela iŋa kayima lɔgerɔ la taaba.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Tu tari la zu'uhegɔ bo'olum en boi yima yima, doohe la ban bo' tu, tu zu'uhegɔ se'em la. Purega puan, tu dela tu puta ti la zuna la tu sakerɛ la.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: tuntuna dela a tuna, ti pa'ala me dɛna a pa'ala.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Kpemehego, daana me dela a kpɛmeha, ti se'em en bo'ori, me bɔ'ɔra yabi yabi, Nɛŋadaana mu'ɛ nini, ti nimbɔ'zɔta me tuna la pupeelum.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Tari ya yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ. Yaaɛ ya sɛla en de be'em, gee gurɛ sɛla en de sum kankaŋi.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Nɔŋɛ ya taaba la giile. Pageri ya taaba la na'ahegɔ tuuma.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti ya inkirega kɔ'ɔhɛ. Kpe'em ya mɛ sia puan, bɔta ti ya tuna bɔ'ɔra Daana la.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Nyɛɛni ya mɛ la beere puti'irɛ, tara ya sugeri ya namehegɔ puan, gee me ŋmibera ya pu'uhegɔ puan.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Lageheri ya suŋera Yinɛ nɛresunduma la, gee tiɛ ya nu'uhi la itesuŋɔ paara saama.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Ka'ari ya sum bɔ'ɔra sɛba en nameheri ya la, ka' sum, gee da pɔti ya bɔ'ɔra ba.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Nyɛɛni ya la sɛba en nyɛɛni, gee kɛla la sɛba en kele.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Boni ya la suma'ahum la taaba, da pa'ala ya mɛhi, gee boni ya la naheduma, da ti'iha ti ya wikɛ gana yan de sɛba.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Da yɔ ya be'em bo' se'em en eŋa ya be'em, gee ni ti'ihɛ ya sɛla en ani suŋa nɛra woo ninin.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Gee la han wan ta'am iŋɛ, lan de yamam la, boni ya la suma'ahum la nɛra woo.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: N suurɔ, da makɛ ya lerege nɛra ya mɛhi, gee bo' ya Yinɛ sunsunyiim la sore ti bu tum. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Leregere dela mam dinɛ, Mam en wan lebehe yɔ. Daana la en yele.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Aayi, ya dinduma kom han duna, diihe ya ba, ko'nyuuro han tara ba, bo' ya ba sɛla ti ba nyu. Ya han ita ŋwana, ya ka'ari la vi bugum pagela ba zuto.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti be'em nyaŋɛ ya, gee tugum nyaŋɛ ya be'em, la ya sum la.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: Mam n wa'am ya zi'an na la n suurɔ, mam ka wa'am na muula Yinɛ sugelum yelehum la la yɛm kirega sɛka bii nɔmaalum puan bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Beni zuo, mam daa iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n ka boti n baŋɛ se'ere ya puan, han dagena Yezu Kiresta, la ban en kpa' en la ma'a.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: N wa'am ya zi'an mɛ na la tɔregɔ, la dabeem la gbi'a zo'e zo'e.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: Mam tugum, la n muulegɔ la daa dagi pã'ahegɔ yetɔgum, gee Sia la tuuma, la paŋa.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: ti la bahɛ ti ya sakerɛ la da yaga asaala yɛm zuo, gee Yinɛ paŋa puan.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Gee la bala za'a, sɛba en bi'ɛ sakerɛ puan la, ti tari yɛm tɔgera mɛ, gee dagi zina beere yɛm wa puan, bii pahi se'ehi en sɔi zina beere wa biha, gã'a gura sageŋɔ paaɛ si wa.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Gee tumam tɔgeri la Yinɛ yɛm en sugɛ, yele sɛ'a ti nɛreba ka mina, ti Yinɛ yuum pun tɔgɛ a yele ti tiŋa wa nam pun ka naam, tumam tibehum zuo.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Se'em ka boi sankana na'duma wa puan bɔkɛ yele ana wa; Ba han bɔkɛ ni mɛ, ba kan kpa' ni Tibehum Za'a Daana la.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Gee wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Sɛla ti niho ka tabelɛ nyɛ, bii tuberɛ wum, bii asaala suure tabelɛ bɔkɛ dɛ, Sɛla ti Yinɛ maahum biŋe sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la —.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Yele ana ti Yinɛ niiɛ pa'alɛ tumam doohe Sia la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Sia la mi' sɛla woo mɛ, pun ta ta' yele sɛ'a en sugɛ Yinɛ zi'an.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Asaala ini en wan ta'am mina sɛla en sirum de asaala, han dagena asaala sia la en boi a puan la? Bala mɛa ti se'em ka bɔna wan bɔkɛ sɛla en sirum de Yinɛ yele, han dagena Yinɛ Sia la.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
