local: Nananawa, han dɛna tumam, tumam ka to'e la tiŋa wa zuo sia la, gee Sɛsɛka en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, ti tu ta'am bɔkera bo'olum sebo ti Yinɛ ka'ɛ bahɛ tu zuo la.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Ti tumam tɔgera yele ana wa mɛ dagi la asaala pa'alegɔ yɛm puan, gee Sia la pa'alegɔ. Lɛregera Sia puan yɛla pa'ala sɛba en tari Sia la.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Sɛba ti Sia la ka bɔna la ba la, ka to'ore Yinɛ Sia bo'olum la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka tari vuurɛ bo' ba, ti ba me kan ta'am bɔkɛ ba, se'ere en sɔi la, ba dela Sia puan yɛla.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Sɛba ti Sia la bɔna la ba la bɔkeri sɛla woo mɛ, ba ka boi nɛra nu'uhi ti a bihera ba.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Ani en mi' Daana la puti'irɛ, wan ta'am pa'alɛ en? Gee tumam tari la Kiresta puti'irɛ.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Bala, yeen dikɛ ya tu ŋwana, ti tu dela Kiresta yamehi, tu dela sɛba en biheri Yinɛ sugelum yɛla la.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Se'ere en gani bala dela, la dele sɛba en biheri Yinɛ lɔgerɔ la ti ba dɛna sɛba ti nɛreba wan nam ba.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Gee han dɛna mam, la dela yelepika bo' mam, ti yamam gakɛ mam, bii saaleba tugum zi'an gakɛ mam. Mam mɛŋa pun ka gakera n mɛŋa.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: Mam ka nyɛ se'ere ti mam tuue n mɛŋa, gee la bala za'a, mam ka piiɛ. La dela Daana la ma'a en gakeri mam.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Bala, da gakɛ ya gee ti saŋa la nyaa paaɛ, gee ti Kiresta nyaa wa'am na, eŋa se'em daana en wan bahɛ ti sɛlawoo en sugɛ lika puan nananawa niiɛ peelumen, gee lilege suure puan yɛla la. Bilam ti nɛrawoo nyaa wan to'e a pɛka Yinɛ zi'an.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Mam bahɛ ti Apolos la mam mɛŋa dɔla yele ana mɛ yamam sum zuo, n suurɔ wa, ti ya zamehɛ tumam puan, la ya se'em da vɔna zɔɛ sɛla en gulehɛ la. Ŋwana ti ya se'em kan ukera a mɛŋa la eŋa en boti se'em gana se'em la zuo.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Beni zuo, ani en nyɛ sɛla en boi la tɔka fum puan? Beni ti fum tara gee ka to'e de Yinɛ zi'an? Fum han to'e la Yinɛ zi'an, ŋwani ti fum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ wuu la dagi la bo'olum ti ba dikɛ bo' fum?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Bala! Yamam pun tara sɛla woo ti ya bɔta la mɛ! Yelemɛŋerɛ ya pun dɛna la buntateba? Ya pun lebege la naduma ti tumam ka te'ele ya? Mam boti ni ti ya sirum lebege ni la naduma, ti tumam me deni naduma la ya.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ ti Yinɛ ka kãalɛ tumam tuntuneba wa pa'ahɛ nɛreba puan, beni zuo, tu ani wuu ba maahum tu yɛ'a la kua la, beni zuo, ba dikɛ tu diilɛ mɛ bo' timpaberɛ wa, la suŋereba, la saaleba.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Bala! Tumam nyaa dela geto Kiresta zuo, gee ti yamam dɛna yɛm duma Kiresta puan. Tumam tari la tɔregɔ, gee yamam dɛna paheduma. Yamam dela zuto duma, gee tumam dɛna fagehum duma.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Wa'am na paaɛ tumam en kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a wa, kom la ko'nyuurɔ en kuuri tɔ, tu yɛ la lɔge-ãahema, ti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra tɔ, tu ka tari yire wan kɛ̃.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: tu taregerɛ mɛ la tu mɛhi nu'uhi tuuma. Ba han ni to' tɔ, tu ni ka' la sum bo' ba duma. Ba han mɛlehɛ tɔ, tu ni ŋmibe mɛ.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: ba han ni zɛregɛ tɔ, tu ni lebehe mɛ la kɔsuŋa. Tumam ani wuu tu dela tiŋa wa zuo sɔgerɔ la, dɛna sɛla woo bunkɛhela wa'am na paaɛ zina.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: Mam ka gulehɛ ŋwana ti n bahɛ ti vi en nyɔkɛ ya, gee n ka'am ya, wuu yan de n sunɔŋerɛ kɔma la.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Ya ta'am tara bihereba tuha pia Kiresta puan, gee ya ka tari sɔduma zo'e. Yelemɛŋerɛ, Kiresta Yezu puan, ti mam lebege ya sɔ, faarɛ yelehum wa zuo.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Ŋwana zuo, mam beleni ya mɛ, ti ya tɔgeha mam.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Dina wa zuo ti n tum Timoti, se'em en de mam sunɔŋerɛ la n sakerɛ dayua Daana la puan, ti a tiihɛ ya mam n dole Kiresta Yezu yɛla la se'em, wuu mam en pa'ali a se'em zi'an woo, la Lageŋɔ woo puan se'em la.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Gee ya sɛba en ti'iheri ti mam kan le wa'am ya zi'an na la, lebege la nɛrewureba.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Gee n wan wa'am ya zi'an nananawa na, la han dɛna Daana la suboolum, la n baŋɛ, dagi la nɛrewureba wa yetɔgum la ma'a, gee ba paŋa la me.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la dagi la tugum ma'a, gee paŋa.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Beni ti ya wan bɔta? N wa'am ya zi'an na la kpa'aherɛ bii, nɔŋerɛ la sigehe mɛŋa?.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Tumam sɛba n kpe'em tu sakerɛ puan la dela tu ŋmibera la sɛba en tari tɔregɔ ba sakerɛ puan la, gee dagi tu kɔ'ɔm biha tu mɛhi nini tika ma'a.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Tu ayima ayima dela tu tigera tu suurɔ nini, tu putɛ'sunɛ en de tu mɛ tu mabia la zuo.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta daa ka biheri la a mɛŋa nini tika ma'a yele; Gee wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Tuurɛ sɛ'a taaba ti sɛba tuura ya la, sigera la mam zuo.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Sɛla woo ti ba gulehɛ diimen diimen ha la, gulehɛ ti la pa'alɛ la tumam, ti sɛ' ze'elego, la kpemehego doohe gulehegɔ la puan, tu wan tara beere puti'irɛ.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori sɛ ze'ele kankaŋi la kpemehego la, iŋɛ ti ya bɔna la suyinɛ la taaba, wuu Kiresta Yezu en boti se'em la.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: bala ti ya wan naaɛ taaba la putɛ'yinɛ la kɔyiŋa, pɛgera Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Bala zuo, to'oheri ya taaba, wuu Kiresta en to'e ya se'em la, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bo' Yinɛ.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: N yeti ya mɛ wa, ti Kiresta lebege la yameŋa bo' ya sɛba en ŋma la, Yinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la zuo, ti a bahɛ ti Yinɛ en biŋe nɔse'ere bo' yaabeduma la tum.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: gee me bahɛ ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la me pɛgɛ Yinɛ a zu'uhegɔ la zuo. Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Bala zuo, n wan purɛ fum yu'urɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la tiŋasuka, gee yuum pɛka yuuma pɛgɛ fum yu'urɛ.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Gee le yele yeti, Nyɛɛni ya mɛ la a nɛreba, yamam sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Gee le yele yeti, Pɛgɛ ya Daana wa, yamam sɛba woo en dagi Yuda buuri wa, gee bahɛ ti nɛra woo pɛgera en.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Azaaya yele pa'ahɛ yeti, Yesi puan, nyɛgerɛ kini na, Se'em en wan ihege na bihera sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la. Eŋa puan ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la wan tara beere puti'irɛ.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Nananawa,Yinɛ se'ere en sɔi beere puti'irɛ la, pirɛ ya la nyɛɛma woo la suma'ahum yan nam la zuo, ya pirɛ la beere puti'irɛ ti bu yabera doohe Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: Mam mɛŋa nam ya mɛ n suurɔ, ti ya mɛhi pirɛ mɛ la itesuŋɔ, pirɛ la mi'ilum za'a, wan ta'am ka'am taaba.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Gee la bala za'a, yeledaahi a sɛ'a ti n gulehɛ la sukãberɛ bo' ya na la dela, n tiihɛ ya, doohe zu'uhegɔ la ti Yinɛ bo' mam.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: ti n dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna bo' sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, malema daana tuuma Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum wa puan, ti sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri wa la, Yinɛ to'e ba kaabegɔ, ti Fa'a Sia la pee ba.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta Yezu puan, mam tari se'ere zuo ti n pagera la mam tuni bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ wa mɛ.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam kan makɛ ti n tɔgɛ sɛla, han dagena Kiresta en ba'ahɛ tuunsɛ'a doohe mam puan, nyaŋɛ sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri ti ba nam Yinɛ, doohe mam tugum bii tuunse'ere woo puan.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: doohe paŋa kirehi, la nyalema, en tum doohe Yinɛ Sia paŋa la puan, pɔhɛ Yerusalɛm ta paaɛ Ilirikum, mam tɔgɛ Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la za'a za'a.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Ŋwana en bahɛ ti mam lu' n suure ti, n kan muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la zi'an ti ba tabelɛ purɛ Kiresta bini, la n da ta mɛ dɔgelɛ ayima ɛberɛ zuo.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: gee woo lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Sɛba ti ba ka tabelɛ yele ba Kiresta yele la, wan nyɛ en, ti sɛba en ka tabelɛ wum a yele la wan bɔkɛ en.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Dina zuo ti saŋa zo'e zo'e mam ka nyɛta fole wan wa'am ya zi'an na la.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Gee nananawa, mam n ka le tara zi'an tinsina wa woo puan ti n kiŋɛ la, n suure nyaa kɔ'ɔm bɔta mɛ wuu yuuma zo'e zo'e ti en bɔta se'em la, ti n wa'am ya zi'an na.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: n han ta paaɛ Sipeen tiŋa, n boti n nyɛ ya mɛ n sore kinɛ la puan, ti ya te'ele mam ti n tole, n han wa'am na di pupeelum la ya dabeha fii wa.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Nananawa, se'ere en boi bini dela, n ye' la Yerusalɛm ti en tari suŋɛ ta bo' nɛresunduma la en boi bini la.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Masedoniya, la Akaiya tihi sakereba la puurɛ pee mɛ ti ba tɔrɛ ba tarega la sɛba en de naheduma nɛresunduma la puan la Yerusalɛm puan la.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Ba puurɛ pee mɛ ti ba tum dina wa. Yelemɛŋerɛ bamam mɛhi la tari la ba sanɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yuuduma la han tɔrɛ ba sia puan maalegɔ bo' sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri la, bamam me dela ba tum inganɛ puan lɔgerɔ maalegɔ tuunɛ me bo' ba.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Bala, n han ta paaɛ tum tuun-dina ba'ahɛ, dikɛ ban bo' ba sɛla la bo' ba ba'ahɛ, n wan doohe ya bɔba na, tole Sipeen tiŋa.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: N me mi' ti n han ta yɛ'a na, n ye' mɛ na, pirɛ la Kiresta maalegɔ la puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan, la Sia la nɔŋerɛ puan, ti ya naaɛ la mam la ninmu'urɛ pu'uhegɔ bo' Yinɛ n zuo.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: ti n piiɛ sɛba en ka nam Yudiya puan la nu'uhen, gee ti n tuunɛ Yerusalɛm puan la me, nɛresunduma la wan to'e bu.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Bala ti Yinɛ sumboolum la puan, n wan wa'am ya zi'an la nyɛɛma, gee tu za'a lagum me tara pupeelum la taaba.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Nananawa, suma'ahum Yinɛ la bɔna la ya za'a. Taari.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: N dikeri la tu taya Fobi en de nɛŋadaana suŋera Sinkireya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la pa'ala ya.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: ti ya to'e en Daana la puan wuu lan nari se'em bo' nɛresunduma la, la ya suŋɛ en la sɛla woo ti a wan bɔta ya zi'an. A dela suŋɛ bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e, ta ta' la mam mɛŋa.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Pirisika la Akuila, sɛba en tuni la mam Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: la bamam en pɔ̃hɛ ba vom mam zuo la. La dagi mam ma'a en pu'uheri ba na, gee sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri lageŋɔ duma la za'a pu'uheri ba na.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya lageŋɔ seko en lageheri ba yire puan la bo' mam. Pu'uhɛ ya mam sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ Epanetuse se'em en de yia daana Esiya sɔ'ɔlum la woo puan ti a tee a suure bo' Kiresta la.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Mɛɛri, se'em en tum zo'e zo'e ya puan la.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Anduronikus la Yuniya en de mam buuri, ti ba daa lagum pagɛ ba la mam la. Bama en de sɛba ti ba ba' mina Yezu tuntuneba la puan. Bama pun dɔla Kiresta mɛ gee ti mam baŋɛ wa'am na.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Ampiliatus en de mam nɔŋerɛ suuɔ, Daana la puan.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Yurebanuse mam tuntuna tadaana Kiresta puan, la Sitakise ti n nɔŋɛ la.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Apeles, se'em tuuma n tigɛ Kiresta nini. Pu'uhɛ ya Aristabulus yire duma la.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya n buuri Hɛrɔdiyɔn. Pu'uhɛ ya sɛba en dole Daana la, dɛna Nasisu yire duma la.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya sɛba en de Daana la tuntuneba dɛna, Tirefaina la Tirefosa. Pu'uhɛ ya n sunɔŋerɛ, Pɛsisi en tum paaɛ paaɛ Daana la puan la.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Pu'uhɛ Arufuse se'em ti ba looe en Daana tuuma puan la, gee pu'uhɛ a ma la en de mam me ma la.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Asinkrituse, Felegɔn, Hɛmese, Patirobase, Hɛmase, la suurɔ la za'a en boi la ba la.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya Filologuse, Yuliya, Nɛruse la a taya la, la Olimpase, la nɛresunduma la za'a en boi la ba la.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya taaba gee kɔkera taaba la pupeelum. Kiresta lageŋɔ la woo pu'uhi ya na.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, ti ya bahɛ ti ya nini bɔna sɛba en tari welegere la daaŋɔ kina ya tiŋasuka na, ban ka sakɛ bo' pa'alegɔ seko ti ya zamehɛ la, lɔkeri ya ba.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre bamam taaba ka tuni bɔ'ɔra tu Daana Kiresta, gee ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la ba mɛhi puya. Ba tari la tɔgɛ malemale la belegere, pã'aha susɛ'a en ka mi' la.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Ya naŋa la en yehe zi'an woo se'em la, en bahɛ ti mam nyɛɛna ya zuo, n boti ya tara la yɛm, sɛla en ani suŋa puan, gee yehe ya mɛhi sɛla en de be'em puan.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: La kan yuue la Suma'ahum Yinɛ la en wɔ̃regɛ Sagena la biŋe ya nama tiŋa. Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Timoti, mam tuntuna tadaana la pu'uheri ya na, la Lukiyuse la Yason, la Sosipata en de mam buuri la pu'uhi ya na.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Mam Tɛtiyuse, se'em nu'o en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la me pu'uhi ya Daana la puan na.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: Gayɔse se'em en biheri mam, la lageŋɔ la za'a ka'lam la pu'uhi ya na. Erastuse en de tiŋa la lige biŋera la, la tu suuɔ Kuwatuse me pu'uhi ya na. .

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
local: Nananawa wa, pɛka dɛna Yinɛ se'em en wan ta'am kpemehe ya la yelehum la ti n bo' ya la, la mam n muulɛ Yezu Kiresta bo' ya se'em, yele se'ere en sugɛ gã'a diimen diimen ha se'em.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: gee nananawa, de nyaa niiɛ na, doohe nɔtɔgeheba en gulehɛ se'em la, de niiɛ bo' sɛba woo en dagi Yuda buuri la, Yinɛ en boi wuuha wa n bo' a nuurɛ se'em, ti a bahɛ ti nɛra woo nam gee sakɛ la.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Pɛka dina Yinɛ, se'em ma'a en de yɛm za'a Yinɛ la dinɛ, doohe Yezu Kiresta puan wuuha wuuha Taari.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Pɔɔl, se'em ti Yinɛ yi' ti, a dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe Yinɛ mɛŋa suboolum la, la tu suuɔ Sositenese.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ Lageŋɔ la en boi Korinti tiŋa, la sɛba ti ba soke ba, Yezu Kiresta puan, gee yi ba ti ba dɛna nɛresunduma, ta pa'ahɛ la sɛba woo en boi ze'sɛka woo yi'ira Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, ti a dɛna bamam la tumam Daana la.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na, bɔna la ya.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mam bo'ori n Yinɛ fara mɛ saŋa woo ya zuo, la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ kãtɛ se'ere ti a ka'ɛ bahɛ ya zuo yoo Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: beni zuo, sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ya pirɛ mɛ a puan, la tugum, la mi'ilum woo.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: doohe la Kiresta yɛla la en ba'ari nyɛga ya puan se'em suŋa suŋa la.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: bahɛ ti ya ka koŋe zu'uhegɔ bo'olum sebo ya puan, yan guri tu Daana Yezu Kiresta niirɛ na wa.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: A me wan sɛ ya ze'ele suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ, ti ya kan tara gbelegere, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana. Eŋa puan ti ba yi' ya ti ya tara Lageŋɔ la a Dayua Yezu Kiresta en de tu Daana la.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: Nananawa, n yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ n suurɔ, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan, ti ya za'a sakera bɔ'ɔra taaba ti bɔgehegɔ da bɔna ya puan, gee ya tugum naaɛ taaba dɛna buyina, la putɛ'yinɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: N suurɔ wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Kuloo yire nɛreba yele mam ti nɔkpɛ'ɛma en boi ya tiŋasuka.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: De vuurɛ dela, ya sɛba yeti, Mam dole la Pɔɔl, bii Mam dole la Apolose, bii Mam dole la Sefase, bii Mam dole la Kiresta.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Aa! Kiresta bɔgehi mɛ yoi? Pɔɔl ti kpa' bo' ya la? Bii ya daa sɔ ko'om la Pɔɔl yu'urɛ puan?.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: Mam bo' Yinɛ fara ti, mam ka sɔ ya mina ko'om, han dagena Kiresepusi la Gayɔse ma'a.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: ti se'em da ta'am ta yeti, ba daa sɔ ko'om fum la mam yu'urɛ puan.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ka tum mam ti n sɔɔra nɛreba ko'om, gee n muula Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la, dagi asaala yɛm kirega puan, ti Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ paŋa la da bahɛ yoo.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Pumpuuredɔɔ yelehum la dela gɔɔnyɛla bo' sɛba en baheri yoo la. Gee han dɛna tumam sɛba en nyɛ faarɛ wa, bu dela Yinɛ paŋa.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Mam wan sagum yɛm duma yɛm, gee burum baŋereba baŋerɛ.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Yɛm daana la bɛɛ? Gɔnmina la bɛɛ? Sankana nɔkpe'en ŋmɛ'ɛra la bɛɛ? Yinɛ ka iŋɛ ti tiŋa wa zuo yɛm la dɛna la gɔɔnɛ?.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Ze'ele Yinɛ yɛm puan na, timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba ka mi Yinɛ ba mɛhi yɛm puan, Yinɛ en iŋɛ a puti'irɛ ti a doohe tumam gɔɔnɛ muulegɔ wa puan, la a faaɛ sɛba en nam la.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yuuduma boti ba nyɛ la kirehi, ti Girikiduma me bɔta yɛm ti ba ta'am nam.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Gee han dɛna tumam, tumam muuli la Kiresta ti ba kpa' la, yelese'ere en de pirega lɔkɔ bo' Yuuduma, gee dɛna gɔɔn yelehum bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Gee sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' la, Yuuduma, la Girikiduma za'a, Kiresta dela Yinɛ paŋa, la Yinɛ yɛm bo' ba.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba en nyɛ sɛla ti'iha ti Yinɛ gɔɔnɛ n bala la, nyani asaala yɛm, ti nɛreba en nyɛ sɛla ti'iha ti Yinɛ tɔregɔ en bala la, nyana asaala paŋa.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: N suurɔ wa, pun bihɛ ya mɛhi puan ti ba yi' ya se'em wa, Dagi ya zo'e zo'e en daa de yɛm duma asaala gakerɛ puan, dagi ya zo'e zo' en de pahi duma, dagi ya zo'e zo'e en daa de nabiihi ya dɔgum puan.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Gee Yinɛ looe la sɛla en de gɔɔnɛ tiŋa wa zuo, ti a diihe yɛm duma la vi. Yinɛ looe la sɛla en ka tari paŋa tiŋa wa zuo, ti a diihe paŋa duma la vi.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: Yinɛ looe la tarema, la sɛla en ka tari zuo tiŋa wa zuo, yelesɛ'a en pun dagena sɛla la, ti a sigehe yelekãra duma la ti ba lebege zaŋa.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: Ŋwana ti nɛra kan ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Yinɛ en de ya vom nyɛgerɛ, Kiresta Yezu puan, se'em en de tu yɛm, ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, dɛna tuuntɔɔ la, se'em en sokere la, la ayɔto'e daana la.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: ti wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Se'em woo en ŋmɛ'ɛri a nyu'ɔ, a ŋmɛ' kɔ Daana la puan la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: Bala la, n suurɔ, mam ka ta'am ni tɔgɛ bo' ya wuu sɛba ti Fa'a Sia la bɔna la ba, gee en tugum tɔgɛ bo' ya wuu sɛba ti Sia la ka bɔna ba puan la. Wuu ya dela kɔma Kiresta puan la.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Mam bo' ya la bi'ihum, gee dagi dikpe'ene, se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa ka maahum ti ya di dikpe'ene. La nananawa me, ya pun kelum ka maahum.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: beni zuo, ya kelum bɔna la ingɔŋɔ puan. Nyuure la nɔkpɛ'ɛma en kelum bɔna ya puan wa, ya ka boi la ingɔŋɔ puan wa, ita saaleba itegɔ?.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Beni zuo, ya ayima han yeti, Mam dole la Pɔɔl, ti ayima yeti, Mam dole la Apolos, ya kɔ'ɔm ka ita la nɛresaalenɛ en bala?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Ani en de Apolos? Ti ani dɛna Pɔɔl? Ba za'a dela yamehi se'ehi ti ya doohe ba puan baŋɛ Yinɛ, wuu Daana la en bo' ba ayima ayima se'em la.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Mam burɛ mɛ, ti Apolos bahɛ ko'om. Gee Yinɛ en bahɛ ti la yehe na.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Bala, dagi se'em en burɛ la, bii se'em en bahɛ ko'om la en de sɛla, gee Yinɛ ma'a, se'em en bahɛ ti la yehe la.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Se'em en burɛ la, la se'em en bahɛ ko'om la dela buyina, ti ba ayima woo wan to'e a yɔɔrɛ doohe la ba ayima woo tuuma en de se'em.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Tumam dela Yinɛ yamehi, tuna la taaba; Yamam en de Yinɛ va'am, dɛna Yinɛ yire.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Doohe la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ ti a zu'uhɛ mam la, mam ani mɛ wuu se'em en mi' mɛa, iibɛ ɛ̃berɛ la biŋe, ti ayima me wa'am na mɛ dɔgelɛ. Mɛta wuu dela a bihɛ suŋa en wan mɛ se'em dɔgelɛ de zuo.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ka boi wan ta'am iibɛ ɛ̃berɛ deyima me, han dagena ɛ̃berɛ se'ere ti ba pun iibɛ la. Ɛ̃berɛ dinɛ dela Yezu Kiresta.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Bala, se'em han mɛta ɛ̃berɛ wa zuo mɛ la salema, salempɛɛla, kugesuma, dɔɔrɔ, kinka, la muɔ.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: mɛta woo tuunɛ wan ta niiɛ na, se'ere en sɔi la, dabeherɛ la daarɛ wan niiɛ dɛ na, beni zuo, bugum en wan ta bahɛ ti de niiɛ na, bugum la wan magehɛ tuunse'ere taaba ti nɛra woo tum la bihɛ.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Ti sɛla en dikɛ mɛ dɔgelɛ ɛ̃berɛ la zuo la han ka di, mɛta la wan to'e yɔɔrɛ.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Gee mɛta la tuunɛ la han di bugum, mɛta la namehɛ bahɛ la yoo. A mɛŋa wan nyɛ faarɛ, gee la wan ana en wuu a doohe la bugum puan tole la.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti ya dela Yinɛ yire, ti Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la bɔna ya puan?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Ya se'em han sagum Yinɛ yire wa, Yinɛ me wan sagum a daana. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yire la dela fa'a, ti yamam en dɛna Yinɛ yire la.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Da pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi. Ya han ti'ihɛ ti yamam en de yɛm duma buuri sankana wa puan, ya tugum lebege geto, la ya ta'am nyɛ yɛm.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tiŋa wa zuo yɛm dela gɔɔnɛ Yinɛ zi'an. Beni zuo, la gulehɛ yeti, Yinɛ nyɔgere yɛm duma mɛ ba mɛhi nyaleŋɔ puan.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: le yɛ'ɛha, Daana la mi' yɛm duma ti'ihegɔ mɛ, ti ba ka tari vuurɛ.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Bala, se'em da ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la saaleba kpe'eŋo. Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛlawoo dela ya dinɛ.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: la dɛna Pɔɔl, bii Apolos, bii Sefase, bii timpaberɛ wa, bii vom, bii kum, bii zina, bii beere yele, la woo dela ya dinɛ.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: ti yamam me dɛna Kiresta dinɛ, ti Kiresta me dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Ba kɔ'ɔm yele mam mɛ ti galehi en boi ya puan. Galehi sɛ'a taaba en pun ka bɔna sɛba en ka dole Yinɛ puan. Beni zuo, buraa en boi ya puan gã'a la ka sɔ pɔga.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Gee ti ya tugum ukera. La dagi ni la ya tugum kɛla, gee ti se'em en tum tuun-dina wa la, ya fooe ni en ya puan bahɛ?.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: La mam pun ka bɔna la ya inganɛ puan la, n boi la ya mɛ Sia puan, ti lan ani wuu mam boi mɛ la ya mɛ la, mam pun purɛ la gakerɛ.

eng: When you meet together in the name of our Lord Jesus, Ill be present in spirit with the power of our Lord Jesus.
local: Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan bahera buraa sɛka en tum tuun-dina wa taaba wa zuo. Bala ya han ta lagehɛ, ti n sia bɔna la ya, la Daana Yezu paŋa la.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: ya dela ya dikɛ nɛre-ina daana bo' Sagena la ti a sagum a inganɛ la, gee ti a sia ta nyɛ faarɛ Daana dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Ya pa'ali mɛŋa la ka tari vuurɛ, yamam ka mi' ti dabelɛ fii wa en ni bahɛ ti zom la za'a ihegera?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Pee ya dabelɛ kɛka la bahɛ, la ya ta'am dɛna dabelɛ paalum ma'a, wuu dabelɛ en sirum ka boi ya puan se'em wa la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba kaabɛ tu azɔɛtole pebila la en de Kiresta la mɛ.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu de'em ya de'eŋo wa, dagi la dabelɛ kɛka la en de pa'ahegɔ, la be'em dabelɛ la, gee tu de'em ku la boroboro zaŋa la en de sira, la susunɛ.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: Mam daa gulehɛ n gɔŋɔ la puan yeti ya da dikɛ ya mɛhi lagum la sɛba en tuni galeni ba mɛhi la.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: ka pa'alɛ la fii me ti sɛba en ka dole Kiresta tiŋa wa zuo la, bii sɛba en nyigere, la fãareba, bii bage kaabereba. La han dɛna bala, la yi dela ya bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo en bala.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Gee nananawa, mam en gulehɛ yɛta ya se'em dela, ya da dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la se'em en yi'iri a mɛŋa ya suuɔ, gee yaala, nyigera, bii kaabera baga, bii nɛreba tua, dabugere, bii fãara. Da pun naaɛ la ena daana di.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Han dagena bala, mam yeti n gakɛ sɛba en ka dole Kiresta iŋɛ la beni? La dagi la sɛba en dole Yinɛ la en de tu gakɛ?.

eng: God will judge outsiders. Expel the evil one from among you! .
local: Yinɛ mɛŋa en wan gakɛ sɛba en ka dole Kiresta la. Fooe ya be'em daana la ya puan bahɛ.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Ya se'em han tara se'ere la a tadaana, a wan sakɛ tari dɛ kiŋɛ sɛba en ka dole Yezu la zi'an ti ba ta gakɛ ya, gana a tari kiŋɛ nɛresunduma la zi'an?.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti nɛresunduma la en wan ta gakɛ timpaberɛ wa? Yamam han wan ta gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, la iŋɛ la ŋwani ti ya kan gakɛ yele-pigehi wa?.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti tumam en wan gakɛ suŋereba, nyaa benti yele ana wa?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Bala, ya han ni nyɛ yele pigehi ana wa, la dela ya digehɛ gakereba sɛba en ka boi lageŋɔ la puan ti ba gakɛ?.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: N yeti ya ŋwana ti vi en nyɔkɛ ya. La wan ta'am dɛna ti, nɛra yinɛ me ka boi ya puan tara yɛm wan ta'am maalɛ sakereba bayi tiŋasuka.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: gee ti sakera tari sakera kiŋɛ tugum zi'an, sɛba en ka dole Yezu nɛŋan yele ana wa zuo?.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya pun tara yɛla la taaba pun pa'alɛ ti ya koŋe mɛ en bala. Ŋwani ti yamam tugum ka bahɛ ti ba tuue ya? Ŋwani ti yamam tugum ka bahɛ ti ba fa' ya?.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Vi ka tari ya, yamam mɛhi en tugum tuura, gee fãara, ti ba me dɛna ya sakereba taaba?.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti be'emduma kan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan? Da pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi. Yalum duma, bage kaabereba, sɛba en tuni kabohi, pɔgenindɔɔrɔ la buraahi bii pɔgehi en gã' taaba.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nageyigehi, nyigereba dabugereba, tuureba, la fãareba, bana wa ayima kan kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Gee bala ti ya sɛba daa ana. Gee ba pee ya mɛ, ba sukɛ ya mɛ, ba bo' ya la buurɔ tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, la tu Yinɛ Sia la puan.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Sɛla woo ka kihi bɔ'ɔra mam, gee dagi sɛla woo en tari nyuurɔ. Sɛla woo ka kihi bɔ'ɔra mam, gee mam kan lebege da'aberɛ bo' sɛla.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Dia dela puurɛ dinɛ, ti puurɛ me dɛna dia dinɛ, gee Yinɛ wan sagum ba za'a. Inganɛ la ka boi la yaluntuuma zuo, gee de boi bo' la Daana la, ti Daana la me bɔna bo' inganɛ la.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Yinɛ en vo'e Daana la kum puan, gee me wan vo'e tumam me a paŋa la puan.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Ya ka mi' ti ya ihe la dela Kiresta dinɛ? Bala, n dikɛ Kiresta lɔgerɔ lebege yaalegɔ lɔgerɔ? La kan tum.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Ya ka mi' ti se'em woo en dikɛ a mɛŋa naaɛ la yalema, dela inganɛ deyina la en? Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Bayi' la wan lebege inganɛ deyina.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Gee se'em woo en dikɛ a mɛŋa naaɛ la Daana la dela buyina la en Sia puan.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Zagehɛ yaluntuuma, se'ere en sɔi la, be'em sebo woo ti nɛra tum ni dɛna la iŋa la yiŋa na. Gee yaluntuuma daana galum la iŋa la za'a.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Bii ya ka mi' ti ya iŋa la dela pu'uhegɔ yire bo' Fa'a Sia la en boi la ya la, se'em ti ya daa to'e Yinɛ zi'an la. Bala, ya ka le sɔna ya mɛhi?.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba da' ya mɛ la da'alum, bala zuo, na'ahɛ ya Yinɛ la ya inganɛ la.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Nananawa, han dɛna yelesɛ'a ti ya gulehɛ sɔkera la, yan yeti, La ani suŋa ti buraa da kalum pɔka.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: Gee yaluntuuma la zuo, buraa woo di a suure pɔga, ti pɔka woo me ele ka suure sira.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: Sira la dela a naara la a pɔga lan nari se'em la , ti pɔka me naara la ka sira lan nari se'em la.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka la ka sɔi ka mɛŋa iŋa la, gee sira la en sɔi, bala mɛa ti sira la me ka sɔna ka mɛŋa iŋa la, gee pɔka la en sɔi.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Da zagehere ya taaba, han dagena ti ya ku la nuurɛ la taaba saŋa ka yima puan, ti ya kaabɛ ya mɛhi bo' pu'uhegɔ. Gee bala han tole, ya lebe naaɛ taaba, ti Sagena la da makɛ ya bihɛ, yan ka ta' ya mɛhi tara la zuo.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: Mam en yeti ya sɛla wa dela n yeen yele mɛ gee dagi nuurɛ ti n bo'.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Mam boti ni ti ya za'a ani ni wuu mam wa, gee nɛra woo tari la a bo'olum sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' en. Ayima tari la bo'olum buna, ti ayima me tara bo'olum buna.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Yamam sɛba en ka di pɔgeba bii ele sireba la, la pokɔpa la, mam yeti, la ani suŋa ti ya kelum bɔna bala ya ma'a woo mam n boi se'em wa.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Gee ba han ka zameha ba mɛhi ti ba tana ba mɛhi tara, ba di pɔgeba, bii ba ele sireba. Se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti ba di pɔgeba bii ba ele sireba nyana kunkoma'ahum en kuuri ba.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Sɛba en tari pɔgeba la sireba la, mam bo' ya la nɔdina wa, dagi mam gee Daana la, ti pɔka da bahɛ ka sira.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: , ti sira me da lebehe a pɔga.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Yamam sɛba en gee la me, mam n yeti se'ere n ŋwana gee dagi Daana la. Ti sakera han tara pɔga ti ka ka sakɛ bo' Yezu, gee bɔta ti ka bɔna la ka sira la, sira la da lebehe ka.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Pɔka han tara ka sira en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu, gee ti buraa la bɔta a bɔna la pɔga la, pɔka la da bahɛ buraa la.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sira wa en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu la lebege la tuulum nɛra, a pɔga wa zuo. Bala mɛa ti pɔgesɛka en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu la me wan lebege tuulum nɛra a sira la zuo. Han dagena bala, ba kɔma la kan dɛna tuulum kɔma. Gee lan de se'em la, ba dela tuulum kɔma.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Gee sɛba en ka dole Yezu la han bahɛ taaba, de dɛna bala, la han iŋɛ bala, buraa la bii pɔka la ka boi bobere puan. Suma'ahum zuo ti Yinɛ yi ya.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Gee ani n mi', Pɔka wa, fum ta baŋera la ti fum faaɛ fum sira la mɛ, sira wa, fum ta baŋera la ti fum faaɛ fum pɔga la mɛ.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Lan pun dɛna se'em woo, nɛrawoo vɔna vomsebo ti Daana la dikɛ bo' en la, vom sebo ti Yinɛ yi' ya ti ya vɔna la. Mam nɔze'elego en ŋwana bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ duma la za'a.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Se'em boi ti ba pun ŋma en gee ti Yinɛ baŋɛ yi' en? A duma da zigera ti a saalum ŋmaa daalehum la bahɛ. Se'em boi ti Yinɛ yi' en gee ti a nan ka ŋma, a da zigera ti a ŋma.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Ŋmaa dagi sɛla, a ka ŋma me dagi sɛla, gee Yinɛ nɔze'elego la naŋa en de sɛla woo.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: Bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo ana wuu en daa boi se'em gee ti Yinɛ yi' en la.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: Fum daa dela da'aberɛ gee ti Yinɛ yi' fum? Da bahɛ ti la daana hɔ, fum han pun wan nyaŋɛ faaɛ fum mɛŋa bɔna fai, dikɛ fum boi se'em zina wa tum gaŋɛ sɛla woo.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em ti ba yi' en ti a dɛna da'aberɛ Daana la puan, dela se'em en boi fai, dɛna Daana la dinɛ, wuu se'em en daa boi fai gee ti Yinɛ yi' fum ti fum dɛna da'aberɛ se'em bo' Kiresta la.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Ba da' ya mɛ la da'alum, da deni ya da'aba bo' saaleba kpɛ'ɛnduma.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Yan daa boi bɔnsɛka taaba gee ti Yinɛ yi' ya la, n suurɔ, kelum boni ya bala la Yinɛ.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Nananawa, pɔgesakaha la me yele en ŋwana, Daana la ka bo' mam nuurɛ, gee mam bo'ori ya la n mɛŋa puti'irɛ, wuu se'em ti Daana la zɔ' n nimbu'ɔ ti n dɛna se'em ti ya wan nam la.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Mam ti'iheri yeti, tumam en nyɛti daaŋɔ seko en boi la zuo, la wan ana suŋa ti ya kɔ'ɔm bɔna yan boi se'em la.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Fum di la pɔga? Da zigera ti fum bahɛ ka. Fum ka tari pɔga? Da ɛɛra pɔga.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Gee fum han di pɔga bii fum ele la sira, fum ka tum be'em. Pɔgesakãherɛ me han ele, a ka tum be'em. Gee sɛba en ele bii di pɔgeba la, ba wan nyɛ ɛregɔ, gee n boti n faaɛ ya mɛ yele ana wa puan bahɛ.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: Mam n yeti se'em wa la, n suurɔ, dela, sansɛka ti Yinɛ looe la ba'aheri mɛ n bala, bala nananawa yɛ'a ha, bahɛ ya ti sɛba en pun tara pɔgeba la, bɔna wuu ba ka tari la pɔgeba la.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: ti sɛba en kele, ana wuu ba ka kele me la, ti sɛba en nyɛɛni, ana wuu ba ka nyɛɛni me la, ti sɛba en da'ari kɔɔhera ana wuu ba ka tari mɛ la.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: ti sɛba en tuni tiŋa wa zuo ana wuu ba ka tuni mɛ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, tinkana wa ba'aheri mɛ n bala.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: Mam boti n faaɛ ya mɛ ninmu'urɛ puan bahɛ. Se'em n ka tari pɔga nini mu'ɛ mɛ la Daana la, en wan iŋɛ se'em ti Daana nini tigɛ.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Gee se'em n tari pɔga nini mu'ɛ mɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, a biheri la en wan iŋɛ se'em pee a pɔga puurɛ.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: a puti'irɛ la dela buyi. Gee pɔge se'ehi en ka ele, la pɔgesakaha la nini mu'ɛ mɛ la Daana la, ti ba dɛna tuulum nɛreba inganɛ la Sia puan. Gee pɔge se'ehi en ele nini mu'ɛ mɛ la tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, eŋa n wan iŋɛ se'em pee a sira puurɛ.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: N tɔgeri yele ana wa woo la ya sum zuo, dagi la n gu'uri ya mɛ se'ere puan, gee n bahɛ ti sɛla woo dɔla ma'a ma'a, gee ti sɛla da gu' ya Yinɛ sore dɔlega la puan.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Se'em han ta bihɛ ti a kan ta'am a mɛŋa tara la pɔgesakãherɛ la ti a bɔna la de la, a kunkoma'ahum han ba' zɔ'ɔra la pɔka la, wuu lan suni ti la ana se'em la, a di ka wuu en boti se'em la, la dagi be'em. Bahɛ ya ti ba di taaba.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Gee se'em han zɛ'a kankaŋi en ku se'em a suure puan, ti pɛregerɛ ka bɔna gee tana a mɛŋa kunkoma'ahum tara, gee iŋɛ a suuren ti a kan kalum pɔgesakãherɛ la, la wan suŋɛ en.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Bala la, se'em en di a pɔgesakãherɛ iŋɛ suŋa, gee fum se'em en ka di a pɔgesakãherɛ en gani za'a.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Pɔka en ele boi la sire ele kiha puan, a sira n nan vɔi la. Gee a sira la han ki, a tari sore ti a malum ele eŋa en boti se'em, gee a ele doohe Daana sumboolum puan.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: Gee mam puti'irɛ puan, sum wan boni la pɔka la, ka han zi' ni bala ka ele. Mam ti'ihɛ ti mam me tari Yinɛ Sia la n puan mɛ.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Nananawa, disɛka ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ bo' baga sɔhega la. Tu mi' ti, Tu za'a tari mi'ilum mɛ. Mi'ilum bo'ori la pa'alɛ mɛŋa gee nɔŋerɛ en mɛti.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Se'em han ti'ihɛ ti eŋa mi' sɛla, nan kelum ka tara mi'ilum sebo en de a tara.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Gee se'em en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, Yinɛ mi' en mɛ.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Bala, han dɛna disɛka en dikɛ kaabɛ baga sɔhega la, tu mi' ti Bagerɛ ka boi tiŋa wa zuo dɛna bunvua, ti yinɛ ka bɔna han dagena Yinɛ deyina wa ma'a.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla ta'am bɔna ti ba yi'ira ba yina ti ba bɔna saazuo bii tiŋa wa zuo, wuu lan de sira se'em ti yina la daanduma boi mɛ la.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: Gee han dɛna tumam, Yinɛ dela ayima, Sɔ la, se'em zi'an ti sɛla woo ze'ele na la, la se'em zuo ti tu za'a vɔna la, Daana ayima, en de Yezu Kiresta la, se'em puan ti sɛla woo ze'ele na, la se'em zuo ti tu vɔna la.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Dagi nɛra woo en tari mi'ilum buna wa, gee yelemɛŋerɛ nɛreba basɛba boi mɛ ti ba suya ba' lu' la baga, tara tara la zina, ba kelum ti'iha dia ti ba di ti ba dikɛ ka kaabɛ bo' la baga, ti ba puti'ira la en ka kpe'em la nyaa galum ba.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Dia kan ta'am tari tu lɛm Yinɛ. Tu ka sagum tole tu han ka di ka, tu me ka nyani se'em tu han di ka.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Gee bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ti ya fai yɔ'ɔ wa da dɛna pirega lɔkɔ bo' sɛba en ka kpe'em la.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, basɛba han nyɛ yamam sɛba en de mi'ilum duma wa ti ya boi baga zɔŋɔ puan dita, la kan bahɛ ti bamam sɛba puti'ira en ka kpe'em la me iŋɛ ba puti'irɛ ta paaɛ ze'sɛka ti ba me bɔta ti ba di dia n dikɛ kaabɛ bagerɛ?.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: Bala, ya mi'ilum la zuo ti sɛba ti Kiresta ki bo' ba, gee ti ba nan ka kpe'em la nyɛta sageŋɔ.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Gee fum han tuna fum yire duma be'em, pɔgelum ba puti'ira la en nan ka kpe'em la, fum tuni la Kiresta be'em.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Bala zuo, dia la en han sɔna ba lua, mam yi kan makɛ obe nɛŋɔ, la n da bahɛ ti ba ayima lu.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: Mam ka boi la fai yoi? Mam dagi la Yezu tuntuna yoi? Mam ka nyɛ la Yezu tu Daana la yoi? Dagi yamam en de mam tuuma Daana la puan?.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Mam han pun ka dɛna Yezu tuntuna bo' basɛba, gee la se'em se'em me, n de mɛ bo' yamam, se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en de mam daalehum pa'ala ti n dela Daana la tuntuna.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Mam pu'uri n mɛŋa se'em n ŋwana, nɛreba han ni ta viihera ti ba baŋɛ nyaa n de Yezu tuntuna yoi.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: Tu ka tari sore ti tu dita gee nyuura tumam en boti sɛla?.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Tu ka tari sore ti tu pɔgeba en de tu sakereba la dɔla tɔ wuu Yezu tuntuneba basɛba la, la Daana suurɔ la, la Sefase en iti se'em la?.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Bii Banabase la mam ma'a n kɔ'ɔm ka tari sore ti tu me bahɛ da tuna diiha tu mɛhi?.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Ani n boi, ni yɔɔra a mɛa pɛntɛrɛ tuuma puan? Ani n sɛ tiihi gee ka dita si wala, bii ani en dole piihi gee kan sũna si iilum?.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: Mam yeti se'em wa dela nɛre saala puti'irɛ? Nɔze'elego la me ka yeti la bala mɛa?.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ Moose nɔze'elego puan yeti, Da lu' lɔlega nuurɛ gee tara ka tulegera ki. La dela lɔlemɛŋehi ti Yinɛ yɛta wa?.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Gee Yinɛ ka tɔgeri ŋwana za'a la tumam zuo, yelemɛŋerɛ, ba gulehɛ dina wa la tumam zuo? Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han kɔɔra, a kɔɔri gee tara la beere puti'irɛ, ti se'em me han tɔɔra ki, a tɔɔri gee tara la puti'irɛ ti a wan to'e a tɔtɛ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Tumam han burɛ la sia sum ya puan, tu nyaa han kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ya ingɔŋɔ puan nyuurɔ, la ba' gaŋɛ mɛ yoi?.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Basɛba han tara sore ti ba to'e sɛla ya zi'an, tumam ka nari gaŋɛ bala? Gee aayi, tumam ka makɛ dikɛ yɔ'ɔ kuna wa tum, gee tumam ŋmiberi mɛ sɛla woo puan, nyana tu dikɛ sɛla beŋe Kiresta faarɛ yelehum wa puan.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti sɛba ti ba dikɛ ti ba tuna pu'uhegɔ yire, nyɛti ba dia la pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, ti sɛba me n tuni kaabegɔ bimbine zuo la me ni to'e ba dinɛ la sɛla ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ la puan?.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Bala mɛa, Daana la bo' nuurɛ ti sɛba woo n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la, nyɛta ba nuurɛ dia faarɛ yelehum mɛa puan.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Gee mam ka dikɛ yɔ'ɔ kuna wa la kuyina me tum, bii n nyaa guleha ŋwana ti ya dikɛ de tum sadɔseko puan. Yelemɛŋerɛ, mam tugum ki nyani nɛre se'em gu' mam se'ere zuo ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ la.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Mam han muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa, dina ka bo' mam sore ti n ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, la dela tuunɛ gã'a n zuo, ti yele n bɔna n zuo, n han ka muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Beni zuo, mam han tuna dina wa la n mɛŋa suboolum, mam tari yɔɔrɛ mɛ, gee la han dagena mam mɛŋa suboolum, ba ye dikɛ tuunɛ la bo' mam mɛ ti n tum ti ba yɔ mam.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: Beni en nyaa de mam yɔɔrɛ la? La dela n muulegɔ la, n bahɛ ti faarɛ yelehum wa paaɛ nɛreba zaŋa la, ti n da ta'am dikɛ mam tari yɔ'ɔ seko faarɛ yelehum wa puan la tum.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: La mam pun bɔna fai la, mam na'ahera nɛra woo mɛ, dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege da'aberɛ bo' nɛra woo, ti n ta'am nyaŋɛ ba zo'e zo'e.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Yuuduma la zi'an, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la Yuda nɛra, ti n ta'am tee Yuuduma la, Sɛba en dole kiha la me zi'an, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la bamam en ani se'em la, , ti n ta'am tee sɛba en boi kiha puan la.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Sɛba en ka dole kiha la, mam ni dikɛ n mɛŋa lebege la sɛba en ka dole kiha la , ti n ta'am tee sɛba en ka boi kiha puan.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Sɛba en tari tɔregɔ, mam ni lebege la tɔregɔ daana, ti n ta'am tee tɔregɔ duma la. Mam lebege la sɛla woo bo' nɛra woo ti sadɔseko woo puan, n ta'am faaɛ basɛba.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: Mam iti ŋwana za'a la faarɛ yelehum la zuo, ti n ta ta'am tara tɔtɛ bu sum la puan.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Yamam ka mi' ti zua puan, zɔteba la woo ni pagera la taaba, gee ba ayima ma'a en ni ta di mi'a? Zɔ ya yan wan zɔ se'em la ya ta'am di.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Zɔteba kpemehere ba mɛhi la ba tana ba mɛhi tara sɛla woo puan. Ba iti dina wa ti ba to'e la muŋa sɛka en ta' Sagena, gee tumam dela muŋa sɛka en ka ta' sageni.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Bala, mam ka zoti yoo yoo, bii n ŋmɛ'ɛra kunkura yoo yoo.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Gee mam kpemehe n iŋa wa mɛ ti ka dɛna da'aberɛ, ti n han ta muulɛ bo' basɛba, mam mɛŋa kan bahɛ yoo.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: Mam ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, ti tu sɔyaabeduma la za'a yuum boi sagbanɛ la tilen la mɛ, ba za'a yuum doohe ateko la puan tole mɛ.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: ba za'a yuum to'e Moose dɔlega ko'om sua la mɛ, sagbanɛ la, la ateko la puan.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: ti ba za'a di sia puan diyiŋa la.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: gee ti ba za'a nyu sia puan ko'yimɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba nyu ko'om la la sia puan taŋa la en yuum dole ba la, tansɛka en de Kiresta la.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Gee la bala za'a, Yinɛ nini da ka tigɛ la ba zo'e zo'e, ti ba kɔrum ba muuen la ha.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Yele ana wa iŋɛ ti a dɛna la yɛm nyɔka bo' tumam, ti tu kɔrehi da le bɔna be'em puan wuu bamam en da iŋɛ se'em la.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Da lebege ya bageditiba wuu ba basɛba n da iŋɛ se'em la, wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Nɛreba la zi'ire mɛ di, gee nyu gee ihege wa'ara wa'yooro la.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Tu ka de tu dikɛ tu mɛhi kɛ̃ yaluntuuma puan wuu ba basɛba en yuum iŋɛ se'em la, ti tuha pihiyi' la a tã yuum ki daarɛ de yina puan la.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Tu ka de tu mageha Kiresta biha wuu basɛba en daa iŋɛ se'em ti kampiihi kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra ba kuura la.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Tu me da nyɔna wuu ba puan basɛba en yuum iŋɛ se'em ti Sagena la sagum ba la.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Yele ana wa iŋɛ ba ti la dɛna la makerɛ, gee ti ba gulehɛ a biŋe ti a pa'alɛ tumam sɛba en boi ba'ahegɔ buuri saŋa wa puan la.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Bala, fum se'em en ti'ihɛ fum ze' mɛ, bihɛ suŋa la fum da lu.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Makerɛ ka boi paaɛ ya gee ka tabelɛ paaɛ nɛrese'em. Yinɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana, a kan bahɛ ti makerɛ paaɛ ya gaŋɛ ya paŋa tɛka. Gee makerɛ la puan, a wan pa'alɛ ya sore ti ya ŋmibe de puan.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Bala zuo, n zɔduma wa, ɛkɛ ya baga kaabegɔ puan yehe.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: Mam mi' ti mam tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la yɛm duma. Gakɛ ya bo' ya mɛhi mam en yeti ya sɛla wa.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Sum wanɛ la ti tu ni ka' bahɛ de puan la, tu ni ka nyuura la Kiresta ziim la n bala de puan la? Boroboro la me ti tu ni gi'ihɛ la, tu ni ka tɔta la Kiresta iŋa la en bala?.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Boroboro la en de deyina la zuo, tumam en zo'e se'em la dela inganɛ deyina, se'ere n sɔi la, tu lagum obe la boroboro deyina la.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Bihɛ ya Izirale nɛreba wa, ba sɛba en diti kaabegɔ dia la, dagi la taaba kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo?.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: Beni ti n dikɛ ŋwana makera wa? Dia la en dikɛ kaabɛ baga la en de sɛla bii, baga la en de sɛla.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Aayi, mam yeti sɛla wa dela, sɛla ti bage kaabereba la ni kaabɛ bo' la, ba ni kaabɛ bo' la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi gee dagi Yinɛ. Mam ka boti ya dikɛ ya mɛhi naaɛ la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Ya kan ta'am nyu Daana wanɛ la puan, gee le nyu kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi wanɛ puan. Yamam kan ta'am naaɛ la Daana di, gee le naaɛ la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi di.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: Bii tu boti tu ihege la Daana la nyuure? Bii tumam kpe'em gana en mɛ?.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Ya yeti, fum ta'am tari sore ti fum ita sɛla woo gee ya me mina ti dagi sɛla wuu en tari nyuurɔ, Ya yeti, fum ta'am tara sore ti fum ita sɛla woo, gee ya me mina ti dagi sɛla woo n mɛti.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Da eere ya, ya mɛhi sum ma'a, gee eere ya suurɔ me sum.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Diti ya sɛla woo en koohe da'a puan, gee da soke ya doohe ya puti'ira en iti ya se'em la.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Timpaberɛ wa, la kaŋa en pirɛ se'em gã'a wa dela Daana la dinɛ.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Se'em en ka dole Yezu sore han yi' fum ti fum wa'am na di, ti fum iŋɛ fum puti'irɛ ti fum kiŋɛ, fum han ta paaɛ, di sɛla woo ti ba tari na ze'ele fum, gee da soke sokere doohe la fum puti'irɛ en iti fum se'em.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Gee nɛra la han yele fum ti, Ba dikɛ dia wa kaabɛ la bagerɛ, da di dikaŋa, se'em daana en yele fum la zuo, la puti'irɛ la en wan ita se'em la zuo.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: Mam yeti sɛla wa dela, nɛre-ina wa puti'irɛ zuo, dagi fum mɛŋa puti'irɛ. Beni zuo, ŋwani ti mam fai bɔŋa wan bahɛ ti ayima puti'irɛ gakera en?.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Mam han bo' Yinɛ fara gee di dia, ŋwani ti ayima wan zɛregera mam disɛka ti mam bo' Yinɛ fara ka zuo gee di?.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Bala, ya diti mɛ, ya nyuuri mɛ, bii sɛla woo ti ya ita, iti ya sɛla woo ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ nyɛta pɛka.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Da boni ya bunsɛka en wan tara daaŋɔ wa'am Yuuduma bii Girikiduma, bii Yinɛ lageŋɔ la puan na.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Wuu mam muuri ti n tigɛ nɛra woo nini sɛla woo ti n ita puan se'em la, n ka eere n mɛŋa sum gee en eere la zo'e zo'e sum, ti ba ta'am nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Tɔgehi ya mam, wuu mam tɔgehɛ Kiresta se'em la.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: N pɛgɛ ya, la yan tɛri mam sɛla woo puan se'em la, gee me tara malema suŋa suna wuu mam dikɛ a se'em bo' ya la.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Gee n boti ya bɔkɛ yeti, Kiresta en de buraa woo zuo daana, ti sira me dɛna a pɔga zuo daana, ti Yinɛ dɛna Kiresta zuo daana.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Burasɛka woo en pu'uhi, bii puta gee ti sɛla pi' ka zuo, diiheri la a zuo daana la vi.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Gee pɔgesɛka en pu'uhi, bii puta gee ka pi' ka zuo, diihe la ka zuo daana la vi, la ŋwɔni mɛ la ka siie ka zuo la mɛ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka han kan pi' ka zuo, ka yi siie ku bahɛ, gee la han dɛna vi ti pɔka kɛ̃ bii siie ka zuo, ka yi vuge sɛla.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, buraa dagi a pi' ka zuo, eŋa en de Yinɛ naanɛ, la Yinɛ suŋa la. Gee pɔka dela buraa suŋa.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ba ka naam buraa yehe pɔka puan, gee pɔka ti ba naam yehe buraa puan.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Ba me ka naam buraa pɔka zuo, gee ba naam la pɔka buraa zuo.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Dina wa zuo, la suŋereba la zuo, pɔka dela ka pi' ka zuo ti la dɛna daalehum ti nɛra en sɔi en.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: La ka boi la fii me Daana la sore puan, ti pɔka sɔna ka mɛŋa buraa zi'an, buraa me sɔi ka mɛŋa pɔka zi'an.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Wuu pɔka en ze'ele buraa puan na se'em la, bala mɛa ti buraa me zɛ'ɛta pɔka puan na, gee sɛla woo ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Gakɛ ya bo' ya mɛhi nyaa, la de ma'a ti pɔka pu'uha Yinɛ gee ka ti sɛla ka pi' ka zuo?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Naanɛ mɛŋa ka pa'alɛ ya ti buraa han tara zomwogero la ka naaɛ en.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: gee pɔka han tara zomwogero, la dela a suŋa? Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo' zomwogero la ti la dɛna la sɛla en pi' ka zuo la.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Gee se'em han ka sakɛ gee ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene, tumam ka tari malema aŋa bii Yinɛ Lageŋɔ la.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Nananawa, n kan pɛgɛ ya yele sɛ'a en kini na wa puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya han ni lagehɛ taaba na, la ni dagena sum, gee la ni tum dɛna la sageŋɔ.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Mam yeti n pɔhɛ sɛla dela, ya han ni lagehɛ na wuu Yezu lageŋɔ la, mam wum ti yelehego en boi ya puan, ti mam n bihɛ dɛ ta paaɛ zi'an la, mam nam ti la dela bala.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: La de sira, yelehego wan ba'ahum bɔna ya puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, yele dina ma'a ti la wan niiɛ peelumen sɛba n de yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ pu'uheba ya puan.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Ya han ni lagehɛ taaba na, ya ni ka lagehɛ na ti ya di la Daana dia la.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dia la saŋa han ni ta paaɛ, nɛrawoo zugeri la a zuka dia la puan, ti ayima kule kom, ti ayima me buge.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Benii! Ya ka tari yiya ha dita gee nyuura? Bii ya zeeni la Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, gee diiha sɛba n ka tari la vi? N wan yele ya yeti bo? N pɛgɛ ya yoi? Yele dina wa puan, mam kan pɛgɛ ya.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Mam to'e se'ere Daana zi'an la, dinɛ ti mam dikɛ bɔ'ɔra ya. Ti Daana Yezu, yu'uŋɔ seko ti ba wan koohe en la, a dikɛ la boroboro.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
