local: en bo' fara ba'ahɛ la, a figehe de mɛ gee yeti, Dina en de mam iŋa ti n dikera bɔ'ɔra ya. Iti ya dɛna la ya tɛra n yele.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Bala mɛa ti a dikɛ wula, ban di yu'uŋɔ ba'ahɛ la, yeti Wula wa dela mam ziim la nɔpu'ulegɔ paalega. Ita ya dɛna sansɛka woo ti ya yuure de puan, la ya tɛra n yele.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sansɛka woo ti ya ɔbera boroboro wa, gee nyuura wula wa puan, ya tiiheri la Daana la kum gura a lɛbega na.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Bala la, fum se'em woo en obe boroboro wa, gee nyu Daana wula la puan ti la ka nara, wan yele se'ere zuo ti fum galum Daana iŋa la la a ziim.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ba'ahɛ, gee ta'am obe boroboro la gee nyu daam wula la puan.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba woo en diti gee nyuura gee ka ti'iha Daana iŋa la tuulum yele, diti gee nyuura la gakerɛ baha ba mɛhi zuto.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Dina wa zuo ti ya zo'e zo'e ihe buge ti ya bɛ̃'ɛra, ti ya sɛba ki.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Gee tu han gakɛ tu mɛhi suŋa suŋa, ba kan gakɛ tɔ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Gee Daana la han gakɛ tɔ, a dɔlege la tu tuba ti tu da lagum nyɛ sageŋɔ la tiŋa zuo nɛreba.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Bala n suurɔ, ya han ni lagum taaba ti ya di Daana dia la, ni gu'uhi ya taaba.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Ya kom han duna, ni di ya yire, ti ya han ta lagehɛ taaba, la da dɛna sageŋɔ bo' ya. Han dɛna yɛla asɛ'a la yele, n han ta wa'am na, n wan suŋɛ ya ti ya maalɛ.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Mam han pun tana tɔgera saaleba tugum buuri buuri, bii tana tɔgera Yinɛ suŋereba yetɔgum, gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, n ani wuu luŋa en yeen vɔɔla, bii kpankpaŋi en ŋmɛ'ɛri la.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: N me han tara purega bo'olum paŋa, bɔkera sɛla en sugɛ, gee tara sɛlawoo mi'ilum, tara sakerɛ woo wan ta'am vɔ zɔya, gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, n dagi sɛsɛla.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: N han pun dikɛ n tarega za'a bo' nɛreba, gee ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ dikɛ n iŋa tihɛ ti ba nyɔ', gee ka tara nɔŋerɛ, nyuurɔ ka boi bini.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Nɔŋerɛ dela sugeri, nɔŋerɛ dela itesuŋɔ. Nɔŋerɛ ka tari nintɛrega bii pa'alɛ n mɛa, bii nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: bii wurebere, nɔŋerɛ dagi mam boti se'em ti n ita, nɔŋerɛ ka eere n ma'a sum, a ka iti suure tɔtɔ, a ka biŋere be'em a suuren.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Nɔŋerɛ ka nyɛɛni sɛla en dagi ma'a, gee a tugum nyɛɛna mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Nɔŋerɛ uheri mɛ sɛla woo, nana sɛla woo, tara beere puti'irɛ sɛla woo puan, ŋmibera sɛla woo puan.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Nɔŋerɛ ka bã'ahi. Gee han dɛna purega, ka wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ. Han dɛna yetɔgum buuri buuri, bu wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ. Han dɛna mi'ilum me, bu wan wa'am na ba'ahɛ.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Tu yeen mina la fii wa, tu puti la fii wa.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Gee mɛ'ɛ la han diŋɛ wa'am na, fii wa la kan le bɔna.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Mam n daa de bia la, n daa tɔgeri la biine, ti'iha wuu biine, bɔkera wuu biine, gee mam nyaa de bunkutɛ la, mam bahɛ biine mɛ.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Nananawa, tu biheri la biheŋa puan ti la ka niiɛ suŋa, gee la kini na, ti tu wan ta nyɛta kpaii. Nananawa, tu nan mi' la fii, gee la kini na, ti tu wan ta mina za'a, wuu mam ti ba mina mam za'a za'a se'em la.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Gee nananawa, sɛla en pakɛ dela, sakerɛ, beere puti'irɛ la nɔŋerɛ. Bana n wan bɔna wuuha, gee sɛla en gana za'a dela nɔŋerɛ.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Ɛ ya nɔŋerɛ ninmu'urɛ, gee me mu'ɛ nini ti ya nyɛ Sia yɛla, yele yele wuu purega bo'olum.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum la ka tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la nɛreba gee Yinɛ. Beni zuo, nɛra ka boi bɔkera ba. Ban tɔgeri sɛla en sugɛ Sia puan la zuo.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Gee zi'an kayima me, puteba la, tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra la nɛreba, ti la mɛ ba, gee kpemehe ba, gee bugelɛ ba suya.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Se'em en tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum la mɛti la a mɛŋa, gee se'em en puti la mɛti la lageŋɔ la.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Lan de se'em dela, mam wan boti ni ti ya za'a tɔgera yetɔgum buuri buuri la, gee n wan ba' boti ni ti ya puta. Se'em en puti la gani se'em en tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri la mɛ, han dagena nɛra en boi lerege a yetɔgum la, ti bu mɛ lageŋɔ la.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: Bala, n suurɔ, mam han wa'am ya zi'an na tɔgera buuri buuri yetɔgum, n wan suŋɛ ya la ŋwani, han dagena n lilege la se'ere pa'alɛ ya bii mi'ilum bii purega bii pa'alegɔ puan?.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: La dela buyina la bumŋmɛ'ɛla, wuu talitiŋa bii kɔ̃legɔ. Limehego han ka limehe, nɛra wan iŋɛ la ŋwani baŋɛ yuunɛ se'ere ti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra, bii pɛɛbera?.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha me tintanɛ han ka peebe gɔlegɛ, ani n wan maahum yehe pɛntɛrɛ?.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: La ani la bala la ya. Fum han bɔna tɔgera buuri buuri puan, ti nɛra ka bɔkera bɔ, nɛra yeti a iŋɛ la ŋwani baŋɛ fum yeti sɛla? Fum tɔgera bahera la sabeeo puan yoo.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Bu'a ka bo' ti, yetɔgum zo'e zo'e boi timpaberɛ wa zuo mɛ, ti yetɔgum ka bɔna ka tara bu bɔkerɛ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: La han dɛna ti mam ka mi' yetɔgesebo vuurɛ, mam dela saana bo' tɔgera la, ti tɔgera la me dɛna saana bo' mam.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: La ani la bala mɛa la ya, yan kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ nini ɛɛra Sia yɛla la. Zigɛ ya suŋa suŋa la ya dikɛ bu tum suŋa ti la mɛ lageŋɔ la.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Bala, se'em en tɔgeri yetɔgum buuri buuri, a pu'uhɛ nyɛ paŋa la a ta'am lerege de.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam han pu'uha la yetɔgum buuri buuri, mam sia la en pu'uhi, gee mam puti'irɛ ka bɔkeri.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Beni ti n nyaa wan iŋɛ? N wan pu'uha la sia la puan, gee me pu'uha la n puti'irɛ la, gee yuuna pɛka yuuma la n sia la, gee yuum pɛka yuuma la n puti'irɛ la me.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Han dagena bala, fum han ka'ara sum sia la puan, ŋwani ti yiŋa daana wan ta'am yeti Taari bo' fum fara bu'a la, yiŋa daana la n en ka bɔkerɛ fum n yeti sɛla la?.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, han dɛna fum, fum ta'am pɛgɛ Yinɛ mɛ paaɛ paaɛ, gee la ka mɛti ayima.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: Mam bo' Yinɛ fara ti n tɔgeri yetɔge buuri buuri gana ya za'a.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: gee la ka boi la fii me, ti lageŋɔ puan, mam wan tugum tɔgɛ la yɛla anuu la n puti'irɛ ti la ta'am ka'am basɛba, nyana mam tɔgɛ yɛla tuha pia buuri buuri yetɔgum puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: N suurɔ, da deni ya kɔma ya ti'ihegɔ puan, gee tugum deni ya kɔmbilɛɛhi be'em tuuma puan, gee ti'ihegɔ puan deni ya bunkureba.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Yinɛ Gɔnɔ la puan, la gulehɛ yeti, Doohe la nɛreba en tɔgeri buuri buuri yetɔgum puan, Doohe saama nɔgbama puan, Ti n wan tɔgɛ bo' nɛrebana wa, gee la bala za'a, ba kan kelehe mam, Daana la n yele.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Bala yetɔge buuri buuri yi dagi daalehum bo' sakereba, gee sɛba en nan de saama sakerɛ la puan, gee purega dagi sɛba en de saama sakerɛ puan la dinɛ, gee sakereba la dinɛ.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Han dɛna bala, la han iŋɛ ti lageŋɔ la za'a lagehɛ na, ti nɛra woo tɔgera buuri buuri, yiŋa nɛra bii sɛba en dagi sakereba kɛ̃ na, ba kan yeti ya zuto ka tuni?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Gee ya za'a han puta, ti se'em en dagi sakera bii yiŋa nɛra kɛ̃ na, ya za'a n bahɛ ti a nyɛ teere, ya za'a en bahɛ ti a mina en de se'em.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Yelesɛ'a en sugɛ a suure puan la han niiɛ na, nɛra la wan kpa' duma, lu tiŋa aliko Yinɛ nɛŋan, na'ahɛ en, gee muulɛ yeti, Yinɛ sirum boi mɛ la ya.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Beni en nyaa de ya iŋɛ n suurɔ wa? Ya han ni lagehɛ taaba na, ya ayima ayima tara yuunɛ, tara pa'alegɔ, tara lilegere, tara buuri yetɔgum yetɔgum bii tara yetɔge buuri buuri leregere. Iŋɛ ya sɛla woo suŋa ti la mɛ lageŋɔ la.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Se'em ya puan han wan tɔgera yetɔge buuri buuri, ba dɛna bayi bii ba han pun wan zo'e batã. Ba tɔgera sɔɔhena, ti nɛra bɔna ya puan lɛregera ba yetɔgum la.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Gee nɛra han ka bɔna wan lerege yetɔgum buuri buuri la, ba ana suram lageŋɔ la puan, gee tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ba mɛhi la Yinɛ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Bahɛ ya ti sɛba en puti la, bayi bii batã tɔgɛ, ti basɛba me bɔna ti'ihɛ ban tɔgɛ sɛla la bihɛ.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Gee Yinɛ han lilege sɛla bo' se'em me en zi' bilam, bahɛ ya ti se'em en puti yia la yee bããlam.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya woo ta'am puta ayima ayima, ti ya woo zamehɛ ti la kpemehe ya.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: Purega paŋa la boi la puteba la nu'uhi.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dagi Yinse'ere n gbiregeri, gee a dela suma'ahum Yinɛ. Wuu nɛresunduma lageŋɔ la woo puan la iti se'em la.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: pɔgehi dela ba ana bããlam lageŋɔ la woo puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ka bo' ba sore ti ba tɔgɛ, gee ba biŋe ba mɛhi tiŋa wuu pa'alegɔ la n yele se'em la.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Se'ere han bɔna ti ba bɔta ti baŋɛ, ba soke ba sireba yire. La dela nyanɛ ti pɔka tɔgera lageŋɔ puan.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Benii! Bii Yinɛ yelehum la pɔhɛ la yamam zi'an na? Bii yamam ma'a n de sɛba ti yelehum la paaɛ.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Se'em en yeti a dela nɔtɔgeha bii a tari Sia pahi, dela a mina ti mam en guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya wa dela Daana la nuurɛ.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Se'em woo en ka dikɛ yele ana wa yi sɛla, se'em me da dikɛ en yi sɛla.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Bala n suurɔ, mu'ɛ ya nini la purega bo'olum la, da gu'uri ya yetɔge buuri buuri la me.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Gee sɛla woo dela ya ita dɛ suŋa, gee ita dɛ ma'a ma'a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Nananawa, han dɛna la Sia yɛla la, n suurɔ, mam ka boti ya bɔna aliko.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Ya mi' ti, yan daa nan de bageditiba la, ba daa pã'ahɛ ya mɛ, tara ya ti ya ta bɔregɛ, dɔla baga en ka tã tɔgera.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Bala zuo, n boti ya bɔkɛ mɛ ti, nɛra ka boi tɔgera Yinɛ Sia la paŋa puan tabelɛ, Puta baha Yezu zuo. Bii nɛra ta'am yeti Yezu en de Daana, han dagena Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Lan de se'em n ŋwana: Zu'uhegɔ bo'olum la boi la yima yima, gee Saa kayima mɛa en bo'ori.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: ti tuuma la me bɔna yima yima gee ba tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana ayima mɛa.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Ti tuuma yima yima tuna, gee Yinɛ deyina mɛa n bo'ori ba za'a paŋa, ti ba tuna nɛrawoo puan.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: A bo' nɛra woo la Sia tuunɛ Sia tuunɛ, ti nɛra woo nyɛta, nɛra woo sum zuo.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Ayima, Sia la bo' en la yɛm tugum puan, ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa n bo' en mi'ilum tugum puan.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa bo' en la sakerɛ, ti ayima me tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: ti ayima me, Sɛkaŋa mɛa bo' en la kirehi tuuma bo'olum, bo' ayima me purega bo'olum, gee bo' ayima me sɛɛhi baŋerɛ bo'olum, bo' ayima tugum buuri buuri bo'olum, bo' ayima me tugum leregere bo'olum.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Nananawa, bo'olum buna wa za'a, Sia kayima wa n bahɛ ti ba tuna. Sia kayima mɛa n bo'ori nɛra woo a bo'olum a bo'olum wuu Sia la mɛŋa n boti se'em la.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Wuu iŋa en de kayina gee tara luga zo'e zo'e se'em la, ti iŋa la luga la zo'e se'em gee dɛna inyiŋa se'em la, la ani la bala Kiresta puan.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Sɛyiŋa mɛa puan ti ba sɔ tu za'a ko'om, ki'ihɛ inyiŋa puan. Yuuduma, la Girikiduma, da'aba, la sɛba en bo' fai, gee bahɛ ti tu za'a nyu Sɛyiŋa la.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, iŋa la dagi lɔge yiŋɔ ma'a, gee lɔgerɔ zo'e zo'e.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Ti na'arɛ han wan ta'am yeti, Mam n dagi nu'o la zuo, mam ka po pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan, bala kan bahɛ ti a kan pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan.

eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Tuberɛ han wa ta'am yeti, Mam dagi niho la zuo, mam ka po pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan, bala kan ta'am bahɛ ti a kan pa'ahɛ iŋa la puan.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Iŋa la za'a han deni la niho, sɛla n wan wuna bɛɛ? Iŋa la za'a han deni la tuberɛ, sɛla n wan nyuuhera nyuuŋo bɛɛ?.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Gee lan de se'em la, Yinɛ en tigehe lɔgerɔ la za'a iŋa la puan, ayima ayima wuu en boti se'em.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Ba za'a han deni la lɔgeyiŋɔ, iŋa wan boni la bɛ?.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Gee lan de se'em la nananawa la, luga la zo'e mɛ, gee dɛna inyiŋa.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Bala la, niho kan ta'am yele nu'o yeti, Mam ka eere fum, bii zuo yele na'arɛ yeti, Mam ka eere fum.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Gee lan tugum dɛna se'em dela, iŋa la lɔgesɛ'a en ani tɔregɔ la yele pakɛ mɛ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: tu iŋa la lɔgesɛ'a ti tu ti'iha ti ba ka tari tuni la, tu biheri ba mɛ suŋa la na'ahegɔ. Tu iŋa lɔgeseto en ka tari nimpiilum la, tu biheri ba mɛ la na'ahegɔ kãtɛ.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Gee tu ihe lɔgesɛ'a en ba' tari na'ahegɔ la ka eere yele ana. Gee Yinɛ tigehe iŋa la la sadɔseko ti lɔgeseto ti tu ka ba' tɛra tu yele la en tugum nyɛta na'ahegɔ kãtɛ.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: A iŋɛ ŋwana ti bɔgehegɔ da bɔna iŋa luga la puan, gee luga la tugum suŋera taaba.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Iŋa la lugerɛ deyima han nyɛ wuŋerɛ, iŋa la luga la za'a ni lagum nameha mɛ; iŋa la lugerɛ deyima han nyɛ na'ahegɔ, iŋa la za'a ni lagum nyɛɛna mɛ.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Nananawa, ya dela Kiresta iŋa, ti ya ayima ayima dɛna iŋa la luga.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Ti Yinɛ en looe sɛba lageŋɔ la puan n ŋwana, yia, tuntuneba, yi'i, nɔtɔgeheba, ta'a pa'aleba, nyaa bo' kirehi, nyaa bo' tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima, suŋɛ yima yima, nɛheduma yima yima, la tugum buuri buuri bo'olum.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Nɛrawoo dela tuntuna yoi? Bii Nɛrawoo en de nɔtɔgeha? Nɛrawoo dela pa'ala yoi? Bii Nɛrawoo en tuni kirehi?.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Nɛrawoo tari la tibegɔ bo'olum yima yima? Bii Nɛrawoo en tɔgeri zɛlempaala? Nɛrawoo en tã lɛregera zɛlempaala yoi?.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya nini mu'ɛ la bo'olum sebo en tari suŋɛ bo' nɛra woo. Gee n wan pa'alɛ ya sadɔseko en de ma'a gana za'a pa'ahɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Nananawa n wan tiɛ ya n suurɔ, faarɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya la, sɛ'a ti ya to'e, gee sɛ zɛ'a a puan la.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: yelehum sebo puan ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, doohe ya han gurɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya la kankaŋi, han dagena ti ya yeen sakɛ la yoo.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam bo' ya la sɛla n de ninmu'urɛ yele yia ti mam to'e de. De dela, Kiresta ki bo' la tumam be'em, doohe gulehegɔ la.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: ti ba laaɛ en, ti a vo'e dabeha atã daarɛ, wuu lan gulehɛ se'em la.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: ti bilam pooren, ti a niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ Sefase, gee nyaa niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ pia la bayi' la.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Gee nyaa le ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ suurɔ en gani kɔbehi sinuu bɔbeyinɛ, ti ba puan basɛba kelum vɔna la zina, ti basɛba me ki.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Ti a nyaa ta niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ Yemesi, gee niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ a tuntuneba la za'a.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, a niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ la mam se'em en ani woo nɛra en ka bi'ɛ gee ti ba dɔgɛ en la.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam n pɔ'ɛ Yezu tuntuneba la puan, ka nara ti ba yi'ira mam a tuntuna, se'ere n sɔi la, mam n daa dole mɛleha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la zuo, mam dela mam en de se'em la, ti a zu'uhegɔ la ti a dikɛ bo' mam la me ka bahɛ yoo. Gee mam tugum tum mɛ paaɛ paaɛ gaŋɛ ba za'a. La dagi mam mɛŋa paŋa, gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la n boi la mam la.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Bala zuo, la dɛna mam bii bamam, tumam en muulɛ se'em n bala, ti bala mɛa ti yamam me wa'am na nam.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Nananawa, ba han muula ti Kiresta vo'e mɛ a kum puan, ŋwani ti ya basɛba wan yeti, vo'ore ka boi bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Vo'ore han ka bɔna bo' sɛba n ki la, Kiresta yi ka vo'e.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Ti Kiresta me han ka vo'e, tu muulegɔ la ye dela yoo, ti ya sakerɛ la me dɛna yoo.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Tumam pun nyaa dɛna la pumparenduma bo' Yinɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam yeti, Yinɛ vo'e Kiresta mɛ, gee ti a me ka vo'e en, la han sirum dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ ti sɛba n ki, ka vo'ore.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n ki la han ka vo'e, Kiresta mɛ ka vo'e.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Kiresta han ka vo'e, ya sakerɛ la yi dela zaŋa, ti ya kelum bɔna la ya be'em la puan.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: Ti sɛba n ki Kiresta puan la me, bahɛ yoo.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: La han dɛna la tiŋa wa zuo vom ma'a zuo ti tu tara puti'irɛ Kiresta puan, tumam n yi de nimbaalega duma gana nɛra woo.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Gee yelemɛŋerɛ la dela, Kiresta vo'e mɛ kum la puan, dɛna kum vo'ore walediinɛ bo' sɛba woo en ki la.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Wuu kum n doohe asaala puan wa'am na la, bala mɛa ti vo'ore me doohe asaala puan wa'am na.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Se'em woo n yehe Adam puan na n kiiri se'em la, bala mɛa ti se'em woo n yeheri Kiresta puan na wan vɔna.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Gee nɛreba la woo tari la ba sore ba sore ti ba wan vo'e. Kiresta n de walediinɛ la, a lɛbega na, sɛba n de a dinɛ la wan vo'e.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Gee ti ba'ahegɔ la nyaa wa'am na, Kiresta han ta dikɛ sɔ'ɔlum la bo' Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, bala ma'a ti a pun sagum bihereba woo, la kpe'eŋo woo, la paŋa woo en zaberɛ la Yinɛ mɛ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ba'ahum dɛna naba, gura ti Yinɛ dikɛ a dinduma za'a biŋe a nama tiŋa.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: Dima sɛka ti a wan sagum ba'ahegɔ za'a dela kum.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dikɛ sɛlawoo biŋe la Kiresta nama tiŋa. Gee lan yeti, Sɛla woo boi la a nama tiŋa la, la niiɛ mɛ ti, se'em daana en dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ Kiresta nama tiŋa la mɛŋa ka lagum pa'ahɛ bini.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Sɛla woo han dikɛ bo' Kiresta, Kiresta en de Dayua la mɛŋa me wan bɔna se'em daana en dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ a nu'uhen la me nu'uhen, ti Yinɛ nyaa sɔna sɛla woo, gee bɔna sɛla woo puan.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Han dagena bala, nɛrebana wan iŋɛ la ŋwani? Bama sɛba en to'ohe ko'om sua bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la? Han dɛna ti sɛba n ki la kan vo'e, ŋwani ti nɛreba tɔ'ɔha ko'om sua bɔ'ɔra sɛba n ki la?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Gee ŋwani ti tumam pɔ̃hera tu nyuurɔ saŋa woo?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Mam kiiri la daarɛ woo. La dela yelemɛŋerɛ n suurɔ, wuu mam ŋmɛ'ɛri n nyu'ɔ ya zuo se'em la. Nyɔ'ɔ ŋmɛ'sɛka ti mam ŋmɛ'ɛra Kiresta Yezu en de tu Daana la puan.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: La han yeen dɛna la mam puti'irɛ boi la nɛresaala vom wa ma'a puan zuo, ti mam ta zabera la nɛreba en ani wuu muuen duhi Ɛfisu, beni nyuurɔ ti n wan nyɛ ni de puan? La han dɛna ti sɛba n ki la ka vo'ore, Yi bahɛ ya ti tu dita gee nyuura, se'ere n sɔi la, beere ŋwana ma'a ti tu ki ya.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pa'ahɛ ya. Nɛreyaalehi sageni la vom sumɔ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Sigehe ya mɛhi, gee nyɛ putɛ'sunɛ, la ya da le tum be'em. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba basɛba ka mi' Yinɛ. N yeti dɛna wa ti la dɛna la vi bo' ya.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Gee basɛba wan soke yeti, Kum ta vo'ore la ŋwani? Beni ihe taaba ti ba wan tara.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Gerego wa, sɛla ti fum burɛ kan ta'am yehe na, han dagena la pu'ɛ mɛ nyaa bule yehe na.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Sɛla ti fum burɛ la me, fum ni ka burɛ la naanɛ en wan yehe na la, gee fum ni burɛ la biiliho en de wuu, mui, bii biili sɛ'a.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Gee Yinɛ en bo'ori naanɛ la eŋa en boti de se'em. A bo'ori biileho woo mɛ la bu naanɛ.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Dagi naanɛ woo en ŋwani taaba, gee saaleba tari la iŋa kayima, ti duhi me tara iŋa kayima, ti niihe me tara naanɛ deyima, ti zim me tara iŋa kayima.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Saazuo ihe boi mɛ, ti tiŋa wa zuo ihe me bɔna. Gee saazuo ihe la suŋa boi mɛ ti tiŋa wa zuo ihe suŋa me bɔna.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Wuntɛɛŋa suŋa boi mɛ, ti ŋmarega suŋa bɔna, ti ŋmarebibehi me suŋa bɔna. Yelemɛŋerɛ ŋmarebibehi suŋa boi la yima yima la taaba.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Bala mɛa ti la wan ana la kum en wan ta vo'e la. Sɛla en burɛ pu'uri mɛ, gee sɛla ti ba vo'e ka pu'uri.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Ba buti la dɛgerɔ puan, gee vɔ'ɔra suŋa puan. Ba buti mɛ la tɔregɔ, gee vɔ'ɔra la paŋa.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Ba buti la ingɔŋɔ iŋa puan, gee ba vo'ore la sia iŋa puan. Ingɔŋɔ puan iŋa han bɔna, sia puan iŋa me boi mɛ.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: Bala ti la gulehɛ yeti, Yia buraa, Adam la, lebege la asaala, gee pooren Adam la lebege la sɛsɛka en bo'ori vom.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Gee la dagi sia iŋa la en de yia, gee ingɔŋɔ iŋa la. Nyaa bo' sia iŋa la.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Yia buraa la dela la timpaberɛ wa zuo dinɛ, a dela tintɔnɔ nɛra. Yi'i buraa la dela saazuo nɛra.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Wuu tintɔnɔ nɛra la n ani se'em la, bala mɛa ti sɛba n de tintɔnɔ nɛreba la ana. Wuu saazuo nɛra la n ani se'em la, bala mɛa ti sɛba n de saazuo nɛreba la ana.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Wuu tumam n tari tintɔnɔ nɛra la naanɛ se'em la, bala mɛa ti tu wan tara saazuo nɛra la me naanɛ.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: Sɛla ti mam yɛta wa n suurɔ, dela dina wa: Ingɔŋɔ la ziim kan ta'am to'e Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum. Bii sɛla en pu'uri to'e sɛla en ka pu'uri.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Kelehe ya, n wan yele ya sɛla en sugɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ tu za'a kan ki, gee tu za'a wan ta tee.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: saŋa pika pika puan, wuu niho en kpamehɛ la puan, ba'ahegɔ tintanɛ la peebego puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, tintanɛ wan ta peebe, ti sɛba n ki wan vo'e kan le pu'ɛ, gee han dɛna tumam tu inganɛ wa wan tee.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ingɔŋɔ seko en pu'uri wa wan ta tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka pu'uri, ti ingɔŋɔ seko en kiiri wan ta tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka kiiri.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Ingɔŋɔ seko en pu'uri wa han tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka pu'uri, ti ingɔŋɔ seko n kiiri wa ta tee yɛ ingɔŋɔ seko en ka kiiri la, bala ti yetɔge sebo ti ba gulehɛ la nyaa wan wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na. Ba gulehɛ yeti, Ba vole kum mɛ, la nyaŋerɛ.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Kum, fum nyaŋerɛ la bɛɛ? Kum, fum duŋa la bɛɛ.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Kum duŋa dela be'em, ti be'em paŋa dɛna kiha la.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Gee pɛka dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ, se'em n bo'ori tu nyaŋerɛ doohe tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Bala, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ze' ya kankaŋi, da bikeri ya, kɔ'ɔm boni ya Daana la tuuma puan saŋa woo malum pa'ahera, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti Daana la puan ya tuunkãra la kan bahɛ yoo.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Nananawa, lige lagehegɔ la ti ya yeti ya lagehɛ bo' nɛresunduma la, doohe ya mam en pa'alɛ Galatia lageŋɔ duma la se'em la.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Dabeha ayopɔi woo pɔhega daarɛ, nɛra woo ni yehe en nyɛ nyuurɔ se'em la puan biŋe, ti la de ta dɛna ya lagehɛ, n han ta wa'am na.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: N han ta paaɛ na, n wan tum se'em ti ya wan looe la, la gɔnɔ seto n pa'alɛ ti tu mi' en, ti a tari ya bo'olum la kiŋɛ Yerusalɛm.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: La han le nara ti n me kiŋɛ, ba wan kɔlegɛ mam.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: N wan doohe na limehe ya guum n han ta kaaɛ Masedoniya duma ba'ahɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, mam iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n kaaɛ la Masedoniya ba'ahɛ.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: gee daanse'ere n wan bɔna la ya, bii n pun ta bɔna la ya ɔɔrɔ saŋa la puan, ti ya bahɛ mam ti n kiŋɛ mam boti n kiŋɛ zi'an woo.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: N ka boti n nyɛ ni ya nananawa n tɔlega wa puan, se'ere en sɔi la, n boti ni ti n kɛ̃ ni la ya dabeha ayi', Daana la han bo' sore.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: Gee n wan bɔna Ɛfisu ta paaɛ dabeha pinuu de'eŋo la.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, kulenkãtɛ en yu'ɛ bo' mam ti n tum tuuma n tari zuo, gee dinduma en zo'e.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Timoti han ta wa'am na, da iŋɛ ya sɛla ti a zɔta ya dabeem, se'ere en sɔi la, a kɔ'ɔm tuna Daana tuuma la mɛ wuu mam la.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Bala la, da bahɛ ya ti se'em zeem en. Suŋɛ ya en ti a kiŋɛ a sore la la suma'ahum, ti a ta'am wa'am n zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, mam guri en mɛ la suurɔ tuseto la.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Nananawa, han dɛna tu suuɔ Apolos yele, mam yele en mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti a wa'am na bihɛ yamam, la suurɔ tuseto la, gee a pun ka bɔta la fii me ti wa'am na nananawa na. A han ta nyɛ yɔ'ɔ a wan wa'am na.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Biheri ya suŋa, ze' ya kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan, kpemehe ya ya mɛhi, gee kpe'em.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Bahɛ ya ti sɛla woo ti ya ita, ya ita dɛ la nɔŋerɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: Nananawa, n suurɔ, ya mi' ti Stefanase yire duma n de yia duma ti ba tee Akaiya tiŋa puan, gee me kaabɛ ba mɛhi tuna bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: Mam ka'ani ya mɛ ninmu'urɛ ti ya dikɛ ya mɛhi tuna tuun aŋa taaba bɔ'ɔra nɛrebamam taaba, la sɛba woo en tuni gee wɔbegera muregere la ba.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la Stefanase, Fotunatuse la Akaikus kinɛ na la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba sooe la ya na'arɛ.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Ba bugelɛ la mam suure la yamam suure. Bala, to'e ya nɛrebana wa la na'ahegɔ.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Lageŋɔ seto n boi Esiya pu'uhi ya na. Akuila la Pirisika ta ta' la lageŋɔ la en boi ba yire puan la pu'uhi ya suŋa suŋa Daana la puan.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Suurɔ la za'a pu'uhi ya na. Pu'uheri ya taaba la yelemɛŋerɛ kɔkerɛ.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Mam Pɔɔl n gulehɛ pu'uhi kuna la n mɛa nu'o pu'uhera ya na.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ka'be'o dɔla se'em woo n ka nɔŋɛ Daana la. Maranata!.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Daana Yezu zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: Mam nɔŋerɛ bɔna la ya za'a Kiresta Yezu puan.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Bala, mam iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti, n kan le wa'am na bihɛ ya la susageŋɔ.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam han sagum ya suya, ani n boi wan bo' mam pupeelum, han dagi se'em ti mam sagum a suure la?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Mam gulehɛ mam gulehɛ se'em wa la ti, la suŋɛ ti n han ta wa'am na n da nyɛ susageŋɔ sɛba n wan bo' ni mam pupeelum la zi'an. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam nam ya za'a ti, mam pupeelum wan dɛna ya za'a pupeelum.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Mam n gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la nimbaalega, la susageŋɔ, la nintam zo'e zo'e wa dagi la n bahɛ ti ya suya sagum, gee n bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ se'ere ti mam tara bɔ'ɔra ya.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Gee se'em han tari susageŋɔ wa'am na, a ka sagum la mam ma'a suuren, gee fum han bihɛ, dagi la n ba' pa'alɛ gaŋɛ mɛ, gee la lagum paaɛ ya za'a mɛ.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Bala, tuberɛ dɔlegerɛ se'ere ti ya za'a naaɛ nuurɛ dɔlege nɛre-ina taaba tuberɛ wa la seke ya.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Bala nananawa, tugum di ya sugeri bo' en, gee bugelɛ a suure, ti a susageŋɔ la da ba' gaŋɛ en.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Bala, n kɔ'ɔm bɛlena ya mɛ ti ya malum pa'alɛ en ya nɔŋerɛ.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Mam n daa gulehɛ ya la yele dina wa zuo. N makɛ ya bihɛ, gee baŋɛ nyaa ya nane sɛla woo puan.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Se'em woo ti ya di sugeri bo' en, mam me di sugeri bo' en mɛ. Mam n di sugeri bo' sɛla la, wuu n han sirum tari se'em yele n suure wa puan gee di sugeri bo' en, la dela yamam sum zuo, Kiresta nɛŋan.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Tu iti dina wa ti Sagena la n da nyaŋɛ tɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka bu'uri a nyaleŋɔ la.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Mam n paaɛ Torowase tiŋa ti n muulɛ Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, kuleŋa n yu'ɛ bo' mam Daana la puan.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: gee mam puti'irɛ daa ka gã'arɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, n ka nyɛ n suuɔ Taitusi bini. Bala ti mam keehe ba, gee kiŋɛ Masedoniya.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Gee pɛka paaɛ Yinɛ, se'em n dole Kiresta puan saŋa woo bahɛ ti tu kina nyaŋerɛ kinɛ, gee dɔla tumam puan bahɛ ti tu yiregera nyuuŋo suŋɔ seko en boi Kiresta baŋerɛ puan zi'an woo la.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam dela Kiresta nyuunsuŋɔ bo' Yinɛ, sɛba n nyɛ Faarɛ, la sɛba n bɔregɛ tiŋasuka.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Sɛba n bɔregɛ la, nyuuŋo suŋɔ la han ŋmɛ'ɛra bamam, ba malum kiira mɛ. Sɛba n nyɛ faarɛ la me, nyuuŋo suŋɔ la han ŋmɛ'ɛra bamam, ba malum vɔna mɛ. Ani n seke wan ta'am tum tuunɛ wa?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam ka bureni Yinɛ yelehum la, wuu sɛba zo'e zo'e en iti se'em la. Gee Kiresta puan, tumam tɔgeri mɛ yelemɛŋerɛ, wuu nɛreba ti Yinɛ tum tɔ, gee ti tu me zɛ'a a nɛŋan saŋa woo la.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Tumam pɔhɛ ti tu pɛgɛ la tu mɛhi le yɛ'ɛha? Yelemɛŋerɛ, dinɛ ka pakɛ tumam wuu sɛba en iti se'em la. Tu gulehɛ pɛka gɔnɔ bo' yamam, bii yamam gulehɛ pɛka gɔnɔ bo' tumam na?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Yamam mɛhi en de tumam gɔŋɔ, gulehɛ bɔna tumam suya puan, ti nɛra woo wan mina, gee kãalɛ.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Ya mɛhi la mɛ pa'alɛ ya ti ya dela Kiresta gɔŋɔ, ti tumam en de sɛba en maahum kɔ. Tu ka dikɛ zigemɔlegɔ gulehɛ, gee tu dikɛ la Yinɛ vua wa Fa'a Sia la gulehɛ ya. Tu ka gulehɛ iŋɛ la kugelama puan, gee tu gulehɛ iŋɛ la saaleba suya puan.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Tumam en nam Yinɛ se'em en bala Kiresta puan.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Dagi la tumam mɛhi en ta' sɛla ita, wan ta'am yeti ŋwana la ŋwana tumam mɛhi zi'an na; tumam tã'alum ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Yinɛ en de se'em en iŋɛ tumam ti tu dɛna tuntuneba a nɔpu'ulepaalɛ la puan, dagi la pa'alegɔ yoo puan, gee Sia la puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, pa'alegɔ yoo la kuuri mɛ, gee Sia la bo'ori la vom.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Nananawa, kum tuuma yɛla la ba daa kɔrum a iŋɛ labelɛ kugelama puan la, han daa wa'am na na la niihum en kpigeri Iziraleduma la nini ti ba ka tana bihera Moose nɛŋa, kan daa ba' nyagera la zuo, dɛna niihum sebo en nyaa gbe'e biŋe pooren wa, nyaa bihɛ!.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: Niihum kãtɛ boni taaba n wan dɔla Sia tuuma yɛla wa!.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Suŋa kana taaba daa han dɔla sageŋɔ tuuma yɛla la, suŋa kãtɛ en gani bala wan dɔla buurɔ tuuma yɛla.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla n tabeli tara suŋa, suŋa la ni ta bɔregɛ mɛ, doohe la suŋa kãtɛ kayima n wa'am na zuo.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla en kan bɔna wuuha daa han tara niihum buna taaba, sɛla n wan bɔna wuuha la niihum wan gana bala mɛ.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Tumam n diŋɛ tara beere putɛ'dina wa la, tu tuni mɛ la sukpe'ene.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Tu ka ani wuu Moose, n daa dikɛ fuo pi' a nɛŋa ti, Izirale nɛreba la da ba' biha niihum sebo n kan bɔna wuuha la.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Gee ba suya yuum ba' kãbɛ mɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, wa'am na paaɛ zina, ba han wum nɔpu'ulegɔ kɛka la kãalegɔ, sɛla n daa pi' ba nini la kelum pi' ba mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta ma'a puan ti sɛla n pi' ba la wan pilege bahɛ.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la zina me, ba han kãalɛ Moose pa'alegɔ la, tanɛ la kelum pi' ba puti'ira la bala.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Gee se'em n ŋmɛregɛ wa'am Daana la zi'an na, tanɛ la ni pilege mɛ.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Lan de se'em dela, Daana la n de Fa'a Sia la, ti zi'an ti Daana la Sia bɔna, fai n boi bini.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Gee tumam za'a pilehe tu nɛhi bahɛ mɛ, gee biha Daana la suŋa la, wuu biheŋa puan la, ti la tee tu, ti tu tara Yezu naanɛ, zɛ'ɛta suŋa puan malum paara suŋa puan. Yele dina wa ze'eti la Daana la n de Fa'a Sia la zi'an.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Tu mi' ti tiŋa zuo sugeri wa ti tu bɔna de puan wa han ta sagum ba'ahɛ, tu tari yire n ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, yise'ere ti nu'uhi ka mɛ, ka tari ba'ahegɔ saazuo ha.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu tiŋa zuo sugerɛ wa ti tu bɔna de puan tu uheri mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, kɔ'ɔm boti ni ti tu yɛ ni tu saazuo fuo la.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu han yɛ tu saazuo fuo wa, tu kan zɛ'a beele.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Tumam en nan boi tiŋa wa zuo inganɛ wa puan la, ti uheri mɛ la too tu zeero puan, se'ere n sɔi la, tu ka boti tu zɛ'a beele, gee ba tum malum bagɛ tɔ la lɔgerɔ pa'ahɛ. Bala ti Yinɛ vom la wan vole sɛla n pu'uri la.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Se'em n maahum tɔ gura yele dina wa dela Yinɛ, se'em en bo' tu a Sia la ti la dɛna daalehum ti a wan tum la.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Bala, saŋa woo tu tari puti'irɛ mɛ, la tum pun mina ti tu boi inganɛ wa puan ka'lam wa la, tu zaaɛ mɛ la Daana la.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Tu vɔi me la sakerɛ gee dagi sɛla ti tu nini nyɛta.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam nam ya ti tu bahɛ inganɛ wa gee ta bɔna la Daana la.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Bala, tu bɔna inganɛ wa puan, la tu ka boi inganɛ wa puan, tumam lu' tu suya ti tu tigɛ la a nini.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a wan ze'ele Kiresta gakerɛ kuka la nɛŋan, ti nɛra woo to'e a yɔɔrɛ en tum sɛla a inganɛ wa puan. La dɛna sum bii be'em.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Bala, tumam n mi' tu zɔta Daana la vuurɛ n de sɛla la, tu tiregerɛ mɛ ti tu bahɛ ti basɛba me baŋɛ en. Gee han dɛna tumam mɛhi, Yinɛ mi' tumam mɛ suŋa suŋa, n me tari puti'irɛ ti yamam me mi' tu suŋa suŋa ya suya puan.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Tumam ka pɛgeri tu mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ya le yɛ'ɛha yoo, gee tu bo'ori ya la yɔ'ɔ ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ tu zuo, la ya ta'am lebehe sɛba n ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ nyu'urɔ ba yiŋa gee dagi ba suure puan la.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu iŋɛ ya gaafara, tumam zuto han ka tuna, la dela Yinɛ zuo, tu han le dɛna tu mɛhi la dela yamam zuo.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Kiresta nɔŋerɛ la en bo'ori tɔ paŋa, se'ere zuo, tu za'a sakɛ ya ti nɛra ayima n ki bo' nɛrawoo, bala la, nɛrawoo ki mɛ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: Yezu ki bo' la nɛra woo, ti sɛba n vɔ kan le vɔna bɔ'ɔra ba mɛhi gee se'em n ki gee ihege bo' ba la.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Yehe nananawa yɛ'ɛha ha, tu ka le bɛha nɛra inganɛ puan, la tumam n pun tabelɛ mina Kiresta inganɛ puan la, tu ka le malum mina n en sadɔkuŋɔ puan.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Bala, se'em han bɔna Kiresta puan, a dela naanɛpaalega, sɛla woo n de kɛka tole ya, bihɛ, sɛla woo nyaa lebege la paalega.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Dina wa za'a ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an, se'em n dikɛ tu naaɛ la a mɛa doohe Kiresta puan, gee bo' tumam naarɛ tuunɛ wa.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Dina dela, Kiresta puan ti Yinɛ dɔla dikera tiŋa wa za'a naara a mɛa, ka dikera ba be'em taala pagela ba zuo, tum dikɛ naarɛ sɔɔleŋa wa iŋɛ tumam nu'uhen.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Bala, tumam dikɛ la Kiresta na'arɛ, se'ere n sɔi la, Yinɛ beleni ba dɔla la tumam puan. Bala tu yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ Kiresta zuo, ti ya lebe naaɛ la Yinɛ.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam zuo ti Yinɛ iŋɛ en ti a ana wuu a dela be'em daana la, eŋa se'em en ka tabelɛ mina be'em, ti doohe eŋa puan tu wan tara Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ vom la.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Wuu tumam n lagum tuna la en la, ti beleni ya mɛ ti ya da to'e Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la yoo.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yeti, To'ore saŋa la n paaɛ la, mam kelehe ya mɛ. Dabeherɛ se'ere en de n faaɛ ya la, mam faaɛ ya mɛ. Bihɛ, to'ore saŋa la n ŋwana. Bihɛ, zina n de faarɛ dabeherɛ la.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Tumam ka dikeri sɛla bɛŋera se'em sore ti, tuure da bɔna tu tuunɛ wa puan.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Gee tumam en de Yinɛ yamehi la, tu nyɔkɛ tu mɛhi mɛ yɛla zo'e zo'e puan. Ŋmibega kãtɛ, namehegɔ, wuŋerɛ, nɔyalegerɛ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: ŋmɛ'a, paka, gbiregeri, tuunkãra, gɔgara, kom.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: Zulaam, mi'ilum, sugeri, itesuŋɔ, Fa'a Sia puan tuntɔɔnɛ, yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: yelemɛŋerɛ tugum, la Yinɛ paŋa, la tuntɔɔnɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ ti tu zãla zuɔ la gɔbega.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: na'ahegɔ la fakerɛ yɛla, bã'ahi, imma'ahum puan. Ba ita tu wuu tu dela pumpã'ahum duma, gee ti tu dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ duma.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: tu ani wuu nɛra n ka mi' tu la, gee nɛreba mi tɔ mɛ. Tu ani wuu tu kiiri mɛ la gee bihɛ, tɔ vɔ mɛ. Tu ani wuu ba namehɛ tu mɛ la gee ba ka ku tɔ.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Tu ani wuu tu suya n sagum la, gee tu nyɛɛni mɛ saŋa woo. Tu ani mɛ wuu naheduma la, gee me iŋɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba dɛna buntateba, tu ani wuu tu ka tari la sɛla la, gee me tara sɛla woo.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Ti tɔgɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ bo' yamam Korintiduma wa. Tu suya kɔ'ɔm yu'ɛ mɛ bo' ya.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Gu'a ka boi tumam n nɔŋɛ ya se'em la puan, gee la boi la yamam ma'a puan.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Mam n boti sɛla ya zi'an dela, n yeti ya mɛ wuu n kɔma la, ti ya me yu'ɛ ya suya za'a.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Da tari ya mɛhi naara yoo yoo la sɛba n ka dole Yezu. Se'ere en sɔi la, beni nɔyinɛ en boi la sɛla n de tuuntɔɔ la sɛla n dagi tuuntɔɔ? Beni sɔhega n boi lika la niihum tiŋasuka?.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Beni sakerɛ ti Kiresta tara la pumparema? Bii beni ti sakera tara la se'em n dagi sakera?.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Beni nuurɛ ti Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la wan ku la baga zɔŋɔ? Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam dela Yinɛ vua wa pu'uhegɔ yire la, wuu Yinɛ mɛŋa en yele yeti, Mam wan bɔna ba puan, kina la ba, n wan dɛna ba Yinɛ, ti ba me dɛna n nɛreba.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Bala, yehe ya ba puan wa'am na, la ya bɔna ya tɔka la ba. Daana la n yele, da kalum ya dɛkɔ lɔkɔ, gee n wan to'e ya.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: N wan dɛna ya Sɔ, ti ya dɛna n kɔma. Daana la en de Gani Za'a Daana la en yele.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, n de Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, doohe la Yinɛ sumboolum, la Timoti tu suuɔ la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ lageŋɔ la n boi Korinti puan la, la nɛresunduma la woo n boi Akaiya tiŋa puan za'a la.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, la suma'ahum n ze'ele Yinɛ n de Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Sum dɛna Yinɛ n de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ, nimbu'ɔ zɔta za'a Sɔ la, Yinɛ se'ere n de sumbugela woo daana la.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Se'em n bugelɛ tu suya tu namehegɔ wuu puan, ti tumam me ta'am bugelɛ sɛba n boi namehegɔ puan, la sumbugelum sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bugelɛ tumam mɛhi la.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Wuu Kiresta namehegɔ la n yage tumam zuo zo'e zo'e se'em la, bala mɛa ti tumam sumbugehum la me wan yaga ya zuo zo'e zo'e Kiresta puan.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Tumam han nyɛta namehegɔ, la dela yamam sumbugehum la ya faarɛ zuo. Tu han nyɛta sumbugehum, la dela yamam sumbugehum, sebo ti ya nyɛ doohe yan ma'ɛ ya mɛhi, gee ŋmibera namehegɔ seko ti tumam namehera ku puan la.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Tumam n tari beere putɛ'se'ere la ya ka miini. Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mi' ti, yamam n nameheri la tɔ wa, bala mɛa ti ya wan nyɛ tumam sumbugehum la tɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: Tumam ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, la tumam n nyɛ namehɛ seko Esiya tiŋa la, se'ere en sɔi la, la daa ba' wuŋɛ tu mɛ gaŋɛ, ta wɔ̃regɛ tɔ, ti tu pun ka le ta bɔta ti tu vɔna.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, la daa ani tɔ woo ba gakɛ tu mɛ ti ba ku la, gee ŋwana wa en iŋɛ la, daa bahɛ ti tu ka dɛla tu mɛhi, gee Yinse'ere n vo'ore kum la.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Se'em en faaɛ tumam nɔyalegerɛ kum wa puan bahɛ la, kelum faara tu mɛ, eŋa puan tu puti'irɛ dela, ti a wan le faaɛ tɔ bahɛ.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: yamam en naaɛ la tɔ, suŋera tɔ la nupu'uŋmɛ'a la, bala zuo ti a bu tu sum doohe nɛreba zo'e zo'e nupu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la puan. La bahɛ ti nɛreba zo'e zo'e bo' Yinɛ fara bo' tu.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'urɔ mɛ, gee me tara yelemɛŋerɛ tu puti'ira puan ti Tumam tum timpaberɛ wa zuo mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ, la Yinɛ zoolum, gee dagena tiŋa wa zuo yɛm, gee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la, ti ŋwana wa dɛna la yamam wa zuo.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya gana sɛla ti ya wan ta'am kãalɛ gee me bɔkɛ. Mam puti'irɛ dela ya wan bɔkɛ bala ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: Tumam n diŋɛ tara nɔbiŋe-ana wa la, n suurɔ, bahɛ ya ti tu pee tu mɛhi ingɔŋɔ la sia puan galeŋɔ woo puan. Tu bahɛ ti tuulum vom pirɛ, doohe tu Yinɛ zoolum puan.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: Wuu yan pun bɔkɛ tɔ zi'ihi sise'ehi la, ti Daana Yezu daarɛ la, tumam n wan dɛna ya nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a wuu yamam n wan dɛna tumam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a se'em la.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Bo' ya tu zi'an ya suya puan. Tumam ka tuue ya se'em, tu ka galum se'em, tu ka bihɛ ya se'em yoo.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: Mam mi' dina wa la zuo, n boti ni ti n wa'am la yamam zi'an yia na, ti ya ta'am nyɛ zu'uhegɔ sugum nuuren buyi'.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Mam ka yeti ŋwana ti n zɛregɛ ya mɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, mam pun diŋɛ yele yeti, ya boi la tumam suya puan, ti tu wan lagum ki la taaba bii tu lagum vɔna la taaba.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Mam boti ni ti n kaaɛ ya bihɛ mɛ n han ta yɛ'a Masedoniya tiŋa, gee n han ta ze'ele Masedoniya lɛbera na, n le kaaɛ ya bihɛ, gee ti ya bahɛ mam ti n kiŋɛ Yudiya.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: Mam tari ya saŋa woo ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ. Mam tari ya pagera mɛ. Buga buga nyaa pirɛ mam mɛ. Nyɛɛma n pirɛ mam tu namehegɔ la woo puan.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: N daa yeen yɛta mɛ, mam daa yeti ya dina wa la mɛ bii? Mam maaheni n maaheŋɔ dɔla la asaala yoi? Ti n yeen yeti Ɛ̃ɛ ɛ̃ɛ ŋmɛregɛ bilam yeti Aayi aayi? Ze'yiŋa?.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: La tumam n daa pun ta kɛ̃ Masedoniya tiŋa na la, tu ihe wa daa ka nyɛ vo'ohego, gee tu daa kɔ'ɔm nyɛta la namehegɔ zi'an woo. Nɔkpɛ'ɛma la sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ tu puan, la dabeem sebo n boi tumam mɛhi tiŋasuka.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Wuu Yinɛ n kɔ'ɔm dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ daana wa la, tumam en yeti sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya ka tabelɛ dɛna Ɛ̃ɛ la Aayi.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Gee Yinɛ, se'em n bugelɛ se'em suure n sagum la, bugelɛ tu suya mɛ la Taitusi paaɛ na.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ Dayua la, Yezu Kiresta, se'em yelehum ti tumam muulɛ bo' ya la, Silevanuse la Timoti la mam daa ka tari Ɛ̃ɛ la Aayi. Gee a puan, saŋa woo la kɔ'ɔm dɛna la Ɛ̃ɛ.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Gee dagi Taitusi kinɛ la ma'a, gee subugehum sebo ti yamam daa bugelɛ a suure, ti a wa'am na yɛta tɔ yan boti ya nyɛ mam se'em, yan kele se'em, yan mu'ɛ nini se'em la. Ti la bahɛ ti mam me nyɛɛna, kɔ'ɔm kelum nyɛɛna yɛ'a ha.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, Yinɛ nɔbiŋe a woo dela Ɛ̃ɛ. Dina wa zuo, la dole la eŋa puan ti tu ni yeti Taari la, Yinɛ sɔna na'ahegɔ.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: La ha pun dɛna la mam sagum la ya suya doohe la n gɔŋɔ la, mam ka lebere pooren, .

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Gee la dela Yinɛ n sɛ' tumam la yamam Kiresta puan, gee bi'ihɛ tu.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Nananawa, mam nyɛɛni mɛ, ti la dagena la ya suya n sagum la, gee ya susageŋɔ la bahɛ ti ya nyɛ la suteere. Ya nyɛ Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ, bala ti ya susageŋɔ la ka daam tɔ sadɔɔ seko puan.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: doohe la en dikɛ a daalehum la daalɛ tɔ, gee bo' tu a Fa'a Sia tu suya puan ti la dɛna a yia lɔkɔ en sɛ tɔ ze'ele.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ bo'ori la suteere se'ere en bo'ori faarɛ ti pooren lɛbega ka bɔna. Gee tiŋa zuo susageŋɔ bo'ori la kum.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Gee mam yi' la Yinɛ ti a dɛna mam aze'nyɛ daana, ti ya da nyɛ susageŋɔ zuo, ti n daa ka le wa'am Korinti na la.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Bihɛ ya yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ zoolum susageŋɔ wa iŋɛ ya puan. Beni ninmu'urɛ ti ya ka mu'ɛ ti ya yehe ya mɛhi, beni sigehe mɛŋa, beni pakerɛ, beni boolum, beni zika, beni namehegɔ. Sadɔɔ seko woo puan ya pa'alɛ mɛ ti ya ka tari tuure yele la puan.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: Mam yeti se'em wa dagi la n boti n ba' dikɛ la zeero pa'ahɛ ya sakerɛ la puan, gee la tugum dɛna la tu dela tuntuneba la ya, ya tara nyɛɛma zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya ze' mɛ kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
local: Bala, la mam en pun gulehɛ bo' ya na la, la dagi tu guleheri la se'em n tuue la yele, bii se'em ti ba tuue la yele, gee tu boti tu bahɛ ti ya ninmu'urɛ se'ere ti ya tara la tumam la n niiɛ bo' ya Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Dina puan ti tu nyɛta suma'ahum. Sɛla en pa'ahɛ tumam mɛhi subugehum la puan dela, tu kelum kɔ'ɔm nyɛɛna mɛ la Taitusi. Se'ere en sɔi la, a suure gã'arɛ la ma'ahum, yamam za'a zuo.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Mam han daa dikɛ ya ŋmɛ' la n nyu'ɔ sadɔɔ seko puan boi en, bu nyanɛ ka di mam. Gee sɛla woo ti tu tɔgera bɔ'ɔra ya en de yelemɛŋerɛ la, tum ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ se'em bɔ'ɔra Taitusi la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Sɛla me en boi dela tu boti ya baŋɛ mɛ tu suurɔ, doohe la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la ti a bo' lageŋɔ seto woo en boi Masedoniya tiŋa la.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Ti a ka'ɛ a suure za'a za'a bɔ'ɔra ya na, en ti'iheri ya za'a n nane se'em, la yan to'e en se'em la Yinɛ zoolum dabeem, la gbi'a se'em la.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: La, ban daa pun nyɛ namehegɔ kãra, nyɛ nɔŋɔ pɛɛlega la, ba nyɛɛma kãtɛ la ti ba tara la bahɛ ti ba bo' mɛ zo'e zo'e.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: La bahɛ ti mam puurɛ pee mɛ la ya, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ba' nam ya mɛ za'a za'a.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: Mam nini en nyɛ se'ere la, ba za'a iŋɛ la ba suure la bu'a, bɔ'ɔra ban tari sɛla, pun ta bo' gaŋɛ ban tari sɛla la.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: Pun ta bɛlena tumam ninmu'urɛ la ban nyɛ yɔ'ɔ seko ti ba lagum bo' sɛla tuunɛ wa puan suŋɛ nɛresunduma wa la.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: la yele dina me, dagi ti tumam en yeen daa ti'iha, gee ba dikɛ la ba mɛhi bo' Daana la yia, gee nyaa dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' tumam me wuu Yinɛ suboolum n de se'em la.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: ti tu ta'am tɔgɛ la Taitusi yeti, wuu eŋa n pun pɔhɛ la, a kɔ'ɔm ba'ahɛ pupeelum bo'olum tuunɛ wa ti ya dikɛ wa.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Nananawa, yamam en zo'ore sɛla woo puan se'em la, sakerɛ, tugum, mi'ilum, yiŋa yɛla ninmu'urɛ, la yan nɔŋɛ tu se'em la, bala mɛa ti tu bɔta ti ya me zo'e la pupeelum bu'a wa puan.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: Mam ka yeti dina ti la dɛna la n bo' ya la nuurɛ, gee n boti n dikɛ la ya yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ la makɛ la basɛba ninmu'urɛ.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam mi' pupeelum bo'olum sebo ti tu Daana Yezu Kiresta bo' doohe la en pun dɛna buntata la, gee yamam zuo ti a lebege naha, ti a nɔŋɔ la puan yamam wan dɛna buntateba.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Yele dina wa puan ti mam zɛ'ɛta bɔ'ɔra ya kã'aŋɔ. La dela de sore bo' yamam sɛba n pɔhɛ yuunɛ n tole ha iŋɛ sɛla la. La dagi la ya yeen iŋɛ la sɛla, gee ya suya n kɔ'ɔm nɔŋɛ ti ya ita sɛla la.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: Bala, kɔ'ɔm tum ya de ba'ahɛ, ti ya ninmu'urɛ la doohe la yan tum de ba'ahɛ se'em la yan tari sɛla la.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ninmu'urɛ han bɔna, ba to'ore nɛra woo n tari sɛla ti a bo' la mɛ, gee dagi nɛra n ka tari sɛla.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: Mam yeti se'em wa dagi la n bahɛ ti ya sɛba zɛ'a fai gee ti la wuna ya sɛba, gee sɛla woo dɛna ma'a ma'a bo' ya.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: yamam tarega nananawa wan bɔ'ɔra bamam ti bamam me tarega wan ta tee bɔ'ɔra yamam me, ti sɛla woo ti sɛla woo dɛna ma'a ma'a.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em yeti, Se'em n tari zo'e, ka ba' tara gaŋɛ, ti se'em n tari fii me ka ba' tara ti la pɔ'ɛ la.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Gee fara bo'ori la Yinɛ, se'em n kalum Taitusi suure, ti a nini mu'ɛ la ya wuu mam mɛŋa nini en mu'ɛ la ya se'em la.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi la a yeen to'e la tumam suhega la ma'a, gee a nini mu'ɛ mɛ gana saŋa woo, ti a mɛŋa daa pun maahum wa'am ya zi'an na la.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Tu tuni Taitusi mɛ la tu suuɔ kuyima en de nɛre kãtɛ tara zuo lageŋɔ la woo puan, muula faarɛ yelehum la mɛ.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Gee dagi bala ma'a, gee lageŋɔ la looe en mɛ ti a lagum ɛɛra la tɔ, tumam en dole ka'ahera nɔŋerɛ bo'olum tuuma wa, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bɔ'ɔra tu Daana la mɛŋa, gee me pa'alɛ tu susunɛ wa.
