eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Tu iŋɛ tu puti'ira mɛ ti se'em da zɛregɛ tɔ doohe pupeelum bo'olum wa ti tu ka'ahera wa.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Tumam iŋɛ tu puti'ira ti tu tum la sɛla n de ma'a, dagi la Daana la ma'a nɛŋan, gee saaleba nɛŋan me.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Bama en ye' na la, tu tuni la tu suuɔ seko ti tu viihɛ en saŋa zo'e zo'e puan, nyɛ ti a dela ninmu'urɛ daana yɛla zo'e zo'e puan, gee a nini nyaa pun mu'ɛ gana yia la mɛ la en nam ya paaɛ paaɛ se'em la.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Han dɛna Taitusi, eŋa dela mam zɔ la mam tuntuna tadaana ya tuuma la puan, gee han dɛna tu suurɔ seto en dole la, bamam dela lageŋɔ la bitunehi, ti Kiresta nyɛta pɛka.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Bala zuo la, ze'ele ya lageŋɔ la nɛŋan la ya pa'alɛ ba ya nɔŋerɛ, la se'ere zuo ti tu tari ya ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ la.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Bala, lan diŋɛ dɛna Yinɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ zuo ti tu bɔna tuun-ana wa puan la, tu yɛm ka yɔ'ɔri.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Tumam zagehɛ nyanɛ tuunsɛ'a ti se'em sugera la mɛ. Tumam zagehɛ mɛ ti tu kan tuna pã'ahegɔ tuuma bii tu burum Yinɛ yelehum la. Gee tumam kɔ'ɔm tɔgera la yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛrawoo nɛŋan la Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Tumam yelehum wa han pun pi' mɛ, bu pi' la sɛba en baheri yoo la zi'an.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Ba yele la dela, tiŋa wa zuo yinɛ la n pi' sɛba n ka nam Yezu la puti'ira, ti a gu ba ti ba da nyɛta faarɛ yelehum niihum la n de Kiresta suŋa la. Kiresta se'em n de Yinɛ mɛŋa naanɛ la.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam ka muuli la tu mɛhi yele, tu muulɛ la Yezu Kiresta se'em n de Daana la yele. Tumam mɛhi dela yamam yamehi Kiresta zuo.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela Yinse'ere n daa yeti, Bahɛ ti niihum ze'ele lika puan niiɛ na la, Yinɛ dinɛ mɛa n niiɛ tumam suya puan, ti a bo' tu a mi'ilum niihum la, n de Yinɛ suŋa niira Yezu Kiresta nɛŋa puan la.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Gee tumam tari tuulum lɔgerɔ tuna wa la yɔgerɔ kaleŋa puan, ti la niiɛ kpaii ti pankãtɛ kana wa n gani za'a wa dela Yinɛ paŋa gee dagi tumam paŋa.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Tu nyɛ namehegɔ mɛ bɔberɛ woo gee ku ka ku tɔ. Tu nɔya maam mɛ gee tu suya ka gu'uhi.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Tu nyɛ mɛlehegɔ, gee ba ka bahɛ tɔ, ti ba ŋmɛ' ta lobe gee ba ka sagum tɔ;.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: saŋa woo ti ziiri la Yezu kum tu ihe wa puan ti, Yezu vom la me wan niiɛ tu ihe la puan.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam n vɔ wa, saŋa woo tumam ka gagɛ kum Yezu zuo, ti Yezu vom la wan ta'am niiɛ tu ihe wa puan.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Ŋwana wa bahɛ ti kum n zabera tumam daare woo, gee ti vom bɔna la yamam.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Wuu tumam en lagum tara sia kayima tu sakerɛ puan doohe la gulehegɔ la n yeti, Mam nam mɛ, ti n tɔgɛ. Bala mɛa ti tumam me nam gee me tɔgɛ.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mi' ti se'em n vo'e Daana Yezu la wan vo'e tumam me na naaɛ la Yezu, gee tari tumam la yamam paaɛ a nɛŋa.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla woo dela yamam zuo, ti zu'uhegɔ la, n pa'aherɛ se'em la paaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e, ti pɛka malum zo'e pa'ahɛ ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ nyɛ pɛka.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Bala la, ti ka kɔ'ɛ suure, la tu inganɛ wa n namehɛ ta ana wuu ka pu'uri me wa, tu puan naanɛ la lebegere la paalega beere yilegeri woo.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, namehegɔ pika wa n kan yuue wa maaheni tu mɛ ti tu ta'am to'e vom kamba'ahɛ sunsɛka n tibegɛ ka tara makerɛ la.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Tumam n ka biheri sɛla n nyɛti, gee tu biheri la sɛla n ka nyɛti zuo, sɛla n nyɛ zina ka yuure, gee sɛla n ka nyɛti ka tari ba'ahegɔ.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Nananawa, la ka pakɛ ti n gulehɛ Nɛresunduma tuuma la yele bo' ya.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' ti ya ninmu'urɛ la n de mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la ti mam tara ya ŋmɛ'ɛra bɔ'ɔra Masedoniya tiŋa nɛreba yeti, Akaiya pun maahum mɛ yuunɛ n tole ha la, ti yamam ninmu'urɛ la n gbirege nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Gee mam tuni tu suurɔ wa na ti, tu nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la ti tu tara ya ŋmɛ'ɛra la kan dɛna zaŋa dina wa puan. Bala la, ya wan maahum, wuu mam n pun yele se'em ti ya wan maahum la.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Han dagena bala, Masedoniya nɛreba basɛba han doohe la mam wa'am na nyɛ ti ya ka maahum, la wan dɛna vi bo' tu, mam pun kan yele ya se'ere yele dina puan.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Bala ti mam ti'ihɛ ti la nari ti n yele suurɔ wa ti ba diŋɛ nɛŋa wa'am ya zi'an na, la ba te'ele maahum bo'olum kãtɛ wa ti ya biŋe nuurɛ ti ya bo' wa, ti bu dɛna pupeelum bo'olum gee dagi pɛregerɛ bo'olum.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Lan de se'em la dela, Se'em daana n buti yaara yaara, kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la yaara yaara, ti se'em me n buti yabi yabi me kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la yabi yabi.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Nɛrawoo bo' wuu en maahum a suure ti a bo' se'em la. Dagi la a bɔ'ɔra la susageŋɔ bii a bɔ'ɔra la pɛregerɛ. Beni zuo, Yinɛ nɔŋɛ la se'em en bo'ori la pupeelum.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Ti Yinɛ wan ta'am bo' ya maalegɔ sɛlawoo puan ti la zo'e gaŋɛ, bala ti saŋa woo ya wan tara sɛla woo ti la seke, ti ya ta'am tɔrɛ ti la zo'e ya itesuŋɔ wuu puan.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Wuu lan gulehɛ se'em ti, A bo'ori la yabi yabi, a bo'ori naheduma mɛ, a tuuntɔɔ tuuma la boi la wuuha.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Yinɛ se'ere en bo'ori bumbuura ti ba buta, gee bɔ'ɔra buum ti ba dita la, wan bo' ya, gee nɔbɛ ya bumbuura ti ya wan burɛ, gee nɔbɛ ya tuuntɔɔ vom wala la ti ya kɛ̃' zo'e zo'e.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Yamam za'a wan pãrɛ mɛ sɛla woo puan, la ya pupeelum bo'olum kãtɛ wa n wan bahɛ ti tumam ze'ele tu nama zuo bo' Yinɛ fara fara.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, yamam en tum tuun-dina wa la, dagi la ya bo' la nɛresunduma la ma'a, gee bu ɔlegeri mɛ la fara bu'a zo'e zo'e bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Doohe la tumam en makɛ ya bihɛ la tuun-dina wa taaba la, ya bo' Yinɛ yu'urɛ pɛka mɛ la yan nam gee sakɛ bo' Yezu faarɛ yelehum la se'em la, la ya pupeelum tɔrega la ti ya tɔrɛ la ba, la basɛba me se'em la.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Ba nɔŋɛ ya, gee pu'uha bɔ'ɔra ya la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ seko en ka tari makerɛ ti Yinɛ ka'ɛ bahɛ ya la zuo.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Fara fara dɛna Yinɛ dinɛ la a bo'olum sebo n ka tari makerɛ wa.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ ti ya wan lagum ŋmibe la mam ti tu tum gɔɔntuuma fii wa. Ya gaafara, Yeen ŋmibe ya la mam.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: Mam tari la Yinɛ nyuure ya zuo paaɛ paaɛ se'ere n sɔi la, mam biŋe la nuurɛ ti n dikɛ ya elehe sira ayima. N dikɛ ya wuu pɔgesa'kaherɛ la bo' Kiresta.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Gee dabeem n tari mam. Wuu kampia la n yuum pa'ahɛ Eva la a wikerɛ la, bala mɛa ti yamam me puti'ira wan yehe, ti ya kan le tara ninmu'urɛ la susunɛ pu'uha Kiresta.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han wa'am na muulɛ Kiresta kayima n dagi sɛka ti tumam muulɛ bo' ya la, bii ya to'e la sia kayima n dagi sɛka ti ya pun to'e la, bii faarɛ yelehum sebo en dagi sebo ti ya pun to'e la, ya me ni sirum to'e a mɛ la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ ti mam dagi bia tuntuneba sɛba n iŋɛ ba mɛhi ami' za'a duma wa nɛŋan.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Daanse'ere n ka mi' la tugum gee dagi mi'ilum. Yelemɛŋerɛ, sadɔɔ seko woo puan, la sɛla woo puan, tu tum pa'alɛ ti tu mi' mɛ.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Mam tum la be'em la mam sigehe en mɛa, ti yamam tee zom saazuo la, la mam n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya zaŋa la?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: Bala, Mam fa' lageŋɔ tuseto, doohe la mam sakɛ to'e suŋɛ bamam zi'an ti n ta'am wa'am na tum bo' yamam la.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Mam n daa boi la ya ti nɔŋɔ tara mam la, mam daa ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya se'em, se'ere en sɔi la, mam n daa boti sɛla woo la, tu suurɔ seto n daa ze'ele Masedoniya tiŋa na la en daa bo' mam. Bala, mam yehe n mɛa gee wan kelum yehe n mɛa, kan le dikɛ n mɛa ziile ya sadɔseto woo puan.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Wuu Kiresta yelemɛŋerɛ la n boi mam puan se'em la, mam yele dina wa n de mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a wa kan yee suram Akaiya tiŋa nɛreba puan.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Ti beni zuo? La dela mam ka nɔŋɛ ya mɛ bii? Yinɛ ma'a en mi'.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Mam n iti se'em la, n wan kelum ita la bala mɛa, la n da bo' sore ti sɛba n boti ba nyɛ sore bahɛ ti ba baŋɛ ba ti mam la bamam zuni me la sore.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Bama sɛba n ŋmɛ'ɛri ba nyu'urɔ bala la dela pumparenduma. Ba dela pa'ahegɔ tuntuneba, ba yeen iŋɛ la ba mɛhi ti bamam dela Kiresta tuntuneba.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: La dagi pakerɛ, Sagena la mɛŋa iŋɛ a mɛŋa la bala ti eŋa dela yelemɛŋerɛ suŋera.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Bala, la dagi pakerɛ, a tuntuneba la me han iŋɛ ba mɛhi ti bamam dela tuuntɔɔ vom la tuntuneba. Ba ba'ahegɔ wan doohe mɛ la ba tuuma la.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: Bahɛ ti n le takɛ pa'ahɛ. Da bahɛ ti ya se'em ti'ihɛ ti mam dela gɔɔŋɔ. Gee ya han sina gee iŋɛ bala, yi yeen to'e ya mam bala ti n dɛna gɔɔŋɔ, ti mam me ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ fii wa.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Mam yeti nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a seko wa, seko ti mam sɛ zɛ'a ku puan wa dela, n yeti dina wa mɛ dagi la Daana la paŋa puan, gee gɔɔnɛ puan.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Nɛreba en diŋɛ ŋmɛ'ɛra ba nyu'ɔ nɛresaalenɛ puan la, mam me wan ŋmɛ' la bala mɛa.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya pee la puurɛ ŋmibera la gɔɔnɔ, gee dɛna yɛmduma ya mɛhi.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Ya ŋmiberi mɛ la se'em woo en dikɛ ya lɛbegera a da'aberɛ, bii se'em n yeti a obe ya, bii a bihɛ ya yoo, bii zɛkera a mɛŋa, bii lɔbera ya pɛgehi.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: La tari mam la vi, la mam n yeti ya dina wa. Tu ka kpe'em gee bahɛ ti yele ana wa ita tu puan. Gee yelese'ere woo puan ti se'em wan ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, n yeti la gɔɔnɛ yɛla la puan, mam me wan ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ a zuo.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Ba dela Hiburu duma? Mam me dela bala. Ba dela Iziraleduma? Mam me dela bala. Ba dela Aberaham tuuhum? Mam me dela bala.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Ba dela Kiresta tuntuneba? N nyaa kɔ'ɔm tɔgera mɛ wuu zɔlegɔ la, han dɛna bala mam yi gani ba. Namehɛ gana ba, kɛ̃ paka deto puan gana ba, to'ohe ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ en ka tari kãlɛ gana ba, saŋa zo'e zo'e, pɔ̃hɛ n nyuurɔ gana ba.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Sugum nuurɛ bunuu ti n to'ohe Kpa'aha deyina ka boi kpa'aha pihinaahi,Yuuduma la nu'uhen.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Nuurɛ butã ti ba ŋmɛ' mam la kuto kpa'aha. Buyina ti ba lobe mam la kuga. Butã' ti uureŋɔ wɔ̃rɛ la mam ko'kãtɛ puan, ti n bɔna ko'om la zuo bala yu'uŋɔ la beyiine.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: Saŋa woo, mam boi kinɛ zuo, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ kulaahi puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la nageyigehi, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la n mɛŋa nɛreba, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la sɛba n dagi Yuuduma, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ tinkãra puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ muɔ puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ ko'kãtɛ puan, pɔ̃hera n nyuurɔ la suurɔ seto n de pumparenduma.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: Namehegɔ la tuunkãra yu'uŋɔ, gɔgara dabeha zo'e zo'e, kom la ko'nyuuro, saŋa zo'e zo'e ka tara wan di, ɔɔrɔ la beele puan.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Gee bala gã'arɛ kinkɛleŋa, mam en de se'em ti mugehego gã'a n zuo mam mu'ɛ nini la lageŋɔ duma la za'a la zuo.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Ani n de tɔregɔ daana, ti mam dagena tɔregɔ daana? Ani ti ba pirɛ ti a lu, ti bugum ka dita mam?.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Mam han wan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ, mam ŋmɛ'ɛri mɛ la yele sɛ'a en bahɛ ti mam tɔregɔ niira la.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Se'em en de Yinɛ, la Daana Yezu Sɔ la, la, mi ti mam ka pareni.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Damasekuse tiŋa puan, Aretase en wa dɛna timbihega nɛŋa daana saŋa la, ba wa bahɛ ti gureba n gura Damasekuse tiŋa nayahi la za'a, ti ba ta'am nyɔkɛ mam.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Gee ba yuum dikɛ mam iŋɛ la pi'ɔ puan, dikɛ mam doohe yoko pika puan labelɛ la nangoone la bala ti mam ta paaɛ tiŋa piiɛ a nu'uhen.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Mam mɛŋa Pɔɔl, mam beleni ya mɛ doohe la Kiresta ma'ma' la a uhega la puan, mam se'em n ni sigehe n mɛŋa n han ni zɛ'a ya nɛŋan, gee kpe'em bo' ya n han ni zaaɛ la ya.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: Mam beleni ya mɛ ti mam han ni bɔna la ya, n dagi n kpe'em la ya, la n bɔta ti n da sakɛ bo' sɛba n ti'ihɛ ti tu iti sɛla woo la nɛresaalenɛ puan la.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu dela nɛresaaleba, gee tumam ka zaberɛ woo nɛresaaleba n zaberɛ se'em la.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: se'ere n sɔi la, tumam zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la yeen dagena la nɛresaala zaberɛ lɔgerɔ, gee tu tari la Yinɛ paŋa en wan ta'am sagum pahi zi'ihi.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: Tu sageni nɔkpɛ'ɛma mɛ la pirega lɔgeseko woo n zaberɛ la Yinɛ mi'ilum la, gee nyɔgera putɛ'sɛ'a woo n boi ti ba nana Kiresta.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Tu maahum mɛ ti tu namehɛ sɛba woo n ka nane la, yamam han nam ba'ahɛ.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Biheri ya la sɛla n ze' ya nɛŋa wa, yamam han nam ti ya dela la Kiresta dinɛ, yi tiiheri ya ya mɛhi yeledina wa, ti wuu yan n de Kiresta deni la, tumam me dela bala mɛa.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Nananawa, mam han pun ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ zo'e fii wa, la pan-sɛka ti tu tara, ti tu Daana la dikɛ bo' tu ti ka mɛta tɔ gee dagi ka aahum tɔ wa la, mam kan tara vi la ka.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: Mam ka boti la ana wuu n zigeri ti n vuuhe ya mɛ dɔla n gulehegɔ wa puan.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yeti, A gɔnɔ tibegɛ mɛ gee kpe'em, gee a han zɛ'a ya nɛŋan, a ka tari paŋa ti a yetɔgum la me ka tara tibehum.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: Bahɛ ya ti nɛrebana wa baŋɛ ti, tum yeti ya sɛla gɔŋɔ puan, tu han ka bɔna la ya la, tu wan yele la bala mɛa tu han bɔna la ya.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Tumam ka sakɛ dikɛ tu mɛhi pa'ahɛ bii makɛ la sɛba n pɛgeri ba mɛhi la. Gee bamam han mageha ba mɛhi, tara ba mɛhi giila la taaba, ba ka pa'alɛ ti ba tari yɛm.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Tumam en de se'em la, tu ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ zɔ'ɔra tu tɛka beene, gee tumam wan bɔna la ze'sɛka beene ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu la, tu dela tu paaɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ta ta' la yamam.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Tumam ka yakɛ tu beene la tumam n daa paaɛ yamam la Yinɛ yelehum la. Tumam n de yia duma ze'ele zi'an n zaaɛ wa'am ya zi'an na la Kiresta faarɛ yelehum wa.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Tumam ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ zɔ'ɔra tu tɛka beene, de vuurɛ dela, tu ka ŋmɛ'ɛri tu nyu'ɔ la basɛba tuuma, gee tumam puti'irɛ dela, wuu ya sakerɛ la n zo'ore la, tumam n tuni zi'an la ya la, wan yalegɛ pa'ahɛ.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: ti tu ta'am muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa tinse'ehi en zɔɛ yamam, gee kan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la tunsɛ'a n pun tum ayima tuuma zi'an.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Bahɛ ya ti se'em en ŋmɛ'ɛrɛ nyu'ɔ a ŋmɛ'ɛra kɔ Daana la puan.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi sɛba en pɛgeri ba mɛhi ti Yinɛ to'e ba, gee sɛba ti Yinɛ mɛŋa pɛgera la.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: La ani suŋa ti tu ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ, gee nyuurɔ ka boi bini tɔ'ɔra. Gee mam wan kelum ŋmɛ'ɛra n nyu'ɔ mɛ la mam en nyɛ sɛla, la Daana la en lilegere sɛla la puan.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: Mam mi' buraa kayima en wa dɔla Kiresta sore wa. Ti yuumpia la anaahi n tole ha, ti sɛla yuum zɛ en zom la tinsuŋan ha. La yuum dela sia puan, bii inganɛ puan, mam ka mi', Yinɛ ma'a n mi'.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Gee mam mi' ti nɛre-ina taaba wa, a boi la inganɛ puan bii sia puan, mam ka mi'. Yinɛ ma'a n mi'.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Sɛla en yuum zɛ en zom zi'suŋan ha , ti a ta wum yɛla n dagi ba tɔgɛ bii asaala tɔgera a yele.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Buraa kana wa zuo ti mam wan ze'ele ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ, gee han dɛna mam mɛŋa ma'a, mam kan ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ, han dagena n ti'iha n tɔregɔ yɛla ma'a.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Gee mam han bɔta ti n ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ, mam kan dɛna gerego, se'ere en sɔi la, mam nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a la wan dɛna la yelemɛŋerɛ, tɔgera la yelemɛŋerɛ la. Gee mam zagehɛ mɛ ti n kan ŋmɛ nyu'ɔ, ti se'em da ti'iha mam yele gana en nyɛti sɛla bii en wum se'ere mam puan la.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: A daana han pun biha mam nyɛ lilegere se'ere en boi deyima la. Bala, sɛla n wan gu' mam ti mam kan ba' zɛkera n mɛŋa la dela, gũ'a n dikɛ lu' mam inganɛ puan, dɛna Sagena la bituneŋa, ti a nameha mam, la a gu' mam ti n da ba' zɛkera n mɛŋa.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Sugum nuuren butã ti mam suhɛ Daana la yele wa zuo ti de bahɛ mam.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: gee Daana la yele mam yeti, Mam zu'uhegɔ la seke bo' fum, se'ere en sɔi la, paŋa niiri ka mɛŋa la tɔregɔ puan. Bala, mam wan tum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ mɛ la nyɛɛma n tɔregɔ la puan, bala ti Kiresta paŋa la wan bɔna n puan.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Bala zuo la, mam nini tigɛ ya la tɔregɔ, tuurɛ, wuŋerɛ, mɛlehegɔ, la nɔyalegerɛ Kiresta zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, sansɛka woo ti tɔregɔ kɛ̃ mam, mam ni nyɛ la paŋa.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: Mam dela gerego. Yamam n pɛregɛ mam kɛ̃ bini. Yelemɛŋerɛ, yamam n tugum deni sɛba en wan pɛgeri ni mam, se'ere en sɔi la, mam dagi bumbila sadɔɔ seko puan bo' sɛba en zɛkeri ba mɛhi ti bamam dela tuntuneba wa zi'an, la mam pun dagena sɛla wa.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Daalehum sebo n pa'ali ti nɛra dela Yezu tuntuna yelemɛŋerɛ la daa tum ya puan mɛ la nyɔkɛ n mɛŋa. Nyalema la kirehi la tuunkãra.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Mam tum la be'em boni bo' ya lageŋɔ wa gana lageŋɔ seto woo en boi la, han dagena mam n ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya wa ma'a? Dina wa han dɛna la tuure, yi di ya sugeri bo' mam de zuo.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Mam n ŋwana, maahum ti n wa'am ya zi'an na sugum nuuren butã wa. N me kan dɛna zeero bo' ya, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka boti sɛla en de ya dinɛ, gee n boti la yamam mɛhi. Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi kɔma en de ba bɔ biŋe ba dɔgereba, gee dɔgereba n de ba bɔ biŋe ba kɔma.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Mam wan yuregɛ n lɔgerɔ la pupeelum, gee bahɛ ti ba yuregɛ mam mɛŋa pa'ahɛ bo' ya. Mam han nɔŋɛ ya zo'e ŋwana, yamam dela ya nɔŋɛ mam fii yoi?.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Yi mini ya suŋa, ti mam daa ka dikɛ n mɛŋa ziile ya. Gee la bala za'a, mam wikɛ mɛ, n daa pa'ahɛ zu ya mɛ.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Mam daa fa' ya mɛ doohe sɛba ti n tum ya zi'an na la puan yoi?.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: Mam daa belum la Taitusi ti a wa'am na, gee tum tu suuɔ la pa'ahɛ a puan. Taitusi daa ka fa' ya, bii a daa fa' ya mɛ? Tu daa ka tari tu mɛhi mɛ sia kayima puan? Tu daa ka tari la putɛ'yinɛ?.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Yamam ni tugum ti'iha ti tumam tɔgeri pɔ'ɔra la tu mɛhi wa? Tumam tɔgeri la yelemɛŋerɛ Kiresta puan, Yinɛ nɛŋan. Sɛla woo ti tumam ita, n suurɔ wa, la dela ya zo'ore zuo.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: Dabeem en tari mam, ti n han wa'am na, n kan nyɛ ya ti ya ana wuu mam boti ni ya ana se'em la, ti yamam me kan nyɛ mam me ti n ana wuu yamam n boti se'em la. Dabeem n tari mam ti daanse'ere gaka, nyuure, sunsunyiim nyika, ukerɛ, la gbirege wan bɔna ya puan.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: Dabeem n tari mam ti, n han le wa'am na, mam Yinɛ wa wan bahɛ ti nimbu'ɔ nyɔkɛ mam, ti n kele nintama'aha bo' sɛba zo'e zo'e en daa tum be'em gee ka tee ba dɛgerɔ, ba yaalegɔ la ba nyanɛ tuuma la ti ba ni pun tuna la puan la.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Nuurɛ butã n ŋwana ti n yɛ'a ya zi'an na wa. Zuuhego woo suni ti ku tara Aze' nyɛduma bayi' bii batã.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: N daa pun ka'am sɛba n daa tum be'em la mɛ, la ya za'a la ninmu'urɛ. Gee kelum ka'ana ba la mam n pun ka bɔna la ya wa. Wuu mam daa boi la ya n kinɛ buyi' puan la. N daa yele ya yeti, n han le lebe na, n ka yeti n tara ninkɔrɛ la se'em.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Yan diŋɛ bɔta sɛla ti la pa'alɛ ti Kiresta tɔgeri dɔla n puan la, a dagi tɔregɔ daana kan ta'am iŋɛ yan en boti se'em, gee a paŋa karegɛ mɛ ya puan.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Ba daa kpa' en mɛ a tɔregɔ puan, gee a vo'e mɛ Yinɛ paŋa la puan. Tumam dela tɔregɔ a puan, gee tumam han tuna la yamam, tu vɔi mɛ la en, Yinɛ paŋa la puan.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ nyaa ya vɔi sakerɛ la puan. Magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ, ya ka baŋɛ ti Kiresta boi ya puan? Han dagena ti ya sirum koŋe la ya magehɛ ya mɛhi bihɛ.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: Mam puti'irɛ dela, ya wan baŋɛ ti tu ka koŋe.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Gee tu pu'uhi Yinɛ ti ya da tum se'ere tuue. Dagi la tu nyaŋɛ magehegɔ la mɛ, gee la dela ya ita sɛla n de ma'a, tu han pun koŋe magehegɔ la mɛ.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu kan ta'am iŋɛ sɛla tuue yelemɛŋerɛ la, han dagena tu tuna yelemɛŋerɛ la ma'a.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Tumam nyɛɛni mɛ, tu han tara tɔregɔ gee ti yamam kpe'em. Tumam n pu'uhi se'ere dela, ya ze'ele suŋa, ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Bala, mam gulehɛ gɔnkuna wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti n ka boi la ya, bala ti n han ta wa'am na, n kan dikɛ sukpe'ene dikɛ pansɛka ti Daana la dikɛ bo' mam ti n mɛ ya la, sagum ya.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a n suurɔ, nyɛɛni ya. Maahum ya sɛla woo suŋa, kpemehe ya taaba suya, sakerɛ ya bo'ori ya taaba, boni ya la suma'ahum, ti Yinɛ En de nɔŋerɛ la suma'ahum Yinɛ la wan bɔna la ya.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya taaba la tuulum suma'ahum kɔkerɛ.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Nɛresunduma la za'a pu'uhi ya na.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Mam Pɔɔl en de tuntuna, dagena la asaala en tum mam bii asaala en bo' mam sore, gee la doohe la Yezu Kiresta puan, la Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, vo'e Yezu a kum puan na la.

eng: and from all the brothers and sisters with me. To the churches in Galatia.
local: la lageŋɔ nɛreba la za'a en boi la mam ka'lam la en guleheri bɔ'ɔra yamam Galatia timpaberɛ Lageŋɔ la za'a na.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: eŋa se'em en dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' tumam be'em zuo, ti a faaɛ tɔ be'em buuri sankana wa puan bahɛ, doohe tu Yinɛ n de tu Sɔ la sumboolum en de se'em la.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: eŋa se'em daana ti suŋa dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: La iŋɛ mam la pakerɛ la yamam en pilum zageha se'em en yi' ya doohe Kiresta zu'uhegɔ puan la, gee ŋmɛregɛ dɔla sɛla ti ba yi'ira faarɛ yelehum sebo en boi bu tɔka.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: dagi ti faarɛ yelehum bu sebo en boi ha, gee basɛba en boi, tulena ya puti'ira, bɔta ti ba tee Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Gee la han pun dɛna la tumam, bii Yinɛ suŋera en ze'ele saazuo ha sige na, muulɛ faarɛ yelehum sebo en boi bu tɔka la tumam en muulɛ sebo bo' ya la, ka' be'o sige a daana zuo!.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Ɛ̃ɛ, wuu tumam en pun yele ya ni se'em la, n le yele ya pa'ahɛ yeti, se'em han wa'am na muulɛ faarɛ yelehum sebo n boi bu tɔka la yan pun to'e sebo la, ka' be'o sige a daana zuo!.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Nananawa, mam nyaa eere la saaleba sakɛ bo' mam yoi? Bii n eere la Yinɛ sakɛ bo' mam? Bii n tugum nyaa zigera ti n pee la saaleba puya? Mam han kelum dole ni la n pɛɛra saaleba puya, mam kan deni Kiresta yameŋa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, n boti ya baŋɛ mɛ, n suurɔ, ti faarɛ yelehum la ti n muulɛ bo' ya wa, ka ze'ele la asaala zi'an na.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: Mam ka to'e bɔ asaala zi'an, bii n zamehɛ bu mɛ, gee mam to'e bu la Yezu Kiresta en niiɛ a mɛŋa pa'alɛ mam la zi'an.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Ya wum ya, gee me ka bu'ura mam en daa vɔi se'em Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ la puan. Mam daa gbiregeri mɛ, mɛleha Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, zigera n sagum kɔ.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: Mam daa ba' tole la nɛŋa Yuuduma pu'uhegɔ la puan n nɛreba tiŋasuka, gana mam la sɛba en de zuo taaba zo'e zo'e. Se'ere en sɔi la, n daa ba' mu'ɛ la nini la n yaabeduma pa'alegɔ la.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Gee Yinɛ, se'em en daa gaŋɛ mam ze'ele gee ti ba baŋɛ dɔgɛ mam la nini tigɛ la mam mɛ, ti a yi' mam doohe a zu'uhegɔ la puan.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: ti a lilege a Dayua la pa'alɛ mam, ti n ta'am muulɛ Kiresta yele bo' tihi. Mam daa ka sɔhɛ bo' asaala sɛka.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: bii n zom Yerusalɛm sɛba en pun dɛna Yezu tuntuneba mam yia la zi'an, gee mam yehe mɛ bilam bilam kiŋɛ Arebiya tinkãtɛ puan, ti pooren ti n lebe Damasekuse tinkãtɛ.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: Yuuma atã en tole, ti n zom Yerusalɛm ti n ta limehe Kaifase guum, gee kɛ̃ la en dabeha pia la anuu.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: Gee n ka nyɛ tuntuna se'em bini, han dagena Yemesi en de Daana suuɔ la.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Mam guleheri sɛla bɔ'ɔra ya wa, Yinɛ nɛŋan ti n zɛ'a wa, n ka pareni!.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Pooren ti n kiŋɛ Siriya la Kilikiya sɔ'ɔlum puan.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: gee bala ma'a ti n nan dela saana bo' Yudiya lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta puan la.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: Ba yeen wum la nɛreba en yeti, Se'em en yuum nameheri tu la, nyaa muuli la sakerɛ se'ere ti a yuum zigera a sagum la.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Ti ba pɛgɛ Yinɛ mam zuo.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Ti yuum pia la anaahi ta tole, ti n le zom Yerusalɛm la Banabase, tara Taitusi pa'ahɛ.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Mam kiŋɛ la dela n nyɛ la lilegere. Ti mam paaɛ dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe ba nɛŋan doohe faarɛ yelehum la ti mam muula bɔ'ɔra sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la, ti n baŋɛ ti mam tuni sɛla la, gee pun tum sɛla la, kan bahɛ yoo.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Gee Taitusi se'em en yuum pun bɔna la mam la, ba ka pɛregɛ en ti a ŋma, la en pun dɛna Giriki la.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Gee doohe la pumpɔreŋɔ sakereba ti nɛreba li'ihɛ tari ba na la, sɛba en iŋɛ bããlam kɛ̃ ti ba bihɛ fai sɛka ti tumam tara Kiresta Yezu puan, la ba ta'am bahɛ ti tu lebege yamehi la.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: tu pun ka nam bo' ba la fii me, ti faarɛ yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ ta'am bɔna la ya saŋa woo.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Ti sɛba en me daa wan deni nɛheduma la, .

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Gee la daa dagi bala, nɛheduma wa en nyɛ ti Yinɛ en dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' ti n muulɛ sɛba en ka ŋma la, wuu Pita ti Yinɛ dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' en ti a muulɛ bo' sɛba en ŋma la.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.

James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Bala, Yemesi, Kaifase, la Yɔn, sɛba ti ba mina ba ti ba dela tiŋasuka nyuuhe la, en ta baŋɛ zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' mam la, ba daa kɔkɛ mam la Banabase mɛ, pa'alɛ ti tu dela buyina tuunɛ la puan. Gee nyaa sakɛ ti tumam kina sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la zi'an, ti bamam me kina Yuuduma la zi'an.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Ba yele tu la yele yinɛ ma'a, ti tu tɛra naheduma yele, sɛla ti mam pun mu'ɛ nini ti n iŋɛ en bala.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Gee Kaifase en ta wa'am Antiyoke tinkãtɛ na la, mam yuum ze'ele la a nɛŋan zɛregɛ en, se'ere en sɔi la, a tuue mɛ.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Han dagena sansɛka ti nɛreba basɛba ze'ele Yemesi zi'an wa'am na la, Pita ni lagum naaɛ me la sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan wa dita. Gee nɛreba la en paaɛ na la, a fooe a mɛŋa lebe la pooren a ma'a. Se'ere en sɔi la, a zoti la sɛba en ŋma la.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: Ti Yuuduma basɛba me lagum pa'ahɛ a birekɔgenɛ wa puan, ti la bahɛ ti Banabase me lagum pa'ahɛ ba birekɔgenɛ wa puan.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: Gee mam en ta nyɛ ti ba itegɔ la ka dole faarɛ yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti n yele Kaifase ba za'a nɛŋan yeti, La fum en pun dɛna Yuu nɛra wa, han vɔna wuu se'em en dagi Yuu nɛra, gee ka vɔna wuu se'em en de Yuu nɛra, ŋwani ti fum wan ta'am pɛregɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma ti ba vɔna wuu Yuu nɛreba.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: Tumam mɛhi dela Yuu buuri tu dɔgum puan, gee dagi be'em duma sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma buuri la puan.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: Gee la bala za'a, tu mi' ti nɛra de ma'a la Yinɛ dagi la en tuni ze'elego la, gee en tari sakerɛ Yezu Kiresta puan. Ti tumam wa'am na nam Yezu Kiresta, ti tu dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ tu sakerɛ Kiresta puan, gee dagi tumam en tuni ze'elego tuuma se'em. Beni zuo, nɛra ka boi n wan ta'am dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ eŋa en tuni ze'elego tuuma la.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Gee tumam han tiregera ti tu dɛna ma'a la Yinɛ doohe Kiresta puan la han dɛna be'emduma, Kiresta nyaa dela be'emduma yameŋa bii? La kan ta'am dɛna bala!.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: Gee mam han le mɛta yele sɛ'a ti mam tabelɛ gbi' bahɛ, n yi pa'alɛ ti n dela be'em ita.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la puan, mam ki mɛ bo' ze'elego la, ti n ta'am vɔna bo' Yinɛ. Ba kpa' mam mɛ la Kiresta.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: La le dagena mam mɛŋa en vɔi, gee la dela Kiresta en vɔi mam puan. Ti vom sebo ti n vɔna inganɛ puan nananawa, n vɔi mɛ la sakerɛ Yinɛ Dayua la, se'em en nɔŋɛ mam gee dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ bo' mam la puan.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Mam ka zagehɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra han ka tara tuure doohe ze'elego la puan, Kiresta kum la yi dela yoo.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Yamam Galatia gɔɔnɔ wa! Ani n yuum ya? Bihɛ ya, dagi ya ninin, la nɛrekuuŋɔ la nini ti ba daa kpa' Yezu Kiresta la?.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Yele yinɛ se'ere ma'a ti mam bɔta ti n baŋɛ ya zi'an dela, Yamam to'e Fa'a Sia la doohe la yan tuni ze'elego la yɛla se'em bii, yan nam sɛla ti ya wum la?.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Yamam ba' gaam la bala? Yamam en pɔhɛ la Fa'a Sia la, ya nyaa ba'aheri mɛ la inganɛ?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Yamam en nyɛ yɛla wa zo'e se'em wa dela yoo? Wuu la han sirum dɛna la yoo.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: La yi han dɛna bala, Yinɛ bo' ya Fa'a Sia la, gee tum kirehi ya puan la doohe la ze'elego la tuuma bii yan nam sɛla ti ya wum la zuo?.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Wuu Aberaham yuum nam, Yinɛ, ti Yinɛ bo' en tuntɔɔnɛ.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: bala ya nyɛ ya, sɛba en nam la en de Aberaham yaahi.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la en pun bihɛ nyɛ ti, Yinɛ wan dikɛ sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la naaɛ la a mɛŋa ba sakerɛ zuo la, a te'ele muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa bo' Aberaham mɛ yeti, Sɛba en ka pa'ahɛ Yuuduma puan la woo wan nyɛ sum la fum puan.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Dina wa zuo, sɛba en nam la nyɛti sum mɛ la Aberaham se'em en daa nam la.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Gee sɛba woo en dikɛ ba mɛhi delum ze'elego tuuma la, nɔpɔtɛ en dole ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, Nɔpɔtɛ en dole se'em woo en ka dole gee ka nana sɛla woo n gulehɛ biŋe ze'elego gɔŋɔ la puan la.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: Nananawa, la kɔ'ɔm niiɛ mɛ gã'a ti nɛra ka boi ka tara tuure Yinɛ nɛŋan ze'elego la dɔlega puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Se'em en de tuntɔɔdaana, wan vɔna la sakerɛ puan.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Gee ze'elego la ka yagɛ sakerɛ zuo. Gee lan tugum dɛna se'em la dela, Fum se'em woo en dole ze'elego la tuuma la wan vɔna mɛ la yele aŋa.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Kiresta yɔ to'e tɔ mɛ ze'elego pɔtɛ la puan bahɛ, doohe eŋa ti nɔpɔtɛ dɔla en tumam zuo la. Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, Nɔpɔtɛ en dole se'em woo en yule tia zuo.

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: La iŋɛ ŋwana ti Yezu Kiresta puan, Aberaham sum la wan paaɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, ti tumam me to'e Fa'a Sia nɔbiŋere la sakerɛ puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: N suurɔ wa, n bo'ori ya la vom puan makerɛ. Nɛra han diŋɛ biŋe a nuurɛ, se'em ka pa'aheri se'ere bii zageha dɛ.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Nananawa, Yinɛ yuum biŋe nɔya la bo' la Aberaham la a yaaŋa. La ka yeti, La a Yaahi wuu ba zo'e mɛ. Gee la yeti, La fum yaaŋa, de vuurɛ dela, nɛra ayima, se'em en de Kiresta la.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Mam n boti n yele se'ere la dela, ze'elego la n wa'am yuum kɔbehi sinaahi la yuum pihitã pooren na la, kan ta'am saalum Yinɛ nɔpu'ulegɔ la ti a pun pu'ulɛ la, ti ze'elego nyaa ta'am saalum nɔbiŋere la.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ bo'olum la han ze'eti ni la ze'elego la puan na, la yi' ka le ze'ele nɔbiŋere la puan. Gee Yinɛ dikɛ bu bo' la Aberaham doohe nɔbiŋere la puan.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Ŋwani ti ze'elego la yi' bɔna? Yinɛ dikɛ ku pa'ahɛ la tu be'em zuo, gura ti Aberaham yaaŋa la ta wa'am na, se'em ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ la en la. Ku me ze'ele la suŋereba la zi'an doohe nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra la puan.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Nananawa, nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra gani nɛreyinɛ mɛ, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa dela ayima.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Ze'elego la yi' ka sakɛ bo' Yinɛ nɔbiŋere la bii? La kan dɛna bala! Se'ere en sɔi la, ba han bo' ni ze'elego seko en wan bo' ni vom, tuntɔɔnɛ yi' wan ze'ele la ze'elego la puan na.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Gee Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la yeti, sɛla woo boi la be'em paŋa la puan, ti sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ doohe sakerɛ n boi Yezu Kiresta puan la, wan dikɛ bo' la sɛba n nam la.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Nananawa, sakerɛ saŋa n daa nan ka paaɛ la, ze'elego la daa pagɛ tu mɛ, gee gura tɔ, tara tara ti sakerɛ saŋa la nyaa ta paaɛ.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Bala, ze'elego la en daa de se'em en guri tu, tara tara ti Kiresta ta wa'am na, ti tu nyaa naaɛ la Yinɛ dɔla tu sakerɛ puan.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Gee nananawa ti sakerɛ wa'am na la, tu ka le bɔna se'em en ni guri tu la nu'uhen.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yezu Kiresta puan, ya za'a dela Yinɛ kɔma doohe sakerɛ puan.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Beni zuo, wuu ya zo'e zo'e en to'e ko'om sua Kiresta puan la, ya pi' ya mɛhi mɛ la Kiresta.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Dinɛ zuo, Yuuduma buuri bii Giriki buuri ka le bɔna, da'aberɛ bii fai daana ka le bɔna, buraa bii pɔka ka le bɔna. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya za'a dela buyina Kiresta Yezu puan.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Ti fum se'em n de Kiresta dinɛ, fum yi dela Aberaham yaaŋa, dɛna asooe sɔna daana wuu nɔbiŋere la en de se'em la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: N suurɔ wa, ya han ta baŋɛ ti be'em sebo en nyɔkɛ ya se'em, yamam sɛba en boi Fa'a Sia la paŋa puan la, malum sɛ en ze'ele, gee iŋɛ ya dɛ la sigehe n mɛŋa. Gee bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa ti yamam me da ta lu pa'ahegɔ be'o puan.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Ziiri ya taaba zeero, ŋwana ti Kiresta ze'elego la wan wa'am asira na.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Sɛba en dagi sɛsɛla la han ti'iha ti ba dela sɛla, ba pã'aheri la ba mɛhi.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Nɛra woo makɛ a mɛŋa tuuma bihɛ, gee dagi ayima tuuma. Bala en wan bahɛ ti fum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra woo ziira la a mɛŋa zeero.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Sɛba ti ba pa'alɛ ya yelehum la dela ya tɔrɛ sɛla woo en ani suŋa la se'em en pa'alɛ ya la.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Da pã'aha ya mɛhi, nɛra kan pim Yinɛ. Nɛra en burɛ sɛla la, bala mɛa ti a kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Fum han burɛ la ingɔŋɔ yɛla, fum kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la ingɔŋɔ sageŋɔ yɛla. Gee fum han burɛ la Sia puan yɛla, fum kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la vom kamba'ahɛ Sia la puan.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Bala, da bahɛ ya ti tu taregɛ la tu ita sɛla en de ma'a. Se'ere sɔi la, tu wan ta kɛ̃', kɛ̃'a saŋa la han paaɛ, ti tu ka bahɛ tu ita sɛla en de ma'a la.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Bala zuo, sansɛka woo puan, bahɛ ya ti tu ita sɛla en ani suŋa bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo, yele yele wuu sɛba en de yire puan, Kiresta sakerɛ la puan.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Bihɛ ya gɔnɔ kãra seto ti n ni gulehɛ la n mɛŋa nu'o bɔ'ɔra ya na!.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: La dela sɛba en boti ba ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la ba ingɔŋɔ yɛla la, n zigeri ti ba pɛregɛ ya ti ya ŋma la. Ba iti ŋwana ti ba da nyɛ namehegɔ Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la yɛla muulegɔ puan.

eng: Those who are circumcised dont observe the Law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised, so they can boast about your physical body.
local: Sɛba en pun ŋma la mɛhi ka dole ze'elego la, gee ba boti ya ŋma mɛ ti ba ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ la ya ingɔŋɔ la.

eng: But as for me, God forbid that I should boast about anything except for the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through him, and I have been crucified to the world.
local: Mam da makɛ ŋmɛ' n nyu'ɔ yele se'ere puan, han dagena tu Daana Yezu Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo ma'a, yele se'ere zuo ti ba kpa' timpaberɛ wa labelɛ mam, gee kpa' mam labelɛ timpaberɛ wa la.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt mean anything. What matters is a new creation.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ŋmaa, la a ka ŋma, dagi sɛsɛla, gee dɔgum paalemɔ en de sɛla woo!.

eng: May peace and mercy be on whoever follows this rule and on Gods Israel.
local: Gee sɛba en wan dɔla pa'ali kuna wa la, suma'ahum, la a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔna la ba, gee bɔna la Yinɛ Izirale paalega la.

eng: From now on, no one should bother me because I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
local: Yehe zina yɛ'ɛha, se'em da ɛ daaŋɔ bo' mam. Se'ere en sɔi la,Yezu yiha en bala to'e mam iŋa wa za'a wa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
local: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ya sia puan, n suurɔ wa. Taari.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will. To the holy and faithful people in Christ Jesus in Ephesus.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna,Yinɛ sumboolum zuo la. N gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra la nɛresunduma en boi Ɛfisu, dɛna sakerɛ duma Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya.

eng: Bless the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! He has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing that comes from heaven.
local: Sum dɛna Yinɛ, se'em en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ. Se'em en bo' tu sum Kiresta puan, Sia puan sum sebo woo en boi tinsuŋan ha la.

eng: God chose us in Christ to be holy and blameless in Gods presence before the creation of the world.
local: wuu en looe tu se'em Kiresta puan gee nyaa baŋɛ naam timpaberɛ wa la, ti tu dɛna tuulum nɛreba, ka tara tuure a nɛŋan, a nɔŋerɛ la puan la.

eng: God destined us to be his adopted children through Jesus Christ because of his love. This was according to his goodwill and plan.
local: A daa pun maahum tu mɛ ti a to'e tu biha, ti tu dɛna a kɔma Yezu Kiresta puan, wuu sum, la boolum sebo en boi a sumboolum puan la.

eng: and to honor his glorious grace that he has given to us freely through the Son whom he loves.
local: ti pɛka paaɛ a suŋa zu'uhegɔ la ti a ka'ɛ saarum tu zuo na yoo a dayɔba'arɛ la puan la.

eng: We have been ransomed through his Sons blood, and we have forgiveness for our failures based on his overflowing grace.
local: Eŋa puan ti Yinɛ yɔ to'e tu a ziim la puan, gee di sugeri bo' tu tuure, doohe a zu'uhegɔ la en seke paaɛ paaɛ la.

eng: which he poured over us with wisdom and understanding.
local: ti a ka'ɛ bu zo'e zo'e bahɛ tu zuo na la. Bo tari la yɛm la mi'ilum za'a.

eng: God revealed his hidden design to us, which is according to his goodwill and the plan that he intended to accomplish through his Son.
local: ti a iŋɛ tɔ ti tu baŋɛ a sumboolum sugelum yele, wuu sum la boolum sebo ti a pun maahum biŋe Kiresta puan.

eng: This is what God planned for the climax of all times: to bring all things together in Christ, the things in heaven along with the things on earth.
local: ti la dɛna sɛla en maahum gura saŋa la ta paaɛ, ti a kã'ahɛ sɛla woo bo' a mɛŋa. Sɛla en boi saazuo, la sɛla en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, ti iŋɛ Yezu dɛna nɛŋa daana.

eng: We have also received an inheritance in Christ. We were destined by the plan of God, who accomplishes everything according to his design.
local: Kiresta puan, tu tari tɔtɛ mɛ, ban pun maahum tɔ bo' eŋa se'em daana en ba'aheri sɛla woo a mɛŋa kã'aŋɔ la a sumboolum puan la.

eng: We are called to be an honor to Gods glory because we were the first to hope in Christ.
local: Bala ti tumam sɛba en de yia duma dikɛ tu puti'irɛ delum Kiresta la, wan vɔna pɛgera a suŋa la.

eng: You too heard the word of truth in Christ, which is the good news of your salvation. You were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit because you believed in Christ.
local: Eŋa puan ti yamam me en wum yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la en de ya faarɛ yelehum la, me daa nam en, ti ba di'e ya la daalehum sebo en de Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan bo' la.

eng: The Holy Spirit is the down payment on our inheritance, which is applied toward our redemption as Gods own people, resulting in the honor of Gods glory.
local: Yele dina en de daalehum, pa'alɛ ti tu tari tɔtɛ en yɔ to'e tu ti tu dɛna Yinɛ mɛŋa nɛreba la dɛna pɛka bo' a suŋa la.

eng: Since I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all Gods people, this is the reason that.
local: N wum yan tari sakerɛ Daana Yezu puan se'em, la yan tari nɔŋerɛ se'em bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a. Ŋwana zuo.

eng: I dont stop giving thanks to God for you when I remember you in my prayers.
local: mam ka malum bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara ya zuo gɔ'ɔra, mam tɛri ya yele mɛ n pu'uhegɔ puan.

eng: I pray that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, will give you a spirit of wisdom and revelation that makes God known to you.
local: N pu'uheri mɛ ti tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Yinɛ la, se'em en de zu'uhegɔ za'a Sɔ la, wan bo' ya a yɛm, la lilegere Sia, yan wa'am na ti ya baŋɛ en wa.

eng: I pray that the eyes of your heart will have enough light to see what is the hope of Gods call, what is the richness of Gods glorious inheritance among believers.
local: Bala ti ya suure wan yu'ɛ, ti ya nyɛ niihum la, la ya baŋɛ beere puti'irɛ se'ere zuo ti a yi' ya na la, la tarega sɛka en boi suŋa tarega la puan bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la.

eng: and what is the overwhelming greatness of Gods power that is working among us believers. This power is conferred by the energy of Gods powerful strength.
local: la a pankãtɛ sɛka en ka tari makerɛ bɔ'ɔra tumam sɛba en nam en, a pankãtɛ tuuma la puan la.

eng: Gods power was at work in Christ when God raised him from the dead and sat him at Gods right side in the heavens.
local: Yinɛ dikɛ pankana wa tum la Kiresta puan, en vo'e en a kum la puan, tari en ta zi'ire a zuɔ, tinsuŋan ha la.

eng: far above every ruler and authority and power and angelic power, any power that might be named not only now but in the future.
local: ti a gana bihereba za'a, kpe'eŋo la pahi za'a, la na'am woo n boi, gee gana yu'ura woo en tabelɛ sigɛ, dagi la zina buuri saŋa wa puan ma'a, gee buuri saŋa se'ehi woo en kini na la.

eng: God put everything under Christs feet and made him head of everything in the church.
local: Yinɛ dikɛ sɛla woo iŋɛ la a nu'uhen, gee bahɛ en ti a dɛna zuodaana sɔna sɛla woo en de lageŋɔ la dinɛ.

eng: which is his body. His body, the church, is the fullness of Christ, who fills everything in every way.
local: Lageŋɔ la dela Kiresta iŋa, kɔ'ɔm pirɛ mɛ la eŋa se'em daana en boi sɛla woo puan la.

eng: At one time you were like a dead person because of the things you did wrong and your offenses against God.
local: Ya yuum ki mɛ ya tuure la ya be'em la puan.

eng: You used to live like people of this world. You followed the rule of a destructive spiritual power. This is the spirit of disobedience to Gods will that is now at work in persons whose lives are characterized by disobedience.
local: ti ya yuum bɔna bu puan, dɔla timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla la, dɔla se'em daana paŋa n boi kusebego wa puan la. Sɛsɛka n boi nananawa tuna la sɛba n ka nane Yinɛ nuurɛ la.

eng: At one time you were like those persons. All of you used to do whatever felt good and whatever you thought you wanted so that you were children headed for punishment just like everyone else.
local: Tu za'a yuum nan vɔi mɛ la ba, dɔla tu inganɛ kɔrɛ, la tu mɛhi puti'ira. Tu yuum dela nɛresɛba ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim dɔla tɔ, wuu kɔma woo en ani se'em la.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: Gee Yinɛ n pirɛ la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la, la nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ se'ere ti a nɔŋɛ tu la.

eng: However, God is rich in mercy. He brought us to life with Christ while we were dead as a result of those things that we did wrong. He did this because of the great love that he has for us. You are saved by Gods grace!.
local: la tumam n daa pun ki tu be'em la zuo la, Yinɛ bahɛ ti tu vo'e mɛ la Kiresta. Zu'uhegɔ zuo ti ya nyɛ faarɛ.

eng: And God raised us up and seated us in the heavens with Christ Jesus.
local: Yinɛ vo'e tu mɛ la Kiresta, gee bahɛ ti tu zi'ire la en saazuo ha, Kiresta Yezu zuo.

eng: God did this to show future generations the greatness of his grace by the goodness that God has shown us in Christ Jesus.
local: Bala ti saŋa la han ta paaɛ, a wan pa'alɛ a zu'uhegɔ la n ka tari makerɛ la, la a sum sebo ti a tara bɔ'ɔra tɔ Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: You are saved by Gods grace because of your faith. This salvation is Gods gift. Its not something you possessed.
local: La dela zu'uhegɔ zuo ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, doohe sakerɛ puan. Dina dagi ya mɛhi en iŋɛ sɛla, gee la dela Yinɛ bo'olum.

eng: Its not something you did that you can be proud of.
local: La dagi ya tum mɛ nyɛ, ti se'em wan ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ.

eng: Instead, we are Gods accomplishment, created in Christ Jesus to do good things. God planned for these good things to be the way that we live our lives.
local: Tumam dela sɛba ti a iŋɛ tɔ ti tu dɛna la. A naam tu la Kiresta Yezu puan, ti tu tuna tunsuma sɛ'a ti Yinɛ pun maahum a ti a dɛna tu vom sore la.

eng: So remember that once you were Gentiles by physical descent, who were called uncircumcised by Jews who are physically circumcised.
local: Bala zuo la, tɛri ya ti saŋa kayima ha, yamam sɛba n dagi Yuuduma buuri ya dɔgum puan na la, sɛba n ŋma la yi'ira ya la Sɛba n ka ŋma. Ŋmaa kana wa dela ingɔŋɔ ŋmaa, ti nɛreba nu'uhi ŋmata.

eng: At that time you were without Christ. You were aliens rather than citizens of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of Gods promise. In this world you had no hope and no God.
local: Tɛri ya ti sankaŋa puan la, ya yuum ka mi' Kiresta, dɛna zanku'uma Izirale timpaberɛ, dɛna saama bo' nɔpu'ulegɔ nɔbiŋere la, ka tara beere puti'irɛ, gee me ka mina Yinɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: But now, thanks to Christ Jesus, you who once were so far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
local: Gee nananawa, Yezu Kiresta zuo, yamam sɛba ti saŋa yuum bɔna ti ya zaaɛ zaaɛ ha la, ba nyaa tari ya lɛm me na, Kiresta ziim la zuo.

eng: Christ is our peace. He made both Jews and Gentiles into one group. With his body, he broke down the barrier of hatred that divided us.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa mɛŋa n de tu suma'ahum. A inganɛ puan, a bahɛ ti yelehego buyi' n lebege buyina, gee gbirege nangoone se'ere en beŋere la, diŋo seko n boi tu tiŋasuka la bahɛ.

eng: He canceled the detailed rules of the Law so that he could create one new person out of the two groups, making peace.
local: A saalum ze'elego la, la ku pa'alegɔ, la ku malema za'a bahɛ mɛ, ti a malum naam asaala paalega bo' a mɛŋa ti a tee ze'ele bo' bayi' la, ti suma'ahum bɔna.

eng: He reconciled them both as one body to God by the cross, which ended the hostility to God.
local: Ti a ta'am bahɛ ti bayi' wa naaɛ taaba bo' Yinɛ, ti ba dɛna iŋa kayima, doohe pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo, nyaa ku diŋo la, doohe a pumpuuredɔɔ la.

eng: When he came, he announced the good news of peace to you who were far away from God and to those who were near.
local: Bala, a wa'am na muulɛ la suma'ahum bo' yamam sɛba n daa zaaɛ zaaɛ ha la, gee bo' yamam sɛba n lɛm la.

eng: We both have access to the Father through Christ by the one Spirit.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan, ti tu za'a tara Sia kayima dɔla paara Sɔ la zi'an.

eng: So now you are no longer strangers and aliens. Rather, you are fellow citizens with Gods people, and you belong to Gods household.
local: Bala zuo, ya ka le dɛna saama bii zanku'uma, gee ya lagum dela tiŋambiihi la nɛresunduma, gee me dɛna Yinɛ mɛŋa yire nɛreba.

eng: As Gods household, you are built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
local: ti ba mɛ ya dɔgelɛ tuntuneba, la nɔtɔgeheba ɛberɛ la zuo, ti Kiresta Yezu mɛŋa dɛna ɛberɛ kugere la.

eng: The whole building is joined together in him, and it grows up into a temple that is dedicated to the Lord.
local: Eŋa puan ti yire la za'a naaɛ taaba, lebege Tuulum pu'uhegɔ yire, Daana la puan.

eng: Christ is building you into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
local: Eŋa puan ti yamam me mɛ naaɛ taaba Sia puan, dɛna Yinɛ bɔŋa zi'an.

eng: This is why I, Paul, am a prisoner of Christ for you Gentiles.
local: Ŋwana wa zuo ti mam Pɔɔl bɔna paka deen bo' Kiresta Yezu, yamam sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa zuo.

eng: Youve heard, of course, about the responsibility to distribute Gods grace, which God gave to me for you, right?.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya pun wum Yinɛ en doohe a zu'uhegɔ puan dikɛ tuunɛ iŋɛ mam nu'uhi yamam zuo.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Mam n yeti se'ere n ŋwana, asooe sɔna duma la han nan dɛna kɔma, ba ka nyanɛ yamehi, la bamam pun sɔna lɔgerɔ la za'a la.

eng: God showed me his secret plan in a revelation, as I mentioned briefly before .
local: la sugelum yele la ti a daa bahɛ ti mam baŋɛ dɛ doohe lilegere puan, mam gulehɛ yelesɛ'a fii wa zuo ha la.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Ba kelum bɔna la bihereba la pa'aleba nu'uhen, gura daan se'ere ti Sɔ la looe la ta paaɛ.

eng: when you read this, youll understand my insight into the secret plan about Christ.
local: Ku wan dɛna gɔŋɔ ti ya han kãalɛ, ya wan baŋɛ mam n bɔkɛ Kiresta sugelum yele la se'em.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: La ani la bala mɛa bo' tumam. Tumam en daa nan de kɔma la, tu daa dela da'aba bo' sɛɛhi se'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa.

eng: Earlier generations didnt know this hidden plan that God has now revealed to his holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit.
local: Buuri sahi se'ehi n tole la, sugelum yele dina wa ka lilege de bo' saaleba wuu dinɛ n nyaa lilege se'em bo' a tuulum tuntuneba, la a nɔtɔgeheba doohe Fa'a Sia puan wa la.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: Gee Yinɛ saŋa la en daa ta pirɛ paaɛ la, Yinɛ tum la a Dayua, ti pɔka dɔgɛ ka, dɔgɛ ka doohe ze'elego la.

eng: This plan is that the Gentiles would be coheirs and parts of the same body, and that they would share with the Jews in the promises of God in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: De vuurɛ dela, sɛba n dagi Yuuduma wa nyaa lagum dɛna la sɛba en tari tɔtɛ, lagum dɛna iŋa kayima la nɛresɛba n wan lagum to'e nɔbiŋere lɔkɔ la Kiresta Yezu puan, faarɛ yelehum la zuo.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: ti a ta'am yɔ to'e sɛba en boi ze'elego la puan bahɛ, ti Yinɛ ta'am to'e tu ti tu dɛna a kɔma.

eng: I became a servant of the gospel because of the grace that God showed me through the exercise of his power.
local: Faarɛ yelehum buna zuo, ti mam lebege yameŋa, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bo'olum la ti a dikɛ bo' mam doohe a paŋa tuuma la puan.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Tumam en de a kɔma la zuo, ti Yinɛ tum a Dayua Sia la tu suya puan na, ti tu tana kaahɛ yeti, Aba! N Sɔ.

eng: God gave his grace to me, the least of all Gods people, to preach the good news about the immeasurable riches of Christ to the Gentiles.
local: Gee la mam n pun pɔ'ɛ nɛresunduma la za'a puan la, Yinɛ dikɛ la zu'uhegɔ kuna bo' mam, ti n tari Kiresta tarega la n ka tari makerɛ la faarɛ yelesuma la na bo' sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Bala, fum le dagena da'aberɛ, gee Yinɛ mɛŋa bia. Ti fum han dɛna la a bia, fum dela la asooe sɔna daana Yinɛ puan.

eng: God sent me to reveal the secret plan that had been hidden since the beginning of time by God, who created everything.
local: ti nɛra woo baŋɛ putɛ'se'ere n boi sugelum yele wa en sugɛ yuuma yuuma ha, Yinɛ se'ere n naam sɛla woo puan la.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Wunse'eren ha la, yan daa nan ka mi' Yinɛ la, ya yuum dela da'aba bo' nɛreba en dagi yina.

eng: Gods purpose is now to show the rulers and powers in the heavens the many different varieties of his wisdom through the church.
local: ti la doohe lageŋɔ la puan, ti Yinɛ yɛm la n ka tari ba'ahegɔ la wan nyaa niiɛ bo' timbihereba, la kpɛ'ɛnduma n boi saazuo ha.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Gee Nananawa, ti ya nyaa wa'am na baŋɛ Yinɛ la, bii n yeti, Yinɛ n baŋɛ ya la, ŋwani ti yamam wan le ŋmɛregɛ dɔla tɔregɔ la, nɔŋɔ sɛɛhi se'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo wa? Ŋwani ti ya le bɔta ti ya dɛna da'aba bo' ba le yɛ'ɛha?.

eng: This was consistent with the plan he had from the beginning of time that he accomplished through Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Ŋwana wa iŋɛ doohe la a putɛ'se'ere n ka tari ba'ahegɔ, ti a tum dɛ tu Daana Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Ya na'aheri la dabeha asɛ'a, ŋmarehi sise'ehi, sahi sise'ehi, la yuuma a sɛ'a.

eng: In Christ we have bold and confident access to God through faith in him.
local: se'em puan ti tu wan paaɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan la sukpe'ene ti dabeem ka bɔna, doohe tumam n sakɛ bo' en se'em.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: Dabeem en tari mam, ti mam tum sɛla bo' ya la wan bahɛ yoo.

eng: So then, I ask you not to become discouraged by what Im suffering for you, which is your glory.
local: Bala zuo, mam pu'uhɛ bɔ'ɔra ya mɛ, ti ya puti'irɛ da kɔ'ɔhɛ mam nameheri se'em ya zuo wa. Ba dela ya sum.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: N suurɔ, n beleni ya mɛ, ani ya wuu mam la, se'ere en sɔi la, mam ani mɛ wuu yamam la. Ya ka iŋɛ se'ere se'ere tuue mam.

eng: This is why I kneel before the Father.
local: Ŋwana zuo, mam kpa' la duma Sɔ la nɛŋan.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Ya mi' ti n daa ka paaɛ n mɛŋa mam daa muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya yia la.

eng: Every ethnic group in heaven or on earth is recognized by him.
local: se'em puan ti nɛra woo n boi saazuo ha la timpaberɛ wa zuo tara ba yu'urɛ.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: La mam bã'a la en daa makɛ ya bihɛ la, ya daa ka keheri mam, bii ya zagehɛ mam. Gee ya daa to'e mam mɛ wuu Yinɛ suŋera la, to'e mam wuu Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: I ask that he will strengthen you in your inner selves from the riches of his glory through the Spirit.
local: Mam pu'uhegɔ dela, a wan ze'ele a tarega suŋa la puan, bo' ya sore ti ya tara paŋa ya puan, doohe Sia la puan.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Beni en iŋɛ ya putɛ'sunɛ la ti ya daa tara la? Se'ere en sɔi la, mam yeti mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti la han wan ta'am ni iŋɛ ni, ya wan kpigɛ ni ya nini bo' ni mam.

eng: I ask that Christ will live in your hearts through faith. As a result of having strong roots in love.
local: ti Kiresta bɔna ya suya puan, sakerɛ zuo, wuu yan ba' nyɛga gee tara nɔŋerɛ puan se'em la.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Mam nyaa lebege la ya dima la mam yele ya yelemɛŋerɛ wa?.

eng: I ask that youll have the power to grasp loves width and length, height and depth, together with all believers.
local: Mam pu'uhegɔ dela, ya tara paŋa n wan ta'am bɔkera la nɛresunduma la za'a, ya faarɛ la yaleŋa, de girega, de woko la de kɔ'ɔlum.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
