local: Ba yeen ita mɛ gee ba tari la puti'irɛ se'ere ha. Ba boti ba yelege ya mɛ, ti ya bɔna la ba.

eng: I ask that youll know the love of Christ that is beyond knowledge so that you will be filled entirely with the fullness of God.
local: gee me mina Kiresta nɔŋerɛ la n gani mi'ilum woo la, la ya ta'am pirɛ la Yinɛ naanɛ paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Han dɛna fai, Kiresta en faaɛ tu bahɛ fai. Ze' ya kankaŋi bala zuo la, gee da le dikɛ ya mɛhi bo' da'aberɛ yɔgehega la.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: La ani suŋa ti ya nɔŋɛ mam la putɛ'sunɛ saŋa woo, gee dagi sansɛka ti n bɔna la ya ma'a.

eng: Therefore, as a prisoner for the Lord, I encourage you to live as people worthy of the call you received from God.
local: Dinɛ zuo, mam en boi paka deen Daana la tuuma zuo beleni ya mɛ, ti ya vɔna vom sebo en nari la yi'sɛka ti ba yi' ya kɛ̃' ka puan la.

eng: Glory to God, who is able to do far beyond all that we could ask or imagine by his power at work within us.
local: Nananawa, eŋa se'em paŋa en tuni tu puan wa, wan ta'am tum zo'e zo'e bo' tu gana tumam n soke en bii ti'iha sɛla yele la.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Kelehe ya! Mam Pɔɔl yeti ya mɛ ti, ya han bahɛ ti ba ŋma ya, Kiresta yi kan tari nyuurɔ bo' ya.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: N kɔmbibehi wa, yamam sɛba zuo ti n le bɔna wuŋerɛ puan wuu n viregere mɛ la, gura ti Kiresta naanɛ ta to'e ya wa.

eng: Conduct yourselves with all humility, gentleness, and patience. Accept each other with love.
local: Voni ya ya vom za'a la sirege n mɛŋa, zulaam, gee ŋmibera la taaba nɔŋerɛ puan.

eng: glory to him in the church and in Christ Jesus for all generations, forever and always. Amen.
local: Eŋa ti lageŋɔ la pɛgera, doohe Kiresta Yezu, la buuri woo, wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, n yeti mɛ la ninmu'urɛ bɔ'ɔra fum buraa sɛka bahɛ ti ba ŋma hɔ yeti, fum daana dela fum nana ze'elego la yɛla la za'a.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: Mam boti ni ti n boni mɛ la ya nananawa, gee tee ni n kua, se'ere en sɔi la, mam maam mɛ la ya.

eng: and make an effort to preserve the unity of the Spirit with the peace that ties you together.
local: Kpeŋe ya mɛhi la ya tara Sia la puan nɔyinɛ la suma'ahum sebo en de bu dikɛ ya naaɛ taaba.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Yamam sɛba n boti ya naaɛ la doohe ze'elego puan la, ŋmaaɛ ya mɛhi mɛ Kiresta zi'an bahɛ. Ya boi la Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la yiŋa.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Yele ya mam, yamam sɛba en boti ya dɛna ze'elego la da'aba wa, dagi ze'elego la ti ya wan kɛleha?.

eng: You are one body and one spirit, just as God also called you in one hope.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Iŋa kayina, la Fa'a Sia kayima ma'a en boi, wuu ban yi' ya beere putɛ'yinɛ wa puan se'em la.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ biŋe yeti, Aberaham yuum tari la dayɔɔhi siyi, ti kayima yuum dɛna a pɔge da'aberɛ la bia, ti kayima la me dɛna a mɛŋa pɔga, en dagi da'aberɛ la bia.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Gee Fa'a Sia la, la sakerɛ puan, tumam mu'ɛ la nini gura beere tuntɔɔnɛ.

eng: There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism.
local: Daana ka ayima, sakerɛ deyina, la ko'om sua buyina.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Kayima la, se'em en de da'aberɛ la bia la, ba dɔgɛ kaŋa doohe la inganɛ puan, gee kayima la me a mɛŋa pɔga la n dagi da'aberɛ la bia la, ba dɔgɛ kaŋa doohe la nɔbiŋere puan.

eng: and one God and Father of all, who is over all, through all, and in all.
local: Yinɛ deyina, la Sɔ ayima en boi bo' sɛla woo, gana sɛla woo, tuna la sɛla woo, gee bɔna la sɛla woo.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Beni zuo, Kiresta puan, fum ŋma ya yoo, fum ka ŋma yoo, la ka tari sɛla tɔ'ɔra. Yele se'ere ma'a en tari sɛla tɔ'ɔra dela, sakerɛ en dole nɔŋerɛ puan.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Yele ana wa dela makerɛ. Pɔgesina wa ze'ele bo' la nɔpu'ulegɔ buyi'. Pɔka kayima, yelemɛŋerɛ, dela Hega, ze'ele Senai Zoore la zuo, dɔgera kɔma en de da'aba.

eng: God has given his grace to each one of us measured out by the gift that is given by Christ.
local: Gee tu ayima ayima, Yinɛ bo' la tu la tu zu'uhegɔ tu zu'uhegɔ ti bu tibehum zuna la Kiresta ti a dikɛ bo' tu se'em la.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Ya daa zoti la suŋa suŋa. Ani en gu' ya ti ya da nam yelemɛŋerɛ la?.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Hega dela Senai Zoore en boi Arebiya timpaberɛ. La ze'ele bo' la zina Yerusalɛm wa, Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔka wa boi la da'aberɛ puan la ka kɔma.

eng: Thats why scripture says, When he climbed up to the heights, he captured prisoners, and he gave gifts to people .
local: Ŋwana zuo ti yelehum la yeti, En zom saazuo ha la, a nyɔkɛ sɛla en nyɔgere la mɛ a mɛŋa, gee bo' a nɛreba bo'olum.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Pã'ahegɔ kuna taaba ka ze'ele Yinɛ se'ere n yi' ya la zi'an na.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Gee pɔka kayima la me ze'ele bo' la Yerusalɛm sɛka en boi saazuo ha la. A dagi da'aberɛ gee me dɛna tu ma.

eng: What does the phrase he climbed up mean if it doesnt mean that he had first gone down into the lower regions, the earth?.
local: Lan yeti, A zom saazuo ha la, de vuurɛ dela deni, gee de vuurɛ me le dɛna la deni, lan yeti, a me sige la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, sige timpaberɛ timpaberɛ ha la?.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Dabelɛ fii wa n ihegere tizom za'a.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Nyɛɛna, kundɔgerɛ wa, fum se'em en ka dɔgɛ kɔma wa, kɛ̃ baaŋa puan la fum voole, fum se'em en ka tabelɛ nyɛ dɔgum too wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, pɔgesɛka en de ze'kpaahenɛ la kɔma zo'e mɛ gana pɔgesɛka en ele la kɔma.

eng: The one who went down is the same one who climbed up above all the heavens so that he might fill everything.
local: Se'em daana n sige na la, eŋa en bala en zom saa zuin zuin ha la, ti a sɔna sɛla woo biha.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Mam mi' ya suŋa suŋa Daana la puan ti ya ka ti'ihɛ buyi' buyi'. Gee se'em daana en tuleni ya puti'ira la wan nyɛ a namehegɔ paaɛ paaɛ la en pun dɛna se'em me.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: Nananawa, yamam n suurɔ wa, ya dela nɔbiŋere la kɔma, wuu Aziki la.

eng: He gave some apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers.
local: Bo'olum la ti a bo' la dela, basɛba wan dɛna tuntuneba, ti basɛba dɛna nɔtɔgeheba, ti sɛba dɛna ayehemuulɛduma, ti basɛba dɛna dunkiinduma la pa'aleba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: Gee n suurɔ, wa, ŋwani ti ba kelum nameha mam, mam han kelum muulɛ ni ŋmaa yɛla? La han de ni la bala, Kiresta kum pumpuuredɔɔ zuo la muulegɔ la kan tara ni daaŋɔ.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Gee wuu bisɛka ti ba dɔgɛ doohe inganɛ puan la, en yuum mɛlehɛ bisɛka ti ba dɔgɛ doohe Sia puan la se'em la, la dela bala mɛa nananawa.

eng: His purpose was to equip Gods people for the work of serving and building up the body of Christ.
local: ti a kpemehe nɛresunduma la ti ba tuna ba tuuma, la ba mɛ lageŋɔ la n de Kiresta iŋa la.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Gee Yinɛ gɔŋɔ la puan yeti bu? La yeti, Dige ya da'aberɛ la, la ka bia la ti ba yehe. Se'ere en sɔi la, da'aberɛ la bia la kan lagum tɔrɛ lɔgerɔ la, la pɔgesɛka en de a mɛŋa pɔga la en dagi da'aberɛ la bia.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Mam boti ni ti nɛresɛba en pɛregeri ya wa la, ba pun kɔ'ɔm lage ni ba mɛhi!.

eng: until we all reach the unity of faith and knowledge of Gods Son. Gods goal is for us to become mature adults—to be fully grown, measured by the standard of the fullness of Christ.
local: gura ti tu za'a ta wa'am na naaɛ taaba la sakerɛ deyina, zo'e Yinɛ Dayua la mi'ilum puan, zo'e dɛna bunkureba ti Kiresta naanɛ la to'e tu za'a.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Bala, n suurɔ, tumam dela kɔma, dagena pɔgesɛka n de da'aberɛ la kɔma, gee tu dela pɔgesɛka en dagi da'aberɛ la kɔma.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: Ba yi' ya wa'am la fai puan na, n suurɔ, gee da dikɛ ya fai kana wa galena ya mɛhi. Gee tugum doohe ya nɔŋerɛ puan, tuna bɔ'ɔra taaba suŋa suŋa.

eng: As a result, we arent supposed to be infants any longer who can be tossed and blown around by every wind that comes from teaching with deceitful scheming and the tricks people play to deliberately mislead others.
local: Tu da le dɛna kɔma le yɛ'ɛha, kaara kina nɛŋa la pooren, ti pa'alegɔ en ani wuu sabeeo ziira ya ɛɛra zi'an woo, pa'ali seko n de nɛreba mɛhi nigeŋɔ la nyaleŋɔ la pã'ahegɔ pã'ahegɔ la.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ze'elego la za'a ninmu'urɛ dela Fum Nɔŋɛ fum suuɔ wuu fum mɛŋa la.

eng: Instead, by speaking the truth with love, lets grow in every way into Christ.
local: Gee tu dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ duma tu vom puan, la nɔŋerɛ, tu dela tu zo'e sɛla woo puan ta paaɛ se'em n de zuodaana, Kiresta la n ani se'em la.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Gee la han dɛna ti ya duni la taaba, gee yi'ira taaba, mini ya suŋa ti la kan yuue la yan n ba'ahɛ taaba lipi.

eng: who is the head. The whole body grows from him, as it is joined and held together by all the supporting ligaments. The body makes itself grow in that it builds itself up with love as each one does its part.
local: Eŋa puan ti iŋa la za'a nyɔkɛ taaba suŋa suŋa dɛna inyiŋa doohe la giile woo n tuni de tuunɛ suŋa se'em bahɛ ti inganɛ la zɔ'ɔra doohe la dinɛ n mɛti de mɛŋa nɔŋerɛ puan la.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: Mam yeti ya yeti, voni ya Sia la n boti se'em la, gee sore da bɔna la fii me ti ya tigehɛ ya ingɔŋɔ kɔrɛ la.

eng: So Im telling you this, and I insist on it in the Lord: you shouldnt live your life like the Gentiles anymore. They base their lives on pointless thinking.
local: Nananawa, mam ka'ani ya mɛ gee nyɛta ya la ninmu'urɛ Daana la puan yeti, da le vɔni ya wuu sɛba n dagi Yuuduma la n vɔi se'em, ba putɛ' yooro la puan la.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ingɔŋɔ la n boti sɛla la, dagi sɛla ti Sia la bɔta. Sia la me en boti sɛla la dagi sɛla ti ingɔŋɔ la bɔta. Bana bayi' wa ka sakerɛ bɔ'ɔra taaba, ti la gu' ti fum da ta'am ita fum boti se'em.

eng: and they are in the dark in their reasoning. They are disconnected from Gods life because of their ignorance and their closed hearts.
local: Lika n pi' ba bɔkerɛ, yehe ba mɛhi Yinɛ vom sum la puan, la ban ka mi' sɛsɛla, gee ti ba suya dagelum la zuo.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Gee Sia la han pa'ala ya, ya dagi yamehi bo' ze'elego la.

eng: They are people who lack all sense of right and wrong, and who have turned themselves over to doing whatever feels good and to practicing every sort of corruption along with greed.
local: Ba zuto ki mɛ ti ba dikɛ ba mɛhi kɔ'ɔm bo' kunkoma'ahum, be'ero, ita dɛgerɔ tuuma woo.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
local: Ingɔŋɔ tuuma la ka sugɛ. Ba dela, yaalegɔ, dɛgerɔ, inganɛ kɔrɛ.

eng: But you didnt learn that sort of thing from Christ.
local: Dagi yamam n zamehɛ Kiresta sore la se'em n bala!.

eng: idolatry, drug use and casting spells, hate, fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, conflict, selfishness, group rivalry.
local: baga kaabegɔ, sɔ̃nini, diŋo, zaberɛ, nyuure, sunsunyiim, nɔkpe'ene, yelehego, Pa'alebe'ero.

eng: Since you really listened to him and you were taught how the truth is in Jesus.
local: La de asira ti ya wum a yele, gee ti ba me pa'alɛ ya a yele ti yelemɛŋerɛ boi la Yezu puan.

eng: jealousy, drunkenness, partying, and other things like that. I warn you as I have already warned you, that those who do these kinds of things wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: nintɛrega, buka, punya'anɛ, la yelesɛ'a n ani ŋwana. N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ wuu mam pun yele ni ya la ninmu'urɛ yia se'em la, ti sɛba en iti yele ana taaba kan lagum kɛ̃ Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la.

eng: change the former way of life that was part of the person you once were, corrupted by deceitful desires.
local: Ba pa'alɛ ya ti ya bahɛ ya vom kɛka sore la, ya naanɛ kɛka la, n de ŋmɛlema naanɛ ti kunkoma'ahum pirɛ dɛ la.

eng: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
local: Gee Sia la walɛ dela, nɔŋerɛ, nyɛɛma, suma'ahum, ŋmibega, pupɛɛla itesuŋɔ, la sakerɛ.

eng: Instead, renew the thinking in your mind by the Spirit.
local: gee malum tee ya mɛhi sia puan la ya puti'ira puan.

eng: gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against things like this.
local: zulaam, la nyɔkɛ n mɛŋa. Ze'elego ka boi gana yele ana.

eng: and clothe yourself with the new person created according to Gods image in justice and true holiness.
local: Gee yɛ ya mɛhi la vompaalega naanɛ la, naam se'ere n naam ŋwana la Yinɛ mɛŋa la, dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ tuntɔɔnɛ la tuulum la.

eng: Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the self with its passions and its desires.
local: Sɛba n de Kiresta Yezu deni la, kpa' ba inganɛ la mɛ, la de kunkoma'ahum la de kɔrɛ la.

eng: Therefore, after you have gotten rid of lying, Each of you must tell the truth to your neighbor because we are parts of each other in the same body.
local: Bala zuo, tumam n baheri pã'ahegɔ vom la, bahɛ ya ti tu za'a tɔgera yelemɛŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra tu suurɔ. Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a dela iŋa kayima.

eng: If we live by the Spirit, lets follow the Spirit.
local: Tu han vɔna la Sia la vom, bahɛ ya ti Sia la me pa'alɛ tɔ.

eng: Be angry without sinning. Dont let the sun set on your anger.
local: Fum suure ta'am ihege gee da tum be'em. Da bahɛ ti yinɛ ma'a la fum sunsunyiihum la.

eng: Lets not become arrogant, make each other angry, or be jealous of each other.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti tu dɛna pumpã'aheduma, pagera taaba, tara nintɛrega la taaba.

eng: Dont provide an opportunity for the devil.
local: me da bo' Sagena la yɔ'ɔ fum puan.

eng: Thieves should no longer steal. Instead, they should go to work, using their hands to do good so that they will have something to share with whoever is in need.
local: Nageyigehi bahɛ zua, gee tugum bahɛ ya ba ti ba tuna la ninmu'urɛ, ba tuna yelemɛŋerɛ la ba mɛhi nu'uhi, la ba ta'am tara, gee me suŋera naheduma.

eng: Dont let any foul words come out of your mouth. Only say what is helpful when it is needed for building up the community so that it benefits those who hear what you say.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti yetɔgepu'uha zɛ'ɛta ya nɔya puan yɛhera na. Gee yetɔge sebo en tari vuurɛ gee mɛta ma'a, sansɛka n de ya tɔgɛ. Bala ti yelehum la wan tara zu'uhegɔ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n wum la.

eng: Dont make the Holy Spirit of God unhappy—you were sealed by him for the day of redemption.
local: Me da dɛgɛ ya Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la suure, sɛ sɛka ti Yinɛ di'e ya, daalɛ ya a mɛŋa daalehum gura faarɛ daarɛ la.

eng: Put aside all bitterness, losing your temper, anger, shouting, and slander, along with every other evil.
local: Dina zuo, yehe ya, ya mɛhi yaarɛ woo puan la sunsuntuulɛ la sunsunyiihum, nyabega, yu'sageŋɔ, ta ta' la pɔgeleŋɔ woo.

eng: Be kind, compassionate, and forgiving to each other, in the same way God forgave you in Christ.
local: Gee iti ya sum bo'ori ya taaba, gee zoti ya taaba nimbu'ɔ, gee dita sugeri bɔ'ɔra taaba wuu Yinɛ n di ya be'em sugeri bo' ya se'em Kiresta zuo la.

eng: Therefore, imitate God like dearly loved children.
local: Dina zuo, tɔgehi ya Yinɛ wuu kɔma ti a nini tigɛ la ya la.

eng: Live your life with love, following the example of Christ, who loved us and gave himself for us. He was a sacrificial offering that smelled sweet to God.
local: gee vɔni ya la nɔŋerɛ wuu Kiresta n nɔŋɛ tɔ se'em, dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' tu se'em, lebege nyuuŋo suŋɔ kaabegɔ bo'olum ti ba kaabɛ bo' Yinɛ la.

eng: Sexual immorality, and any kind of impurity or greed, shouldnt even be mentioned among you, which is right for holy persons.
local: Gee yaluntuuma la dɛgerɔ tuuma woo, bii nyika tuuma da pun puta ya puan, wuu la nari se'em nɛresunduma tiŋasuka la.

eng: Obscene language, silly talk, or vulgar jokes arent acceptable for believers. Instead, there should be thanksgiving.
local: Bahɛ ya ti nyanɛ tugum, gɔɔm tugum la yetɔgum yooro kɔ'ɔm yehe ya zi'an. Gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya pɛgera Yinɛ.

eng: Because you know for sure that persons who are sexually immoral, impure, or greedy—which happens when things become gods—those persons wont inherit the kingdom of Christ and God.
local: Mini ya dina suŋa suŋa, ti se'em n tuni yaluntuuma, bii galeŋɔ daana, bii se'em n bɛ' , kan tara tɔtɛ kɛ̃' Kiresta la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: Nobody should deceive you with stupid ideas. Gods anger comes down on those who are disobedient because of this kind of thing.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pã'ahɛ ya la gɔɔm yetɔgum. Se'ere en sɔi la, yele ana zuo ti Yinɛ sunsunyiihum wan sige sɛba n ka nane la zuo.

eng: So you shouldnt have anything to do with them.
local: Dinɛ zuo, da tari ya yelese'ere ita la nɛrebamam taaba.

eng: You were once darkness, but now you are light in the Lord, so live your life as children of light.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa nan boi la lika puan, gee nananawa Daana la puan, ya dela la niihum. Bala Vɔni ya wuu kɔmsɛba n boi niihum puan la.

eng: Light produces fruit that consists of every sort of goodness, justice, and truth.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, niihum wa wala boi sɛla woo n ani suŋa, gee dɛna tuuntɔɔ, la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

eng: Therefore, test everything to see whats pleasing to the Lord.
local: Zigeri ya ti ya ɛ gee zamehɛ sɛla n tigeri Daana la nini.

eng: and dont participate in the unfruitful actions of darkness. Instead, you should reveal the truth about them.
local: Da lagum tuna ya lika puan tuunsɛ'a n ka tari walesuma la, gee tugum viie ya ba fihi.

eng: Its embarrassing to even talk about what certain persons do in secret.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la pun dɛna la vi ti fum tɔgera yelesɛ'a ti nɛrebana taaba sugera ita a la yele.

eng: But everything exposed to the light is revealed by the light.
local: Gee sɛla woo ti bugum niiɛ ze'ele, la ka tari suka.

eng: Everything that is revealed by the light is light. Therefore, it says, Wake up, sleeper! Get up from the dead, and Christ will shine on you. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla woo n ka sugɛ dela niihum n niiɛ bahɛ la zuo. Bala ti la gulehɛ yeti, Gɔgihera wa, ihege! Ihege kum la puan, ti Kiresta niiɛ bahɛ fum zuo.

eng: So be careful to live your life wisely, not foolishly.
local: Bala, Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa, yan vɔi se'em. Ya da vɔna wuu nɛreba n ka tari yɛm, gee ya vɔna wuu yɛmduma la.

eng: Take advantage of every opportunity because these are evil times.
local: Ya tuna tuunsuma saŋa woo puan, se'ere sɔi la, dabeha la dela dabeha tooro.

eng: Because of this, dont be ignorant, but understand the Lords will.
local: Bala, da deni ya geto gee tugum bɔkeri ya Daana la sumboolum n de se'em.

eng: Dont get drunk on wine, which produces depravity. Instead, be filled with the Spirit in the following ways.
local: Da lɛɛra ya mɛhi la daam bugera,bala dela yaalegɔ. Gee tugum pirɛ ya mɛhi la Fa'a Sia la.

eng: speak to each other with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; sing and make music to the Lord in your hearts.
local: tari yuum suma tɔgera bɔ'ɔra taaba, kpemehego yuuma la na'ahegɔ yuuma, la Sia yuuma sɛ'a n boi ya puan. Yuuni ya la pupeelum ya suya puan bɔ'ɔra Daana la.

eng: always give thanks to God the Father for everything in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bo'ori ya Yinɛ n de Sɔ la fara saŋa woo puan, la sɛla woo puan tu Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la puan.

eng: and submit to each other out of respect for Christ.
local: Sigehe ya mɛhi bo' taaba, ti la dɛna ya na'ahegɔ n bala bɔ'ɔra Kiresta.

eng: For example, wives should submit to their husbands as if to the Lord.
local: Pɔgeba, sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ya sireba wuu yan ani se'em bo' Daana la.

eng: A husband is the head of his wife like Christ is head of the church, that is, the savior of the body.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, asira n de a pɔga zuodaana wuu Kiresta n de lageŋɔ la zuodaana se'em la. Lageŋɔ seko n de a iŋa la, ti a mɛŋa dɛna ka faarɛ la.

eng: So wives submit to their husbands in everything like the church submits to Christ.
local: Wuu Lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta nu'uhen se'em la, bala mɛa ti pɔgehi me wan sigehe ba mɛhi bo' ba Sireba sɛla woo puan.

eng: As for husbands, love your wives just like Christ loved the church and gave himself for her.
local: Sireba, nɔŋɛ ya pɔgeba, wuu Kiresta n nɔŋɛ lageŋɔ la se'em, gee dikɛ a mɛŋa vom bo' ku se'em la.

eng: He did this to make her holy by washing her in a bath of water with the word.
local: A iŋɛ ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la dɛna la tuulum lageŋɔ doohe en duuhe ko'om en de a yelehum la.

eng: He did this to present himself with a splendid church, one without any sort of stain or wrinkle on her clothes, but rather one that is holy and blameless.
local: wuu n boti a dikɛ lageŋɔ la, la ku suŋa la bo' a mɛŋa, ti sɛla da ti'ɛ, bii lirum, bii sɛla kalum kɔ la. Ɛ̃ɛ, ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la wan dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ ka tara dɛkɔ.

eng: Thats how husbands ought to love their wives—in the same way as they do their own bodies. Anyone who loves his wife loves himself.
local: Bala mɛa ti sireba tara ba pɔgeba nɔŋerɛ sanɛ wuu ban nɔŋɛ ba mɛhi ihe se'em la. Fum se'em n nɔŋɛ fum pɔga, nɔŋɛ la fum mɛŋa.

eng: No one ever hates his own body, but feeds it and takes care of it just like Christ does for the church.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ka boi yaaɛ a mɛŋa iŋa. Gee a tugum diiha ka mɛ la dinu'uma, gee bihera ka suŋa, wuu Kiresta n iŋɛ se'em bo' lageŋɔ la.

eng: because we are parts of his body.
local: Se'ere zuo, tumam dela a iŋa la luga a sɛ'a.

eng: This is why a man will leave his father and mother and be united with his wife, and the two of them will be one body .
local: Yele dina wa zuo, buraa wan bahɛ ka Sɔ la ka ma gee dikɛ ka mɛŋa naaɛ buyina la ka pɔga ti ba bayi' la lebege bunyinɛ.

eng: Marriage is a significant allegory, and Im applying it to Christ and the church.
local: Yele dina wa dela yelekãtɛ se'ere n sugɛ ti mam dikɛ dɛ makera Kiresta la Lageŋɔ la.

eng: In any case, as for you individually, each one of you should love his wife as himself, and wives should respect their husbands.
local: Nɛra woo, lan pun ana se'em me, dela a nɔŋɛ a pɔga wuu a mɛŋa la, ti pɔga la me dɛna ka na'aha ka asira.

eng: As for children, obey your parents in the Lord, because it is right.
local: Kɔma, nane ya ya dɔgereba, se'ere en de ma'a en bala Daana sore la puan.

eng: The commandment Honor your father and mother is the first one with a promise attached.
local: Na'aha fum Sɔ, la fum ma. Dina en de yia nɔze'elego, tara nɔbiŋere n yeti.

eng: so that things will go well for you, and you will live for a long time in the land .
local: Bala ti la wan ana fum suŋa, gee ti fum nyuura wugɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: As for parents, dont provoke your children to anger, but raise them with discipline and instruction about the Lord.
local: Sɔduma, da daani ya ya kɔma ti ba suure nyiira, gee tugum bihɛ ya ti ba zɔ'ɔra la tube-dɔlegerɛ la Daana la sore puan kã'aŋɔ.

eng: As for slaves, obey your human masters with fear and trembling and with sincere devotion to Christ.
local: Da'aba, nane ya ya timpaberɛ wa zuo kpɛ'ɛnduma, la dabeem la gbi'a, la suyinɛ wuu yan nane Kiresta se'em la.

eng: Dont work to make yourself look good and try to flatter people, but act like slaves of Christ carrying out Gods will from the heart.
local: Da iti ya ŋwana ba nɛŋan ma'a ti ya yeen pee ba suure, gee ya dela Kiresta yamehi, tuna Yinɛ sumboolum la ya suya za'a za'a.

eng: Serve your owners enthusiastically, as though you were serving the Lord and not human beings.
local: Tuni ya ya tuuma la pupeelum kãtɛ wuu ya tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana la, gee dagi asaala.

eng: You know that the Lord will reward every person who does what is right, whether that person is a slave or a free person.
local: Tu mina ti, sum sebo woo ti tu iŋɛ tu wan lebehe to'e la bala mɛa tu Daana zi'an, tu dɛna yamehi bii tu bɔna fai.

eng: As for masters, treat your slaves in the same way. Stop threatening them, because you know that both you and your slaves have a master in heaven. He doesnt distinguish between people on the basis of status.
local: Yamam kpɛ'ɛnduma me, iti ya ya yamehi bala mɛa. Da vuuheri ya ba, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti yamam la bamam za'a tari la Daana ayima ti a bɔna saazuo ha, se'em ti yelehego ka bɔna a puan.

eng: Finally, be strengthened by the Lord and his powerful strength.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, kpe'em ya mɛ Daana la, la a pankãtɛ la puan.

eng: Put on Gods armor so that you can make a stand against the tricks of the devil.
local: Bobe ya mɛhi la Yinɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la za'a, la ya ta'am ze'ele zabɛ la Sagena la vuuhego la za'a.

eng: We arent fighting against human enemies but against rulers, authorities, forces of cosmic darkness, and spiritual powers of evil in the heavens.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka zaberɛ mɛ la dima en tari ziim la kuba, gee tu zaberɛ mɛ la bumbɛ'ɛhi en sɔi zi'ihi biha, tu zaberɛ mɛ la pahi, la kpe'eŋo en boi sabeeo puan, buuri sankana wa en boi lika puan wa. Tu zaberɛ mɛ la sia puan pambɛ'ɛhi en boi zi'ihi se'ehi saazuo ha.

eng: Therefore, pick up the full armor of God so that you can stand your ground on the evil day and after you have done everything possible to still stand.
local: Bala zuo la, bobe ya mɛhi la Yinɛ zaberɛ lɔgerɔ la za'a, bala ti ya wan ta'am sɛ' ze'ele dabehebe'o la daarɛ kankaŋɛ, dabehebe'o daarɛ la han ta paaɛ ya na. Ti ya me han ta zabɛ paaɛ de ba'ahegɔ, ya wan kelum ta'am sɛ' ze'ele kankaŋɛ.

eng: So stand with the belt of truth around your waist, justice as your breastplate.
local: Bala zuo, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋɛ. Gee bahɛ ti yelemɛŋerɛ ana wuu sɛganɛ la lu' ya sɛɛhi la, gee me bahɛ ti tuntɔɔnɛ dɛna ya nyu'ɔ tɛ'ɛlega.

eng: and put shoes on your feet so that you are ready to spread the good news of peace.
local: Tagera sɛ'a ti ya wan pire ni, pirɛ ya sɛla woo n wan ta'am maahum ya ti ya muulɛ suma'ahum faarɛ yelehum wa.

eng: Above all, carry the shield of faith so that you can extinguish the flaming arrows of the evil one.
local: Lɔge tuna wa za'a la, dikɛ ya sakerɛ ti la dɛna zaba tɛ'ɛlega, ti ya wan ta'am kiihera yabega pɛɛma la woo ti be'emdaana la tɛɛra la.

eng: Take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is Gods word.
local: Tara ya faarɛ ti la dɛna ya zaberɛ zuo wanɛ, la Sia puan su'a la n de Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: Offer prayers and petitions in the Spirit all the time. Stay alert by hanging in there and praying for all believers.
local: Bala pu'uheri ya Sia la puan saŋa woo, ya pu'uhegɔ la ya suhega woo puan. Ti ba'ahegɔ puan, da ga' ya gihera gee sɔheri ya saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a.

eng: As for me, pray that when I open my mouth, Ill get a message that confidently makes this secret plan of the gospel known.
local: Pu'uheri ya bo'ori ya mam mɛ, ti n han ni yeti n tɔgɛ, Yinɛ bo' mam yelehum, ti n tɔgɛ la sukpe'ene ti nɛreba baŋɛ sugelum yele se'ere n boi faarɛ yelehum wa puan.

eng: Im an ambassador in chains for the sake of the gospel. Pray so that the Lord will give me the confidence to say what I have to say.
local: sebo zuo ti mam sooe Kiresta na'arɛ ti ba ba' mam la bãhi. Pu'uheri ya Yinɛ bɔ'ɔra mam ti n ta'am muulɛ dɛ la sukpe'ene, wuu lan de n tɔgɛ se'em la.

eng: Tychicus, my loved brother and faithful servant of the Lord, can inform you about my situation and what Im doing.
local: Bala me ti yamam me mina lan ani mam se'em, la mam iti sɛla. Takikuse wan yele sɛla woo. A dela tu suuɔ suŋɔ gee me tara sakerɛ Daana tuuma la puan.

eng: Ive sent him for this reason—so that you will know about us. He can reassure you.
local: Dina wa zuo ti n tuna en na, ti a bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ tumam n ani se'em, gee kpemehe ya suya.

eng: May there be peace with the brothers and sisters as well as love with the faith that comes from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Suma'ahum bɔna la lageŋɔ la nɛreba za'a, la nɔŋerɛ n dole la sakerɛ, ze'ele tu Sɔ Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an bɔna la ya.

eng: May grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ forever.
local: Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bɔna la yamam sɛba woo n tara nɔŋese'ere n ka tari ba'ahegɔ la bɔ'ɔra tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: From Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus. To all those in Philippi who are Gods people in Christ Jesus, along with your supervisors and servants.
local: Mam Pɔɔl la Timoti en de Kiresta Yezu yamehi la en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa, bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ nɛresunduma la za'a en boi Kiresta Yezu puan Filipi timpaberɛ la, la lageŋɔ nɛheduma, la kiinduma la.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an, bɔna la ya.

eng: I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Mam bo'ori n Yinɛ fara mɛ, saŋa woo ti n ni ta tiɛ ya yele.

eng: Im thankful for all of you every time I pray, and its always a prayer full of joy.
local: pu'uheri la nyɛɛma n pu'uhegɔ woo puan bɔ'ɔra ya za'a.

eng: Im glad because of the way you have been my partners in the ministry of the gospel from the time you first believed it until now.
local: Se'ere zuo, ya lagum suŋɛ mam mɛ faarɛ yelehum wa puan, ze'ele pɔhega daarɛ la wa'am na paaɛ zina.

eng: Im sure about this: the one who started a good work in you will stay with you to complete the job by the day of Christ Jesus.
local: Bala, mam tari sakerɛ la yele dina wa puan yeti, se'em daana en pi'ilum tunsunɛ dina wa ya puan la, wan tari dɛ bala ta ba'ahɛ, Yezu Kiresta lɛbega dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: I have good reason to think this way about all of you because I keep you in my heart. You are all my partners in Gods grace, both during my time in prison and in the defense and support of the gospel.
local: La dela ma'a bo' mam ti n ti'iha ya za'a yele ŋwana, beni zuo, ya tari mam mɛ ya suya puan, la yamam za'a en lagum tɔrɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la mɛ la mam, n paka wa puan, la mam en ka sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum wa zuo, gee sɛɛra bɔ zɛ'ɛta se'em la.

eng: God is my witness that I feel affection for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
local: Yinɛ en de mam aze'nyɛ daana, mam n mu'ɛ nini ba' bɔta ti n nyɛ ya za'a se'em, la Kiresta Yezu nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la.

eng: This is my prayer: that your love might become even more and more rich with knowledge and all kinds of insight.
local: Mam pu'uhegɔ en de se'em en ŋwana, ti ya nɔŋerɛ wan zo'e yabera, ti ya mi'ilum malum pa'ahɛ, ti ya pirɛ la bɔkerɛ.

eng: I pray this so that you will be able to decide what really matters and so you will be sincere and blameless on the day of Christ.
local: ti la suŋɛ ya, ti ya zamehɛ mina sɛla en ani suŋa gana za'a, bala ti Kiresta lɛbega dabeherɛ na la, ya wan dɛna Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba, ka tara dɛkɔ.

eng: I pray that you will then be filled with the fruit of righteousness, which comes from Jesus Christ, in order to give glory and praise to God.
local: Yan diŋɛ wam tuuntɔɔ wala sɛ'a en ze'eti Yezu Kiresta zi'an na, tara na'ahegɔ la pɛka bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the things that have happened to me have actually advanced the gospel.
local: N boti ya baŋɛ mɛ sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ti sɛla en iŋɛ mam la, tugum suŋɛ mam mɛ ti n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum wa.

eng: The whole Praetorian Guard and everyone else knows that Im in prison for Christ.
local: ti la niiɛ bo' gureba la za'a, la nɛra woo en boi bilam, ti ba baŋɛ yeti, mam en boi pakadeen wa dela Kiresta zuo.

eng: Most of the brothers and sisters have had more confidence through the Lord to speak the word boldly and bravely because of my jail time.
local: Ŋwana n bahɛ ti tu suurɔ zo'e zo'e nyɛ inkpeŋere Daana la puan mam, paka wa puan ka zɔta ti ba tɔgɛ yelehum la, la sukpe'ene ti dabeem ka bɔna.

eng: Some certainly preach Christ with jealous and competitive motives, but others preach with good motives.
local: Sɛba muuli Kiresta mɛ, nintɛrega la nyuure zuo, gee sɛba me muulegɔ mɛ la putɛ'sunɛ.

eng: They are motivated by love, because they know that Im put here to give a defense of the gospel.
local: Nɛrebana wa muuli Kiresta la nɔŋerɛ puan, gee mina ti ban tari mam wa'am ka'lam na wa dela mam en ka sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum la zuo.

eng: the others preach Christ because of their selfish ambition. They are insincere, hoping to cause me more pain while Im in prison.
local: Gee sɛba me muuli Kiresta la nyika puti'ira puan, dagi la ba susunɛ puan, gee ba malum pa'ahɛ mam namehegɔ, n paka deo bɔŋa wa puan.

eng: What do I think about this? Just this: since Christ is proclaimed in every possible way, whether from dishonest or true motives, Im glad and Ill continue to be glad.
local: Gee sɛla en pakɛ dela, yele dina wa ma'a. Ti ba yeen muulɛ Kiresta sadɔtɔ seto woo puan, pumpɔreŋɔ puan, bii yelemɛŋerɛ puan, mam nyɛɛni mɛ ŋwana puan. Ɛ̃ɛ, mam wan kelum nyɛɛna la bala.

eng: Im glad because I know that this will result in my release through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' ti ya pu'uhegɔ zuo, la Yezu Kiresta Fa'a Sia la suŋɛ zuo, la wan ta tee dɛna mam faarɛ.

eng: It is my expectation and hope that I wont be put to shame in anything. Rather, I hope with daring courage that Christs greatness will be seen in my body, now as always, whether I live or die.
local: La dela sɛla ti mam kɔ'ɔm ana kɛli, gee me tari puti'irɛ, ti vi kan di mam sadɔɔ seko woo puan, gee doohe mam en muuli la sukpe'ene wa zuo, Kiresta yu'urɛ wan nyɛ pɛka nananawa, wuu la ni pun ani se'em mam iŋa wa puan la, mam vom bii n kum la.

eng: Because for me, living serves Christ and dying is even better.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, han dɛna mam wa, mam vom dela n tuna bɔ'ɔra Kiresta, ti n kum dɛna n nyuurɔ.

eng: If I continue to live in this world, I get results from my work.
local: N han yeti n vɔna n ingɔŋɔ wa puan, la dela tuunsuma n tari wala bɔ'ɔra mam. La bahɛ ti mam ka mina mam wan looe sɛla.

eng: But I dont know what I prefer. Im torn between the two because I want to leave this life and be with Christ, which is far better.
local: La kpe'em bo' mam mɛ yele ana ayi' wa puan. Mam puti'irɛ puan, mam boti ni ti n bahɛ vom buna wa mɛ, gee kiŋɛ ta bɔna la Kiresta, bala kɔ'ɔm nyana mɛ.

eng: However, its more important for me to stay in this world for your sake.
local: Gee n kelum vɔna ingɔŋɔ wa puan me ani suŋa mɛ yamam zuo.

eng: Im sure of this: I will stay alive and remain with all of you to help your progress and the joy of your faith.
local: Mam n diŋɛ mina yele dina wa suŋa suŋa la, mam mi' ti n wan vɔna, la n tuna la ya za'a, ya nɛŋa tɔlega, la ya nyɛɛma ya sakerɛ puan zuo.

eng: and to increase your pride in Christ Jesus through my presence when I visit you again.
local: Ŋwana ti n wan ta'am lagum ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ paaɛ paaɛ la ya Yezu Kiresta puan, n han ta le wa'am ya zi'an na.

eng: Most important, live together in a manner worthy of Christs gospel. Do this, whether I come and see you or Im absent and hear about you. Do this so that you stand firm, united in one spirit and mind as you struggle together to remain faithful to the gospel.
local: Yamam yeen vɔna ya vom, sadɔɔ seko en naaɛ la Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, ti n han nyaŋɛ wa'am na bihɛ ya, bii n han pun ka bɔna la ya gee wum ya yele, n wan mina ti ya ze' la kankaŋɛ, Sia kayima puan, naaɛ taaba la putɛ'yinɛ, zigera la taaba ti ya tara sakerɛ faarɛ yelehum la puan.

eng: That way, you wont be afraid of anything your enemies do. Your faithfulness and courage are a sign of their coming destruction and your salvation, which is from God.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti ya dinduma bihɛ ya yoo. Han dɛna bamam, ŋwana dela aze'nyɛ daana bo' ba sageŋɔ la, gee tugum dɛna yamam faarɛ. Ŋwana wa dela Yinɛ en iŋɛ dɛ.

eng: God has generously granted you the privilege, not only of believing in Christ but also of suffering for Christs sake.
local: Yinɛ ka doohe a zu'uhegɔ la puan bo' tu la yɔ'ɔ ti tu nam la Kiresta ma'a, gee tu nameheri a zuo me.

eng: You are having the same struggle that you saw me face and now hear that Im still facing.
local: yan diŋɛ zigera zika sɛka ti ya nyɛ ti mam zigeri la, gee wum ti n kelum zigera ka la zuo.

eng: Therefore, if there is any encouragement in Christ, any comfort in love, any sharing in the Spirit, any sympathy.
local: Han dɛna ti kpemehego seko boi Kiresta puan, ti subugehum en ze'eti nɔŋerɛ puan na bɔna, han dɛna ti Fa'a Sia la puan lageŋɔ boi, han dɛna ti nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la taaba boi, ti taaba giile tarega bɔna.

eng: complete my joy by thinking the same way, having the same love, being united, and agreeing with each other.
local: yi bahɛ ya ti mam nyɛɛŋɔ pirɛ, doohe ya tari putɛ'yinɛ, nɔŋerɛ deyina la taaba, nɔyinɛ la suure deyina.

eng: Dont do anything for selfish purposes, but with humility think of others as better than yourselves.
local: Da iti ya sɛla nyika puti'irɛ puan, bii pa'alɛ n mɛŋa yooro puan, gee sigehe n mɛhi puan, tugum bihɛ ya nyɛta sɛba gana ya mɛhi.

eng: Instead of each person watching out for their own good, watch out for what is better for others.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti ya se'em kɔ'ɔm biha a mɛŋa ma'a sum yele, gee ya biha basɛba sum yele me.

eng: Adopt the attitude that was in Christ Jesus.
local: Bahɛ ya ti putɛ'se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la, bɔna yamam me puan.

eng: Though he was in the form of God, he did not consider being equal with God something to exploit.
local: Eŋa se'em, en daa pun dɛna Yinɛ mɛŋa naanɛ la, a daa ka bihɛ eŋa en zuni la Yinɛ la, ti la dɛna sɛla bo' en. Dɛna sɛla ti a gurɛ kankaŋi.

eng: But he emptied himself by taking the form of a slave and by becoming like human beings. When he found himself in the form of a human.
local: gee a daa bihɛ a mɛŋa la zaŋa, dikɛ a mɛŋa lebege yameŋa, ti ba dɔgɛ en ti a dɛna asaala. Tara asaala naanɛ.

eng: he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
local: sigehe a mɛŋa, nam Yinɛ ta paaɛ kum, kum sebo en pun dɛna pumpuuredɔɔ zuo kum.

eng: Therefore, God highly honored him and gave him a name above all names.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ me zɛkɛ en saazuo gee bo' en yu'se'ere en gani yu'ura za'a.

eng: so that at the name of Jesus everyone in heaven, on earth, and under the earth might bow.
local: ti Yezu yu'urɛ la, ti duma woo wan kpa'ara bɔ'ɔra, saazuo ha, la timpaberɛ wa zuo, la timpaberɛ timpaberɛ ha.

eng: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
local: ti zɛleŋa woo puta yeti, Yezu Kiresta en de Daana, ti la dɛna na'ahegɔ bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de Sɔ la.

eng: Therefore, my loved ones, just as you always obey me, not just when I am present but now even more while I am away, carry out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
local: Ŋwana zuo, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, wuu yamam en nane mam saŋa woo se'em la, ti la dagena la mam ni bɔna la ya la ma'a, gee nam ya mam gana bala nananawa, ti n ka bɔna la ya wa. Tuni ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ pa'alɛ ti ya nyɛ faarɛ, la dabeem la gbi'a.

eng: God is the one who enables you both to want and to actually live out his good purposes.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela Yinɛ en boi ya puan tuna la ninmu'urɛ, bɔ'ɔra ya paŋa ti ya tana sakera gee tuna bɔ'ɔra a yu'urɛ la.

eng: Do everything without grumbling and arguing.
local: Iti ya sɛla woo gee da gɔgena, bii ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la taaba.

eng: so that you may be blameless and pure, innocent children of God surrounded by people who are crooked and corrupt. Among these people you shine like stars in the world.
local: bala ti ya kan tari gbelegere la taalɛ, ya wan dɛna Yinɛ kɔma en ka tari dɛkɔ nɛre ŋmɛlema, la fãareba buuri saŋa nɛreba wa tiŋasuka. Niiri ya ba puan timpaberɛ wa zuo wuu ya dela bugum la.

eng: because you hold on to the word of life. This will allow me to say on the day of Christ that I havent run for nothing or worked for nothing.
local: La dela yamam en tari vom yelehum wa kankaŋi la zuo, ti n wan ta ta'am ŋmɛ' nyu'ɔ Kiresta lɛbega daarɛ na, ti n daa ka zɔ bahɛ yoo bii n tum bahɛ yoo.

eng: But even if I am poured out like a drink offering upon the altar of service for your faith, I am glad. Im glad with all of you.
local: Gee ba han pun ka'ɛ mam wuu buum kaabegɔ la bahɛ kaabegɔ bo'olum la zuo, yamam sakerɛ zuo, la de mam la pupeelum, ti n nyɛɛni la yamam za'a.

eng: You should be glad about this in the same way. Be glad with me!.
local: Bala mɛa ti yamam me dɛna ya nyɛɛna la pupeelum la mam.

eng: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to see you soon so that I may be encouraged by hearing about you.
local: Mam tari puti'irɛ Daana Yezu zi'an ti n tum Timoti ya zi'an kalam na, la n nyɛɛm doohe mam wan wum yelesuma sɛ'a ya zi'an la.

eng: I have no one like him. He is a person who genuinely cares about your well-being.
local: Mam ka tari se'em en ani wuu eŋa, ba' tara ya giile bala.

eng: All the others put their own business ahead of Jesus Christs business.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, bana la za'a eere la ba mɛhi ma'a sum yele, dagi Yezu Kiresta yelehum la sum yele.

eng: You know his character, how he labors with me for the gospel like a son works with his father.
local: Gee ya mɛhi mi' Timoti tibehum en ani se'em. A ani wuu dayua la ka Sɔ la, lagum tum la mam faarɛ yelehum tuuma wa puan la a ziim za'a.

eng: So he is the one that I hope to send as soon as I find out how things turn out here for me.
local: Bala zuo, mam ti'ihɛ ti n tum en mɛ kalam na, wuu mam n nyɛ ti la ani mam se'em la.

eng: I trust in the Lord that I also will visit you soon.
local: Gee mam nam Daana la, ti mam me wan wa'am ya zi'an tɔtɔ na.

eng: I think it is also necessary to send Epaphroditus to you. He is my brother, coworker, and fellow soldier; and he is your representative who serves my needs.
local: Le pa'ahɛ, mam ti'ihɛ ti la nari ti n tum Epafurodituse ya zi'an na. A dela n suuɔ, la n tuntuna tadaana, la n pɛntiira tadaana en de ya bituneŋa, gee dɛna tuntuna bo' mam en boti sɛla.

eng: He misses you all, and he was upset because you heard he was sick.
local: A ba' bɔta ti a nyɛ ya za'a mɛ, gee a suure sagum mɛ la yan wum ti a bɛ̃'ɛri mɛ la.

eng: In fact, he was so sick that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him—and not just on him but also on me, because his death would have caused me great sorrow.
local: A daa sirum ba' bɛ̃' mɛ, gee la a ki. Gee ti Yinɛ zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, gee dagi la eŋa ma'a ti Yinɛ daa zɔ a nimbu'ɔ, gee mam me, ti n susageŋɔ la da tagelɛ taaba.

eng: Therefore, I am sending him immediately so that when you see him again you can be glad and I wont worry.
local: Mam ba' bɔta ti n tum en mɛ ya zi'an kalam na, ti ya nyɛɛm la yan le nyɛ en la, gee ti mam me susageŋɔ la sige.

eng: So welcome him in the Lord with great joy and show great respect for people like him.
local: To'e ya en Daana la puan la ya pupeelum za'a, gee na'ahɛ ya nɛrebana taaba.

eng: He risked his life and almost died for the work of Christ, and he did this to make up for the help you couldnt give me.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a dela se'em en daa gee la a ki' Kiresta tuuma zuo, pɔhɛ a vom, tum tuunse'ere ti yamam kan nyaŋɛ ni tum bo' mam la.

eng: So then, my brothers and sisters, be glad in the Lord. Its no trouble for me to repeat the same things to you because they will help keep you on track.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, n suurɔ wa, nyɛɛni ya Daana la puan. N gulehɛ yele sɛ'a ti n pun gulehɛ bo' ya yia la le bo' ya, dagi daaŋɔ bo' mam. Han dɛna yamam me, la suŋeri ya mɛ.

eng: Watch out for the dogs. Watch out for people who do evil things. Watch out for those who insist on circumcision, which is really mutilation.
local: Mini ya baahi la yele, mini ya be'em tuntuneba la yele, mini ya sɛba ti inganɛ wa ŋmaa dɛna ba ninmu'urɛ la yele!.

eng: We are the circumcision. We are the ones who serve by Gods Spirit and who boast in Christ Jesus. We dont put our confidence in rituals performed on the body.
local: Bihɛ ya, tumam en de sɛba n sirum ŋma la, tumam en de sɛba n na'aheri Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la puan, gee ŋmɛ'ɛra tu nyu'ɔ Yezu Kiresta puan, ka dikɛ tu mɛhi delum inganɛ malema.

eng: though I have good reason to have this kind of confidence. If anyone else has reason to put their confidence in physical advantages, I have even more.
local: la mam en pun tara sore ti n ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la inganɛ la. Se'em han bɔna ha tara se'ere zuo ti a wan ŋmɛ'ɛra nyu'ɔ la a inganɛ, mam yi tari gana bala.

eng: I was circumcised on the eighth day. I am from the people of Israel and the tribe of Benjamin. I am a Hebrew of the Hebrews. With respect to observing the Law, Im a Pharisee.
local: Ba ŋma mam la dabeha anii daarɛ, mam dela Izirale buuri, yehe Benyamin yizuo puan, dɛna Hiburu, ti Hiburu pɔga la buraa naaɛ taaba dɔgɛ. La han dɛna la ze'elego la, mam dela Farasii paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: With respect to devotion to the faith, I harassed the church. With respect to righteousness under the Law, Im blameless.
local: la han dɛna la ninmu'urɛ, mam en daa dole mɛleha lageŋɔ la, la han dɛna la tuuntɔɔ vom doohe ze'elego la puan, mam ka tari tuure.

eng: These things were my assets, but I wrote them off as a loss for the sake of Christ.
local: Gee ba han pun tara nyuurɔ seto woo, mam bihɛ ba za'a ti ba dela zaŋa Kiresta zuo.

eng: But even beyond that, I consider everything a loss in comparison with the superior value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. I have lost everything for him, but what I lost I think of as sewer trash, so that I might gain Christ.
local: Sɛla en gani bala dela, mam bihɛ sɛla woo ti ba dela zaŋa beni zuo, tibehum sebo en gani za'a en boi mam n baŋɛ Yezu Kiresta en de mam Daana la. Eŋa zuo, ti mam bɔregɛ sɛla woo bahɛ, gee biha ba ti ba dɛna sɔgerɔ, ti n ta'am tara Kiresta.

eng: and be found in him. In Christ I have a righteousness that is not my own and that does not come from the Law but rather from the faithfulness of Christ. It is the righteousness of God that is based on faith.
local: la n vɔna a puan, da tara n mɛŋa tuntɔɔnɛ en yehe ze'elego la naŋa puan na, gee tuntɔɔnɛ en yeheri n sakerɛ Kiresta puan na. Tuntɔɔnɛ se'ere en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an dɔla sakerɛ puan.

eng: The righteousness that I have comes from knowing Christ, the power of his resurrection, and the participation in his sufferings. It includes being conformed to his death.
local: Mam boti n baŋɛ Kiresta mɛ, la a vo'ore paŋa la, la n lagum namehɛ a namehegɔ la, doohe la n ana wuu en, a kum la puan.

eng: so that I may perhaps reach the goal of the resurrection of the dead.
local: wuu ma beere puti'irɛ en de n ti Yinɛ wan ta ihege mam kum puan la.

eng: Its not that I have already reached this goal or have already been perfected, but I pursue it, so that I may grab hold of it because Christ grabbed hold of me for just this purpose.
local: Dagi la mam pun tara ŋwana mɛ, bii n pun di la mi'a. Gee mam kɔ'ɔm zigera mɛ ti la dɛna mam dinɛ, beni zuo, Kiresta Yezu iŋɛ mam ti n dɛna la a dinɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I myself dont think Ive reached it, but I do this one thing: I forget about the things behind me and reach out for the things ahead of me.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mam ka biheri ti de pun dɛna la mam dinɛ, gee yele dina wa ma'a ti mam ita. Mam tameni sɛla woo en boi mam pooren, gee tiregɛ kina nɛŋa ti n paaɛ sɛla en boi n nɛŋan ha la.

eng: The goal I pursue is the prize of Gods upward call in Christ Jesus.
local: N tiregeri mɛ ti n di mi'a, la n to'e yɔɔrɛ la en de saazuo yi'a wa yɔɔrɛ, ti Yinɛ yi' mam doohe Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: So all of us who are spiritually mature should think this way, and if anyone thinks differently, God will reveal it to him or her.
local: Bahɛ ya ti tumam sɛba en bi'ɛ la, tara puti'irɛ deyina, gee se'em puti'irɛ han bɔna deyima yelese'ere puan, la han dɛna bala, Yinɛ wan lilege dɛ bo' ya.

eng: Only lets live in a way that is consistent with whatever level we have reached.
local: Yeen bahɛ ya ti tu zãla sɛla ti tu tara la kankaŋi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, become imitators of me and watch those who live this way—you can use us as models.
local: N suurɔ, naaɛ ya taaba la ya tɔgeha mam, gee la ya bihera sɛba en vɔi dɔla tumam n iŋɛ sɛla pa'alɛ ti yamam nyɛ la.

eng: As I have told you many times and now say with deep sadness, many people live as enemies of the cross.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba zo'e zo'e vom dela dinduma la Kiresta pumpuuredɔɔ la. Mam ni pun yɛta ya ba yele, gee n nyaa yeti ya mɛ la nintam.

eng: Their lives end with destruction. Their god is their stomach, and they take pride in their disgrace because their thoughts focus on earthly things.
local: Ba ba'ahegɔ dela sageŋɔ. Ba yinɛ dela ba puya, ti ba suŋa bɔna ba vi la puan, ba puti'ira boi la timpaberɛ wa zuo lɔgerɔ puan.

eng: Our citizenship is in heaven. We look forward to a savior that comes from there—the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Gee tumam timbiine la boi la saazuo ha. Bilam ti tu gura ti tu faara la wan ze'ele na, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: He will transform our humble bodies so that they are like his glorious body, by the power that also makes him able to subject all things to himself.
local: A wan tee tu inganɛ se'ere n nyɛti mugehego wa, ti ka ana wuu eŋa insɛka en tari suŋa wa, doohe la a pansɛka n baheri ti a ta'na bahera ti sɛla woo bɔna a nu'uhen la.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters whom I love and miss, who are my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord. Loved ones.
local: Dinɛ zuo, n suŋɛ duma ti n nɔŋɛ, gee bɔta ti n nyɛ ya paaɛ paaɛ wa, mam nyɛɛma la mam na'am muŋa wa. Ze' ya kankaŋi Daana la puan, sadɔɔ kuna puan, n sunɔŋerɛ wa.

eng: I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to come to an agreement in the Lord.
local: N beleni Eyodia, la Sintike, ti ba dɛna putɛ'yinɛ Daana la puan.

eng: Yes, and Im also asking you, loyal friend, to help these women who have struggled together with me in the ministry of the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my coworkers whose names are in the scroll of life.
local: Gee yɛta fum se'em ti mam la fum bɔna yɔgehega kayima puan wa yeti, fum suŋɛ pɔgehi wa. Se'ere en sɔi la, si lagum zabɛ mɛ suŋɛ mam faarɛ yelehum wa tuuma puan. Bamam la Kilɛmɛnti la basɛba la en de mam tuntuneba taaba la, sɛba yu'ura en boi vom gɔŋɔ la puan la.

eng: Be glad in the Lord always! Again I say, be glad!.
local: Nyɛɛni ya Daana la puan saŋa woo. Le yɛ'ɛha, n yeti, nyɛɛni ya.

eng: Let your gentleness show in your treatment of all people. The Lord is near.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ya sigehe n mɛŋa la niiɛ bo' nɛra woo. Daana la pɔri na.

eng: Dont be anxious about anything; rather, bring up all of your requests to God in your prayers and petitions, along with giving thanks.
local: Da fabele ya sɛsɛla yele, gee sɛla woo puan, doohe ya pu'uhegɔ, gee suhega, la fara bu'a puan, bahɛ ti Yinɛ baŋɛ yan boti sɛla la.

eng: Then the peace of God that exceeds all understanding will keep your hearts and minds safe in Christ Jesus.
local: Ti Yinɛ suma'ahum la n gani bɔkerɛ za'a la, gu'ura ya suya la ya ti'ihegɔ sɛ ti zɛ'a kankaŋi Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: From now on, brothers and sisters, if anything is excellent and if anything is admirable, focus your thoughts on these things: all that is true, all that is holy, all that is just, all that is pure, all that is lovely, and all that is worthy of praise.
local: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, n suurɔ, sɛla woo en de yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛla woo en tari na'ahegɔ, sɛla woo en de ma'a, sɛla woo en ka tari dɛgerɔ, sɛla woo en peere puurɛ, sɛla woo n nari. Sɛla han bɔna di mi'a, sɛla en boi nara pɛka, ti'iheri ya yele ana wa yele.

eng: Practice these things: whatever you learned, received, heard, or saw in us. The God of peace will be with you.
local: Tara ya yele sɛ'a ti ya zamehɛ, gee to'e, gee wum, gee nyɛ mam puan la tuna, ti suma'ahum Yinɛ la, wan bɔna la ya.

eng: I was very glad in the Lord because now at last you have shown concern for me again.
local: Mam nyɛɛni la nyɛɛmkãtɛ Daana la puan nananawa, ti ba'ahegɔ za'a, ya malum ihege yan daa ti'iheri n yele se'em la mɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya yuum ti'iheri n yele, gee ya yuum ka nyɛ yɔ'ɔ pa'alɛ dɛ.

eng: Im not saying this because I need anything, for I have learned how to be content in any circumstance.
local: Dagi mam n yeti ŋwana wa dela, n boti la sɛla ya zi'an. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam zamehɛ mɛ ti sadɔɔ seko woo puan, n nini tigɛ la sɛla ti n tara mɛ.

eng: I know the experience of being in need and of having more than enough; I have learned the secret to being content in any and every circumstance, whether full or hungry or whether having plenty or being poor.
local: Mam mi' mam tari n mɛŋa se'em mɛ, n han ta sige, gee mina mam tari n mɛŋa se'em, n han le ta zom paaɛ paaɛ. Yele ana wa, de se'ere puan, la yɛla za'a puan, mam zamehɛ la sugelum yele se'ere en boi putigerɛ puan, la kom puan, tara zo'e zo'e puan, la ka tari puan.

eng: I can endure all these things through the power of the one who gives me strength.
local: Mam ta'am iŋɛ sɛla woo se'em daana en bo'ori mam paŋa la puan.

eng: Still, you have done well to share my distress.
local: Gee sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ya daa iŋɛ la itesuŋɔ, la yan lagum di mam nimbu'ɔ la.

eng: You Philippians know from the time of my first mission work in Macedonia how no church shared in supporting my ministry except you.
local: Yamam Filipiduma sirum mi' ti, faarɛ yelehum pɔhega dabeha la puan ha la, mam en bahɛ Masedoniya la, lageŋɔ daa ka boi lagum tum bo' mam bu'a bii to'ore yɛla wa puan, han dagena yamam ma'a.

eng: You sent contributions repeatedly to take care of my needs even while I was in Thessalonica.
local: Mam a daa pun ta bɔna Tɛsalonika timpaberɛ ha la, ya bo' mam suŋɛ me na gaŋɛ buyina.

eng: Im not hoping for a gift, but I am hoping for a profit that accumulates in your account.
local: Dagi la n eere la bo'olum la, gee mam eere la nyuurɔ seto n wan kã'ahɛ zo'e zo'e beŋe bo' ya la.

eng: I now have plenty and it is more than enough. I am full to overflowing because I received the gifts that you sent from Epaphroditus. Those gifts give off a fragrant aroma, an acceptable sacrifice that pleases God.
local: Ya kɔ'ɔm yɔ mam la za'a za'a, ti n tara gaŋɛ. Ya kɔ'ɔm tigehɛ mam mɛ suŋa suŋa, yele yele wuu mam nyaa to'e ya bo'olum la ti ya dikɛ tum Epafurodituse na la. Bo dela nyuuŋo suŋɔ kaabegɔ bo'olum. Bo dela kaabegɔ n tigeri Yinɛ nini.

eng: My God will meet your every need out of his riches in the glory that is found in Christ Jesus.
local: Bala zuo, Mam Yinɛ wa wan tigeha ya sɛla woo ti ya bɔta, doohe la a tarega sum sebo n boi Yezu Kiresta puan la.

eng: Let glory be given to God our Father forever and always. Amen.
local: Pɛka dɛna tu Yinɛ, la tu Sɔ la dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Greet all Gods people in Christ Jesus. The brothers and sisters with me send you their greeting.
local: Pu'uhɛ nɛresunduma la za'a en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la. N suurɔ n boi la mam ka'lam pu'uheri ya na.

eng: All Gods people here, especially those in Caesars household, send you their greeting.
local: Nɛresunduma la woo pu'uheri ya na, yele yele wuu timbihera nɛŋadaana la yireduma la.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirits.
local: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya Sia puan.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother.
local: Mam Pɔɔl en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe la Yinɛ suboolum, la tu suuɔ Timoti en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa.

eng: To the holy and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae. Grace and peace to you from God our Father.
local: Tu gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la nɛresunduma, la sakereba en de tu suurɔ Kiresta puan, bɔna Kolose tiŋa la. Zu'uhegɔ dɛna ya dinɛ, la Yinɛ en de tu sɔ la suma'ahum.

eng: We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you.
local: Tumam en ni pu'uhera bɔ'ɔra ya la, saŋa woo tu kɔ'ɔm bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la la fara.

eng: Weve done this since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and your love for all Gods people.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wum ya sakerɛ en ani se'em Kiresta Yezu puan, la nɔŋerɛ se'ere ti ya tara bɔ'ɔra nɛresunduma la za'a la.

eng: You have this faith and love because of the hope reserved for you in heaven. You previously heard about this hope through the true message, the good news.
local: beere putɛ'se'ere en boi saazuo gura ya la zuo. Ya pun tabelɛ wum beere putɛ' dina wa yele mɛ, gee baŋɛ wum yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum en de faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: which has come to you. This message has been bearing fruit and growing among you since the day you heard and truly understood Gods grace, in the same way that it is bearing fruit and growing in the whole world.
local: n wa'am ya zi'an na la. Woo bumɔ en wani wala gee zɔ'ɔra se'em tiŋa wa zuo za'a se'em la, bala mɛa ti bu wuna wala ya puan, pɔhɛ daan se'ere daarɛ ti ya wum, gee bɔkɛ Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la.

eng: You learned it from Epaphras, who is the fellow slave we love and Christs faithful minister for your sake.
local: Dina ti ya zamehɛ Epafaras zi'an, tu yameŋa tadaana, se'em ti tu nɔŋɛ la. A dela Kiresta yelemɛŋerɛ tuntuna ya zuo.

eng: He informed us of your love in the Spirit.
local: a bahɛ ti tu baŋɛ yan tari nɔŋerɛ Sia puan se'em.

eng: Because of this, since the day we heard about you, we havent stopped praying for you and asking for you to be filled with the knowledge of Gods will, with all wisdom and spiritual understanding.
local: Dina wa zuo, daan se'ere daarɛ ti tu wum dɛna wa la, tu ka go'e tu pu'uha bɔ'ɔra ya, gee suhera ti ya pirɛ la Yinɛ suboolum mi'ilum, la Sia puan yɛm woo, la bɔkerɛ woo.

eng: Were praying this so that you can live lives that are worthy of the Lord and pleasing to him in every way: by producing fruit in every good work and growing in the knowledge of God.
local: ti ya vɔna vom sebo en nari bɔ'ɔra Daana la, ti ya vom za'a tigɛ a nini wuu yan wan wuna wala tuunsunɛ woo puan se'em la, la yan zo'ore Yinɛ mi'ilum puan se'em la.

eng: by being strengthened through his glorious might so that you endure everything and have patience.
local: Ya kpe'em la paŋa sɛka woo en ze'ele Yinɛ paŋa puan na, ya dɛna sɛba en maahum ti ya ŋmibera sɛla woo puan la sugeri, gee nyɛɛna.

eng: and by giving thanks with joy to the Father. He made it so you could take part in the inheritance, in light granted to Gods holy people.
local: la pɛka bɔ'ɔra Sɔ la fara. Sɔ se'em en iŋɛ ya ti ya lagum tara tɔtɛ la nɛre sunduma la en boi niihum puan la.

eng: He rescued us from the control of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of the Son he loves.
local: Yinɛ faaɛ tu mɛ yehe lika paŋa puan bahɛ, gee tee dikɛ tu ki'ihɛ a Dayɔba'arɛ sɔ'ɔlum la puan.

eng: He set us free through the Son and forgave our sins.
local: se'em puan ti tu nyɛ faarɛ, la yele be'ero sugeri dia.

eng: The Son is the image of the invisible God, the one who is first over all creation.
local: Kiresta dela Yinɛ se'ere ti tu ka nyɛta wa naanɛ, kiima naaŋɔ wa woo puan.

eng: Because all things were created by him: both in the heavens and on the earth, the things that are visible and the things that are invisible. Whether they are thrones or powers, or rulers or authorities, all things were created through him and for him.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa puan ti sɛla woo en boi saazuo la timpaberɛ wa zuo naam. Sɛla ti tu nyɛta, la sɛla ti tu ka nyɛta, ba dɛna na'am, pahi, bihereba bii kpe'eŋo, sɛla woo naam doohe la eŋa puan, gee dɛna eŋa dinɛ.

eng: He existed before all things, and all things are held together in him.
local: Kiresta mɛŋa pun bɔna mɛ gee ti sɛla woo nyaa baŋɛ naam, eŋa puan ti naaŋɔ wa za'ana bɔna suŋa.

eng: He is the head of the body, the church, who is the beginning, the one who is firstborn from among the dead so that he might occupy the first place in everything.
local: Eŋa en de zuo daana, lagehegɔ la puan. Eŋa en de pi'ileŋo, dɛna kiima kum vo'ore puan yia, ti a ta'am wa'am na dɛna yiadaana sɛla woo puan.

eng: Because all the fullness of God was pleased to live in him.
local: Eŋa puan ti Yinɛ nini tigɛ ti a za'a za'a bɔna a puan.

eng: and he reconciled all things to himself through him— whether things on earth or in the heavens. He brought peace through the blood of his cross.
local: eŋa ti Yinɛ nini tigɛ ti a doohe a puan dikɛ sɛla woo naaɛ taaba bo' a mɛŋa, sɛla woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, bii saazuo, doohe la Yinɛ en iŋɛ suma'ahum doohe la a ziim la pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo la.

eng: Once you were alienated from God and you were enemies with him in your minds, which was shown by your evil actions.
local: Wunse'eren ha la ya daa nan zaaɛ mɛ, gee dɛna dinduma ya puti'ira puan, tuna be'em tuuma la.

eng: But now he has reconciled you by his physical body through death, to present you before God as a people who are holy, faultless, and without blame.
local: e a nyaa dikɛ ya naaɛ mɛ, doohe amɛŋa saala ingɔŋɔ kum la puan, ti a bahɛ ti tu dɛna tuulum nɛreba, ka tara dɛkɔ bii geere a nɛŋan.

eng: But you need to remain well established and rooted in faith and not shift away from the hope given in the good news that you heard. This message has been preached throughout all creation under heaven. And I, Paul, became a servant of this good news.
local: wuu ya han kelum gu'ura ya mɛhi suŋa, zɛ'a kankaŋi ya sakerɛ la puan, ka vuregera ya beere puti'irɛ se'ere ti faarɛ yelesuma la biŋe nuurɛ ti ya wum la, se'ere en muulɛ bo' bunvua en boi tiŋa wa zuo wa. Mam Pɔɔl dela se'em ti ba looe bo' faari yelesuma wa.

eng: Now Im happy to be suffering for you. Im completing what is missing from Christs sufferings with my own body. Im doing this for the sake of his body, which is the church.
local: Nananawa, mam nyɛɛni mɛ n namehegɔ la puan yamam zuo, mam inganɛ wa puan, bahɛ ya ti n yele de ŋwana yeti, mam ba'aheri la namehegɔ seko en gee Kiresta namehegɔ la puan, a iŋa la en de lageŋɔ la zuo.

eng: I became a servant of the church by Gods commission, which was given to me for you, in order to complete Gods word.
local: Mam lebege la lageŋɔ la yameŋa, doohe la Yinɛ en bo' mam tuunse'ere ya zuo la, ti n bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ Yinɛ yelehum la suŋa suŋa.

eng: Im completing it with a secret plan that has been hidden for ages and generations but which has now been revealed to his holy people.
local: sɛla en sugɛ asaaleba zi'an yuuman yuuman, la buuri sahi puan ha, gee nyaa lilege bo' a tuulum nɛreba la.

eng: God wanted to make the glorious riches of this secret plan known among the Gentiles, which is Christ living in you, the hope of glory.
local: Bama ti Yinɛ looe ti a doohe ba puan bahɛ ti ba baŋɛ Yinɛ suŋa en zo'e se'em yele se'ere en sugɛ wa puan bo' sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la, yele wa dela Kiresta n boi ya puan, en de ya beere puti'irɛ la suŋa.

eng: This is what we preach as we warn and teach every person with all wisdom so that we might present each one mature in Christ.
local: La dela eŋa ti tu muula wa, ka'ana nɛra woo gee pa'ala nɛra woo yɛm puan, ti tu wan bi'ihɛ nɛra woo Kiresta puan.

eng: I work hard and struggle for this goal with his energy, which works in me powerfully.
local: Dina zuo ti mam zigera la pansɛka woo ti a dikɛ iŋɛ mam puan la paŋa la inkpeŋere la.

eng: I want you to know how much I struggle for you, for those in Laodicea, and for all who havent known me personally.
local: Mam boti ya baŋɛ mam mu'ɛ nini tuna se'em ya zuo, la sɛba en boi Laodisiya, la ya sɛba za'a en pun ka nyɛ mam la nini.

eng: My goal is that their hearts would be encouraged and united together in love so that they might have all the riches of assurance that come with understanding, so that they might have the knowledge of the secret plan of God, namely Christ.
local: Mam boti ba suya nyɛ la kpemehego, naaɛ taaba nɔŋerɛ puan, gee nyɛ bɔkerɛ se'ere en gani za'a, tara tibehum, gee me tara mi'ilum doohe Yinɛ sugelum yele la en de Kiresta a mɛŋa la.

eng: All the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden in him.
local: La dela Kiresta puan ti yɛm la mi'ilum tarega za'a bɔna.

eng: Im telling you this so that no one deceives you with convincing arguments.
local: Mam yeti ŋwana la dela, nɛra da pa'ahɛ ya la nɔmaalum.

eng: because even though I am absent physically, Im with you in spirit. Im happy to see the discipline and stability of your faith in Christ.
local: La mam en ka boi la ya ingɔŋɔ puan bilam la, gee n boi la ya sia puan, gee nyɛɛna, bɔta ti n nyɛ yan ze' se'em kankaŋi la ya sakerɛ Kiresta puan.

eng: So live in Christ Jesus the Lord in the same way as you received him.
local: Wuu yan to'e Kiresta Yezu en de Daana la, kelum vɔni ya ya vom la a puan.

eng: Be rooted and built up in him, be established in faith, and overflow with thanksgiving just as you were taught.
local: ba' ya nyɛga, gee mɛ ya mɛhi yagelɛ a zuo, gee sɛ' ya ze'ele kankaŋi sakerɛ la puan, wuu ban pa'alɛ ya se'em la, pirɛ yabera la fara bu'a.

eng: See to it that nobody enslaves you with philosophy and foolish deception, which conform to human traditions and the way the world thinks and acts rather than Christ.
local: Bihɛ ya suŋa ti nɛra da dikɛ ya da'aba doohe gɔŋɔ wogerɔ la pã'aheyooro doohe asaala malema, la sɛse'ehi en sɔi timpaberɛ wa bihera la, gee dagi Kiresta en boti se'em la.

eng: All the fullness of deity lives in Christs body.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa Kiresta en de Yinɛ naanɛ de mɛŋa de mɛŋa en niiɛ inganɛ puan na.

eng: And you have been filled by him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.
local: Yamam n wa'am a zi'an na wa, sɛla woo wan ze'ele tuuntɔɔ bo' ya. Eŋa en de se'em en de zuo daana, sɔna pahi, la kpe'eŋo za'a.

eng: In him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not administered by human hands. The circumcision of Christ is realized in the stripping away of the whole self dominated by sin.
local: Eŋa puan ti ba ŋma yamam me sia puan ŋmaa, yan wan ŋmaaɛ ni ya ingɔŋɔ la zi'an bahɛ ni la, ya ŋmaa dɛ la Kiresta ŋmaa la puan.

eng: You were buried with him through baptism and raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
local: Ban daa laaɛ ya la Kiresta ko'om sua puan la, ba daa lagum vo'e ya mɛ la en ya sakerɛ puan,Yinɛ paŋa la puan, Yinɛ se'em en vo'e en kum puan la.

eng: When you were dead because of the things you had done wrong and because your body wasnt circumcised, God made you alive with Christ and forgave all the things you had done wrong.
local: Yan daa ki ya tuure puan, ya ingɔŋɔ la en ka ŋma la puan la, Yinɛ bahɛ ti ya vo'e mɛ la en, eŋa en daa di tu za'a tuure sugeri bo' tu.

eng: He destroyed the record of the debt we owed, with its requirements that worked against us. He canceled it by nailing it to the cross.
local: Saalum gulehegɔ seko en daa gulehɛ gã'a gura tɔ, la ku namehegɔ seko en nari tu la. A daa uue ku bahɛ la kinkɛleŋa ha, kpa' kɔ pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: When he disarmed the rulers and authorities, he exposed them to public disgrace by leading them in a triumphal parade.
local: A yɛɛhɛ pahi duma la kpe'eŋo mɛ, gee diihe ba nyanɛ peelumen, nyaŋɛ ba za'a za'a pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo.

eng: So dont let anyone judge you about eating or drinking or about a festival, a new moon observance, or sabbaths.
local: Bala la, da bahɛ ya ti se'em zɛregɛ ya doohe dia bii nyua bii kiha dabeha, bii ŋmarehi paalehi, bii de'eŋo bii vo'ohego dabeha.

eng: These religious practices are only a shadow of what was coming—the body that cast the shadow is Christ.
local: Bana wa daa dela yele sɛ'a n wan wa'am na la yinyiligo, gee Kiresta en de yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: Dont let anyone who wants to practice harsh self-denial and worship angels rob you of the prize. They go into detail about what they have seen in visions and have become unjustifiably arrogant by their selfish way of thinking.
local: Da bahɛ ti nɛra sigehe fum, doohe eŋa en boti se'em doohe sigehe n mɛŋa yooro, suŋereba pu'uhegɔ, zaaheŋɔ yɛla, banaduma ukeri mɛ la asaala puti'irɛ la pa'alɛ mɛŋa.

eng: They dont stay connected to the head. The head nourishes and supports the whole body through the joints and ligaments, so the body grows with a growth that is from God.
local: gee ka gi' Kiresta en de zuo la ti iŋa la za'a nyɛta diihum, gee naaɛ taaba doohe la a giila la innaanɛ la, zɔ'ɔra Yinɛ puan la.

eng: If you died with Christ to the way the world thinks and acts, why do you submit to rules and regulations as though you were living in the world?.
local: La han dɛna ti Kiresta puan, ya ki mɛ la pansɛka en sɔi tiŋa wa la, ŋwani ti ya vɔna wuu ya kelum dɛna la tiŋa wa zuo dinɛ la? Ŋwani ti ya nana malema en de.

eng: Dont handle! Dont taste! Dont touch.
local: Da gurɛ, da lem, da kalum?.

eng: All these things cease to exist when they are used. Such rules are human commandments and teachings.
local: Malema ana za'a tari bɔ'ɔra la sɛla en ni tum ta kuregɛ sagum. Ba yeen dɛna la asaala ze'elego la pa'alegɔ.

eng: They look like they are wise with this self-made religion and their self-denial by the harsh treatment of the body, but they are no help against indulging in selfish immoral behavior.
local: Ba tari la naanɛ en ani wuu yɛm duma la, ba ta' zɛkera la ba mɛhi en boti se'em, sigehe n mɛŋa, ta'na nameha ba ihe, gee ba ka tari nyuurɔ bo' nɛra, la han wa'am yaalegɔ yɛla puan na.

eng: Therefore, if you were raised with Christ, look for the things that are above where Christ is sitting at Gods right side.
local: Bala, la han dɛna ti ya vo'e mɛ la Kiresta, ye eere ya yele sɛ'a en boi tinsuŋan ha la, zi'an ti Kiresta zi'a Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o bɔba la.

eng: Think about the things above and not things on earth.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ya puti'ira bɔna la yele sɛ'a en boi tinsuŋan ha la, dagi yele sɛ'a en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: You died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, ya ki mɛ, ti ya vom la sugɛ la Kiresta puan, Yinɛ puan.

eng: When Christ, who is your life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.
local: Kiresta en de ya vom la han ta niiɛ na, yamam me wan lagum niiɛ la en, la na'ahegɔ.

eng: So put to death the parts of your life that belong to the earth, such as sexual immorality, moral corruption, lust, evil desire, and greed .
local: Bala zuo, ku ya sɛla woo en boi ya puan dɛna tiŋa dinɛ zuo bunɔ wuu, yaluntuuma, dɛgerɔ tuuma, kunkoma'ahum, be'em kɔrehi, la nyika .

eng: The wrath of God is coming upon disobedient people because of these things.
local: Yele ana wa zuo ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim yɛ'a sɛba en ka nane la zuo na la.

eng: You used to live this way, when you were alive to these things.
local: Wunse'eren ha la, Yele ana mɛa ti yamam me yuum ita, yan yuum nan vɔi vom bumɔ taaba la.

eng: But now set aside these things, such as anger, rage, malice, slander, and obscene language.
local: Gee nananawa, la dela ya bahɛ yele aŋa woo, sunsunyiim, putuulum, nɛreba yu'ura sageŋɔ, kɛhega yetɔgum da zɛ'ɛta ya nɔya puan.

eng: Dont lie to each other. Take off the old human nature with its practices.
local: Da pareni ya bɔ'ɔra taaba, yan diŋɛ yɛɛ naanɛ kɛka la, la de itegɔ la.

eng: and put on the new nature, which is renewed in knowledge by conforming to the image of the one who created it.
local: gee yɛ ya naanɛ paalega la pi' ya mɛhi, naanɛ se'ere en teere de mɛŋa saŋa woo ti de baŋɛ de Naana la mi'ilum la paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: In this image there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcised nor uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave nor free, but Christ is all things and in all people.
local: Teere dina puan la, Giriki ka boi ti Yuda bɔna; sɛba en ŋma ka boi, ti sɛba en ka ŋma bɔna; nɛreyooro ka boi ti nɛreyaalehi bɔna; da'aba ka boi ti sɛba en boi fai bɔna. Gee Kiresta en de za'a gee bɔna ba za'a puan.

eng: Therefore, as Gods choice, holy and loved, put on compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience.
local: Wuu Yinɛ en looe ya la, ti ya dɛna tuulum nɛreba ti a nɔŋɛ la, bagɛ mɛhi la ya nɛreba giile, itesuŋɔ, sigehe mɛa, ma'ma', la sudi'ihego.

eng: Be tolerant with each other and, if someone has a complaint against anyone, forgive each other. As the Lord forgave you, so also forgive each other.
local: Ŋmiberi ya la taaba, ti se'em han tara se'ere la a tadaana, di ya sugeri bo' taaba. Wuu Daana la en di sugeri bo' yamam se'em la, bala mɛa ti ya me suna ti ya di sugeri bu taaba.

eng: And over all these things put on love, which is the perfect bond of unity.
local: Se'ere en gani za'a dela, bagɛ ya mɛhi la nɔŋerɛ, sɛla en bobere ya lagena taaba, ti ya tara nɔyinɛ sunɛ la taaba en bala.

eng: The peace of Christ must control your hearts—a peace into which you were called in one body. And be thankful people.
local: Gee me bahɛ ti Kiresta suma'ahum pirɛ ya suya, yele se'ere zuo ti ba yi' ya na pa'ahɛ inganɛ deyina lageŋɔ la puan en bala. Gee la ya me bo'ori Yinɛ fara.

eng: The word of Christ must live in you richly. Teach and warn each other with all wisdom by singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs. Sing to God with gratitude in your hearts.
local: Bahɛ ya ti Kiresta yelehum la bɔna ya puan seke. Pa'ali ya gee ka'ana taaba la yɛm, gee ti nyɛɛma en boi ya suya puan la, tari ya saaŋɔ la baaŋa, la Sia puan yuuma ya suya puan bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ.

eng: Whatever you do, whether in speech or action, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus and give thanks to God the Father through him.
local: Gee ti sɛla woo ti ya ita, la dɛna tugum bii tuuma, iti ya sɛla woo Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la puan, bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ en de Sɔ la fara dɔla Yezu puan.

eng: Wives, submit to your husbands in a way that is appropriate in the Lord.
local: Pɔgeba, sigeha ya mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ya sireba, wuu lan nari se'em Daana dɔlega puan la.

eng: Husbands, love your wives and dont be harsh with them.
local: Sireba, nɔŋɛ ya pɔgeba, gee de makɛ iti ya ba vɛgela vɛgela.

eng: Children, obey your parents in everything, because this pleases the Lord.
local: Kɔma, nane ya ya dɔgereba yele se'ere woo puan, gee yele dina en tigeri Daana la nini.

eng: Parents, dont provoke your children in a way that ends up discouraging them.
local: Sɔduma, da nyiiha ya kɔma suya gee bala wan bahɛ ti ba sia yehe ya.

eng: Slaves, obey your masters on earth in everything. Dont just obey like people pleasers when they are watching. Instead, obey with the single motivation of fearing the Lord.
local: Da'aba, nane ya sɛba en sɔi ya tiŋa zuo wa la sɛla woo puan, dagi la ban boi ya nɛŋan la ma'a, ti ya ita ti ba puurɛ, gee ita ya bala la ya suure za'a, la Daana la zoolum.

eng: Whatever you do, do it from the heart for the Lord and not for people.
local: Fum tuunɛ en de se'ere woo la, dikɛ fum mɛŋa bo' de wuu fum tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana la gee dagi fum pee asaala puurɛ.

eng: You know that you will receive an inheritance as a reward. You serve the Lord Christ.
local: yan diŋɛ mina ti Daana la puan ya wan ta to'e tɔtɛ ti la dɛna ya yɔɔrɛ la. Ya tuni bɔ'ɔra la Daana Kiresta la.

eng: But evildoers will receive their reward for their evil actions. There is no discrimination.
local: Se'em en ka tuni sɛla en de ma'a la ba wan lebehe yɔ en sɛla woo ti a ka tum ma'a la, ti lugehego ka bɔna.

eng: Masters, be just and fair to your slaves, knowing that you yourselves have a master in heaven.
local: Kpɛ'ɛnduma, bo'ori ya ya da'aba buurɔ, la sɛla en de ma'a, gee ya mi' ti yamam me tari kpɛ'ɛma mɛ en boi tinsuŋan ha.

eng: Keep on praying and guard your prayers with thanksgiving.
local: Boni ya pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo gurɛ yan pu'uheri se'ere zuo la ya suya puan suŋa suŋa la fara bu'a.

eng: At the same time, pray for us also. Pray that God would open a door for the word so we can preach the secret plan of Christ—which is why Im in chains.
local: Gee me pu'uha bɔ'ɔra tumam me, ti Yinɛ yu'ɛ kuleŋa bo' yelehum la, ti tu muulɛ Kiresta yele sɛ'a en sugɛ la, yele sɛ'a zuo ti mam bɔna paka deen wa.

eng: Pray that I might be able to make it as clear as I ought to when I preach.
local: ti n ta'am lilege de suŋa wuu mam suni ti n muulɛ de se'em la.

eng: Act wisely toward outsiders, making the most of the opportunity.
local: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa la yɛm yiŋa duma zi'an, tari ya sansɛka woo ti ya nyɛ tuna suŋa suŋa.

eng: Your speech should always be gracious and sprinkled with insight so that you may know how to respond to every person.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ya tugum ana suŋa saŋa woo, ti saalum bɔna bu puan, ti ya mina yan wan lebehe nɛra woo se'em.

eng: Tychicus, our dearly loved brother, faithful minister, and fellow slave in the Lord, will inform you about everything that has happened to me.
local: Takikuse wan yele ya mam yɛla woo. A dela nɔŋerɛ suuɔ, sakerɛ yameŋa, gee me dɛna mam da'aberɛ tadaana Daana la puan.

eng: This is why I sent him to you, so that youll know all about us and so he can encourage your hearts.
local: Mam tum en ya zi'an na la dina wa zuo, ti ya mina tumam ani se'em, gee me kpemehe ya suya.

eng: I sent him with Onesimus, our faithful and dearly loved brother, who is one of you. They will let you know about everything here.
local: A kini me na la Onesimuse, en de sakerɛ daana, tu suuɔ ti tu nɔŋɛ, a dela yamam ayima. Ba wan yele ya sɛla woo en iti ka'lam.

eng: Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, says hello to you. So does Mark, Barnabas cousin .
local: Aristakuse ti ba lagum pagɛ la mam la pu'uheri ya na, bala mɛa ti Maki en de Banabase taya bia la, se'em ti tu yɛta ya a yele wa me pu'uha ya na. A han wa'am ya zi'an na, to'e ya en suŋa.

eng: Jesus, called Justus, also says hello. These are my only fellow workers for Gods kingdom who are Jewish converts. They have been an encouragement to me.
local: Yezu se'em ti ba yi'ira Yusetuse la pu'uhi ya na. Bana ma'a en de sɛba n ŋma bɔna mam tuntuneba tadaanduma la puan Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum tuuma la puan. Bana en de sɛba en bugelɛ mam suure.
