eng: Epaphras, who is one of you, says hello. Hes a slave of Christ Jesus who always wrestles for you in prayers so that you will stand firm and be fully mature and complete in the entire will of God.
local: Epafaras, en de ya ayima la, dɛna Kiresta Yezu da'aberɛ pu'uhi ya na. A mɛɛrɛ saŋa woo pu'uhegɔ puan ya zuo, ti ya wan ze'ele bi'ɛ suŋa, gee ka bu'ura ya suure puan la Yinɛ sumboolum en de se'em.

eng: I can vouch for him that he has worked hard for you and for those in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
local: Mam tɔgɛ a yele mɛ, doohe en mu'ɛ nini tuna se'em bɔ'ɔra yamam la sɛba en boi Laodisiya la Hirapolise tihi puan la.

eng: Luke, the dearly loved physician, and Demas say hello.
local: Luki en de sunɔŋerɛ tiba la, la Demase pu'uhi ya na.

eng: Say hello to the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, along with Nympha and the church that meets in her house.
local: Pu'uhi ya n suurɔ seto en boi Laodisiya la, gee pu'uhɛ ya tu taya Nyimfa, la lageŋɔ la en boi a yire la.

eng: After this letter has been read to you publicly, make sure that the church in Laodicea reads it and that you read the one from Laodicea.
local: Ba han ta kãalɛ gɔŋɔ wa bo' ya ba'ahɛ, bahɛ ya ti ba kãalɛ kɔ bo' lageŋɔ la en boi Laodisiya tiŋa puan la me, gee la ya me kãalɛ gɔnseko en gulehɛ bo' Laodisiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la me.

eng: And tell Archippus, See to it that you complete the ministry that you received in the Lord.
local: Gee me yele Akipus yeti, Bihɛ suŋa la fum ba'ahɛ tuunse'ere ti fum to'e Daana zi'an la.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting personally. Remember that Im in prison. Grace be with you.
local: Mam Pɔɔl en gulehɛ pu'uhegɔ wa la n mɛŋa nu'o. Tɛri ya mam boi paka deen la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya.

eng: As you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, our visit with you wasnt a waste of time.
local: Yamam mɛhi mi', n suurɔ, ti tu kinɛ ya zi'an na la, ka bahɛ yoo.

eng: On the contrary, we had the courage through God to speak Gods good news in spite of a lot of opposition, although we had already suffered and were publicly insulted in Philippi, as you know.
local: gee la tumam en pun nyɛ namehegɔ, la zufagehum sebo itegɔ seko Filipi timpaberɛ wuu yan mi' de se'em la, tumam kelum tara inkpegere mɛ muula Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la, la sɛba en pun ka sakɛ bo' tu la en zo'e se'em la.

eng: Our appeal isnt based on false information, the wrong motives, or deception.
local: Tumam en yeti ya sɛla la ka ze'ele la pa'ahegɔ bii putɛ'yoo, bii nigeŋɔ puan na.

eng: Rather, we have been examined and approved by God to be trusted with the good news, and thats exactly how we speak. We arent trying to please people, but we are trying to please God, who continues to examine our hearts.
local: gee wuu Yinɛ en bihɛ nam tɔ ti a dikɛ faarɛ yelehum wa iŋɛ tu nu'uhen se'em la, bala ti tumam ka tɔgera ti tu pee saaleba suya, gee tu pee Yinɛ suure, se'em en makere tu suya bihɛ la.

eng: As you know, we never used flattery, and God is our witness that we didnt have greedy motives.
local: Wuu yan mi' se'em la, ti Yinɛ me dɛna tumam aze'nyɛ daana se'em la, tu ka tabelɛ wa'am ya zi'an na la nɔmaalum yetɔgum, bii la kunkonyiim puti'irɛ.

eng: We didnt ask for special treatment from people—not from you or from others.
local: bii tu ɛɛra pɛka saaleba zi'an, la dɛna yamam zi'an bii basɛba zi'an.

eng: although we could have thrown our weight around as Christs apostles. Instead, we were gentle with you like a nursing mother caring for her own children.
local: tu ta'am soke ya sɛla yele, wuu tumam en de Kiresta tuntuneba la. Gee tu daa ani la ma'ma la ya, wuu pɔgesɔɔ en biheri ka mɛŋa kɔma la giile se'em la.

eng: We were glad to share not only Gods good news with you but also our very lives because we cared for you so much.
local: Tu ba' tara la ya giile gaŋɛ zuo, ti tu iŋɛ tu puti'irɛ ti tu tɔrɛ la ya, dagi la Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la ma'a, gee tu mɛhi vom. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya ba' bɔna la tu suya puan.

eng: You remember, brothers and sisters, our efforts and hard work. We preached Gods good news to you, while we worked night and day so we wouldnt be a burden on any of you.
local: Ya tɛri tumam n daa tuni gee nameha se'em, n suurɔ, tu daa tuni la bala yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa za'a, ti tu da dɛna zeero bo' ya, tumam n muulɛ Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la bɔ'ɔra ya la.

eng: You and God are witnesses of how holy, just, and blameless we were toward you believers.
local: Yamam dela aze'nyɛduma, la Yinɛ me, tumam en de tuulum, la tuuntɔɔ, ka tara tuure tu itegɔ puan se'em bɔ'ɔra yamam sɛba en nam la.

eng: Likewise, you know how we treated each of you like a father treats his own children.
local: Wuu yan mi' se'em la, tumam iti ya woo mɛ, wuu Sɔ en iti a kɔma se'em la.

eng: We appealed to you, encouraged you, and pleaded with you to live lives worthy of the God who is calling you into his own kingdom and glory.
local: ka'ana, gee kpɛmeha ya, gee me bɛlena ya ti ya vɔna vom sebo taaba n nari bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, se'em en yi' ya ki'ihɛ a mɛŋa sɔ'ɔlum, la a suŋa puan la.

eng: We also thank God constantly for this: when you accepted Gods word that you heard from us, you welcomed it for what it truly is. Instead of accepting it as a human message, you accepted it as Gods message, and it continues to work in you who are believers.
local: Tu me kelum bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara mɛ saŋa woo yele dina wa zuo. Ti yan to'e Yinɛ yelehum la ti ya wum tu zi'an la, ya nam bu mɛ, ti bu dagi asaala yelehum, gee bu sirum dela Yinɛ yelehum sebo en tuni la yamam sɛba en nam la suŋa suŋa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you became imitators of the churches of God in Judea, which are in Christ Jesus. This was because you also suffered the same things from your own people as they did from the Jews.
local: Gee yamam n suurɔ wa, ya dela sɛba en tɔgehi Yinɛ lageŋɔ la en boi Kiresta Yezu puan bɔna Yudiya la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya namehɛ la bala mɛa ya mɛhi nɛreba zi'an, wuu ban yuum iŋɛ Yuuduma puan se'em la.

eng: They killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and drove us out. They dont please God, and they are hostile to the entire human race.
local: Sɛba en ku' Daana Yezu, la nɔtɔgeheba la, gee sa'ɛ tu bahɛ yiŋa la. Ba ba' tum sagum la Yinɛ suure, gee ka sakera bɔ'ɔra se'em se'em.

eng: when they try to stop us from speaking to the Gentiles so they can be saved. Their sins are constantly pushing the limit. Gods wrath has caught up with them in the end.
local: doohe la ban pɛregeri tɔ ti tu da tɔgera yelehum la bɔ'ɔra sɛba en dagi Yuuduma, ti ba nyɛ faarɛ la. Ŋwana ti saŋa woo, ba be'em la malum pa'ahera, gee ti Yinɛ sunsunyiim wan sige ba zuo mɛ ba'ahegɔ puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we were separated from you for a while physically but not in our hearts. We made every effort in our desire to see you again face-to-face.
local: Gee han dɛna tumam n suurɔ, saŋa fii wa tumam han lebege kiibehe la tumam en welege tu mɛhi ya zi'an ingɔŋɔ wa puan la, gee dagi suure puan. Tu ba' bɔta mɛ la sunɔŋerɛ ti tu nyɛ taaba la tu nini.

eng: We wanted to come to you—I, Paul, tried over and over again—and Satan stopped us.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ba' boti ni ti tu wa'am ya zi'an me na. Yelemɛŋerɛ, mam Pɔɔl kɔ'ɔm boti ni n lebe ya zi'an mɛ na yɛ'ɛha, gee Sagena la en gu' tu sore la.

eng: What is our hope, joy, or crown that we can brag about in front of our Lord Jesus when he comes? Isnt it all of you?.
local: Beni en de tumam beere puti'irɛ, bii nyɛɛma, bii nyu'ɔ ŋmɛ'a muŋa tu Daana Yezu nɛŋan, a lɛbega daarɛ na la? La dagi la yamam?.

eng: You are our glory and joy!.
local: Ɛ̃ɛ, yamam en de tumam na'ahegɔ la tu nyɛɛma!.

eng: So when we couldnt stand it any longer, we thought it was a good idea to stay on in Athens by ourselves.
local: Ŋwana, tumam en daa kan le ta'am ŋmibe la zuo, tu daa iŋɛ tu puti'irɛ ti tu yee la tu ma'a Atense timpaberɛ puan.

eng: and we sent you Timothy, who is our brother and Gods coworker in the good news about Christ. We sent him to strengthen and encourage you in your faithfulness.
local: gee tum Timoti tu suuɔ, la tu tuntuna tadaana Yinɛ puan, se'em en muuli Kiresta faarɛ yelehum la, ti a wa'am na bo' ya paŋa, gee kpemehe ya ya sakerɛ la zuo.

eng: We didnt want any of you to be shaken by these problems. You know very well that we were meant to go through this.
local: ti ya se'em da miina mɛlehegɔ kuna wa zuo. Yelemɛŋerɛ, ya mɛhi mi' ti tumam n lu' tu suya ti tu paaɛ zi'an n bala.

eng: In fact, when we were with you, we kept on predicting that we were going to face problems exactly like what happened, as you know.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tumam n daa boi la ya la, tu pun daa yele ya mɛ, gee ti la nan ka paaɛ, ti tu wan nyaa mɛlehɛ, ti la sirum wa'am na dɛna bala, wuu yan mi' de se'em la.

eng: Thats why I sent Timothy to find out about your faithfulness when I couldnt stand it anymore. I was worried that the tempter might have tempted you so that our work would have been a waste of time.
local: Yele dina wa zuo, mam daa kan le ta'am ŋmibe la zuo, ti n tum na, ti n baŋɛ ya sakerɛ la en ze' se'em. Mam zoti la dabeem ti daan se'ere mageha la magehɛ ya bihɛ mɛ, ti tu namehegɔ tuuma la bahɛ yoo.

eng: Now Timothy has returned to us from you and has given us good news about your faithfulness and love! He says that you always have good memories about us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
local: Gee Timoti nan kɔ'ɔm ze'ele la ya zi'an wa'am tu zi'an na wa. A tari la yelesuma na bo' tu doohe ya sakerɛ, la ya nɔŋerɛ en ani se'em. A me yele tu ti ya kɔ'ɔm tɛra tu yele mɛ suŋa suŋa, gee mu'ɛ bɔta ti ya nyɛ tɔ, wuu tumam en kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti tu nyɛ ya se'em la.

eng: Because of this, brothers and sisters, we were encouraged in all our distress and trouble through your faithfulness.
local: Ŋwana zuo, n suurɔ, tumam susageŋɔ, la tu mɛlehegɔ wa puan za'a, tumam ihe kpeŋe mɛ yamam zuo, doohe la ya sakerɛ la.

eng: For now we are alive if you are standing your ground in the Lord.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam wan vɔna, ya han kelum sɛ zɛ'a kankaŋi Daana la puan.

eng: How can we thank God enough for you, given all the joy we have because of you before our God?.
local: Tu wan iŋɛ la ŋwani bo' Yinɛ fara yamam zuo la ti la seke, la nyɛɛma sɛ'a ti tumam tara Yinɛ nɛŋan yamam zuo wa?.

eng: Night and day, we pray more than ever to see all of you in person and to complete whatever you still need for your faith.
local: Yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, tu pu'uhi mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti tu nyɛ ya la tu nini, la tu malum lebehe sɛla woo en pɔ̃hegɛ ya sakerɛ puan la.

eng: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus guide us on our way back to you.
local: Nananawa, tu Yinɛ, la Sɔ la mɛŋa, la tu Daana Yezu pa'alɛ tu sore, ti tu wa'am ya zi'an na.

eng: May the Lord cause you to increase and enrich your love for each other and for everyone in the same way as we also love you.
local: Gee ti Daana la iŋɛ ya ti ya zo'e, gee pirɛ la nɔŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra taaba la nɛra woo, wuu tumam en pirɛ la nɔŋerɛ bɔ'ɔra ya se'em la.

eng: May the love cause your hearts to be strengthened, to be blameless in holiness before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his people. Amen.
local: A wan kpemehe ya suya la Yinɛ tuulum vom puan, ti ya ka tara tuure tu Yinɛ, la tu Sɔ la nɛŋan, tu Daana Yezu, la a nɛresunduma la kinɛ puan na.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we ask and encourage you in the Lord Jesus to keep living the way you already are and even do better in how you live and please God—just as you learned from us.
local: Ba'ahegɔ puan, n suurɔ, tu yeti ya mɛ gee ka'ana ya Daana Yezu puan ti, wuu yan zamehɛ tu zi'an, yan wan vɔna se'em tigɛ Yinɛ nini la, , malum iŋɛ ya bala paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: You know the instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' pa'alegɔ seko ti tu dikɛ bo' ya doohe Daana Yezu puan la.

eng: Gods will is that your lives are dedicated to him. This means that you stay away from sexual immorality.
local: Ŋwana en de Yinɛ sumboolum, ti ya sukɛ ya mɛhi. Ya ayima ayima yehe ya mɛhi yaluntuuma puan.

eng: and learn how to control your own body in a pure and respectable way.
local: Ti ya ayima ayima mina yan wan ta'am tara ya ihe tuulum tarega, la na'ahegɔ tarega.

eng: Dont be controlled by your sexual urges like the Gentiles who dont know God.
local: Dagi la ya tara kunkoma'ahum wuu sɛba en dagi Yuuduma en ka mi' Yinɛ la.

eng: No one should mistreat or take advantage of their brother or sister in this issue. The Lord punishes people for all these things, as we told you before and sternly warned you.
local: Ti ya se'em da tuue, bii fã a suuɔ yele ana wa puan, beni zuo, Daana la mɛŋa en wan lerege yele ana woo puan, wuu tumam en pun te'ele yele ya gee ka'am ya la ninmu'urɛ se'em la.

eng: God didnt call us to be immoral but to be dedicated to him.
local: Yinɛ ka yi' tu na bahɛ dɛgerɔ tuuma puan, gee tu dɛna Yinɛ tuulum duma.

eng: Therefore, whoever rejects these instructions isnt rejecting a human authority. They are rejecting God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
local: Ŋwana zuo, se'em woo en zagehɛ yele ana wa, ka zagehɛ la asaala kpe'eŋo gee Yinɛ, se'em me en bo'ori tu a Fa'a Sia la.

eng: You dont need us to write about loving your brothers and sisters because God has already taught you to love each other.
local: Nananawa, han dɛna suurɔ nɔŋerɛ yele, ya ka eere se'em gulehɛ bo' ya na. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en pun pa'alɛ yamam ti ya nɔŋɛ taaba mɛ.

eng: In fact, you are doing loving deeds for all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. Now we encourage you, brothers and sisters, to do so even more.
local: Ti la me sirum dɛna asira ti ya nɔŋɛ ya suurɔ woo en boi Masedoniya timpaberɛ puan mɛ. Gee tumam ka'ani ya mɛ n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ti ya malum iŋɛ bala paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: Aim to live quietly, mind your own business, and earn your own living, just as I told you.
local: Ya kpeŋe ya mɛhi bɔna bããlam, biha ya mɛhi yɛla, gee sakɛ ya tuna la ya mɛhi nu'uhi wuu tumam en pa'alɛ ya se'em la.

eng: That way youll behave appropriately toward outsiders, and you wont be in need.
local: la ya ta'am tara ya mɛhi suŋa, ita sum bɔ'ɔra yiŋa duma, gee da dikɛ ya mɛhi delum se'em.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want you to know about people who have died so that you wont mourn like others who dont have any hope.
local: Gee tu ka boti ya bɔna aliko n suurɔ, doohe sɛba en ki la yele, ti ya suya da ba' sagum wuu sɛba en ka tara beere puti'irɛ la.

eng: Since we believe that Jesus died and rose, so we also believe that God will bring with him those who have died in Jesus.
local: Tumam en diŋɛ nam ti Yezu ki' mɛ gee ihege la, bala mɛa ti Yezu puan, Yinɛ wan tari sɛba en ki la en la wa'am na.

eng: What we are saying is a message from the Lord: we who are alive and still around at the Lords coming definitely wont go ahead of those who have died.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti tu yɛta ya Daana yelehum la puan yeti, tumam sɛba en vɔi wa, la sɛba en yee gura Daana kinɛ na la, sore ka boi ti tumam wan tole sɛba en ki la nɛŋa.

eng: This is because the Lord himself will come down from heaven with the signal of a shout by the head angel and a blast on Gods trumpet. First, those who are dead in Christ will rise.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la mɛŋa wan sige na, doohe la nuurɛ ti ba bo' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, ti suŋereba nɛŋa daana la yi' na, ti Yinɛ tintanɛ la pɛɛbera, ti sɛba en ki' Kiresta puan la ihege yia.

eng: Then, we who are living and still around will be taken up together with them in the clouds to meet with the Lord in the air. That way we will always be with the Lord.
local: ti tumam sɛba en vɔi wa, la tu sɛba en yee wa, wan naaɛ la ba sagbama puan, ti tu to'ohe Daana la kusebego puan, bala ti tu wan bɔna la Daana la wuuha wuuha.

eng: So encourage each other with these words.
local: Bala, kpemehe ya taaba la yelehum buna wa.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy: To the church of the Thessalonians, which is in God our Father, and in the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, Silevanuse, la Timoti, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra Tɛsalonikaduma lageŋɔ la n boi doohe Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta paŋa puan la.

eng: Grace and peace to all of you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na bɔna la ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we must always thank God for you. This is only right because your faithfulness is growing by leaps and bounds, and the love that all of you have for each other is increasing.
local: Tu dela tu bo' Yinɛ fara saŋa woo ya zuo n suurɔ, wuu lan nari se'em. Beni zuo, ya sakerɛ la zo'ore mɛ yabera, ti ya woo en nɔŋɛ taaba se'em la malum pa'aha.

eng: Thats why we ourselves are bragging about you in Gods churches. We tell about your endurance and faithfulness in all the harassments and trouble that you have put up with.
local: Ŋwana zuo, tumam mɛhi ŋmɛ'ɛri la nyɔ' ya zuo, Yinɛ lageŋɔ la puan, la yan sɛ ze'ele kankaŋi gee tara sakerɛ ya mɛlehegɔ a too seko puan ti ya ŋmibera wa.

eng: This shows that Gods judgment is right, and that you will be considered worthy of Gods kingdom for which you are suffering.
local: Ŋwana en de daalehum bo' Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ gakerɛ la, a maahum ti a bahɛ ti ya nara mɛ la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, ti ya me namehera bu zuo la.

eng: After all, its right for God to pay back the ones making trouble for you with trouble.
local: La dela ma'a bo' Yinɛ ti a lerege pɔgeleŋɔ bo' sɛba en pɔgelum ya la.

eng: and to pay back you who are having trouble with relief along with us. This payback will come when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his powerful angels.
local: gee bo' sɛba ti ba pɔgelum la me fai, ta ta' la tumam me, Daana Yezu han ta ze'ele saazuo niiɛ na, la a suŋereba bumpahi la.

eng: He will give justice with blazing fire to those who dont recognize God and dont obey the good news of our Lord Jesus.
local: Kina na la bugum en diti zigi zigi, lɛregera sɛba en ka mi' Yinɛ, la sɛba en ka nam Daana Yezu faarɛ yelehum la.

eng: They will pay the penalty of eternal destruction away from the Lords presence and away from his mighty glory.
local: Bana n wan ta nyɛ namehegɔ n ka tari ba'ahegɔ sageŋɔ la. Sɛba ti ba yelege ba Daana la nɛŋan, a suŋa, la a paŋa la puan.

eng: This will happen when he comes on that day to receive honor from his holy people and to be admired by everyone who has believed—and our testimony to you was believed.
local: De daarɛ la, a han ta wa'am na ti a Niiɛ a suŋa la, a suŋa la wan niiɛ a nɛresunduma la puan, ti sɛba woo en nam zɛ'a yɛli a suŋa la zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, ya nam tumam aze'nyɛ muulegɔ la mɛ.

eng: We are constantly praying for you for this: that our God will make you worthy of his calling and accomplish every good desire and faithful work by his power.
local: Lan paaɛ ŋwana beene la, tu pu'uheri saŋa woo mɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, suhera ti Yinɛ wan bahɛ ti ya nara la a yi'a la ti a yi' la. A wan doohe a paŋa la puan bahɛ ti ya putɛ'suma la ya sakerɛ tum.

eng: Then the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored by you, and you will be honored by him, consistent with the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ti tu Daana Yezu yu'urɛ la nyɛ na'ahegɔ ya puan, ti yamam me nyɛ na'ahegɔ a puan, doohe tu Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have a request for you concerning our Lord Jesus Christs coming and when we are gathered together to be with him.
local: Gee han dɛna tu Daana Yezu Kiresta kinɛ na la, la tumam n wan ta lagehɛ taaba bɔna la en la, tu beleni ya mɛ, tu suurɔ.

eng: We dont want you to be easily confused in your mind or upset if you hear that the day of the Lord is already here, whether you hear it through some spirit, a message, or a letter supposedly from us.
local: ti ya da bu'ura ya puti'ira puan, bii ya pabegɛ Sia puan bii yelehum bii gɔŋɔ seko en zi'im ze'ele tumam zi'an na yɛta ti, Daana dabeherɛ la pun paaɛ mɛ.

eng: Dont let anyone deceive you in any way. That day wont come unless the rebellion comes first and the person who is lawless is revealed, who is headed for destruction.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pã'ahɛ ya sadɔɔ seko puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, daan dinɛ daarɛ la kan wa'am na, han dagena pobiriga la n wa'am yia na. Se'em daana en ka kihi yele la, niiɛ a mɛŋa na, se'em ti la pun maahum biŋe ti a nyɛ sageŋɔ la.

eng: He is the opponent of every so-called god or object of worship and promotes himself over them. So he sits in Gods temple, displaying himself to show that he is God.
local: A ka sakɛ bo' se'em, gee zɛkɛ a mɛŋa gana sɛla woo ti ba yi'ira yinɛ bii na'ahegɔ daana woo, la a nyaa ta'am tara a kuka Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire puan, dikɛ a mɛŋa lebege Yinɛ.

eng: You remember that I used to tell you these things while I was with you, dont you?.
local: Yamam ka tɛri ti mam pun yele ya yele ana wa, mam daa kelum bɔna la ya la?.

eng: Now you know what holds him back so that he can be revealed when his time comes.
local: Ya mi' sɛla en gu'uri be'em daana wa kinɛ nananawa mɛ, gura a ta niiɛ a mɛŋa, a saŋa la han ta paaɛ.

eng: The hidden plan to live without any law is at work now, but it will be secret only until the one who is holding it back is out of the way.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em daana en ka kihi yele la nyaleŋɔ la pun tuna mɛ, gee la wan niiɛ la se'em ma'a ti se'em en gu'uri a paŋa nananawa la ta bahɛ.

eng: Then the person who is lawless will be revealed. The Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath from his mouth. When the Lord comes, his appearance will put an end to him.
local: Bala ma'a ti se'em daana en ka kihi yele wa nyaa wan niiɛ a mɛŋa na, se'em ti Daana Yezu wan sagum en la a nuurɛ sabeeo, diihe en vi doohe a lɛbega la puan na.

eng: When the person who is lawless comes, it will happen through Satans effort, with all kinds of fake power, signs, and wonders.
local: Se'em daana en ka kihi yele la kini na niiɛ mɛ Sagena la tuuma puan, se'em en tari pahi woo, kirehi, la pumpɔreŋɔ nyalema tuuma.

eng: It will happen with every sort of wicked deception of those who are heading toward destruction because they have refused to love the truth that would allow them to be saved.
local: la pa'ahegɔ kãra woo, bɔ'ɔra sɛba en wan bahɛ yoo, ban zagehɛ ba nɔŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la ba nyɛ faarɛ la.

eng: This is why God will send them an influence that will mislead them so that they will believe the lie.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ tum pa'ahegɔ paŋa na, ti ka bahɛ ti ba nam bo' pumpɔreŋɔ.

eng: The result will be that everyone will be judged who is not convinced by the truth but is happy with injustice.
local: ti la bahɛ ti sɛba woo n ka nam yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee dikɛ la kunkɔma'ahum la dɛgerɔ tuuma la nyɛ sageŋɔ.

eng: But we always must thank God for you, brothers and sisters who are loved by God. This is because he chose you from the beginning to be the first crop of the harvest. This brought salvation, through your dedication to God by the Spirit and through your belief in the truth.
local: Gee tumam dela tu bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ fara saŋa woo ya zuo, n suurɔ ti Daana la nɔŋɛ wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en looe ya ti ya dɛna wala diina faarɛ puan, doohe Fa'a Sia la en pee ya la, la yan nam yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: God called all of you through our good news so you could possess the honor of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Dina zuo, ti a yi' ya doohe tumam n muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la bo' ya la, ti ya ta'am tara tu Daana Yezu Kiresta suŋa la.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold on to the traditions we taught you, whether we taught you in person or through our letter.
local: Bala zuo, n suurɔ, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi, gee gurɛ ya pa'ali seko ti tu pa'alɛ ya la. La dɛna tu nuurɛ pa'alegɔ, bii tu gulehɛ la gɔŋɔ bo' ya na.

eng: Our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and a good hope.
local: Nananawa, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta mɛŋa, la Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ se'em en nɔŋɛ tɔ gee doohe a zu'uhegɔ puan, bo' tu sumbugehum la beere putɛ'sunɛ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la.

eng: May he encourage your hearts and give you strength in every good thing you do or say.
local: bugelɛ ya suya, gee kpemehe ya suya tunsuma woo la yetɔge sumɔ woo puan.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy. To the Thessalonians church that is in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace and peace to all of you.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, Silevanuse, la Timoti en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra sakereba Lageŋɔ la en boi Tɛsalonika la, Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Daana Yezu Kiresta. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum bɔna la ya.

eng: We always thank God for all of you when we mention you constantly in our prayers.
local: Tumam daarɛ woo bo'ori Yinɛ fara mɛ ya za'a zuo, gee puta ya yu'ura tu pu'uhegɔ puan saŋa woo.

eng: This is because we remember your work that comes from faith, your effort that comes from love, and your perseverance that comes from hope in our Lord Jesus Christ in the presence of our God and Father.
local: Tu tɛɛra tu Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la nɛŋan ya sakerɛ tuuma, la ya nɔŋerɛ tuuma, la yan ze' se'em kankaŋi ya beere puti'irɛ la puan se'em tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are loved by God, and we know that he has chosen you.
local: Tu mi', tu suurɔ wa en de Yinɛ sunɔŋerɛ nɛreba wa, ti eŋa en looe ya.

eng: We know this because our good news didnt come to you just in speech but also with power and the Holy Spirit and with deep conviction. You know as well as we do what kind of people we were when we were with you, which was for your sake.
local: beni zuo, tu faarɛ yelehum muulegɔ la ka wa'am ya zi'an na dɛna la yetɔgum ma'a, gee bu wa'am mɛ na la paŋa, la Fa'a Sia, la suteere paŋa. Wuu yamam en mi' tumam en pa'alɛ ya tumam en de nɛresɛba taaba bɔna la ya yamam zuo la.

eng: You became imitators of us and of the Lord when you accepted the message that came from the Holy Spirit with joy in spite of great suffering.
local: Ti yamam me tɔgehɛ tumam, la tu Daana la, se'ere en sɔi la, la yan pun nyɛta namehegɔ la, ya to'e yelehum la mɛ la nyɛɛma sɛ'a ti Fa'a Sia bo' la mɛ.

eng: As a result you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
local: Bala ti ya dɛna makerɛ bo' sakereba la woo en boi Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi la.

eng: The message about the Lord rang out from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia but in every place. The news about your faithfulness to God has spread so that we dont even need to mention it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana yelehum la doohe ya puan yehe mɛ, dagi la bu paaɛ la Masedoniya la Akaiya tihi ma'a puan, gee zi'an woo, ya sakerɛ se'ere tara Yinɛ puan la niiɛ mɛ, ti la pun ka pakɛ ti tumam le tɔgɛ de yele.

eng: People tell us about what sort of welcome we had from you and how you turned to God from idols. As a result, you are serving the living and true God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre sɛba en boi tihi sina puan la, puti tu yele mɛ yan to'e tu se'em, la yan ŋmɛregɛ baga kaabegɔ puan se'em lebe Yinɛ zi'an na, ti ya tuna bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ vua, la yelemɛŋerɛ Yinɛ wa se'em.

eng: and you are waiting for his Son from heaven. His Son is Jesus, who is the one he raised from the dead and who is the one who will rescue us from the coming wrath.
local: gee gura a Dayua la en wan ze'ele saazuo na la, se'em ti a ihege en kum puan la. Yezu, se'em en faaɛ tɔ Yinɛ sunsunyiim sebo en kini na la.

eng: We dont need to write to you about the timing and dates, brothers and sisters.
local: Nananawa, han dɛna saŋa la bii dabeherɛ la, n suurɔ, ya dagi ba gulehɛ sɛsɛla bo' ya.

eng: You know very well that the day of the Lord is going to come like a thief in the night.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mɛhi mi' suŋa ti Daana la dabeherɛ la, wan wa'am na wuu yu'uŋɔ nageyiga la.

eng: When they are saying, There is peace and security, at that time sudden destruction will attack them, like labor pains start with a pregnant woman, and they definitely wont escape.
local: Ba han ta yeti, Suma'ahum bii fai nyaa boi. Bilam bilam ti sageŋɔ wan sige ba zuo na, ti too la ana wuu pɔka n virege la, ti piirɛ kan bɔna!.

eng: But you arent in darkness, brothers and sisters, so the day wont catch you by surprise like a thief.
local: Gee yamam n suurɔ, ya ka boi lika puan, ti de daarɛ la ta iŋɛ ya pakerɛ wuu nageyiga la.

eng: All of you are children of light and children of the day. We dont belong to night or darkness.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya za'a dela niihum kɔma, dɛna beyiine kɔma. Tumam dagi yu'uŋɔ, bii lika puan kɔma.

eng: So then, lets not sleep like the others, but lets stay awake and stay sober.
local: Bala, da bahɛ ya ti tu gihere ya wuu basɛba n iti se'em la, gee bahɛ ya ti tu piga, gee tara tu mɛhi suŋa.

eng: People who sleep sleep at night, and people who get drunk get drunk at night.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba n gihere, gihere la yu'uŋɔ, ti sɛba n bugeri, bugera yu'uŋɔ.

eng: Since we belong to the day, lets stay sober, wearing faithfulness and love as a piece of armor that protects our body and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
local: Gee tumam en de beyiine kɔma la, bahɛ ya ti tu tara tu mɛhi suŋa, gee yɛ sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ ti la dɛna tu nyu'ɔ tɛ'ɛlega, Gee han dɛna tu zuo wanɛ, tu vuge faarɛ en de tu beere puti'irɛ la.

eng: God didnt intend for us to suffer his wrath but rather to possess salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka maahum tu ba'ahegɔ zi'an bahɛ la sunsunyiim puan, gee tu nyɛ faarɛ doohe tu Daana Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: Jesus died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with him.
local: se'em en ki bo' tu, ti tu pige woo, tu gihi woo, tu ta'am vɔna la en.

eng: So continue encouraging each other and building each other up, just like you are doing already.
local: Ŋwana zuo, kpemehe ya taaba gee mɛta taaba wuu yan pun ita se'em la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we ask you to respect those who are working with you, leading you, and instructing you.
local: Gee tu beleni ya mɛ tu suurɔ, na'aheri ya sɛba en tuni ya puan la, sɔna ya biha Daana la puan, gee ka'ana ya la.

eng: Think of them highly with love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
local: To'ohe ya ba suŋa suŋa la nɔŋerɛ, ba tuuma la zuo. Boni ya la suma'ahum la taaba.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are disorderly. Comfort the discouraged. Help the weak. Be patient with everyone.
local: Tu yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ tu suurɔ, ti ya ka'ana sɛba en iti bura bura la, la ninmu'urɛ, gee kpɛmeha sɛba puti'ira en kɔ'ɛ la. Suŋeri ya tarenduma, tari ya sugeri bɔ'ɔra ba za'a.

eng: Make sure no one repays a wrong with a wrong, but always pursue the good for each other and everyone else.
local: Bihɛ ya suŋa ti ya se'em da dikɛ be'em yɔ be'em. Gee saŋa woo, eere ya ti ya ita sum bɔ'ɔra taaba, la nɛra woo.

eng: Rejoice always.
local: Nyɛɛni ya saŋa woo.

eng: Pray continually.
local: pu'uheri ya da gɔ'ɔra.

eng: Give thanks in every situation because this is Gods will for you in Christ Jesus.
local: bo'ori ya Yinɛ fara yelese'ere woo puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sumboolum n bala bo' ya Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: Dont suppress the Spirit.
local: Da kiiheri ya Fa'a Sia la.

eng: Dont brush off Spirit-inspired messages.
local: Da zɛregerɛ ya nɔtɔgeheba yelehum la.

eng: but examine everything carefully and hang on to what is good.
local: gee magehere ya sɛla woo biha. Gurɛ ya sɛla en ani suŋa la mɔpi.

eng: Avoid every kind of evil.
local: Yehe ya mɛhi be'em woo puan.

eng: Now, may the God of peace himself cause you to be completely dedicated to him; and may your spirit, soul, and body be kept intact and blameless at our Lord Jesus Christs coming.
local: Suma'ahum Yinɛ la mɛŋa wan sukɛ ya za'a za'a. Ya sia za'a, la ya puti'irɛ za'a, la ya iŋa za'a, la ya inma'ahum za'a, maahum ya biŋe gura tu Daana Yezu Kiresta kinɛ na.

eng: The one who is calling you is faithful and will do this.
local: Se'em en yi' ya la dela sakerɛ daana, a wan iŋɛ ŋwana wa.

eng: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
local: N suurɔ, pu'uheri ya bo'ori tɔ.

eng: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
local: Pu'uheri ya suurɔ woo la tuulum kɔkerɛ.

eng: By the Lords authority, I order all of you to have this letter read aloud to all the brothers and sisters.
local: Mam ka'ani ya mɛ Daana la puan, ti ya kãalɛ gɔnkuna wa bo' suurɔ la za'a.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ wan bɔna la ya.

eng: From Paul, who is an apostle of Jesus Christ by the command of God our savior and of Christ Jesus our hope.
local: Mam Pɔɔl en de Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, doohe la nuurɛ ti Yinɛ en de tu faara la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu beere puti'irɛ la bo' mam la.

eng: To Timothy, my true child in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: N gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra la Timoti, en de mam mɛŋa dayua sakerɛ la puan. Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu Daana la zi'an na la dɛna fum dinɛ.

eng: When I left for Macedonia, I asked you to stay behind in Ephesus so that you could instruct certain individuals not to spread wrong teaching.
local: N yeti fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, wuu mam daa yele fum se'em mam daa wa yɛ'a Masedoniya la, ti fum yee Ɛfisu, la fum pa'alɛ nɛreba basɛba ti ba da pa'ala pa'alegɔ en boi kuyima.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to myths and endless genealogies. Their teaching only causes useless guessing games instead of faithfulness to Gods way of doing things.
local: ba da Sagena saŋa la yele yooro la yaabeduma tuuhum wogerɔ seto en yeen tara nɔkpɛ'ɛma kina na, ka bahera ti nɛra bɔkera Yinɛ suboolum la en dole sakerɛ puan la.

eng: The goal of instruction is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
local: Gee pa'alegɔ kuna wa tuunɛ dela, la bahɛ ti ba tara nɔŋerɛ se'ere en ze'ele susunɛ puan na, putɛ'sunɛ, la yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ.

eng: Because they missed this goal, some people have been distracted by talk that doesnt mean anything.
local: Nɛreba basɛba bɔregɛ mɛ yele ana puan, gee dikɛ ba mɛhi ta bo' yetɔge yooro en ka tari vuurɛ.

eng: They want to be teachers of Law without understanding either what they are saying or what they are talking about with such confidence.
local: bɔta ti ba dɛna ze'elego la pa'aleba, gee me ka bɔkera ba mɛhi en yeti sɛla la, bii sɛla ti ba tɔgera la yele la.

eng: Now we know that the Law is good if used appropriately.
local: Tu mi' ti ze'elego la ani suŋa mɛ, se'em han dɔla dɛ nima nima.

eng: We understand this: the Law isnt established for a righteous person but for people who live without laws and without obeying any authority. They are the ungodly and the sinners. They are people who are not spiritual, and nothing is sacred to them. They kill their fathers and mothers, and murder others.
local: Ŋwana vuurɛ dela, fum bɔkɛ yeti, ze'elego la ka ze'ele bo' sɛba en ka tari tuure, gee sɛba en ka dole kihere, la sɛba en ka nane, la sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ, la be'em duma, sɛba en dagi tuuntɔɔ, la dɛgerɔ duma, sɛba en ku ba ma bii ba sɔ, la nɛre kuureba.

eng: They are people who are sexually unfaithful, and people who have intercourse with the same sex. They are kidnappers, liars, individuals who give false testimonies in court, and those who do anything else that is opposed to sound teaching.
local: yalum duma, buraahi en gã' taaba, sɛba en koohe nɛreba, pumparenduma, pumparum gakereba, la sɛla woo en ka dole yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la.

eng: Sound teaching agrees with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that has been trusted to me.
local: en dole faarɛ yelehum la suŋa en de sum Yinɛ wa dinɛ, ti a dikɛ bo' mam la.

eng: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength because he considered me faithful. So he appointed me to ministry.
local: Mam puurɛ pee mɛ la Kiresta Yezu tu Daana, se'em en bo' mam paŋa, beni zuo, a bihɛ nyɛ ti n nari mɛ, ti a looe mam ti n tuna bɔ'ɔra en.

eng: even though I used to speak against him, attack his people, and I was proud. But I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and without faith.
local: la mam daa pun tuura, dɔla sakereba la nama mɛleha ba, dɛna zabe-zaberega. Gee Yinɛ zɔ mam nimbu'ɔ mɛ, beni zuo, mam daa ka mi' mɛ gee tuna bala, mam ka nam la zuo.

eng: Our Lords favor poured all over me along with the faithfulness and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Daana zu'uhegɔ la ɔkɛ mam mɛ, la sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—and Im the biggest sinner of all.
local: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, dɛna tu to'e bu nu'uhi siyi, ti Kiresta Yezu wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na ti a faaɛ la be'em duma, sɛba ti mam be'em gana ba za'a.

eng: But this is why I was shown mercy, so that Christ Jesus could show his endless patience to me first of all. So Im an example for those who are going to believe in him for eternal life.
local: Gee dinɛ zuo ti a zɔ mam nimbu'ɔ, ti mam puan, be'em nɛŋa daana wa puan, ti Yezu Kiresta wan pa'alɛ a sugeri en ka tari makerɛ la, ti mam dɛna makerɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba n wan wa'am na nam en ti ba nyɛ vom kamba'ahɛ.

eng: Now to the king of the ages, to the immortal, invisible, and only God, may honor and glory be given to him forever and always! Amen.
local: Na'kãtɛ sɛka en boi wuuha, se'em en ka kiiri, Na'liliŋɔ se'em ma'a en de Yinɛ la, sɔna na'ahegɔ la pɛka wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Timothy, my child, Im giving you these instructions based on the prophecies that were once made about you. So if you follow them, you can wage a good war.
local: Pa'alegɔ kuna ti n dikɛ ita fum nu'uhen n bia Timoti, doohe la puresɛka en pun purɛ bahɛ fum zuo la. Ti fum han dɔla a, fum wan zabɛ zabesunɛ.

eng: because you have faith and a good conscience. Some people have ruined their faith because they refused to listen to their conscience.
local: fum tara sakerɛ la putɛ'sunɛ. Ban zagehɛ ba suya en pa'alɛ ba sɛla la, basɛba sakerɛ wɔ̃rɛ mɛ wuu yoore en lu wɔ̃rɛ la.

eng: such as Hymenaeus and Alexander. Ive handed them over to Satan so that they can be taught not to speak against God.
local: Ba puan dela Hameneyɔsi la Alɛgezanda, sɛba ti mam dikɛ ba bo' Sagena la, ti ba nyɔgɛ yɛm da tuura Yinɛ.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us so that the Lords message will spread quickly and be honored, just like it happened with you.
local: Ba'ahegɔ puan, n suurɔ, pu'uheri ya bo'ori ya tɔ, ti Daana yelehum la yiregɛ kalam gee nyɛ na'ahegɔ zi'an woo, wuu bumɔ en ani se'em ya puan wa.

eng: Pray too that we will be rescued from inappropriate and evil people since everyone that we meet wont respond with faith.
local: gee ti tumam me piiɛ putɔpa, la nɛre ŋmɛlema duma nu'uhen. Se'ere en sɔi la, dagi nɛra woo en tari sakerɛ.

eng: But the Lord is faithful and will give you strength and protect you from the evil one.
local: Gee Daana la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana. A wan sɛ ya ze'ele, gee gu' ya nɛrebɛ'a la zi'an.

eng: We are confident about you in the Lord—that you are doing and will keep doing what we tell you to do.
local: Tumam nam Daana la mɛ ya zuo, ti ya iti, gee wan kelum ita yelesɛ'a za'a ti tu bo' ya nuurɛ ti ya ita la.

eng: May the Lord lead your hearts to express Gods love and Christs endurance.
local: Daana la bahɛ ti ya suya kiŋɛ Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ, la ŋmibega Kiresta puan.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we command you in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to stay away from every brother or sister who lives an undisciplined life that is not in line with the traditions that you received from us.
local: Nananawa, tu bo'ori ya la nuurɛ tu zɔduma, Daana Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ puan ti, ya zaaɛ la sakereba sɛba en vɔi zinyagerɛ vom, ka dɔla la pa'aleŋɔ la ti ba to'e tumam zi'an la.

eng: You yourselves know how you need to imitate us because we were not undisciplined when we were with you.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mɛhi mi' yan de ya tɔgehɛ tumam se'em. Tu daa dagi zinyage duma tumam n daa boi la ya la.

eng: We didnt eat anyones food without paying for it. Instead, we worked night and day with effort and hard work so that we would not impose on you.
local: tu daa ka di se'em dia gee ka yɔ. Gee tu daa tuni mɛ gee wɔbegera muregere yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa, ti tu da dɛna zeero bo' ya se'em.

eng: We did this to give you an example to imitate, not because we didnt have a right to insist on financial support.
local: Ŋwana dagi la tu ka tari la sore ti tu soke ya tumam en boti sɛla, gee tu boti tu iŋɛ biŋe mɛ ti ya tɔgehɛ.

eng: Even when we were with you we were giving you this command: If anyone doesnt want to work, they shouldnt eat.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam en daa pun bɔna la ya la, tu bo' ya nɔdina wa mɛ yeti, Se'em en ka boti a tum, da di.

eng: We hear that some of you are living an undisciplined life. They arent working, but they are meddling in other peoples business.
local: Tumam wum ti ya sɛba vɔi la zinyagerɛ vom, yeen kiilena, ka tara tuun vua.

eng: By the Lord Jesus Christ, we command and encourage such people to work quietly and put their own food on the table.
local: Nananawa, nɛre bana taaba wa, tu bo'ori ya nuurɛ, gee ka'ana ya Daana Yezu Kiresta puan, ti nɛra woo tum ti a nu'o paaɛ a nuuren .

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont get discouraged in doing what is right.
local: N suurɔ, da taregɛ ya la itesuŋɔ.

eng: Take note of anyone who doesnt obey what we have said in this letter. Dont associate with them so they will be ashamed of themselves.
local: Mini ya sɛba en ka nam sɛla ti tu yɛta ya gɔnkuna wa puan wa. Da tari ya sɛsɛla ita la ba, bala ti vi wan di ba.

eng: Dont treat them like enemies, but warn them like you would do for a brother or sister.
local: gee la bala za'a, da bihɛ ya ba wuu ba dela ya dinduma, gee ka'ani ya ba la ninmu'urɛ wuu ya suurɔ la.

eng: May the Lord of peace himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
local: Nananawa, suma'ahum Daana la mɛŋa wan bo' ya suma'ahum saŋa woo, sadɔɔ seko woo puan. Daana la bɔna la ya za'a.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand. This verifies that the letter is from me, as in every letter of mine. This is how I write.
local: Mam Pɔɔl, n gulehɛ pu'uhegɔ kuna wa la n mɛŋa nu'o. Ŋwana wa en de daalehum mam gɔŋɔ woo puan, mam n gulehɛ se'em n bala.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

eng: First of all, then, I ask that requests, prayers, petitions, and thanksgiving be made for all people.
local: Sɛla en de yia, mam yeti, ya tara suhega, pu'uhegɔ, nupu'ɔ la fara bu'a pu'uha bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo.

eng: Pray for kings and everyone who is in authority so that we can live a quiet and peaceful life in complete godliness and dignity.
local: Pu'uhi ya bɔ'ɔra naduma, la sɛba woo en boi kpe'em kãra puan. Bala ti tu wan bɔna bããlam la suma'ahum, la Yinɛ zoolum, la tibehum.

eng: This is right and it pleases God our savior.
local: Dina en de tuuntɔɔ gee tigɛ Yinɛ en de tu Faara la nini.

eng: who wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
local: se'em en boti nɛra woo nyɛ faarɛ gee wa'am na baŋɛ mi'ilum sebo en boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan la.

eng: There is one God and one mediator between God and humanity, the human Christ Jesus.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dela deyina, nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra me dela ayima, Yinɛ la saaleba tiŋasuka Ena daana la Kiresta Yezu, se'em en wa'am na dɛna asaala a mɛŋa la.

eng: who gave himself as a payment to set all people free. This was a testimony that was given at the right time.
local: se'em en dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ to'ore kaabegɔ bo' nɛra woo, ti la dɛna aze'nyɛ saŋa ti a pun maahum.

eng: I was appointed to be a preacher and apostle of this testimony—Im telling the truth and Im not lying! Im a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
local: Dina zuo ti a looe mam ti n muula, gee dɛna tuntuna , mam dela sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la pa'ala, sakerɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ puan.

eng: Therefore, I want men to pray everywhere by lifting up hands that are holy, without anger or argument.
local: Mam yi boti ni ti, lageŋɔ woo puan, buraahi pu'uha, gee zɛgera tuulum nu'uhi saazuo, ti susuya bii nɔkpɛ'ɛma da bɔna ba puan.

eng: In the same way, I want women to enhance their appearance with clothing that is modest and sensible, not with elaborate hairstyles, gold, pearls, or expensive clothes.
local: Bala mɛa ti pɔgehi la me dɛna ba yɛɛra lɔgerɔ seto en nari, makɛ ba suŋa, dɛna futo en ani suŋa. Dagi zuto pã'aŋɔ nyalema nyalema, bii salema lɔgerɔ, bii peranini, bii futo da'a en ba' kpe'em, gee sɛla en tari tibehum gani bala dela.

eng: They should make themselves attractive by doing good, which is appropriate for women who claim to honor God.
local: ba nɔna la tuunsuma, wuu lan nari se'em bo' pɔge se'ehi en mi ban na'ahɛ Yinɛ se'em la.

eng: A wife should learn quietly with complete submission.
local: Bahɛ ti pɔgehi zameha bããlam la sigehe n mɛŋa paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: I dont allow a wife to teach or to control her husband. Instead, she should be a quiet listener.
local: Mam ka bo' sore ti pɔka pa'ala bii a tara kpe'eŋo gana buraa, a dela a ana bããlam.

eng: Adam was formed first, and then Eve.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Adam ti ba naam yia gee nyaa naam Eva.

eng: Adam wasnt deceived, but rather his wife became the one who stepped over the line because she was completely deceived.
local: la me dagi Adam ti ba yuum pã'ahɛ la, gee pɔka la ti ba yuum pa'ahɛ ti a lebege be'em daana.

eng: But a wife will be brought safely through childbirth, if they both continue in faith, love, and holiness, together with self-control.
local: Gee la bala za'a, a wan nyɛ faarɛ doohe kɔma dɔgum puan, ba han kelum bɔna sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ, tuuntɔɔ vom, la narehum.

eng: This saying is reliable: if anyone has a goal to be a supervisor in the church, they want a good thing.
local: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti, se'em woo en boti a dɛna Yinɛ Lageŋɔ puan nɛŋa daana, a boti la tuunsunɛ.

eng: So the churchs supervisor must be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, sober, modest, and honest. They should show hospitality and be skilled at teaching.
local: Bala, Yinɛ Lageŋɔ nɛŋa daana dela a zɔɛ tuurɛ, a dɛna pɔge yiŋa daana, a ana ma'ma', a tara puti'irɛ, a tara zuo, a tɔ'ɔha nɛreba, a tana pa'ala.

eng: They shouldnt be addicted to alcohol or be a bully. Instead, they should be gentle, peaceable, and not greedy.
local: a da dɛna bugera, a da dɛna nɛre gbiri gbiri, gee a ana bããlam, a da dɛna zabe-zaberega, a da ba' nɔŋɛ ligeri.

eng: They should manage their own household well—they should see that their children are obedient with complete respect.
local: A dela a tana tara a mɛŋa yire suŋa, bahɛ ti a kɔma sigehe ba mɛhi suŋa gee na'aha nɛreba sadɔɔ seko woo puan.

eng: because if they dont know how to manage their own household, how can they take care of Gods church?.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em han ka ta'am tara a mɛŋa yire suŋa, a wan iŋɛ la ŋwani ta'am tara Yinɛ lageŋɔ la?.

eng: They shouldnt be new believers so that they wont become proud and fall under the devils spell.
local: A da dɛna bia sakerɛ la puan, gee a wan ta pirɛ la pa'ali mɛŋa, lu Sagena la sageŋɔ puan.

eng: They should also have a good reputation with those outside the church so that they wont be embarrassed and fall into the devils trap.
local: Se'ere en gani bala dela, a dɛna se'em ti yiŋa duma nam en, ti a da ta lu zufagehum puan, la Sagena la bɛregɔ puan.

eng: In the same way, servants in the church should be dignified, not two-faced, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
local: Yinɛ lageŋɔ Kiimbibehi me dela ba tara ninmu'urɛ, ba da dɛna lebe ke, lebe ke duma, ba da yuura daam zɔ'ɔra, ba da ba' dɛna bu n bɔteba.

eng: They should hold on to the faith that has been revealed with a clear conscience.
local: ba gurɛ sakerɛ yelehum la en gaŋɛ asaala bɔkerɛ la kankaŋi, la putɛ'sunɛ.

eng: They should also be tested and then serve if they are without fault.
local: Ba magehɛ ba yia bihɛ, ti ba han ze'ele ti tuure ka bɔna ba puan, bahɛ ya ti ba tuna kiimbibehi tuuma.

eng: In the same way, women who are servants in the church should be dignified and not gossip. They should be sober and faithful in everything they do.
local: Pɔgehi me dela ba tara ninmu'urɛ, ba da Sagena nɛreba yu'ura gee ba ana ma'ma', ba dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ nɛreba sɛla woo puan.

eng: Servants must be faithful to their spouse and manage their children and their own households well.
local: Kiimbibehi wa woo dela ba dɛna pɔge yiŋa duma, tana ba kɔma, la ba yiya biha suŋa.

eng: Those who have served well gain a good standing and considerable confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Sɛba en tum suŋa ban de kiimbibehi la zuo la, ba nyɛ la ze'ele suŋɔ bo' ba mɛhi, gee tara buraanɛ sakerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: I hope to come to you quickly. But Im writing these things to you so that.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ ti n wa'am ya zi'an me na nananawa, gee n gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti.

eng: if Im delayed, youll know how you should behave in Gods household. It is the church of the living God and the backbone and support of the truth.
local: n han yuura, ya wan baŋɛ lan nari ya ita se'em la taaba Yinɛ yire la en de Yinɛ vua wa pu'uhegɔ yire la, en de yelemɛŋerɛ la ziirɛ, la ɛ̃berɛ.

eng: Without question, the mystery of godliness is great: he was revealed as a human, declared righteous by the Spirit, seen by angels, preached throughout the nations, believed in around the world, and taken up in glory.
local: Bu'a ka boi, tumam pu'uhegɔ wa puan yele sɛ'a en gani asaala bɔkerɛ la, dela yele kãra, A daa niiɛ a mɛŋa la inganɛ puan, di buurɔ Sia la puan, suŋereba nyɛ en mɛ, ba muulɛ a yele mɛ sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la puan ti tiŋa wa zuo nam en, a zom la zuin zuin ha la na'ahegɔ.

eng: The Spirit clearly says that in latter times some people will turn away from the faith. They will pay attention to spirits that deceive and to the teaching of demons.
local: Nananawa, Fa'a Sia la yele ti la niiɛ mɛ, ti ba'ahegɔ saŋa la, sɛba wan zagehɛ ba Kiresta kɔma sakerɛ la, doohe la ban kelehe pã'ahegɔ sɛɛhi, la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi pa'alegɔ.

eng: They will be controlled by the pretense of lying, and their own consciences will be seared.
local: doohe la birekɔgen-pumparenduma sɛba zuto en ka ŋmɛ'ɛri kibegera la.

eng: They will prohibit marriage and eating foods that God created—and he intended them to be accepted with thanksgiving by those who are faithful and have come to know the truth.
local: Ba pa'alegɔ la yeti pɔgedire kihi mɛ, gee pɛregera nɛreba ti ba da dita diihe sise'ehi, ti Yinɛ naam ti sɛba en nam gee mina yelemɛŋerɛ la to'e la pɛka dita la.

eng: Everything that has been created by God is good, and nothing that is received with thanksgiving should be rejected.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛlawoo ti Yinɛ naam, ani la suŋa, sɛla ka de tu zagehɛ, tu han to'e de mɛ la pɛka.

eng: These things are made holy by Gods word and prayer.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ sukɛ de mɛ la yelehum la, la pɛka.

eng: If you point these things out to the believers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus who has been trained by the words of faith and the good teaching that youve carefully followed.
local: Fum han dikɛ pa'ali kuna biŋe suurɔ la nɛŋan, fum wan dɛna Kiresta Yezu tuntuna suŋa, se'em n tigehɛ a mɛŋa la yelehum sebo en bo'ori sakerɛ la, la pa'ali suŋɔ seko ti fum dɔla wa la.

eng: But stay away from the godless myths that are passed down from the older women. Train yourself for a holy life!.
local: Da tara sɛla ita la yetɔge yooro la pɔgenya'ahi sɔlema, gee zamehɛ fum mɛŋa Yinɛ zoolum.

eng: While physical training has some value, training in holy living is useful for everything. It has promise for this life now and the life to come.
local: Beni zuo, wuu giila kpemehego en suŋeri se'em la, bala mɛa ti Yinɛ zoolum tari tuulum sadɔɔ seko woo puan, tara zina vom wa, la vom sebo en kini na nɔbiŋere la.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves complete acceptance.
local: Yetɔgum buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, dɛna tu to'e bu nu'uhi siyi.

eng: We work and struggle for this: Our hope is set on the living God, who is the savior of all people, especially those who believe.
local: Tara tara la zina, tu nameheri mɛ gee zigera, beni zuo, tu dikɛ tu beere puti'irɛ delum la Yinɛ vua wa en de asaala woo Faara wa, yele yele wuu sɛba en nam la.

eng: Command these things. Teach them.
local: Yele ana ti fum wan mu'ɛ nini muula, gee pa'ala a.

eng: Dont let anyone look down on you because you are young. Instead, set an example for the believers through your speech, behavior, love, faith, and by being sexually pure.
local: Da bahɛ ti nɛra nɛra zeem fum bumbile paalum la zuo, gee tugum dɛna makerɛ bo' sakereba la, fum tugum, fum aŋa, nɔŋerɛ, sakerɛ, la fum filam vom.

eng: Until I arrive, pay attention to public reading, preaching, and teaching.
local: Gee ti n ta paaɛ na la, dikɛ fum mɛŋa bo' fum kãala yelehum la bɔ'ɔra nɛrekuuŋɔ, la kpemehego, la pa'alegɔ.

eng: Dont neglect the spiritual gift in you that was given through prophecy when the elders laid hands on you.
local: Da zagehɛ Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en boi fum puan la. Sebo ti ba dikɛ bo' fum doohe purega, ti kiinduma la daa dikɛ ba nu'uhi pagelɛ fum zuo pu'uhɛ la.

eng: Practice these things, and live by them so that your progress will be visible to all.
local: Tuna yele ana, kaabɛ fum mɛŋa bo' a, ti nɛra woo wan nyɛ fum tole nɛŋa se'em.

eng: Focus on working on your own development and on what you teach. If you do this, you will save yourself and those who hear you.
local: Bihɛ fum mɛŋa suŋa, la fum pa'alegɔ la. Kɔ'ɔm ita yele ana, se'ere en sɔi la, fum han ita yele ana, fum wan faaɛ fum mɛŋa, la fum kɛleheba la.

eng: Dont correct an older man, but encourage him like hes your father; treat younger men like your brothers.
local: Da tahɛ bunkutɛ, gee tɔgera bɔ'ɔra en wuu sɔ la, tɔgera bɔ'ɔra budibeto la wuu fum suurɔ la.

eng: treat older women like your mother, and treat younger women like your sisters with appropriate respect.
local: tɔgera bɔ'ɔra pɔgenya'ahi la me, ba wuu maduma la, pɔge sa'ara la me wuu, fum tapa la, la susunɛ.

eng: Take care of widows who are truly needy.
local: Na'aha pokɔpa sɛba en sirum de pokɔpa.

eng: But if a particular widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to respect their own family and repay their parents, because this pleases God.
local: Pokuurɛ han tara kɔma bii yaahi, yia dela ba zamehɛ tuna ba pu'uhegɔ tuuma la ba yiya puan, ba me lebehe bɔ'ɔra ba dɔgereba la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ nini tigɛ mɛ la yele aŋa.

eng: A widow who is truly needy and all alone puts her hope in God and keeps on going with requests and prayers, night and day.
local: Gee se'em en sirum de Pokuurɛ ka tara bihera, dikɛ a mɛŋa delum la Yinɛ, bɔna suhega la pu'uhegɔ puan yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa.

eng: But a widow who tries to live a life of luxury is dead even while she is alive.
local: Gee pɔkɔ-se'ere en diti nyɛlum pun ki mɛ gee vɔna la.

eng: Teach these things so that the families will be without fault.
local: Mu'ɛ nini bo' ba yele ana wa me pa'ahɛ, ti ba vom da tara zɛregerɛ.

eng: But if someone doesnt provide for their own family, and especially for a member of their household, they have denied the faith. They are worse than those who have no faith.
local: Se'em woo en ka biheri a yire, yele yele wuu a yire duma, a zagehɛ sakerɛ la mɛ, a be'em gani se'em en ka sakɛ bo' Yezu.

eng: Put a widow on the list who is older than years old and who was faithful to her husband.
local: Pokuurɛ se'ere yu'urɛ en wan bɔna pokɔpa yu'ura puan la dɛna yuuma pihiyoobe beene yɛ'a ha, gee me ele la buyina.

eng: She should have a reputation for doing good: raising children, providing hospitality to strangers, washing the feet of the saints, helping those in distress, and dedicating herself to every kind of good thing.
local: A dɛna se'em ti nɛreba wan tɔgera a sum yɛla, se'em en bihɛ a kɔma ti ba zo'e suŋa na, a tɔ'ɔha saama, tɔ'ɔha nɛresunduma, suŋera sɛba en boi namehegɔ puan, gee kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' itesuŋɔ woo.

eng: But dont accept younger widows for the list. When their physical desires distract them from Christ, they will want to get married.
local: Gee da digeha pɔkɔbibehi yu'ura pa'aha yu'ura la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba kunkoma'ahum la han ni ta ihege, ve'e ba Kiresta zi'an, ba ni bɔta la ba le ele.

eng: Then they will be judged for setting aside their earlier commitment.
local: bala ni bahɛ ti ba tari la sageŋɔ wa'am ba mɛhi zuto na, la bamam en zagehɛ ba yia nɔbiŋere la.

eng: Also, they learn to be lazy by going from house to house. They are not only lazy, but they also become gossips and busybodies, talking about things they shouldnt.
local: Gee dagi bala ma'a, gee ba zamehɛ la zinyagerɛ, ɛɛra lɔ'ɔha yiya kaara, ba ka ze' la yoo ma'a, gee ba dela yelebibeheduma, la sɛba n kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛreba yɛla puan, tɔgera sɛla en dagi ba tɔgɛ.

eng: So I want younger widows to marry, have children, and manage their homes so that they wont give the enemy any reason to slander us. .
local: Bala, mam wan bɔta ti pɔkɔbibehi ele, dɔgɛ kɔma, bihera ba yiya suŋa, la ba da bo' dima la yɔ'ɔ ti a dɛgɛ tɔ.

eng: Some have already turned away to follow Satan.
local: Se'ere en sɛ la sɛba pun ŋmɛregɛ mɛ dɔla Sagena la.

eng: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should take care of them and not burden the church, so that it can help other widows who are truly needy.
local: Pɔge sɛka en nam Yezu han tara pokɔpa yelemɛŋerɛ a yire, a suŋɛ ba, bala ti zeero kan bɔna lageŋɔ la zuo, ŋwana ti lageŋɔ la wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba me en sirum de pokɔpa la.

eng: Elders who lead well should be paid double, especially those who work with public speaking and teaching.
local: Kiinduma sɛba en tuni lageŋɔ tuuma suŋa la nari la na'ahegɔ en ka tari makerɛ, yele yele wuu sɛba en mu'ɛ nini muula gee pa'ala Yinɛ yelehum la.

eng: The scripture says, Dont put a muzzle on an ox while it treads grain , and Workers deserve their pay .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la gulehɛ yeti, Da lu' lɔlega nuurɛ gee tara ka tulegera ki. Tuntuna dela ba yɔ en.

eng: Dont accept an accusation made against an elder unless it is confirmed by two or three witnesses.
local: Da malum sakɛ zɛregerɛ ti ba zɛregɛ kiinsɛka, han dagena aze'nyɛduma n paaɛ bayi' batã.

eng: Discipline those who are sinning in front of everyone so that all the others will be afraid.
local: Han dɛna sɛba en kɔ'ɔm bɔna be'em puan la, tahɛ ba nɛreba nɛŋan, ti sɛba en gee la, dabeem wan kɛ̃ ba.

eng: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to follow these practices without bias, and without playing favorites.
local: Yinɛ nɛŋan, la Kiresta Yezu nɛŋan, la suŋereba sɛba ti ba looe la nɛŋan, n ka'ani fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti fum to'e yele ana wa za'a, da kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra se'em, ti lugehego da bɔna bini.

eng: Dont rush to commission anyone to leadership, and dont participate in the sins of others. Keep yourself morally pure.
local: Da kabelɛ tara fum nu'uhi pagela nɛra zuo, da lagum naara la sɛba tuna be'em, tara fum vom la ti bu ka tara dɛgerɔ.

eng: Dont drink water anymore, but use a little wine because of your stomach problems and your frequent illnesses.
local: Da le yuura ko'kahegɛ ma'a, gee nyuura daam fii wa, fum puurɛ la, la fum bã'a la en gbirehe la zuo.

eng: The sins of some people are obvious, and the sins are judged before the people must face judgment, but the sins of other people show up later.
local: Nɛreba basɛba be'em ba' niiɛ mɛ gee wan tari gakerɛ wa'am ba zuo na, gee ti sɛba be'em me wan niiɛ la pooren.

eng: In the same way, the good that people do is also obvious and cant be hidden.
local: Bala mɛa ti tuunsuma ba' niiɛ na, gee la han pun dagena bala, ba kɔ'ɔm kan sugɛ gã'a.

eng: Those who are under the bondage of slavery should consider their own masters as worthy of full respect so that Gods name and our teaching wont get a bad reputation.
local: Bahɛ ti sɛba woo en de da'aba na'aha ba kpɛ'ɛnduma sɛla woo puan, ŋwana ti Yinɛ yu'urɛ, la pa'alegɔ la kan nyɛ tuurɛ.

eng: And those who have masters who are believers shouldnt look down on them because they are brothers. Instead, they should serve them more faithfully, because the people who benefit from your good service are believers who are loved. Teach and encourage these things.
local: Sɛba kpɛ'ɛnduma en de sakereba la, ba da zɛɛna ba, se'ere zuo, ba dela lageŋɔ la puan nɛreba. Gee ba tugum tuna suŋa suŋa bɔ'ɔra ba, se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba en diti ba tuuma la nyuurɔ dela sakereba, en de ba suurɔ ti ba nɔŋɛ. Pa'ala gee ka'ana ba yele ana wa la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: If anyone teaches anything different and doesnt agree with sound teaching about our Lord Jesus Christ and teaching that is consistent with godliness.
local: Se'em han pa'ala sɛla en boi la tɔka gee ka sakɛ bo' Daana Yezu Kiresta yelehum, la pa'alegɔ seko en dole Yinɛ zoolum yɛla la.

eng: that person is conceited. They dont understand anything but have a sick obsession with debates and arguments. This creates jealousy, conflict, verbal abuse, and evil suspicions.
local: a pã'ahɛ la a mɛŋa, ka bɔkera sɛla, gee bɔta la gaka la nɔkpe'en yaalehi doohe yu'ura puan. Bana puan nyuure, welehego, tuurɛ, dagebo'.

eng: There is constant bickering between people whose minds are ruined and who have been robbed of the truth. They think that godliness is a way to make money!.
local: ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma la sɛba en ka ti'ihɛ suŋa, ka tara yelemɛŋerɛ la, ti'iha ti Yinɛ zoolum dela bɔɔbɔ sore.

eng: Actually, godliness is a great source of profit when it is combined with being happy with what you already have.
local: La de sira, tarehum kãtɛ boi Yinɛ zoolum puan mɛ, ti la seke.

eng: We didnt bring anything into the world and so we cant take anything out of it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka tari sɛla wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, bala ti tu kan dikɛ sɛla lebe.

eng: well be happy with food and clothing.
local: gee tu han tara dia la lɔgerɔ, tu bahɛ ti la seke tu.

eng: But people who are trying to get rich fall into temptation. They are trapped by many stupid and harmful passions that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
local: Gee sɛba en boti ba ba' tara la luti la magehegɔ puan, ti yeleyaalehi zo'e zo'e la kunkoma'ahum tooro ni nyɔgɛ ba, seto en ni za'ɛ nɛreba bahɛ wuŋerɛ la sageŋɔ puan.

eng: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. Some have wandered away from the faith and have impaled themselves with a lot of pain because they made money their goal.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ligeri nɔŋerɛ en de be'em woo nyɛgerɛ, ti ban mu'ɛ nini ti ba tara la zuo, basɛba zagehɛ sakerɛ la mɛ, lu' ba mɛhi la wuŋerɛ zo'e zo'e.

eng: But as for you, man of God, run away from all these things. Instead, pursue righteousness, holy living, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
local: Gee han dɛna fum, Yinɛ tuntuna wa, zagehɛ yele ana wa woo, dɔla tuuntɔɔ vom, Yinɛ zoolum, sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ, ŋmibega, la ma'ma' bɔŋa.

eng: Compete in the good fight of faith. Grab hold of eternal life—you were called to it, and you made a good confession of it in the presence of many witnesses.
local: Zabɛ sakerɛ zabesunɛ la, gurɛ vom kamba'ahɛ la, se'ere zuo ti ba yi' fum la, la se'ere ti fum purɛ yelemɛŋerɛ purega la nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɛŋan la.

eng: I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when testifying before Pontius Pilate.
local: Yinɛ nɛŋan, se'em en bo'ori sɛla woo vom la, la Kiresta Yezu, se'em en yuum tɔgɛ dinɛ n de se'em Pɔntiyɔsi Palete nɛŋan, gee purɛ yelemɛŋerɛ purega la nɛŋan, ti n bɔ'ɔra hɔ nuurɛ wa.

eng: Obey this order without fault or failure until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ti fum tara ze'elego la, ti dɛkɔ bii zɛregerɛ da bɔna bini ta paaɛ Yezu Kiresta lɛbega na.

eng: The timing of this appearance is revealed by God alone, who is the blessed and only master, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: se'em ti Yinɛ wan bahɛ ti a wa'am sansɛka en nari la puan na la. Eŋa se'em en de sum daana, la se'em ma'a en gani za'a la, Naduma Naba, la Daanduma Daana la.

eng: He alone has immortality and lives in light that no one can come near. No human being has ever seen or is able to see him. Honor and eternal power belong to him. Amen.
local: La dela eŋa ma'a en ka kiiri, gee bɔna niihum sebo en ka kaleni puan. Se'em ti nɛra ka tabelɛ nyɛ en bii wan nyɛ en, eŋa se'em ti na'ahegɔ, la pansɛka en ka ba'aheri dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha la. Taari.

eng: Tell people who are rich at this time not to become egotistical and not to place their hope on their finances, which are uncertain. Instead, they need to hope in God, who richly provides everything for our enjoyment.
local: Han dɛna zina beere buntateba wa, ka'am ba ti ba da pa'ala ba mɛhi, bii ba dikɛ ba puti'irɛ delum sɛla en kan bɔna wuuha, gee ba tugum dɛla Yinɛ, se'em en bo'ori tu sɛla woo zo'e zo'e ti tu dita la pupeelum.

eng: Tell them to do good, to be rich in the good things they do, to be generous, and to share with others.
local: Ba dela ba ita itesuŋɔ, ba dɛna buntateba itesuŋɔ puan, ba bɔ'ɔra, ba dɛna sɛba en maahum ti ba tɔta.

eng: When they do these things, they will save a treasure for themselves that is a good foundation for the future. That way they can take hold of what is truly life.
local: Ŋwana ti ba wan ka'aha lɔgeseto en wan bo' ba beere ɛ̃berɛ sunɛ la biŋera saazuo ha, la ba beere tara vom sebo en sirum de vom la.

eng: Timothy, protect what has been given to you in trust. Avoid godless and pointless discussions and the contradictory claims of so-called knowledge.
local: Timoti, gura sɛla en dikɛ iŋɛ fum puan la suŋa. Zagehɛ yetɔge yooro, la zaba nɔkpɛ'ɛma, la sɛla ti ba parum yi'ira mi'ilum la.

eng: When some people adopted this false knowledge, they missed the goal of faith. May grace be with you all.
local: Ban ti'ihɛ ti bamam mi'ilum la zuo, basɛba buregɛ ba sakerɛ la puan mɛ. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la hɔ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, to promote the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Pɔɔl, Kiresta Yezu tuntuna, Yinɛ suboolum puan, doohe nɔbiŋere la en biŋe doohe vom sebo en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la zuo.

eng: To Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la Timoti en de mam sunɔŋerɛ dayua. Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de tu Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu tu Daana la zi'an na, bɔna la hɔ.

eng: Im grateful to God, whom I serve with a good conscience as my ancestors did. I constantly remember you in my prayers day and night.
local: Mam suure pee mɛ la Yinɛ, se'em ti mam pu'uha la putɛ'sunɛ, wuu mam sɔyaabe duma en yuum iti se'em la, wuu mam en tɛri fum yele saŋa woo se'em n yu'uŋɔ la wuntɛɛŋa pu'uhegɔ puan.

eng: When I remember your tears, I long to see you so that I can be filled with happiness.
local: Mam en tiiheri fum nintam la, n kɔ'ɔm bɔta ti n nyɛ fum mɛ, ti n poore pee.

eng: Im reminded of your authentic faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice. Im sure that this faith is also inside you.
local: La tiiheri mam mɛ fum yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ la yele, sakerɛ se'ere en yuum boi fum mayaaba Lois puan yia, tagelɛ fum ma Yunise, ti nananawa, mam mina ti de boi fum me puan.

eng: Because of this, Im reminding you to revive Gods gift that is in you through the laying on of my hands.
local: Dɛna zuo ti mam tɛɛra hɔ ti fum malum gbirege Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en boi fum puan, ti mam daa dikɛ n nu'uhi pagelɛ fum zuo pu'uhɛ la.

eng: God didnt give us a spirit that is timid but one that is powerful, loving, and self-controlled.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ ka bo' tu la dabeem Sia, gee a tugum bo' tu la Sia en tari paŋa, la nɔŋerɛ, la fum mɛŋa tarega.

eng: So dont be ashamed of the testimony about the Lord or of me, his prisoner. Instead, share the suffering for the good news, depending on Gods power.
local: Bala zuo da tara valum la tu Daana yɛla la muulegɔ, bii mam se'em en boi paka deen a zuo la, gee naaɛ la mam, namehɛ faarɛ yelehum wa zuo, dɛla Yinɛ paŋa la.

eng: God is the one who saved and called us with a holy calling. This wasnt based on what we have done, but it was based on his own purpose and grace that he gave us in Christ Jesus before time began.
local: se'em en faaɛ tɔ, gee yi' tu tuulum yi'a, dagi la tu tuuma zuo, gee eŋa mɛŋa en boti se'em, la a zu'uhegɔ zuo. Zu'uhegɔ kuna wa dikɛ bo' tu doohe la Kiresta Yezu puan, diimen diimen ha.

eng: Now his grace is revealed through the appearance of our savior, Christ Jesus. He destroyed death and brought life and immortality into clear focus through the good news.
local: gee de nyaa niiɛ mɛ na, doohe tu Faara Kiresta Yezu niirɛ na la puan, se'em en wa'am na sagum kum paŋa, gee tari vom la sɛla en ka kiiri, ti la niiɛ peelumen na, doohe faarɛ yelehum la puan.

eng: I was appointed a messenger, apostle, and teacher of this good news.
local: Faarɛ yelehum buna wa ti ba looe mam ti n dɛna bu muula, bu tuntuna, la bu pa'ala.

eng: This is also why Im suffering the way I do, but Im not ashamed. I know the one in whom Ive placed my trust. Im convinced that God is powerful enough to protect what he has placed in my trust until that day.
local: dina wa zuo ti mam nameha wuu mam en namehɛ se'em wa. Gee vi ka tari mam, se'ere en sɔi la, mam mi' se'em ti mam delum en la mɛ, N me mi' suŋa suŋa ti a wan ta'am gura mam ta paaɛ de daarɛ, la sɛla ti a dikɛ iŋɛ mam puan la.

eng: Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching that you heard from me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Gurɛ yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la suŋa, sebo ti fum wum mam zi'an la, la sakerɛ la nɔŋerɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: Protect this good thing that has been placed in your trust through the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
local: Tara tuulum yelesɛ'a en dikɛ iŋɛ fum puan la suŋa, doohe la Fa'a Sia la en boi tu puan la.

eng: You know that everyone in Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
local: Fum mi' ti sɛba woo en boi Esiya tiŋa la zagehɛ mam mɛ, bamam basɛba dela, Figeluse la Hɛmogenese.

eng: May the Lord show mercy to Onesiphorus household, because he supported me many times and he wasnt ashamed of my imprisonment.
local: Daana la wan zɔ Onesiforuse yire duma za'a nimbu'ɔ, beni zuo, a dela se'em en baheri ti mam puti'irɛ gã'a tiŋa, ti vi ka tara en la mam bãhi wa.

eng: After I arrived in Rome, he quickly looked for me and found me.
local: Eŋa paaɛ Arome tiŋa la, a ɛ mam mɛ la ninmu'urɛ ta nyɛ mam.

eng: May the Lord allow him to find his mercy on that day .
local: Daana la bahɛ ti a ta nyɛ nimbɔ'zɔrɛ Daana la zi'an de daarɛ. Fum me mi' en tum zo'e se'em Ɛfisu puan.

eng: So, my child, draw your strength from the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Nananawa, fum n bia wa, kpe'em mɛ la zu'uhegɔ seko en boi Kiresta Yezu puan la.

eng: Take the things you heard me say in front of many other witnesses and pass them on to faithful people who are also capable of teaching others.
local: la sɛla ti fum wum mam zi'an nɛreba zo'e zo'e nɛŋan la. Dikɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ duma sɛba en wan ta'am pa'alɛ basɛba me.

eng: Accept your share of suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
local: Lagum nameha wuu fum de Kiresta Yezu pɛntiira suŋa la.

eng: Nobody who serves in the military gets tied up with civilian matters, so that they can please the one who recruited them.
local: Nɛra ka boi tuna pɛntiireba tuuma, gee le gɛ̃ta a mɛŋa la daare woo yɛla. Pɛntiira puti'irɛ dela a pee a daana la puurɛ.

eng: Also in the same way, athletes dont win unless they follow the rules.
local: Gee mina en zoti puan, nɛra ka boi zɔta dita mi'a gee ka zɔ doohe zua ze'elego la.

eng: A hardworking farmer should get the first share of the crop.
local: Kaara se'em n kɔ la, eŋa en de a to'e a tɔtɛ va'am dia la puan yia.

eng: Think about what Im saying; the Lord will give you understanding about everything.
local: Ti'ihɛ mam en yele fum sɛla wa, se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la wan bo' fum bɔkerɛ sɛla woo puan.

eng: Remember Jesus Christ, who was raised from the dead and descended from David. This is my good news.
local: Tɛra ti Yezu Kiresta ihege mɛ a kum la puan, a dela Deviti yaaŋa, mam faarɛ yelehum en bala.

eng: This is the reason Im suffering to the point that Im in prison like a common criminal. But Gods word cannot be imprisoned.
local: Dina zuo ti mam nyɛ namehegɔ too, pun ta paaɛ ba ba' mam wuu tuunfi'iha daana la. Gee Yinɛ yelehum ka ba'ari.

eng: This is why I endure everything for the sake of those who are chosen by God so that they too may experience salvation in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
local: Bala zuo, mam ŋmiberi mɛ sɛla woo puan, sɛba ti ba looe la zuo, ti ba me nyɛ faarɛ se'ere en boi Kiresta Yezu puan, la na'ahegɔ seko en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la.

eng: This saying is reliable: If we have died together, we will also live together.
local: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ, ti tu han ki mɛ la en, tu me wan vɔna la en.

eng: If we endure, we will also rule together. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
local: Tu han ŋmibe, tu me wan di na'am la en, Tu han zagehɛ en, a mɛ wan zagehɛ tɔ.

eng: If we are disloyal, he stays faithful because he cant be anything else than what he is.
local: Tumam han ka tara sakerɛ, eŋa nuun dela a sakerɛ daana. Se'ere en sɔi la, eŋa kan ta'am zagehɛ a mɛŋa.

eng: Remind them of these things and warn them in the sight of God not to engage in battles over words that arent helpful and only destroy those who hear them.
local: Tiɛ ba dina wa, gee kagɛ ba la ninmu'urɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan, ti ba bahɛ ba tara yɛla ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma, sɛla en ka tari suŋɛ, gee Sagena kɛleheba la.

eng: Make an effort to present yourself to God as a tried-and-true worker, who doesnt need to be ashamed but is one who interprets the message of truth correctly.
local: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum kaabɛ fum mɛŋa suŋa bo' Yinɛ wuu se'em ti Yinɛ mɛŋa nam la. Tuntuna sɛka en ka de a zɔta valum, pa'ala yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la tuuntɔɔ.

eng: Avoid their godless discussions, because they will lead many people into ungodly behavior.
local: Lɔkera sɛba en tɔgeri yetɔge yaalehi, se'ere en sɔi la, bu wan tari nɛreba malum kɛ̃ dɛgerɔ yɛla puan.

eng: and their ideas will spread like an infection. This includes Hymenaeus and Philetus.
local: Ba tugum la wan taaɛ wuu kɔhi kɛka la. Nɛre bana wa basɛba dela Hɛmenayɔse la Filetuse.

eng: who have deviated from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already happened. This has undermined some peoples faith.
local: sɛba en buregɛ yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ la puan, yɛta ti ihegere la pun iŋɛ mɛ. Ba dole tulena la basɛba sakerɛ.

eng: Gods solid foundation is still standing with this sign: The Lord knows the people who belong to him, and Everyone who confesses the Lords name must avoid wickedness. .
local: Gee Yinɛ ɛ̃berɛ la kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a la kankaŋi, tara daalehum buna wa: Daana la mi' sɛba en de a dinɛ. Bala la, bahɛ ti Sɛba woo en yi'iri Daana la yu'urɛ la yehe ba mɛhi be'em puan.

eng: In a mansion, there arent just gold and silver bowls but also some bowls that are made of wood and clay. Some are meant for special uses, some for garbage.
local: Yikãtɛ puan, lɔgerɔ boi bini mɛ, gee dagi la salema la salempɛɛla lɔgerɔ ma'a, gee dɔɔrɔ la yɔgerɔ lɔgerɔ. Basɛba dela la tuulum lɔgerɔ, basɛba mɛ dela tuni woo lɔgerɔ.

eng: So if anyone washes filth off themselves, they will be set apart as a special bowl. They will be useful to the owner of the mansion for every sort of good work.
local: Sɛba woo en pee ba mɛhi yehe yele sɛ'a ti n yele wa puan, wan dɛna tuulum lɔgerɔ en yehe ze'ele, tari tuunɛ bɔ'ɔra yire la daana. Maahum gura tuunsunɛ woo.

eng: Run away from adolescent cravings. Instead, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace together with those who confess the Lord with a clean heart.
local: Zɔ yehe bumbile paalumen kɔrehi puan, gee dɔla tuuntɔɔ vom la ninmu'urɛ, sakerɛ, nɔŋerɛ la suma'ahum la sɛba en pu'uhi Yinɛ la susunɛ la.

eng: Avoid foolish and thoughtless discussions, since you know that they produce conflicts.
local: Da tara sɛla ita la geto la nɔkpe'en yooro. Fum mi' ti, ba tari la zaba kina na.

eng: Gods slave shouldnt be argumentative but should be kind toward all people, able to teach, patient.
local: Daana la tuntuna ka de a dɛna zabe-zabera, gee a ana ma'ma' la nɛrawoo, a tana pa'ala gee tara sugeri.

eng: and should correct opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will change their mind and give them a knowledge of the truth.
local: A pa'ali sɛba en ka sakeri bɔ'ɔra en la, la sigehe mɛŋa. Ani en mi,Yinɛ ta'am bahɛ ti ba tee gee baŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: They may come to their senses and escape from the devils trap that holds them captive to do his will.
local: la ba ta'am piiɛ bumbɛ'a bɛregɔ la, bamam sɛba ti a pun nyɔkɛ ti ba dɛna a da'aba ti ba tum a puti'irɛ la.

eng: Understand that the last days will be dangerous times.
local: Fum dela fum mina dina wa, ti, ba'ahegɔ dabeha la, sukpɛlehi saŋa wan wa'am na.

eng: People will be selfish and love money. They will be the kind of people who brag and who are proud. They will slander others, and they will be disobedient to their parents. They will be ungrateful, unholy.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba wan nɔŋɛ ba mɛhi, nɔŋɛ ligeri, nyɔ'ŋmɛ'ɛreba, nɛre wureba, kɛhega, ka nana dɔgereba, nini ka tigera, dɛgerɔ vom.

eng: unloving, contrary, and critical. They will be without self-control and brutal, and they wont love what is good.
local: ka tara giile, ka dita sugeri, yu'sageneba, ka tana nyɔkera ba mɛhi, sukãberɛ, kiha sum.

eng: They will be people who are disloyal, reckless, and conceited. They will love pleasure instead of loving God.
local: taaba ŋma'a, bura bura, pa'alɛ mɛŋa, nɔŋɛ nyɛlum gana a nɔŋɛ Yinɛ.

eng: They will look like they are religious but deny Gods power. Avoid people like this.
local: kɔ'ɔm gurɛ yiŋa tuunsuma, gee zagehɛ Yinɛ paŋa la. Lɔkera ba.

eng: Some will slither into households and control immature women who are burdened with sins and driven by all kinds of desires.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba puan basɛba dela sɛba en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri yiya puan pã'aha pɔgese'ehi en de geto, pirɛ la be'em, ti kɔrehi woo tana ba.

eng: These women are always learning, but they can never arrive at an understanding of the truth.
local: ti ba pa'ala ba saŋa woo gee ba kan malum baŋɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: These people oppose the truth in the same way that Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. Their minds are corrupt and their faith is counterfeit.
local: Wuu Yenesi la Yamberese en yuum ka sakɛ bo' Moose se'em la, bala ti nɛrebana wa en tari putɛ'be'o la sakerɛ se'ere en dagi yelemɛŋerɛ wa, me ka sakɛ bo' yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: But they wont get very far. Their foolishness will become obvious to everyone like those others.
local: Gee ba kan ba' nyɛ nɛŋa tɔlega bala, beni zuo, wuu lan daa ani se'em buraahi siyi la puan la, bamam gɔɔn tuuma la me wan niiɛ peelumen ti nɛra woo nyɛ.

eng: But you have paid attention to my teaching, conduct, purpose, faithfulness, patience, love, and endurance.
local: Nananawa, fum Timoti, mi' mam pa'alegɔ, la mam aŋa, mam puti'irɛ en de se'em vom puan, n sakerɛ, n sugeri, n nɔŋerɛ, mam n ze' kankaŋi se'em.

eng: You have seen me experience physical abuse and ordeals in places such as Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I put up with all sorts of abuse, and the Lord rescued me from it all!.
local: n mɛlehegɔ la n namehegɔ, la yelesɛ'a en paaɛ mam Antiyoke, Ikonium la Lisitera tihi, namehegɔ seko ti n ŋmibera ku puan! Gee la bala za'a, Daana la faaɛ mam ba za'a puan bahɛ mɛ.

eng: In fact, anyone who wants to live a holy life in Christ Jesus will be harassed.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sɛba woo en boti ba vɔna Yinɛ zoolum vom Kiresta Yezu puan, wan nyɛ mɛlehegɔ.

eng: But evil people and swindlers will grow even worse, as they deceive others while being deceived themselves.
local: Gee tuunfi'iha duma, la pumpã'ahum duma wan ze'ele sageŋɔ paaɛ nɔyalegerɛ. Ba wan pã'aha basɛba, ti basɛba me pã'aha ba.

eng: But you must continue with the things you have learned and found convincing. You know who taught you.
local: Gee han dɛna fum, bɔna sɛla ti fum zamehɛ la puan, gee nam suŋa suŋa, mina se'em daana zi'an ti fum zamehɛ a la.
