eng: Since childhood you have known the holy scriptures that help you to be wise in a way that leads to salvation through faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: ze'ele fum biilemen na, fum mi' fa'a gulehegɔ wa, en wan ta'am pa'alɛ fum ti fum nyɛ faarɛ doohe sakerɛ Kiresta Yezu puan.

eng: Every scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for showing mistakes, for correcting, and for training character.
local: Gulehegɔ la za'a ze'ele la Yinɛ Fa'a Sia la puan na, ana suŋa ti ba tara pa'ala, tahera, dɛmeha, la tuuntɔɔ vom kpemehego.

eng: so that the person who belongs to God can be equipped to do everything that is good.
local: bala ti se'em woo en de Yinɛ dinɛ wan seke, bi'ɛ wan ta'am tum tuunsunɛ woo.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: Yinɛ, la Kiresta Yezu nɛŋan, se'em en wan gakɛ vɔpa la ki'induma la, la a lɛbega na, la a sɔ'ɔlum la zuo, n kageri fum mɛ la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: Muula yelehum la, kɔ'ɔm zɛ'a mɛ kankaŋi, sum saŋa bii be'ero saŋa, tɔgera tɛɛra, tahɛ, kpɛmeha, la pa'alegɔ sugeri zo'e, zo'e.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Gee saŋa la kini na, ti nɛreba kan bɔta yelemɛŋerɛ pa'alegɔ, gee ban tari tuba en zakeni la, ba wan kã'ahɛ pa'aleba bo' ba mɛhi, wuu ba kɔrehi la en boti se'em la.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Gee wan biri pɔya, kan kɛleha yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee tuuna yɛ'a solum yooro puan.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: La han dɛna fum, saŋa woo ta'na fum mɛŋa tara suŋa, ŋmibera namehegɔ puan, tuna ayehemuulɛ daana tuuma, tum fum tuunɛ ba'ahɛ suŋa suŋa.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Nananawa, la han dɛna mam, ba pun ka'ɛ mam mɛ wuu kaabegɔ ko'om la, mam ye'ehego saŋa paaɛ ya.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: Mam zabɛ la zabesunɛ, mam ba'ahɛ n zua la, mam gurɛ en sakerɛ la mɛ.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Nananawa yɛ'a ha, sɛla en dikɛ biŋe gura mam dela, tuuntɔɔ vom muŋa, sɛka ti Daana la en de tuuntɔɔ gakera la, wan dikɛ bo' mam de daarɛ, gee dagi mam ma'a, gee sɛba woo en me guri Yezu lɛbega na la.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa gee wa'am n zi'an kalam na.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, Demase en nɔŋɛ zina beere tiŋa wa la, bahɛ mam gee kiŋɛ la Tɛsalonika tiŋa. Gee Kiresensi kiŋɛ Galatia, ti Taitusi me kiŋɛ Dalematiya tiŋa.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Luki ma'a en boi la mam ka'lam. Ɛ Maki la fum doohe en na, se'ere en sɔi la, a tari tuulum mam la tuuma wa puan.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: Mam tum Takikuse la Ɛfisu.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: Fum han kina na, tari n fuwoko la ti n bahɛ la Kapuse en boi Torowase tiŋa la, la gɔnɔ la, yele yele woo gɔnɔ seto ti n kilum la.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Alɛgezanda se'em en kuti la ba' pɔgelum mam mɛ, Daana wan lebehe yɔ en a be'em tuuma la.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: Yamam me dela ya mina a yele, se'ere en sɔi la, a kɔ'ɔm mum la nini zagehɛ tumam yelehum la.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Mam wa kiŋɛ yia ti n tɔgɛ n nuurɛ la, nɛra daa ka ze'ele ti a suŋɛ mam, gee ba za'a zagehɛ mam mɛ. Yinɛ bahɛ ba taalɛ bo' ba.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Gee Daana la en ze'ele la mam, gee bo' mam paŋa, ti doohe mam puan, yelehum la wan muulɛ suŋa, ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la wan wam dɛ. Bala ti a faaɛ mam gbigenɛ nuuren la.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Daana la wan faaɛ mam be'em zaberɛ woo puan bahɛ, gee tara mam kɛ̃ a saazuo sɔ'ɔlum la puan. Eŋa en sɔi na'ahegɔ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: Pu'uhɛ Pirisila la Akuila, la Onesiforuse yire duma.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Pu'uhɛ Erastuse en yee Korinti, la Tirofimuse en ka paaɛ a mɛŋa ti mam bahɛ en Militusi la.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum wa'am na gee ti ko'oro saŋa paaɛ. Yubuluse pu'uhi fum na, bala mɛa ti Pudɛnsi, Linuse, Kulodiya la tu suurɔ za'a pu'uha fum na.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Daana la bɔna la hɔ sia, zu'uhegɔ bɔna la hɔ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Mam Pɔɔl en de Yinɛ da'aberɛ, gee dɛna Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la sakerɛ la zuo, la yelemɛŋerɛ mi'ilum sebo en dole Yinɛ zoolum la.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: beere puti'irɛ en de vom kamba'ahɛ la, ti Yinɛ en ka pareni wa biŋe nuurɛ gee baŋɛ naam tiŋa wa la.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: Sansɛka en nari la puan, ti a niiɛ a yelehum la doohe muulegɔ puan, muulegɔ seko en dikɛ bo' mam, doohe Yinɛ en de tu faara la nuuren.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: Mam en guleheri bɔ'ɔra fum Taitusi en de mam sunɔŋerɛ dayua, sakerɛ se'ere ti tu lagum dɔla la puan. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Kiresta Yezu en de tu Faara la bɔna la ya.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: Mam bahɛ hɔ Kerete tiŋa puan la dina wa zuo, ti fum maalɛ yelesɛ'a en gee dɛna a tum la, gee loohe kiinduma tiŋa woo puan, wuu mam en pa'alɛ fum se'em la.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: Ba dɛna sɛba en ka tari zɛregerɛ ba vom puan, sire-sɛka en di pɔgeyiŋa, sɛba kɔma en nam Yezu, sɛba en dagi kɔm-wureba, bii tube-kpirehi.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Wuu eŋa en de Yinɛ lageŋɔ nɛŋadaana la, se'em en biheri Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, a tara zɛregerɛ zi'an. A da dɛna nɛre wurebere, bii sunsuya daana, bii a koohe a mɛŋa bo' daam, bii gbiregeri daana, bii a ba' nɔŋɛ ligeri.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: Gee a dɛna se'em en to'oheri nɛreba a yire, nɔŋɛ itesuŋɔ, yɛm daana, dɛna tuuntɔɔ ka tara dɛgerɔ, tana a mɛŋa tara.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: A dela a gurɛ yelehum la en dole pa'alegɔ la, ti la bahɛ ti a tana pa'ala pa'ali seko en de ma'a la, gee tana valegera sɛba en ka pa'ali dɔla la.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: Ba zo'e mɛ dɛna tube-kpirega duma, tɔgɛ fai duma, la pumpã'ahum duma, yele yele wuu sɛba en ŋma la puan.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Ba dela ba ŋmɛkɛ ba nɔya, la ban gbiregeri nɛreba yiya la ba pa'alegɔ la, doohe ban pa'ali pa'alegɔ seko en bo'ori bamam ma'a nyuurɔ, gee dagi na sɛla en dagena ba pa'ala.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: La yuum dela ba ayima, ba mɛŋa nɔtɔgeha en yuum yeti, Kereteduma dela pumparenduma saŋa woo, ana wuu muuen dumbɛ'ɛhi, zinyagehi, gee dɛna punya'anduma.

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Ban tɔgɛ sɛla la dela asira. Dina zuo la, tahɛ ba la ninmu'urɛ, ti ba lebe bɔna sakerɛ la puan suŋa suŋa.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: Da kɛleha Yuuduma yetɔge yooro la, bii tɔ'ɔha sɛba en zagehɛ yelemɛŋerɛ nɔze'elego.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Sɛba suya en de tuuntɔɔ la, sɛla woo dela tuuntɔɔ bɔ'ɔra ba, sɛba en ŋmɛleni gee ka nam Yezu la, sɛla ka boi dɛna tuuntɔɔ bo' ba. Ba mɛhi puti'ira la ba zuto ki mɛ.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Ba iŋɛ ba mɛhi ti bamam mi' Yinɛ, gee ba zagehɛ en mɛ la ba itegɔ la. Ba dela nɛre fi'iha, tube-kpirega duma, ka nara tuunsunɛ se'ere woo puan.

eng: But you should talk in a way that is consistent with sound teaching.
local: Gee han dɛna fum, pa'ala sɛla en dole la ma'a pa'alegɔ la.

eng: Tell the older men to be sober, dignified, sensible, and healthy in respect to their faith, love, and patience.
local: Yele buraa kureba la ti, ba da dɛna susuya duma, gee ba dɛna ninmu'urɛ duma, yɛm duma, kpe'em sakerɛ puan, nɔŋerɛ la ŋmibega puan.

eng: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in their behavior, teaching what is good, rather than being gossips or addicted to heavy drinking.
local: Bala mɛa, yele pɔge kureba la ti ba tara na'ahegɔ ba vom puan, ba da dɛna sɛba en sageni nɛreba yu'ura, bii da'aba bo' daam-nyuure. Ba pa'ala sɛla en ani suŋa.

eng: That way they can mentor young women to love their husbands and children.
local: ti la nyaŋɛ kpemehe pɔge paalehi la ti ba nɔŋɛ ba sireba, nɔŋɛ ba kɔma.

eng: and to be sensible, morally pure, working at home, kind and submissive to their own husbands, so that Gods word wont be ridiculed.
local: ba tana ba mɛhi tara, nyɔkera ba mɛhi, tana bihera ba yiya, tara itesuŋɔ, sigeha ba mɛhi bɔ'ɔra ba sireba, ti Yinɛ yelehum la da nyɛ zɛregerɛ.

eng: Likewise, encourage the younger men to be sensible.
local: Bala mɛa, pa'alɛ bura paalehi la ti ba nyɔkera ba mɛhi.

eng: in every way. Offer yourself as a role model of good actions. Show integrity, seriousness.
local: Bahɛ ti naŋa bɔna fum puan zi'an woo, ti fum dɛna tuunsuma daana ti ba wan tɔgehɛ, fum pa'alegɔ puan, pa'ala la yɛm la paŋa.

eng: and a sound message that is above criticism when you teach, so that any opponent will be ashamed because they wont find anything bad to say about us.
local: la yɛm yetɔge sebo en ka tari sokere, ti se'em woo en ka sakɛ la, vi wan di en ti a kan nyɛ be'em sebo tɔgɛ tɔ.

eng: Tell slaves to submit to their own masters and please them in everything they do. They shouldnt talk back.
local: Yele da'aba ti ba sigehe ba mɛhi bo' ba kpɛ'ɛnduma gee bɔ'ɔra ba na'ahegɔ en seke. Ba da lɛbeha ba kpɛ'ɛnduma la.

eng: or steal. Instead, they should show that they are completely reliable in everything so that they might make the teaching about God our savior attractive in every way.
local: ba da zuura, gee ba pa'alɛ ti ba dela nɛresuma suŋa suŋa, bala ti sɛla woo puan, ba wan ana wuu nunɔm zɔtɔ la bo' ma'a pa'alegɔ la en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Faara zi'an na la.

eng: The grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la wa'am na tari la faarɛ na bo' nɛra woo.

eng: It educates us so that we can live sensible, ethical, and godly lives right now by rejecting ungodly lives and the desires of this world.
local: pa'ala tɔ ti tu bahɛ be'em woo, la tiŋa wa zuo nyɛlum yooro, la saŋa kana wa, tu vɔna vom sebo en de fum tana n mɛŋa tara, tuntɔɔnɛ la Yinɛ zoolum.

eng: At the same time we wait for the blessed hope and the glorious appearance of our great God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: tumam n guri beere putɛ'sunɛ wa, la Yinɛ kãtɛ wa, la tu Faara Yezu Kiresta niirɛ na la na'ahegɔ la.

eng: He gave himself for us in order to rescue us from every kind of lawless behavior, and cleanse a special people for himself who are eager to do good actions.
local: Eŋa en kaabɛ a mɛŋa bo' tu, ti a yɔ to'e tu tuure woo puan, gee pee tu bo' a mɛŋa, ti tu dɛna a mɛŋa nɛreba, sɛba en mu'ɛ nini tuna tuunsunɛ.

eng: Talk about these things. Encourage and correct with complete authority. Dont let anyone disrespect you.
local: Muula yele ana wa, kpɛmeha, gee tahera nɛra woo la paŋa. Da bahɛ ti se'em bihɛ zeem fum.

eng: Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities. They should be obedient and ready to do every good thing.
local: Tiihɛ ba ti ba sigehe ba mɛhi bo' nɛhi duma, la kpɛ'ɛnduma, ba nana ba. Ba maahum ba mɛhi bo' tunsunɛ woo.

eng: They shouldnt speak disrespectfully about anyone, but they should be peaceful, kind, and show complete courtesy toward everyone.
local: ba da tɔgera be'em bɔ'ɔra nɛra, ba bahɛ nɔkpɛ'ɛma, ba ana ma'ma, gee tara nɔsunɛ la nɛrawoo.

eng: We were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, and slaves to our desires and various pleasures too. We were spending our lives in evil behavior and jealousy. We were disgusting, and we hated other people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam mɛhi tabelɛ dɛna la geto, ka nana, bɔregɛ, dɛna da'aba bo' kɔrehi la nyɛlum yima yima, dikɛ tu dabeha tum pa'ahegɔ tuuma, nintɛrega, zɛregera nɛreba, yaaɛ taaba.

eng: But when God our saviors kindness and love appeared.
local: Gee Yinɛ se'em en de tu Faara la itesuŋɔ la a nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ la en niiɛ paaɛ nɛra woo la.

eng: he saved us because of his mercy, not because of righteous things we had done. He did it through the washing of new birth and the renewing by the Holy Spirit.
local: a faaɛ tu mɛ, dagi la tu tuntɔɔnɛ tuuma ti tu tum zuo, gee a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la, doohe ko'om dɔgum paalemɔ la, la teere se'ere ti Fa'a Sia la tee tu la.

eng: which God poured out upon us generously through Jesus Christ our savior.
local: Sɛkana wa ti a ka'ɛ bahɛ tu zuo zo'e zo'e doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Faara la puan.

eng: So, since we have been made righteous by his grace, we can inherit the hope for eternal life.
local: ti, en bo' tu buurɔ a zu'uhegɔ la zuo la, tu beere wan sɔna vom kan ba'ahɛ la ti tu tara bu puti'irɛ la.

eng: This saying is reliable. And I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God might give careful attention to doing good. These things are good and useful for everyone.
local: Yetɔge buna wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ. Mam suure boti fum tɔgera yele ana wa suŋa, ti sɛba en wa'am na nam Yinɛ la wan kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' tuunsuma. Yele ana wa dela ma'a, gee tara nyuurɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo.

eng: Avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, and fights about the Law, because they are useless and worthless.
local: Gee yehe ya mɛhi nɔkpe'en yooro, yaabeduma yɛla, welehego, la nɔze'elego la zaberɛ yɛla, ba ka tari nyuurɔ, gee me ka tara vuurɛ.

eng: After a first and second warning, have nothing more to do with a person who causes conflict.
local: Fum han diŋɛ ka'am nɛra yia, le pa'ahɛ yi'i, da le tara sɛla ita la se'em en welegere nɛreba.

eng: because you know that someone like this is twisted and sinful—so they condemn themselves.
local: fum diŋɛ mina ti nɛre-ina taaba ka boti yelemɛŋerɛ, dɛna be'em daana, pun sagum a mɛŋa.

eng: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, try to come to me in Nicopolis, because Ive decided to spend the winter there.
local: N han tum Atemas bii Taikikuse fum zi'an na, kpeŋe fum mɛŋa gee wa'am n zi'an Nikopolisi tiŋa na, se'ere en sɔi la, n iŋɛ n puti'irɛ ti n bɔna la bilam ɔɔrɔ saŋa la.

eng: Help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with enthusiasm so that they wont need anything.
local: Kpeŋe fum mɛŋa la fum tum Zenase en de yetɔge tɔgera, la Apolos na. Gee la fum kpeŋe fum mɛŋa ti sɛla da koŋe ba ba sore kinɛ la puan.

eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: Gee bahɛ ti nɛreba la zamehɛ ba kaabɛ ba mɛhi bo' tuunsuma, la ba ta'am suŋera yelesɛ'a en ni di'e na la, ti ba da bahɛ yoo.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: Sɛba woo en boi la mam la pu'uheri ya na. Pu'uhi sɛba woo en nɔŋɛ tɔ sakerɛ la puan la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Pɔɔl, se'em ti ba page en Kiresta Yezu zuo, la Timoti tu suuɔ. N guleheri bɔ'ɔra la Filemɔn tu zɔ ti tu suure nɔŋɛ, la tu tuntuna tadaana.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: n kelum guleha bɔ'ɔra la Afiya, tutã la Arekipus en de tu pɛntiira tadaana, la lageŋɔ seko en boi fum yire la.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ tu Sɔ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta zi'an na.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: Mam han ta tiɛ fum yele n pu'uhegɔ puan, mam ni bɔ'ɔra la Yinɛ fara saŋa woo.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, mam wum fum nɔŋɛ Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba la woo se'em, la fum sakerɛ Daana Yezu puan.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: Mam pu'uheri mɛ ti fum wan tara fum sakerɛ la tuna, fum han bɔkɛ tuunsuma sɛ'a za'a ti tu tara Kiresta puan la.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ la kpemehego seko en ze'ele fum nɔŋerɛ puan, beni zuo, tuulum nɛreba la suya malum gã'a la timpaberɛ fum puan, n suuɔ.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Dina wa zuo, la mam n pun tara paŋa Kiresta puan, ti n bo' fum nuurɛ ti fum tum fum tuunɛ.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: gee mam wan tugum suhɛ fum la nɔŋerɛ puan, se'ere en sɔi la mam Pɔɔl nyaa kuregɛ mɛ, gee me le bɔna paka deen Kiresta Yezu zuo.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: Mam sɔheri fum mɛ n bia Onesimuse zuo, mam dela a Sɔ n paka saŋa la.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: A daa ka tari vuurɛ bo' fum, gee nananawa, a tari vuurɛ bo' fum, ta ta' la mam me.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: N dikeri en bɔ'ɔra fum me na, la en pun dɛna mam suure bia la, en dikeri en bɔ'ɔra fum me na.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: Mam boti ni ti a boni mɛ la mam, la a sooe ni fum na'arɛ tuna bɔ'ɔra mam ban daa pagɛ mam faarɛ yelehum wa zuo la;.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: gee n ka boti n iŋɛ sɛla gee ti fum ka mina, ti fum tuunsuma la wan dɛna la fum suure en boti gee dagi sɛla en de pɛregerɛ.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: Daanse'ere bala en sɔi ti a daa yehe fum zi'an saŋa fii wa, gee a wan lebe na bɔna la hɔ wuuha.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: a han lebe na a kan le dɛna da'aberɛ, a gana da'aberɛ mɛ, a dela sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ bu — yele yele mam, gee la wan gana bala fum zi'an, inganɛ puan, la Daana la me puan.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Bala la, fum han nam ti tu lagum tuna mɛ, to'e en wuu fum wan to'e ni mam se'em la.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: A han tuue fum se'ere puan, bii a tari la fum sanɛ, bahɛ ti la dɛna mam sanɛ.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Mam Pɔɔl mɛŋa n gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa la n nu'o. Mam wan lebehe yɔ dɛ. Mam ka yele se'ere la fum mɛŋa en tari mam sanɛ la pun ta ta' fum mɛa.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Ɛ̃ɛ n suuɔ, bahɛ ti n nyɛ nyuurɔ dina fum puan Daana la zuo! Bahɛ ti n suure gã'a timpaberɛ Kiresta puan.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: Mam mi' suŋa suŋa ti fum wan nam mam guleheri bɔ'ɔra fum wa, mam mi' ti, fum wan pun iŋɛ gaŋɛ mam en yele fum sɛla wa.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Yele Yinɛ en gee — maahum saama bɔ'ɔ bo' mam, mam tari puti'irɛ ti, fum pu'uhegɔ puan n wan bɔna la hɔ.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: Epafarase, se'em ti ba lagum pagɛ en la mam Kiresta Yezu zuo la, pu'uhi fum na.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: bala mɛa ti Maki, Aristakuse, Demase la Luki, n tuntuna tadaanduma la me pu'uhi fum na.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Daana Yezu Kiresta zu'uhegɔ bɔna la fum sia.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Diimen diimen ha la, Yinɛ da tɔgɛ bo' tu yaabeduma mɛ saŋa zo'e zo'e, la sadɔtɔ zo'e zo'e, dɔla nɔtɔgeheba puan.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: gee ba'ahegɔ dabeha wa puan, a tɔgɛ bo' tu doohe la a dayua puan, sɛka ti a looe ti ka sooe sɔna sɛla woo, se'em puan ti Yinɛ doohe naam sɛla woo en boi wa.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: A dela Yinɛ suŋa niihum paaɛ paaɛ, dɛna Yinɛ vore vore, bahɛ ti sɛla woo bɔna, doohe a yelehum paŋa puan. Eŋa en duuhe saaleba be'em ba'ahɛ la, a zi'ire la Gani za'a Daana la zuɔ nu'o saazuin ha.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: A gani suŋereba mɛ, wuu a yu'urɛ se'ere ti a to'e la en de yu'kãtɛ gana suŋereba yu'ura se'em la.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Suŋera kani ti Yinɛ tabelɛ yele en yeti, Fum dela mam Dayua, zina, mam dɔgɛ hɔ? Bii a tabelɛ yeti, N wan dɛna a sɔ, ti a dɛna n Dayua?.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, Yinɛ en yeti a ta bahɛ ti a dayɔkiima wa wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na la, a tɔgɛ bala yeti, Bahɛ ti Yinɛ suŋereba la za'a na'aha dayua wa.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Yinɛ tɔgɛ a suŋereba yele yeti, A iŋɛ a suŋereba ti ba dɛna la sabeeo, gee iŋɛ a yamehi ti ba dɛna bugum zɛlehi.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Gee a dayua la, Yinɛ tɔgɛ eŋa yele yeti, Fum na'am ka tari ba'ahegɔ, Oo Yinɛ wa, ti fum na'am doore la dɛna tuuntɔɔ doore.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: Fum nɔŋɛ la tuntɔɔnɛ, gee yaaɛ be'em. Bala zuo, Yinɛ wa, fum Yinɛ la so fum zuo mɛ la nyɛɛma kaam gana fum taaba.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Gee le yeti, Pi'ileŋo puan na la, Daana, fum n biŋe timpaberɛ wa ɛ̃berɛ, ti saazuo wa dɛna fum nu'uhi tuuma;.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: gee ba woo wan mɛlum gee fum boi mɛ. Ba woo wan ki ŋwana nuga wuu tanɛ la;.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: wuu fuo ti ba ni kilum se'em la, fum wan kilum ba la bala, wuu futo en pelegere se'em la, bala mɛa ti ba wan tee. Gee fum kɔ'ɔm ana la fum ani se'em la, ti fum yuuma ka tara ba'ahegɔ.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Gee suŋera kani ti Yinɛ tabelɛ yele en yeti, Zi'ire n zuɔ nu'o na, gura ti n ta bahɛ ti fum dinduma lebege fum nama dafeere.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Suŋereba la za'a dagi la sabeeo Sia tuuma la puan, ti Yinɛ tuna ba ti ba tuna bɔ'ɔra sɛba en wan ta sooe vaaɛ faarɛ la?.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Bala, tu dela tu iŋɛ tube wuŋa la sɛla ti tu wum la, la tu da bɔregɛ yehe de puan.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Ti, yelehum sebo en yuum muulɛ doohe suŋereba puan la yuum han tara tibehum, ti bu tuure, la tube-kpirega woo tɔ'ɔra namehegɔ kãtɛ.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: ŋwani ti tu wan ta'am piiɛ, tu han zagehɛ faarɛ kãtɛ dina wa? Tu wum bu yele yia la Daana la mɛŋa zi'an, ti sɛba en wum bu a mɛŋa nuuren la me yele tu ti bu dela bala.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: ti Yinɛ mɛŋa en de aze'nyɛ daana la ba, la nyalema la kirehi, la paŋa tuuma yima yima, doohe Fa'a Sia la bo'olum la ti a tɔrɛ bo' doohe a mɛŋa sumboolum la.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Lan de se'em la dela,Yinɛ ka dikɛ tinsɛka en ye' na, ti tu tɔgera ka yele wa, iŋɛ suŋereba la nu'uhen.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Gee nɛra ayima en tɔgɛ gɔŋɔ la puan zi'an kayima yeti, Asaala dela beni ti fum ba' tɛra ba yele bala, Bii a dela ani me, ti fum ba' tara ba bala?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: Fum iŋɛ en ti saŋa fii wa, a da zuna la suŋereba. Fum vugele en la suŋa, la na'ahegɔ muŋa.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe a nama tiŋa. Yinɛ en dikɛ sɛla woo biŋe a nama tiŋa la, a ka gee sɛla bahɛ ti la ka bɔna a nu'uhen. Gee lan de se'em la dela, tu nan ka nyɛ ti sɛla woo bɔna a nu'uhen.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: gee tu nyɛ Yezu, se'em ti saŋa fii wa, Yinɛ iŋɛ en ti a bɔna suŋereba la tiŋa, gee nananawa nyaa vuge suŋa la na'ahegɔ muŋa doohe a namehegɔ kum la zuo, ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la zuo,Yezu wan lem kum bo' nɛra woo.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: La daa dela ma'a, ti Yinɛ se'em en sɔi, la se'em puan ti sɛla woo bɔna la, en boti a tari nɛreba zo'e zo'e wa'am suŋa puan na la zuo, a bahɛ ti se'em en de ba faarɛ nɛŋa daana la bi'ihɛ a mɛŋa kahegɛ namehegɔ puan.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Beni zuo, se'em en peere la, la sɛba be'em ti a pee la, za'a ze'ele la zi'an kayina na. Beni zuo, a ka tari valum, ti a yi' ba a suurɔ.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: bala ti a yeti, Mam wan muulɛ fum yu'urɛ bo' n suurɔ la, Lageŋɔ la puan, mam wan pɛgɛ hɔ.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, a yeti, Mam wan delum la eŋa. A me le yeti, Mam en ŋwana, la kɔma sɛba ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' mam la.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Lan yi de ti, kɔma la za'a dela inganɛ la ziim la, eŋa Yezu mɛŋa me wa'am na ana mɛ wuu bamam la, ti a ta'am doohe kum puan sagum se'em en tari kum paŋa la. Sagena la en bala.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: gee faaɛ sɛba woo vom saŋa woo en boi yamenɛ puan doohe ban zoti kum la zuo.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: La kɔ'ɔm niiɛ mɛ gã'a, ti Yezu ka wa'am na ti a suŋɛ la suŋereba, gee a tugum wa'am na ti a suŋɛ la Aberaham yaahi.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Bala zuo,Yezu dela a lebege ana wuu a suurɔ la sɛla woo puan, la a dɛna nimbɔ'zɔta, la sakerɛ malema kiima, Yinɛ tuuma la puan, la a kaabera be'em duuhego kaabegɔ bɔ'ɔra nɛreba la.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Beni zuo, eŋa mɛŋa nyɛ la makerɛ, a namehegɔ la puan, a wan ta'am suŋɛ sɛba woo en nyɛti makerɛ la.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: Bala zuo n suurɔ, n tuulum nɛre tadaanduma Yinɛ saazuo yi'a wa puan wa, bihɛ ya suŋa suŋa Yezu en de tuntuna, gee dɛna malema kiima bo' tumam sakerɛ yelehum la wa la.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: a daa tari sakerɛ mɛ la se'em en looe en la, wuu Moose en yuum de sakerɛ daana la pu'uhegɔ yire tuuma la za'a se'em la.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Gee la bala za'a, Yezu tari zuo la suŋa gana Moose mɛ, wuu yire daana en tari zuo gana yire la mɛŋa se'em la.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Yi bihɛ, Moose yuum dela sakerɛ daana Lageŋɔ tuuma la za'a, dɛna de yameŋa, tɔgera yele sɛ'a ti ba wan tɔgɛ beere.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Gee Kiresta en de se'em la, yuum tari la sakerɛ la Yinɛ lageŋɔ la, dɛna dayua, ti tumam dɛna yire la, tu han gurɛ tu inkpeŋere wa la tu nyu'ɔ ŋma'a sɛka en de tu beere puti'irɛ zuo wa kankaŋi.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Bala zuo, wuu Fa'a Sia la en yele se'em ti, Zina, ya han wum Yinɛ kua.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: da kãbɛ ya suya wuu ya yaabeduma pobiriga la, wuu ban yuum boi makerɛ dabeherɛ puan kpaahenɛ puan ha la.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: zi'an ti ya yaabeduma la yuum makɛ mam bihɛ, la bamam en pun nyɛ mam tum se'em, yuuma pihinaahi la.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Bala ti mam yuum iŋɛ suure la sankaŋa buuri la, yele ba yeti, bamam saŋa woo kɔ'ɔm bɔregera mɛ ba suya puan, ba ka makɛ mina mam sore la.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Mam sunsunyiim la puan, mam pɔ mɛ yeti, ba kan kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan.

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa n suurɔ, ti ya se'em da tara be'em suure, suse'ere en ka nane, gee ŋmɛregera de mɛŋa yɛha Yinɛ vua wa zi'an.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Gee tugum kpemehe ya taaba daare woo wuu ban yi'iri se'em ti zina wa la, ti ya se'em da bɔna ti be'em pã'ahɛ en ti a kãbɛ a suure.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam nyaa dela taaba la Kiresta, tu han gurɛ tu yia sakerɛ la kankaŋi ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Wuu lan yele se'em yeti, Zina ya han wum Yinɛ kua, da kãbɛ ya suya wuu ya yaabeduma pobiriga puan la.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Nananawa, bani en bala yuum wum gee bire pɔya la? Dagi sɛba woo en yuum bahɛ Igipiti ti Moose tari ba yehe na la?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Gee bani ti Yinɛ iŋɛ suure la ba yuuma pihinaahi la? Dagi sɛba en yuum tum be'em la, sɛba en yuum kiiri muɔ la puan la?.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Gee bani ti Yinɛ yuum pɔ nuurɛ ti ba kan kɛ̃ eŋa vo'ohego la puan la, han dagena sɛba en yuum ka nane la?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Bala, tu nyɛ ya ti ba yuum ka nyaŋɛ kɛ̃, doohe la ban ka nam la zuo.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Bala zuo, vo'ohego puan kɛ̃'a nɔbiŋere la en kelum bɔna la, bahɛ ya ti tu biha tu mɛhi suŋa, ti ya se'em da ta yaa koŋe a paaɛ vo'ohego la.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Asira, faarɛ yelehum la wa'am tumam zi'an na wuu bumɔ en yuum wa'am bamam zi'an se'em na la. Gee yelehum la ti bamam yuum wum la, yuum ka bo' ba nyuurɔ, beni zuo, ba yuum ka naaɛ taaba ba sakerɛ puan, la sɛba en yuum kelehe yelehum la.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tumam sɛba en sakɛ la, kɛ̃ vo'ohego la puan mɛ wuu Yinɛ en yele se'em yeti, Wuu mam sunsunyiim puan ti n pɔ yeti, Ba kan makɛ kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan la, la eŋa en pun tum sɛla woo ba'ahɛ tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan ha la.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Zi'an kayima gɔŋɔ la puan, la tɔgɛ dabeha ayopɔi la yele yeti, Ti Yinɛ vo'ohe dabeha ayopɔi daarɛ la, a tuuma la woo puan.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, ze'kana mɛa puan yeti, Ba kan kɛ̃ mam vo'ohego la puan.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Bala vo'ohego la en kelum yu'ɛ bo' basɛba ti ba kɛ̃ la, ti sɛba en yuum pɔhɛ to'e faarɛ yelehum wa yia la koŋe ba kɛ̃ mɛ ban ka nane la zuo.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: bala zuo ti a le yehe la dabeherɛ ze'ele, yi'ira dɛ Zina, yuum tɔgɛ de yele doohe Deviti puan, pooren pooren na Yetɔge sebo en yuum pun tɔgɛ gã'a yeti, Zina, ya han wum Yinɛ kua, Da kãbɛ ya suya.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yosua yuum han bo' ni nɛreba wa la vo'ohego, Yinɛ kan le tɔgeri ni pooren, dabeherɛ deyima yele.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Bala, vo'ohego dabeherɛ kelum bɔna mɛ bo' Yinɛ nɛreba.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Beni zuo, Sɛba en kɛ̃ Yinɛ vo'ohego la puan la, me ni vo'ohe ba tuuma puan mɛ wuu Yinɛ en vo'ohe se'em a tuuma za'a puan la.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Bala, bahɛ ya ti tu tiregɛ ya suŋa suŋa la tu ta'am kɛ̃ vo'ohego wa puan, ti se'em da bɔna ya puan lu doohe ka nam wuu bamam en yuum ka nam se'em la.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, Yinɛ yelehum la vɔi mɛ gee tuna, dita gana su'a en tari nɔya ayi' la, ni ŋmaaɛ vo'ohum la ta paaɛ sia la, la inganɛ zi'an woo, ta ta' la kɔba, la a tomehego zi'ihi woo la sɛla en boi kuba la puan ha za'a. Yelehum la mi' suure puan ti'ihegɔ woo mɛ la de maaheŋɔ woo.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: A nɛŋan, sɛla ka boi sugɛ, gee sɛla woo ze' la beele, gã'a kpaii, se'em daana ti tu wan ta yele en tu yɛla za'a la ninin.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Lan de ti tu tari la malema kiima kãtɛ en kɛ̃ tinsuŋan la, dɛna Yezu, Yinɛ dayua la, bahɛ ya ti tu gurɛ tu sakerɛ yelehum la kankaŋi.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu ka tari la malema kiima sɛka en ka zoti tu tɔregɔ nimbu'ɔ, gee tu tari la se'em en nyɛ mageha sadɔseko woo puan wuu tumam la, gee a ka tum be'em.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu paaɛ ya Yinɛ na'am kuka la nɛŋan ti zu'uhegɔ bɔna la, la sukpe'ene, la tu ta'am to'e a nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la, nyɛ zu'uhegɔ, ti la suŋɛ tɔ sansɛka woo n nari.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Malemaduma kiima woo ti ba looe en saaleba puan, ba ni bahɛ ti a sɔna la yelesɛ'a en de Yinɛ yɛla biheri bɔ'ɔra saaleba. A kaabera bo'olum, gee kaabera be'em zuo.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: A dela nɛre ma'ma', ana bããlam gee maala ba yɛmka'alum, la ban kini buta la, eŋa mɛŋa en boi tɔregɔ nu'uhen la zuo.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Ti ŋwana zuo ti la dɛna a ni kaabɛ a mɛŋa be'em zuo yia, gee nyaa kaabɛ bo' nɛreba la me.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Nɛra ka dikeri na'ahegɔ kuna bɔ'ɔra a mɛŋa, gee a ni to'e de mɛ Yinɛ han yi' en wuu Aron ti a yi' se'em la.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Bala mɛa ti Kiresta yuum ka dikɛ na'ahegɔ kuna bo' a mɛŋa ti a dɛna malema kiima, gee se'em en looe en la en yeti, Fum dela mam Dayua, Zina mam de fum sɔ;.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: wuu en le tɔgɛ gɔŋɔ la zi'an kayima se'em yeti, Fum dela malema daana kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a ha, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Yezu en yuum boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, a inganɛ puan la, a yuum dikeri la pu'uhegɔ, la suhega la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa la nintam bɔ'ɔra se'em daana en wan ta'am faaɛ en kum puan bahɛ la, ti a ni kelehe en, a Yinɛ zoolum la zuo.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: La en pun dɛna Dayua la, a zamehɛ naŋa mɛ, en namehɛ sɛla woo puan.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: Ti en doohe ŋwana woo puan tole la, a nyaa bi'ɛ kahegɛ mɛ, a nyaa dela se'em puan ti vomkan ba'ahɛ faarɛ bɔna bo' sɛba woo en nane en la.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Ŋwana ti Yinɛ en yehe en ti a dɛna malema kiima doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Yele deni wa puan, tu tari la yɛla zo'e zo'e en wan yele, gee a pa'alegɔ en tɔi, yan ka bɔkerɛ kalam kalam la zuo.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: La sankana ti ya deni ya dɛna pa'aleba la, ya kelum bɔta la nɛra le pa'alɛ ya pɔhega yele sɛ'a en boi Yinɛ yelehum la puan. Ya boti la bi'ihum gee dagi di kpe'ene.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en mɔgeri bi'ihum kelum dɛna la bilia, ka mina tuuma tuuntɔɔ vom yɛla puan.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Gee dikpe'ene dela sɛba en bi'ɛ kahegɛ bunɔ, sɛba en nyɛ kpemehego tuuma puan, tana wɛleha be'em la sum la.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Bala zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ nɛŋa bi'ihɛ suŋa suŋa, tu bahɛ pi'ileŋo pa'alegɔ ti ba pun pa'alɛ tu yia Kiresta puan pooren, da le bahɛ ya ti tu le tagehɛ ɛberɛ iibegɔ, la en de tu tee kum tuuma, tu tara sakerɛ Yinɛ puan.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: ko'omsua yɛla, nu'uhi pagelegɔ yɛla, kum vo'ore yɛla, la ba'ahegɔ gakerɛ yɛla.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Tumam wan iŋɛ sɛla en bala, Yinɛ han bo' tu sore.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la kpe'em mɛ ti ba le maahum nɛrebana wa ti ba le tee. Bama sɛba en tabelɛ nyɛ niihum, tabelɛ lem saazuo bo'olum la, tabelɛ tɔrɛ Fa'a Sia la.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: Bama sɛba en tabelɛ nyɛ sum sebo en boi Yinɛ yelehum puan la, gee me mina Yinɛ paŋa n wan ta ana se'em saŋa en kini na.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: gee han sina gee lu, ba mɛhi en boti se'em, ba malum kpa'ara la Yinɛ dayua la en bala, malum diihera en nyanɛ nɛrekuuŋɔ nɛŋan.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Samanɛ se'ere en nyuuri saa ko'om en niira baha de zuo saŋa woo la, gee maala disuŋa bɔ'ɔra sɛba en kɔɔri de la, to'ore la maalegɔ Yinɛ zi'an.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Gee samanɛ la han yɛhera gu'uhi la tuntuuro, de ka tari vuurɛ, ka zaaɛ ba pɔ bahɛ de zuo, ti de ba'ahegɔ ta dɛna la ba nyɔ' dɛ.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: La tumam en pun tɔgera ŋwana wa la, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, tu mi' suŋa suŋa ti yelesuma en gani ŋwana boi ya puan mɛ. Yelesuma sɛ'a en de faarɛ yɛla.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Yinɛ dagi se'em n ka bo'ori buurɔ. A kan mum a nini la ya tuuma la, la ya nɔŋerɛ la ti ya pa'alɛ a yu'urɛ zuo la, doohe la yan bihɛ nɛresunduma la se'em, wuu yan kelum ita se'em wa la.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Tu boti ya ayima ayima woo me pa'alɛ la ninmu'urɛ dinɛ taaba, la ya ta'am nyɛ beere puti'irɛ za'a za'a, ta paaɛ de ba'ahegɔ.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Bala ti se'em kan bukɛ, gee ya wan tɔgehɛ sɛba woo en doohe sakerɛ, la ŋmibega puan ta to'e Yinɛ en biŋe nuurɛ ti a iŋɛ se'ere la.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Yinɛ en yuum biŋe nuurɛ la Aberaham la, en yuum ka tari se'em en gani eŋa mɛŋa ti a wan pɔ a daana la, a yuum pɔ la a mɛŋa.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, n wan iŋɛ fum sum, n wan bahɛ ti fum wi'ɛ.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Bala ti Aberaham me yuum tari sugeri ŋmibera la, a yuum sirum ta to'e nɔbiŋere la mɛ.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Saaleba ni pɔ la se'em n gani ba. Pɔrɛ han diŋɛ pɔ, ti ba dikɛ sɛla taaɛ pɔtɛ la, nɔkpe'ene woo ba'ahɛ ya.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Bala mɛa ti Yinɛ en daa ta bɔta ti a pa'alɛ ti la niiɛ bo' sɛba en wan ta sooe sɔna a nɔbiŋere la en de a naanɛ se'ere en ka malum tɛɛra la, a pɔ la pɔtɛ ti de dɛna daalehum.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: ti la doohe yele sɛ'a ayi' en ka teere puan, yele sɛ'a puan ti deyima dɛna Yinɛ kan ta'am parum la, tumam sɛba en sugɛ a zi'an la wan nyɛ inkpeŋere kankaŋi ta'am to'e putɛ'se'ere ti a dikɛ biŋe tu la.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tu tari la putɛ'dina, ti de ana wuu ɛberɛ kãtɛ, la tiŋasuka nyuuŋa la bo' tumam sɛɛhi. Beere puti'irɛ se'ere en kɛ̃ saraa la puan ha, tama la en lu yula la pooren ha la.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: zi'an ti Yezu dɛna nɛŋa daana bini tumam zuo, kɛ̃ bini, dɛna malema kiima kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a ha, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
local: Naba Mɛlekizɛdɛki wa n yehe Salem na wa, dɛna Gani za'a Yinɛ malema daana la, wa tu'uhɛ Aberaham ti a ze'ele la en kiŋɛ ta zabɛ nyaŋɛ naduma gee lɛbera na la, ti a purɛ sum bahɛ a zuo.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Ti Aberaham yehe lɔgeseto woo ti a nyɛ la puan, Seko woo en pa'aheri pia la bo' en. Yia, a yu'urɛ la vuurɛ dela, Tuntɔɔ naba Buyi', a dela Salem tiŋa naba, ti de vuurɛ dɛna la, Suma'ahum naba.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: A ka tari sɔ, a ka tari ma, a ka tari buuri tuuhum. A ka tari pi'ileŋo dabeha, bii vom ba'ahegɔ dabeha. Gee a ani wuu Yinɛ Dayua la, dɛna malema daana yɛ'a ha.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Bihɛ ya en de nɛre kãtɛ se'em. Ti Aberaham n pun dɛna yaaba la, ta bo' en pia pia bo'olum a lɔgerɔ la woo puan.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Sɛba en de Levi buuri, tuna malema tuuma la, Yinɛ bo' ba la nɔze'elego, ti ba tɔ'ɔha pia pia bo'olum nɛreba la zi'an, ba suurɔ la zi'an, la bamam en pun dɛna Aberaham yaahi la.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Gee buraa kana wa en dagi yaaŋa wa, to'e pia pia bo'olum mɛ Aberaham zi'an, gee purɛ sum bahɛ Aberaham, se'em en pun to'e nɔbiŋere.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Nɔkpe'ene ka boi bini, tarema en to'ore sum kpɛ'ɛma zi'an.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Yele wa zi'an kayima dela, saaleba en kiiri to'ohe pia pia bo'olum, gee zi'an kayima me, se'em ti ba mina ti a pun bɔna mɛ na la me to'ohe pia pia bo'olum la mɛ.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Se'em wan ta'am pun yeti, Levi mɛŋa la en yuum to'ohe pia bo'olum la, me yuum bo'ori pia bo'olum mɛ a yaaba Aberaham puan na.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum boi a yaaba Aberaham iŋa puan mɛ, gee ti Mɛlekizɛdɛki tu'uhɛ en la.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Han dɛna ti vom sebo en bi'ɛ kahegɛ han daa bɔna nɛreba la puan doohe la Levi buuri malema duma tuuma la, beni ti la le nari ni ti ba le tɔgera malema daana ayima yele, se'em en doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la, gee ka tɔgera ti a doohe la Aron?.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: Teere han wa'am malema duma la puan na, teere me wan ba'ahum wa'am nɔze'elego la me puan na.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Se'em daana zuo ti ba tɔgera yele ana wa la yehe la buuri deyima me puan na, buuri se'ere nɛra en ka tabelɛ tum kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: La niiɛ mɛ gã'a ti tu Daana la yehe la Yuda buuri puan na, buuri dina wa puan, Moose ka tɔgɛ malema duma yele de puan.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: La pun kɔ'ɔm ba' niiɛ mɛ gã'a, ti malema daana ayima han ihege na doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki la.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: eŋa daana la en lebege malema daana la dagi ze'elego en bu en sore bii saala buuri tuuhum, gee a la doohe la vom sebo n ka sageni la paŋa puan.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba purɛ a yele mɛ na yeti, Fum dela malema daana ka tara ba'ahegɔ, doohe Mɛlekizɛdɛki.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Zi'an kayima me yeti, teere wan bɔna yia nɔze'elego la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ku daa ka tari paŋa, gee me ka tuna.

eng: . On the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
local: . Zi'an kayima me pa'ali ti, beere putɛ'sunɛ se'ere boi gana ŋwana, ti tu wan dɔla pu'uha Yinɛ.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Pɔrɛ en daa pɔ pa'alɛ ti dina wa wan tum, se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba woo en de malema duma, ni ka pɔ gee tuna.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: gee malema daana ena wa la, pɔ mɛ gee lebege malema daana, doohe se'em daana en yele en yeti, Daana la pɔ mɛ, gee kan tee a puti'irɛ, ti Fum dela malema daana ka tara ba'ahegɔ; .

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: lan de se'em la, Yezu ze'ele bo' la nɔpu'ulegɔ n gani bala.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Se'ere en le pa'ahɛ dela, diimen diimen malema duma la yuum zo'e mɛ ba kãlɛ puan beni zuo, kum en yuum gu'uri ba ti ba ka ta'am bɔna tuunɛ la puan wuuha.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Gee malema daana ena kɔ'ɔm bɔna a malema tuuma la puan mɛ wuuha wuuha, se'ere en sɔi la a boi la wuuha.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Dina zuo la a nyaŋeri mɛ faara, nananawa la saŋa woo sɛba en dole a puan paara Yinɛ la baha, eŋa en vɔi wuuha la zuo, a ŋmɛ'ɛra nupu'ɔ ba zuo la.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: La dela ma'a ti tu tara malema kiima kana taaba, en de fa'a, ka tara gbelegere, ka galum, ti ba yehe en ze'ele a ma'a ti a ka pa'ahɛ be'em duma puan, ti ba zɛkɛ en zom zuin zuin ha gaŋɛ saazuo wa.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Eŋa ka ani wuu malema duma bana la, a ka de a ni kaabera daare woo, ni kaabɛ a mɛŋa be'em zuo yia, gee nyaa kaabɛ bo' nɛreba la be'em. A kaabɛ la kaabegɔ buyina za'a, en daa dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ la.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Nɔze'elego la daa loohe la sɛba en de tɔregɔ duma la ti ba dɛna malema kiinduma, gee nɔpɔrɛ la en wa'am ze'elego la pooren na la, looe la Dayua sɛka en bi'ɛ kahegɛ, saŋa woo la.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Yeledaa sɛka en boi tumam en yeti sɛla wa puan dela dina wa. Tu tari la malema kiima en boi a tɔka, se'em en zi' Gani za'a Daana la na'am kuka la zuɔ nu'o saazuo ha.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: dɛna Yinɛ tuntuna saraa la puan, yelemɛŋerɛ kaabegɔ paka sɛka ti Daana la mɛŋa, gee dagi saaleba ti' la.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Ba loore Malema kiima woo ti a kaabera la bo'olum kaabegɔ, gee ti la me dɛna ti malema kiima kana wa mɛŋa me ni tara sɛla na kaabɛ.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: La han deni la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla, a pun kan ta'am deni malema daana, se'ere en sɔi la, malema duma pun bɔna mɛ kaabera bo'olum kaabegɔ la wuu ze'elego la en pa'alɛ se'em la.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Bama na'aheri Yinɛ la tanɛ deo seko puan ti asaala mɛŋa ti', gee dɛna saazuo pu'uhegɔ yire la yinyiligo la. Se'ere en sɔi la, Moose en yuum ta maahena ti a ti' pu'uhegɔ tanɛ deo wa la, Yinɛ yuum kagɛ en mɛ yeti, Bihɛ suŋa la fum ti' de sɛla woo wuu mam magehɛ de se'em pa'alɛ hɔ zoore la zuo la.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Gee nananawa Yezu to'e la tuunɛ en gani ŋwana, pun nyaa ta dɛna nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔpu'ulegɔ en gani ŋwana paaɛ paaɛ la puan, nɔpu'ulegɔ en yage nɔbiŋere en gani ŋwana zuo.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum han ka tari ni tuure, la ka le pakɛ ni ti ba le ɛɛra nɔpu'ulegɔ buyi.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Yinɛ yuum nyɛ la tuure la yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la puan en sɔi ti a yuum yeti, Dabeha sirum kini na, Daana la en yele, ti n wan pu'ulɛ nɔpaalɛ la Iziraleduma wa, la Yuuduma wa.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: ti de kan ana wuu mam yuum pu'ulɛ nɔse'ere la ba yaabeduma la, daan se'ere daarɛ ti mam tari ba la n mɛŋa nu'o ze'ele Igipiti tiŋa yehe na la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum ka dole mam nɔpu'ulegɔ la, bala, ba yele yuum ka pakɛ mam. Daana la en yele se'em n bala.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Nɔpu'ulegɔ kuna ti mam wan pu'ulɛ la Iziraleduma wa, dabeha aŋa han ta tole, Daana la en yele, N wan dikɛ n nɔze'elego la iŋɛ ba puti'ira puan, gee gulehɛ a iŋɛ ba suya puan, dɛna ba Yinɛ, ti ba dɛna mam nɛreba.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Ti ba kan le pa'ala taaba, bii yɛta ba mabia yeti, Baŋɛ Daana la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba woo wan mina mam, ze'ele ba bumbila ta paara ba nɛre kãtɛ.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, n wan zɔ ba tuure la nimbu'ɔ, gee kan le tɛra ba be'em la.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Yinɛ en tɔgeri nɔpu'ulepaalɛ yele la, pa'alɛ ti, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la dela kɛka. Ti sɛla en kuregɛ ki nuga, kan yuue gee mɛlum.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Nananawa, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum tara la malema, ti ba dɔla na'aha Yinɛ, timpaberɛ zuo pu'uhegɔ zi'an.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ti' yia tanɛ la mɛ, ti yia bɔ'ɔ la puan butolela zɛ'ɛlega bɔna de puan, ti teebula ti boroboro se'ere en kaaberi bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ la paga de zuo; ti ze'kana wa yu'urɛ yi'iri fa'a zi'an.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Ti tama la en yule beŋe deo seko en pa'ahɛ buyi' la, deo kuŋɔ yu'urɛ yi'iri Fa'a Saraa.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Ti sɛla en ze' bini dela nyuuŋo digelum nyɔ'a daka la, la nɔpu'ulegɔ tuulum daka la, ti salema dikɛ kaaɛ ka luga la za'a la, salema kaleŋa bɔna ka puan la maana dia la, la Aron kurego doore la en yuum puue puuro la me bɔna ka puan, ti kuge lama la ti ba gulehɛ ze'elego la iŋɛ a zuo la me bɔna ka puan;.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: ti tuulum daka wa zuo, kelumpagehi duma en nyagere en yage ka zuo, tɔ taaba pi' sugeri dia zi'an la. Yele ana wa tu kan ta'am pa'alɛ a yɛla vuurɛ paaɛ paaɛ nananawa.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Ban maahum yele ana ba'ahɛ la, ti malema duma la kɛ̃'ɛra daarɛ woo yia bɔ' la puan na, ti ba malena ba malema la;.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: gee malema kiima la ma'a en ki'ire saraa la puan gee buyina yuunɛ woo puan, tara ziim ti a kaabɛ bo' a mɛŋa, la nɛreba la be'em sebo ti ba tum gee ka mina la.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Ŋwana ti Fa'a Sia la pa'alɛ yeti, sadɔɔ seko en dole kɛ̃'ɛra saraa la puan la nan ka yu'ɛ, yia tanɛ deo la en nan kelum zɛ'a la.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Ŋwana dela daalehum bɔ'ɔra zina beere wa, ti bo'olum bu'a, la kaabegɔ kaabera kan ta'am bahɛ ti na'ahereba la puti'ira bi'ɛ kahegɛ.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: gee la yeen dɛna la dia la nyua, la ko'om sua malema yima yima, la kiha sɛ'a en dikɛ bo' ingɔŋɔ, gura sansɛka en wan ta ze'ele sɛla woo suŋa la.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Gee Kiresta en wa'am na la a dela malema kiima bo' yelesuma sɛ'a n wa'am na bɔna doohe tanɛ yire n ka tari makerɛ dɛna yire n ba'ahɛ yire .

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: a kɛ̃ la buyina za'a za'a Fa'a Saraa wa puan, ka tari buuhi la nagebibehi ziim, gee a mɛŋa ziim, n ze'ele bo' faarɛ en ka tari ba'ahegɔ.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Han dɛna ti buuhi la lɔlehi ziim, la nagepole tampɛgelum la en tari miiha sɛba en galum la zuo, ti la pɛɛra ba ingana, duuhe ba la.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Ŋwani ti Kiresta ziim la kan tum gaŋɛ bala zo'e zo'e se'em en doohe Sia sɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan, kaabɛ a mɛŋa fa'a kaabegɔ bo' Yinɛ la. Ziim buna wan sirum pee tu puti'ira yehe kum tuuma puan, tari tu lebe ti tu na'aha Yinɛ Vɔa wa!.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Dina wa zuo ti a dɛna nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra bo' nɔpu'ulepaalɛ la, ti bala ti sɛba ti ba yi' la wan ta'am to'e nɔbiŋere lɔkɔ la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la. Se'ere en sɔi la, kum n ki to'e ba, ba tuure la en daa boi yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la puan la.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Zi'an ti nɔpu'ulegɔ biŋe, la dela nɔpu'ula la ki gee ti nɔpu'ulegɔ la nyaa tuna.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɔpu'ulegɔ la wan pɔhɛ tuna la nɛra la en ki, de ka tuni se'em ma'a ti nɔpu'ulegɔ la nan vɔna la.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Ŋwana zuo, yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la yuum ka makɛ tuna, ti ziim ka ka'ɛ.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Moose yuum muulɛ nɔze'elego la woo mɛ bo' nɛreba la doohe pa'alegɔ la en de se'em la ba'ahɛ, gee nyaa dikɛ nagebibehi, la buuhi ziim, la ko'om la gunguno mɔlegɔ, la nyunhunyuuŋa tuhe bini, nyaa dikɛ miihi gɔŋɔ la mɛŋa, gee miihi nɛreba la za'a.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: gee yɛta yeti, Nɔpu'ulegɔ la ziim en ŋwana ti Daana la maahum bo' ya.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Bala mɛa ti a dikɛ ziim la miihi tanɛ deo la, la lɔge seto woo ti ba tari na'ahera la.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, pa'alegɔ la puan, sɛla woo peere mɛ la ziim, ti ziim han ka ka'ɛ, sugeri dia ka boi bo' be'em.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Bala ti la daa nara ti saazuo lɔgerɔ la ti ba tɔgehɛ maalɛ la, ba duuha ba la malema ana, gee saazuo lɔgerɔ la mɛŋa nari la kaabegɔ seko en gani ŋwana.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Se'ere ni sɔi la, Kiresta ka kɛ̃ la pu'uhegɔ deo seko ti saaleba nu'uhi mɛ, yeen dɛna yelemɛŋerɛ deo la ti ba tɔgehɛ, gee a kɛ̃ la tinsuŋan mɛŋa puan ha, ti a bɔna Yinɛ nɛŋan nananawa, tumam zuo.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Bii a kaabera a mɛŋa tulena wuu malema kiima la en ni kɛ̃ Fa'a Saraa la puan buyina yuunɛ woo, tara ziim en dagi a mɛŋa ziim se'em la;.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: la ha deni bala, a ye wan nameheri mɛ tulena, pɔhera tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan na la. Gee lan de se'em la, a wa'am la buyina za'a za'a ba'ahegɔ saŋa buuri wa puan, ti a fooe be'em bahɛ, doohe a dikɛ a mɛŋa kaabɛ.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Wuu lan gã' se'em bo' saaleba ti ba wan ba'ahum ki mɛ buyina, gee kum la pooren, gakerɛ en boi la.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: bala mɛa ti Kiresta, en kaabɛ a mɛŋa buyina bo' nɛreba zo'e zo'e be'em la ba'ahɛ, a wan le niiɛ na buyi' puan na, dagi a le zabɛ la be'em, gee a faaɛ sɛba en ani kɛli gura en la.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Bala, ze'elego la en yeen dɛna yinyiligo bo' yelesuma la en kini na la, gee dagi yelesuma la yelemɛŋerɛ naanɛ n bala la, kaabegɔ yiŋɔ la en kaaberi yuunɛ woo kan ta'am bahɛ ti sɛba en dole ku la dɛna nɛreba en bi'ɛ kahegɛ.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Han dagi ni bala, ba kan bahɛ ni ba kaabegɔ, sɛba en na'aheri ku la, han duuhe ba mɛhi buyina za'a za'a, ka le tari ni be'em puti'ira?.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Gee, kaabegɔ kuna puan, la tiiheri la be'em yele, yuunɛ en kaara yuunɛ.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la kan ta'am iŋɛ ti lɔlehi la buuhi ziim ta'am saalum be'em.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Lan vuti fifihe ti Kiresta ta wa'am timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, a yeti, Kaabegɔ, la bo'olum fum ka boti, gee iŋa ti fum maahum bo' mam.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: Nyɔ'a bo'olum, la be'em kaabegɔ bo'olum, fum suure ka boti ba.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Bala ti mam yeti, Yinɛ bihɛ, mam wa'am na ti n tum la fum sumboolum Oo Yinɛ, .

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: En yele se'em zuin wa yeti, Fum suure bii fum nini tika ka boi kaabegɔ, la bo'olum bii nyɔ'a bo'olum la be'em duuhego bo'olum puan la .

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: gee nyaa pa'ahɛ yeti, Bihɛ, mam wa'am na ti n tum la fum sumboolum. Bala, a saalum yia nɔpu'ulegɔ la bahɛ mɛ, ti a ta'am sɛ yi'i nɔpu'ulegɔ la.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Ti la dɛna la Yinɛ mɛŋa sumboolum zuo, ti ba pee tumam doohe Yezu Kiresta inganɛ la en dikɛ kaabɛ buyina za'a za'a bo' nɛra woo la.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Gee malema daana woo ze' mɛ beere en yilegeri beere a tumam la puan, kaabera kaabegɔ yiŋɔ mɛa tulena, kaabegɔ yiŋɔ mɛa en kan malum pee be'em bahɛ la.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Gee Kiresta kaabɛ la buyina bo' saŋa woo, kaabegɔ kuyina ma'a la bo' be'em ba'ahɛ la, a nyaa zi'ire la Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: pɔhɛ bala ma'a ha, a guri mɛ sansɛka ti a dinduma wan lebege a nama dafeere.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, kaabegɔ kuyina ma'a puan, ti a bahɛ ti ba bi'ɛ kahegɛ saŋa woo, bamam sɛba en kɔ'ɔm dɛna filam la.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Fa'a Sia la dela aze'nyɛdaana bo' dina wa, en yele tu yeti.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Nɔpu'ulegɔ kuna ti n wan pu'ulɛ la ba, dabeha aŋa la han ta tole. Daana la n yele. N wan dikɛ n nɔze'elego iŋɛ ba suyan, gee gulehɛ a iŋɛ ba puti'ira puan.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: a me pa'ahɛ yeti, N kan tɛra ba be'em, la ba ka fabele se'ere tuuma la le yɛ'ɛha.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Zi'an ti sugeri dia bɔna bo' yele ana, be'em duuhego kaabegɔ ka le bɔna.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: Bala zuo n suurɔ, tumam n diŋɛ tari puti'irɛ ti tu wan kɛ̃ Fa'a saraa la puan Yezu ziim la zuo la.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: doohe la sadɔɔ paalega vua la ti a yu'ɛ bo' tu, doohe la en ahegɛ tanɛ se'ere n beŋe la bahɛ la, .

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: la tumam en tari malema kiima kãtɛ Yinɛ yire la puan la.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: bahɛ ya ti tu vurege du' la yelemɛŋerɛ suure, tari puti'irɛ n pirɛ la sakerɛ, ti tu suya miihi ti ba duuhe a be'em puti'irɛ, ti tu ihe pee la tuulum ko'om.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Bahɛ ya ti tu gurɛ suŋa suŋa tu beere puti'irɛ sakerɛ yelehum la gee da miina, se'ere en sɔi la se'em daana en biŋe nuurɛ la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Gee bahɛ ya ti tu biha tumam n wan iŋɛ se'em pagera taaba la nɔŋerɛ la itesuŋɔ.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: da zagehera ya lagena taaba, wuu basɛba itegɔ en de se'em la, gee ya kpɛmeha taaba, malum ita de gana bala, wuu yan nyɛ ti dabeherɛ la pɔri la.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Gee tu han kɔ'ɔm mina gee bɔna be'em puan tumam en to'e yelemɛŋerɛ mi'ilum la ba'ahɛ la, kaabegɔ ka le yee wan kaabɛ bo' tu be'em.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: gee sɛla en wan wa'am na dela dabeem gakerɛ, la sunsunyiim bugum la en wan di dinduma la.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Se'em woo en tuue Moose pa'alegɔ la, kiiri mɛ ti nimbu'ɔ ka bɔna, Aze'nyɛ duma bayi' batã han diŋɛ tɔgɛ.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Bala, namehɛ kãtɛ deni taaba ti yamam ti'iha ti ba en wan dikɛ bo' sɛba en ka dikɛ Yinɛ Dayua la yi' sɛla, tuura nɔpu'ulegɔ ziim la n dikɛ pee ba la, gee nyiiha zu'uhegɔ Sia la?.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu mi se'em Daana en yeti, Leregere dela mam dinɛ, N wan lebehe yɔ la. Gee le yeti,Daana la wan gakɛ a nɛreba la.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: La dela dabeem yele ti fum lu Yinɛ vua wa nu'uhen.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Gee tiihɛ dabeha aŋa ha la, yan daa nyɛ niihum la, ya daa ŋmiberi mɛ la zika kãtɛ namehegɔ puan.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: saŋa se'ehi ba ni ba' diihe ya la vi nɛreba nɛŋan, la mɛlehegɔ, ti sahi se'ehi, ya naaɛ la sɛba ti ba ba' mɛleha.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya tari nimbu'ɔ mɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en boi paka deto puan, gee sakɛ la nyɛɛma ba han fãara ya lɔgerɔ, gee mina ti yamam mɛhi tari la sɛla en ani suŋa gee bɔna wuuha gana bala.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Bala zuo, da bahɛ ya puti'irɛ dinɛ taaba, de tari la yɔɔrɛ kãtɛ kina na.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Bala zuo, ya boti la ŋmibega, ti ya han ta tum Yinɛ sumboolum la ba'ahɛ, ya wan to'e nɔbiŋere la.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Gee la kelum wan, iŋɛ fii wa, ti se'em daana en ye' na la wan sirum wa'am na, gee kan gu'um;.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
