local: Gee mam tuuntɔɔ daana wa wan vɔna mɛ la sakerɛ. Mam sia ka nyɛɛni la se'em en lebere pooren.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Gee tumam ka pa'ahɛ sɛba en lebere pooren, ni ta bɔregɛ la, gee sɛba en tari sakerɛ, bala zuo nyɛ faarɛ la puan.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: Dagi ya zo'e zo'e en wan dɛna pa'aleba n suurɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti tu sɛba en pa'alɛ la, ba wan gakɛ tu la nima nima.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, tu za'a tuure mɛ. Se'em en ka tuure a tugum puan dela tuuntɔɔ, tana a a iŋa za'a tara la gareŋenɛ.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Tu han lɛ' Yire nɔya la gareŋenɛ ti tu tana ba, tu nyaŋɛ ba iŋa la za'a mɛ, ŋmɛreha ba tumam n boti zi'an.

eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Bii bihɛ ya uurenɔ, ban tibegɛ se'em ti kusebego ma'a za'ara ba kina la. Gee la bala za'a, zugɔ pika ti uuren-mɔneba la dikɛ yule uureŋɔ la pulin, tari ku kina bamam en boti se'em.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: Bala mɛa ti zɛleŋa dɛna iŋa la lɔge pika, gee bahɛ ti asaala ŋmɛ'ɛra nyɔ' la yele kãra. Bihɛ goo en karegɛ se'em, gee ti bugum pãreŋa ni taaɛ kɔ di filam!.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Zɛleŋa dela bugum. Zɛleŋa en boi tu iŋa wa puan la, dela be'em timpaberɛ. Ka ni galum la inganɛ wa za'a, nyaa taaɛ asaala naaŋɔ la za'a di ta ba'ahɛ gee nyaa taaɛ a mɛŋa dita, bugum timpaberɛ la puan.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, goo duhi woo, niihe, bunsɛlenduma, ko'om puan bunvɔɔhi, ta'am nyɛ malehegɔ mɛ, ti asaala en sirum malehɛ ba.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: gee nɛra ka boi ta'am malehɛ a zɛleŋa wa. A dela bumbe'o en ka tuni vɔ'ɔha, pirɛ la yabega.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Zɛleŋa kaŋa ti tu tara pɛgera Daana la, la Sɔ la, kaŋa mɛa ti tu tara pɔta bahɛ sɛba ti ba naam ba ti ba wuna la Yinɛ.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Nɔdinɛ mɛa puan ti sum, la ka' be'o zɛ'ɛta na. N suurɔ, la dagi la ana bala.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Buleniho wan ta'am bula ko'sumɔ la ko'be'ero me yoi?.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: N suurɔ wa, Kinkaŋa wan ta'am wam ta'ama? Bii sinsabega wam mu'uro? La kan malum iŋɛ ti ko'om en tari yaarum, bule ko'sumɔ.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Ani en tari yɛm, tara bɔkerɛ ya puan? Pa'alɛ bɔ doohe fum vom sumɔ la puan, ti fum tuni fum tuuma la ma'a ma'a la yɛm.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Gee ya han tara nintɛrega en ba' gaŋɛ, nyika puti'ira ya puan, da dagum ŋmɛ'ɛri ya nyu'ɔ, gee parena yelemɛŋerɛ la nɛŋan.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Yɛm buna taaba ka ze'ele saazuo na, gee bu dela timpaberɛ wa zuo, ka dɛna sia puan, gee kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi yɛm.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, zi'an ti nintɛrega la nyika puti'irɛ bɔna, gbiregeri la tuunfi'iha yima yima me wan bɔna.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Gee yɛm en ze'ele saazuo na, yia bu dela fa'a, tara suma'ahum, ma'ma, tara waŋa suboolum, pirɛ la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, tara walesuma, ti fum kan nyɛ lugehego, bii birikɔgenɛ bu puan la fii me.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Ba burɛ la tuntɔɔnɛ biili suma'ahum puan, bo' sɛba en boti suma'ahum la.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Nananawa, sakerɛ dela suure en ku gura beere puti'irɛ, suure en kɔ'ɛ gura sɛla ti nini ka nyɛ.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, sakerɛ zuo, ti nɛreba yuum tɔgera ba yele.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti tu bɔkɛ ti Yinɛ naam saazuo la timpaberɛ wa mɛ la a yelehum ma'a, bala, sɛla ti tu nyɛta wa ze'ele la sɛla ti tu ka nyɛta puan na.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Abɛl bo' Yinɛ bo'olum sebo en tigɛ nini gana Keen bo'olum la. Doohe ŋwana zuo, ti ba tɔgera a yele ti a dela tuuntɔɔ nɛra. Yinɛ mɛŋa en de aze'nyɛ daana bo' a bo'olum la. La en pun ki la, gee la bala za'a, a sakerɛ la zuo, a kelum tɔgera mɛ.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yinɛ tee Enɔki kiŋɛ ti a ka makɛ lem kum. Ba yuum ka nyɛ en se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en dikɛ en kiŋɛ. Gee ti teere wa wa'am na la, ba pun tɔgɛ a yele mɛ ti, a tigɛ la Yinɛ nini.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Bala, sakerɛ han ka bɔna, la kpe'em mɛ ti fum tigɛ Yinɛ nini, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em woo en wa'am a zi'an na, dela a mina ti Yinɛ boi mɛ, gee yɔɔra sɛba woo en eere en la ninmu'urɛ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Nowa, Yinɛ kagɛ en doohe yele sɛ'a en wan wa'am na, ti Nowa nam yetɔgum la ninmu'urɛ, gee kpa' daka kãtɛ la ti a faaɛ a yire. Yele dina ti Nowa yuum dikɛ gakɛ timpaberɛ wa, gee nyaa sooe vaaɛ tuntɔɔnɛ en dole sakerɛ puan la.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aberaham nam en yuum yi' en ti a nɔgɛ kiŋɛ zi'an ti a wan ta sooe ti ka dɛna a sɔ'ɔlum la. A yuum sirum ihege yɛ'a mɛ, gee ka mina en ye' zi'an la.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aberaham ta zi'ire saŋa fii wa, tinsɛka puan ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a wan dikɛ bo' en la, wuu en de saana ka puan la, a ki'ire la suga puan, bala mɛa ti Aziki la Yakɔbe me iŋɛ, bamam sɛba la eŋa en lagum vaaɛ nɔbiŋere sɔ'ɔlum bumɔ mɛa la.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Aberaham yuum guri la tinkãtɛ en tari ɛ̃berɛ, ti ka gula, la ka mɛta dɛna Yinɛ.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti a to'e dɔgum paŋa nɔbiŋere, la en daa pun ba' kuregɛ la, ti Saara me ka dɔgera la, beni zuo, a yuum nam mɛ ti se'em en biŋe nuurɛ la dela yelemɛŋerɛ daana.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Bala, ze'ele nɛra ayima wa puan na, nɛre se'em en ani wuu a ki mɛ la puan na, ti yaahi wa'am na, zo'e wuu ŋmarebibehi en zo'e se'em sagbama zuo ha la, zo'e kan ta'am kãalɛ wuu kulaa tintombi'ihego la.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Bana woo wa'am na ki mɛ la ba sakerɛ la, gee ka to'e Yinɛ en biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' ba sɛla la, gee ba nyɛ la mɛ ba suyan zaaɛ zaarɛ ha ti la kini na, ti ba to'e la. Ba daa yeti bamam dela saama, dɛna ɛɛreba timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Nɛresɛba en tɔgeri ŋwana la, la niiɛ mɛ ti ba eere la ba mɛŋa tiŋa ti ba zi'ire.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ ba han ti'ihɛ ni la ba tiŋa ti ba bahɛ pooren ha la yele, ba tari ni sore ti ba lebe.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Gee nananawa lan de se'em la dela, ba suure kɛ̃ mɛ la tinsɛka en gani bala, tinsɛka en ze'eti saazuo na la. Bala zuo, Yinɛ ka tari vi la ba yi'ira en ba Yinɛ. Yelemɛŋerɛ, a sirum maahum la tiŋa biŋe ba.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Sakerɛ zuo, ti Aberaham, Yinɛ wa makɛ en, ti a dikɛ Aziki kaabɛ. Eŋa se'em en to'e a nɔbiŋere lɔkɔ la, yuum maahum mɛ ti a dikɛ a dayɔba'arɛ wa kaabɛ.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: A dayɔsɛka ti Yinɛ yele en yeti, Aziki puan ti fum wan nyɛ yaahi ti ba dikɛ sigɛ hɔ la.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Aberaham yuum ti'ihɛ nyɛ ti la de sira, ti Yinɛ wan ta'am ihege nɛra kum puan lebena, tɔgɛ biŋere puan a yuum sirum lebe to'e en mɛ.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Aziki ka' sum bahɛ Yakɔbe la Esɔ beere puan.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yakɔbe en wa pɔra kum la, a ka' sum bahɛ Yosefe dayɔɔhi zuo, Nyiga a doore zuo na'ahera.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yosefe ta pɔra kum gee tɔgɛ bo' Iziraleduma, ba yɛhega yele, gee pa'alɛ ban wan pi' en se'em.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Moose dɔgereba yuum sugɛ en ban yuum dɔgɛ en ti a ta paaɛ ŋmarehi sitã la. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum nyɛ ti bia la sum mɛ, ti dabeem ka tara ba la naba la nuurɛ la ti a bo' la.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Moose en yuum ta zo'e la, a zagehɛ ti, ba da yi'ira eŋa Faro poyua bia.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: gee tugum looe ti a nameha la Yinɛ nɛreba la nyana a dita nyɛlum be'em puan dabeha fii wa.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: A yuum nam ya ti Kiresta zuo a wan to'e kɛhega la namehegɔ, ti la dɛna tarega kãtɛ bo' en, nyana Igipiti tarega la, se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum biheri la yɔɔri kãtɛ la en boi nɛŋa ha la.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti a bahɛ Igipiti, ka zɔta dabeem la naba la sunsunyiim la. A yuum kɔ'ɔm ŋmibera mɛ kina wuu a nyɛ la se'em daana ti ba ka nyɛta la.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti a doohe azɔɛtole malema la, la ziim miihego, ti Sagena la en dole kuura bikiinduma la da ta'am kalum Izirale bikiima.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti nɛreba la daa sige mɔgemɔlegɔ la puan kiŋɛ yakɛ wuu la dela kɛ'ɛŋa la, gee Igipitiduma la en se'em ti ba me iŋɛ bala la, ba yee la ko'om la puan.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Yeriko nangɔɔma la lu, Izirale nɛreba la en kaari dɛ muna ta paaɛ dabeha ayopɔi daarɛ la.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Sakerɛ zuo ti Arahab en de yalema yuum ka lagum nyɛ sageŋɔ la sɛba woo en yuum ka nam la, se'ere en sɔi la, a yuum to'e Izirale viihereba la mɛ la suma'ahum.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Beni ti n wan le yele pa'ahɛ? Saŋa kan bɔna bo' mam ti n yele Gidiyɔn, Barake, Samsin Yɛfita, Deviti la Samuwɛle, la nɔtɔgeheba la.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: sɛba en yuum ze'ele sakerɛ puan nyaŋɛ tinkãra, gakɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ, ze'ele to'e sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ la, mum gbigema nɔya.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: kiihe bugum en taaɛ dita ka tara makerɛ, piiɛ kuura sɔ'a nuuren, nyɛ paŋa tɔregɔ puan lebege kansanhi pɛntɛrɛ puan, bahɛ ti pɛntiireba en de saama zɔ zɔpɛɛlega.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Ti pɔgehi nɛreba en tabelɛ ki, le ihege ti ba to'ohe ba. Ba mɛlehɛ basɛba mɛ, gee ti ba zagehɛ ti bamam kan yehe paka deo la puan, ti ba to'e ihegere sunɛ se'ere n gani bala.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Basɛba to'ohe la kɛhega la ŋmɛ'kãtɛ, pun ta paaɛ kuto zɔtɔ ba'a, la paka deto puan.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Ba lobe basɛba mɛ la kuga ku, aahum basɛba wele wele, kɔrum basɛba la sɔ'ɔhi. Ba yɛ la piihi la buuhi gana ɛɛra la nimbu'ɔ, kua, la mɛlehegɔ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: ba eere nyaara la kpaahenɛ zi'ihi, zɔya zuto, tahi leŋen, tiŋan yogero puan. Tiŋa wa ka ana suŋa bo' ba.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Gee yele ana za'a la, la ban pun pɛgɛ ba la ba sakerɛ la, ba ka nyaŋɛ to'e sɛla ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' la.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ maahum la sɛla en gani bala biŋe, ti tumam la bamam lagum taaba bi'ɛ kahegɛ.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Bala, aze'nyɛ wa en zo'e se'em wuu sagbanɛ wa kaaɛ tu mum wa zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu dikɛ tibehegɔ seko woo, la be'em sebo woo en ba' tabera, gee ana la tɔ kankaŋi la biŋe kinkɛleŋa, gee bahɛ ya ti tu zɔta la ŋmibega, zɔ sɛka en gã' tu nɛŋan wa.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Tu biha suŋa suŋa Yezu en de sakerɛ nɛŋa daana, se'em daana n de sakerɛ la ba'ahegɔ daana la. Se'em ti nyɛɛma sɛ'a en dikɛ biŋe en la zuo, a daa ŋmibe mɛ ta paaɛ pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo, ka fabela dɔɔ zuo kum la nyanɛ la, nyaa wa'am na zi'a a zi'a zi'an Yinɛ na'am kuka la zuo, zuɔ nu'o.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Ti'ihɛ ya bihɛ Yezu en daa ŋmibe se'em namehegɔ seko taaba ti be'em duma daa dikɛ namehɛ en la, bala ti yamam me kan taregera bii ya suure sagum.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Yan zigeri la be'em la, ya nan ka zigɛ paaɛ ze'sɛka en de ya ziim ka'ɛ.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Ya me pun tamɛ kpemehego seko en gulehɛ pa'alɛ ti ya dela Yinɛ kɔma la. La yeti, N bia, da bihɛ Daana tube-dɔlegerɛ la yoo, bii fum suure sagum, a han dɔlegɛ fum tuberɛ.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la nameheri la sɛba ti a nɔŋɛ, gee dɔlegera bisɛka woo ti a to'e.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Ya ha ŋmibera makerɛ puan tube-dɔlegerɛ zuo. Yinɛ iti ya mɛ wuu a kɔma la. Bikani en boi ti ka dɔgera ka dɔlegera ka tuberɛ?.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Ya han ka to'e tube-dɔlegerɛ se'ere ti kɔma woo tɔ'ɔha la, ya dela kɔm-yooro, gee dagi Yinɛ kɔma.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha, tu tari tu dɔgereba mɛ, ti ba dɔlegera tu tuba, gee ti tu nana ba. La tugum dagena tu malum bɔta ti tu dikɛ tu mɛhi iŋɛ Sɔ sɛka en sɔi sɛɛhi la nu'uhen la tu vɔna?.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Tu dɔgereba ni dɔlegɛ tu tuba la saŋa fii wa wuu bamam en bihɛ ti la nari se'em la, gee Yinɛ dɔlegeri tu tuba la tumam mɛhi sum zuo, ti la bahɛ ti tu ta lagum tara a tuulum la.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Tube dɔlegerɛ woo tɔi mɛ, gee la nyani nyɛlum en wan bɔna saŋa fii wa. Ba'ahegɔ za'a, tube-dɔlegerɛ la ni tari la suma'ahum wala sɛ'a en de tuuntɔɔ vom bɔ'ɔra sɛba ti tube-dɔlegerɛ bi'ihɛ ba la.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Bala, zɛkɛ ya nu'uhi wa en ki yula wa, kpemehe ya duma wa en tɛteni wa.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: maalɛ fum sore la suŋa bo' fum nama, ti sɛla en de wobete da sugehe, gee a tugum naaɛ taaba.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Eere ya suma'ahum ninmu'urɛ nɛra woo, la fa'a vom sebo en han ka bɔna, nɛra kan ta'am nyɛ Daana la.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Bihɛ ya suŋa, ti ya se'em da koŋe Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la. Da bahɛ ya ti too nyɛgerɛ yehe ya puan na, tari daaŋɔ wa'am na, ti ya doohe de puan galum nɛreba zo'e zo'e.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Bihɛ ya mɛhi suŋa suŋa ti ya se'em da ana wuu Esɔ, se'em en ka ta' a mɛŋa, ka zɔta Yinɛ la, se'em en yuum koohe a kinɔ dia buyina ma'a zuo la.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: Ya mi' ti pooren, en yuum ta bɔta ti a sooevaaɛ ka'am a sɔ zi'an la, a sɔ la yuum zagehɛ en mɛ se'ere en sɔi la, sore yuum ka boi pika pika bo' en ti a wan ta'am tee yele la, la en yuum pun ɛɛra a ka'am la la nintam la.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Ya nan ka paaɛ zi'an ti ya wan ta'am kalum la na, bugum peelum la puan, la lika gbareti la zi'an na, la ze'sɛka en gbiregeri la.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: la tintanɛ peebego la, la kua sɛka tugum ti ka kɛleheba bɛlena ti ka pun da le tɔgɛ yele yinɛ pa'ahɛ la.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la ba kan nyaŋɛ tara en bo' a nuurɛ la se'em la, ti Duŋa han pun kalum zoore la, ka dela ba lobe ka ku la.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, zi'an la yuum ba' tara la dabeem gaŋɛ ti Moose yeti, Mam gbi'iri mɛ la dabeem.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Bahɛ ya ti suuɔ nɔŋerɛ yee saŋa woo.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Gee yamam paaɛ la zoore se'ere yu'urɛ en yi'iri Zaayɔn la puan na, tinsɛka en de Yinɛ vua wa tiŋa la puan, tinsɛka en de Yerusalɛm sɛka en boi saazuo ha la, ya paaɛ la suŋereba en zo'e kan ta'am kãalɛ la lageŋɔ kãtɛ en boi kuyima.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Da zagehɛ ya tɔ'ɔha saama, gee sɛba iŋɛ la bala ta to'ohe suŋereba gee ka baŋɛ.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: ya wa'am la lageŋɔ seko en de kɔmdiina sɛba yu'ura en gulehɛ bɔna tinsuŋan puan la na, ya wa'am la Yinɛ se'ere en de sɛla woo gakera la zi'an na, Ya wa'am la tuuntɔɔ duma sɛɛhi la n bi'ɛ kahegɛ la zi'an na.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Tɛri ya sɛba en boi paka deen la yele, wuu ya lagum bɔna la paka deen la ba la, sɛba ti ba mɛleha ba la, wuu yamam mɛhi ti ba mɛleha la.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Ya wa'am la Yezu en de nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔpu'ule paalega la puan la zi'an na, la ziim sebo en dikɛ miihi, gee tɔgera yelesuma gana Abɛl ziim la zi'an na.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Bahɛ ya ti pɔgedire la sire-ele tara na'ahegɔ ya za'a zi'an, ti pɔgedire la sire-ele suŋɔ la da galum, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ wan gakɛ yaluntuuma duma, la kabohi duma mɛ.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Bihɛ ya suŋa la ya da zagehɛ se'em en kageri ya wa. Nɛreba daa han ka piiɛ ban da zagehɛ se'em en daa kagɛ ba tiŋa wa zuo la, tumam yeti tu iŋɛ la ŋwani piiɛ la fii me, tu ha zagehɛ se'em en kageri tɔ saazuo na wa.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Tari ya ya vom suŋa ti bu da kɛ̃ ligenɔŋerɛ puan, gee bahɛ ya ti ya nini tigɛ la sɛla ti ya tara. Se'ere en sɔi la, a yele tu yeti, N kan malum bahɛ ya, bii n zagehɛ ya.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Sankaŋa ha la, a kua ma'a en yuum miim timpaberɛ wa, gee nananawa, a biŋe la nuurɛ ti Le yɛ'ɛha, n wan le miim, gee dagi timpaberɛ wa ma'a, gee la saazuo za'a.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Ŋwana zuo la, tu wan ta'am yele gee ti dabeem ka tara tɔ yeti, Daana la en de mam suŋera, N kan zɔta dabeem. Beni ti nɛra wan ta'am iŋɛ mam.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Yetɔge buna wa Le yɛ'ɛha wa, pa'ali ti, a yeti a vuɛ la sɛla en miini la bahɛ bana dela naaŋɔ lɔgerɔ n bala, ti sɛla woo n ka miini la yee.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Tɛra ya nɛheduma, sɛba en tɔgɛ Yinɛ yelehum la bo' ya la. Bihɛ ya ban daa vɔi ba vom se'em, la ya tɔgehɛ ba sakerɛ la.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Bala zuo, tumam en yeti tu to'e sɔ'ɔlum sebo en ka miini la zuo, bahɛ ya ti tu bo' ya Yinɛ fara, doohe la na'ahegɔ kaabegɔ seko taaba en tigɛ nini, ti duma kpa'a, la dabeem bɔna bini.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yezu Kiresta dela bala mɛa zaam, zina, la saŋa woo.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu Yinɛ la dela bugum sebo en diti kãbera.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti pa'ali yooro yima yima ziira ya kaara zi'an woo, se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti zu'uhegɔ kpemehe suure la, nyana dia kihega yɛla, yele sɛ'a en ka suŋɛ sɛba en dole a la.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Tumam tari la kaabegɔ bimbine se'ere ti sɛba en ze' tanɛ deo la puan kaabera la ka tari sore ti ba dita kaabegɔ dia la.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, duhi se'ehi ziim ti malema kiima la tari kɛ̃ Fa'a saraa la puan ti bu dɛna be'em duuhego kaabegɔ la, ba ni nyɔ' si la tiiheŋɔ la yiŋa ha.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Bala zuo ti Yezu namehɛ tiŋa la yiŋa ha, ti a pee nɛreba be'em la a mɛŋa ziim.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Bahɛ ya ti tu kiŋɛ ya a zi'an tiiheŋɔ la yiŋa la ha, la tu ta lagum di nyanɛ se'ere ti a ŋmibe de puan la.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Tiŋa wa zuo, tu ka tari tinsɛka n wan bɔna wuuha, gee tu biheri la tiŋa sɛka n ye' na la.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Doohe eŋa puan, yi bahɛ ya ti daarɛ woo, tu bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ tu na'ahegɔ kaabegɔ en de tu nɔgbama wala ti tu puta a yu'urɛ la.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Da zagehɛ ya ti ya tuna sum, gee tɔta yan tari sɛla la taaba, gee kaabegɔ kuna taaba en tigeri Yinɛ nini.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Nane ya ya nɛhi duma, gee sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ba, gee bamam en biheri ya vom, gee wan ta yele ban tuni se'em. Bo' ya ba sore ti ba tuna ba tuuma la nyɛɛma gee dagi ba si'ina. La han ana bala, ya kan nyɛ nyuurɔ.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Pu'uhi ya bɔ'ɔra tɔ, gee tumam mi' ti tumam tari la putɛ'sunɛ, ti tu suure bɔta ti tu tuna la yelemɛŋerɛ sɛla woo puan.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: N yeti ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, ti ya tuna ŋwana wa gana sɛla woo, ti n ta'am le lebe ya zi'an kalum na.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Nananawa, Yinɛ se'em en de suma'ahum daana la, se'em daana en tari a piihi dunkiin kãtɛ wa ze'ele kum puan lebe na la, tu Daana Yezu Kiresta doohe a ziim la nɔpu'ulegɔ la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: wan bahɛ ti ya bi'ɛ tunsunɛ woo puan, ti ya ta'am tum a sumboolum, doohe la en boi tu puan tuna la tɔ, tuunsɛ'a en tigeri a nini Yezu puan, se'em ti suŋa dɛna a dinɛ saŋa woo. Taari.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: N beleni ya mɛ n suurɔ, ŋmibe ya la n kpemehego yelehum wa, gee n yeen gulehɛ la girega girega bo' ya na.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: N boti ya mina ti ba bahɛ tu suuɔ Timoti, ti a han wa'am tɔtɔ na, a wan doohe la mam na ti n nyɛ ya.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Zaba la nɔkpɛ'ɛma sɛ'a en boi ya puan wa, ze'ele la bɛha na? Ba ka ze'ele la ya kunkoma'ahum la en zaberɛ ya puan la?.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Pu'uhɛ ya nɛheduma, la nɛresunduma la woo bo' tu. Sɛba woo en boi Itali tiŋa pu'uheri ya na.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Yan boti sɛla gee kan nyɛ la zuo, ti ya kuura.Ya nini en tɛti la sɛla gee kan nyɛ la zuo, ti ya ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpɛ'ɛma gee zabera taaba. Ya ka tari, beni zuo, ya ka soke.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Yemesi en de yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la Daana Yezu Kiresta la, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra yizuto pia la tuyi' la en nɔgɛ bɔna zi'an woo la. Mam pu'uheri ya na.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya za'a.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Ya soke ya gee ka to'e, beni zuo, ya ni soke tuue mɛ, ti ya ta'am di ya ma'a nyɛlum.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: N suurɔ, ya han ni ta nyɛta maketooro seto woo, da dikɛ ya ku yi' sɛsɛla, gee tugum nyɛɛni ya.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Bunyaalehi wa! Ya ka mi' ti ya nyɔgɛ zɔtɔ la timpaberɛ wa, dela ya dɛna dinduma la Yinɛ? Ŋwana zuo, fum se'em woo en boti fum dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo zɔ, lebege la Yinɛ dima.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti ya sakerɛ makerɛ bo'ori ya la ŋmibega.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Bii ya ti'ihɛ ti la dela yoo ti gulehegɔ la yeti, Yinɛ si'ini mɛ la nyuure la a Sia la ti a bahɛ ti ka bɔna la tɔ la?.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Bahɛ ya ti ya ŋmibega la pirɛ, ti ya ta'am bi'ɛ kahegɛ ti sɛla da koŋe ya.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Gee a malum bɔ'ɔra nɛra woo la zu'uhegɔ zo'e zo'e. Ŋwana zuo ti la gulehɛ yeti, Yinɛ zagehɛ pa'alɛ n mɛŋa duma mɛ, gee bɔ'ɔra la sigehe n mɛŋa duma zu'uhegɔ.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Ya se'em han pɔ̃hegɛ yɛm, a suhɛ Yinɛ, se'em en bo'ori yabi yabi gee ka fabela la, gee a wan bo' fum.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Sigehe ya mɛhi ŋwana zuo, bo' Yinɛ. Da bo' ya kulekpa'bɛ'a la sore, gee a wan ɛkɛ gee bahɛ ya.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Gee suhɛ en la sakerɛ, gee da bu'ura. Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em n bu'uri ani wuu Ateko ko'mi'a ti kusebego tara ŋmɛ'ɛra kaara se'em la.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Vurege ya du' Yinɛ, gee eŋa me wan vurege du' ya. Pee ya nu'uhi, be'em duma wa, gee pee ya suya, puti'ira ayi' ayi' duma wa.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Nɛre se'em en ani ŋwana da ti'iha ti a wan to'e sɛla Daana la zi'an.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Fabele ya, si'ini ya, gee kɛla. Bahɛ ya ti ya lɔ'ɔrɔ lebege kuunɔ, ti ya pupeelum dɛna susageŋɔ.

eng: They are double-minded, unstable in all their ways.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la nɛre-ina tari la puti'ira ayi' ayi', ka zɛ'ɛta se'ere puan.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Sigehe ya mɛhi Daana la nɛŋan, gee a wan zɛkɛ ya.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Bahɛ ya ti suuɔ seko en sigehe ku mɛŋa la, ŋmɛ' a nyu'ɔ, la Yinɛ en zɛkɛ en la.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: Da tɔgera ya be'em bɔ'ɔra taaba, n suurɔ wa. Fum se'em woo en tɔgɛ be'em bo' fum tadaana, bii gakɛ fum tadaana, tɔgɛ la be'em bahɛ ze'elego la zuo, gee gakɛ ze'elego la. Gee fum han gakɛ ze'elego la, fum ka tuni ze'elego la tuuma, gee fum dela ku gakera.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: ti buntata me sigehe a mɛŋa, beni zuo, tarega wan mɛlum wuu mɔpuuro la.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Nɛra ayima ma'a en bo'ori ze'elego la, gee dɛna ku gakera, se'em en wan ta'am faaɛ gee sagum. Bala, fum dela ani ti fum gakɛ fum tadaana?.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, wuntɛɛŋa zoni mɛ na la tuulega, ku'ura vatɔ la, ti bu puuro woo luta, ti bu suŋa la Sagena. Bala mɛa ti buntateba wan ta bahɛ yoo ban mu'ɛ nini ba bumbɔrɛ la puan se'em la.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Kelehe ya, yamam sɛba en yeti, Zina bii beere tu wan kiŋɛ ke la ke ha, ta kɛ̃' bini yuunɛ, lɛɛbera nyɛta ligeri la.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Sum boi mɛ la se'em en ŋmiberi makerɛ puan. Ena daana nyaŋɛ magehegɔ la mɛ, wan to'e vom muŋa la, ti Daana la biŋe nuurɛ ti a bo' sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Gee ya pun ka mina sɛla ti beere wan tari na. Ya vom dela bem? Se'ere en sɔi la, ya dela mɛgelum en lu yee fii wa, gee mɛlum.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Se'em da bɔna ti makerɛ se'ere han paaɛ en, ti a yeti, Yinɛ en makerɛ mam biha. Se'ere en sɔi la, be'em kan ta'am makɛ Yinɛ bihɛ, Yinɛ mɛŋa me ka makerɛ se'em la be'em biha.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Gee yan tugum ni wan yele se'em dela, La han dɛna Daana la putɛ ɛrɛ, tu wan vɔna, gee tum ŋwana la ŋwana.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Gee la han ta iŋɛ bala, nɛra mɛŋa kunkoma'ahum en ni pã'ahɛ en, ve'e en kɛ̃' makerɛ la puan.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Gee lan de se'em la, ya ŋmɛ'ɛri nyu'ɔ la ya pa'alɛ mɛhi puan. Nyɔ'ɔ ŋmɛ'a kana taaba woo dela be'em.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: Ti kunkoma'ahum buna wa nyaa han ta nyɔkɛ puurɛ, bu dɔgeri la be'em, ti be'em buna wa han ta bi'ɛ kahegɛ, bu dɔgeri la kum.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Gee se'em woo en mi' sɛla en de ma'a ti a tum, gee koŋe a tum dɛ, tuue mɛ.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: Da bahɛ ya ti se'em pã'ahɛ ya, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Beni zuo, abo'yabiyabi woo, la bo'olum sumɔ woo ze'eti la saazuo, Sɔ la en de niihum daana la zi'an sigera na. Se'em puan ti ŋmɛlema la lika ka bɔna doohe teere se'ere puan.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: Doohe a mɛŋa puti'irɛ en de se'em la zuo, a dɔgɛ tu mɛ doohe a yelemɛŋerɛ yelehum la puan, ti tu dɛna wala diina a naaŋɔ la puan.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: Ya dela ya bɔkɛ dina wa, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, bahɛ ya ti nɛra woo kɛleha kalam kalam, gee da tɔgera kalam kalam, bii ita suure kalam kalam.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya sunsunyiim la ka ŋwɔni Yinɛ tuuntɔɔ vom la.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Dinɛ zuo, yehe ya mɛhi yaalegɔ woo puan, la tuunfi'iha kãra puan, gee sigehe ya mɛhi to'e yelehum la en burɛ ya puan tara paŋa wan ta'am faaɛ ya sɛɛhi bahɛ la.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Gee me iti ya yelehum la en de se'em la, gee da yeen kelehe ya kelehego ma'a pã'ahɛ ya mɛhi.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛba han yeen kɛleha yelehum la ma'a, gee ka tari bu tuna, ba ani mɛ wuu sɛba en ni bihɛ ba mɛhi biheŋa puan.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: ba han bihɛ ba mɛhi gee kiŋɛ, bilam bilam ba ni tamɛ ban ani ni se'em la mɛ.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Gee sɛba en bihɛ nɔze'ele seko en ka tari gbelegere la, fai nɔze'elego la en ka tari gbelegere la, tara kɔ, dagena kɛleheba sɛba en tameni, gee sɛba en tari tuna, ba wan nyɛ sum ba itegɔ la puan.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Sɛba han ti'ihɛ ti bamam pu'uheri Yinɛ, gee ka tana ba zɛlehi tara, gee pã'aha ba suya, ba pu'uhegɔ la dela yoo.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Pu'uhegɔ seko en de fa'a, ka tara galeŋɔ Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nɛŋan en ŋwana. Tu bihera kiibehi, la pokɔpa ba nimbu'ɔ puan, gee ka galena ya mɛhi la timpaberɛ wa zuo yɛla.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: N suurɔ wa, yamam en tari ninkɔɔra se'em wa sirum nam Daana Yezu Kiresta, en de suŋa daana?.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra han wa'am ya lageŋɔ puan na, pire salema bãhi, yɛ fusunɔ, ti naha me en ka yɛ lɔgesunɔ me kɛ̃' na.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: ti ya han bihɛ nyɛ se'em daana en yɛ fusunɔ la, yele en yeti, N beleni fum mɛ, zi'ire ka'lam, gee yele naha la yeti, Zɛ'a ke ha, bii Zi'ire tiŋa.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: ya ka lugehe la ya puan en bala, lebege gakereba en tari putɛ'be'ero?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: Kelehe ya, n sunɔŋerɛ duma. Yinɛ ka looe la naheduma timpaberɛ wa zuo ti ba dɛna buntateba sakerɛ puan, la ba ta sɔna sɔ'ɔlum la ti Yinɛ biŋe nuurɛ bo' sɛba en nɔŋɛ en la?.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Gee yamam ka nane naha. Dagi buntateba la en bala mugehera ya la? Dagi bamam en dati ya kina tugum zi'ihen la?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Dagi bamam en tuuri yu'sunɛ se'ere en dikɛ sigɛ ya la?.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ya wan iŋɛ suŋa, ya han sirum tum na'am nɔze'elego la wuu gulehegɔ la en yele se'em yeti, Fum nɔŋɛ fum tadaana wuu fum mɛŋa la.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Gee yamam han lugeha nɛreba, ya tum la be'em, ti ze'elego la nyɔkɛ ya ti ya tuue mɛ.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum se'em woo en dole ze'elego la za'a, gee tuue zi'an kayima, tuue ze'elego la za'a mɛ.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, se'em daana en yeti, Da tum kabohi la, eŋa mɛa en yeti, Da ku nɛra. Bala, fum han ka tum kabohi, gee ku nɛra, fum tuue ze'elego la mɛ.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Bala, tɔgera gee ita wuu sɛba ti ba wan gakɛ la fai bɔŋa ze'elego la.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la gakerɛ kan ta tara nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bo' se'em woo en ka tari nimbɔ'zɔrɛ. Nimbɔ'zɔrɛ ta' gakerɛ mɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: Beni nyuurɔ en boi bini n suurɔ, se'em han yeti a tari la sakerɛ gee ka tari tuna? Fum sakerɛ la wan faaɛ hɔ bahɛ yoi'?.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Fum suuɔ bii taya han ɛɛra beele, ka tara a nuurɛ dia.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: ti ya ayima yele en yeti, Kina la suma'ahum. Ɔɔrɔ kan kɛ̃ hɔ, ti fum di tigɛ, gee ka bo' en, en a inganɛ la en boti sɛla la, beni sum en boi bini?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Bala, sakerɛ ma'a, han ka tara tuna ki mɛ.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Gee ayima wan yeti, Fum tari la sakerɛ, ti mam me tara tuuma. Eii! Yi pa'alɛ mam fum sakerɛ la ti tuuma ka bɔna bini, gee mam me wan dikɛ la n tuuma la pa'alɛ hɔ n sakerɛ.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: Ya nam ti Yinɛ dela ayima. Ya mɔ ya. Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi me pun nam la bala, gee gbi'ira.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Ya boti ba pa'alɛ ya mɛ? Gɔɔŋɔ wa, ti sakerɛ en ka tari tuuma, ki mɛ?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: Tu sɔyaaba Aberaham yuum ka di buurɔ a tuuma puan, eŋa en yuum kaabɛ a dayua Aziki kaabegɔ bimbine la zuo la?.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: Ya nyɛ ti sakerɛ la yuum vɔi mɛ la a tuuma la, ti a tuuma la bahɛ ti sakerɛ la bi'ɛ.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Bala ti gulehegɔ la wa'am asira na, gulehegɔ seko en yeti, Aberaham nam Yinɛ mɛ, ti Yinɛ bo' en tuntɔɔnɛ, ti ba yi'ira en Yinɛ zɔ.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Ya nyɛ ti nɛra diti buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan la a tuuma puan gee dagi sakerɛ ma'a zuo.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: Dagi bala mɛa ti Arehabe, pɔge yalema la yuum di buurɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan a tuuma puan, la eŋa en yuum to'e viihereba la gee bahɛ ti ba doohe sadɔɔ kuyima yehe la?.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Wuu iŋa en ka tari sia ki se'em la, bala mɛa ti sakerɛ en ka tari tuuma me ki.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Kelehe ya, yamam buntateba wa, kele ya gee si'ina la nimbɔ'seko en ye' ya zuo na la.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Ya tarega la pu'ɛ mɛ, ti bunmɔgela mɔgɛ ya fukãra la.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Ya salema, la salempɛɛla la di la tiŋanziim, ti ba tiŋanziim la wan dɛna aze'nyɛ daana zɛregɛ ya, bu wan di ya iŋa wuu bugum la. Bihɛ ya, yamam tugum lagehɛ la lɔgerɔ biŋe ba'ahegɔ dabeha wa puan?.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Kelehe ya! Yɔɔrɛ se'ere en de tuntuneba sɛba en tum ya vatɔ tuuma la ti ya fa' biŋe la kaahi baha me na, ti buneba la kaahega me lu pɛntiireba za'a nɛŋa Daana la tuban.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Ya vɔi timpaberɛ zuo mɛ la tarega la nyɛlum. Ya di mɛ karegɛ gura ya kum daarɛ.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Ya ni gakɛ mɛ, gee ku tuuntɔɔ daana, se'em en ka zigɛ la ya.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: N suurɔ wa, tari ya sugeri gee gura Daana la lɛbega na. Ti'ihɛ ya bihɛ Kaareba en ni tara sugeri se'em gura ba bumbuura suma la ti sigesaa la burega saa ta wa'am na ni.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Yamam me tara sugeri bala, gee da kɔ'ɛ ya puti'irɛ. Daana la kinɛ na paaɛ mɛ na en bala.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: N suurɔ wa, da gɔgeni ya la taaba, bala ti ba kan gakɛ ya. Bihɛ ya, Gakera la en bala ze'a kuleŋa nuuren wa!.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: La han dɛna la namehegɔ la sugeri yɛla, n suurɔ wa, dole ya sɛla ti nɔtɔgeheba sɛba en tɔgɛ Daana la yu'urɛ puan la daa doohe namehɛ seko woo puan tole la.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ, tu yi'iri sɛba en sakɛ ŋmibega la, la sunduma. Ya wum Yobe ŋmibega la yele, ya me baŋɛ Daana la puti'irɛ yele dinɛ, Daana la en tari giile la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ se'em.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: Se'ere en gani za'a n suurɔ wa, da pɔti ya saazuo, bii timpaberɛ wa, bii pɔ yele se'ere. Bahɛ ya ti ya Ɛ̃ɛ dɛna ɛ̃ɛ, ti ya Aayi dɛna aayi', ti ya da lu sageŋɔ puan.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Sɛba boi ya puan nameha? Ba pu'uhɛ. Sɛba boi ya puan tara pupeelum? Ba yuuna pɛka yuuma.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Sɛba boi ya puan bɛ̃'ɛra? Ba yi' lageŋɔ kiinduma ti ba pu'uhɛ bo' ba, gee sɔ' en tuulum sua la kaam Daana la yu'urɛ puan.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Sakerɛ pu'uhegɔ la wan faaɛ bã'duma la bahɛ, ti Daana la ihege ba, ti ba se'em en tum be'em wan nyɛ sugeri dia.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Ŋwana zuo, puti ya ya be'em bɔ'ɔra taaba, gee pu'uha bɔ'ɔra taaba bala ti ya wan nyɛ ya tibegɔ. Tuntɔɔ daana pu'uhegɔ tari la paŋa, gee tuna.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Elia yuum dela asaala wuu tumam la, gee yuum pu'uhɛ la ninmu'urɛ ti saa da ni, ti la dikɛ yuuma atã la ŋmarehi siyoobe ti saa sirum ka tɔkɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Ti a le pu'uhɛ, ti saazuo ha bo' saa na, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo maalɛ dia.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: N suurɔ, se'em han bɔna ya puan bɔregɛ yelemɛŋerɛ wa puan, ti se'em tari en lebena.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: ya mina ti, se'em woo en tari be'em daana a tuure puan lebe na la, wan faaɛ be'em daana la sia kum puan bahɛ, gee ti Yinɛ pi' be'em daana la be'em zo'e zo'e.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Mam Pita en de Yezu Kiresta tuntuna, en gulehɛ gɔŋɔ wa bɔ'ɔra sɛba en nɔgɛ bɔna zi'an woo, Pɔntusi, Galatia, Kapadosia, Esiya, la Bitiniya tihi puan la.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Sɛba ti Yinɛ en de Sɔ la daa pun te'ele looe ba, maahum ba, gee duuhe ba la a Fa'a Sia la, ti ba nana Yezu Kiresta, gee ti a miihi ba la Yezu ziim la. Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum zo'e zo'e dɛna ya dinɛ.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Sum dɛna Yinɛ en de tu Daana Yezu Kiresta Sɔ la dinɛ! A nimbɔ'zɔrɛ kãtɛ la zuo, a bo' tu la dɔgum paalemɔ ti tu tara beere putɛ'vua doohe Yezu Kiresta vo'ore a kum la puan.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: A yi ya mɛ ti ya to'e lɔgerɔ seto en ka sageni, ka galum, ka kuregera, en maahum biŋe saazuo ha gura ya.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ paŋa gu'ura ya doohe ya sakerɛ puan la, ya faarɛ la wan niiɛ ba'ahegɔ saŋa la na.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Dina puan ti ya nyɛɛna, la han pun dɛna ti nananawa, saŋa fii wa, ya namehɛ kãra yima yima doohe mageha yima yamam puan.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: ti ya yelemɛŋerɛ sakerɛ la en tari tibehum gana salema, sakerɛ wa en tari tibehum gana salema wa, en pun tana Sagena la, gee ba wan kelum magehɛ bu la bugum bihɛ, bala mɛa ti ba wan magehɛ ya sakerɛ la bihɛ, gee de wan tara pɛka la suŋa, la na'ahegɔ, Yezu Kiresta han ta niiɛ na.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: La yan pun ka nyɛ en la, ya nɔŋɛ en mɛ. La yan pun ka nyɛ en nananawa la, ya nam en mɛ, gee nyɛɛna mɛ la nyɛɛma kãtɛ sɛ'a en ka tari tɛka pirɛ suŋa.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya to'ore la ya sakerɛ yɔɔrɛ en de ya sɛɛhi faarɛ la.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Han dɛna faarɛ dina wa yele, nɔtɔgeheba sɛba en yuum purɛ zu'uhegɔ la en wan dɛna ya dinɛ la, ma'ɛ ba mɛhi mɛ, viihɛ, gee ɛ bu paaɛ paaɛ.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: viihera ɛɛra nɛresɛka, la sansɛka ti Kiresta Sia la en boi ba puan la ta pa'alɛ ba, doohe la Sia la en te'ele yele ba namehegɔ seko taaba en maahum gura Kiresta, la na'ahegɔ seko en dole ku poore.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: La yuum niiɛ pa'alɛ ba yeti, ba ka tuni bɔ'ɔra la ba mɛhi, gee tumam, doohe yelesɛ'a en nyaa muulɛ bo' ya nananawa, doohe sɛba en tari faarɛ yelehum wa na bo' ya la, doohe Fa'a Sia la ti Yinɛ tum en saazuo ha bahɛ na la.Yele sɛ'a ti suŋereba mu'ɛ nini ti ba nyɛ la.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Ŋwana zuo, maahum ya puti'ira ti ya tum. Tana ya mɛhi tara. Bahɛ ya ti ya puti'ira za'a bɔna zu'uhegɔ seko ti Yezu Kiresta wan tari na bo' ya, a han ta niiɛ na la puan.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Wuu yan de kɔma en nane la, da naaɛ ya la kunkoma'ahum sebo taaba ti ya yuum pun tara, yan yuum nan ka mi' la.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Gee wuu se'em en yi' ya la, en de fa'a se'em la, deni ya Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba ya mɛhi, ya itegɔ woo puan.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: La gulehɛ yeti, Deni ya Yinɛ tuulum nɛreba, se'ere en sɔi la mam dela Fa'a.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Yamam han yi'ira en Sɔ, se'em en gakeri nɛra woo ka tara ninkɔɔra, gee nɛra woo la a tuuma en de se'em la, voni ya la dabeem ya nɔka saŋa wa puan.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Ya mi ti Yinɛ yɔ mɛ to'e ya yehe vom yoo sebo ti ya to'e ya yaabeduma zi'an la. A ka yɔ mɛ la lɔgeseto en sageni, ana wuu salempɛɛla bii salema.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: gee Kiresta tuulum ziim en ani wuu kaabegɔ pebila en ka bɛ̃' bii tara dɛkɔ se'em la.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Yinɛ yuum pun maahum en mɛ gee ti timpaberɛ wa baŋɛ pi'ilum, gee ta niiɛ en ba'ahegɔ saŋa wa puan, yamam zuo.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Doohe eŋa puan ti ya wa'am na nam Yinɛ, se'em en ihege en a kum puan la, gee bo' en na'ahegɔ, ti ya sakerɛ, la ya beere puti'irɛ la bɔna Yinɛ zi'an.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Nananawa ti ya pee ya sɛɛhi doohe yan nam yelemɛŋerɛ la, nyaa tara yelemɛŋerɛ nɔŋerɛ la, nɔŋɛ ya taaba paaɛ paaɛ ya suya puan ha.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Ba dɔgɛ ya la dɔgum paalum, dagena la biiliho sebo en pu'uri, gee biiliho sebo en ka pu'uri, ba malum dɔgɛ ya doohe la Yinɛ yelehum vua la en ka malum ba'aha la puan.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Asaala woo ani mɛ wuu muɔ la ti ba suŋa za'a ana wuu mɔpuuro la. Muɔ ku'uri mɛ, ti mɔpuuro zɔgera.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: gee Daana yelehum la kɔ'ɔm bɔna mɛ wuuha wuuha. Yelehum buna wa dela faarɛ yelehum sebo ti ba muulɛ bo' ya la.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Bala, yehe ya mɛhi, yele ana wa puan. Putoo, wikerɛ, birekɔgenɛ, nintɛrega, la yu'sageŋɔ.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Wuu yan de bilɛɛhi la, kɔ'ɔm leebere ya mɛ la Sia puan bi'ihum sumɔ la, bala ti bu wan bahɛ ti ya zɔ'ɔra ya faarɛ la puan.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: ya han sirum lem mɛ nyɛ Daana la en ani suŋa se'em.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Wa'am ya a zi'an na, eŋa en de vom ɛ̃berɛ kugere la, la saaleba en pun zagehɛ en la, gee eŋa ti Yinɛ looe, ti a dɛna tuulum lɔkɔ Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Wuu yan de vom kuga en dikɛ ya mɛhi mɛ Sia yire, deni ya tuulum malema duma, kaabera sia puan kaabegɔ en tigɛ Yinɛ nini doohe Yezu Kiresta puan.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la boi gulehegɔ la puan yeti, Bihɛ, n biŋere la ɛ̃berɛ kugere Zaayɔn, ɛ̃berɛ kugere se'ere ti ba looe, gee ti de dɛna tuulum kugere. Se'em woo en nam en, vi kan di en.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Ti fum se'em woo en nam, a dela tuulum lɔkɔ bo' fum, gee sɛba en ka nam la, Ɛ̃berɛ kuge se'ere ti mɛteba zagehɛ la, dinɛ en de kuge sunɛ, tara ɛ̃berɛ la.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: gee Me dɛna kuge se'ere ti ba sɛrena de zuo luta, dɛna tansɛka en piti ba lɔbera. Ban piti luta la dela, ban ka nam yelehum la, wuu ban pun maahum ba ti ba iŋɛ se'em la.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Gee yamam dela buuri se'ere ti Yinɛ looe, ya dela malemaduma nabiihi, dɛna tuulum timpaberɛ, Yinɛ mɛŋa nɛreba, ti ya muulɛ tuunkãra sɛ'a en de se'em daana en yi' ya lika puan yehe niihum kãtɛ puan na la dinɛ.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Ya daa nan ka de a nɛreba, gee nananawa, ya dela Yinɛ nɛreba. Ya daa nan ka to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, gee nananawa, ya to'e nimbɔ'zɔrɛ.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma mam ka'ani ya mɛ la ninmu'urɛ, wuu yan de saama, la nɔgereba la, ti ya yehe ya mɛhi ingɔŋɔ wa kɔrehi sɛ'a en zaberɛ la ya sia la puan.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Tara ya mɛhi suŋa sɛba en dagi Yuuduma la zi'an, bala ti ban pun dikɛ ya yi' be'em duma la, ba wan nyɛ ya tunsuma la, gee pɛgera Yinɛ, a han ta wa'am na ti a gakɛ.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Daana la zuo, nane ya asaala tigere woo kpe'eŋo, yele yele wuu Timbihega nɛŋa daana se'em en sɔi ya za'a bihera la.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: bii timbihega duma ti a tum ba, ti ba namehɛ tuunfi'iha duma, gee pɛgera sɛba en tuni ma'a la.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela Yinɛ sumboolum ti ya han ita sɛla en de ma'a, ya wan ma'ɛ gɔɔnɔ la en ka mi' la nɔya.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Yamam en de Yinɛ yamehi la, vɔni ya fai, gee la bala za'a, da dikɛ ya fai bɔŋa la tuna be'em.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Na'ahɛ ya nɛra woo. Nɔŋɛ ya suurɔ seto en de sakereba la. Zoti ya Yinɛ. Na'aheri ya timbihega nɛŋa daana la.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Yamehi, nane ya ya kpɛ'ɛnduma kpe'eŋo woo la na'ahegɔ, dagi la sɛba en iti ya suŋa, gee ana ma'ma la ya la ma'a, gee sɛba me en vɛgeleni ya.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la dela yɔɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra ya, yan mi' Yinɛ la, ya han ŋmibera too, gee nyɛta namehegɔ seko en dagi tuuntɔɔ.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Ya han ŋmibera ban ŋmɛ'ɛri ya sɛla ti ya tuue puan, beni nyuurɔ en boi bini? Gee ya han ŋmibera yan tum ma'a gee ti ba namehɛ ya de zuo, Yinɛ nini tigɛ mɛ la ya.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ yi' ya la, beni zuo, Kiresta namehɛ mɛ bo' yamam ti ya biha bala niira a nama la.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: A ka tari be'em, pã'ahegɔ ka tabelɛ ze'ele a nuuren na.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: Ban wa tu' en la, a ka leregere la tuurɛ. Ban wa nameha en la, a ka lebehe ba. Gee a dikɛ a mɛŋa bo' la se'em en gakeri tuuntɔɔ la.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: Eŋa mɛŋa en zi' tu be'em a iŋa puan pumpuuredɔɔ la zuo. Be'em la en ki tumam puan la, tu nyaa wan vɔna tuuntɔɔ vom. A pɔgeleŋɔ la puan, ti ya nyɛ tibegɔ.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya daa bɔregɛ mɛ wuu piihi la, gee nananawa, ya ŋmɛregɛ lebe la dunkiina sɛka en guri ya sɛɛhi la zi'an na.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Pɔgeba, bala mɛa, sigehe ya mɛhi bo' ya mɛhi sireba, ti basɛba han pun ka nam yelehum la, ba wan nyaŋɛ ba, gee pun ka yele se'ere, gee ba pɔgeba aŋa la ma'a.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: ba han nyɛta ya zulaam vom la, la ya na'ahegɔ seko en boi ya vom puan la.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Da yeen nɔni ya ya mɛhi yiŋa, doohe ya zuto gulega, la salema lɔgerɔ yɛa, bii futo da'a en kpe'em.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: gee tugum bahɛ ya ti ya nunɔm la bɔna ya puan, ya ana ma'ma, gee tara bããlam sia vom la en de sunsɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ, tara tuulum kãtɛ Yinɛ nɛŋan la.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Sadɔɔ kuna ti diimen diimen tuulum pɔgeba la en yuum tari beere puti'irɛ Yinɛ puan la, yuum nɔna. Nana ba sireba en tari kpe'eŋo seko woo la.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: Bala ti Saara nam Aberaham, pun ta yi'ira en a daana.Ya me dela a poyɔɔhi, wuu ya han ita sɛla en ani suŋa, gee ka bahɛ ti dabeem sãɛ ya.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Sireba, bala mɛa, me zu'uhi ya ya pɔgeba, yan boi la taaba la, na'ahera fum pɔga, la eŋa en pun dɛna tɔregɔ lɔkɔ la, se'ere en sɔi la, ba wan ta to'e Yinɛ vom bo'olum la mɛ. Iti ya bala ti sɛla da gu'ura ya pu'uhegɔ.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Ba'ahegɔ, ya woo tara Sia la nɔyinɛ vom la, zɔta taaba nimbu'ɔ, nɔŋɛ ya taaba, tari ya taaba giile, gee sigehe ya mɛhi ya suya puan bo' taaba.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Da dikɛ be'em yɔ be'em, bii kɛhega yɔ kɛhega. Gee lan de se'em dela, lɛbehera sum. Ŋwana zuo ti Yinɛ yi' ya, ti ya wa'am na to'e sum la.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Sɛba en boti vom, gee bɔta ti ba nyɛ dabeha suma la, ba ta'na ba zɛlehi da tɔgera be'em, ba da tara ba nɔya pã'aha.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: Ba bire ba pɔya bahɛ be'em, gee ita suŋa. Ba ɛɛra suma'ahum, gee mu'ɛ nini dɔla bɔ.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la nini gã' la tuuntɔɔ duma zuo, ti a tuba ana kɛli la ba pu'uhegɔ. Gee Daana la nɛŋa ka loregere la sɛba en tuni be'em.

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Nananawa, ani n wan daam ya, ya han mu'ɛ nini ti ya ita sɛla en ani suŋa?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: Gee ya han pun nameha yan iti sɛla en de ma'a zuo, sum boi mɛ la ya. Da zoti ya ba, gee me da bahɛ ti la daana ya.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: gee ya suya puan, tugum malum gurɛ ya Kiresta ti a dɛna daana. Saŋa woo, maahum ya ti ya tɔgɛ po'e ya mɛhi, se'em en soke ya ti ya pa'alɛ en ya beere puti'irɛ wa, lerege ya en bããlam la Yinɛ zoolum.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: Tari ya susunɛ, ti ba han pun yi'ira ya yu'yooro, sɛba en keheri ya itesuŋɔ Kiresta puan la, vi wan di ba.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, la ani suŋa ti fum nyɛ namehegɔ fum itesuŋɔ zuo, namehegɔ la han dɛna Yinɛ sumboolum, nyana fum nyɛ namehegɔ be'em tuuma zuo.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Kiresta me wa namehɛ mɛ buyina, tumam za'a be'em zuo. Tuntɔɔ daana en namehɛ bo' sɛba en dagi tuuntɔɔ duma, ti a tari ya na bo' Yinɛ. Ba ku en mɛ ingɔŋɔ wa puan, gee ba vo'e en mɛ Sia puan.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: bilam ti a kiŋɛ ta muulɛ bo' sɛɛhi la en boi paka deo puan la.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: sɛ se'ehi yuuman yuuman ha, en yuum ka nam, Yinɛ en yuum gu'uhi la sugeri Nowa dabeha la puan, uureŋɔ la maalegɔ saŋa ha la, ti nɛreba fii wa, en de nɛreba ba nii ma'a yuum nyɛ faarɛ ko'om la puan la.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: Ko'om sua wa ti yele dina wa yuum ze'ele bo' la, en faaɛ ya bahɛ nananawa. Dagi la bu yehe la dɛgerɔ ya iŋa puan, gee bu dela Ko'om sua sɛka ti tu suhera Yinɛ ti a bo' tu putɛ'sunɛ doohe Yezu Kiresta vo'ore la puan.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: Kiresta, en bala zom saazuo ha, ta bɔna Yinɛ zuɔ nu'o bɔba, la suŋereba, kpɛ'ɛnduma, la pahi se'ehi ti ba dikɛ biŋe a nama timpaberɛ.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Simɔn Pita, en de yameŋa, dɛna Yezu Kiresta tuntuna la, en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en doohe Yinɛ la Faara Yezu Kiresta tuntɔɔnɛ la puan to'e sakerɛ en de tuulum lɔkɔ wuu tumam dinɛ la.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Zu'uhegɔ la suma'ahum en pirɛ paaɛ dɛna ya dinɛ doohe yan mi' Yinɛ, la tu Daana Yezu Kiresta se'em la.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Yinɛ sia puan paŋa la bo' tu sɛla woo ti tu bɔta, ti tu ta'am vɔna gee tara Yinɛ zoolum doohe tumam mi' se'em daana en yi' tu a mɛŋa suŋa, la a itesuŋɔ puan la.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Bala ti a doohe yele ana wa puan, bo' tu a tuulum nɔbiŋe kãra la, ti bamam puan, ya wan ta'am piiɛ ŋmɛlema sɛ'a en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo, kunkoma'ahum zuo la, gee tugum lagum tara a sia puan naanɛ la.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: Putɛ'dina zuo la, kpeŋe ya mɛŋa sadɔɔ seko woo puan, la ya tiregɛ ya sakerɛ la, la tunsuma. Ti tuunsuma la puan, mi'ilum pa'ahɛ bini.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: ti mi'ilum la puan, zulaam bɔŋa pa'ahɛ bini, ti zulaam bɔŋa la puan, ŋmibega pa'ahɛ bini, ti ŋmibega la puan, vom sumɔ pa'ahɛ bini.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: ti vom sumɔ la puan, taaba giile pa'ahɛ bini, ti taaba giile la puan, taaba nɔŋerɛ bɔna bini.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Yele ana han sirum bɔna ya puan, gee malum pa'aha ya puan, la wan gu', ti ya ka tara tɔregɔ, gee gu' sɛla en wan bahɛ ti ya kan wuna yan mi' tu Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: Se'em woo en koŋe yele ana wa, ka nyɛti zaara, dɛna fua, gee me tamena ti Yinɛ duuheri a be'em sebo en tole la bahɛ mɛ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Ŋwana zuo, n suurɔ, kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ ya nini ti ya pa'alɛ, ti Yinɛ en yi' ya gee looe ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, ya han ita yele ana, ya kan malum sɛregɛ.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Bala, ya han ita ŋwana, ya kɛ̃'a tu Daana, la tu Faara Yezu Kiresta sɔ'ɔlum la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la puan, wan yu'ɛ yɔlaam bo' ya.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: Ŋwana zuo, mam ku n puti'irɛ mɛ ti n tiihera ya yele ana wa, la yan pun mina a, gee sɛ zɛ'a yelemɛŋerɛ se'ere en wa'am ya zi'an na la.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: Mam ti'ihɛ ti la de la ma'a ti mam nan boi timpaberɛ zuo sugerɛ wa en kan yuue wa puan la, n malum tiihɛ ya.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: la mam pun mi' ti n kum dabeha ka zaaɛ, n wan yehe sugerɛ wa puan wuu tu Daana Yezu Kiresta en niiɛ de se'em bo' mam la.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: N me wan kpeŋe n mɛŋa sadɔɔ seko woo puan, ti n han ta bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo, ya wan ta'am tiiha yele ana wa, sansɛka woo puan.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Tumam ka yeen kɔ'ɔm dɔla la pumpɔreŋɔ kãra sɔlema, tumam en bahɛ ti ya baŋɛ tu Daana Yezu Kiresta paŋa, la a lɛbega na la, gee tumam dela aze'nyɛduma bo' a pankãtɛ la.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a to'e la na'ahegɔ la pɛka Yinɛ en de Sɔ la zi'an. Doohe la kɔsɛka en wa ze'ele Pɛntiireba za'a Daana la zi'an tɔgɛ bo' en na yeti, Mam Dayua en ŋwana, mam Dayɔba'arɛ, se'em ti mam nini tigɛ.

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Tumam mɛhi en daa wum kua wa en ze'ele saazuo ha tɔgɛ bahɛ na. Tumam en daa boi la en tuulum zoore la zuo.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
