local: Bala, tumam en tari purega yelehum la yelemɛŋerɛ. La wan ana ya suŋa, ya han kpeŋe ya mɛhi kɛleha yelehum wa en de butolela niira lika puan wa, gura ti beere ta yilegɛ, ti bulika belum ŋma'arɛ niira ya suya puan.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: Yia, ya mina ti purega ka boi gɔŋɔ la puan dɛna asaala mɛŋa puti'irɛ.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: beni zuo, purega ka tabelɛ ze'ele asaala sumboolum puan na, gee saaleba, buraahi la pɔgehi en ze' zulaam, ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgɛ Yinɛ zi'an doohe ba puan.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: Nananawa, mam mɛŋa en de kiima, gee dɛna aze'nyɛ daana bo' Kiresta namehegɔ la, gee me dɛna se'em en wan ta nyɛ tɔtɛ suŋa la en wan ta niiɛ na la puan. Mam kpemehe kiinduma en boi ya puan la.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Lan de ti Kiresta namehɛ mɛ ingɔŋɔ wa puan la, me bobe ya mɛhi la zaba lɔgerɔ, la putɛ' dinɛ mɛa. .

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: yeti, ya gura Yinɛ piihi la en boi ya nu'uhen la suŋa, dagena pɛregerɛ puan gee la susunɛ, wuu Yinɛ en wan boti ni ti ya ita se'em la, dagi kunkonyiim puan gee ya kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la nini la tuuma la.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Bala yan vɔi vom dabeha sɛ'a en gee timpaberɛ wa zuo wa, da voni ya bu la asaala kunkɔma'ahum, gee Yinɛ suboolum vom.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Da dikɛ ya zeero ziile sɛba en boi ya nu'uhen la, gee deni ya makerɛ bo' piihi la.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Ya pun diŋɛ dikɛ la saŋa zo'e zo'e tuna sɛla ti sɛba en dagi Yuuduma ita la. Zufagehum, kunkɔma'ahum, buka, dikãra, yaalegɔ, la baga kaabegɔ.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: Ti dunkiin naba la han ta niiɛ na, ya wan di suŋa muŋa sɛka en ka pelegere la.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: La iŋɛ nɛreba la pakerɛ ti yan ka le lagena la ba tuna tuunyooro tunɔ taaba la, bala ti ba yaala ya la.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Bala mɛa, ti yamam bumbile paalehi la me dɛna ya nana ya kiinduma la puan. Ya za'a yɛ sigehe mɛŋa ti a dɛna ya fuo, ya bɔŋa la taaba. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ zagehɛ zɛkɛ n mɛŋa daana mɛ, gee bɔ'ɔra sigehe n mɛŋa daana zu'uhegɔ.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Gee ba wan ta yele bo' eŋa se'em en maahum ti a gakɛ vɔpa la ki'induma la,se'ere zuo ti ba yaala ya la.

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: Bala, sigehe ya mɛhi Yinɛ nu'uhen, ti a zɛkɛ ya, saŋa la han ta paaɛ.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: Ŋwana zuo, ti ba muulɛ faarɛ yelehum la, pun ta paaɛ ki'induma la, ti la bahɛ ti ban pun gakɛ ba ingɔŋɔ puan wuu nɛra woo ti ba gakɛ se'em la, ba wan vɔna Sia puan wuu Yinɛ en ani se'em la.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Dikɛ ya ninmu'urɛ za'a bahɛ Yinɛ nu'uhen, beni zuo eŋa en biheri ya.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Sɛla woo ba'ahegɔ pɔri mɛ. Bala, mu'ɛ ya nini, gee ta'na ya mɛhi tara suŋa, ya pu'uhegɔ zuo.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Tana ya mɛhi tara, gee me maahum. Gee ya dindaana la en de Sagena la kɔ'ɔm mu'ɛ la nini nyaara wuu gbigenɛ la, ɛɛra nɛra ti a obe.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Sɛla en gani bala, tari ya nɔŋerɛ saŋa woo bɔ'ɔra taaba, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔŋerɛ pi'ire la be'em zo'e zo'e.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Da bo' ya en sore, sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋɛ ya sakerɛ la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ya mi' ti ya suurɔ en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, me dole la namehegɔ kuna mɛa puan tɔla.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: To'oheri taaba suŋa ya yiya puan, gee da gɔgeni ya.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Ti ya han namehɛ yuue fii wa, Yinɛ en de zu'uhegɔ za'a daana la, se'em en yi' ya ti ya wa'am na bɔna sunsɛka en ka tari ba'ahegɔ Kiresta puan la, a mɛŋa wan lebehe ze'ele ya, te'ele ya, kpemehe ya, gee sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Wuu yan de tuntuneba suma, ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ pirɛ ya la, tuni ya bɔ'ɔra taaba, la bo'olum sebo woo ti yamam ayima ayima to'e la.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: Eŋa ti paŋa dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Fum se'em en tɔgeri, dela a tɔgera wuu Yinɛ mɛŋa en tɔgeri la. Fum se'em en tuni bɔ'ɔra, dela a tuna la pansɛka za'a ti Yinɛ bo' en la, bala ti Yinɛ wan nyɛ na'ahegɔ sɛla woo puan, doohe Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa en sɔi na'ahegɔ la paŋa, wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Doohe Silevanuse, se'em ti mam nyɛ ti a dela suuɔ en de sakerɛ daana, ti mam gulehɛ gɔŋɔ girega wa, ti n kpemehe ya, gee pa'alɛ ti Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ yelemɛŋerɛ en ŋwana. Sɛ ya ze'ele kankaŋi de puan.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, da bahɛ ya ti la iŋɛ ya pakerɛ, la namehɛ tuulɛ wa en boi ya puan ti bu makɛ ya bihɛ wa, ti la ana wuu yele saanɛ bo' ya.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: Ya lageŋɔ suurɔ en boi Babilɔn, ti Yinɛ lagum looe ya la, pu'uhi ya na. Bala mɛa ti mam dayua Maki me pu'uha ya na.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Gee nyɛɛni ya, la yan lagum tuta Kiresta namehegɔ la, bala ti ya me wan ta vɔɔla la nyɛɛma, a han ta niiɛ na la a suŋa la.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Pu'uhi ya taaba la suurɔ kɔkerɛ. Suma'ahum bɔna la ya woo en boi Kiresta puan la.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Ba han tuura ya Kiresta yu'urɛ zuo, sum en dole ya, beni zuo, sia sɛka en de suŋa la, dɛna Yinɛ Sia la, en boi la ya.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Gee da bahɛ ya ti ya se'em nyɛ namehegɔ, ti a ku mɛ, bii nageyiga, tuunfi'iha, bii se'em en kɛ̃'ɛ̃ra nɛreba yɛla puan.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Gee la bala za'a, ya se'em han nyɛ namehegɔ yan de Kiresta kɔma la zuo, da bihɛ ya ti la dɛna vi' bɔ'ɔra ya, gee pɛgeri ya Yinɛ, la yan tari yu'urɛ dina wa la zuo.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Saŋa la paaɛ mɛ ti gakerɛ la pɔhɛ Yinɛ yire la puan yia, ti la han pɔhera tumam, la wan ba'ahɛ la ŋwani la sɛba en ka nam Yinɛ faarɛ yelehum la?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: Gee La han kpe'em bo' tuuntɔɔ duma ti ba nyɛ faarɛ, la wan ana la ŋwani bo' putɔpa, la be'em duma la.

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Bala, bahɛ ya ti sɛba en nameheri dɔla Yinɛ sumboolum la, dikɛ ba mɛhi bo' Naana la en de sakerɛ daana la, gee kelum ita ba sum la.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Gee nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma en yuum ihege na bɔna nɛreba la puan, wuu pumparenduma pa'aleba en wan bɔna ya puan se'em la, sɛba en wan li'ihɛ tari sageŋɔ puti'ira wa'am ya puan na. Ba wan pun ta zagehɛ ba Daana se'em en da' ba la, tari sageŋɔ wa'am ba mɛhi zuo na kalam.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: Gee bala za'a, zo'e zo'e wan dɔla ba yaalegɔ sadɔtɔ la. Pumpɔrum-pa'aleba wa zuo, nɛreba wan tuura yelemɛŋerɛ sore wa.

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
local: Ba nyika la zuo, ba wan fa' ya la ba pã'ahegɔ yelehum la. Ba namehegɔ la en purɛ bahɛ ba zuo yuuman yuuman ha la, dagi yoo, ba sageŋɔ la kan gã'arɛ.

eng: God didnt spare the angels when they sinned but cast them into the lowest level of the underworld and committed them to chains of darkness, keeping them there until the judgment.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ yuum han ka bahɛ suŋereba, ban yuum tum be'em la, gee lobe ba bahɛ bugum timpaberɛ, ba' ba la bahɛ lika puan ha, ti ba bɔna bilam gura gakerɛ.

eng: And he didnt spare the ancient world when he brought a flood on the world of ungodly people, even though he protected Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
local: Yinɛ yuum han ka bahɛ diimen diimen saŋa la, la eŋa en yuum pun faaɛ Nowa, se'em en muulɛ tuuntɔɔ vom yele la, la nɛreba bayopɔi basɛba la, gee yuum bahɛ ti ko'om di sankaŋa be'em buuri la.

eng: God condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to total destruction, reducing them to ashes as a warning to ungodly people.
local: En yuum bahɛ ti Sodom la Gomora tihi lebege tampɛgelum, sagum ba puga puga ti maalegɔ ka bɔna la, ti ba dɛna makerɛ pa'alɛ sɛla en wan wa'am be'em duma zuo na la.

eng: And he rescued righteous Lot, who was made miserable by the unrestrained immorality of unruly people. .
local: Gee yuum faaɛ Lɔti en de tuuntɔɔ nɛra, gee yuum dita ɛregɔ a buuri saŋa yaalegɔ tuuma la zuo la.

eng: While that righteous man lived among them he felt deep distress every day on account of the immoral actions he saw and heard.
These things show that the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from their trials, and how to keep the unrighteous for punishment on the Judgment Day.
local: bala, Daana la mi' en faari tuuntɔɔ duma magehegɔ puan se'em bahɛ mɛ, gee bahɛ ti be'em duma bɔna namehegɔ puan gura gakerɛ dabeherɛ la.

eng: This is especially true for those who follow after the corrupt cravings of the sinful nature and defy the Lords authority. These reckless, brash people arent afraid to insult the glorious ones.
local: yele yele wuu sɛba en tari ba ihi tuna yaalegɔ tuunsɛ'a taaba, la sɛba en ka zoti kpe'eŋo seko woo la. Ba kpe'em mɛ, ti ba suya kãbɛ, ti ba ka zɔta ba sagum Saazuo pãkara duma yu'ura.

eng: yet angels, who are stronger and more powerful, dont use insults when pronouncing the Lords judgment on them.
local: ti suŋereba en tugum kpe'em, dɛna paŋa duma, ka malum tara tuurɛ yɛla gakeri ba Daana la nɛŋan.

eng: These false teachers are like irrational animals, mere creatures of instinct, born to be captured and destroyed. They slander what they dont understand and, like animals, they will be destroyed.
local: Nɛrebana wa ani mɛ wuu duhi gɔɔnɔ la, ba yeen dɛna la bunvɔɔhi en ka tari vuurɛ, ti ba yeen dɔgɛ ba ti ba nyɔgɛ ba ku la. Ba tuurɛ la sɛla ti ba ka bɔkɛ, ti ba han ta sagum bunvɔɔhi sina la ba'ahɛ, ba wan sagum bamam me.

eng: In this way, they will receive payment for their wrongdoing. They even enjoy unruly parties in broad daylight. They are blots and blemishes, taking delight in their seductive pleasures while feasting with you.
local: Ba namehɛ la ba tuure namehegɔ. Ba dikɛ dɛ ti la dɛna la nyɛlum bo' ba, ti ba ɛɛra dita dikãra peelumen peelumen. Ba dela nɛrefi'iha, la zufagehum duma, nɔŋɛ ba dita ba dikãra mɛa la fagehum ya nɛŋan.

eng: They are always looking for someone with whom to commit adultery. They are always on the lookout for opportunities to sin. They ensnare people whose faith is weak. They have hearts trained in greed. They are under Gods curse.
local: Ba nini kɔ'ɔm biha la yaalegɔ yɛla ma'a, ka tigera be'em. Ba ta' pa'aha la sɛɛhi se'ehi en ka ze' suŋa la. Ba zamehi ba suya la nyika. Nɔpɔrɛ kɔma en de ba!.

eng: Leaving the straight path, they have gone off course, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the payment of doing wrong.
local: Ba bahɛ tuuntɔɔ sore la mɛ gee tuuna, dɔla Balaam, Boso dayua la sore la, se'em en nɔŋɛ be'em yɔɔrɛ.

eng: But Balaam was rebuked for his wrongdoing. A donkey, which has no voice, spoke with a human voice and put a stop to the prophets madness.
local: ti a mɛŋa be'em wa tahɛ en la. Boŋa en ka ta' tɔgera en wa tɔgɛ asaala kua bo' en, gu' nɔtɔgeha wa a zɔlegɔ tuunɛ la puan.

eng: These false teachers are springs without water, mists driven by the wind. The underworld has been reserved for them.
local: Nɛre bana wa dela bulekɛ'ɛhi, dɛna pika ti kusebego pɛ'ɛra kina. La dela bana ti lika paaɛ paaɛ pun maahum biŋe gura ba la.

eng: With empty, self-important speech, they use sinful cravings and unrestrained immorality to ensnare people who have only just escaped life with those who have wandered from the truth.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ta' pageri la page yooro, tara ba yaalegɔ kunkoma'ahum la, pa'ahɛ nɛresɛba en nan kɔ'ɔm piiɛ sɛba en boi be'em puan la.

eng: These false teachers promise freedom, but they themselves are slaves of immorality; whatever overpowers you, enslaves you.
local: Ba biŋere ba nɔya, ti fai wan bɔna, gee ba mɛhi dela ŋmɛlema tuuma da'aba. Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛreba dela da'aba bo' sɛla woo en ta' ba.

eng: If people escape the moral filth of this world through the knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ, then get tangled up in it again and are overcome by it, they are worse off than they were before.
local: La han dɛna ti, ban piiɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo galeŋɔ la, doohe ban mi' tu Daana, la tu faara Yezu Kiresta la, ba le lebe lu la ba puan, ti la nyaŋɛ ba, ti ba'ahegɔ puan, ba pun nyaa yalegɛ nuurɛ gana ba yia la mɛ.

eng: It would be better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having come to know it, to turn back from the holy commandment entrusted to them.
local: La ani ni suŋa bo' ba, ti ba pun da baŋɛ ni tuuntɔɔ sore la, nyana ban baŋɛ dɛ gee biri ba poore bahɛ fa'a ze'elego la ti ba yuum dikɛ bo' ba la.

eng: They demonstrate the truth of the proverb: A dog returns to its own vomit, and a washed sow wallows in the mud.
local: La iŋɛ ba doohe la yelemɛŋerɛ tɔgɛ biŋere la en yeti, Baa lebere la ka tiiro puan la, la, ba sɔɔri kurikuri ti ka lebe ta lirum la bɛgerɔ puan.

eng: My little children, Im writing these things to you so that you dont sin. But if you do sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
local: N kɔmbibehi wa, n gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya ti, la bahɛ ti ya da tara tuure. Gee se'em han tuue, tu tari nupu'ŋmɛ'ɛra mɛ en boi la Sɔ la, Yezu Kiresta, tuuntɔɔ daana la.

eng: He is Gods way of dealing with our sins, not only ours but the sins of the whole world.
local: Eŋa en de akaabɛ to'e kaabegɔ bo' tumam tuure la, dagi tumam ma'a tuure la, gee tiŋa wa zuo za'a tuure.

eng: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
local: Nananawa, yele dina wa zuo ti tu wan baŋɛ ti tu mi' en paaɛ paaɛ, tu han nana a nɔze'elego la.

eng: The one who claims, I know him, while not keeping his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in this person.
local: Se'em woo en yeti, Mam mi' en, gee ka nana a nɔze'elego la, dela pumparema, ti nɛre-ina daana, yelemɛŋerɛ ka boi a puan.

eng: But the love of God is truly perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know we are in him.
local: Gee se'em woo en nane a yelehum la, yelemɛŋerɛ, nɛre-ina daana puan, Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ la bi'ɛ paaɛ mɛ a puan. Dina ti tu wan baŋɛ ti tu boi a puan.

eng: The one who claims to remain in him ought to live in the same way as he lived.
local: Se'em woo en yeti, Mam boi a puan. Dela a ita itegɔ wuu eŋa en iti se'em la.

eng: Dear friends, Im not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the message you heard.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, n ka guleheri nɔze'elego paalega bɔ'ɔra ya, gee nɔze'elego kɛka la ti ya pun tara de pi'ileŋen na la. Nɔze'elego kɛka la dela, yelehum la ti ya pun wum la.

eng: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.
local: Gee n kelum guleha la nɔze'elepaalega bɔ'ɔra ya, en de yelemɛŋerɛ a puan, la yamam me puan, beni zuo, lika la ye' tɔla mɛ, gee ti yelemɛŋerɛ niihum la pun niira na.

eng: The one who claims to be in the light while hating a brother or sister is in the darkness even now.
local: Se'em woo en yeti, Mam boi la niihum la puan, gee yaaɛ a suuɔ, kelum bɔna la lika la puan.

eng: The person loving a brother and sister stays in the light, and there is nothing in the light that causes a person to stumble.
local: Se'em woo en nɔŋɛ a suuɔ, boi la niihum la puan, ti nɛre-ina daana, sɛla ka boi wan bahɛ ti a gɛ̃ta.

eng: But the person who hates a brother or sister is in the darkness and lives in the darkness, and doesnt know where to go because the darkness blinds the eyes.
local: Gee se'em woo en yaaɛ a suuɔ boi la lika la puan, kina lika la puan, gee ka mina sose'ere ti a wan doohe, beni zuo, lika la tari la fɔɔnɛ na ki'iha a ninin.

eng: Little children, Im writing to you because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus name.
local: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la yamam, n kɔmbibehi wa, beni zuo, Yinɛ bahɛ ya tuure bo' ya a yu'urɛ la zuo la.

eng: Parents, Im writing to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, Im writing to you because you have conquered the evil one.
local: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam, sɔduma, beni zuo, ya mi' en, se'em en pun bɔna pi'ileŋen na la. N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam bumbile-pɔɔlegɔ, beni zuo, ya nyaŋɛ be'em daana la mɛ.

eng: Little children, I write to you because you know the Father. Parents, I write to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, I write to you because you are strong, the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
local: N gulehɛ bo' yamam kɔma, beni zuo, yamam mi' sɔ la. N gulehɛ bo' yamam sɔduma, beni zuo, yamam mi' se'em en pun bɔna pi'ileŋo puan ha la. N gulehɛ bo' yamam bumbile paalehi, beni zuo, yamam tari paŋa, ti Yinɛ yelehum la bɔna ya puan, ti ya me nyaŋɛ Sagena la.

eng: Dont love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in them.
local: Da nɔŋɛ ya tiŋa wa zuo, bii yelesɛ'a en boi ka puan wa. Se'em han nɔŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo, Sɔ la nɔŋerɛ ka boi eŋa daana puan.

eng: Everything that is in the world—the craving for whatever the body feels, the craving for whatever the eyes see and the arrogant pride in ones possessions—is not of the Father but is of the world.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, sɛla woo en boi tiŋa wa zuo la, ingɔŋɔ wa kɔrehi, nini wa kɔrehi, vom wa puan ya'anɛ, ka ze'ele Sɔ la zi'an na, gee tiŋa wa zuo.

eng: And the world and its cravings are passing away, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
local: Tiŋa wa zuo la ka kɔrehi la ye' tɔla mɛ, gee sɛba en tuni Yinɛ suboolum wan bɔna wuuha.

eng: Little children, it is the last hour. Just as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. This is how we know it is the last hour.
local: N kɔma wa, la dela ba'ahegɔ saŋa. Yan pun wum ti Kiresta dima daana la kini na la, bala mɛa ti nananawa, Kiresta dima duma zo'e zo'e wa'am na. Ŋwana puan ti tu baŋɛ ti la dela ba'ahegɔ saŋa la.

eng: They went out from us, but they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But by going out from us, they showed they all are not part of us.
local: Ba ze'ele la tumam zi'an yehe, gee ba daa dagi tumam. Ba han deni la tumam puan, ba wan yee ni bɔna la tɔ. Gee ban yehe la, ba bahɛ ti la niiɛ ti ba se'em ka de tumam puan.

eng: But you have an anointing from the holy one, and all of you know the truth.
local: Gee Fa'a Daana la en sɔi yamam, ti ya za'a mina dina wa.

eng: I dont write to you because you dont know the truth but because you know it. You know that no lie comes from the truth.
local: Mam en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra ya wa, dagi la ya ka mi' yelemɛŋerɛ la, gee ya mi' de mɛ, gee mina ti pumpɔreŋɔ ka ze'eti yelemɛŋerɛ puan na.

eng: Who is the liar? Isnt it the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This person is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
local: Ani en de pumparema la? Han dagena se'em en zagehɛ ti Yezu dagi Kiresta la. Ena daana en de Kiresta dima la, se'em en zagehɛ Yinɛ en de Sɔ, la Dayua la.

eng: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father, but the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
local: Se'em woo en zagehɛ Dayua la, kan tara Sɔ la. Se'em woo en sakɛ bo' Dayua la tari Sɔ la me mɛ.

eng: As for you, what you heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you will also remain in relationship to the Son and in the Father.
local: Gee han dɛna yamam bahɛ ya ti sɛla ti fum wum pi'ileŋen ha la bɔna fum puan. Sɛla woo ti fum wum pi'ileŋen ha la han bɔna fum puan, fum wan bɔna la Dayua la, la Sɔ la.

eng: This is the promise that he himself gave us: eternal life.
local: Sɛla ti Yinɛ mɛŋa biŋe nuurɛ la tɔ n ŋwana, Yinɛ vom sumɔ n kamba'ahɛ.

eng: I write these things to you about those who are attempting to deceive you.
local: N gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra ya la sɛba en wan pã'ahɛ ya la zuo.

eng: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains on you, and you dont need anyone to teach you the truth. But since his anointing teaches you about all things , remain in relationship to him just as he taught you.
local: Han dɛna yamam, bi'ihegɔ seko ti ya to'e a zi'an la, boi ya puan mɛ, bala, ya ka eere nɛra ti a pa'alɛ ya. Gee a bi'ihegɔ la en pa'ali ya sɛla woo la, ti la me dɛna sira gee dagena pumpɔreŋɔ, wuu a bi'ihegɔ la en pun pa'alɛ ya se'em la, boni ya a puan.

eng: And now, little children, remain in relationship to Jesus, so that when he appears we can have confidence and not be ashamed in front of him when he comes.
local: Gee Nananawa n kɔmbibehi wa, boni ya a puan, ti a han ta niiɛ na, tu wan tara inkpeŋere ti vi ka tara tɔ a nɛŋan, a han ta wa'am na.

eng: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that every person who practices righteousness is born from him.
local: Ya han mina ti Yinɛ dela tuuntɔɔ, ya wan mina ti se'em woo en iti sɛla en de tuuntɔɔ, eŋa en dɔgɛ en.

eng: From the elder. To the chosen gentlewoman and her children, whom I truly love.
local: Kiima la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra pɔgesɛka ti Yinɛ looe la, la ka kɔma, ti mam nɔŋɛ ba yelemɛŋerɛ la puan. Dagi mam ma'a, la sɛba woo en me mi' yelemɛŋerɛ la.

eng: because of the truth that remains with us and will be with us forever.
local: Yelemɛŋerɛ la en boi tu puan la zuo, gee wan bɔna la tu wuuha la zuo.

eng: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, will be ours who live in truth and love.
local: Zu'uhegɔ, nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la suma'ahum en ze'ele Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la Yezu Kiresta en de Sɔ la dayua la zi'an na wan bɔna la tɔ, yelemɛŋerɛ la nɔŋerɛ puan.

eng: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as we had been commanded by the Father.
local: Mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ la mam en nyɛ fum kɔma la basɛba en kinɛ yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, wuu Sɔ la en bo' tu nuurɛ se'em la.

eng: Now, dear friends, I am requesting that we love each other. Its not as though Im writing a new command to you, but its one we have had from the beginning.
local: Gee nananawa, pɔge mɛnka wa, mam yeen suhera fum mɛ gee dagi la n gulehɛ la nɔze'elego paalega bɔ'ɔra hɔ, gee se'ere ti tu tara pi'ileŋo na la yeti, bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

eng: This is love: that we live according to his commands. This is the command that you heard from the beginning: live in love.
local: Nɔŋerɛ n ŋwana, ti tu vɔna dɔla a nɔze'elego la, nɔze'elego la dela, wuu fum wum de se'em pi'ileŋo na la, dɔla a.

eng: Many deceivers have gone into the world who do not confess that Jesus Christ came as a human being. This kind of person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
local: Pumpã'ahum duma zo'e zo'e en wa'am tiŋa wa zuo na, sɛba en ka sakɛ ti Yezu Kiresta wa'am inganɛ puan na. Nɛre-ina daana dela pumpã'aha, la Kiresta dindaana la.

eng: Watch yourselves so that you dont lose what weve worked for but instead receive a full reward.
local: Biheri ya mɛhi suŋa, la ya da bɔregɛ sɛla ti tumam tum bo' ya ti ya tara la, gee ya wan to'e yɔɔrɛ la za'a.

eng: Anyone who goes too far and does not continue in the teaching about Christ does not have God. Whoever continues in this teaching has both the Father and the Son.
local: Se'em woo en ka dole Kiresta pa'alegɔ la gee zɔɛ ku, ka tari Yinɛ a puan. Se'em woo en dole pa'alegɔ la, gee bɔna ku puan, tari Sɔ la mɛ, la Dayua la mɛ a puan.

eng: Whoever comes to you who does not affirm this teaching should neither be received nor welcomed into your home.
local: Da to'e se'em en wa'am fum yire na, bii fum kã sum bo' se'em en wa'am fum zi'an na gee ka tara pa'alegɔ kuna.

eng: because welcoming people like that is the same thing as sharing in their evil actions.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, fum han kan' sum bo' nɛre-ina daana, fum lagum tuna la a daana be'em la mɛ.

eng: I have a lot to tell you. I dont want to use paper and ink, but I hope to visit you and talk with you face-to-face, so that our joy can be complete.
local: La mam en pun tara yɛla zo'e zo'e ti n gulehɛ bo' ya la, n kan gulehɛ a gɔŋɔ puan, gee n iŋɛ puti'irɛ ti n wa'am ya zi'an na tɔgɛ bo' ya, ya nini, ti tu pupeelum la pirɛ.

eng: Your chosen sisters children greet you.
local: Fum suuɔ kɔma sɛba ti Yinɛ looe la pu'uhi fum na.

eng: My dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. I have written both letters to stir up your sincere understanding with a reminder.
local: Nananawa n zɔduma wa, n yi'i gɔŋɔ en ŋwana ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra ya na wa. N gɔnɔ wa puan, n zigeri ti n gbirege la ya putɛ' sunɛ la ti ya tara la, doohe mam tiiheri ya.

eng: I want you to recall what the holy prophets foretold as well as what the Lord and savior commanded through your apostles.
local: ti ya tɛra yelehum la en tɔgɛ bo' ya yuuman yuuman ha doohe tuulum nɔtɔgeheba la puan la, la nɔsɛ'a ti tu Daana, la tu Faara la tɔgɛ doohe a tuntuneba puan la.

eng: Most important, know this: in the last days scoffers will come, jeering, living by their own cravings.
local: Yia, ya za'a dela ya bɔkɛ ti, ba'ahegɔ dabeha la puan, sinsɛhi wan wa'am na, sɛna la ba mɛhi kunkoma'ahum.

eng: and saying, Where is the promise of his coming? After all, nothing has changed—not since the beginning of creation, nor even since the ancestors died.
local: gee yɛta ti A lɛbega na nɔbiŋere la yee la bɛ? Tu sɔyaabe duma en tole na ki ba'ahɛ la, sɛla woo kelum bɔna la lan yuum boi se'em timpaberɛ wa naaŋɔ puan na la.

eng: But they fail to notice that, by Gods word, heaven and earth were formed long ago out of water and by means of water.
local: Ba kɔ'ɔm mina mɛ gee zagehɛ yelemɛŋerɛ dina wa, ti Yinɛ yelehum la en naam saazuo wa yuuman yuuman la timpaberɛ wa, la ko'om wa en gã' wa.

eng: And it was through these that the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
local: ko'bumɔ mɛa en yuum di timpaberɛ wa, ti ka za'a sagum la.

eng: But by the same word, heaven and earth are now held in reserve for fire, kept for the Judgment Day and destruction of ungodly people.
local: Yelehum bumɔ mɛa en tɔgɛ biŋe, ti zina beere saazuo la timpaberɛ wa gura ba sige bugum puan la. Ba gã' gura la gakerɛ dabeherɛ la, la sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ la sageŋɔ dabeherɛ la.

eng: Dont let it escape your notice, dear friends, that with the Lord a single day is like a thousand years and a thousand years are like a single day.
local: Gee da bahɛ ya yelemɛŋerɛ dina wa, n sunɔŋerɛ duma, ti Daana la zi'an, daan yinɛ ani mɛ wuu yuuma tuha la, ti yuuma tuha ana wuu daan yinɛ la.

eng: The Lord isnt slow to keep his promise, as some think of slowness, but he is patient toward you, not wanting anyone to perish but all to change their hearts and lives.
local: Daana la ka gu'uni a nɔbiŋere la, wuu sɛba en ti'ihɛ gu'uŋɔ se'em la, gee a di'ihe la suure la ya, ka bɔta ti se'em bahɛ yoo, gee ya za'a wa'am na tee.

eng: But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will pass away with a dreadful noise, the elements will be consumed by fire, and the earth and all the works done on it will be exposed.
local: Gee Daana dabeherɛ la yeti la wa'am me na wuu nageyiga la, ti saazuo wa mɛlum la voole kãtɛ, ti bugum nyɛlegɛ lɔgerɔ, ti sɛla woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo nyɛlegɛ la bugum.

eng: Since everything will be destroyed in this way, what sort of people ought you to be? You must live holy and godly lives.
local: Yele ana za'a en wan ta nyɛlegɛ ŋwana la, yan wan dɛna nɛre sɛba taaba dela, ya vɔna Yinɛ tuulum vom, la Yinɛ zoolum vom.

eng: waiting for and hastening the coming day of God. Because of that day, the heavens will be destroyed by fire and the elements will melt away in the flames.
local: gura, gee bɔta ti Yinɛ kinɛ na dabeherɛ la wa'am kalam na, beni zuo, de daarɛ ti saazuo wan taaɛ bugum mɛlum, ti sɛla woo wan nyɛlegɛ la bugum la?.

eng: But according to his promise we are waiting for a new heaven and a new earth, where righteousness is at home.
local: Gee, doohe a nɔbiŋere la puan, tu guri la saazuo paalega, la timpaberɛ paalega, sɛka puan ti tuuntɔɔ vom bɔna la.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, make every effort to be found by him in peace—pure and faultless.
local: Bala, n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, yan guri yele ana wa la, zigɛ ya mɛ ti ya nyɛ en la suma'ahum, ti dɛkɔ ka lama ya, bii tuure.

eng: Consider the patience of our Lord to be salvation, just as our dear friend and brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.
local: gee biheri ya tu Daana sugeri la ti bu dɛna tu faarɛ zuo. Bala mɛa ti tu sunɔŋerɛ suuɔ Pɔɔl guleha bɔ'ɔra ya, la yɛm sebo ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' en la.

eng: speaking of these things in all his letters. Some of his remarks are hard to understand, and people who are ignorant and whose faith is weak twist them to their own destruction, just as they do the other scriptures.
local: tɔgera yele ana wa yele wuu en iti se'em a gɔnɔ woo puan la. Yɛla asɛ'a boi a gɔnɔ la puan ti a bɔkerɛ kpe'em, ti sɛba en ka mi' la, la sɛba en ka ze' bɔbeyinɛ la ŋmɛreha a, tari sageŋɔ kina ba mɛhi zuto na, wuu ban pun ita vom gɔŋɔ la luga a sɛ'a se'em la.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, since you have been warned in advance, be on guard so that you arent led off course into the error of sinful people, and lose your own safe position.
local: Ŋwana zuo la, yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mam en te'ele ka'am ya la zuo, bihɛ ya suŋa ti yalum duma wa tuure la da ve'e ya kiŋɛ ti ya da ta'am ze'ele ya mɛŋa.

eng: Instead, grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ. To him belongs glory now and forever. Amen.
local: Gee tugum zo'ore ya zu'uhegɔ, la ya mi'ilum tu Daana la tu faara Yezu Kiresta puan. Eŋa ti pɛka za'a dɛna a dinɛ, nananawa ta paara dabeherɛ se'ere en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la.

eng: We announce to you what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have seen and our hands handled, about the word of life.
local: Tumam muuli la se'em en daa pun bɔna pi'ileŋen ha yele, se'em yele ti tumam wum, se'em ti tumam nyɛ la tu nini, se'em ti tu bihɛ, gee gurɛ en la tu nu'uhi. Yelemɛŋerɛ, eŋa en de vom yelehum la.

eng: The life was revealed, and we have seen, and we testify and announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us.
local: Vom buna wa, Yinɛ daa niiɛ bu mɛ na, ti tumam nyɛ bɔ, dɛna bu aze'nyɛduma, muula bu bɔ'ɔra ya, Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kan ba'ahɛ wa en daa boi la Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nyaa niiɛ bɔ bo' tu.

eng: What we have seen and heard, we also announce it to you so that you can have fellowship with us. Our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
local: Le yɛ'ɛha tumam muuli la sɛla ti tu nyɛ gee wum bɔ'ɔra ya, ti yamam me bɔna la tɔ kankaŋi, gee yelemɛŋerɛ, tu boi mɛ la Yinɛ en de Sɔ la, la a Dayua Yezu Kiresta la kankaŋi.

eng: We are writing these things so that our joy can be complete.
local: Tu gulehɛ yele ana wa, ti la bahɛ ti tu nyɛɛma en pirɛ.

eng: This is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light and there is no darkness in him at all.
local: Sɔɔleŋa sɛka ti tumam wum a zi'an n ŋwana ti tu muula bɔ'ɔra ya wa ti, Yinɛ dela niihum, ti a puan, lika ka boi la fii wa me.

eng: If we claim, We have fellowship with him, and live in the darkness, we are lying and do not act truthfully.
local: Tu han yeti tumam boi mɛ la en kankaŋi, gee bɔna lika puan, tu pareni mɛ, gee ka tuna yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: But if we live in the light in the same way as he is in the light, we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from every sin.
local: Gee tu han vɔna niihum la kankaŋi, wuu eŋa mɛŋa en boi niihum puan se'em la, tu wan bɔna taaba kankaŋi, ti a Dayua Yezu ziim la pɛɛra tɔ, tuure woo puan bahera.

eng: If we claim, We dont have any sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
local: Tu han yeti tu ka tari tuure, tu pã'ahɛ la tu mɛhi, ti yelemɛŋerɛ ka bɔna tu puan.

eng: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from everything weve done wrong.
local: Gee tu han purɛ tu tuure la, eŋa se'em daana en de yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuntɔɔ daana la, wan di sugeri bo' tu tuure la gee pee tu yehe sɛla woo en dagi ma'a puan.

eng: If we claim, We have never sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.
local: Tu han yeti tumam ka tari tuure, tu iŋɛ en la pumparuma, ti a yelehum la ka bɔna tu puan.

eng: See what kind of love the Father has given to us in that we should be called Gods children, and that is what we are! Because the world didnt recognize him, it doesnt recognize us.
local: Bihɛ ya nɔŋerɛ kãtɛ se'ere en boi de tɔka ti Sɔ la dikɛ bo' tu, ti ba yi'ira tumam Yinɛ kɔma. Ɛ̃ɛ tumam en sirum de sɛba n bala. Se'ere en sɔi ti tiŋa wa ka mina tu la dela, tiŋa wa yuum ka ka mi' en.

eng: Dear friends, now we are Gods children, and it hasnt yet appeared what we will be. We know that when he appears we will be like him because well see him as he is.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, tu dela Yinɛ kɔma nananawa. Tumam n wan ta ana se'em nan ka niiɛ. Tumam n mi' sɛla dela dina wa, Kiresta han ta niiɛ na, tu wan ana wuu en la, tu wan nyɛ en wuu en ani se'em la.

eng: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves even as he is pure.
local: Se'em woo en tari putɛ'dina Yezu Kiresta puan, peere la a mɛŋa wuu eŋa Kiresta en de fa'a se'em la.

eng: Every person who practices sin commits an act of rebellion, and sin is rebellion.
local: Se'em woo en tuue me galum mɛ, tuure dela galeŋɔ.

eng: You know that he appeared to take away sins, and there is no sin in him.
local: Ya mi' ti Kiresta niiɛ na ti a dikɛ la tuure, ti eŋa puan ti tuure ka bɔna.

eng: Every person who remains in relationship to him does not sin. Any person who sins has not seen him or known him.
local: Nɛra ka boi bɔna a puan gee tuura. Se'em en tuure ka nyɛ en, bii a mina en.

eng: Little children, make sure no one deceives you. The person who practices righteousness is righteous, in the same way that Jesus is righteous.
local: Kɔmbibehi wa, da bahɛ ya ti nɛra nɛra pa'ahɛ ya. Se'em woo en tuni sɛla en de tuuntɔɔ dela tuuntɔɔ daana, woo eŋa Kiresta en de tuuntɔɔ daana se'em la.

eng: The person who practices sin belongs to the devil, because the devil has been sinning since the beginning. Gods Son appeared for this purpose: to destroy the works of the devil.
local: Se'em woo en tari tuure dela Sagena la bia, se'ere en sɔi la, Sagena la pun tuura mɛ la bala pi'ileŋen na. Yinɛ Dayua la niiɛ na la yele dina zuo, ti a lorege Sagena la tuuma za'a za'a.

eng: Those born from God dont practice sin because Gods DNA remains in them. They cant sin because they are born from God.
local: Se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ en kɔ'ɔm ka bɔna tuure puan, beni zuo, Yinɛ naanɛ en boi a puan, a kan ta'am bɔna tuure, beni zuo, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ en.

eng: This is how Gods children and the devils children are apparent: everyone who doesnt practice righteousness is not from God, including the person who doesnt love a brother or sister.
local: Yinɛ kɔma, la Sagena la kɔma en niiɛ ba mɛhi se'em en ŋwana. Se'em woo en ka tuni sɛla en de tuuntɔɔ ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, bii fum se'em woo en ka nɔŋɛ fum suurɔ.

eng: This is the message that you heard from the beginning: love each other.
local: Bala, yelehum la n ŋwana ti ya wum pi'ileŋo na la, ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

eng: Dont behave like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he kill him? He killed him because his own works were evil, but the works of his brother were righteous.
local: Tu da ana wuu Keen se'em en ze'ele Sagena la zi'an na, ku a mɛa suuɔ la. Beni zuo ti a ku en? Se'ere zuo, a mɛŋa tuuma la dela be'em, gee ti a suuɔ la tuuma dɛna tuuntɔɔ.

eng: Dont be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
local: Da ze' ya yɛli n suurɔ, la tiŋa wa zuo en yaaɛ ya la.

eng: We know that we have transferred from death to life, because we love the brothers and sisters. The person who does not love remains in death.
local: Tu mi' ti, tumam zɔɛ la kum paaɛ vom, beni zuo tu nɔŋɛ la taaba. Se'em woo en ka nɔŋɛ, ŋwɔni ti a ki mɛ n bala.

eng: Everyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know that murderers dont have eternal life residing in them.
local: Bala se'em woo en yaaɛ ba suuɔ dela kuureba, ya mi ti kuureba ka tari Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kan ba'ahɛ la.

eng: This is how we know love: Jesus laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
local: Tu mi' nɔŋerɛ la dina wa puan ti, a dikɛ la a vom biŋe bo' tumam, ti tumam me dɛna la tu dikɛ tu vom biŋe bo' tu taaba.

eng: But if someone has material possessions and sees a brother or sister in need but refuses to help—how can the love of God dwell in a person like that?.
local: Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ boi se'em daana en tari tiŋa wa lɔgerɔ la ŋwani gee nyɛ a suuɔ en boti suŋɛ, gee kɔ'ɔm kãbɛ suure zagehɛ ti a suŋɛ en.

eng: Little children, lets not love with words or speech but with action and truth.
local: Kɔmbibehi wa, da bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ taaba la nuurɛ bii tugum ma'a, gee tu nɔŋɛ ya taaba la, yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuma.

eng: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts in Gods presence.
local: Dina puan ti tu wan, mina ti tu ze'ele la yelemɛŋerɛ puan na, ti tu han wa'am a nɛŋan na, tu suya wa ma'ɛ lɔge lɔge.

eng: Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts and knows all things.
local: Ti suya han ni ta zɛregɛ tɔ, Yinɛ gani tu suya la mɛ, gee mina sɛla woo.

eng: Dear friends, if our hearts dont condemn us, we have confidence in relationship to God.
local: Sunɔŋerɛ duma, tu suya han ka zɛregɛ tɔ, tu wan ta'am ze'ele Yinɛ nɛŋan ti dabeem kana.

eng: We receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
local: gee to'e a zi'an sɛla woo ti tu soke, beni zuo, tu nane a nɔze'elego la mɛ, gee ita sɛla n peere a puurɛ.

eng: This is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love each other as he commanded us.
local: A nɔze'elego la n ŋwana, ti tu nam a Dayua Yezu Kiresta yu'urɛ la, gee nɔŋɛ taaba, wuu eŋa en bo' tu nuurɛ se'em la.

eng: Those who keep his commandments dwell in God and God dwells in them. This is how we know that he dwells in us, because of the Spirit he has given us.
local: Se'em woo en nane a nɔze'elego la boi la a puan, ti eŋa me bɔna a puan. Dina puan ti tu mina ti a boi la tu mɛ, doohe la a Fa'a Sia la ti a bo' tu la.

eng: Dear friends, dont believe every spirit. Test the spirits to see if they are from God because many false prophets have gone into the world.
local: Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, da nam ya sia woo, gee magehɛ sɛɛhi la bihera nyaa, ba ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma zo'e zo'e en kɛ̃ tiŋa wa zuo.

eng: This is how you know if a spirit comes from God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come as a human is from God.
local: Dina puan ti ya wan mina Yinɛ Sia la. Sɛsɛka woo en purɛ ti Yezu Kiresta wa'am me na la ingɔŋɔ, ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na.

eng: and every spirit that doesnt confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and is now already in the world.
local: gee sɛsɛka woo en ka purɛ ti Yezu Kiresta wa'am me na la ingɔŋɔ ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na, sɛkana en de Kiresta dima la sia la ti ya wum ti ka kini na la, ka pun diŋɛ bɔna tiŋa wa zuo mɛ.

eng: You are from God, little children, and you have defeated these people because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
local: Kɔmbibehi wa, yamam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, gee nyaŋɛ ba, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em n boi ya puan la gani se'em en boi tiŋa wa zuo la mɛ.

eng: They are from the world. So they speak from the worlds point of view and the world listens to them.
local: Ba ze'ele la tiŋa wa zuo na, bala la, ban yeti sɛla dela tiŋa wa zuo yɛla, ti tiŋa wa zuo me kɛleha ba.

eng: We are from God. The person who knows God listens to us. Whoever is not from God doesnt listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
local: Gee tumam ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, se'em woo en mi' Yinɛ kelehe tumam mɛ, se'em woo n ka ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na ka kelehe tu. Dina puan ti tu mina yelemɛŋerɛ sia, la pã'ahegɔ sia la.

eng: Dear friends, lets love each other, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born from God and knows God.
local: N Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, bahɛ ya ti tu nɔŋɛ ya taaba, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔŋerɛ ze'eti la Yinɛ zi'an na. Se'em woo en tari nɔŋerɛ, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ en, ti a mina Yinɛ.

eng: The person who doesnt love does not know God, because God is love.
local: Se'em woo en ka tari nɔŋerɛ ka mi' Yinɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ dela nɔŋerɛ.

eng: This is how the love of God is revealed to us: God has sent his only Son into the world so that we can live through him.
local: Yinɛ niiɛ a nɔŋerɛ pa'alɛ tɔ la sadɔɔ kuna puan. Yinɛ tum la a mɛŋa dayua ba'arɛ bahɛ tiŋa wa zuo na, ti tu wan vɔna vom sumɔ en de Yinɛ vom, dɔla a puan.

eng: This is love: it is not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that deals with our sins.
local: Dina wa puan ti nɔŋerɛ bɔna, dagi ti tumam en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, gee eŋa en nɔŋɛ tɔ, gee tum a Dayua ti a wa'am na dɛna akaabɛ to'e kaabegɔ bo' tumam tuure.

eng: Dear friends, if God loved us this way, we also ought to love each other.
local: N Sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, Yinɛ en ba' nɔŋɛ tumam ŋwana wa la, tumam me dela tu nɔŋɛ taaba.

eng: No one has ever seen God. If we love each other, God remains in us and his love is made perfect in us.
local: Se'em nan ka tabelɛ nyɛ Yinɛ. Gee tu han nɔŋɛ taaba, Yinɛ boi tu puan mɛ, ti a nɔŋerɛ la niiri mɛ suŋa tu puan.

eng: This is how we know we remain in him and he remains in us, because he has given us a measure of his Spirit.
local: Dina puan ti tu mina ti, tu boi la a puan, ti eŋa me bɔna tu puan, se'ere en sɔi la, a bo' tu a Fa'a Sia la.

eng: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the savior of the world.
local: Tu me nyɛ de mɛ, gee muula ti, Yinɛ en de Sɔ la tum la a Dayua ti a dɛna Faara bo' tiŋa wa zuo.

eng: If any of us confess that Jesus is Gods Son, God remains in us and we remain in God.
local: Yinɛ boi mɛ la se'em woo en purɛ ti Yezu dela Yinɛ Dayua, gee ti a daana me bɔna Yinɛ puan kankaŋi.

eng: We have known and have believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who remain in love remain in God and God remains in them.
local: Bala tumam mi' gee me nam nɔŋerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ tara bɔ'ɔra tu la mɛ. Yinɛ dela nɔŋerɛ, se'em woo en boi nɔŋerɛ puan, boi la Yinɛ puan, ti Yinɛ me bɔna a daana puan.

eng: This is how love has been perfected in us, so that we can have confidence on the Judgment Day, because we are exactly the same as God is in this world.
local: A nɔŋerɛ la niiɛ suŋa suŋa tu puan la ŋwana. Ti dabeem kan ta bɔna tu puan la fii me gakerɛ daarɛ la, beni zuo wuu eŋa en ani se'em la, tumam me ani la bala tiŋa wa zuo.

eng: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear expects punishment. The person who is afraid has not been made perfect in love.
local: Dabeem ka boi nɔŋerɛ puan, gee nɔŋerɛ en pirɛ sa'ahɛ la dabeem. Dabeem dole mɛ la sɛla en de tuberɛ dɔlegerɛ, ti se'em woo en tari dabeem nan ka paaɛ nɔŋerɛ en pirɛ ba'ahɛ.

eng: We love because God first loved us.
local: Tumam tari nɔŋerɛ, beni zuo, eŋa en nɔŋɛ tu yia.

eng: Those who say, I love God and hate their brothers or sisters are liars. After all, those who dont love their brothers or sisters whom they have seen can hardly love God whom they have not seen!.
local: Se'em han yeti, Mam nɔŋɛ Yinɛ gee yaaɛ a suuɔ, dela pumparema. Se'ere en sɔi la, ŋwani ti eŋa ka nɔŋɛ a suuɔ ti a nyɛta la nini, gee wan ta'am nɔŋɛ Yinɛ se'em ti a ka nyɛta?.

eng: This commandment we have from him: Those who claim to love God ought to love their brother and sister also.
local: Nɔze'elego la ti tu to'e a zi'an la dela dina wa. Ti se'em en nɔŋɛ Yinɛ, dela a nɔŋɛ a suuɔ me.

eng: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born from God. Whoever loves someone who is a parent loves the child born to the parent.
local: Se'em woo en nam ti Yezu en de Kiresta la, Yinɛ en dɔgɛ a daana, se'em woo en nɔŋɛ dɔgera la, me nɔŋɛ se'em ti a dɔgɛ la mɛ.

eng: This is how we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep Gods commandments.
local: Dina puan ti tu mina ti, tu nɔŋɛ Yinɛ kɔma, tu han nɔŋɛ Yinɛ gee nana a nɔze'elego la.

eng: This is the love of God: we keep Gods commandments. Gods commandments are not difficult.
local: Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ en de se'em ŋwana, ti tu nana a nɔze'elego la. A nɔze'elego la me ka tibegɛ vilegera.

eng: because everyone who is born from God defeats the world. And this is the victory that has defeated the world: our faith.
local: se'ere n sɔi la, se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ nyaŋeri tiŋa wa mɛ. Nyaŋerɛ se'ere en nyaŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo n ŋwana, tu sakerɛ.

eng: Who defeats the world? Isnt it the one who believes that Jesus is Gods Son?.
local: Bala, Ani en de en nyaŋɛ tiŋa wa zuo, han dagena fum se'em nam ti Yezu dela Yinɛ Dayua?.

eng: This is the one who came by water and blood: Jesus Christ. Not by water only but by water and blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
local: Yezu Kiresta kana en bala doohe ko'om la ziim puan wa'am na na la. Dagi la a doohe la ko'om la ma'a puan wa'am na, gee ko'om la la ziim. Fa'a Sia la en de aze' nyɛ daana, se'ere en sɔi la, Sia la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: The three are testifying.
local: Ba dela batã en de aze' nyɛ duma. Tinsuŋan, Sɔ la, Yelehum la, la Fa'a Sia la. Bana batã dela bunyinɛ.

eng: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and the three are united in agreement.
local: Ti ba dela batã en de aze'nyɛduma tiŋa wa zuo. Fa'a Sia la, ko'om la, la ziim la, bana batã wa naaɛ la taaba.

eng: If we receive human testimony, Gods testimony is greater, because this is what God testified: he has testified about his Son.
local: Tu han tɔ'ɔha nɛreba en iŋɛ se'em yɛla, Yinɛ en iŋɛ sɛla la yɛla gani bala, Yinɛ en tɔgɛ a Dayua la yɛla se'em n ŋwana.

eng: The one who believes in Gods Son has the testimony within; the one who doesnt believe God has made God a liar, because that one has not believed the testimony that God gave about his Son.
local: Se'em woo en nam Yinɛ Dayua la dela aze' nyɛ daana a mɛŋa suure puan. Se'em woo en ka nam bo' Yinɛ, iŋɛ Yinɛ ti a dɛna la pumparema, la ban ka nam yelesɛ'a ti Yinɛ tɔgɛ doohe a Dayua la.

eng: And this is the testimony: God gave eternal life to us, and this life is in his Son.
local: Yinɛ en iŋɛ sɛla la yele en ŋwana. Yinɛ bo' tu la vom sumɔ en kamba'ahɛ, ti vom buna wa boi la a Dayua la puan.

eng: The one who has the Son has life. The one who doesnt have Gods Son does not have life.
local: Se'em woo en tari Dayua la, tari vom mɛ, se'em woo en ka tari Yinɛ Dayua la, ka tari vom.

eng: I write these things to you who believe in the name of Gods Son so that you can know that you have eternal life.
local: Mam gulehɛ yele ana wa bɔ'ɔra la yamam sɛba en nam Yinɛ Dayua la yu'urɛ la, ti ya mina ti ya tari Yinɛ vom sumɔ en kamba'ahɛ la.

eng: This is the confidence that we have in our relationship with God: If we ask for anything in agreement with his will, he listens to us.
local: Buraanɛ se'ere ti tu tara a puan dela, ti tu han soke en sɛla doohe a suboolum la, a kelehe tu mɛ.

eng: If we know that he listens to whatever we ask, we know that we have received what we asked from him.
local: Ti tu han mina ti a wum sɛla woo ti tu soke, tu mi' ti, tu to'e sɛla ti tu soke en la.

eng: If anyone sees a brother or sister committing a sin that does not result in death, they should pray, and God will give life to them—that is, to those who commit sins that dont result in death. There is a sin that results in death—Im not saying that you should pray about that.
local: Fum han nyɛ ti fum suuɔ tuue tuue se'ere en dagi a ki, fum suhɛ Yinɛ bo' en, ti a bo' a daana vom, sɛba tuure en dagi ba ki la. Tuure boi mɛ dɛna fum ki. Mam ka yeti ya suhɛ tuure dinɛ.

eng: Every unrighteous action is sin, but there is a sin that does not result in death.
local: Sɛla en dagi tuuntɔɔ woo dela tuure gee tuure boi mɛ ka kuura.

eng: We know that everyone born from God does not sin, but the ones born from God guard themselves, and the evil one cannot touch them.
local: Tu mi' ti, se'em woo ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ la kɔ'ɔm ka bɔna tuure puan, se'ere en sɔi la, Kiresta se'em ti Yinɛ dɔgɛ la en gu'uri en, ti Sagena la ka kalena ba.

eng: We know we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
local: Tumam mi' ti tu dela Yinɛ kɔma, gee be'em en ba' zo'e tiŋa wa zuo za'a.

eng: We know that Gods Son has come and has given us understanding to know the one who is true. We are in the one who is true by being in his Son, Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
local: Tu me mi' ti, Yinɛ Dayua la wa'am na bo' tu la bɔkerɛ, ti tu mina eŋa se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ la, tu me boi la eŋa se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, Yelemɛŋerɛ dina wa dela Yinɛ Dayua Yezu Kiresta. Eŋa en de Yinɛ de mɛŋa mɛŋa, la Yinɛ vomsumɔ kamba'ahɛ.

eng: Little children, guard yourselves from idols!.
local: Lan de ŋwana la, N kɔmbibehi wa, gu' ya mɛhi suŋa suŋa baga puan.

eng: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I truly love.
local: Kiima la, en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra n sunɔŋerɛ Gayɔse, se'em ti n nɔŋɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: Dear friend, Im praying that all is well with you and that you enjoy good health in the same way that you prosper spiritually.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, mam pu'uhegɔ dela sɛla woo kina suŋa bɔ'ɔra hɔ, ti fum tara inma'ahum wuu lan ani suŋa se'em la fum sia la.

eng: I was overjoyed when the brothers and sisters arrived and spoke highly of your faithfulness to the truth, shown by how you live according to the truth.
local: Mam puurɛ ba' pee mɛ tole, n zɔduma la basɛba en paaɛ na tɔgɛ fum en tari sakerɛ se'em yelemɛŋerɛ la puan, yele yele wuu fum boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan se'em la.

eng: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are living according to the truth.
local: Mam ka tari pupeelum kãtɛ gana ŋwana, ti n wum ti n kɔma boi yelemɛŋerɛ la puan.

eng: Dear friend, you act faithfully in whatever you do for our brothers and sisters, even though they are strangers.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, fum tuni mɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ puan, sɛla woo ti fum ita bɔ'ɔra zɔduma la, la ban pun dɛna saama bo' fum la.

eng: They spoke highly of your love in front of the church. You all would do well to provide for their journey in a way that honors God.
local: Ba yele fum nɔŋerɛ la yele mɛ lageŋɔ la nɛŋan. Fum wan iŋɛ suŋa, fum han wan iŋɛ ba suŋa ti ba tole, wuu Yinɛ en boti de se'em la.

eng: Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James. To those who are called, loved by God the Father and kept safe by Jesus Christ.
local: Mam Yude en de Yezu Kiresta da'aberɛ la, dɛna Yemesi suuɔ la en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ yi' la, yamam sɛba ti Yinɛ en de Sɔ la nɔŋɛ, gee dikɛ ya sugɛ biŋe bo' Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: because they left on their journey for the sake of Jesus Christ without accepting any support from the Gentiles.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba pɔhɛ ba sore wa la Kiresta zuo, ka tɔ'ɔha suŋɛ sɛba n ka pa' ahɛ tu puan la zi'an.

eng: May you have more and more mercy, peace, and love.
local: Nimbɔ'zɔrɛ, la suma'ahum, la nɔŋerɛ en ka tari makerɛ dɛna ya dinɛ.

eng: Therefore, we ought to help people like this so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
local: Bala, tu dela tu suŋera nɛrebamam taaba, ti tu lagum dɛna tuntuneba yelemɛŋerɛ tuuma la puan.

eng: Dear friends, I wanted very much to write to you concerning the salvation we share. Instead, I must write to urge you to fight for the faith delivered once and for all to Gods holy people.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, wuu mam en ma'ɛ n mɛŋa maahena ti n gulehɛ faarɛ se'ere ti tu tara wa yele bo' ya na la, mam bihɛ ti la nari mɛ ti n gulehɛ yele ya ti, nɛra woo zabɛ a sakerɛ se'ere ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' tu buyina la zuo.

eng: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, doesnt welcome us.
local: Mam gulehɛ sɛla bo' lageŋɔ la. Gee Diyoterefise en boti a dikɛ a mɛŋa iŋɛ ba nɛŋa daana la ka bihɛ nyɛ tumam kpe'eŋo la.

eng: Godless people have slipped in among you. They turn the grace of our God into unrestrained immorality and deny our only master and Lord, Jesus Christ. Judgment was passed against them a long time ago.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, nɛre be'ero tuseto en wa'am na zu gɛregɛ ya puan, nɛre sɛba ba'ahegɔ zi'an en boi sageŋɔ puan ban ka zoti Yinɛ la zuo, sɛba en tee Yinɛ zu'uhegɔ la ti ku dɛna yaalegɔ tuuma, ka nam tu kpɛ'ɛma ayima ma'a en de Daana Yezu Kiresta la.

eng: Because of this, if I come, I will bring up what he has done—making unjustified and wicked accusations against us. And as if that were not enough, he not only refuses to welcome the brothers and sisters but stops those who want to do so and even throws them out of the church!.
local: Bala, n han wa'am na, n wan bahɛ ti a baŋɛ en iti sɛla la, en dole yiregera pumpɔreŋɔ seko bɔ'ɔra tumam la. Ti bala ka seke en, ti a zagehɛ ti a kan to'e zɔduma la, gee gu'ura sɛba en boti ba iŋɛ bala la, pun ta sa'ahɛ ba lageŋɔ la puan.

eng: I want to remind you of something you already know very well. The Lord, who once saved a people out of Egypt, later destroyed those who didnt maintain their faith.
local: Nananawa, n ba' bɔta mɛ ti n tiɛ ya yele dina wa, la yan pun mina de suŋa suŋa la. Ti, Daana se'em en yuum faaɛ nɛreba buyina za'a za'a yehe Igipiti nu'uhen la, pooren a yuum sagum ba puan sɛba en yuum ka nam en la mɛ.

eng: Dear friend, dont imitate what is bad but what is good. Whoever practices what is good belongs to God. Whoever practices what is bad has not seen God.
local: N sunɔŋerɛ daana wa, da tɔgeha sɛla en de be'em, gee tugum tɔgeha sɛla en ani suŋa. Se'em woo en iti sum ze'ele la Yinɛ zi'an na, se'em woo en tuni be'em nan ka nyɛ Yinɛ.

eng: I remind you too of the angels who didnt keep their position of authority but deserted their own home. The Lord has kept them in eternal chains in the underworld until the judgment of the great day.
local: Gee ti suŋereba sɛba me en yuum ka ze' ba zɛ'a zi'an, gee yuum bahɛ ba zɛ'a zi'an la, a bobe ba mɛ la bãhi dikɛ ba bahɛ lika kãtɛ puan gura gakerɛ dabehekãtɛ daarɛ la.

eng: Everyone speaks highly of Demetrius, even the truth itself. We also speak highly of him, and you know that what we say is true.
local: Nɛra woo tɔgeri la sum yɛhera Demetereyose yɛla, bala mɛa ti yelemɛŋerɛ sore la me tɔgera a yele. Tumam me tɔgera a yele la bala, fum me mi' ti tumam en tɔgeri se'ere la dela yelemɛŋerɛ.

eng: In the same way, Sodom and Gomorrah and neighboring towns practiced immoral sexual relations and pursued other sexual urges. By undergoing the punishment of eternal fire, they serve as a warning.
local: Bala mɛa ti Sodom la Gomora, la timbibehi la za'a en kaaɛ ba la me en yuum ani wuu bamam la, kɛ̃ yaluntuuma puan, bɔta buraa en gã' la taaba ti pɔgehi me gã'a la taaba la, dela sɛla en pa'alɛ nɛreba en nyɛ namehegɔ se'em bugum en diti ka kiŋera la puan.

eng: I have a lot to say to you, but I dont want to use pen and ink.
local: N tari la yɛla zo'e zo'e wan gulehɛ bo' fum, gee n tugum kan gulehɛ a iŋɛ gɔŋɔ puan.

eng: Yet, even knowing this, these dreamers in the same way pollute themselves, reject authority, and slander the angels.
local: Gee la bala za'a, bala mɛa ti zaahum zaaheneba wa galena ba ingɔŋɔ, ka nana kpɛ'ɛnduma, gee tuura saazuo pãkara duma.

eng: I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face-to-face.
local: gee n iŋɛ la n puti'irɛ ti n kan yuue gee nyɛ hɔ, ti tu nyɛta taaba gee sɔhɛ.

eng: The archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil about Moses body, did not dare charge him with slander. Instead, he said, The Lord rebuke you.
local: Gee Yinɛ suŋereba nɛŋadaana Maikɛl en yuum ta zigera la Sagena la, ŋmɛ'ɛra nɔkpe'ene la en Moose iŋa zuo la, a yuum ka tu' en, bii sagum a yu'urɛ, gee a yuum yele en yeti, Daana en tahɛ hɔ la.

eng: Peace be with you. Your friends here greet you. Greet our friends there by name.
local: Suma'ahum bɔna la hɔ. Zɔduma la pu'uhi hɔ na. Pu'uhɛ zɔduma la en boi bilam la, nɛra woo a yu'urɛ a yu'urɛ.

eng: But these people slander whatever they dont understand. They are destroyed by what they know instinctively, as though they were irrational animals.
local: Gee nɛrebana wa tuurɛ la sɛla ti ba ka bɔkɛ, ti yele aŋa mɛa en Sagena ba. Ba ani mɛ wuu duhi la, ba ka zamehi sɛla.

eng: They are damned, for they follow in the footsteps of Cain. For profit they give themselves over to Balaams error. They are destroyed in the uprising of Korah.
local: Yele boi ba zuo. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba dikɛ la Keen sore la, gee dikɛ ba mɛhi luhɛ Balaam tuure la en yuum de ligeri zuo la puan, ba bahɛ la yoo wuu Kuura pobiriga la en yuum iŋɛ en se'em la.

eng: These people are like jagged rocks just below the surface of the water waiting to snag you when they join your love feasts. They feast with you without reverence. They care only for themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; fruitless autumn trees, twice dead, uprooted.
local: Ba dela galeŋɔ ya nɔŋerɛ lageŋɔ la puan, ban diti la ya ti dabeem ka tara ba, gee ti ba yeen ɛɛra ba pirɛ ba puya ma'a la. Ba dela sagbama yooro en ka tari ko'om, ti sabeeo ziira ba kaara zi'an woo. Ba dela dɔwalega tiihi en ka tari wala la, ba ki la buyi', ba vuuɛ ba mɛ ta ta' la nyɛga.

eng: wild waves of the sea foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the darkness of the underworld is reserved forever.
local: Ba dela komi'ihe yooro, yɛhera ba nyanɛ fulenfuka na, ba dela ŋmarebibehi en kaari bura bura, sɛba ti lika kãtɛ gã'a gura ba wuuha.

eng: Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, prophesied about these people when he said, See, the Lord comes with his countless holy ones.
local: Bana en bala ti Enɔki en de Adam buuri saŋa buyopɔi puan la yuum purɛ ba yele yeti, Bihɛ ya, Daana la kini mɛ na la a tuulum nɛreba tuha pia.

eng: to execute judgment on everyone and to convict everyone about every ungodly deed they have committed in their ungodliness as well as all the harsh things that sinful ungodly people have said against him.
local: ti a gakɛ sɛla woo, gee bahɛ ti nɛra woo nyɛ ban ka zoti Yinɛ gee tum tuunfi'iha sɛ'a taaba la, la yetɔge yooro seto taaba ti sɛba en ka zoti Yinɛ tɔgɛ la ba nɔya tuue Yinɛ la.

eng: These are faultfinding grumblers, living according to their own desires. They speak arrogant words and they show partiality to people when they want a favor in return.
local: Ba dela gɔgeleŋɔ duma, sɛba en nyɛti nɛreba tuure. Ba dole la ba mɛhi kunkoma'ahum, tɔgera ka du'ura, tara nɔmaalum la nɛreba, suŋera ba mɛhi ma'a.

eng: But you, dear friends, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Gee han dɛna yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, ya dela ya tɛra tu Daana Yezu Kiresta tuntuneba la en daa pun diŋɛ tɔgɛ se'em biŋe la.

eng: They said to you, In the end time scoffers will come living according to their own ungodly desires.
local: Ba daa yele ya yeti, Ba'ahegɔ saŋa, nɛre kɛhereba wan bɔna, dɔla ba mɛhi en ka zoti Yinɛ la kunkoma'ahum.

eng: These people create divisions. Since they dont have the Spirit, they are worldly.
local: La dela tiŋa zuo nɛrebana wa n ka tari Fa'a Sia la, n tari welehego kina ya puan na.

eng: But you, dear friends: build each other up on the foundation of your most holy faith, pray in the Holy Spirit.
local: Gee yamam n sunɔŋerɛ duma wa, mɛ ya mɛhi yagelɛ tuulum sakerɛ la zuo. Pu'uheri Fa'a Sia la puan.

eng: keep each other in the love of God, wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, who will give you eternal life.
local: Dikɛ ya mɛhi iŋɛ Yinɛ nɔŋerɛ la puan, gee la ya biha tu Daana Yezu Kiresta nimbɔ'zɔrɛ la en dole yɛ'a vom kamba'ahɛ puan la.

eng: Have mercy on those who doubt.
local: Gee zoti ya sɛba en bu'uri ba sakerɛ puan la nimbu'ɔ.

eng: Save some by snatching them from the fire. Fearing God, have mercy on some, hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful urges.
local: Faaɛ ya basɛba bahɛ wuu ya tum la bugum puan zɛ ba fooe na la. Gee me tari ya Yinɛ zoolum nimbɔ'zɔrɛ bɔ'ɔra sɛba. Gee kiha ba futo ti ba yaluntuuma la galum la.

eng: To the one who is able to protect you from falling, and to present you blameless and rejoicing before his glorious presence.
local: Nananawa, se'em Daana en wan ta'am tara ya ti ya kan lu la, gee me iŋɛ ya ti ya zɛ'a filam ti dɛkɔ ka lama ya ya Daana la suŋa la nɛŋan, la nyɛɛma.

eng: to the only God our savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, belong glory, majesty, power, and authority, before all time, now and forever. Amen.
local: Yinɛ deyina ma'a wa en de tu faara doohe Yezu Kiresta tu Daana puan la, sɔna na'ahegɔ la kpe'eŋo la paŋa la asɔeza'a, saŋa se'ehi en pun tabelɛ tole la, la nananawa, la wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Yezu Kiresta lilegere, ti Yinɛ lilege bo' en, ti a pa'alɛ a da'aba, sɛla en wan ba'ahum iŋɛ nananawa. A niiɛ dɛ doohe la en tum a suŋera, a yameŋa Yɔn zi'an na la.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Yɔn wa dela se'em en muuli Yinɛ yelehum, gee pa'ala Yezu Kiresta yele, ta ta' la eŋa en nyɛ sɛla woo se'em la.

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Sum boi mɛ la fum se'em en kãalɛ yelehum wa yɛla la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, ti sum bɔna la sɛba woo me en kelehe bu gee tara sɛla woo en gulehɛ bɔna gɔŋɔ wa puan la. Se'ere en sɔi la, saŋa la pɔri mɛ.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: Mam Yɔn en gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la en boi Esiya tiŋa puan la. Zu'uhegɔ bɔna la ya, ti suma'ahum en ze'ele se'em daana en de, gee daa dɛna, la se'em en ye' na la, la sɛɛhi siyopɔi la en ze' a na'am kuka la nɛŋan la.

eng: and from Jesus Christ—the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and freed us from our sins by his blood.
local: la Yezu Kiresta en de yelemɛŋerɛ aze'nyɛ daana la, dɛna ki'induma bikiima la, dɛna timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma naba la. Eŋa se'em en nɔŋɛ tɔ, gee faaɛ tɔ tu be'em puan bahɛ, la a mɛŋa ziim la.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: gee iŋɛ tɔ ti tu dɛna sɔ'ɔlum, gee dɛna malema duma, tuna bɔ'ɔra a Yinɛ la Sɔ la, eŋa ti na'ahegɔ la kpe'eŋo dɛna a dinɛ wuuha wuuha. Taari.

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Bihɛ ya! Eŋa en bala bɔna sagbama puan kina na la. Niho woo wan nyɛ en pun ta ta' sɛba en daa lu' en la. Eŋa en kinɛ na la zuo, buuri woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wan kele. Lan yeti la ana se'em en bala. Taari.

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Mam en de Alifa la Omega , Daana Yinɛ la en yele. Se'em en de, se'em en daa de, la se'em en wan wa'am na la. Gani za'a Daana la.

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: Mam Yɔn, ya suuɔ, se'em la yamam en lagum bɔna Yezu puan, lagum nyɛ namehegɔ, a sɔ'ɔlum la, la a sugeri ŋmibega la, n wa bɔna la tiŋa en boi ko'om tiŋasuka ti ba yi'ira ka Patimose, mam muuli Yinɛ yelehum la, la mam en de Yezu aze'nyɛ daana la zuo.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: Mam wa bɔna la Sia puan Daana dabeherɛ la puan, gee wum n pooren, ti kua kɛ'ɛŋa en ani wuu tintanɛ en peeberi la.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: yɛta mam yeti, Gulehɛ iŋɛ gɔŋɔ puan, sɛla ti fum nyɛ wa, la fum bo' lageŋɔ tu yopɔi la. Bo' Ɛfisu, bo' Semirina, bo' Pɛgamum, bo' Tiyatira, bo' Sadise, bo' Filidelefiya gee bo' Laodisiya.

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: Ti mam ŋmɛregɛ ti n bihɛ nyaa ani kua en bala tɔgɛ bo' mam la, ti mam ŋmɛregɛ la, mam nyɛ la salema butolela ze'elehi siyopɔi.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: ti ze'elehi wa tiŋasuka, mam nyɛ la nɛra ayima ti a ana wuu Asaala Dayua la, yɛ fuwoko, ti salema tanɛ gabegɛ a nyu'ɔ.

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: Ti a zuo, zonto kɔ'ɔm pelege, fari wuu gunguno la, pelege wuu sakuga la, ti a nini ana wuu bugum peelum la.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: ti a na'tala wa nyakera wuu nyɔgum ti ba sɛ' bugum puan paaɛ paaɛ ti ba nyakera se'em la, ti a kua ana wuu kulaa kãtɛ ko'om en sũni la.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: A zuɔ nu'o puan, a zãli la ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi, ti a nuurɛ puan, su'a en diti, tara nɔya ayi' en yeheri na, ti a nɛŋa kpikera wuu wuntɛɛŋa en ba' kpikera se'em la.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: Mam nyɛ en la, mam lu la a nama tiŋa wuu n ki mɛ la. Gee ti a dikɛ a zuɔ nu'o pagelɛ mam zuo gee yeti, Da zɔta dabeem, Mam en de Pɔhega, la Ba'ahegɔ.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Mam de se'em en vɔi la. Mam yuum ki mɛ, gee bihɛ, n vɔi mɛ wuuha wuuha. Mam en tari kum, la ki'intiŋa kparehega la.

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: Nananawa, gulehɛ sɛla ti fum nyɛ, sɛla en iŋɛ wa, la sɛla en wan iŋɛ ŋwana pooren.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Han dɛna sugelum yele se'ere en boi ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi wa ti fum nyɛ n zuɔ nu'o wa puan wa, la salema butolela ze'elehi siyopɔi la, ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi la ze'ele bo' la Lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la Suŋereba.Ti ze'elehi siyopɔi me ze'ele bo' Lageŋɔ tuyopɔi la.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Ɛfisu lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em daana en zãli ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi a zuɔ nu'o puan la, se'em en eere salema butolehi siyopɔi ze'elehi la tiŋasuka la yetɔgum.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
local: Mam mi yan tuni se'em, ya namehegɔ la yan tari sugeri se'em ŋmibega puan. Mam mi' ti ya ka ta' ŋmibera la be'em duma, ya magehɛ sɛba en yeti bamam dela Yezu tuntuneba gee dagena la, baŋɛ ti ba pareni mɛ.

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: N me kelum mina ti ya ŋmiberi mɛ la sugeri, gee uhera mam yu'urɛ la zuo, gee me mina ti ya ka taregeri.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: Gee mam tari la yele dina la ya. Ya bahɛ yan daa tari nɔŋerɛ se'ere la mam yia la mɛ.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: Tiɛ ya sɛla en bahɛ ti ya lu la, la ya tee, la ya tuna tuunsɛ'a ti ya daa pun tuna yia la. Han dagena bala, n wan wa'am ya zi'an na dikɛ ya butolela zɛ'ɛlega la ka ze'elego zi'an la, han dagena ya tee mɛ.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: Gee la bala za'a, ya tuunsuma en ŋwana. Ya ka boti Nikolatan duma tuuma la ti ba tuna la, tuunsɛ'a ti mam mɛŋa me pun ka bɔta la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ duma la. Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, N wan bo' en sore ti a di vom tia la wala la en boi Yinɛ vo'ohego zi'an la.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life.
local: N gulehɛ bɔ'ɔra la Suŋera se'em en boi Semirina tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em en de Pɔhega gee dɛna Ba'ahegɔ, se'em en daa ki gee le vɔna la yelehum.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: N mi' ya pɔgeleŋɔ, la ya nɔŋɔ, la yan pun tara la za'a. N mi' yu'sageŋɔ seko n paari ya, doohe sɛba en yeti ba dela Yuda buuri gee dagena, gee dɛna Sagena la lageŋɔ yire la.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Da zoti ya dabeem la yan boi bini ti ya namehɛ namehegɔ seko wa. Mini ya suŋa ti bumbe'o la maahum mɛ ti a bahɛ ti ba pagɛ ya sɛba paka deto puan, ti la dɛna makerɛ bo' ya, dabeha pia ti ba wan dikɛ pɔgelum ya. Gee tari ya sakerɛ ta paaɛ kum, gee ti n bo' ya vom muŋa la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo n tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la. Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, kum buyi' la kan paaɛ en.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Pɛgamum tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la, ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela se'em daana n tari sɔ'ditega en tari nɔya ayi'.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: N mi' ya bɔŋa zi'an, la dela zi'an ti Sagena la na'am kuka la bɔna. Gee la bala za'a ya kɔ'ɔm gurɛ mam yu'urɛ la mɛ kankaŋi, gee ka zagehɛ ya sakerɛ la mam, pun ta paaɛ zahera Antipas se'em en daa de mam aze'nyɛ daana, tara sakerɛ la mam, ti ba daa ku en ya tiŋasuka, Sagena la bɔŋa zi'an la.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: Gee mam tari la yele dina la ya. Ya tari basɛba ya puan ti ba dɔla Balaam pa'alegɔ la mɛ, se'em en daa pa'alɛ Balake ti a dikɛ sɛla en piti beŋe Izirale buuri la ti ba dita baga dia gee tuna yaluntuuma la.

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: Bala mɛa ti yamam basɛba me dɔla Nikolatan pa'alegɔ kankaŋi la.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: Tee ya. Han dagena bala, n wan wa'am ya zi'an nananawa na, la n zabɛ la ba, la sɔ'ditega la en ze'ele mam nuuren na la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuberɛ, a kɛleha Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la. Fum se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' en maana dia la basɛba en dikɛ sugɛ la, gee bo' en maanɛ kugepeele, ti kugere wa zuo, ba gulehɛ la a yu'paalɛ iŋɛ de zuo, yu'se'ere ti se'em ka mina, han dagena fum se'em daana en to'e de la ma'a.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Taitira lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna dela Yinɛ Dayua la, se'em nini en ani wuu bugum peelum la, la se'em nama en nyagere wuu nyɔgum la dinɛ.

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: N mi' ya tuuma, ya nɔŋerɛ, ya sakerɛ, ya tuuma, ya sugeri la ya ŋmibega. N mi' ti ya pooren tuuma wa karegɛ gana ya yia tuuma la.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: Gee mam tari la dina la ya. Ya to'e Yezebɛle mɛ, pɔgesɛka en yi'iri ka mɛŋa nɔtɔgeha la, se'em pa'alegɔ en bahɛ ti mam yamehi bɔregɛ, tuna yaluntuuma, gee dita dia ti ba dikɛ kaabɛ baga la.

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: Mam bo' en saŋa mɛ ti a tee, gee a zagehɛ mɛ ka tee a yaluntuuma la puan.

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices.
local: Mini ya suŋa, ti n lobere en baha la suŋɔ zuo, ti sɛba me en naaɛ la en tuna kabohi la me, n wan lobe ba bahɛ susageŋɔ kãtɛ puan, han dagena ba tee mɛ yehe ba mɛhi a tuuma la puan.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: N wan damɛ a kɔma, ku ba, ti mam lageŋɔ la woo baŋɛ, ti mam en de se'em en mi' puti'ira la suya puan, gee wan bo' nɛra woo a tuuma yɔɔrɛ en nari se'em.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: Gee ya sɛba woo en gee bɔna Taitira la, ka dɔla Yezebɛle pa'alegɔ la, ka zamehɛ sɛla ti ba yi'ira ti Sagena la tuunkãra la, n yeti yamam yeti, n kan dikɛ zeero seto pa'ahɛ ya.

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: yeen gurɛ ya yan tari sɛla la kankaŋi, gura ti n ta lebe na.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
local: Se'em woo en nyaŋɛ, gee kelum tuna mam tuuma la ta paaɛ ba'ahegɔ, N wan bo' en paŋa ti a biha tihi.

eng: to rule the nations with an iron rod and smash them like pottery.
local: A wan biha ba la kuto doore, wɔ̃ta ba wuu yɔgerɔ dugerɔ la.

eng: just as I received authority from my Father. I will also give them the morning star.
