local: Wuu mam en to'e paŋa se'em n Sɔ zi'an la, bala mɛa ti se'em daana woo n nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' a daana bulika beŋma'arɛ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuberɛ, a kɛleha sɛla ti Fa'a Sia la tɔgera bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Sadise tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'eti la se'em n tari Yinɛ Sɛɛhi siyopɔi la, gee zãla ŋmarebibehi siyopɔi la zi'an na. Mam mi ya tuuma, ya tari la vɔpa yu'urɛ, gee ya ki mɛ.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Niiɛ ya, la ya kpemehe sɛla en gee, gee me yɛ'a kum la. Se'ere en sɔi la, mam ka nyɛ ya tuuma la ti a tigɛ Yinɛ ninin.

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Bala, tɛri ya yan to'e sɛla, la yan wum sɛla la, la ya tara di kankaŋi, gee tee. Ya han ka ihege, n wan wa'am na wuu nageyiga la, ti ya me kan mina sansɛka ti n yɛ'a ya zi'an na.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: Gee la bala za'a, ya kelum tara yu'ura fii wa Sadise tiŋa la puan en ka lirum ba lɔgerɔ. Bama en wan kina la mam, yɛ ba lɔgepeelo, se'ere n sɔi la, ba nari mɛ.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: Ya han nyaŋɛ, ba me wan bagɛ ya la fupɛɛla, ti n kan saalum ya yu'ura vom gɔŋɔ la puan bahɛ. N wan purɛ ya yu'urɛ n Sɔ nɛŋan, la a suŋereba nɛŋan.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em en tari tuba, a kɛleha Sia la en yeti se'em bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la woo la.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Filadelefiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'ele la Fa'a daana la, se'em en de yelemɛŋerɛ daana la, zi'an na. Se'em en tari Deviti yire kparehega la, Se'em en yu'ɛ ti se'em ka bɔna yu'ura la, Se'em en yu' ti se'em ka bɔna wan yu'ɛ la.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: N mi' ya tuuma. Bihɛ yaa, N yu'ɛ la kuleŋa biŋe ya, ti se'em ka bɔna wan yu' ka. Mam mi' ti ya tari paŋa fii wa, gee la bala za'a, ya tari mam yelehum la mɛ suŋa suŋa, ka zagehɛ mam yu'urɛ la.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Bihɛ ya, n wan iŋɛ Sagena lageŋɔ en yeti bamam dela Yuuduma gee dagena, gee parena la, n wan iŋɛ ba ti ba wa'am na kpa' duma ya nɛŋan, la ba zamehɛ bɔkɛ yeti, mam sirum nɔŋɛ ya.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Yamam en tari mam yelehum la en de sugeri, la ŋmibega la zuo, n wan gu' ya makerɛ se'ere en ye'ehe timpaberɛ wa zuo na ti de makɛ tiŋa wa zuo nɛreba za'a la puan.

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: N ye' la nananawa na. Gurɛ ya yan tari sɛla la kankaŋi, ti se'em da ta faaɛ ya muŋa la.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: Ya han nyaŋɛ, n wan iŋɛ ya ti ya dɛna ziirɛ mam Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, kan malum yehe de puan. N wan gulehɛ n Yinɛ la yu'urɛ labelɛ ya, gee gulehɛ n Yinɛ tiŋa la yu'urɛ labelɛ ya, Yerusalɛm paalega la en ze'ele mam Yinɛ la zi'an saazuo ha na la, la mam mɛŋa yu'paalɛ labelɛ ya.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha sɛla ti Fa'a Sia la yɛta bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: Suŋera se'em en boi Laodisiya tiŋa lageŋɔ la puan la ti n guleha bɔ'ɔra. Yetɔgebuna ze'ele la se'em en de Amɛn la zi'an na. Sakerɛ la yelemɛŋerɛ aze'nyɛ Daana la, se'em en de Yinɛ naaŋɔ la za'a pi'ileŋo daana la zi'an na.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: N mi' ya tuuma. Ya ka ma'ɛ bii ya tula. Mam boti ni ti ya ma'ɛ ni mɛ, bii ya tole ni.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: Gee lan de se'em la, yan de ko'bugere la zuo, ka ma'ɛ, gee me ka tula la zuo, mam boi bini ti n tubehɛ ya mɛ n nuuren bahɛ na.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, ya yeti, Tu tari mɛ, tu pãrɛ mɛ, ka bɔta sɛla sɛla le yɛ'ɛha. Gee ya ka baŋɛ ti ya ku'urɛ mɛ, dita nimbu'ɔ, nameha, fɔ, gee ɛɛra beele.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Bala zuo, mam ka'ani ya mɛ ti, ya wa'am na da' salema sɛ'a ti bugum bi'ihɛ a paaɛ la mam zi'an, la ya dɛna buntateba. Da' ya futo pɛɛla mam zi'an bagɛ pi' ya nyanɛ la, ti nɛreba da nyɛta ya beele la, gee da' tisumɔ mam zi'an tigɛ ya nini, la ya nyɛta suŋa.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Mam taheri gee dɔlegera la sɛba ti n nɔŋɛ la tuba. Bala, mu'ɛ ya nini gee tee.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Kelehe ya, mam ze' la kuleŋa la nuuren ŋmɛ'ɛra, se'em han wum mam kua gee yu'ɛ kuleŋa la, n wan kɛ̃ a zi'an na, di la en, ti a me di la mam.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: Se'em daana woo en nyaŋɛ, n wan bo' a daana zi'an ti a bɔna la mam, n na'am kuka la zuo, wuu mam mɛŋa en nyaŋɛ, gee ta zi'a la n Sɔ a na'am kuka la zuo se'em la.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha Fa'a Sia la en yeti sɛla bɔ'ɔra lageŋɔ la za'a la.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Ŋwana en tole la, ti mam biha, ti tinsuŋan ha, kuleŋa yu'ɛ bahɛ. Ti yia kua sɛka ti mam pun wum ni ti ka tɔgera bɔ'ɔra mam ana wuu tintanɛ en peeberi la, yele mam yeti, Zom ka'lam na, ti n pa'alɛ hɔ sɛla en wan iŋɛ ŋwana wa pooren.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Kalam ti mam pun bɔna Sia la puan, paaɛ bini. Tinsuŋan ha la, mam nyɛ ti na'am kuka kãtɛ en ze', ti nɛra ayima zi'a ka zuo.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Ti se'em en zi' ka zuo la nyagere mɛ wuu tuulum kuga en de dɛlemadɛtɔ la mɔlegɔ, ti sɛla en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ wa mum la dɛna, gumatia tahɔ en ani biin wuu tuulum kuge sabela la.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Ti sɛla en ze' kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ wa mum, dela na'am kugehi pihiyi' la sinaahi, ti sɛba en zi' na'am kugehi sina wa zuto dɛna kiinduma pihiyi' la banaahi en bagɛ la na'am fupɛɛla, ti salema muhi vuga ba zuto.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Ti sɛla en ze'eti na'am kuka kãtɛ la puan na, dela saa bugum en nyagere, la dameŋa la saa tahega, gee ti na'am kuka kãtɛ wa nɛŋan na, bugum zɛlehi siyopɔi en ze' dita, dɛna Yinɛ sɛɛhi siyopɔi la.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Na'am kuka kãtɛ mɛa nɛŋan, sɛla en gã' wuu salema ateko la, ana yilaam. Ti sɛla en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, ka luga anaahi la za'a, dela bunvɔɔhi banaahi, ti ba zi'an woo tara nini, ba nɛŋa la ba poore za'a .

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Ti se'em en boi yia la ana wuu gbigenɛ, ti se'em en tagelɛ la me ana wuu lɔlega, ti ta'a daana la tara nɛŋa en ani wuu asaala nɛŋa la, ti naahi daana la nɛŋa ana wuu tugeho en ɛgeri la.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Ti bunvɔɔhi sinaahi wa ayima woo tari la kelumpagehi siyoobe, pirɛ la nini ba yiŋa, la ba puan. Ti bulika la yu'uŋɔ za'a, ba kɔ'ɔm yuuna mɛ ka malum gɔ'ɔra. Ba yuuni yeti, Fa'a, Fa'a, Fa'a, Daana Yinɛ, Gani za'a daana, se'em en daa de, gee kelum dɛna, gee wan wa'am na.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Ti saŋa woo, bunduma wa han ni na'ahera, gee pɛgera, gee pu'uha se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la, se'em en boi wuuha wuuha la.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: Kiinduma pihiyi la banaahi la ni lu la aliko, se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la nɛŋan, na'aha eŋa se'em daana en boi wuuha wuuha la. Ba ni vugehe ba muhi la lobe la na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan, gee yuuna yeti.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: Fu nari mɛ, tu Daana la tu Yinɛ, ti fum to'e pɛka la na'ahegɔ la paŋa, se'ere en sɔi la, fum naam sɛla woo , ti fum sumboolum zuo ti ba bɔna, ti fum en naam ba.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Pebila la en lorege daalehum buyopɔi la, la ani la suram tinsuŋan la, paaɛ wuu gogo kaareŋa girego.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋereba bayopɔi la en ze' Yinɛ nɛŋan la, ti ba bo' ba tintana a yopɔi.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Ti suŋera ayima zãla zu'uhi nyɔ'a dugela en de salema, wa'am na ze'ele kaabegɔ bimbine la zi'an, ti ba bo' en nyuuŋo suŋɔ digelum zo'e zo'e, ti a dikɛ kaabɛ pa'ahɛ nɛresunduma la woo pu'uhegɔ la, kaabegɔ daka la en de salema la zuo, na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Ti nyuuŋo suŋɔ digelum zu'uhi la, la nɛresunduma pu'uhegɔ la ze'ele suŋera la nu'uhen ihegera saazuo, Yinɛ nɛŋan.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Ti suŋera wa dikɛ zu'uhi nyɔ'a dugela wa, mu'ɛ bugum kaabegɔ daka la zuo, pirɛ ka, dikɛ dugela la lobe bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti saa tahega, saa dameŋa, saa nyaka, la tinmiiŋo kɔ'ɔm gbiregera.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Ti suŋereba bayopɔi la en zãli tintana ayopɔi la, maahum ti ba peebe tintana la.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Ti yia suŋera la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti sakuga la bugum en gɛregɛ la ziim, ti ba ka'ɛ bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti timpaberɛ wa buyina puregerɛ butã puan taaɛ bugum di. Tiihi buyina puregerɛ butã di, ti mɔ ma'aha za'a di.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Ti yi'i suŋera la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti sɛla en ani wuu zoore kãtɛ la, taaɛ bugum dita ti ba za'ɛ bahɛ n ateko puan na.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Ti ateko la buyina puregerɛ butã puan, lebege ziim, ti bunvɔɔhi en boi ko'om la puan la buyina puregerɛ butã puan ki, ti uurenɔ en boi ko'om la zuo buyina puregerɛ butã puan, sagum.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Ti suŋera butã daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti ŋmarebili kãtɛ ze'ele saazuo lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na, nyagera la bugum. Ka me lu la kulaahi la kulehi woo buyina puregerɛ butã puan.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Ŋmarebila wa yu'urɛ yi'iri la buntoo. Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a ko'om, buyina puregerɛ butã puan tɔna lanlaŋi, ti zo'e zo'e ki, ko'om la nyua puan, bu too la zuo.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Ti suŋera bu naahi daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti wuntɛɛŋa niihum buyina puregerɛ butã kiŋe, ti ŋmarega niihum buyina puregerɛ butã puan kiŋe, ti ŋmarebibehi me buyina puregerɛ butã puan me kiŋe, ti la bahɛ ti ba woo niihum buyina puregerɛ butã puan kiŋe, ti wuntɛɛŋa ka niira dabeherɛ la buyina puregerɛ butã puan, ti yu'uŋɔ me dɛna bala mɛa.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Ti mam bihɛ, wum ti tugeho kaaheri kɔkɛ'ɛŋa gee ɛgera saazuo ha, yɛta yeti, Yele kãtɛ, Yele kãtɛ, Yele kãtɛ boi timpaberɛ wa nɛreba zuo, suŋereba batã wa han peebe ba tintana la ti ba bɔna bini ti ba peebe la.

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la a zuɔ nu'o puan, ti gɔŋɔ ti ba gulehɛ ku puan, la ku pooren za'a, pɔbɛ ku la daalehum buyopɔi.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera en de paŋa daana, muula la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Ani en boi nara ti a lorege gɔŋɔ wa, la a lakɛ daalehum la gee bihɛ ku puan.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Ti se'em ka bɔna tinsuŋan la, bii timpaberɛ wa zuo, bii timpaberɛ wa tiŋa tiŋa ha, wan ta'am lakɛ gɔŋɔ la bihɛ ku puan.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Ti mam pɔhɛ kɛla ninmu'urɛ, se'ere en sɔi la, se'em ka boi nara a yu'ɛ gɔŋɔ la, bii a bihɛ ku puan.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Ti kiinduma la ayima yele mam yeti, Da kɛla. Bihɛ, Gbigenɛ la en yehe Yuda buuri puan na la, se'em tuŋo en yehe Deviti puan na la nyaŋɛ ya, wan ta'am yu'ɛ gɔŋɔ la, la ku daalehum buyopɔi la.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la, la kiinduma la tiŋasuka, ti Pebila ze', ana wuu ba tabelɛ kɔregɛ ka mɛ la, tara iila ayopɔi la nini iyopɔi en de Yinɛ sɛɛhi siyopɔi la, ti Yinɛ tum si timpaberɛ wa zuo zi'an woo na la.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Ti a kiŋɛ paaɛ to'e gɔŋɔ la se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la zuɔ nu'o la puan.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: En to'e gɔŋɔ la, bunvɔɔhi banaahi la za'a, la kiinduma pihiyi' la banaahi la, lu mɛ Pebila la nama tiŋa, ti ba woo zãla duureŋa duureŋa, la salema laahi ti si pirɛ la nyuuŋo suŋɔ digelum en de Yinɛ nɛre sunduma la pu'uhegɔ.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Ti ba za'a yuum yuumpaalɛ yeti, Fum nari mɛ ti fum to'e gɔŋɔ la la fum lorege daalehum se'ere en sɔi la, ba yuum kɔregɛ fum mɛ, ti fum dikɛ fum ziim yɔ to'e nɛre sunduma, buuri woo, tugum woo, nɛreba woo la tihi woo puan bo' Yinɛ.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: Fum iŋɛ ba ti ba dɛna la malema duma buuri sɔ'ɔlum, tuna bɔ'ɔra Yinɛ, ba wan sɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo biha.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Ti mam bihɛ, wum suŋereba kuuhi zo'e zo'e en kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunduma la, la kiinduma la. Ti ba kãlɛ kɛ̃'ɛra tuha pia la tuha tuha.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: yuuna la ba kuuhi za'a yeti, Pebila la ti ba kɔregɛ la nari mɛ ti ka to'e paŋa, tarega, yɛm la kpe'eŋo la na'ahegɔ, la suŋa, la sum.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Ti mam wum ti bunvua woo en boi tinsuŋan, timpaberɛ wa zuo, la tiŋa tiŋa ha, la ateko puan, la sɛla woo en boi ba puan, ti ba za'a yuuna yeti, Se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la, la Pebila la, sum, la na'ahegɔ, suŋa la kpe'eŋo dɛna fum dinɛ wuuha wuuha.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la yeti, Taari. Ti kiinduma la za'a lu tiŋa la na'ahegɔ.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti Pebila la lorege daalehum bayopɔi la a yima, ti mam wum ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la a yima yi' bahɛ na la kua en ani woo saa dameŋa yeti,Wa'ana.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: Ti mam bihɛ, ti yeeho pɛɛlega en ze', ti bu ba'ara zãla tahɔ, ti ba dikɛ muŋa bo' en, ti a yehe na, dɛna nyaŋera, ti a nini mu'ɛ la nyaŋerɛ.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Pebila la en lorege yi'i daalehum la, ti mam wum ti bunduma la, yi'i daana la yi' bahɛ na yeti, Wa'ana.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Ti yeeho buyima me yehe na, mu'ɛ ziŋiliŋ, ti ba bo' bu ba'ara la paŋa, ti a doohe kã'ahɛ suma'ahum timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a yehe, ti la bahɛ ti nɛreba kɔrum taaba. Ba me bo' en la sɔ'kãtɛ.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: En lorege daalehum butã la, mam wum ti bunduma la, ta'a daana la, yi' bahɛ na yeti,Wa'ana. Ti mam bihɛ ti yeeho sɔbelegɔ en ze', ti bu ba'ara la zãla magehega bayi' a nu'uhen.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: ti mam wum ti sɛla ana wuu kua la, ze'ele bunvɔɔhi banaahi la zi'an na yɛta yeti, Makɛ ki la wula ti la dɛna daanyinɛ tuuma yɔɔrɛ, gee dikɛ wambila makɛ kemɔlega ti la dɛna daanyinɛ yɔɔrɛ, gee da sagum olive kaam la, la daam la.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: En lorege daalehum sebo en pa'ahɛ bu naahi la, mam wum la bunduma la bunaahi daana la en yi' bahɛ na yeti,Wa'ana.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: Ti mam bihɛ ti yeeho wugelegɔ en ani biin zɛ'a. Ti bu ba'ara la yu'urɛ yi'ira Kum, ti sageŋɔ dɔla la en. Ti ba bo' ba paŋa, ti ba sɔna timpaberɛ wa puregerɛ bunaahi, la ba kɔrum la su'a, kom, la bã'ahi, la muuen duhi en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: En lorege daalehum bunuu la, mam nyɛ la kaabegɔ bimbine la tiŋa, ti sɛɛhi en de sɛba ti ba kɔrum ba Yinɛ yelehum la zuo la, la ban muulɛ ban nyɛ sɛla la zuo la.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Ti ba kaahɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Gaŋeza'a Daana, Fa'a la Yelemɛŋerɛ Daana, la yeti la yuue la ŋwani gee ti fum gakɛ gee lerege timpaberɛ zuo duma wa en ka'ɛ tumam ziim wa se'em la.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Ti ba bo' ba za'a fupɛɛlega fupɛɛlega, gee yele ba yeti, ba lebe ta gã'arɛ fii wa, gura ti kãlɛ se'ere ti ba yeti ba kɔrum ba tuntuneba tadaanduma la, la ba suurɔ la ti ba yeti ba ku ba nananawa la paaɛ.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: En lorege daalehum buyoobe la, mam bihɛ ti tinmiiŋo kãtɛ en sige, ti wuntɛɛŋa wa sobege wuu susageŋɔ fuo la, ti ŋmare pɛɛlega mu'ɛ wuu ziim la.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: ti ŋmarebibehi en boi sagbama zuo ha luta timpaberɛ wa zuo na wuu kinkaŋa ti sabeeo lobe ti ka mu'uro zɔgera se'em la.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: ti sagbanɛ wa mɛlum wuu gɔŋɔ en kilum ku mɛŋa se'em la, ti zoore woo, la ko'om tiŋasuka tiŋa wuu mɛlum ka bɔŋa zi'an la.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo na'duma, nɛrekãreba, pɛntiireba kpɛ'ɛnduma, nɛrezura, la nɛra woo, da'aberɛ bii se'em en boi fai, za'a zɔta sugera yogero puan, tahi leŋen la zɔya tilan.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: gee yi'ira zɔya, la tahi yeti, Lu ya tu zuo na, lile tu, ti tu da nyɛta se'em daana en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la nɛŋa. Tu da nyɛ Pebila la sunsunyiim la.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: se'ere en sɔi la, ba sunsunyiim dabehekãtɛ la paaɛ ya.Ti ani n wan ta'am ze'ele.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Ti suŋera bunuu daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti mam nyɛ ti ŋmarebila la en ze'ele saazuo ha lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, ti ba bo' ka kparehega, ti ka yu'ɛ boko kãtɛ en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Ti ka yu'ɛ boko la en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha la, ti zu'uhi tu'ura boko kãtɛ la puan na wuu bugum kãtɛ en taaɛ dita zu'uhi la, ti zu'uhi la gu' wuntɛɛŋa, la sabeeo wa.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Ti sunwa'ara ze'ele zu'sɔbelegɔ wa puan sige timpaberɛ wa zuo na, ti ba bo' ba kpe'eŋo ti ba kpe'eŋo la ana wuu nahi kpe'eŋo la timpaberɛ wa zuo.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Ti ba yele ba yeti, ba da sagum timpaberɛ wa muɔ, bii bumbula, bii tiihi, gee ba sagum nɛre sɛba woo en ka tari Yinɛ daalehum la ba gbɛ'ɛran.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Ba bo' ba sore ti ba mɛlehɛ nɛrebana wa taaba ŋmarehi sinuu, gee ba da ku ba. Gee bunduma wa duŋa too ani mɛ wuu naŋa duŋa la, ba han dum nɛra.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Dabeha aŋa puan, nɛreba wan ɛɛra kum, gee ba kan nyɛ. Ba wan bɔta ti ba ki, gee kum wan ɛkɛ gee bahɛ ba.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Fum han bihera sunwa'ara wa, ba ani mɛ wuu yire en bagɛ, maahum yɛ'a pɛntɛrɛ la. Ba zutin, sɛla en vuge ba wuu salema muhi la, ti ba nɛhi ana wuu saaleba nɛhi la.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: ti ba zuo zonto ana wuu pɔgehi zuo zonto la, ti ba nyɛna ana wuu gbigema nyɛna la;.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: ti sɛla ti'ɛ ba nyu'urɔ wa wuu zaba kuto tɛ'ɛlega la, ti ba kelumpagehi la dameŋa ana wuu zaba yiiri toto kekeko en zoti yɛ'a pɛntɛrɛ damena se'em la.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Ba tari la zuya wuu nahi la, ti duŋa zi'an bɔna, ti ba paŋa bɔna la ba zuya la puan, ti ba mɛlehɛ nɛreba ŋmarehi sinuu.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Ba tari la naba ti a biha ba, suŋera se'em en tari tiŋa tiŋa boko kãtɛ la kparehega la. A yu'urɛ Hiburu puan dela Abadon. Giriki puan a yu'urɛ dela Apoliyon.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Yia namehegɔ la tole ya, bihɛ, namehegɔ buyi' en wan wa'am na.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Ti suŋera buyoobe daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti mam wum ti kua ze'ele iila a naahi la en boi kaabegɔ salema daka la en ze' Yinɛ nɛŋa la.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: yɛta suŋera buyoobe daana la n tari tintanɛ la yeti, Lorege suŋereba banaahi la ti ba bobe ze'ele Yufaratesi kulaa kãtɛ la zi'an la bahɛ.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Ban bahɛ suŋereba banaahi la se'em n bala. Suŋereba sɛba ti ba gurɛ ba ze'ele gura sansɛka, dabeherɛ se'ere, ŋmare sɛka, la yuunɛ se'ere, ti ba ku nɛresaaleba buyina puregerɛ butã puan timpaberɛ wa zuo la.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Pɛntiireba sɛba en yage yire zuo, maahum la, kãlɛ dela, tuha kɔbehi siyi. Mam wum ba kãlɛ dina wa mɛ.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Mam nyɛ yire la se'em lilegere la puan en ŋwana. Yire la ba'areba la yɛ la tɛ'ɛlega kuto futo n mu'ɛ wuu bugum, la tuulum kuge sɔbele puuhego, kuge dɔboolum, ti yire wa zuto ana wuu gbigema zuto la, ti bugum la zu'uhi la bugum peelum yɛhera ba nɔyan na.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Tooro tuna tutã' wa en ku timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba buyina puregerɛ butã. Bugum la, zu'uhi la, la bugum peelum la en ze'eti ba nɔyan yɛhera na la.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, yire wa paŋa boi la ba nɔyan la ba zuyan. Ba zuya la ani mɛ woo bunsɛlenduma la, tara zuto, ti ba tara ba mɛleha saaleba la.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Gee nɛre sɛba woo en gee, ti too kuna wa ka ku ba la, ka makɛ tee ba mɛhi ba nu'uhi en iti sɛla puan, bii ba bahɛ ba na'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, la baga ti ba dikɛ salema, salempɛɛlega, la nyɔgum, la kuga, la dɔɔrɔ maalɛ ba ti ba ka nyɛta, bii wuna, bii kina la.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Ba me ka tee ba mɛhi ba nɛre kua la, bii ba sɔ̃niniduma la, bii ba yaluntuuma, bii ba nageyigum la puan.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera paŋa daana ayima ze'ele saazuo ha sigera na, ti sagbama pi' en, ti gumatia tahɔ yula a zuin, ti a nɛŋa nyagera wuu wuntɛɛŋa la, ti a nama ana wuu bugum la.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: Ti a zãla gɔŋɔ pika a nu'en ti ku yu'ɛ. Ti a dikɛ a zuɔ na'arɛ tagelɛ ko'kãtɛ wa zuo, gee ti a gɔbega na'arɛ bɔna doone na, tinkɛ'ɛŋa zuo.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Ti a tahɛ la paŋa wuu gbigenɛ en taheri la. Ti en tahɛ la, ti saahi siyopɔi uhe bahɛ na.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Ti saahi siyopɔi la en uhe bahɛ na la, ti mam bɔna bini ti n gulehɛ, gee mam wum la kua saazuo na en yeti, Sugɛ saahi siyopɔi wa en yele sɛla la, gee da gulehɛ de biŋe.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Ti suŋera sɛka ti mam nyɛ ti a na'arɛ deyima bɔna ko'kãtɛ puan ti deyima bɔna doone na la, Zɛkɛ a zuɔ nu'o tɔ saazuo ha.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: gee pɔ se'em en boi wuuha wuuha ha wa se'em en naam saazuo la sɛla woo en boi ba puan ha, la timpaberɛ wa la sɛla woo en boi ka zuo, la ko'kãtɛ la puan, la sɛla woo en boi bu puan yeti, Gu'uŋɔ ka le bɔna.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: gee dabeha sɛ'a ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la wan peebe a tintanɛ la, Yinɛ yele se'ere en sugɛ la wan wa'am yelemɛŋerɛ na, wuu en muulɛ de se'em doohe a yamehi la en de nɔtɔgeheba puan se'em la.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Ti kua sɛka ti mam pun wum ni ti ka tɔgɛ saazuo bahɛ na la, le tɔgɛ bo' mam na yeti, Kiŋɛ la fum ta to'e gɔŋɔ la en lakɛ bɔna suŋera se'em na'arɛ deyima en boi ko'kãtɛ la puan ti deyima me bɔna doone la zi'an.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Bala ti mam kiŋɛ suŋera wa zi'an, ta yele en ti a bo' mam gɔŋɔ pika la, ti a yele mam yeti, To'e la fum obe. Ku wan tɔna fum puuren, gee za'ahum wuu seero la fum nuuren.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Bala ti mam to'e gɔŋɔ pika la suŋera la nu'en obe ku, ti ku za'ahum wuu seero la mam nuuren, gee mam obe ku vole la, ku tɔi mɛ mam puuren ha.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Ti ba nyaa yele mam yeti, Fum dela fum le purɛ bahɛ nɛreba kekeko zuo, la tihi, la yetɔgum buuri buuri, la na'duma zuto.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Ti ba nyaa dikɛ doore en tara mageha la, bo' mam gee yele mam yeti, Wa'ana magehɛ Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire wa, la kaabegɔ nyɔ'a daka la, la sɛba en pu'uheri ku puan la.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: gee da makɛ zinzaka la en boi pu'uhegɔ yire la yiŋa ha la. Bahɛ bilam, se'ere en sɔi la, ba dikɛ bilam bo' la sɛba en dagi Yuda buuri nɛreba, ti ba niira tuulum tiŋa wa puan ŋmarehi pihinaahi la siyi.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: Gee n wan bo' n aze'nyɛduma bayi' la paŋa, ti ba dɔla puta dabeha tuhere, la dabeha kɔbehi siyi, la dabeha pihiyoobe, yɛ susageŋɔ futo.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Bana bayi' wa en de olive tiihi siyi, la butolela ze'elehi siyi la en ze' Daana se'em en sɔi timpaberɛ wa nɛŋan la.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Ti se'em han yeti a daam ba, bugum wan ze'ele ba nɔyan na di ba dinduma. Se'em woo en yeti a daam ba, wan ki la kum buna taaba.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Ba tari kpe'eŋo n wan yu' sagbama ti saa da ni, ba purega saŋa la puan, gee me tara kpe'eŋo la ko'om woo, wan ta'am bahɛ ti ko'om woo lebege ziim, gee bahɛ ti tooro yima yima to'e timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, bamam en boti se'em ma'a.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Ba han purɛ ba yelehum la ba'ahɛ, bunsilemasio la en ze'ele tiŋa tiŋa yoko la puan dooe na la, wan kɔ' zaberɛ la ba, nyaŋɛ ba, ku ba.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: ti ba ki ti ba ihi la gã'a sore zuo, tinkãtɛ sɛka ti ba ze'ele ti ba yi'ira ka Sodom la Igipiti la, zi'an ti ba tabelɛ kpa' ba Daana la bini la.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Dabeha atã la girego, ti nɛreba, la ba suurɔ, buuri yima yima, la tugum yima yima, la tihi nɛreba wan kina limeha kum la, gee zagehɛ ti ba kan pi' ba yɔɔ seko puan.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba wan nyɛɛna la ba kum la, bɔ'ɔra taaba bo'olum, se'ere en sɔi la, nɔtɔgeheba bana ba yi' wa daa ba kpa' la timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a nyɔya.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Gee dabeha atã la girego la en tole la, vo'ohum n tari vom en ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an na kɛ̃ ba, ti ba ihege, ti sɛba woo en nyɛ ba la, dabeem kãtɛ en kɛ̃ ba.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Ti ba wum ti kua kɛ'ɛŋa ze'ele saazuo na yele ba yeti, Zom ya ka'lam na! Ti ba zɔna saazuo sagbanɛ puan, ba dinduma la ninin.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Ŋwana en tole la, ti mam nyɛ ti suŋereba banaahi zɛ'a timpaberɛ wa luga anaahi la puan, gurɛ timpaberɛ wa luga anaahi la za'a kusebego, ti kusebego da lobe timpaberɛ wa zuo, bii ateko puan bii ku lobe bahɛ tisɛka zuo.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Sankaŋa puan, ti tinmiiŋo kãtɛ damɛ, ti tiŋa la yiya puregerɛ pia lu, ti nɛreba tuha ayopɔi ki, tinmiiŋo la puan, ti dabeem kɛ̃ sɛba en yee la, ti ba dikɛ pɛka bo' Yinɛ en sɔi saazuo ha la.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Ti mam nyɛ suŋera ayima, ti ka ze'ele dapɔya sigera na tara Yinɛ vua wa daalehum, gee yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa, suŋereba banaahi la ti ba bo' ba paŋa ti ba sagum timpaberɛ wa, la ateko la.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Yi'i yele la tole ya. Bihɛ ta'a yele la me kɔ'ɔm yɛ'a la nananawa na.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: A yele ba yeti, Da sagum ya timpaberɛ la, bii ateko la, bii tiihi la, gura ti tu daalɛ tu Yinɛ yamehi la daalehum busebo ba gbɛ'ɛra ba'ahɛ.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la peebe a tintanɛ la, ti kuuhi kekeko ze'ele saazuo ha vɔɔla baha na yeti, Tiŋa zuo sɔ'ɔlum la nyaa dela tumam Daana la dinɛ la a Kiresta la dinɛ, eŋa en wan dɛna ka naba, wuuha wuuha.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Ti mam wum ti sɛba ti ba wan daalɛ ba la dela tuha kɔbega la tuha pihinaahi la a naahi, Izirale buuri woo puan.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Ti kiinduma pihiyi la banaahi la en zi' ba na'am kugehi zuto Yinɛ nɛŋan la, lu tiŋa aliko na'ahɛ Yinɛ.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Yuda buuri puan nɛre tuha pia la ayi' Aruben buuri puan, nɛre tuha pia la ayi'. Gad buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: yuuna yeti, Tu bo' fum fara fara, Gani za'a Yinɛ wa, Se'em en de, gee daa pun dɛna, Se'ere en sɔi la, fum dikɛ la fum pankãtɛ la, pɔhɛ bihera tiŋa wa.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Asere buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Nafetali buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Manaasɛ buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Tihi se'ehi en ka dole fum ihege la suure, gee fum leregere saŋa en ŋwana. Saŋa la paaɛ ya ti fum gakɛ sɛba woo en ki la, la fum yɔ fum yamehi la en de fum nɔtɔgeheba la, la fum nɛresunduma la, la sɛba woo en daa pu'uheri fum yu'urɛ la, ba tarenduma, la kpɛ'ɛnduma za'a, gee me sagum sɛba woo en daa sagum timpaberɛ wa la.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: Semiyɔn buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Levi buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Isaka buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Ti Yinɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la en boi saazuo ha la yu'ɛ, ti nɔpu'ulegɔ daka la zɛ'a pu'uhegɔ deo la puan, ti bugum nyagera, ti dameŋa damena, ti saa tahera, ti tiŋa miina, ti sakuga kãra ka'ara na.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Zebulun buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Yosefe buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi'. Benyamin buuri puan, tuha pia la ayi' en daalɛ.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Ŋwana en tole la, mam nyɛ la nɛrekuuŋɔ ti nɛra kan ta'am kãalɛ ba, en ze'ele tihi woo, buuri woo, nɛreba woo, la tugum woo puan na, zɛ'a na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la Pebila la nɛŋan, yɛ salema lɔge pɛɛla, zãla kunkɔɔreŋɔ vɔtɔ ba nu'uhen.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Kaahɛ bahɛ na la kɔ kɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Faarɛ ze'eti la tumam Yinɛ wa, se'em en yage na'am kuka kãtɛ wa zuo wa, la Pebila wa zi'an na.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Ti suŋereba za'a ze'ele kaaɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la kiinduma la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la, ti ba za'a lu aliko na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan, na'ahɛ Yinɛ.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: yuuna yeti, Taari, Sum, la suŋa la yɛm la fara fara la na'ahegɔ la paŋa la kpe'eŋo dɛna tumam Yinɛ la dinɛ wuuha wuuha, Taari.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Ti kiinduma la ayima nyaa yele mam yeti, Bani en de ba yɛ fupɛɛla wa, ba ze'ele la bɛha na.

eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Ti mam yele en yeti, Naa, fum en de se'em en mi'. Ti a nyaa yele mam yeti, Bana dela sɛba en ze'ele namehegɔ kãtɛ puan yehe na. Ba pee ba futo mɛ, ti tu pelege kirikiri Pebila la ziim la puan.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Ŋwana zuo ti ba ta'am paaɛ Yinɛ na'am kuka wa nɛŋan na wa, gee na'aha en beere en yilegɛ woo, la yu'uŋɔ a pu'uhegɔ yire wa puan wa, ti se'em en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ wa zuo la wan gura ba.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Ba kan mina kom, bii ko'nyuuro le yɛ'ɛha Wuntɛɛŋa ka le si'ira ba, bii wɔlegɔ ŋmɛ'ɛra ba.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Pebila la en boi na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la en wan dɛna ba dunkiina, wan tari ba kina vom ko'sumɔ kulaa la puan ti ba nyuura, gee ti Yinɛ wan duuhe ba nintam woo ba ninin bahɛ.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Ti daalehum kãtɛ niiɛ saazuo ha, ti pɔka yɛ wuntɛɛŋa wa wuu futo la, ti ŋmarega bɔna ka nama tiŋa, ti ka vuga muŋa en de ŋmarebibehi pia la siyi.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: Ti pɔka wa tara puurɛ, virege kaaha la too, kɔ'ɔm wula ti ka dɔgɛ.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Ti daalehum kãtɛ bu yima me niiɛ saazuo ha, ti bunsɛla kãtɛ mɔlegɔ kuyima niiɛ na, tara zuto tu yopɔi, la iila pia, vuge muhi siyopɔi zuo kuyina kuyina woo.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Ti a zuurɛ ve'e ŋmarebibehi buyina puregerɛ butã puan saazuo ha bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na. Ti bunsɛla wa nyaa zɛ'a pɔka wa en boi bini ti ka dɔgɛ wa nɛŋan, ti a obe bia la, ka han kɔ'ɔm lu na.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Ti pɔka la dɔgɛ bi budibela en yeti ka dɛna tihi wa za'a naba, la ka tara kuto doore biha ka nɛreba. Gee bia wa en ta luta na la, ba zɛ ka mɛ tɔ', tari ka kiŋɛ Yinɛ zi'an, Yinɛ na'am kuka la zuo.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Gee ti pɔka la zɔ kɛ̃ muuen ha, ta nyɛ zi'an ti Yinɛ pun maahum biŋe ka, ti ka bɔna bilam ti ba biha ka suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ dabeha tuhere, la dabeha kɔbehi siyi, la dabeha pihiyoobe.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Ti zaberɛ kɔ' Yinɛ yire ha, ti Maikɛl, la Yinɛ suŋereba la zabera la bunsɛla la. Ti bunsɛla la, la a suŋereba la me zabera lɛbeha na.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: gee ba daa nyaŋɛ ba mɛ, ti zi'an ka le bɔna tinsuŋan la ha bo' ba.

eng: So the great dragon was thrown down. The old snake, who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world, was thrown down to the earth; and his angels were thrown down with him.
local: Ti ba lobe bunsɛla kãtɛ wa bahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo na. Se'em en de diimen diimen zugɔ la ti ba yi'ira Sagena la Sitaani, se'em en pa'aheri tiŋa wa zuo za'a nɛreba la. Ba lobe en bahɛ la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, lagum lobe a suŋereba la, la en bahɛ na.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Ti mam wum kɔkɛ'ɛŋa saazuo la ha en muuli yeti, Nananawa ti Faarɛ, la paŋa, la Yinɛ sɔ'ɔlum la, la Kiresta, kpe'eŋo la saŋa nyaa paaɛ. Se'ere en sɔi la, ba lobe se'em en zuuheri tu suurɔ la, bahɛ mɛ. Se'em en daa zuuheri ba bulika la yu'uŋɔ tu Yinɛ wa nɛŋan la.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Gee ba nyaŋɛ en mɛ la Pebila la ziim la, la ba nuurɛ yetɔgum, se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa ka fabele ba vom yele, la kum nɛŋan me.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Bala zuo, nyɛɛna, tinsuŋan wa, la yamam sɛba en boi bini la. Gee yele kãtɛ boi timpaberɛ wa, la ko'kãtɛ wa zuo, la Sagena la en sige ya puan na, la sunsunyiim kãtɛ la, Se'ere en sɔi la, a mi' ti a saŋa ka wugɛ.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Bunsɛla wa en ta baŋɛ ti ba lobe en bahɛ la timpaberɛ wa zuo na la, ti a pɔhɛ pɔka la en dɔgɛ bibudibela la dika.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Gee ba bo' pɔka wa la tugeho kãtɛ kelumpagehi siyi, ti ka ɛkɛ piiɛ bunsɛla la, kɛ̃ muuen ha, ta bɔna zi'an ti ka wan bɔna ti ba biha en suŋa suŋa ta paaɛ saŋa, la sahi la saŋa girego.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Ti bunsɛla wa ɔkɛ ko'om a nuuren na, ti bu zɔta wuu kulega la, digera pɔka wa, ti bu di pɔka wa.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Gee timpaberɛ en suŋɛ pɔka la, doohe la tiŋa la ya'ɛ mɛ vole kulaa la ti bunsɛla la ɔkɛ a nuuren bahɛ na la.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Ti bunsɛla la suure nyiie la pɔka la, ti a lebe, ta kɔ' zaberɛ la pɔka la kɔma basɛba la, sɛba en dole Yinɛ nuurɛ, gee tari Yezu yɛla la.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Ti bunsɛla la ba' a mɛŋa ze'ele ko'kãtɛ tintombi'ihego la puan.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti bunsilemasio ihege ko'kãtɛ wa puan, tara iila pia, la zuto tuyopɔi, ti muŋa vuge iilɛ woo, gee ti ba gulehɛ tuurɛ yu'ura labelɛ a zuo woo.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Bunsilemasio sɛka ti mam nyɛ la ani mɛ wuu yeeobaa la, ti a nama ana wuu saheŋa nama, ti a nuurɛ ana wuu gbigenɛ nuurɛ la. Ti bunsɛla la bo' en a paŋa, la a na'am la, la a kpe'eŋo kãtɛ la.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Ti bunsilemasio wa zuo la kuyima ana wuu ba pɔgelum mɛ yɛ'a kum, gee ti pɔgeleŋɔ kɛka la wa'am na bɛ'e. Ti timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a dɔla bunsilemasio wa la pakerɛ.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Ba na'aheri bunsɛla la mɛ, la eŋa en dikɛ a kpe'eŋo kãtɛ la bo' bunsilemasio la, gee na'aha bunsilemasio la me yeti, Ani en boi ana wuu bunsilemasio wa, ani en boi wan ta'am zabɛ la ka.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Ti ba bo' bunsilemasio wa nuurɛ se'ere en tɔgeri yele kãra, la tuurɛ kãra. Ti ba bo' en sore ti a dikɛ a paŋa la tum ŋmarehi pihinaahi la siyi.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Ti bunsɛla wa ya'ɛ a nuurɛ tuura Yinɛ, tuura a yu'urɛ la, la a tanɛ pu'uhegɔ yire la, la sɛba woo en boi tinsuŋan ha la.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Ti ba me le bo' en sore ti a zabɛ la nɛresunduma la nyaŋɛ ba. Ba bo' en la kpe'eŋo ti a sɔna buuri woo, la nɛreba woo, la tugum woo la tihi woo.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Ti nɛreba woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo wan na'aha en. Se'em woo yu'urɛ en ka gulehɛ iŋɛ vom gɔŋɔ la gee ti ba baŋɛ naam timpaberɛ wa la, gɔŋɔ wa dela pebila wa gɔŋɔ, pebila sɛka ti ba kɔregɛ la.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em woo en tari tuba, a kɛleha.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: La han dɛna la ba nyɔgɛ ya, ba nyɔgere ya yɛ'a mɛ, ya han ku mɛ la su'a, su'a en kuuri ya. Ŋwana dela sɛba en de nɛresunduma la, ya ŋmibera, gee sɛ zɛ'a ya sakerɛ la puan.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Ti mam le nyɛ ti bunsilemasio kayima me tahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo yehe na, tara iila ayi' wuu pebila iila la, ti a kua ana wuu bunsɛla la.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: Ti a tum yia bunsilemasio la pahi la za'a bo' yia bunsilemasio la, bahɛ ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a, na'aha yia bunsilemasio la, se'em nɔre-kɛka en be'ɛ la.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Ti yi'i bunsilemasio wa tum kirehi kãra, ta pun bahɛ ti bugum ze'ele saazuo ha lu timpaberɛ wa zuo na, timpaberɛ wa nɛreba za'a ninin.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Ti doohe la kirehi wa ti ba bo' sore ti a ze'ele yia bunsilemasio la na'arɛ tum bo' en la, ti a nyaŋɛ pã'ahɛ timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba za'a, yɛta ba yeti, ba maalɛ yia bunsilemasio la ti su'a pɔgelum en gee ti a sina gee vɔna la naanɛ.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: ti bunsilemasio wa bo' naanɛ la vo'ohum, ti naanɛ wa pun tana tɔgera, gee kuura sɛba en ka sakɛ ba na'aha bunsilemasio wa naanɛ la.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Gee ti naanɛ wa doohe nɛra woo, bumbila la bunkutɛ, buntata la naha, se'em en boi fai, la da'aberɛ za'a daalɛ ba zuɔ nu'uhi, bii ba gbɛ'ɛra.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: ti se'em ka bɔna wan ta'am da' bii koohe sɛla gee ka tara bunsilemasio la daalehum la . Daalehum la en de bunsilemasio la yu'urɛ, bii a yu'urɛ la kãlɛ.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Yɛm en nari ka'lam. Bahɛ ti se'em en tari yɛm lagum bunsilemasio wa kãlɛ la, se'ere en sɔi la, de dela nɛresaala kãlɛ. Kãlɛ la dela kɔbehi siyoobe, la pihiyoobe la buyoobe.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Ti mam bihɛ, ti pebila la en bala, zɛ'a Zaayɔn Zoore la zuo! Ti sɛba en boi la en la dela, nɛre tuha kɔbega la tuha pinaahi la tuha anaahi la ti ba gulehɛ Pebila la yu'urɛ la a Sɔ yu'urɛ labelɛ ba gbɛ'ɛra.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Ti mam wum ti kua ze'ele tinsuŋan na, sũna wuu kulaahi ko'om en sũni la, gee tahera wuu saa en taheri la. Kua la ti mam wum la ani mɛ wuu kɔ̃len-ŋmɛ'ɛreba en ŋmɛ'ɛri ba kɔlɔ la.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Ti ba yuuna yuum paalɛ na'am kuka kãtɛ la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la kiinduma la nɛŋan. Ti nɛra ka bɔna mina yuunɛ la, han dagena tuha kɔbega la tuha pinaahi la anaahi la ma'a ti ba yɔ to'e ba timpaberɛ wa zuo la.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Nɛrebana wa ka galum ba mɛhi la pɔgehi, se'ere en sɔi la, ba daa dela burakaha. Bana en dole Pebila la zi'an woo ti ka yɛ'a. Ba yɔ to'e ba mɛ saaleba woo puan ti ba dɛna wala diina bo' Yinɛ, la Pebila la.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: ti pumpɔreŋɔ ka tabelɛ ze'ele ba nɔyan na, ba ka tari gbelegere zi'an.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ayima ɛgeri sagbama wa zuo, tara faarɛ yelehum la en ka tari ba'ahegɔ la, ti a muulɛ bo' sɛba en vɔi timpaberɛ wa zuo, tiŋa woo, buuri woo, la tugum woo, la nɛreba woo.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Ti a yele la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Zoti ya Yinɛ, gee bo' ya en pɛka, se'ere en sɔi la, a gakerɛ saŋa la paaɛ ya. Me na'ahɛ ya en, eŋa se'em en naam saazuo la tiŋa, ko'kãtɛ, la kulaahi, ko'om zi'ihi woo la.

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Ti suŋera ayima, en pa'ahɛ buyi' la, me dɔla yeti, A lu ya, Babilɔn paŋadaana la lu ya! A bahɛ ti tihi woo nyu a yaalegɔ sunsunyiim daam la mɛ.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Ti suŋera ayima, butã daana me dɔla na, kaaha la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Sɛba woo en na'ahɛ bunsilemasio la, la ka naanɛ la, gee to'e ka daalehum ba gbɛ'ɛran bii ba nu'uhen la.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: ba me wan nyu Yinɛ sunsunyiim daam la en ka'ɛ iŋɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim wula puan, ti sɛla ka gɛregɛ bɔ la. Ba wan nyɛ namehegɔ bugum puan, la tahi bugum puan, Fa'a suŋereba la nɛŋan, la Pebila la nɛŋan.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Ti ba namehegɔ zu'uhi kɔ'ɔm gulegera zɔna saazuo ka tara ba'ahegɔ. Vo'ohego ka boi, beyiine, bii yu'uŋɔ bɔ'ɔra sɛba en na'ahɛ bunsilemasio la, la a naanɛ la, la se'em woo en to'e bunsilemasio la daalehum la en de a yu'urɛ la.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Ŋwana yi'iri la nɛresunduma la ti ba ŋmibe, la sɛba woo en tari Yinɛ nuurɛ la, gee gurɛ Yezu sakerɛ la kankaŋi la.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Ti mam wum kua tinsuŋan la, ti ka yeti, Gulehɛ dina wa biŋe yeti, Yehe zina yɛ'a ha, Sum wan bɔna la sɛba woo en ki Daana la sore dɔlega puan. Sia la yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, Ba wan vo'ohe ba namehegɔ la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ba tuuma la wan dɔla ba.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: Ti mam bihɛ nyɛ ti sagbanɛ peele niiɛ na, ti se'em en zi' de zuo la wuna la Asaala Dayua la, ti salema muŋa vuge a zuo, ti kɛɛrega en diti bɔna a nu'en!.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Ti suŋera ayima ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan yehe na, yi' se'em daana en yage sagbanɛ la zuo la, la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Dikɛ fum kɛɛrega la kɛ̃, se'ere en sɔi la kɛ'a saŋa la paaɛ ya. Beni zuo, timpaberɛ wa zuo dia la bi'ɛ mɛ.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Bala ti se'em en zi sagbanɛ la zuo la, lobege a kɛɛrega la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, kɛ̃ timpaberɛ wa zuo filam.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Ti suŋera ayima le ze'ele tinsuŋan pu'uhegɔ yire la puan yehe na, me zãla kɛɛrega n diti.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Ti suŋera ayima me ze'ele nyɔ'a daka la bɔba yehe na, ti suŋera sɛka en tari bugum paŋa la, yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa bo' se'em en zãli kɛɛrega la en diti la yeti, Dikɛ fum kɛɛrega la en diti la kɛ̃ tiihi wala timpaberɛ wa zuo za'a, se'ere en sɔi la, si wala la bi'ɛ mɛ.

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Ti suŋera la lobege a kɛɛrega la timpaberɛ wa zuo na, kɛ̃ tiihi wala za'a, ka'ɛ ba iŋɛ daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko kãtɛ en de Yinɛ sunsunyiim daam boko la puan .

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Daam boko wa me boi la tiŋa la yiŋa ha, ti ziim ze'ele daam ŋmɛkerɛ boko wa puan ɔkera, paara yeeho gareŋenɛ, zɔta yɛ'a wuu miila kɔbehi siyi.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Ti mam nyɛ yele tinsuŋan ha, yele n karegɛ gee yalegɛ nuurɛ. Suŋereba bayopɔi en tari namehegɔ tuyopɔi, en de ba'ahegɔ namehegɔ. Bama en wan tum ba'ahɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim la.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti sɛla niiɛ na, ana wuu ateko la, gee dɛna salema nyagera, ti bugum gɛregɛ ko'om la puan. Ti sɛba en nyaŋɛ bunsilemasio la, ka pu'uhɛ a naanɛ la, ka to'e a yu'urɛ la, la a kãlɛ la, nyaa zɛ'a salema ateko wa nuuren, pɛga kɔlɔ ti Yinɛ dikɛ bo' ba.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: Ti ba nyaa yuuna Moose en de Yinɛ yameŋa yuunɛ la, la Pebila la yuunɛ la yeti, Fum tuuma karegɛ mɛ gee dɛna pakerɛ, Daana Yinɛ kãtɛ wa. Fum sadɔtɔ za'a dela tuuntɔɔ la yelemɛŋerɛ, tihi wa za'a naba wa.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Daana, ani en kan zɔta fum, gee na'aha fum yu'urɛ wa? Se'ere en sɔi la, fum ma'a en de Fa'a. Tihi za'a wan wa'am fum nɛŋan na, na'aha hɔ, se'ere en sɔi la, fum tuuntɔɔ tuuma la za'a niiɛ mɛ na.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Dina pooren ti mam bihɛ, ti pu'uhegɔ yire wa tanɛ deo la en de aze'nyɛ deo bɔna tinsuŋan la yu'ɛ.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: ti tanɛ deo wa puan, ti suŋereba bayopɔi la ze'ele bini yehe na la bã'ahi siyopɔi, yɛ fupeelo n nyagere, ti salema tama paga ba nyu'urɔ zuto.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Ti bunvɔɔhi banaahi la ayima, bo' suŋereba bayopɔi wa, salema laahi siyopɔi en pirɛ la Yinɛ sunsunyiim, Yinɛ se'ere en vɔi wuuha wuuha la.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Ti pu'uhegɔ yire la pirɛ la zu'uhi en ze'eti Yinɛ suŋa la puan na, la a paŋa la puan na, ti se'em ka bɔna wan kɛ̃ pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, gura ti bã'ahi siyopɔi la ti suŋereba bayopɔi la tara la ta tum ba'ahɛ.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Ti suŋereba bayopɔi la puan ayima, sɛba en zãli laahi siyopɔi la, wa'am na yele mam yeti, Wa'ana ti n pa'alɛ hɔ, yiyɛkɛka la en ki'ire lɛm kulaahi wa ti Yinɛ wan ta sagum en se'em.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Se'em ti timpaberɛ wa zuo na'duma tum yaluntuuma la en, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba tum yaalum tuuma la en ta buge wuu ba nyu la daam la.

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Lilegere wa puan, ti suŋera wa tari mam sia puan, ta kɛ̃ muuen ha, ti mam ta nyɛ ti pɔka yage bunsilemasio mɔlegɔ zuo, ti tuurɛ yu'ura ma'a gulehɛ lama duŋa la iŋa za'a. Ti duŋa la tara zuto tuyopɔi, la iila pia.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Ti pɔka wa bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fumɔlɔ en gulɛ la salema, bãhi, la peranini, ti a nu'en a zãli la salema wula, ti ka pirɛ la tuunfi'iha, la a yaalegɔ la dɛgerɔ ma'a.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Ti yu'urɛ en de sugelum yu'urɛ, gulehɛ lama pɔka la gbi'iri yeti, Babilɔn kãtɛ la en de yiyɛkɛka la, Yaalegɔ za'a ma la ti timpaberɛ wa za'a galeŋɔ lɔkɔ.

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti pɔka wa buge mɛ la en nyu Yinɛ nɛresunduma ziim, la sɛba en muuli Yezu yele la sukpe'ene la ziim la. Mam en nyɛ ka la, la ba' iŋɛ mam la pakerɛ.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Gee suŋera la yele mam yeti,Beni ti la ba' iŋɛ hɔ pakerɛ bala? N wan yele fum pɔka wa sugelum yele, la duŋa la en tara zuto tuyopɔi, la iila pia ti ka yaga ka zuo la.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Bunsilemasio la ti fum nyɛ ni la, daa vɔi mɛ, gee ki, gee nyaa bɔna bini ti ku ze'ele boko kãtɛ la en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha la dooe na, ti ba sagum en. Ti sɛba woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo ti ba yu'ura ka gulehɛ bɔna vom gɔŋɔ la puan tiŋa wa pi'ileŋo puan na la, la wan ta iŋɛ ba pakerɛ ba han le ta nyɛ bunsilemasio la, beni zuo, ku daa boi mɛ, gee ki, gee le yeti ku vo'e na.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: Putɛ'se'ere en tari bɔkerɛ n wan baŋɛ dina wa. Zuto tuyopɔi la dela zɔya ayopɔi ti pɔge yiyɛa wa yaga a zuo, gee me ze'ele bo' naduma bayopɔi.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: ti ba puan banuu ki, ti ayima vɔna, gee ti ayima la nan ka wa'am na. Gee a han ta wa'am na, a wan bɔna la dabeha fii wa.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Gee bunsilemasio seko en daa boi gee ki la, eŋa pa'aheri la banii, lagum pa'ahɛ bayopɔi la ti ba yeti ba sagum ba la puan.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: Gee iila pia la me ti fum nyɛ la dela, naduma pia en nan ka to'e sɔ'ɔlum biha, gee ba wan to'e na'am kpe'eŋo, gogo kaareŋa buyina ma'a, tum la bunsilemasio la.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Bana za'a iŋɛ la puti'irɛ deyina, ti ba dikɛ ba paŋa, la ba kpe'eŋo bo' bunsilemasio la.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Ba wan kɔ' zaberɛ la Pebila la, gee Pebila la wan nyaŋɛ ba. Se'ere en sɔi la, Pebila la en de Daanduma Daana, la Naduma naba. Ti sɛba en boi la en la, a yi' ba mɛ, gee loohe ba ti ba dɛna sakereba.

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Ti suŋera la yele mam yeti, Ko'om la ti fum nyɛ ni sebo ti yiyɛkɛka la zi' ni bu zuo la, ze'ele bo' la timpaberɛ wa zuo nɛreba, nɛrebɔbegɔ, tihi, la tugum.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Ti iila pia la ti fum nyɛ ni la, bamam la bunsilemasio la wan ta yaaɛ yiyɛkɛka wa, mugehe en ta bahɛ en beele, ba wan aahum a iŋa obe, gee nyɔ' en bugumen.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ en lu' ba suya puan, ti ba tum eŋa en boti se'em la, doohe ba dikɛ ba sɔ'ɔlum bo' bunsilemasio la, gura ti Yinɛ yelehum la ta wa'am na tum.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: Pɔka la ti fum nyɛ la ze'ele bo' la tinkãtɛ sɛka en sɔi naduma woo en boi timpaberɛ wa zuo biha la.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Ti mam wum kɔkɛ'ɛŋa pu'uhegɔ yire la puan ti ka tɔgera bɔ'ɔra suŋereba bayopɔi la yeti, Kiŋɛ ya ta ka'ahɛ Yinɛ sunsunyiim laahi siyopɔi la bahɛ timpaberɛ la zuo.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Ti yia suŋera la kiŋɛ ta ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ timpaberɛ la zuo, ti nɔtankɛ'ɛhi la nɔre-kãra en dune paaɛ paaɛ sige sɛba woo en tari bunsɛla la daalehum la, la sɛba woo en pu'uheri bunsilemasio la naanɛ la zuo.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Ti yi'i suŋera la me kiŋɛ ta ka'ɛ a laa la, bahɛ ateko puan, ti ateko la lebege ana wuu kum ziim la, ti bunvua woo en boi ateko la puan ki.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Ti ta'a suŋera la wa'am na ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ mɔga, la kulaahi puan, ti ba ko'om la lebege ziim.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Ti mam wum ti suŋera sɛka en biheri ko'om la yeti, Fum dela sira Daana, Fa'a Daana wa, Se'em en de, gee daa pun Dɛna, Se'ere en sɔi la fum gakɛ yele ana wa mɛ.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, bamam en daa kɔrum nɛresunduma la, nɔtɔgeheba la. Fum bo' ba la ziim ti ba nyu. Sɛla en nari ba en bala.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Ti mam wum ti kaabegɔ bimbine la kɔkɛ yeti, Ɛ̃ɛ, Daana Yinɛ, Gani za'a Daana, Fum gakerɛ wa dela yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuntɔɔ.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Lilegere dina en tole la, ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ayima ze'ele saazuo sigera na tara kpe'eŋo en ka tari makerɛ. Ti timpaberɛ wa za'a niiɛ piim la a suŋa.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Ti suŋera bunaahi daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ yintule zuo, ti ba bo' yintule la sore ti a taaɛ nɛreba la bugum.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Ti suŋera wa yi' la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yeti, Wai wai, a lu ya, a lu ya, Babilɔn en de tinkãtɛ la lu ya! Ka nyaa lebege la kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi bɔŋa zi'an, Ka dela kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi se'ehi en fi'ɛ bɔŋa zi'an. Ka dela niihe se'ehi en fi'ɛ bɔŋa zi'an, la bunsɛlebɛ'ɛhi bɔŋa zi'an.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Ti wuntɛɛntuulega sirum si'ira ba, ti ba tugum ŋmɛregɛ ka'ara be'em baha Yinɛ yu'urɛ la, se'em en tari namehegɔ yele ana wa paŋa la, gee tugum ka tee ba suya, dikɛ pɛka bo' en.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Se'ere en sɔi la, a bahɛ ti tihi woo en nyu a yaalegɔ sunsunyiim daam la, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma tum yaluntuuma la en. Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo leebeduma pãrɛ la a tarega la paŋa.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Ti suŋera bunuu daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ bunsilemasio la sɔ'ɔlum la puan, ti a sɔ'ɔlum la lebege lika ma'a, ti nɛreba duna ba zɛlehi la wuŋerɛ.

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Ti mam wum ti kua ka yima ze'ele saazuo na yeti, Yehe ya a puan na n nɛreba wa, la ya da lagum tum a be'em la, ta lagum to'e a namehegɔ la.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: gee ka'ara be'em baha Yinɛ se'ere en sɔi saazuo wa yu'urɛ la, ba wuŋerɛ la, la ba nɔra kãra la zuo, gee la bala za'a, ba ka tee ba tuumbe'ero la puan.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: A be'em la duurum mɛ wuu zoore la, paara saazuo ha. Yinɛ tiɛ a tuure kãra la mɛ.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Ti suŋera buyoobe daana la ka'ɛ a laa bahɛ Yufaratesi mɔgekãtɛ la puan, ti de ko'om la kiihɛ za'a, ti la ta'am maalɛ sore bo' dapɔya bɔba naduma la.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Bo' en wuu eŋa en bo' se'em la, lebehe yɔ a be'em la sugum nuuren buyi'. Pirɛ a wanɛ sugum nuuren buyi' la daam kpe'eŋo bo' en wuu eŋa en pirɛ se'em bo' la.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti sɛfi'iha sitã, en ani wuu lono la, ti kayima ze'ele bunsɛla la nuuren yehe na, ti kayima ze'ele bunsilemasio la nuuren yehe na, ti kayima ze'ele nɔtɔgeha pumparema la nuuren yehe na.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Wuu eŋa en daa pa'ali a mɛŋa se'em, dɛla se'em fai a na'am puan la, lebehe namehegɔ la susageŋɔ bala mɛa bo' en. Wuu en diŋɛ yele a suuren ti, N dela pɔge naba, n dagi pokuurɛ, susageŋɔ kan malum paaɛ mam la.
