eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Bana wa dela kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi se'ehi en tuna kirehi, kina timpaberɛ wa zuo na'kãreba nɛŋan, ti ba lagehɛ ba gura zaberɛ, dabeherɛ kãtɛ la en de Gani Za'a Yinɛ dabeherɛ la puan.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: Bala zuo, la kan yuue la a namehegɔ en sigeri a zuo na; nɔyalegerɛ, la kaahega la kom pɛɛlega. Bugum wan taaɛ en di ta bugehe bugehe. Se'ere en sɔi la, Kpe'eŋo daana la, Daana Yinɛ la en gakeri en la.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Ba wan lagehɛ la zi'an ti ba yi'ira Hiburu puan ti Hamagedɔn la.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma sɛba en tuni yaluntuuma la en, gee dɛla fai a puan la, wan kɛla gee si'ina a zuo, ba han ta nyɛ en diti bugum ti a zu'uhi gulegera la.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Ti suŋera buyopɔi daana la ka'ɛ a laa la bahɛ sabeeo puan, ti kɔkɛ'ɛŋa ze'ele pu'uhegɔ yire la puan, na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo na yeti, La tum ya.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Ba wan zɛ'a zaaɛ zaarɛ ha ti dabeem kɛ̃ ba, la namehegɔ seko en sigeri a zuo la. Ba wan yɛta yeti, Wai, wai, tinkãtɛ la, Babilɔn tinkãtɛ la. Nananawa ma'a ti gakerɛ dina sige fum zuo ŋwana na.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Ti bugum nyagera, ti dameŋa damena, ti saa tahera, ti tinmiiŋo kãtɛ sige, tinmiiŋo seko en ka tabelɛ sige timpaberɛ wa zuo, nɛreba en pɔhɛ bɔna timpaberɛ wa zuo daarɛ na. Tinmiiŋo la ba' gaŋɛ mɛ.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Ti timpaberɛ wa zuo leebeduma wan kɛla gee si'ina a zuo, se'ere en sɔi la, nɛra ka le da'ara ba lɔgerɔ la.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Ti tinkãtɛ la bɔgɛ butã, ti tihi woo tinkãra wɔ̃rɛ. Ti Yinɛ tiɛ tinkãtɛ Babilɔn yele, bo' en daam wanɛ en de a sunsunyiim kãtɛ daam .

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: Ba lɔgerɔ la en de salema, salempɛɛlega, nunɔm zɔtɔ la peranini, tanpɛɛla, tanmɔla, tansaala, tansaalehi mɔlɔ, la dɔsunɔ yima yima, la wɔbenyɛna lɔgerɔ yima yima, la dɔsunɔ lɔgerɔ yima yima, nyɔgum, kuto, la bɔrumbɔlehi kuga.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Ti tihi se'ehi en boi ko'kãra tiŋasuka la mɛlum, ti zoore la deyina me ka bɔna.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: sinamɔn tiihi kaam, puuro seto en tari nyɛlum, digelum, la Mɛɛ, nyuuŋo suŋɔ puuro, daam, olive kaam, kisuma zom la mui, nii, piihi, yiiri la yiiri toto, la nɛresaaleba vom ti ba kɔɔha ba da'aba.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: ti saa kuga kãra en tibegɛ gana nɛɛrɛ kugere ze'ele saazuo ha ka'ara baha nɛreba la zuo na, tara tara ti nɛreba la ta pɔta be'em baha Yinɛ yu'urɛ la zuo, saa kuga la zuo. Bã'ahi sina ba' tara la dabeem.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Fum lɔgesuma seto ti fum sia bɔta la woo tole ya gee bahɛ hɔ, ti fum dikãresuma la za'a tole, fum lɔgeseto woo en nyagere la za'a bɔregɛ mɛ gee bahɛ hɔ, kan le malum lebe na.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Leebeduma sɛba en sɔi lɔge tuna woo la, sɛba en doohe fum puan pãrɛ la za'a wan zɛ'a zaaɛ zaarɛ ha la dabeem, wuŋerɛ se'ere en sigeri fum zuo la, kɛla gee si'ina baha na.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: Wai, wai, tinkãtɛ la, en daa ni bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fusunɔ la, bagɛ ka mɛŋa la fumɔlɔ suma, en gulɛ la salema, bãhi, la peranini.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: ti nananawa ma'a ti lɔge tuna woo mɛlum. Ti uurenɔ kãra kpɛ'ɛnduma, sɛba en tari uurenɔ tuna, la sɛba woo en dole ko'kãtɛ la zuo kɔɔha la za'a ze' la zaaɛ zaarɛ ha.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: gee kaaha baha na, ban nyɛ ti a taaɛ bugum dita, ti a zu'uhi gulegera na la. Tinkani en boi wuna la tinkãtɛ kana wa.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Gee nyaa vaara tintɔnɔ yɛɛla ba zuto, gee kɛla gee fabela, kaaha vɔɔla yeti, Aba, aba, tinkãtɛ wa, Fum zi'an ti sɛba woo en tari uurenɔ fum ko'kãra wa zuo, pãrɛ la lɔgerɔ. Gee nananawa ma'a en bala ti tiŋa wa gã'a voore wa.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Nyɛɛna ya baha a zuo na saazuo duma wa, yamam nɛresunduma, tuntuneba, la nɔtɔgeheba wa, nyɛɛni ya. Se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ gakɛ en mɛ ya zuo.

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Ti suŋera ayima en de bunkpe'eŋo ɔ̃begɛ kugere en ani wuu nɛɛrɛ kugekãtɛ en ka tari makerɛ la lobe bahɛ ko'kãtɛ puan, gee yeti, Gbiregere dina taaba en wan lubɛ Babilɔn tinkãtɛ wa ti ka, kan le malum bɔna.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Kɔlɔ ŋmɛ'a kan le bɔna, ti baam baanduma, la nyiihe, la benkuna peebego, kan le wuna fum puan le yɛ'ɛha ti se'em daana en mi' koohego kan le bɔna fum puan yɛ'ɛha Nɛɛŋɔ kuga dameŋa, kan le bɔna fum puan yɛ'ɛha.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Butolela bugum niihum, kan le niiɛ fum puan, ti pɔge dita kua, la sire-ɛla kua kan le bɔna fum puan. Se'ere en sɔi la, fum leebeduma la en daa de tiŋa wa zuo nɛrekãreba, ti fum dikɛ fum nini la pã'ahɛ tihi wa za'a.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Fum en ka'ahɛ nɔtɔgeheba la, la nɛresunduma la za'a ziim, la sɛba woo ti ba kɔrum ba la su'a timpaberɛ wa zuo la.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Ŋwana en tole la, ti mam wum ti sɛla ana wuu nɛrekuuŋɔ kuuhi la, tinsuŋan ha, yɛta yeti, Haleluya! Faarɛ, la suŋa, la kpe'eŋo dɛna tu Yinɛ dinɛ.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: A gakerɛ dela yelemɛŋerɛ la tuuntɔɔ. A gakɛ yiyɛkɛka wa en doohe galum timpaberɛ wa za'a la, a yaalegɔ la mɛ, gee lerege en ka'ahɛ yamehi la ziim la.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Ti kuuhi wa le voole pa'ahɛ yeti, Haleluya a zu'uhi en bala gulegera ka ba'aha la.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Ti kiinduma pihiyi' la banaahi la, la bunvɔɔhi banaahi la, lu tiŋa na'ahɛ Yinɛ en zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la yeti, Amɛn. Haleluya.

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Ti na'am kuka kãtɛ la puan, kua ze'ele bini na yeti, Pɛgɛ ya tu Yinɛ la, yamam a yamehi wa woo, yamam sɛba en zoti en wa, tarenduma, la kpɛ'ɛnduma za'a.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Ti mam wum ti sɛla ana wuu nɛrekuuŋɔ kuuhi la, damena wuu ko'kiŋɔ en sũni la, damena wuu saa en dameni la, kaaha baha na yeti, Haleluya! Tu Daana Yinɛ la, Gani za'a Daana la eŋa en de naba.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Bahɛ ya ti tu nyɛɛna, gee zɛkera a yu'urɛ, gee bo' ya en pɛka, se'ere en sɔi la, Pebila la pɔgedire la paaɛ ya, ti a pɔga la me maahum ka mɛŋa suŋa ba'ahɛ.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Eŋa ti ba bo' en sore ti a bagɛ a mɛŋa la fusunɔ en nyagere gee pelege, fusunɔ la dela nɛresunduma la tuuntɔɔ tuuma la.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Ti suŋera la yele mam yeti, Gulehɛ dina wa: Sum boi mɛ la sɛba ti ba yi' ba, ti ba wa'am Pebila la pɔgedire zaanuurɛ dia la puan na la. Gee le yele mam yeti, Yele dina wa dela Yinɛ mɛŋa yelemɛŋerɛ yetɔgum.

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Ti mam nyaa lu a nama tiŋa ti n na'ahɛ en, gee a yele mam yeti, Da iŋɛ bala! Mam dela fum tuntuna tadaana bɔna la hɔ, la fum tansugere la en gurɛ Yezu yelehum muulegɔ kankaŋi la. Na'ahɛ Yinɛ! Se'ere en sɔi la, Yezu yelehum la muulegɔ en de purega sia.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti tinsuŋan yu'ɛ, ti mam yeti n bihɛ, ti yepɛɛlega en ze bini! Ti se'em en ba' bɔ la yu'urɛ yi'ira Sakerɛ la Yelemɛŋerɛ, a tari la tuntɔɔnɛ gakera nɛreba, gee zabera.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: A nini ani mɛ wuu bugum peelum la, ti muhi kekeko vuga a zuo, ti a tara yu'urɛ en gulehɛ lama en, ti se'em kan malum baŋɛ dɛ, han dagena a mɛŋa ma'a.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: A bagɛ la fuo en dikɛ luhɛ ziim puan, ti a yu'urɛ yi'ira Yinɛ yelehum.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Ti tinsuŋan pɛntiireba la, yɛ ba fusunɔ en de pɛɛlega gee nyagera, dɔla se'em en yage yepɛɛlega wa zuo.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Ti su'a ze'ele a nuuren yehe na, sɔ'sɛka ti a wan dikɛ zabɛ tihi nyaŋɛ, sooe si biha la pɛregerɛ. A wan sagum daam ŋmɛkerɛ zi'an la, la Gani za'a Yinɛ wa sunsunyiim tuulɛ.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: A fuo zuo, la a gbere zuo, a tari la yu'urɛ ti ba gulehɛ bini yeti, Naduma naba, la Daanduma Daana.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ayima ze' yintule puan, ya'ɛ la kɔkɛ'ɛŋa yi' niihe woo en ɛgeri saazuo wa yeti, Wa'am ya na, lagehɛ ya na di Yinɛ zaanuurɛ dikãtɛ ti a maahum.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: Wa'am ya na gɔ' naduma ihe nɛnɔ, pɛntiireba nɛhi duma ihe nɛnɔ, pahi duma ihe nɛnɔ, yiiri la i ba'areba ihe nɛnɔ, nɛrawoo nɛnɔ, sɛba en boi fai, la sɛba n de da'aba ihe nɛnɔ, la tarenduma la kpɛ'ɛnduma ihe nɛnɔ.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti bunsilemasio la, la timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma, la ba pɛntiireba, ti ba lagehɛ taaba ti ba tɛ' pɛɛma la se'em en ban' yepɛɛlega la, la a pɛntiireba la.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Ti ba nyɔkɛ bunsilemasio la, la a nɔtɔgehe pumparenduma la en daa ze'ele a nɛŋan tum nyalema sɛ'a, pã'ahɛ sɛba en to'e bunsilemasio la daalehum la, la sɛba en na'ahɛ bunsilemasio la naanɛ la. Bana bayi' wa, ba dikɛ ba lobe la voto bahɛ bugum mɔgerɛ la ti tahi bugum dita ka kiŋera la puan.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Gee ti sɛba woo en gee la, se'em en ban' yepɛɛlega la su'a en kɔrum ba za'a, sɔ'sɛka en ze'ele a nuuren yehe na la. Ti niihe za'a sãɛ di ta siiɛ.

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti suŋera ze'ele saazuo sigera na, ti a nu'uhen, a zãli la kparehega sɛka en tari kparegera boko seko en boi tiŋa tiŋa ha la, la zɔregɔ kãtɛ.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Ti a nyɔkɛ bunsɛla la, diimen diimen wɔbekimɔ se'em yu'urɛ en yi'iri Sagena, la Sitaani la, lu' en biŋe yuuma tuhere.

eng: He threw him into the abyss, then locked and sealed it over him. This was to keep him from continuing to deceive the nations until the thousand years were over. After this he must be released for a little while.
local: dikɛ en lobe bahɛ bilam kpãrɛ en gee li' en lebehe ha, ti a da le nyaŋɛ pã'ahɛ nɛreba, gura ti yuuma tuhere la ta ba'ahɛ. Ŋwana han tole, la dela ba le yu'ɛ en bahɛ na, ti la le iŋɛ fii.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Ti mam nyɛ na'am kugehi, ti sɛba en zi' si zuto la, ba bo' ba la paŋa ti ba gakera. Mam me nyɛ sɛɛhi en de sɛba ti ba fige ba zuto, ban nam Yezu, gee muula Yinɛ yelehum la zuo. Bama ka lagum pu'uhɛ bunsilemasio la bii a naanɛ la, bii ba to'e a daalehum la ba gbɛ'ɛran bii ba nu'uhen. Ba vo'e me na, dɛna kiinduma, la Kiresta yuuma tuhere la puan.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Sum, la tuntɔɔnɛ dela sɛba en ta lagum vo'e yia vo'ore la dinɛ. Bana ti kum buyi' la ka le tana la. Gee ba wan dɛna malema duma bo' Yinɛ, la a Kiresta la, gee dɛna kiinduma la en, yuuma tuhere la puan.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Yuuma tuhere la han ta ba'ahɛ, ba wan bahɛ Sitaani la yehe a paka zi'an la puan na.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: A wan yehe na ti a pã'ahɛ nɛreba en boi timpaberɛ wa luga anaahi wa puan za'a. Gɔg la Magog, la a lagehɛ ba ti ba tɛ' pɛɛma. Ba kãlɛ zo'e mɛ wuu kulaa tintombi'ihego la.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Ti ba lagehɛ kiŋɛ zom timpaberɛ wa zuɔ la gɔbega, kiŋɛ ta kaaɛ nɛresunduma bɔŋa zi'an la, la tinkãtɛ la mum, gee bugum en ze'ele saazuo na di ba za'a filam.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Ti Sagena se'em en pã'ahɛ ba la, ba lobe eŋa bahɛ la bugum mɔgerɛ la en diti la tahi bugum la puan, zi'an ti bunsilemasio la, la a pumpã'ahum nɔtɔgeha la bɔna la, ti ba bɔna mɛlehegɔ puan bulika la yu'uŋɔ za'a, ka malum tara ba'ahegɔ.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti na'am kuka kãtɛ pɛɛlega en ze', ti se'em en zi' ka zuo la, timpaberɛ wa la saazuo wa mɛlum mɛ a nɛŋan ti zi'an ka bɔna bo' ba.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Ti mam nyɛ ti sɛba en ki la, nɛre kãreba la tarenduma, zɛ'a na'am kuka kãtɛ la nɛŋan, ti gɔnɔ lagehɛ, gee ti gɔŋɔ kuyima me lakɛ, gɔŋɔ seko en de vom gɔŋɔ la, ti ba gakɛ sɛba en ki la, doohe la ban tum sɛla wuu lan gulehɛ se'em gɔnɔ la puan la.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Ti ateko dikɛ sɛba en ki bu puan la tihɛ, ti kum la ki'intiŋa dikɛ sɛba en ki ba puan tihɛ, ti ba gakɛ ba za'a doohe ban tum sɛla la.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Ti ba nyaa lobe kum, la ki'intiŋa bahɛ bugum mɔgerɛ la puan. Kum buyi' zi'an la en bala en de bugum mɔgerɛ la puan la.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: ti se'em woo yu'urɛ ti ba ka nyɛ ti de gulehɛ bɔna vom gɔŋɔ la puan la, ba lobe a daana bahɛ la bugum mɔgerɛ la puan.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Ti mam nyɛ saazuo paalega, la timpaberɛ paalega, se'ere en sɔi la, yia saazuo la, la yia timpaberɛ la mɛlum mɛ, ti ateko ka bɔna.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Ti mam nyɛ tuulum tiŋa la, Yerusalɛm paalega, ti ka ze'ele saazuo ha, Yinɛ zi'an, sigera na, ti Yinɛ maahum ka wuu pɔka en nɔm yɛ'a sire-ele la.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Ti mam wum ti kɔkɛ'ɛŋa ze'ele na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo tɔgɛ bahɛ na yeti, Bihɛ, Yinɛ yire en bala bɔna la saaleba wa. A wan bɔna la ba, dɛna ba Yinɛ, ti ba dɛna a nɛreba, Yinɛ mɛŋa en wan bɔna la ba.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: A wan duuhe nintahɔ woo ba ninin bahɛ. Kum kan le bɔna. Si'iŋɔ la kɛlega, la too kan le bɔna, Se'ere en sɔi la, yia yɛla la tole ya.

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Ti se'em en daa zi' na'am kuka kãtɛ la zuo la yeti, Bihɛ, n iti sɛla woo la paalega. Gee me yele pa'ahɛ yeti, Gulehɛ ŋwana wa biŋe, se'ere en sɔi la, yetɔge buna wa dela sakerɛ gee dɛna sira.

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Ti a nyaa yele mam yeti, La tum ya. Mam en de Alifa , la Omega , Pi'ileŋo, la Ba'ahegɔ. Sɛba ti ko'nyuuro tara ba, n wan bo' ba ko'om ti la dɛna bo'olum, ze'ele vom ko'om kulaa la puan na.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Se'em en nyaŋɛ la, en wan to'e lɔge tuna, ti n dɛna a Yinɛ, ti a dɛna n bia.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Gee han dɛna dabɛɛbehi la, la sɛba en ka sakɛ la, sɛba ti ba galum ba la, la nɛrekuureba la, yaluntuuma duma la, sɔ̃niniduma, bage kaabereba, la pumparenduma la za'a, ba bɔŋa zi'an ta bɔna la bugum mɔgerɛ la ti tahi taaɛ bugum dita la puan. Yi'i kum la en bala.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Ti suŋereba bayopɔi la ayima, se'em en zãli ni laahi siyopɔi la en pirɛ la ba'ahegɔ namehegɔ kãra ayopɔi la, wa'am na yele mam yeti, Wa'ana, n wan pa'alɛ hɔ pɔka la en yeti ka ele la. Pebila la pɔga la.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Ti a dikɛ mam sia puan, tara mam kiŋɛ zoore kãtɛ, zoore en wugɛ, ta pa'alɛ mam tuulum tiŋa Yerusalɛm, en ze'ele saazuo, ze'ele Yinɛ zi'an sigera na la.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Ti ka tari Yinɛ suŋa, nyagera wuu salema zɔtɔ la, nyagera wuu kugesuma la, ana yilaam wuu bihɛ nyagera la.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Ti nangoone kãtɛ, wugɛ kaaɛ ka, ti ka tara nayahi pia la siyi, ti suŋereba pia la bayi' la gura nayahi la. Ti nayaŋa woo puan, ba gulehɛ la Izirale yizuto pia la tuyi' la yu'ura labelɛ bini.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Dapɔya bɔba, nayahi sitã, zuɔ bɔba nayahi sitã, gɔbega bɔba nayahi sitã, zanɔya bɔba nayahi sitã.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Ti tiŋa la nangoone la me tara ɛ̃ba pia la ayi', ti ɛ̃berɛ woo puan, Pebila la tuntuneba pia la bayi' la yu'ura en gulehɛ labelɛ ɛ̃berɛ woo puan.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Suŋera se'em en tɔgeri bɔ'ɔra mam la zãli la magehegɔ doore en de salema, ti a magehɛ tiŋa la, la ka nayahi la, la ka nangɔɔma la.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Tiŋa la gã' la mɔkɔ mɔkɔ, ti ka luga la woko dɛna buyina la ka yaleŋa. Ti a magehɛ tiŋa la, la a salema magehegɔ doore la, ti ka woko dɛna miila tuhere la kɔbehi sinuu. Tiŋa la woko,la ka yaleŋa, la ka saazuo woko dela buyina.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Gee me makɛ nangɔɔma la, ti bamam me dɛna santira kɔbega, la santira pihinaahi la anaahi. Nɛreba en makere se'em en bala ti suŋera wa daa mageha la.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Kuga suma en nyagere ti ba dikɛ mɛ nangɔɔma la kaaɛ tiŋa la, gee salema ma'a en ani yilaam ti ba dikɛ mɛ tiŋa la mɛŋa.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Tiŋa la nangɔɔma ɛ̃ba la, ba gulɛ de mɛ la kuge suma yima yima kaaɛ. Yia ɛ̃berɛ kugere la dela kuge dɛlemadɛtɔ la sɔbele puuhego, yi'i dela kuge sɔbele puuhego, ta'a dela, kuge pumpɔreŋɔ, naahi dela, kuge sunɛ en nyageri.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: bunuu dela, kuge mɔlegɔ buyoobe dela, kuge saala en nyagere, buyopɔi dela, kuge dɔboolum, bunii dela, kuge sɔbelegɔ en de dɛlemadɛtɔ. , buwai' dela, kugere en de dɔboolum. , pia dela kuge dɛlemadɛtɔ , pia la buyina dɛna kugemɔlegɔ dɔboolum , ti pia la buyi' dɛna, kuge-mɔlepeelego.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Ti nayahi pia la siyi la, kayina woo dela nunɔm lɔkɔ. Ti tiŋa sadɔtɔ la woo dɛna salema nyagera wuu kɔleba la.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Mam ka nyɛ pu'uhegɔ yire tiŋa la puan, se'ere en sɔi la, ka pu'uhegɔ yire dela Daana Yinɛ la, Gani za'a Daana la, la Pebila la mɛŋa.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Ti wuntɛɛŋa bii ŋmarega niihum yele ka pakɛ tiŋa la, se'ere en sɔi la, Yinɛ suŋa la niihum la en de tiŋa la bugum, ti ka butolela dɛna Pebila la.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Tihi za'a wan bihera la a niihum buna kina, ti timpaberɛ wa zuo naduma wan tari ba suŋa wa'am la ka puan na.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Tiŋa la nayahi kan malum yu, ti yu'uŋɔ me kan bɔna ka puan.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Nɛreba wan zɛ'ɛta tihi tara ba pɛka, la ba na'ahegɔ kina ka puan na.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Gee sɛla en de galeŋɔ lɔkɔ kan kɛ̃ ka puan, bii se'em en iti be'em bii pumpɔreŋɔ tuuma. Gee sɛba ma'a yu'ura en gulehɛ bɔna Pebila la Vom gɔŋɔ la puan.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Ti suŋera wa pa'alɛ mam kulaa sɛka en tari vom ko'om la, ti ka nyagera, ana yilaam, ze'ele Yinɛ, la Pebila la na'am kuka kãtɛ la puan zɔta na.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
local: dɔla tiŋa la tiŋasuka palɛ la. Ti kulaa wa luga la za'a, vom tia la en ze' bini, wuna wala yima yima, pia la ayi', ŋmarega woo. Ti tia la vɔtɔ dɛna tibegɔ tibera tihi nɛreba.

eng: There will no longer be any curse. The throne of God and the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him.
local: Sɛla en de be'em lɔkɔ kan le puta bilam. Gee na'am kugekãtɛ en de Yinɛ la Pebila la dinɛ la wan bɔna bilam, ti a yamehi la na'aha en.

eng: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
local: Ba wan nyɛ a nɛŋa, ti a yu'urɛ gulehɛ lama ba gbɛ'ɛra.

eng: Night will be no more. They wont need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will shine on them, and they will rule forever and always.
local: Ti yu'uŋɔ kan le bɔna. Nɛreba kan le bɔta bugum bii butolela, bii wuntɛɛŋa. Daana Yinɛ la en wan dɛna ba niihum, ti ba dɛna naduma wuuha ka tara ba'ahegɔ.

eng: Then he said to me, These words are trustworthy and true. The Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants what must soon take place.
local: Ti suŋera la yele mam yeti, Yelehum buna wa dela sakerɛ gee dɛna sira, se'ere en sɔi la, Daana la, Yinɛ se'ere en sɔi nɔtɔgeheba la sɛɛhi la, en tum a suŋera wa na, ti a pa'alɛ a yamehi wa sɛla en yeti la iŋɛ nananawa.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. Favored is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll.
local: Bihɛ, n kini mɛ kalam kalam na! Sum wan bɔna la fum se'em daana en gurɛ nɔtɔgeheba yelehum la en boi gɔnkuna wa puan la suŋa suŋa.

eng: I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown them to me.
local: Mam Yɔn, mam de se'em en wum gee nyɛ yele ana wa. Mam wum gee nyɛ a la, mam lu la tiŋa suŋera sɛka en pa'alɛ mam yele ana wa la nama tiŋa ti n na'ahɛ en.

eng: But he said to me, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters, the prophets, and those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God.
local: Gee a yele mam yeti, Fum dagi fum na'ahɛ mam, mam me dela fum tuntuna tadaana, la fum taaba la, la nɔtɔgeheba la, la sɛba woo en dole gɔnkuna wa yelehum la. Bala na'ahɛ Yinɛ ma'a.

eng: Then he said to me, Dont seal up the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll, because the time is near.
local: Ti a yele mam yeti, Da pi' gɔŋɔ wa purega yelehum wa, se'ere en sɔi la, saŋa la pɔri mɛ.

eng: Let those who do wrong keep doing what is wrong. Let the filthy still be filthy. Let those who are righteous keep doing what is right. Let those who are holy still be holy.
local: Bahɛ ti se'em en tuni be'em, kelum tuna a be'em, ti se'em en tuni a dɛgerɔ tuuma, kelum tuna a dɛgerɔ tuuma, ti se'em en de tuuntɔɔ kelum vɔna tuuntɔɔ, ti se'em en de tuulum nɛra, kelum vɔna a tuulum vom.

eng: Look! Im coming soon. My reward is with me, to repay all people as their actions deserve.
local: Bihɛ, n kini mɛ kalam kalam na, n tari n yɔɔrɛ mɛ, ti n yɔ nɛra woo en tum se'em.

eng: I am the alpha and the omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
local: Mam n de Alifa , la Omega , yia la pooren, Pɔhega la ba'ahegɔ.

eng: Favored are those who wash their robes so that they may have the right of access to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
local: Sum wan bɔna la sɛba en pee ba futo, bala ti ba wan tara sore ti ba paaɛ vom tia la, gee me ta'am doohe tiŋa la nayahi la puan kɛ̃.

eng: Outside are the dogs, the drug users and spell-casters, those who commit sexual immorality, the murderers, the idolaters, and all who love and practice deception.
local: Yiŋa la ha, baahi, sɔ̃niniduma, yaluntuuma duma, nɛrekuureba, la bage kaabereba, la sɛba woo en nɔŋɛ gee tuna pumpɔreŋɔ tuuma en boi bilam.

eng: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to bear witness to all of you about these things for the churches. Im the root and descendant of David, the bright morning star.
local: La dela mam Yezu en tum n suŋera fum zi'an na, ti a yele fum, lageŋɔ zi'ihi la yɛla. Mam en de nyɛgerɛ la, gee dɛna Deviti yaaŋa la. Mam en de bulika beŋma'arɛ la.

eng: The Spirit and the bride say, Come! Let the one who hears say, Come! And let the one who is thirsty come! Let the one who wishes receive life-giving water as a gift.
local: Sia la, la pɔgeditiga la yeti, wa'ana. Bahɛ ti se'em woo en wum yelehum wa yeti, Wa'am na. Gee bahɛ ti se'em woo ti ko'nyuuro tari en wa'am na. Bahɛ ti se'em woo en boti, wa'am na to'e vom ko'om wa ti bu dɛna bo'olum bo' en.

eng: Now I bear witness to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy contained in this scroll: If anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues that are written in this scroll.
local: N kageri se'em woo en wum gɔŋɔ kuna wa purega wa. Ti se'em han dikɛ sɛla pa'ahɛ bini, Yinɛ me wan dikɛ too seko woo ti a tɔgɛ ku yele gɔŋɔ wa puan la, pa'ahɛ a daana zuo.

eng: If anyone takes away from the words of this scroll of prophecy, God will take away that persons share in the tree of life and the holy city, which are described in this scroll.
local: Se'em me han yehe sɛla gɔŋɔ wa purega wa puan bahɛ, Yinɛ me wan yehe nɛra eŋa daana tɔtɛ en de se'em vom tia la puan, la tuulum tiŋa la ti ba tɔgɛ ba yele gɔŋɔ wa puan la bahɛ.

eng: The one who bears witness to these things says, Yes, Im coming soon. Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!.
local: Se'em daana en nyɛ yele ana wa la yeti, Yelemɛŋerɛ, n kini la kalam kalam na. Taari, wa'am na Daana Yezu.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
local: Daana Yezu zu'uhegɔ, bɔna la nɛresunduma la woo.
